At the school function, when asked to thank someone, he looked at the crowd. He went to Miss Mary. "Miss, neenga illa na enaku idhu kedaikkadhu. Romba nandri." Miss Mary's eyes filled with tears.
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Miss, neenga illa na enaku indha scholarship kedaikkadhu.'
Miss Mary: 'Adhu un kastam dhaan kanna.'
Kumar: 'Aana neenga dhaan enaku padikka sonninga.'
Miss Mary: 'Un nandri dhaan enaku periya parisu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared near the school stage, clapping gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nandri illadhavan manushan illa. Nee nandri solla therinjavan. Athu unna periya aal aakkum."
thirukkural: எப்பொருள் யார்யார்வாய்க் கேட்பினும் அப்பொருள் மெய்ப்பொருள் காண்பது அறிவு.
english_translation: Whatever you hear from whomever you hear  Discern its truth — that is wisdom
moral: Never forget those who helped you. Gratitude is the mark of a good heart.

title: பேச்சு துணையாகுமா?
theme: Right Speech / The Value of Pleasant Words
hook: Leela was a quiet girl who barely spoke. She thought words were unnecessary. But an old pilgrim's loneliness showed her the power of a kind word.
story: At the temple, Leela saw an old woman sitting alone, looking lost. "Paati, ungalukku enna venum?" Leela asked shyly. The woman's face broke into a smile. "En kooda Konjam pesalaam?" They talked about the sea, the temple, and her grandchildren far away. The woman wept. "En kooda yarum pesa maatraanga. Nee pesina, enaku romba nalla irukku." Leela understood. A simple word can be a gift.
tamil_dialogues: Leela: 'Paati, ungalukku enna venum?'
Paati: 'En kooda konjam pesu da kanna. Yarum illa.'
Leela: 'Enna pesanum Paati?'
Paati: 'Un kannu, un granny, un school pathi sollu. Naan kettu sandhosha padren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared near the Agni Theertham, his white robes flowing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Innisai pesu. Athu dhaan manushanoda aayudham. Sarakku illadha vaarthai solla vendam. Aana innisai vaarthai dhaan manushanoda aayudham."
thirukkural: இனிய உரைத்தலின் இன்னாது இயக்குவது என்? இனிது ஆதல் அறிந்து.
english_translation: What is worse than harsh words?  Knowing the good and still not speaking it
moral: A kind word costs nothing but can be worth everything. Never withhold it.

title: பிறவி பயனுள்ளதா?
theme: The Purpose of Birth / Being Useful
hook: Surya asked a question that troubled him: "Appa, naan yenna ponna pora?" He felt lost watching the tea pickers. What was his purpose?
story: In the misty Ooty plantation, Appa was picking tea. "Appa, naan periya aal aaga mudiyuma?" Appa stopped. "Yenna periya aal? Nee periya aal aaga dhaan pora. Aana adhu vera level la?" "Enna level?" "Nee ullathula nalla irukka, athu dhaan periya level. Nee yarukavadhu udhava pora-nu irukka, athu dhaan periya visayam."
tamil_dialogues: Surya: 'Appa, naan enna ponna pora?'
Appa: 'Nee nalla irukka pora, athu dhaan un velai.'
Surya: 'Adhu pothuma?'
Appa: 'Nee yarukavadhu udhavi panna pora. Athu dhaan un piravi payan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stood among the tea bushes, breathing the cool air.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Piravi payan-nadhu mathavanga nalla irukka dhaan. Nee nalla iruntha, adhu pothum. Aana mathavangalukum nalla irukka dhaan."
thirukkural: மனத்துக்கண் மாசு இலன் ஆதல் அனைத்து அறன் ஆகும்.
english_translation: Purity of mind is the essence of virtue
moral: A life without purpose is like a ship without a rudder. Find your way to serve.

title: மனசு சுந்தரமா இருந்தா
theme: Beauty of the Mind / Inner Beauty
hook: Anjali looked in the mirror and hated what she saw. She compared herself to her "perfect" friend. Could a trip to the rose garden teach her about real beauty?
story: "Divya, unaku romba azhaga irukku. Enakku azhaga illa." Divya laughed. "Dei! Nee enna sollura? Azhagu-nadhu mookula, kannula irukkaadhu. Adhu ullathula irukku." Anjali didn't understand. Divya pointed at a rose. "Adhu evlo azhaga irukku! Aana rose-la evlo mul irukku theriyuma? Adhu dhaan unakezhu. Nee nallavala irukka, athu dhaan azhagu."
tamil_dialogues: Anjali: 'Divya, unaku romba azhaga irukku. Enakku apdi illa.'
Divya: 'Ullathula nalla irukka, athu dhaan azhagu. Mela azhagu poochu maadhiri.'
Anjali: 'Aana yarum enna paarkala.'
Divya: 'Paarkiravangaluku theriyum. Nee nallavala irukka, adhu azhagu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar was watering roses in the garden.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ullathula azhagu illa na, veliya azhagu enna? Azhagu ullathula iruntha dhaan veliya theriyum."
thirukkural: முகத்தான் இளமை நோக்கி அகத்தானாம் இன்னாது இயக்கும் எனின்.
english_translation: Outer beauty fades; inner beauty lasts forever
moral: True beauty is not in the face but in the heart. A beautiful heart outshines everything.

title: மூதுரை
hook: Prasanna thought old people's advice was boring. "Thatha always says 'old is gold' but that's for old times!" She discovered that ancient wisdom was surprisingly modern.
story: Thatha was telling stories again. "Unaku theriyuma, andha naatkalula..." Prasanna groaned. "Thatha, adhu ellam pazhaiya kathai." Thatha smiled. "Nee college-la eppadi friends kooda pesanum theriyuma? Adhukku 2000 varudham munnadiye solli irukkaanga." He quoted Thiruvalluvar about friendship. Prasanna was shocked. "Actually, adhu nalla dhaan irukku!" Ancient wisdom was still relevant.
tamil_dialogues: Prasanna: 'Thatha, adhu ellam pazhaiya kathai.'
Thatha: 'Pazhaiya kathai-nu illa. Adhu nithya kathai.'
Prasanna: 'Enaku adhu ellam bore.'
Thatha: 'Kural solli irukku — pazhaiya kathai-la dhaan nithya unmai irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared near the temple's golden roof, sitting cross-legged.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pazhaiya kathai-la dhaan periya unmai irukku. Adha nambu. Adhu unna periya aal aakkum."
thirukkural: நிறைந்த நிலமும் நிமிர்ந்த மரமும் இல்லை.
english_translation: There is no land so full, no tree so tall
moral: Old wisdom is timeless. Listen to your elders — their words carry centuries of truth.

title: கல்வி கண் திறக்கும்
hook: Murugan hated school. He wanted to work in the fields like his older brothers. But one science experiment changed his mind forever.
story: The teacher brought a magnet and some pins. "Paru Murugan. Ithu magnet. Ithu enna pannudhu theriyuma?" Murugan watched, fascinated. The pins jumped to the magnet. "Edhu! Eppadi?" The teacher explained. "School rumba bore-nu nenachiya? Idhu dhaan padikka vendiyadhu. Ulagam eppadi irukku-nu therinjukka." Murugan sat up straight. For the first time, he wanted to learn.
tamil_dialogues: Murugan: 'School romba bore sir.'
Teacher: 'Paru da, magnet enna pannudhu? Athu therinja bore illa.'
Murugan: 'Eppadi adhu ennama irukku?'
Teacher: 'Adhu dhaan education. Neraya therinjukka mudiyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared in the classroom, holding a book.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kalvi dhaan manushanoda kannu. Athu illa na manushan kuru nadha. Kalvi thaangum dhaan ulagam theriyum."
thirukkural: கல்வி கண் திறக்கும்; கண் திறந்தால் கல்வி கண்ணே.
english_translation: Learning opens the eye; when the eye opens, learning is the eye
moral: Education is the eye of the soul. Without it, we walk in darkness.

title: நல்ல நண்பன்
hook: Selvi was new in town. No friends, no one to talk to. She sat alone on the beach. Would she ever find a friend in this strange place?
story: Janani saw Selvi sitting alone. "Nee pudhu school ah?" Selvi nodded. "Enna peru?" They started talking. Janani shared her lunch. "Nee nalla padikra? Vaa, naan unakku help pannuraen." Selvi smiled for the first time in days. They watched the sunset together. "Nee en friend," Janani said simply. Selvi's heart filled with warmth. A friend was all she needed.
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Nee yaru kodai illama irukka?'
Selvi: 'Enaku yaarum illa inga.'
Janani: 'Naan irukken. Vaa, namma friendship aarambikkalaam.'
Selvi: 'Unaku nandri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sat on the promenade wall, watching the waves.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla friend-ukku mela oru nalladhu illa. Avan kastathula dhaan theriyum. Nee avana vittu kuda vida koodadhu."
thirukkural: உடையர் என்பர் உயர்ந்தோர் உறவினர்; உறவு இல்லார் உயர்ந்தோர் அல்லர்.
english_translation: The great are those with friends;  The friendless are not truly great
moral: A true friend is a treasure beyond compare. Cherish them.

title: அன்பு வழி
hook: Vetri was angry. His mother made him share his new toy with his little sister. "It's not fair!" He didn't understand why love had to be shared.
story: "Amma, adhu enoda! Enakku kudukkala?" His mother sat down. "Vetri, un mela enakku anbu irukku. Un thangachi melayum anbu irukku. Adhu nalla dhaaney?" "Aana adhu enoda!" "Nee avaloda share panna, adhu anbu. Anbu-na adhu share pannradhu dhaan." Vetri reluctantly gave the toy. His sister's smile was like sunshine. He felt warm inside.
tamil_dialogues: Vetri: 'Amma, adhu enoda dhaan!'
Amma: 'Anbu-na adhu share panna dhaan. Nee share panna, adhu anbu.'
Vetri: 'Aana adhu enoda!'
Amma: 'Nee share panna, adhu dhaan anbu valarudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared at the market, buying flowers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anbu dhaan manushana periya aal aakkum. Anbu illadha manushan kalla maadhiri. Anbu daan unna serntha aal aakkum."
thirukkural: அன்பு இல்லாதவன் அழகு இல்லை; அன்பு இல்லாதவன் அறிவு இல்லை.
english_translation: Without love there is no beauty;  Without love there is no wisdom
moral: Love is the foundation of all virtues. Share it freely.

title: வீடு
theme: The Home / A Place of Love
location: Chennai · Slum near the railway track
hook: Rajesh lived in a small hut near the tracks. He was ashamed of his home. His friend had a big house. But Appa showed him what a real home is made of.
story: "Appa, namma veedu romba sithu. Raju veedu periya veedu." Appa was fixing the roof. "Paru da. Namma veedu sithu dhaan. Aana inga anbu irukku. Namma ellam ore team. Raju veedu periya, aana avanga appa amma sanda podraanga. Sandhoshama irukkaanga? Namma veedu la, namma ore kooda koodi sirikkurome." Rajesh understood. Home is not a building. Home is love.
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Namma veedu romba sithu.'
Appa: 'Sithu dhaan, aana inga anbu irukku. Athu dhaan veedu.'
Rajesh: 'Raju veedu periya veedu.'
Appa: 'Periya veedula sandhosham iruntha dhaan. Namma veedu sithu, aana sandhosham irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared at their doorstep, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Veedu-na building illa. Athu anbu irukka oru idam. Anbu illa na adhu building dhaan."
thirukkural: வீடு என்பது இல்லம்; இல்லம் என்பது அன்பு உறைவிடம்.
english_translation: A home is not a house; a home is where love dwells
moral: A house becomes a home only when filled with love. Cherish your family.

title: பொறுமை பலம்
hook: Dinesh had a short temper. He would fight anyone who looked at him wrong. His uncle decided to teach him patience through a strange experiment.
story: "Dinesh, nee eppadi kovikkira, adha paathu enakku sirippa varudhu." Dinesh frowned. "Kovam dhaan enakku balam." Uncle brought a glass of water. "Nee kovam vara na, indha thanni-la uppu potu paru. Adhu enna aagum?" "Karaiyum." "Nee alaiya mattum panna, athu karaikadhu. Atha maadhiri dhaan kovam. Nee enga alaiya, adhu kovam aagum." Dinesh understood. He took a deep breath.
tamil_dialogues: Dinesh: 'Enaku kovam vantha dhaan mudiyum.'
Siva: 'Kovam unna balam aakkadhu. Athu unna tholaikum.'
Dinesh: 'Aana enna pannanum?'
Siva: 'Mudichu kovam iruntha, adhu dhaan balam. Thirachchu kovam iruntha, adhu balan illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared at the factory gate, sitting on a pile of cloth.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kovam varadha-nu irukka koodadhu. Aana kovama iruntha, atha madichu irukka dhaan. Madichu iruntha, adhu dhaan periya balam."
thirukkural: பொறுமை என்னும் கடல் நீந்தி; பொறுமை இல்லாதவர் வெள்ளம்.
english_translation: Patience is an ocean to cross;  The impatient drown in a stream
moral: Patience is not weakness. It is strength under control.

title: இயற்கை அழகு
theme: Nature / The Beauty of the Natural World
hook: Saranya was obsessed with her tablet. Games, videos, everything digital. Thatha took her to the lake and showed her something more beautiful than any screen.
story: "Thatha, enakku lake romba bore!" Thatha pointed at a kingfisher diving into the water. "Paru! Adha paaru!" The bird emerged with a fish. Saranya gasped. "Edhu! Adhu eppadi thanni-la mungi?!" Thatha laughed. "Indha maadhiri neraya visayangal irukku. Screen-la theriyum. Aana nijama paatha dhaan adhu azhagu." Saranya put down her tablet and watched.
tamil_dialogues: Saranya: 'Thatha, game vella dhaan nalla irukku.'
Thatha: 'Ulagatha paaru. Idhu dhaan nijamana game.'
Saranya: 'Adhu enna?'
Thatha: 'Paravai eppadi paarai-la irunthu meen pidikudhu. Adhu oru game maadhiri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared by the lake, feeding the ducks.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Iraivan padaippu romba alaga irukku. Adha paathu sandhosha padu. Adha kuthu. Adhu unna nalla aal aakkum."
thirukkural: உலகம் உயிர்க்கும் உடல்; உலகு அளிக்கும் உயிர் இல்லை.
english_translation: The world is the body of life; nothing supports life like the world
moral: Nature's beauty is the greatest gift. Protect and cherish it.

title: கருணை கடல்
hook: Bala was known as the class bully. He pulled chairs, hid books, teased everyone. But one day, he saw the result of his cruelty. A girl was crying. And he felt something new.
story: Priya was new. Bala hid her lunch box. She searched everywhere. "En lunch box enge?" she asked, tears in her eyes. Bala was about to laugh. But then he saw her face. It was just a cheap box. "Veetla appa kaasu kedaikadhu, avanga kastama vaangunanga." Bala's laugh died. He quietly placed the box on her desk. "Sorry. Engo vandhu irundhudhu." And he stopped bullying from that day.
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Nee en lunch box edukka...'
Priya: 'Adhu enakku romba mukkiyam. Enaku appa kaasu koda maata.'
Bala: 'Sorry. Nan dhaan eduthu vachirundhen.'
Priya: 'Nee enna?'
Bala: 'Inimel apdi panna maaten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared at the salt pans, watching the workers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karunai dhaan manushana periya aal aakkum. Karunai illadhavan manushan illa."
thirukkural: கருணை இல்லாதவன் கண் இல்லை; கருணை இல்லாதவன் நெஞ்சு இல்லை.
english_translation: Without mercy, there are no eyes;  Without mercy, there is no heart
moral: Compassion is what makes us human. Without it, we are nothing.

title: உண்மை பேசு
hook: Suresh broke his father's favorite turmeric scale. He was terrified. Should he lie? His father would be so angry. But he chose truth. What happened next surprised him.
story: The scale fell and cracked. Suresh's heart stopped. "Appa kovikkiraanga." He could hide it. He could blame the cat. But then he remembered: "Solludadhu dhaan nalladhu." He went to Appa. "Appa, naan thappu pannitten. Scale odachchu pochu." Appa looked at the scale, then at Suresh. "Nee unmai sonnadhu dhaan periyadhu." He hugged him. "Scale kooda pudhusa vaangalam. Aana unmai pudhusa kidaikadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Appa, naan odachchuten.'
Appa: 'Enna odachchu?'
Suresh: 'Scale. Enaku bayama irukku.'
Appa: 'Nee unmai sonnadhu dhaan periya visayam. Scale kooda vaanga mudiyum. Aana unmai kedaikadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared at the market, weighing turmeric.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unmai dhaan manushana periya aal aakkum. Poi solli enna? Poi adhilum unmai nalladhu."
thirukkural: உண்மை என்பது மெய்; மெய் என்பது மெய்யே.
english_translation: Truth is reality; reality is truth itself
moral: Truth may be hard in the moment but it brings peace. Lies only make things worse.

title: பொறுத்தல்
hook: Gayathri was teased by some older kids at the temple festival. She wanted to fight back. But her sister's words stopped her. Is enduring really a strength?
story: The older boys called her "fatty". Gayathri's face burned. "Avangala sanda poda poran!" Meenakshi held her hand. "Adha vida oru periya balam irukku. Thanga. Sanda poda maata. Avanga enna solla, adha thanga." Gayathri was furious. "Enna! Avanga enna ketta sollitu irukkaanga!" Meenakshi said softly: "Nee avanga kettadha thanga mudiyuma? Adhu dhaan periya balam."
tamil_dialogues: Gayathri: 'Avanga enna kettadha solluraanga. Sanda poda poran.'
Meenakshi: 'Adha vida periya balam irukku. Thanga.'
Gayathri: 'Aana adha thanga mudiyadhu!'
Meenakshi: 'Adhaan dhaan un test kanna. Kural solradhu — thavathai vida periya sudhar porai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared in the temple courtyard, the golden roof of Nataraja Temple behind him. He sprinkled holy water on Gayathri's head.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thavathula irundha sudhar. Aana veraththavaaiya thanga — adhu athai vida periya sudhar. Adhaan Kural-la solli irukkaanga."
thirukkural: தவத்தில் தங்கும் சுடர்; தவத்தில் தங்கும் சுடர் என்? தவம் இல்லாதவர் தாங்கும் சுடர்.
english_translation: More than ascetics they are pure  Who bitter tongues meekly endure
moral: Those who endure harsh words patiently are purer than ascetics who renounce the world.

title: யாரும் தவறு செய்யலாம்
hook: Kathir admired the temple priest who fasted for 40 days. "Look at his penance!" he said. But a simple village elder taught him that patience with people is an even greater penance.
story: "Priyar romba periya aalu da. 40 days upavasam irukkaar," Kathir said. Anbu smiled. "Kural solradhu — upavasathai vida periyadhu pirandha kettadha thanga dhaan. Avan Upavasam irundhaalum — pirandha avan thappu thangamaaten-na adhu periyadhu illa." The priest was known to get angry when kids played near. But old Kumar, a daily fisherman, never shouted at anyone. He was poor but always smiled. Kathir understood — forbearance with people is the greater penance.
tamil_dialogues: Kathir: 'Priyar upavasam irukkaar. Periya aalu!'
Anbu: 'Upavasathai vida periyadhu — pirandha ponnu thanga. Adhu dhaan Kural.'
Kathir: 'Aana Kumar uncle nalla illadhavan...'
Anbu: 'Avan yaraiyum kovikkala da. Adhaan periya penance.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared on the beach, sitting on an upturned boat. His hair was silver in the morning light. He looked at Kumar fishing and smiled.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Saptadha vida — pirandha kettadha thanga dhaan periya seyal. Upavasam pannravan periyavan kooda aagalaam. Aana pirandha kettadha thanga — adhu avana vida periyavan."
thirukkural: தவத்தில் தங்கும் சுடர்; தவத்தில் தங்கும் சுடர் என்? தவம் இல்லாதவர் தாங்கும் சுடர்.
english_translation: Who fast are great to do penance  Greater are they who bear offence
moral: Those who fast are great, but those who patiently bear others' harsh words are greater.

title: அழுக்காறு இல்லாத நெஞ்சம்
hook: Ananya's friend got a shiny new pencil box but she didn't. Can she be happy for her friend without feeling jealous inside?
story: Ananya and her friend Mithra were coloring in the temple courtyard. Mithra's appa bought her a beautiful new pencil box with cartoon stickers. Ananya felt a pang, but remembered what Appa always said — "ஒழுக்காறாக் கொள்க ஒருவன்தன் நெஞ்சத்து அழுக்காறு இலாத இயல்பு." She smiled and said, "Superaa irukku Mithra!" Appa saw her and gave a proud hug.
tamil_dialogues: Ananya: 'Mithra, un pencil box romba superaa irukku! Enakum oru vaati kaatuna paravalla?'
Mithra: 'Sure Ananya! Paru, ithula stickers ellam ottirukken.'
Appa: 'Nee Mithra va paathu poramai illama sonnala? Athaan un nenchukku nalladhu. Poramai illadha nencham dhaan nalla nencham.'
Ananya: 'Appa, poramai varadha irukka enna pannanum?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared from behind the stone pillar, a gentle smile on his face. His white beard glowed softly in the temple lamplight as he nodded at Ananya.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Dei kanna, poramai illadha nencham dhaan unakku oru nalla vazhakkaaga irukkum. Vera yaaroda nalla vishayatha paathu, atha pathi sandhosha padradhu dhaan unmaiyana nencham."
thirukkural: ஒழுக்காறாக் கொள்க ஒருவன்தன் நெஞ்சத்து அழுக்காறு இலாத இயல்பு.
english_translation: Deem your heart as virtuous  When your nature is not jealous
moral: A heart without envy is the truest virtue.

title: யாருக்கும் பொறாமை இல்லை
hook: Karthik's cousin came first in the drawing competition while Karthik came third. Instead of feeling bad, he discovered something wonderful.
story: Every year the temple held a big kolam competition. Karthik's cousin won first prize. Karthik felt sad and looked down at his third prize ribbon. Amma took his hand and said, "Paaru Karthik, nee unmaiya sandhosha padra maadhiri irukka? Un cousinoda drawing romba nalla irundhadhu dhaane?" Karthik thought about it and slowly nodded. "Amma, ava nalla varaiyara, athula enakku sandhosham dhaan."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Amma, en cousin ku first prize kedaichu, enakku third mattum dhaan.'
Amma: 'Adhunaala avanga sandhosha pada koodadhu? Un cousin nalla varainjadha poi paarthu sandhosha padu.'
Karthik: 'Aama Amma, ava drawing nalla dhaan irundhadhu. Ava deserve pannaa first prize!'
Amma: 'Appo dhaan un nencham nalladhu. Poramai illadha nencham dhaan periya vishesham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar emerged from the shadow of the massive vimana, his white robes blending with the stone carvings. He placed a hand on Karthik's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kanna, oruvan yar mellaam poramai illama irundhaa athai vida periya vishesham inga illa. Un nencham sandhoshama irukka, poramaiya vitaachu dhaan."
thirukkural: விழுப்பேற்றின் அஃதொப்பது இல்லையார் மாட்டும் அழுக்காற்றின் அன்மை பெறின்.
english_translation: No excellence excels the one  That by nature envies none
moral: Not envying anyone is the greatest excellence of all.

title: பிறர் ஆக்கத்தில் மகிழ்ச்சி
hook: Divya's Periyappa runs a small tea stall near the college. When a new fancy shop opens nearby, Divya starts feeling sour.
story: New tea shop opened right across Periyappa's stall. Divya glared at the new shop. "Periyappa, avanga shop romba aalu vaangaraanga. Namma shop la yaarum varadhu pola?" But Periyappa smiled. "Dei, avanga business nalla nadandha adhula enakku enna kashtam? Nammala vida avanga kastapadraanga." Divya understood — her jealousy was silly. She went and bought a biscuit from the new shop and smiled at the owner.
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Periyappa, avanga shop pakkathula vechutu namma business ku mudi panraanga!'
Periyappa: 'Divya, avanga nalla business nadathuraangana adhula poramai pada vendam. Namma nalla pannanum, avanga pannalaam.'
Divya: 'Aana Periyappa, enakku kovamaa irukku.'
Periyappa: 'Poramai apdinu oru aalu irukkaan. Avan namma nenchathula irundhaa, namma aakamum kettuppom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar was leaning against the tea stall, holding a small clay cup. His eyes twinkled as he overheard the conversation.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Poramaiyai vachukittu yaarum valara mudiyadhu. Vera yaaroda aakkama paathu poramai padaar, avar avaroda aakkamum keduppaar. Antha aalu avanai vida vendam."
thirukkural: அறன்ஆக்கம் வேண்டாதான் என்பான் பிறனாக்கம் பேணாது அழுக்கறுப் பான்.
english_translation: Who envies others' good fortune  Can't prosper in virtue of his own
moral: Whoever envies another's success cannot grow in virtue themselves.

title: அழுக்காற்றால் தீமை செய்யார்
theme: Not Envying / The Wise Avoid Evil through Envy
hook: Arjun sees his classmate winning all the prizes at the school function. A dark thought creeps into his mind.
story: Walking down the 400+ steps of Rockfort, Arjun kicked a stone angrily. His classmate had won three prizes. "Uncle, avan marks enakku vida kammi dhaan. Aana avanukku prizes!" Uncle stopped climbing. "Arjun, adha nenachu nee kettadhuna panna poreeya? Poramaiyaala ketta aalOcha varum, adha vida kedu vera edhum illa." Arjun took a deep breath. The cool breeze from the Kaveri calmed him.
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Uncle, avan enakku theriyaama prize vangi irukaan nu nenakren!'
Uncle: 'Adhu sariyaana vazhi illa. Poramai irundhaa kettadhu panna thonnum. Adha ni sollaama irundha nee nalla paiyyan.'
Arjun: 'Aana avana vida naan nalla padichen.'
Uncle: 'Adhum sari, avanum padichirukaan. Poramaiya vida nee nalla iru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sat atop the Rockfort steps, carving something on a palm leaf. He looked up as Arjun passed by.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arjun, poramaiyaala aalungal ketta vishayangala panna thonnum. Aana adhu avangala kedutaathu, namma dhaan keduvom. Nee adha therinju vachukitta, athu dhaan unakku nalladhu."
thirukkural: அழுக்காற்றின் அல்லவை செய்யார் இழுக்காற்றின் ஏதம் படுபாக்கு அறிந்து.
english_translation: The wise through envy don't others wrong  Knowing that woes from evils throng
moral: The wise avoid doing wrong through envy, knowing evil brings its own sorrow.

title: பொறாமை கொண்டவன் கேடு
theme: Not Envying / Envy Destroys the Envier
hook: Meena's neighbor got a new bicycle. Meena wanted one so badly that she started saying bad things about the neighbor.
story: Paati was feeding the pigeons near the Mahamaham tank. Meena sat grumbling. "Paati, Pooja veetu cycle oda tyre ennaamo ootudhu. Avalum nalla ootadhu." Paati frowned. "Meena, nee innorutharoda poramaikkaga avangala mosama sollura. Adhu unakku kedu dhaan. Poramai oru fire maadhiri, adhula unnai dhaan sutrukkum." Meena felt ashamed. She went and asked Pooja for a ride instead.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Paati, Pooja cycle nalla illa, avalum nalla ootamudiyadhu.'
Paati: 'Meena, nijama sonna, nee avala cycle vaangi kodukka sollale. Nee avala poramaikkaga dhaan apdi solra.'
Meena: 'Enaku cycle venum Paati!'
Paati: 'Poramai unnai nalla aalu aaka vidaadhu. Avanukku avanga cycle, unakku un cycle varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar was sitting on the tank steps, feeding the fishes. He looked up as Meena passed by with Paati.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Poramai undaanaalu adhu avanai suttu varum. Yethanai periya edhirigal irundhaalum poramai irukaaroda keduku poramai dhaan dhaan kaaranam."
thirukkural: அழுக்காறு உடையார்க்கு அதுசாலும் ஒன்னார் வழுக்காயும் கேடீன் பது.
english_translation: Man shall be wrecked by envy's whim  Even if enemies spare him
moral: Envy alone can destroy a person, even when no enemy attacks them.

title: கொடுப்பதற்கும் பொறாமையா?
hook: Vikram's friend shared his lunch with a poor boy. Vikram got angry and said his friend was showing off.
story: During lunch break, Vikram's friend Sandeep shared his biryani with a younger boy who had no lunch. Vikram muttered, "Appa, Sandeep show-off pannuraan. Ellarum paarkara maadhiri kudukuraan." Appa put down his paper. "Dei, innoruthar kudukuraadhula poramai padaara? Athu avanoda nalladhu. Nee enna podhum nalla vishayatha mosama solra?" Vikram realized the meanness in his own words.
tamil_dialogues: Vikram: 'Appa, Sandeep paaru, bayangara show-off! Lunch kudukuraan ellarkum theriya!'
Appa: 'Adhula poramai pada vendam. Avan avalavu dhairiyamaa nalla kaaryam pannuraan. Athai paarthu nee kathukka vendam.'
Vikram: 'Aana avan...'
Appa: 'Poramai unnai nalla paiyyan aaka vidaadhu. Vera yaaroda nalla karyaathai thappa eduthukka koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared under the big banyan tree in the school ground, holding a palm leaf. He smiled at Vikram.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kanna, innoruthar kudukuraanga atha kooda poramai padaar avanoda veetu aalum, sootti pottu saapaadu illaama keduvaan. Poramai periya ketuku kaaranam aagum."
thirukkural: கொடுப்பது அழுக்கறுப்பான் சுற்றம் உடுப்பதூஉம் உண்பதூஉம் இன்றிக் கெடும்.
english_translation: Who envies gifts shall suffer ruin  Without food and clothes with his kin
moral: Envying others' generosity brings ruin upon oneself and one's family.

title: பொறாமைக்காரனை விட்டு ஓடும் லட்சுமி
theme: Not Envying / Fortune Leaves the Envious
hook: Priya kept comparing herself with her cousin and wishing bad for her. But then something unexpected happened to Priya.
story: Priya's cousin was a great singer and won many competitions. Priya would sulk every time. One day, she accidentally dropped her cousin's medal and it got scratched. Periyamma saw this. "Priya, nee avala poramaila ava medalai kooda ketu panna prayasichu. Aana ava unnai vida naan sandhosham?" Priya started crying. "Illamma, enaku theriyama vizhundhudhu." Periyamma hugged her. "Poramai irundha adhu dhaan nammala thappu vazhiya nadathum."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Periyamma, avala poramaiku enakku kovamaa irukku!'
Periyamma: 'Poramai dhaan unnai indha maadhiri aakudhu. Avanga poramai illadhavanga kita dhaan selvam irukkum.'
Priya: 'Aana avala poramai padaama irukka mudiyala.'
Periyamma: 'Poramai periya pavi. Adhu namakku kedu mattum dhaan tharum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stood beside the thousand-pillar mandapam, his figure blending with the ancient stone. He watched Priya with kind eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Poramai utaiya aalungalai poi sella, enga poi selvar? Adhu dhaan avanga kedukku kaaranam. Poramai illadhavanga pakkam dhaan sellam irukkum."
thirukkural: அவ்வித்து அழுக்காறு உடையானைச் செய்யவள் தவ்வையைக் காட்டி விடும்.
english_translation: Fortune deserts the envious  Leaving misfortune omnious
moral: Fortune leaves the envious and brings misfortune in its place.

title: பொறாமை என்கிற பாவி
theme: Not Envying / Envy as a Sinner
hook: Ravi's thatha is a famous silk weaver. When the neighbor's silk sold at a higher price, Ravi wanted to ruin their loom.
story: Ravi watched as tourists bought silk from the neighboring weaver. "Thatha, avanga silk thread kaila kettuppom. Appo nammota silk dhaan vanguraanga!" Thatha stopped weaving. "Ravi! Poramai periya pavi maadhiri. Adhu namma irukura selvatthayum keduthidu, kaaranam kutty vida poramai nammala kuzhithula thalli vitum." Ravi felt his face burn with shame. He went back to weaving with Thatha.
tamil_dialogues: Ravi: 'Thatha, avanga silk namma silk vida mosam! Aana avanga rate nammala vida jaasthi!'
Thatha: 'Poramai oru pavi. Adhu irundhaa, avan irukura aakkamum pogum. Athoda kaariyam nammala kettadhu panna thoomum.'
Ravi: 'Aana enaku romba poramaiyaa irukku Thatha.'
Thatha: 'Poramai kedukka varadha vida, athai nenchil irunda koodathu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared among the silk sarees hanging on the racks, his white robes the only plain cloth in the room.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Poramai oru pavi maadhiri. Athu namma irukura selvatthai keduthu, nammala kettadhu panna thoonum. Atha vida periya ketu vera edhum illa."
thirukkural: அழுக்காறு எனஒரு பாவி திருச்செற்றுத் தீயுழி உய்த்து விடும்.
english_translation: Caitiff envy despoils wealth  And drags one into evil path
moral: Envy is a wicked force that destroys wealth and leads to evil.

title: பொறாமைக்காரனுக்கும் செல்வமா?
theme: Not Envying / The Puzzle of Envy and Wealth
hook: Shruti noticed that her uncle who was always jealous of others had a big house and car. She was confused about how that could be.
story: At the Golden Temple, Shruti asked, "Amma, en uncle romba poramai padaar. Aana avar romba seemaana veetu kaaran. Epdi?" Amma sat down near the temple pond. "Shruti, ivan dhaan poramai padaar, Ivan dhaan valara maataar — apdinu yaarum solla mudiyadhu. Poramai avana vida namma dhaan ketuppom. Avan irukura oru nalladhu kooda poramaila kettadhaaga maari, avan sandhosham illama iruppaan."
tamil_dialogues: Shruti: 'Amma, uncle poramai padaar aana avaruku bungalow irukku!'
Amma: 'Poramai irundha kooda veliya aakam irukalam. Aana poramai avara vida nammala dhaan kedukkum.'
Shruti: 'Aana appo poramai padaama irukka enna?'
Amma: 'Poramai padaara aalungal nenchula sandhosham irukkaadhada. Athu dhaan periya kedu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sat beside the temple pond, his reflection clear in the still water. He beckoned Shruti closer.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Poramai padaara aalungaluku veliya selva irukalaam. Aana nenchula avanga kedu dhaan irukkum. Poramai padaama irukaarungaloda nenchu sandhoshama irukkum, adhu dhaan periya selva."
thirukkural: அவ்விய நெஞ்சத்தான் ஆக்கமும் செவ்வியான் கேடும் நினைக்கப் படும்.
english_translation: Why is envy rich, goodmen poor  People with surprise think over
moral: The envious may prosper outwardly, but the pure-hearted have true wealth.

title: பொறாமையற்றவன் வாழ்வு
hook: Ganesh was tired of always losing to his best friend in every game. He finally decided to stop wishing bad for him.
story: Ganesh and his friend Kumar played marbles every evening by the Bhavani river. Kumar always won. Ganesh would go home grumpy. One day, Appa joined them. "Ganesh, Kumar thanichu jaisikkuraana? Nee apdi poramaikaaga avana keduka prayasa padura?" Ganesh looked down. "Appa, avan jaisikuraan. Enakku kovam varudhu." Appa said, "Poramai illadhavan dhaan peruvalam aakuvaan. Avan jaisichaa avaloda sandhoshama iru."
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Appa, Kumar dhaan jaisichittu irukaan. Enakku bayangara poramai!'
Appa: 'Poramai padaara aalungal valara maataanga. Poramai illadhavanga dhaan perisaa valaraanga.'
Ganesh: 'Aana enakku poramaiya vida mudiyala.'
Appa: 'Nee poramai padaadha neram un nenchu sandhoshama irukkum. Appo unaku nalladhu dhaan nadakkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stood knee-deep in the Bhavani river, washing a palm leaf. His voice carried over the water like a song.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Poramai padaara oruvan valara maatahan. Poramai illadhavan dhaan perisaa valaranum. Eththanai poramai padaara aalungal keduchu irundhaalum poramai illadhavanga dhaan nilaichu varuvaanga."
thirukkural: அழுக்கற்று அகன்றாரும் இல்லை அஃதுஇல்லார் பெருக்கத்தில் தீர்ந்தாரும் இல்.
english_translation: The envious prosper never  The envyless prosper ever
moral: Those who envy never truly prosper; those who don't envy never stop growing.

title: பிறர் பொருளை விரும்பாதே
theme: Not Coveting / Coveting Ruins the Family
hook: Lakshmi saw her neighbor's beautiful gold earrings and wanted them so badly that she planned to take them.
story: At Silver Beach, Lakshmi's neighbor was playing with her gold earrings. Lakshmi whispered to Amma, "Amma, ava earrings romba azhaga irukku. Enakum adhe maadhiri earrings venum." Amma looked serious. "Lakshmi, innorutharoda porulai kavalichaa adhu namma veetuku kedu dhaan tharum." Lakshmi kept quiet. Later she saw that the earrings slipped off and fell in the sand. She could have taken them but she returned them instead.
tamil_dialogues: Lakshmi: 'Amma, ava earrings kai la vizhundhudhu! Yaarum paarkala. Naan eduthukalaama?'
Amma: 'Lakshmi! Innoruthar porulai edutha athu namma kudumbathe kedukkum. Nee adha thirumba koduthu.'
Lakshmi: 'Aana amma, avanga antha earrings paathutu namma kitta vandhaanga!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhaan sari. Un nenchu nalla irukka kadaisila nalladhu dhaan varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar was sitting under a palm tree, writing something on a leaf. He looked up as Lakshmi walked by.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nerama illama innorutharoda porula kavalicha namma kudumbamum, nalladhum kedum. Nee adha thirumba kuduthadhu nalla kaaryam."
thirukkural: நடுவின்றி நன்பொருள் வெஃகின் குடிபொன்றிக் குற்றமும் ஆங்கே தரும்.
english_translation: Who covets others' honest wealth  That greed ruins his house forthwith
moral: Coveting another's possessions ruins the family and brings immediate harm.

title: நாணம் உடையவர் வெஃகார்
hook: Venkat found an expensive fountain pen that fell from a visitor's pocket. His hands itched to keep it.
story: A big businessman visited Periyappa's cotton mill. His expensive pen fell near Venkat's feet. Nobody noticed. Venkat picked it up quickly. His heart was racing. Periyappa saw him. "Enna kaila?" Venkat hid his hand. "O... onnum illa." Periyappa said softly, "Innoruthar porul vekkura aal nammala mosam aakuvaan. Nee nalla paiyyan, atha thirumba koduthudu." Venkat hesitated, then ran after the visitor.
tamil_dialogues: Venkat: 'Periyappa, antha aalu pen vizhuthaachu. Yaarum paarkala. Enaku vaichukalaama?'
Periyappa: 'Nee innoruthar porulai kavalichaa, naalu perum unna kevalam solluvaanga. Unakke vedhamaa irukkum.'
Venkat: 'Aana Periyappa, romba azhaga irukku!'
Periyappa: 'Nalladhu iruntha kooda vechikira kosham unna vida vendam. Athu dhaan unakku nalla kaaval.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared beside the cotton spinning machine, his white clothes spotless despite the dust.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naanam ulla aalungal innoruthar porulai kavala maataanga. Avanga dhaan kettadhu panna maataanga. Avanga nenchu avanga kattu patta irukkum."
thirukkural: படுபயன் வெஃகிப் பழிப்படுவ செய்யார் நடுவன்மை நாணு பவர்.
english_translation: Who shrink with shame from sin, refrain  From coveting which brings ruin
moral: Those who feel shame at wrongdoing never covet what brings ruin.

title: சிற்றின்பம் வெஃகி அறனல்ல செய்யார்
theme: Not Coveting / Lasting Joy vs Fleeting Pleasure
hook: Jessica's classmate had the newest video game. Jessica wanted to "borrow" it without asking.
story: In the cool Kodaikanal air, Jessica and Appa sat among the flowers in Bryant Park. "Appa, Sarah-ku oru pudiya game irukku. Ava kaila irundhu naan vangi vilayaaduradhu pathi yosichaan." Appa smiled. "Adhu thirudu dhaane?" "Aana avalukku theriyaathu," Jessica whispered. Appa said, "Nee konjam naal sandhosham iruppa. Aana approm nee kavalai paduva. Periya sandhosham venum na, vaai maathi kekka koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Jessica: 'Appa, Sarah game kaila irundhu konjam vilayaada eduthutu vandhudalaama?'
Appa: 'Athu thirudu madhiri dhaan. Konja naal sandhosham, aana adhuku approm kashtam.'
Jessica: 'Adhu periya vishayama?'
Appa: 'Nee periya sandhosha pathi yosichaa, innoruthar porulai vekkura dhairiyama varadhu. Athu dhaan periya inbam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stood among the rose bushes, a single flower in his hand. His gaze was soft as he watched Jessica.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periya inbam venumna aalungal innoruthar porulai vekkura maataanga. Siriya inbathukkaaga periya inbatha vida koodadhu."
thirukkural: சிற்றின்பம் வெஃகி அறனல்ல செய்யாரே மற்றின்பம் வேண்டு பவர்.
english_translation: For spiritual bliss who long  For fleeting joy commit no wrong
moral: Those who seek lasting joy do not covet and do no wrong.

title: இல்லாத போதும் வெஃகமாட்டார்
theme: Not Coveting / The Wise Don't Covet Even in Need
hook: Abdul's family was going through a hard time. When he found a wallet full of money on the beach, he was torn.
story: Abdul collected shells on Nagapattinam beach to sell to tourists. Business was slow. He found a leather wallet buried in the sand — full of hundred rupee notes. His heart pounded. He could buy food for a week. He ran to Amma. "Amma, paaru! Kedaichudhu!" Amma looked at the wallet. "Idhu yaaroda? Nee adha thedi kodukanum." Abdul cried, "Aana amma, namma kai la onnum illa!" Amma hugged him. "Nee aasai pada koodadhu innoruthar porulai."
tamil_dialogues: Abdul: 'Amma, ivvalavu kasu! Naama vachikalaama?'
Amma: 'Namma kastama irundha kooda innoruthar porulai aasai pada koodadhu. Yaarotha munnadi vizhundhadho avangala thirumba koduthuvom.'
Abdul: 'Aana nammakum kasu venum.'
Amma: 'Nijamana aalungal kasu illadha podhum innoruthar porulai vekkara maataanga. Nalla aalunga apdi dhaan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stood near the fishing boats, watching the sunset. His silhouette glowed gold as Abdul approached.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kasthama irundha kooda unmaiya aalungal innoruthar porulai aasai pada maataanga. Avanga nenchu avanga kaaval dhaan avanga."
thirukkural: இலமென்று வெஃகுதல் செய்யார் புலம்வென்ற புன்மையில் காட்சி யவர்.
english_translation: The truth-knowers of sense-control  Though in want covet not at all
moral: The wise who have mastered themselves do not covet, even in poverty.

title: அறிவு இருந்தும் வெஃகினால்?
theme: Not Coveting / Wisdom Worthless with Coveting
hook: Nandini was known as the cleverest girl in class. But Athai caught her trying to steal flowers from the garden.
story: In the Botanical Gardens, Nandini saw beautiful purple flowers. She looked around and started picking them. Athai stopped her. "Nandini! Idhu gardenoda flower. Nee edutha koodadhu." Nandini pouted. "Aana athai, enakku romba pudichirukku!" Athai said, "Nee padichavan. Aana innoruthar porulai vekkura alavu unakku ariyudha? Padichavan avanuko, adhu dhaan avana kettadhu seiyaadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Nandini: 'Athai, oru flower edutha enna thappu?'
Athai: 'Adhu gardenoda, namma flower illa. Nee padichavan, unakku sari theriyum. Aana innoruthar porulai vekkura aal aagidaadh dhaan vena.'
Nandini: 'Solla theriyum, aana manasula aasai varudhu.'
Athai: 'Un ariyu un kaila irundha adhuku vila illa. Nee adha visayamaaga poda koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar was sitting on a bench among the ferns, writing on a palm leaf. He looked up with a gentle smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Evvalavu padichaalum innoruthar porulai aasai pada arambichaa, antha ariyu onnumum ila. Avan nenchathula nalladhu irunda dhaan antha ariyuku vilai."
thirukkural: அஃகி அகன்ற அறிவென்னாம் யார்மாட்டும் வெஃகி வெறிய செயின்.
english_translation: What is one's subtle wisdom worth  If it deals ill with all on earth
moral: What good is wisdom if one covets and does wrong to others?

title: பொருள் வெஃகிப் பொல்லாத சூழ்ச்சி
theme: Not Coveting / Coveting Destroys the Righteous
hook: Siddharth's friend told him about an easy way to make money — taking things from the temple hundial. Siddharth was tempted.
story: Climbing the 69 steps to Palani Murugan, Siddharth was quiet. His friend had whispered, "Rathiri gopuram saathu irukrapo, namakku eduthukkalam, yaarukkum theriyaadhu." Thatha noticed his silence. "Enna da yosichitu varaiya?" Siddharth finally confessed. Thatha sat down on a step. "Nee arul pathi yosicha aalu. Innoruthar porulai kavali kavali, nee nalla aalu aaga maata. Arul vekura aalu porul vekkura maatavu."
tamil_dialogues: Siddharth: 'Thatha, en friend solli thandha plan oru... temple la irundhu eduthukalam.'
Thatha: 'Siddharth! Nee arul vekura paiyaan. Aana innoruthar porulai vekkuraal un arulum pogum, nalladhum pogum.'
Siddharth: 'Aana evlo kasu kedaikkum Thatha.'
Thatha: 'Arul nenchula irunda, porul aasai nammala kedukkum. Nee arul paathu nalla vazhiya nadai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar was standing at the top of the hill, his white robes flowing in the wind. He looked down at Siddharth.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arul vekuvar porul vekkura maataanga. Porulai aasai padra aalungal, avanoda arulum kettu, avan valiya vida, avan keduvaan."
thirukkural: அருள்வெஃகி ஆற்றின்கண் நின்றான் பொருள்வெஃகிப் பொல்லாத சூழக் கெடும்.
english_translation: Who seeks for grace on righteous path  Suffers by evil covetous wealth
moral: One who walks the path of grace is ruined by coveting wealth.

title: வெஃகிய ஆக்கம் வேண்டாம்
hook: Anitha saw tourists buying pearls at high prices. She thought of secretly diving to get pearls from the protected zone.
story: Anitha's chithi sold pearls at the harbor. Some areas were closed for pearl collection. "Chithi, andha side la niraiya pearl irukku. Raathiriku poi eduthukalaam. Yaarukum theriyaadhu!" Chithi dropped her basket. "Dei, adhu koodadhu. Seiyara vilaiyellam mosam dhaan. Antha pearl vitta varum kasanum, athai vida nalla iruka vendam." Anitha was disappointed. But later she saw two men being caught by the coast guard for the same thing.
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Chithi, raathiriku andha protected area la pearl eduthukalaama?'
Chithi: 'Koodadhu. Antha pearl nammala sandhosha pada vaikkum? Illa, nammala keduthidum. Adha vida namba vekkama irukradhu dhaan periya aakam.'
Anitha: 'Aana evlo kasu kedaikkum.'
Chithi: 'Vekkura aalungal sambathikkura kasanum perire. Aana antha vilai atha vida mosam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sat on a stack of fishing nets, mending a hole. His eyes never left Anitha as he worked.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vekki vangura aakam perisa irundha kooda, athoda payan mosam dhaan. Adhu nammala nallaa vidaadhu. Adhanaala vekkama irukradhu dhaan nalladhu."
thirukkural: வேண்டற்க வெஃகியாம் ஆக்கம் விளைவயின் மாண்டற் கரிதாம் பயன்.
english_translation: Shun the fruit of covetousness  All its yield is inglorious
moral: Shun wealth gained through coveting — its fruit is always bitter.

title: வெஃகாமையே நிலையான செல்வம்
theme: Not Coveting / Lasting Wealth is Not Coveting
hook: Kumar's friend had every toy imaginable. Kumar felt poor and wanted what his friend had.
story: At the busy Karur textile market, Kumar watched his friend Aravind buy another new toy. "Appa, Aravind-ukku ellam irukku. Enakku onnum illa." Appa was folding cloth. "Nee enna nenaikira? Aravind-oda porul ellam avanukku sandhosham tharudha? Atho pakkathula paaru — namma naikku kuda onnum illa, aana athu happy-a irukku." Kumar looked at the street dog wagging its tail. "Nee innoruthar porulai aasai pada maata, adhu dhaan nilaichana selva."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Appa, Aravind-ukku evlo porul irukku! Enakku onnum illa.'
Appa: 'Aravind-oda porul avanukku sandhosha tharalaam. Aana adhu nilaichadhu illa. Nee innoruthar porulai aasai padaama iruntha, adhu dhaan nilaichana selva.'
Kumar: 'Adhu epdi Appa?'
Appa: 'Vekkama irukaarukku avanga selva kedaiyadhu. Athu dhaan nilaichana selva.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stood between two cloth stalls, his plain white dhoti contrasting with the colorful textiles around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nilaichana selva endraal adhu innoruthar porulai vekkama irukradhu dhaan. Vekkura aalungal selva avanga kooda nilaikadhu. Aana vekkama irukaarungaloda selva nilaichu irukkum."
thirukkural: அஃகாமை செல்வத்திற்கு யாதெனின் வெஃகாமை வேண்டும் பிறன்கைப் பொருள்.
english_translation: The mark of lasting wealth is shown  By not coveting others' own
moral: The secret to lasting wealth is never coveting what belongs to others.

title: வெஃகா அறிவுடையாரைத் தேடும் திரு
theme: Not Coveting / Fortune Finds the Non-Coveter
hook: Bhavani's family lost their shop in a storm. Her friend's family bought a new shop the next week. Bhavani felt like the world was unfair.
story: At the temple, Bhavani watched her friend light lamps for the deity. "Periyamma, avanga shop pudichi vachitanga. Namma keta porom. Illaina koduthu vachuruppaanga!" Periyamma closed her eyes. "Bhavani, nee yosikira. Avar Sri Mahalakshmi kadavul, ellam sari pannuva. Nee innoruthar porulai aasai padaama iru. Nee aasai padaama irundha, thirusuthi unnai thedi varum." Bhavani didn't understand then. But a month later, a stranger helped them restart their shop.
tamil_dialogues: Bhavani: 'Periyamma, avangalukku koduthu vachurukkaanga. Nammakku onnum kodukala.'
Periyamma: 'Kavalai padaadha. Nee innoruthar porulai aasai padaama iru. Arivudaiya aalungal apdi dhaan iruppaanga.'
Bhavani: 'Aana enakku kovama irukku.'
Periyamma: 'Arivudaiya aalungal innoruthar porulai aasai pada maataanga. Avanga pakkam dhaan thiru thirumbum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stood beneath the temple's tall gopuram, his face lit by the flickering oil lamps. He smiled at Bhavani.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Innoruthar porulai aasai padaadha arivudaiya aalungal pakkam dhaan thiru poga. Vekkura aalungal vanangala kuda thiru avanga pakkam irukkaadhu."
thirukkural: அறனறிந்து வெஃகா அறிவுடையார்ச் சேரும் திறனறிந் தாங்கே திரு.
english_translation: Fortune seeks the just and wise  Who are free from coveting vice
moral: Fortune naturally finds those who are wise and free from coveting.

title: வேண்டாமை என்னும் செருக்கு
hook: Shiva's classmate had a fancy new bicycle. When he left it unlocked, Shiva had a moment of temptation.
story: The new bicycle was shining in the sun, keys still in the lock. Shiva's hands were shaking. "Amma, avan cycle vida paathaan. Naan eduthuttu oru round ponaalum avanu theriyaadhu." Amma was feeding the temple fish. "Adhu thirudu. Unaku adhu sari nu theriyum. Vekkama iruka aalungal dhaan periya aalungal. Nee periya aal aaga aasai padra, illa siriya aal aaga?" Shiva slowly walked away from the bicycle.
tamil_dialogues: Shiva: 'Amma, avan cycle vida paathu vachurukaan. Orutharukkum theriyaadhu.'
Amma: 'Adhu sariyaana kaaryam nu nenaikra? Vekkama irukradhu dhaan nammala perisaa kaatum.'
Shiva: 'Aana enakku cycle romba venum.'
Amma: 'Vekkama irukradhu dhaan periya veeram. Adhu dhaan yellaathayum jeyikkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sat cross-legged by the temple pond, the coiled serpent of the Nagaraja deity visible behind him. His voice was calm and strong.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vekkama irukaarungal dhaan periya aalungal. Avanga vekkama irukradhu dhaan avanga periya aakka kaatum. Aana vekkura aalungal ketu poraanga."
thirukkural: இறலீனும் எண்ணாது வெஃகின் விறல்ஈனும் வேண்டாமை என்னுஞ் செருக்கு.
english_translation: Desireless, greatness conquers all;  Coveting misers ruined fall
moral: The pride of desirelessness brings greatness; coveting brings only ruin.

title: புறங்கூறாதவன் புகழ்
hook: Padma's friend said something mean about another girl. Padma wanted to join in, but something stopped her.
story: Padma and her friends sat by the Ramanathapuram shore. Her friend started gossiping about a girl in their class. "Avala pathi theriyuma? Ava dhaan teacher table mela..." Padma's Appa had told her about backbiting. She stood up. "Nama avala pathi pesa vendam. Ava illadha idathula avala pathi solradhu sari illa." Her friends got quiet. Later, Appa heard and hugged her. "Nee nalla paiyyan da."
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Avala pathi theriyuma? Ava class la bayangara show-off!'
Padma: 'Ava illadha idathula apdi pesa vendam. Nama avala pathi sari illadhadha pesa koodadhu.'
Friend: 'Adhan enna da? Nama unmaiya dhaan solrom.'
Padma: 'Ava illadha idathula avala pathi pesa, athu dhaan thappu. Nama face-la kooda keka maatom apdi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar walked on the water's edge, his feet not disturbing the waves. He came to Padma and smiled.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvan evvalavu thappu panna kooda, avan munnadi illadha idathula avan pathi kettadhu solradhu dhaan periya kedu. Nee adha pannaadha, unaku nalladhu."
thirukkural: அறங்கூறான் அல்ல செயினும் ஒருவன் புறங்கூறான் என்றல் இனிது.
english_translation: Though a man from virtue strays,  To keep from slander brings him praise
moral: Even if one errs in other virtues, never backbiting brings its own praise.

title: முன்னால் சிரித்துப் பின்னால் குத்து
hook: Manoj saw his friend laugh with a boy and then whisper bad things about him behind his back. It disturbed him deeply.
story: Climbing the steep Dindigul fort, Manoj was quiet. "Amma, Ravi naan paarka munnadi Suresh kooda sirichitu, avan pona udane enna verum aalu nu solraan." Amma stopped climbing. "Avaloda nalladhu illada? Munnadi sirichitu pinnadi kettadhu pesuravan, avan vida ketta aalu inga yarum illa." Manoj asked, "Naan enna pannanum?" Amma said, "Avala maadhiri iruka vendam. Munnadi yenna solra, adhe pinnadiyum solra aalu aaga vendam."
tamil_dialogues: Manoj: 'Amma, Ravi Suresh kooda sirichitu, pona udane avan pathi kettadhu pesuraan.'
Amma: 'Idhu dhaan kettadhu. Munnadi oru maadhiri, pinnadi innoru maadhiri. Ithu nammala kedukkum.'
Manoj: 'Aana Ravi nalla friend maadhiri dhaan nadandhukuran.'
Amma: 'Unnoda friend Ravi unmai illa. Nee Ravi maadhiri aagadha. Munnadi yenna solra adhe pinnadi solradhu dhaan nalla paavam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar leaned against the fort wall, looking out at the valley below. He turned and spoke.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvan munnadi sirichitu pinnadi kettadhu pesuraan, avan vida periya ketta aalu illa. Avan nalla paavam dhaan adhu."
thirukkural: அறனழீஇ அல்லவை செய்தலின் தீதே புறனழீஇப் பொய்த்து நகை.
english_translation: Who bite behind, and before smile  Are worse than open traitors vile
moral: Those who smile to your face and speak behind your back are worse than open enemies.

title: பொய்ச்சிரிப்பை விட சாவு மேல்
hook: Swetha's friends wanted her to spread a rumor about a girl they didn't like. She had to choose between friendship and doing right.
story: Three friends cornered Swetha near the cracker shop. "Nee dhaan antha ponna pathi solanum. Nee sonna dhaan yarum nambuvaanga." Swetha felt trapped. She went home to Appa, crying. "Appa, avanga nanna vida maataanga. Enna pannanu?" Appa hugged her. "Swetha, sariyaana kaaryam panna bayapada vendam. Un friend kootam unna vida vittutaanga na, adhuku saavadhu nalladhu. Pinnadi pesi-siri maathiri irukradhu vida saavadhu nalladhu."
tamil_dialogues: Swetha: 'Appa, avanga nanna vida maataanga. Pinnadi pesa solluraanga.'
Appa: 'Nee enna pannanum nu nenaikra?'
Swetha: 'Enaku panna pidikkala. Aana naan avanja vida mudiyadhu.'
Appa: 'Pinnadi pesi, poy sirichu vazhadha vida, nijama irukra, unmaiya pesa, sariyaana vazhiyila irukradhu dhaan nalladhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar walked through the spark-lit streets, the firecracker light dancing around him. He stopped at their doorstep.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pinnadi pesi, poy sirichu irundhu vazhalum, atha vida saavadhu nalladhu. Nee avanga maadhiri aagadha, unakku nalladhu dhaan."
thirukkural: புறங்கூறிப் பொய்த்துயிர் வாழ்தலின் சாதல் அறங்கூற்றும் ஆக்கத் தரும்.
english_translation: Virtue thinks it better to die,  Than live to backbite and to lie
moral: It is better to face rejection than to live by backbiting and false smiles.

title: முன்னிலையில் கடுமை, மறைவில் நயமற்ற பேச்சு
theme: Not Backbiting / Open Harshness vs Secret Slander
hook: Rajesh's teacher scolded him harshly in front of everyone. But his friend kept smiling at the teacher and gossiped about him later.
story: After school, Rajesh sat in the palace courtyard. "Mama, teacher enna munnadi nalla apdi adichaan. Aana Ramesh, avana munnadi sirichitu vidaya vitudu pesuraan." Mama was drawing a kolam. "Teacher munnadi sonnadhu kadumaya irundha kooda, athu unmai. Aana Ramesh munnadi sirichitu pinnadi kettadhu pesuraan, adhu dhaan kettadhu. Mukathula sonna kooda sari, aana maraithu pesi kondathavan vida mosama."
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Mama, teacher munnadi kattu kattu nu adichaan aana...'
Mama: 'Teacher munnadi sonnadhu kettadha? Athum unmai dhaan. Aana un friend munnadi sirichitu pinnadi pesuraan, adhu vida kettadhu.'
Rajesh: 'Aana teacher sonnadhu enaku kastama irundhadhu.'
Mama: 'Mukathula sonna dhaan prasantham. Maraiku romba periya vishayam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appeared in the palace garden, sitting on an old stone bench. His voice was gentle.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mukathula yenna solraanga, adhu unmai. Aana munnadi illadha idathula innoruthar pathi kettadhu solradhu dhaan periya kedu. Adha vida mukathula kattu nu solradhu nalladhu."
thirukkural: கண்ணின்று கண்ணறச் சொல்லினும் சொல்லற்க முன்னின்று பின்நோக்காச் சொல்.
english_translation: Though harsh you speak in one's presence  Abuse is worse in his absence
moral: Speaking harshly to someone's face is better than speaking ill behind their back.

title: புறங்கூறுவான் அறம் பேசினும் வீண்
theme: Not Backbiting / Slander Betrays False Virtue
hook: Deepa's aunt always talked about being a good person. But Deepa heard her gossiping about the neighbors.
story: In the Arani silk market, Deepa's aunt was buying sarees. "Paati, athai romba nallavanga maadhiri pesuvaanga. 'Nallavanga apdi panna maataanga' nu solvaanga. Aana naetiku pakkathu veetu aunty pathi kettadhu pesinaanga." Paati sighed. "Deepa, oru velai avan nalla mathiri dhaan iruppaan. Aana avan innoruthar pathi kettadhu pesuraan na, avanoda nalladhu ellam poi dhaan. Nee athai maadhiri aagadha."
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Paati, athai nallavanga maadhiri pesuvaanga aana avanga dhaan kettadhu pesinaanga.'
Paati: 'Innoruthar pathi kettadhu pesuravan, avan evvalavu perisa nalladhu sollinaalum, adhu poi dhaan. Avan nenchu nalladhu illa-nu adhu kaatum.'
Deepa: 'Aana athaiku apdi solren?'
Paati: 'Appa solra maadhiri, adhu dhaan nalladhu. Pinnadi pesuravan nalla aalu aaga mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stood among the silk sarees, holding a single strand of thread. He spoke without looking up.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvan evvalavu perisa aram sollinaalum, avalonnu oruthar pathi pinnadi kettadhu pesuraan na, avanoda aram ellam poi dhaan. Adhu avanoda nijama kaatum."
thirukkural: அறஞ்சொல்லும் நெஞ்சத்தான் அன்மை புறஞ்சொல்லும் புன்மையாற் காணப் படும்.
english_translation: Who turns to slander makes it plain  His praise of virtue is in vain
moral: A slanderer's talk of virtue is exposed as hollow by their own words.

title: பிறர் பழி சொன்னால் சொந்த பழி வெளிவரும்
theme: Not Backbiting / The Boomerang of Blame
hook: Senthil loved pointing out others' mistakes. But one day, someone pointed out his own mistake in the same way.
story: Near the big gopuram, Senthil was telling Thatha about his friend. "Muthu dhaan ellam thappu pannuraan. Avan dhaan cricket ball pottinga vituta, avan dhaan class la noise pannuraan." A man nearby turned around — it was Muthu's father. "Senthil, Muthu dhaan ellam pannuraana? Aana nee dhaan naetiku Muthu veetu window kalla potta?" Senthil's face went red. Thatha said softly, "Innoruthar kuttram solra aalungal kitta avanga kuttram theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Senthil: 'Thatha, Muthu dhaan ellam thappu pannuraan!'
Muthu's father: 'Sari Senthil, aana naetiku andha window kalla pottadhu yaaru? Muthu illa nee dhaan?'
Senthil: (silent, looking down)
Thatha: 'Paartha? Innoruthar kuttram sollum podhu, un kuttram dhaan munnadi varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar walked down the gopuram steps, each step echoing like a lesson. He stopped beside Senthil.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Innoruthar kuttram solluravan, avanoda kuttram vera aalungal solli kaatapadum. Munnaadi yenna solla pora, apdi dhaan avan vida kettadhu avan mela solvaanga."
thirukkural: பிறன்பழி கூறுவான் தன்பழி யுள்ளும் திறன்தெரிந்து கூறப் படும்.
english_translation: His failings will be found and shown,  Who makes another's failings known
moral: Those who point out others' faults will have their own faults exposed.

title: நகைத்துப் பேசி நட்பை கெடுப்பவர்
theme: Not Backbiting / Words That Break Friendship
hook: Mona noticed two girls who pretended to be best friends, but always whispered about each other when apart.
story: In the park, Mona watched Priya and Divya. "Athai, Priya Divya kooda irukrapo romba close. Aana Divya illadha idathula, Priya avala pathi kettadhu pesura." Athai was feeding the ducks. "Athu dhaan kettadhu. Munnadi natchathu pinnadi pesina, athu dhaan nalla aalungal vida ketta aalungal. Nee apdi panna vendam." Mona said, "Aana avanga friend dhaane?" Athai replied, "Un friend ah nee munnadi sirichitu pinnadi avala kettadhu pesina, athu nalla friend illa."
tamil_dialogues: Mona: 'Athai, Priya Divya kooda irukrapo romba close maadhiri irukkaanga. Aana Divya illadha idathula kettadhu pesuraanga.'
Athai: 'Adhu nallavanoda lakshanam illa. Pinnadi pesina, avanga friend ah keduvaanga. Nee apdi panna vendam.'
Mona: 'Aana avanga unmaiya friend dhaane?'
Athai: 'Unmaiya friend na munnadi unmai solluvom. Pinnadi pesa maatom. Adhu dhaan unmaiyana nanbu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sat on a park bench, feeding the ducks from his palm. His eyes met Mona's.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Munnadi sirichitu pinnadi pathi kettadhu pesuraavan, avanoda nanbara keduthu kolvaan. Avan natchathu pannamattaan, pinnadi dhaan pesuvan. Adhu namma nanbuku periya kedu."
thirukkural: பகச்சொல்லிக் கேளிர்ப் பிரிப்பர் நகச்சொல்லி நட்பாடல் தேற்றா தவர்.
english_translation: By pleasing words who make not friends  Sever their hearts by hostile trends
moral: Those who can't be genuinely friendly break friendships with backbiting.

title: நண்பரின் குற்றத்தைச் சொல்பவர் அந்நியருக்கு என்ன செய்வார்?
hook: Prakash saw his father's friend talking badly about his father to another person. He realized something terrible.
story: Prakash was hiding behind a pillar near the temple tank. Appa's friend was talking to another man. "Avan avalavu nallavan illa da. Avan dhaan business la..." Prakash couldn't believe his ears. This man ate at their home every week! At night, Prakash told Appa. Appa was quiet. "Prakash, innoruthar kooda sirichitu, avan illadha idathula avana pathi kettadhu pesuravan, innoruthar kitta enna pannuvaan? Avan avanoda friend ah kooda kedukuravan."
tamil_dialogues: Prakash: 'Appa, naetiku namma veetuku varavanga, unga friend madhiri irundhavanga, unga pathi kettadhu pesinaanga.'
Appa: 'Ama? Yaaru?'
Prakash: 'Ravi uncle dhaan. Andha aalu kitta unga pathi kettadhu solraanga.'
Appa: 'Avanga namma kooda sirichitu, namma illadha idathula nammala kettadhu pesuraanga na, innoruthar kitta enna pannuvaano?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sat beside the temple tank, dipping his toes in the holy water. He looked up at the stars.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Namma close aalungaloda kuttram kooda pinnadi sollura aalungal, innoruthar kitta enna pannuvano? Avanga yaraiyum vida maataanga. Avanga nanbu dhaan avanga pakkam illa."
thirukkural: துன்னியார் குற்றமும் தூற்றும் மரபினார் என்னைகொல் ஏதிலார் மாட்டு.
english_translation: What will they not to strangers do  Who bring their friends' defects to view?
moral: If someone speaks ill of their friends, imagine what they'd do to strangers.

title: புறம் பேசுபவனை தாங்கும் பூமி
theme: Not Backbiting / The World Bears the Slanderer
hook: Yashini's neighbor spent all day gossiping about everyone. Yashini wondered why the earth didn't swallow her up.
story: Climbing the Namakkal Fort hill, Yashini held Amma's hand. "Amma, antha aunty ellar pathiyum apdi kettadhu pesura. Edhu nalla illa, idhu mosam, avan apdi, ivan apdi. Aana yaarum avalukku onnum solradhu illa." Amma laughed gently. "Yashini, ulagam innoruthar pathi pinnadi pesura avanoda saramum thangudhu. Athu dhaan periya karunai. Nee apdi iruka vendam."
tamil_dialogues: Yashini: 'Amma, antha aunty ellar pathiyum kettadhu dhaan pesuraanga. Aana yaarum onnum solradhu illa!'
Amma: 'Ulagam avanoda kettadhai thangudhu. Athu periya karunai. Nee apdi panna, ulagam unna thangaadhu.'
Yashini: 'Enaku apdi panna thonraadhu.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhaan nalladhu. Pinnadi pesuravan evlo periya saram, adha ulagam thangudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stood at the fort's highest point, looking down at the town. His white beard moved in the wind.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pinnadi kettadhu solli suthuvar avanoda saramai ulagam periya karunaiyoda thangudhu. Nee apdi panna koodadhu. Karunai yosichu, avanai maathi vida mudiyadha."
thirukkural: அறன்நோக்கி ஆற்றுங்கொல் வையம் புறன்நோக்கிப் புன்சொல் உரைப்பான் பொறை.
english_translation: The world in mercy bears his load  Who rants behind words untoward
moral: The world mercifully bears the burden of those who speak ill of others behind their backs.

title: நம் குற்றம் நமக்குத் தெரிந்தால்
hook: Harish always complained about his cousin's bad habits. Until Chithappa made him look in a mirror.
story: At the busy Pollachi cattle market, Harish was complaining again. "Chithappa, antha Karthik dhaan romba mosam. Avan dhaan time-ku vara maatan, avan dhaan..." Chithappa held up a small hand mirror. "Un mookula enna irukku?" Harish was confused. "Onnum illa." Chithappa said, "Nee innoruthar kuttram paakura maadhiri, un kuttram paaru. Nee dhaan Karthik moonji la yenna solra, avanu kooda ore maadhiri thappa dhaan pannura." Harish was stunned. He realized he was late too, just like Karthik.
tamil_dialogues: Harish: 'Chithappa, Karthik dhaan time-ku vara maatan, apdi inga vara maatan...'
Chithappa: 'Nee innoruthar kuttram paakura maadhiri, un kuttram paaru. Nalla padikra, aana nee dhaan weekend la ellam homework miss pannura.'
Harish: 'Aana adhu...'
Chithappa: 'Nee innoruthar kuttram parthu solla arambichaa, munnadi un kuttram podu. Adhu dhaan sari.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar leaned against a cattle shed, a calf nuzzling his hand. He smiled at the realization on Harish's face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ellarum innoruthar kuttram paathu solla arambichitaanga. Aana avanga kuttram avanga paathukitta, inga periya kedu varadhu. Un kuttram nee paathu sari pannu."
thirukkural: ஏதிலார் குற்றம்போல் தங்குற்றங் காண்கிற்பின் தீதுண்டோ மன்னும் உயிர்க்கு.
english_translation: No harm would fall to any man  If each his own defect could scan
moral: If everyone examined their own faults as they examine others', no harm would come to anyone.

title: பயனில சொல்
hook: Karthik can't stop blurting nonsense in front of everyone.
story: Karthik walked through the temple corridor, telling jokes that made devotees frown. His Appa pulled him aside gently. "என்னடா பேச்சு இது? யாருக்கும் பிடிக்கலையே," Appa whispered. Karthik hung his head, understanding at last.
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'சும்மா சொன்னேன் Appa, அதுக்கென்ன அவ்வளவு பெரிய விஷயமா?'
Appa: 'பயனில்லாத வார்த்தை எல்லாருக்கும் போரடிச்சிடும் கண்ணா.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar materialises beside the temple lamp, his white robe glowing softly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "பல பேருக்கும் போரடிக்கிற மாதிரி பயனில்லாத வார்த்தைய சொல்லிக்கிட்டே இருந்தா, கடைசில எல்லாரும் உன்ன கேவலமா பார்ப்பாங்க. பயன் உள்ள வார்த்தைய மட்டும் பேசு."
thirukkural: பல்லார் முனியப் பயனில சொல்லுவான் எல்லாரும் எள்ளப் படும்.
english_translation: With silly words who insults all Is held in contempt as banal
moral: Speak words with purpose, or the world will tune you out.

title: வீண் பேச்சு
hook: Anjali's endless chatter costs her a friend.
story: Anjali kept rambling about nothing while her friend waited to share something important. Her Thatha sat her on the stone steps. "கதவு சாத்திட்டா மாதிரி ஆயிருச்சு," he said. Anjali went to apologise the next morning.
tamil_dialogues: Anjali: 'எனக்கு பேசிக்கிட்டே இருக்கணும் போல இருக்கு Thatha.'
Thatha: 'பயனுள்ள பேச்சு மட்டும் பேசுடி, மத்ததெல்லாம் வெட்டி.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears among the stone pillars, holding an ancient palm leaf.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "வெட்டி வார்த்தைய நிறைய பேர் முன்னாடி சொல்லுறது, நண்பனுக்கு கெடுதல் பண்ணின மாதிரி கெட்டதுண்ணு சொல்லியிருக்கேன்."
thirukkural: பயனில பல்லார்முன் சொல்லல் நயனில நட்டார்கண் செய்தலிற் றீது.
english_translation: Vain talk before many is worse Than doing to friends deeds adverse
moral: Idle chatter before others hurts more than a bad deed against a friend.

title: பேச்சின் அடையாளம்
hook: Ravi realizes his words have made him a label he hates.
story: At school, kids called Ravi "vetti payyan" because he talked nonsense all day. Sitting by Ukkadam lake with Amma, he broke down. "ஏன் அப்படி சொல்றாங்க?" he cried. His mother held him close. "உன் வார்த்தைதானே அந்த பேரை வாங்கிக் கொடுத்துச்சு."
tamil_dialogues: Ravi: 'நான் பெரிய விஷயமா பேசறேன்ல Amma?'
Amma: 'பயனுள்ள விஷயம் பேசுனா தானே ஞானி, இல்லைன்னா வெட்டிப் பேச்சுக்காரன்தானே?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears, leaning against an old stone lamp post.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "யாருக்கும் பிரயோஜனம் இல்லாத வார்த்தைய பேசுறவன், 'நல்லதுக்கும் ஆகாதவன்' அப்படினு பேரு வாங்கிடுவான்."
thirukkural: நயனிலன் என்பது சொல்லும் பயனில பாரித் துரைக்கும் உரை.
english_translation: The babbler's hasty lips proclaim That "good-for-nothing" is his name
moral: Your words create your reputation.

title: நல்ல பேச்சின் வலிமை
hook: Priya ruins the temple festival mood with her endless nonsense.
story: During temple festival, Priya's idle talk annoyed the women making kolams. Everyone moved away from her. Crying near the temple pond, her Paati said gently, "வெட்டிப்பேச்சு நல்லதையும் கலைச்சிடும் பாப்பா." Priya bit her tongue and helped silently instead.
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'பாக்க மட்டும் தான் ரொம்ப நல்லாருக்கு, ஆனா பேசலாம்ல Paati?'
Paati: 'சரியான நேரத்துல சரியான வார்த்தை சொல்ல வேணும் கண்ணு.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar rises from the mist above the temple pond.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "பயனில்லாத வார்த்தை, திறமையானவன் முன்னாடி சொன்னாலும் அவனோட நன்மையை கெடுத்துடும்."
thirukkural: நயன்சாரா நன்மையின் நீக்கும் பயன்சாராப் பண்பில்சொல் பல்லா ரகத்து.
english_translation: Vain words before an assembly Will make all gains and goodness flee
moral: Worthless words in public destroy all your good qualities.

title: புகழ் கெடும்
hook: The smartest boy in class loses his crown because of senseless talk.
story: Suresh was a class topper, but he kept making silly comments during the school assembly. His teacher took him to Muthu Mandapam. "உன் பேச்சு உன்னை மாத்திடுச்சு பாரு," sir said. Suresh resolved to speak only when meaningful.
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'எனக்கு சிரிப்பு வருது, அதான் சொல்லிட்டேன்.'
Teacher: 'அறிவாளி வெட்டியா பேசினா அழகும் கெட்டுரும், சிறப்பும் கெட்டுரும்.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands beneath the mandapam's central dome.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "ஞானமுள்ளவன் வெட்டி வார்த்தைய பேசினா, அவனோட புகழும் மரியாதையும் போயிடும் அப்படினு அர்த்தம்."
thirukkural: சீர்மை சிறப்பொடு நீங்கும் பயனில நீர்மை யுடையார் சொலின்.
english_translation: Glory and grace will go away When savants silly nonsense say
moral: Even the wise lose their shine when they speak foolishly.

title: மக்கட் பதடி
hook: Mani prides himself on his big words until nobody listens.
story: Mani used big English words and long stories nobody cared about. At the Nellaiappar temple, his uncle watched people walk away. "நீ வெறும் சப்பைக் கட்டை மாதிரி," his uncle sighed. Mani stopped showing off and started speaking from the heart.
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'நான் பெரிய ஆளா தெரியலையா uncle?'
Uncle: 'வெட்டி பேச்சை வெச்சுக்கிட்டு குள்ளமான மனுஷன் தானே நீ.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears at the temple entrance, leaning on his staff.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "பயனில்லாத வார்த்தைய பெருமையா சொல்லிக்கிறவன, வெறும் சப்பைக் கட்டைன்னுதான் சொல்லணும். அவன் மனுஷனுக்குள்ள மனுஷன் இல்லை."
thirukkural: பயனில்சொல் பாராட்டு வானை மகன்எனல் மக்கட் பதடி யெனல்.
english_translation: Call him a human chaff who prides Himself in weightless idle words
moral: A person who boasts empty words is mere chaff among men.

title: நல்ல பழக்கம்
hook: Gowri learns that even great people avoid pointless talk.
story: At Yercaud Lake, Gowri watched her quiet father help a stranger without a word. "Appa, நீங்க ஏன் எதுவும் பேசல?" she asked. He smiled. "தேவைப்பட்டா கண்டிசனா சொல்லலாம். சும்மா வெட்டிப்பேச்சு வேணாம்னு தானே பெரியவங்க சொல்றாங்க?"
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'உங்களுக்கு பேசவே பிடிக்காதா Appa?'
Appa: 'கண்டிசனா பேசுறது நல்லது. வெட்டி பேச்சு எதுக்கு? அதான் சான்றோர் வழி.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears beside the shimmering lake surface.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "சான்றோர் கடுமையா பேசினாலும் பரவாயில்லை. ஆனா வெட்டியா பேசுறது மட்டும் கூடாது. பயனுள்ள பேச்சு மட்டும் பேசணும்."
thirukkural: நயனில சொல்லினுஞ் சொல்லுக சான்றோர் பயனில சொல்லாமை நன்று.
english_translation: Let not men of worth vainly quack Even if they would roughly speak
moral: A noble person may speak harshly, but never idly.

title: அறிவின் அடையாளம்
hook: Lakshmi watches the temple priest and learns the weight of words.
story: Lakshmi noticed the priest only said few words during prayers. "ஏன் அவ்வளவு சுருக்கமா பேசுறாரு?" she asked Paati. Her grandmother explained while walking the thousand-pillared hall: "அறிவுசாலி பயனுள்ள வார்த்தைய மட்டும் தான் பேசுவாரு பாப்பா."
tamil_dialogues: Lakshmi: 'அவர் இன்னும் நிறைய பேசலாமே Paati?'
Paati: 'ஞானி வெட்டியா பேச மானு. அருமையான பயன் உள்ள வார்த்தை மட்டும் பேசுவாரு.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears between the ancient stone pillars.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "அரிய பயனை ஆராய்ஞ்சு தெரிஞ்சவங்க, பெரிய பயன் இல்லாத வார்த்தைய ஒரு நாளும் பேசமாட்டாங்க. பேசினாலும் வெட்டிப்பேச்சு கூடாது."
thirukkural: அரும்பயன் ஆயும் அறிவினார் சொல்லார் பெரும்பயன் இல்லாத சொல்.
english_translation: The wise who weigh the worth refrain From words that have no grain and brain
moral: The wise never utter words without substance.

title: மறந்தும் சொல்லார்
hook: Kumar discovers why his headmaster never says a wasteful word.
story: Amid the science fair at VOC Park, the headmaster gave a short but powerful speech. "எப்படி சார் உங்களுக்கு வெட்டி பேச்சு வராது?" Kumar asked. The headmaster patted his shoulder. "நல்ல பழக்கம்தான் கண்ணா. மறந்தாலும் வெட்டிப்பேச்சு வராதவன் தான் ஞானி."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'எனக்கும் அப்படி வரணும் சார்.'
Headmaster: 'பயனுள்ளத மட்டும் வாய் திறந்து பேசு, பழகிடும்.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears behind the stage under a gulmohar tree.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "ஞானமுள்ளவன் மறந்தாலும் வெட்டி வார்த்தைய சொல்லமாட்டான். காரணம்? சுத்தமான மனசு. அவன் கண்ணுக்கு பயனுள்ளதுமட்டுமே தெரியும்."
thirukkural: பொருள்தீர்ந்த பொச்சாந்துஞ் சொல்லார் மருள்தீர்ந்த மாசறு காட்சி யவர்.
english_translation: The wise of spotless self-vision Slip not to silly words-mention
moral: One with pure vision never speaks empty words, even by mistake.

title: பயனுள்ள சொல்
hook: Divya learns to sort her words like shells.
story: Divya picked sea shells at Thoothukudi harbour, throwing away broken ones. Her Amma said, "அதே மாதிரிதான் வார்த்தையும்! பயனுள்ளத சொல்லு, மத்தத காலியில போடு." Divya smiled, understanding.
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'கொஞ்சம் நல்லா சொல்லக் கூடாதா Amma?'
Amma: 'பயனுள்ளத சொல்லி பழகு, பயனில்லாதத தவிர்க்கிறது தான் ஞானம்.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands by the shore, waves touching his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "பயனுள்ள வார்த்தைய தான் பேசணும். பயனில்லாத வார்த்தைய பேசவே கூடாது. இது தான் என் குறளோட சுருக்கம்."
thirukkural: சொல்லுக சொல்லிற் பயனுடைய சொல்லற்க சொல்லிற் பயனிலாச் சொல்.
english_translation: To purpose speak the fruitful word And never indulge in useless load
moral: Speak what is useful; never utter a word without value.

title: தீவினை அச்சம்
hook: Bala dares Ramesh to steal but learns the real fear.
story: On the Rockfort steps, Bala dared Ramesh to steal from the shop. Ramesh refused, trembling. "எனக்கு மனசு சரியில்ல பாலா, இது தப்பு," he said. Bala laughed, but later that night, he couldn't sleep, remembering his friend's wise fear.
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'பயந்தாங்கொள்ளி! சும்மா திங்க வாங்கலாம்ல!'
Ramesh: 'இல்ல பாலா. நல்லவன் தீய காரியத்துக்கு பயப்படுவான்.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears on the Rockfort steps, glowing in the evening light.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "கெட்டவன் தீமை செய்ய பயப்படமாட்டான். ஆனா நல்லவன் தீமைன்னா பயந்து விலகிட்டுப் போவான்."
thirukkural: தீவினையார் அஞ்சார் விழுமியார் அஞ்சுவர் தீவினை என்னும் செருக்கு.
english_translation: Sinners fear not the pride of sin The worthy dread the ill within
moral: The wicked do not fear evil; the righteous tremble at the very thought.

title: தீயும் அஞ்சும்
hook: Meena plays with fire and learns why evil should scare her more.
story: Meena watched kids burn plastic on the beach. She wanted to join, but stopped. "என்ன மீனா?" Appa asked. "தப்புன்னு தோணுது Appa." He nodded. "தீய விஷயம் தீயை விட பயங்கரமானது கண்ணு. அதனால தான் நல்லவங்க அத விட்டு ஒதுங்குவாங்க."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'நானும் சேரலாமா Appa?'
Appa: 'தீய காரியம் தீயை விட கொடுமை. அது தீமையே தரும்.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar walks toward them from the sunset-lit waves.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "தீமை தீய பயனையே கொடுக்கும். அதனால தீயை விட தீய காரியத்திற்கு பயப்படணும்."
thirukkural: தீயவை தீய பயத்தலால் தீயவை தீயினும் அஞ்சப் படும்.
english_translation: Since evil begets evil dire Fear ye evil more than fire
moral: Fear evil deeds more than fire, because their consequences are equally destructive.

title: எதிரிக்கும் நன்மை
hook: Arul wants revenge but his sister shows him a greater strength.
story: A boy pushed Arul at the palace grounds. Furious, Arul wanted to push back hard. Pooja held his arm. "அவனுக்கு திருப்பி செய்யாதே. நல்லவன் தன்னோட எதிரிக்குகூட தீமை செய்யமாட்டான்," she said. Arul calmed down, amazed.
tamil_dialogues: Arul: 'விட மாட்டேன்! திருப்பி அடிக்கணும்!'
Pooja: 'ஞானி எதிரிக்குகூட தீமை செய்ய மாட்டானடா.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears in the palace's open courtyard, leaning on a stone pillar.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "எல்லா அறிவுக்குள்ளயும் மேலானது எதிரிக்குக் கூட தீமை செய்யாம இருப்பது."
thirukkural: அறிவினுள் எல்லாந் தலையென்ப தீய செறுவார்க்கும் செய்யா விடல்.
english_translation: The wisest of the wise are those Who injure not even their foes
moral: The highest wisdom is to do no harm, even to your enemy.

title: தீமை திரும்பும்
hook: Venkat plots a prank but karma has other plans.
story: At the bird sanctuary, Venkat planned to scare birds for fun. Senthil stopped him. "நீ தீமை நினைச்சா அது உனக்கே தீமையா திரும்பும்," he warned. Venkat ignored him, but his foot slipped into the marsh. Soaked and muddy, he understood.
tamil_dialogues: Venkat: 'சும்மா சின்ன குறும்புதான் செய்யப்போறேன்.'
Senthil: 'தீமை நினைச்சவனுக்கே அறம் தீமையா திரும்ப கொடுக்கும்.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands by the water, birds circling around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "மறந்து கூட இன்னொருத்தனுக்கு கேடு நினைக்காதே. நினைச்சா, அந்த தீமை உன்னையே சுத்தி வந்து அடிக்கும்."
thirukkural: மறந்தும் பிறன்கேடு சூழற்க சூழின் அறஞ்சூழம் சூழ்ந்தவன் கேடு.
english_translation: His ruin virtue plots who plans The ruin of another man's
moral: Plotting another's harm invites virtue to plot yours.

title: ஏழைமையும் தீமையும்
hook: Selvi thinks stealing is okay because she's poor.
story: Near the waterfall, Selvi saw fruits she could steal. "நம்ம கிட்ட பணம் இல்ல Amma. அதான்..." Amma held her hand tight. "இல்லாததுன்னு தப்பு செய்யக் கூடாது மா. அப்படி செய்தா இன்னும் ஆழமா விழுந்துருவ."
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'ஏழைங்களுக்கு சின்ன தப்பு பண்ணலாம்ல?'
Amma: 'இல்லடி. இல்லாம இருக்கேன்னு தப்பு பண்ணா இன்னும் இல்லாம போயிடுவ.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands on a misty rock near the waterfall.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "நான் ஏழைன்னு சொல்லி தீமை செய்யாதே. அப்படி செய்தா இன்னும் ஏழையா மாறுவ."
thirukkural: இலன்என்று தீயவை செய்யற்க செய்யின் இலனாகும் மற்றும் பெயர்த்து.
english_translation: Who makes poverty plea for ill Shall reduce himself poorer still
moral: Poverty is no excuse for evil; doing wrong only deepens your lack.

title: நோயும் தீமையும்
hook: Rani hurts a stray kitten and regrets it immediately.
story: Rani threw a stone at a stray kitten in the Botanical Gardens. Her Thatha caught her hand. "இன்னொருத்தனுக்கு கஷ்டம் கொடுத்தா அது திரும்பி வந்து உன்னைத் தாக்கும்," he said gently. She fed the kitten that evening.
tamil_dialogues: Rani: 'சும்மா விளையாட்டுக்குத்தான் Thatha.'
Thatha: 'தன்னை நேசிக்கிறவன் மத்தவங்களுக்கு தீமை பண்ணமாட்டான்.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sits quietly on a garden bench, patting the kitten.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "தீய காரியங்களை இன்னொருத்தருக்கு செய்ய வேணாம். ஏன்னா உனக்கு தீமை வரக்கூடாதுன்னு நினைச்சா, நீயும் மத்தவங்களுக்கு தீமை செய்யக்கூடாது."
thirukkural: தீப்பால தான்பிறர்கண் செய்யற்க நோய்ப்பால தன்னை அடல்வேண்டா தான்.
english_translation: From wounding others let him refrain Who would from harm himself remain
moral: If you wish to stay safe from harm, never harm another.

title: வினைப்பகை
hook: Murugan thinks he can escape any trouble.
story: Murugan cheated in a game and laughed at getting caught. "யாரும் பிடிக்க முடியாது," he boasted by the river. Kavi shook his head. "மத்த பகைல இருந்து தப்பிச்சுடலாம். ஆனா செஞ்ச தப்பு விடாது முருகா." Murugan's win felt hollow.
tamil_dialogues: Murugan: 'யாருக்கும் தெரியாது நான் பண்ணது.'
Kavi: 'மனுஷன் பகைல இருந்து தப்பிச்சுடலாம். ஆனா இந்த பாவம் உன்ன விடாது.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands under a banyan tree on the riverbank.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "எத்தனை பகைவர் இருந்தாலும் தப்பிச்சுடலாம். ஆனா செய்த தீமை மட்டும் உன்ன விட்டு போகாமே பின்னால வந்து அடிக்கும்."
thirukkural: எனைப்பகை யுற்றாரும் உய்வர் வினைப்பகை வீயாது பின்சென்று அடும்.
english_translation: Men may escape other foes and live But sin its deadly blow will give
moral: You can escape enemies, but the evil you do will always catch up.

title: நிழல் போல் தீமை
hook: Mano lies about breaking a vase, but guilt follows him.
story: Mano broke a vase at home and blamed the cat. At Ayyanar Falls, he saw his shadow following him. "தீமை பண்ணினா நிழல் மாதிரி பின்னாடி வரும் மனோ," Amma said softly. He confessed that night.
tamil_dialogues: Mano: 'அம்மா எனக்கு பயமா இருக்கு.'
Amma: 'தீமை பண்ணினவனுக்கு கேடு காத்திருக்கும். நிழல் மாதிரி விடாது வந்துக்கிட்டே இருக்கும்.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sits by the waterfall, the mist glowing around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "தீய செயல் பண்ணினவன் கெட்டுப் போவான். அது நிழலை மாதிரி அவன விட்டுப் போகாம வந்து ஒட்டிக்கும்."
thirukkural: தீயவை செய்தார் கெடுதல் நிழல்தன்னை வீயாது அஇஉறைந் தற்று.
