thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'நமக்கு இன்னாவா இருக்குமோ அத நாம மத்தவங்ககிட்ட செய்யக்கூடாது. இது மிக எளிமையான ஆனா முக்கியமான பாடம்.'
thirukkural: இன்னா எனத்தான் உணர்ந்தவை துன்னாமை வேண்டும் பிறன்கண் செயல்.
english_translation: What you feel as pain to yourself  Do it not to the other-self
moral: Never do to others what you wouldn't want done to you.

title: நினைப்பிலும் தீங்கு / Not Even In Thought
theme: Not Doing Evil / Purity of Mind
hook: Did you know hurting someone inside your head is also wrong?
story: Sanjay's neighbour accused him of stealing mangoes. He didn't. He felt rage boiling. In his mind, he imagined pushing her. Amma saw his tight fists. "What's in your head?" she asked. He confessed. "That's also wrong," Amma said. Sanjay took deep breaths. He let the anger go.
tamil_dialogues: Sanjay: 'அவளை நினைப்புல தள்ளறதா நினைச்சேன்'
Amma: 'மனசுல கூட தீங்கு நினைக்கக் கூடாது கண்ணா'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears near the temple gopuram, his silhouette backlit by sunset.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'எவ்வளவு சின்னதா இருந்தாலும், எப்பவும், யாருக்கும், நினைப்புல கூட தீங்கு செய்யக்கூடாது. அதுதான் மிகப் பெரிய ஒழுக்கம்.'
thirukkural: எனைத்தானும் எஞ்ஞான்றும் யார்க்கும் மனத்தானாம் மாணாசெய் யாமை தலை.
english_translation: Any, anywhere injure not  At any time even in thought
moral: Harmful thoughts are the seeds of harmful actions.

title: வலி தெரியும் / I Know That Pain
hook: If you know how much something hurts, how can you do it to someone else?
story: Priya fell from a cycle and scraped her arm badly. The wound burned for days. At school, she saw a boy teasing a girl who had a similar wound. She stepped between them. "You know how that feels?" she asked. The boy remembered his own fall. He stopped. "Sorry," he said quietly.
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'இது எவ்வளவு வலிக்கும் உனக்குத் தெரியுமா?'
Boy: '...நானும் விழுந்திருக்கேன், சாரி'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands by a nearby flower cart, petals falling around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'தனக்கு வலி எப்படி இருக்குன்னு தெரிஞ்சவன், மத்த உயிர்களுக்கு எப்படி தீங்கு பண்ண முடியும்? வலி தெரிஞ்சவங்க அத வேற யாருக்கும் கொடுக்க மாட்டாங்க.'
thirukkural: தன்னுயிர்ககு ஏன்னாமை தானறிவான் என்கொலோ மன்னுயிர்க்கு இன்னா செயல்.
english_translation: How can he injure other souls  Who in his life injury feels
moral: Knowing pain means understanding why we must never cause it.

title: முற்பகல் தீங்கு / Morning Harm
hook: Whatever you throw out in the morning comes back by evening.
story: Gopi hid Kumar's chappals as a joke. Kumar walked home barefoot. Gopi laughed. By evening, Gopi's cycle tire got a nail. He had to walk home. His feet hurt. Thatha said, "Morning fun, evening pain." Gopi understood. He bought new chappals for Kumar the next day.
tamil_dialogues: Gopi: 'காலைல பண்ணேன் சாயந்தரம் எனக்கே வந்துச்சு'
Thatha: 'அதான் குறள் சொல்லுது முற்பகல் செஞ்சா பிற்பகல் தானே வரும்'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar is at the Rockfort steps, his white robe glowing in the evening light.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'காலையில மத்தவங்களுக்குத் தீங்கு பண்ணினா, அதே தீங்கு மத்தியானம் நமக்கு வந்து சேரும். இது இயற்கை நியதி.'
thirukkural: பிறர்க்கின்னா முற்பகல் செய்யின் தமக்குஇன்னா பிற்பகல் தாமே வரும்.
english_translation: Harm others in the forenoon  Harm seeks thee in afternoon
moral: Every hurt you send out returns to you in time.

title: நோய் செய்யாதவர் / Pain-Free Lives
hook: Want to live without pain? Stop causing it.
story: A cycle hit Rani's leg. She yelled at the boy. He ran away. Amma brought her inside. "That boy will feel bad all evening," Amma said. "Is that what you want?" Rani shook her head. She went to his house with a sweet. "It's okay," she said. The boy smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Rani: 'அவன் மேல கோவமா இருந்தாலும் அவனுக்கும் வலிக்கும்னு தோணிச்சு'
Amma: 'வலி கொடுக்காதவங்களுக்கு வலி வராது'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears at the street corner, leaning on a young palm tree.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'எல்லா வலியும் வலியை உண்டாக்கினவங்களைத் தானே சேரும். வலி வேண்டாம்னு நினைக்கிறவங்க மத்தவங்களுக்கு வலி செய்ய மாட்டாங்க.'
thirukkural: நோயெல்லாம் நோய்செய்தார் மேலவாம் நோய்செய்யார் நோயின்மை வேண்டு பவர்.
english_translation: No harm is done by peace-lovers  For pains rebound on pain-givers
moral: A peaceful life comes from never being the source of someone else's pain.

title: கொல்லாமை / Not Killing
hook: If virtue had a first rule, what would it be?
story: Balu found a baby sparrow that fell from its nest. A boy said, "Let's throw it in the tank." Balu grabbed the sparrow. "No!" he shouted. He carried it home. Amma helped him feed it with milk. The sparrow lived. Balu named it Chinna. Every morning Chinna sat on Balu's finger.
tamil_dialogues: Balu: 'இத கொல்லக் கூடாது! இது ஒரு உயிர்!'
Amma: 'நீ சொல்றது தான் முதல் அறம்'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears beside the temple tank, his hand gently touching the water.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'அறம் அப்படினா என்ன? கொல்லாமை தான். கொல்லறது மத்த எல்லா பாவத்தையும் கொண்டு வந்து சேர்க்கும். உயிரைக் காப்பாத்தறது தான் மிகப்பெரிய அறம்.'
thirukkural: அறவினை யாதெனின் கொல்லாமை கோறல் பிறவினை எல்லாந் தரும்.
english_translation: What is Virtue? 'Tis not to kill  For killing causes every ill
moral: The first and greatest virtue is to never take a life.

title: பகிர்ந்து உண்ணல் / Share The Food
hook: What is the greatest rule of all? Share your food with every living being.
story: Selvi had one idli for breakfast. A beggar with a starving dog sat nearby. Selvi looked at her idli. Her stomach growled. She broke the idli in three. One for her, one for the beggar, one for the dog. Appa watched and smiled. "That's real dharma," he said.
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'இவங்களுக்கும் பசிக்கும் Appa, நான் பங்கு போட்டுக் கொடுத்தேன்'
Appa: 'நீ பண்ணது தான் எல்லா நூலும் சொல்ற மிகப்பெரிய நல்லது'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sits on the temple steps, a plate of prasadam in his lap.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'பகுத்துண்டு பல உயிர்களைக் காப்பாற்றறது தான் எல்லா நூல்களும் சொல்ற மிக முக்கியமான நல்லது. பசிய மாற்றறது மிகப்பெரிய புண்ணியம்.'
thirukkural: பகுத்துண்டு பல்லுயிர் ஓம்புதல் நூலோர் தொகுத்தவற்றுள் எல்லாந் தலை.
english_translation: Share the food and serve all lives  This is the law of all the laws
moral: Sharing your food with all beings is the highest law of life.

title: கொல்லாமல் இருத்தல் / Beyond Not Killing
hook: If not killing is the best, what comes next?
story: Kannan saw fishermen pulling nets full of tiny fish. The fish gasped. He felt sick. Uncle said, "Why sad? This is our job." Kannan said, "But smaller fish... they haven't even lived." Uncle was quiet. Kannan didn't eat fish that night. He told everyone: "Next best thing to not killing is not lying about it."
tamil_dialogues: Kannan: 'கொல்லாம இருக்கறதுக்கு அப்புறம் பொய் சொல்லாம இருக்கறது தானாம்'
Uncle: 'நீ சரியா தானே சொல்ற'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar walks on the beach, sand not touching his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'ஒண்ணா நல்லது கொல்லாமை. அதுக்கு அப்புறம் வரது உண்மை பேசறது. மத்த நல்ல விஷயங்கள் எல்லாம் இதுக்கு பின்னாடிதான் வரும்.'
thirukkural: ஒன்றாக நல்லது கொல்லாமை மற்றதன் பின்சாரப் பொய்யாமை நன்று.
english_translation: Not to kill is unique good  The next, not to utter falsehood
moral: After protecting life, speaking truth is the highest good.

title: நல்ல வழி / The Good Path
hook: What is the good path? The one where no one gets hurt.
story: Deepak saw two men fighting near the temple. One pushed the other. "Amma, what is the right way to live?" Amma took him to the priest. "Which way is good?" he asked. The priest said: "The path where you never think of killing — that's the good way." Deepak felt the answer in his heart.
tamil_dialogues: Deepak: 'யாருக்கும் கொலை நினைப்பு வராத வழிதானா நல்ல வழி?'
Priest: 'ஆமா கண்ணா, கொல்லாமை சூழற நெறிதான் உண்மையான நல்லாறு'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands in the long corridor, the setting sun behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'நல்ல வழி அப்படினா என்ன? எந்த ஒரு கொலையும் நினைக்காத நெறி தான். மற்ற வழிகள் எல்லாம் அதுல இருந்து வரும் வழிகள்.'
thirukkural: நல்லாறு எனப்படுவது யாதெனின் யாதொன்றும் கொல்லாமை சூழும் நெறி.
english_translation: What way is good? That we can say  The way away from heat to slay
moral: The good path is the one stained by no violence, not even in thought.

title: கொலை அச்சம் / Fear of Killing
hook: Among all those who have given up everything, who is the greatest?
story: Muthu saw a sadhu who gave up his home, his clothes, everything. "Thatha, is he the greatest?" Thatha said, "There's someone greater: the one who has given up not things, but the thought of killing." Muthu met the local animal rescuer who never harmed even an ant. "I want to be like him."
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'யாரும் கொல்லாம இருக்கறவன்தான் பெரியவனா Thatha?'
Thatha: 'ஆமா தம்பி, உயிருக்கு பயந்து எல்லாத்தையும் விட்டவங்களுக்குள்ள கூட கொலை பயந்து கொல்லாம சூழ்றவன் தான் தலை'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sits on an old carved stone, his face serene.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'இந்த உடம்பு நிலையில்லாதுன்னு விட்டவங்கள்ள கூட, கொலை புரிய பயந்து கொல்லாமே சூழ்றவன் தான் மிகப் பெரியவன். உயிர் மேல பாசம் இல்லாதவன் யாரும் இல்லை.'
thirukkural: நிலைஅஞ்சி நீத்தாருள் எல்லாம் கொலைஅஞ்சிக் கொல்லாமை சூழ்வான் தலை.
english_translation: Of saints who renounce birth-fearing  The head is he who dreads killing
moral: The greatest among the great is the one who fears taking any life.

title: கொல்லாமை காக்கும் / Non-Killing Protects
hook: Can being kind to animals actually protect you from death?
story: A trader wanted to kill a newborn calf. Vasu begged Amma to buy it. They spent their savings. The calf grew, followed Vasu everywhere. Months later, Vasu got very sick. As he lay in bed, the calf stayed by him. Even the village doctor said, "This boy's will to live is strong." Vasu recovered.
tamil_dialogues: Vasu: 'கொல்லாம இருக்கறவங்க ஆயுசுக்கு ஆபத்தே இல்லைதானா Amma?'
Amma: 'ஆமா கண்ணா, அந்தக் கன்று உன்னைக் காப்பாத்துச்சு'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears near the fort wall, the calf now grown beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'கொல்லாமைய மேற்கொண்டு நடக்கிறவன் வாழ்நாள மேல எமன் கூட போக மாட்டான். உயிர் உண்ணற கூத்தன் அவன் ஆயுள எட்டவே முடியாது.'
thirukkural: கொல்லாமை மேற்கொண் டொழுகுவான் வாழ்நாள்மேல் செல்லாது உயிருண்ணுங் கூற்று.
english_translation: Life-eating-Death shall spare the breath  Of him who no life puts to death
moral: The one who never takes life is protected by life itself.

title: உயிருக்கும் மேல் / Dearer Than Life
theme: Not Killing / Don't Kill Even To Live
hook: Would you kill to save your own life? What if the answer is no?
story: A snake bit a farmer. The village said, "Kill the snake!" Arun ran with a stick. But the snake was a mother, with eggs nearby. He couldn't do it. He chased it away instead. Some called him foolish. But the farmer recovered anyway. "I nearly killed a mother," Arun whispered.
tamil_dialogues: Arun: 'என் உயிர் போனாலும் பரவாயில்லை, இத கொல்ல முடியாது'
Appa: 'நீ பண்ணது ரொம்ப தைரியமான காரியம்'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sits under a coconut tree, the river flowing peacefully behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'நம்ம உயிர் போனாலும், மத்த உயிரை எடுக்கிற காரியத்த செஞ்சிடக் கூடாது. இன்னொரு உயிரைப் பறிக்கறது எப்பவும் தப்பு.'
thirukkural: தன்னுயிர் நீப்பினும் செய்யற்க தான்பிறிது இன்னுயிர் நீக்கும் வினை.
english_translation: Kill not life that others cherish  Even when your life must perish
moral: Never take another life, even to save your own.

title: கொலை ஆக்கம் / Blood Profit
theme: Not Killing / No Wealth From Blood
hook: Is money earned through killing ever worth it?
story: Pazhani's uncle offered him 500 rupees to help clean chicken. Pazhani wanted new shoes badly. But he saw the birds alive, then dead. He couldn't. "I won't do it," he said. His uncle shrugged. "You lose." Pazhani walked home. His old shoes were fine. His heart felt clean.
tamil_dialogues: Pazhani: 'நிறைய காசு கிடைச்சாலும் கொல்லறதுல இருந்து வர்ற ஆக்கம் நல்லதில்ல'
Uncle: 'உனக்கு புரியல தம்பி, ஆனா உன் மனசு சொல்றது சரி'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands near a vegetable stall, observing quietly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'எவ்வளவு பெரிய ஆக்கமா இருந்தாலும், கொலை பண்ணி வர்ற ஆக்கம் கடைசியானது. அதனால தான் நல்லவங்க அத விரும்ப மாட்டாங்க.'
thirukkural: நன்றாகும் ஆக்கம் பெரிதெனினும் சான்றோர்க்குக் கொன்றாகும் ஆக்கங் கடை.
english_translation: The gain of slaughter is a vice  Though deemed good in sacrifice
moral: No profit earned through killing can ever be good.

title: கொலை புலை / Killing is Filth
theme: Not Killing / The Stain of Blood
hook: What kind of person kills for a living — and what does it do to their soul?
story: Senthil saw a butcher laughing while killing a goat. He felt sick. "Paati, why is he laughing?" Paati said, "Some people don't realize what they're doing." Senthil asked the priest, "Is killing a job?" The priest shook his head. "Those who kill for a living lose their own inner purity."
tamil_dialogues: Senthil: 'கொல பண்றவங்க உண்மையில கெட்டவங்களா Paati?'
Paati: 'கொலை செஞ்சு வாழறவங்க, அழுக்கான ஆள் மாதிரி தான்மா'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands on the hill steps, looking down at the town.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'கொலை வேலையா வைச்சிருக்கிற மனுஷங்க புலை வேலையா வைச்சவங்க மாதிரி. உண்மையில நல்லது கெட்டது தெரிஞ்சவங்க மத்துல இவங்களுக்கு இடமில்லை.'
thirukkural: கொலைவினைய ராகிய மாக்கள் புலைவினையர் புன்மை தெரிவா ரகத்து.
english_translation: Those who live by slaying are  Eaters of carrion bizarre!
moral: Those who kill for a living carry an invisible stain on their soul.

title: நோய் உடம்பு / Sick Bodies
hook: Why are some people born sick and suffering?
story: Jyothi saw a disabled boy at the hospital. His body was twisted. "Amma, why him?" Amma was quiet. Jyothi asked the doctor. The doctor said softly, "Some say it's because of past lives — the soul carries memories." Jyothi held the boy's hand. "I'm sorry for whatever happened before," she whispered.
tamil_dialogues: Jyothi: 'இது முன்னே பண்ணின தப்புக்கு வந்த வேதனையா Amma?'
Amma: 'கொலை செய்தவங்களுக்கு இப்படி பிறப்பு வரும்."
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears in the hospital corridor, a soft glow around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'உடம்பு சரியில்லாதவங்க, வலியால நடக்க முடியாதவங்க — அவங்க முன்னொரு பிறப்புல கொலை பண்ணினவங்க. உயிரைப் பறிக்கறது இப்படி நம்மைப் பின்தொடரும்.'
thirukkural: உயிருடம்பின் நீக்கியா ரென்ப செயிருடம்பின் செல்லாத்தீ வாழ்க்கை யவர்.
english_translation: The loathsome poor sickly and sore  Are killers stained by blood before
moral: The body we are born into reflects the karma of our past.

title: நிலையாமை / Nothing Lasts
hook: What's the biggest mistake people make? Thinking things will last forever.
story: Mani loved his grandmother's old mansion. "It'll be mine forever," he said. Paati laughed softly. "Nothing is forever, kanna." A month later, the old wall collapsed in rain. Mani cried. Paati held him. "I told you. Even mansions leave. Only love stays."
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'இந்த வீடு எப்பவும் இருக்கும்னு நினைச்சேன்'
Paati: 'நிலையாதத நிலையானதுன்னு நினைக்கறதுதான் பெரிய முட்டாள்தனம்'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sits on the collapsed wall, his hand on the old bricks.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'நில்லாதத நிலையானதுன்னு நினைக்கறது மிகப்பெரிய முட்டாள்தனம். உலகத்துல எதுவும் நிரந்தரமில்ல. அத புரிஞ்சிக்கணும்.'
thirukkural: நில்லாத வற்றை நிலையின என்றுணரும் புல்லறி வாண்மை கடை.
english_translation: The worst of follies it is told  The fleeting as lasting to hold
moral: Thinking temporary things are permanent is the greatest foolishness.

title: செல்வம் நாடகம் / Wealth is a Drama
hook: Like a crowd at a drama — wealth gathers, and then it's gone.
story: Lakshmi's father won a gold coin at the fair. She danced with joy. They bought toys and sweets. By evening, someone stole the coin. Lakshmi cried. "It's gone." Appa pointed at the drama stage. "See how the audience came and left? Wealth is like that. Like a drama crowd."
tamil_dialogues: Lakshmi: 'எல்லாம் போயிடுச்சு'
Appa: 'செல்வமும் அப்படித்தான்மா — நாடகம் மாதிரி வந்து போச்சு'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands by the drama stage, the crowd dissolving around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'பெரிய செல்வமும் நாடகக் கூட்டம் மாதிரிதான். போறதும் அதுமாதிரி, வந்த மாதிரி போயிடும். அத நிலையானதுன்னு நினைக்கக் கூடாது.'
thirukkural: கூத்தாட்டு அவைக்குழாத் தற்றே பெருஞ்செல்வம் போக்கும் அதுவிளிந் தற்று.
english_translation: Like a drama-crowd wealth gathers  Like passing show its pride too goes
moral: Wealth comes and goes like an audience at a play — never permanent.

title: செல்வம் நிலைக்காது / Wealth Won't Stay
theme: Impermanence / Do Good While You Can
hook: If wealth never lasts, what should we do with it while we have it?
story: Kumar's father got a big bonus. Kumar wanted a new cricket bat. On the way, they saw an old woman whose house had burned. She had nothing. Kumar looked at his bat money. "Amma, give it to her." Amma smiled. His heart felt full. That night, he slept better than ever.
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'செல்வம் நிலைக்காது Amma, நல்லதுக்கு செலவு பண்ணணும்'
Amma: 'செல்வம் கிடைச்ச உடனே நல்ல காரியம் பண்ணணும்'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands near the burned house, blessing the family with his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'செல்வம் நிலைக்காத ஒண்ணு. அது கிடைச்ச உடனேயே நிலைக்கிற நல்ல காரியம் பண்ணிடணும். அப்பதான் செல்வத்துக்கு அர்த்தம்.'
thirukkural: அற்கா இயல்பிற்றுச் செல்வம் அதுபெற்றால் அற்குப ஆங்கே செயல்.
english_translation: Wealth wanes away; but when it comes  Take care to do enduring things
moral: When wealth comes, use it immediately for things that last — good deeds.

title: நாள் ஒரு வாள் / The Day is a Sword
hook: Tick tock. Each passing day is a sword cutting away your life.
story: Raji's uncle lay sick. "I wasted so many days," he whispered. Raji didn't understand. Periyamma said: "Each day looks harmless, but it's a sword slicing your life." That evening, Raji hugged her uncle tight. "I won't waste a single day," she promised. She started drawing in her diary every night.
tamil_dialogues: Raji: 'ஒரு நாள் ஒரு வாள் மாதிரியா?'
Periyamma: 'ஆமா கண்ணு, அது உயிர அறுக்கும்'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands at the window, the moonlight tracing his outline.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'நாள்னு ஒண்ணு வாள் மாதிரி காட்டி உயிரை வெட்டறது. இத புரிஞ்சவங்க முன்னாடியே நல்ல காரியங்க பண்ணிடுவாங்க.'
thirukkural: நாளென ஒன்றுபோற் காட்டி உயிர்ஈரும் வாளது உணர்வார்ப் பெறின்.
english_translation: The showy day is but a saw  Your life, know that, to file and gnaw
moral: Each day seems harmless but it's steadily cutting away your life — act now.

title: நாக்கு செத்து / Before The Tongue Dies
hook: What if tomorrow your tongue goes numb and it's too late?
story: Velu's thatha couldn't speak anymore. His tongue was paralyzed. He just looked at Velu with sad eyes. Velu cried. "I never told him I loved him." That night, Velu started writing down everything he wanted to say. He read it aloud by thatha's bed every day. Thatha's eyes smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Velu: 'நாக்கு செத்து விக்குள் மேல வர்றதுக்குள்ள நல்ல காரியத்த பண்ணணும்'
Amma: 'இப்பவே சொல்லு கண்ணா நாளைக்கு ரொம்ப லேட்டா ஆயிடும்'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar appears near the old man's bed, the room glowing softly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'நாக்கு செத்து விக்குள் வர்றதுக்குள்ள நல்ல விஷயத்த முன்னாடியே பண்ணிடணும். தாமதிச்சா எதுவும் பண்ண முடியாமப் போயிடும்.'
thirukkural: நாச்செற்று விக்குள்மேல் வாராமுன் நல்வினை மேற்சென்று செய்யப் படும்
english_translation: Ere tongue benumbs and hiccough comes  Rise up to do good deeds betimes
moral: Don't wait until it's too late — do good deeds before death silences you.

title: நேற்று இருந்தான் / Here Yesterday, Gone Today
hook: Someone was here yesterday. Today, they're not. That's the truth of this world.
story: Chitra's neighbour Uncle Pandi died suddenly. She had waved at him just yesterday. "Appa, where did he go?" Appa said nothing. Just held her hand at the funeral. "This world is like that. Yesterday he was here. Today he's not." Chitra hugged her father tight. She never missed saying goodbye again.
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'நேத்த இருந்தாரு இல்ல Appa, இன்னைக்கு இல்ல'
Appa: 'அதான் இந்த உலகத்துல பெரிய விஷயம்'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar walks slowly behind the funeral procession, his head bowed.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'இந்த உலகத்துல நேத்து இருந்தவன் இன்னைக்கு இல்லை. அதுதான் இந்த உலகத்தின் பெருமை — எதுவும் நிரந்தரமில்லை.'
thirukkural: நெருநல் உளனொருவன் இன்றில்லை என்னும் பெருமை உடைத்துஇவ் வுலகு.
english_translation: One was yesterday; not today!
moral: This world has a harsh truth — no one stays forever.

title: அடுத்த நொடி / Next Moment
hook: You plan for millions of things but don't know if you'll live the next hour.
story: Saravanan planned his whole summer — cricket camp, bike trip, video games. "I'm going to do everything!" he said. His teacher laughed gently. "You plan for millions of things. But you don't even know if you'll be alive next hour." Saravanan was stunned. He went home and hugged his mom — right now.
tamil_dialogues: Saravanan: 'நான் கோடி கோடி விஷயம் நினைக்கிறேன்'
Teacher: 'ஆனா அடுத்த பொழுது கூட உயிரோட இருப்பியான்னு தெரியாது'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands at the street corner, a book in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'ஒரு பொழுது கூட வாழறது நமக்கு தெரியாது. ஆனா நாம கோடி கணக்கா திட்டம் போடறோம். இந்த உண்மை நமக்கு எப்பவும் நினைவு இருக்கணும்.'
thirukkural: ஒருபொழுதும் வாழ்வது அறியார் கருதுப கோடியும் அல்ல பல.
english_translation: Man knows not his next moment  On crores of things he is intent
moral: You plan millions of things but don't know if you'll live another hour.

title: உடம்பும் உயிரும் / Body And Soul
hook: Like a bird leaving its eggshell — that's how the soul leaves the body.
story: Seetha watched a pigeon egg hatch. The chick broke out and flew away. The empty shell lay there. "Paati, the shell is lonely." Paati said, "One day our soul will leave our body like that bird. The body is just an eggshell." Seetha touched her chest. "Who am I then?" "The bird inside."
tamil_dialogues: Seetha: 'உடம்பு முட்டை ஓடு மாதிரி, உயிர் பறவை மாதிரி?'
Paati: 'ஆமா கண்ணு, எப்ப வேணும்னாலும் பறந்துடும்'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands by the pond, a pigeon perched on his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'குடம்பைய விட்டு புள் பறக்கற மாதிரிதான், உடம்ப விட்டு உயிர் பறக்கும். உயிர்க்கும் உடம்புக்கும் இருக்கிற நட்பு அப்படிப்பட்டது. அது எப்ப வேணும்னாலும் முடிஞ்சிடும்.'
thirukkural: குடம்பை தனித்துஒழியப் புள்பறந் தற்றே உடம்பொடு உயிரிடை நட்பு.
english_translation: The soul from body any day  Like bird from egg-shell flies away
moral: The soul is just a temporary guest in the body — ready to leave anytime.

title: உறக்கம் சாவு / Sleep And Death
hook: Death is just a deep sleep. Birth is like waking up from it.
story: Bhavani was scared of death. "Appa, what happens after we die?" Appa pointed at a sleeping dog. "Look. Sleep, right? Death is like that." She nodded. "And birth?" "Like waking up from that sleep. Into a new day. A new life." Bhavani looked at the flowers. "So dying isn't scary?"
tamil_dialogues: Bhavani: 'சாவுனா தூக்கம் மாதிரி, பிறப்புனா எழுந்திருக்கற மாதிரி?'
Appa: 'ஆமா கண்ணு, ஒரு தூக்கம் முடிஞ்சா இன்னொரு விழிப்பு'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar sits on a garden bench, surrounded by blooming lilies.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'உறங்கறது மாதிரிதான் சாவு. உறங்கி விழிக்கறது மாதிரிதான் பிறப்பு. பயப்பட ஒண்ணுமில்ல. இது இயற்கையின் சுழற்சி.'
thirukkural: உறங்கு வதுபோலுஞ் சாக்காடு உறங்கி விழிப்பது போலும் பிறப்பு.
english_translation: Death is like a slumber deep  And birth like waking from that sleep
moral: Death is just sleep; birth is waking up. Both are part of one cycle.

title: வீடில்லா உயிர் / Homeless Soul
hook: The soul lives in the body like a traveller in a lodge — never a permanent home.
story: Gowri saw a traveller at the station who stayed one night and left. "Thatha, why do people travel?" Thatha said, "Everyone is a traveller. Even our soul travels. This body is just a lodge." Gowri looked at her hands. "So I'm not this body?" "No. You're the traveller inside."
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'உயிருக்கு வீடு எங்க Thatha?'
Thatha: 'உயிருக்கு வீடே கிடையாதும்மா, உடம்புல தங்கியிருக்கு, அவ்வளவுதான்'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Thiruvalluvar stands on the railway platform, watching the departing train.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: 'உடம்புல உயிர் தங்கியிருக்கறது ஒரு தங்கும் விடுதில தங்கற மாதிரி. அது நிரந்தரமான வீடு இல்ல. உயிர் எப்ப வேணும்னாலும் அங்க இருந்து புறப்படத் தயாரா இருக்கு.'
thirukkural: புக்கில் அமைந்தின்று கொல்லோ உடம்பினுள் துச்சில் இருந்த உயிர்க்கு.
english_translation: The life berthed in this body shows  A fixed home it never knows
moral: The soul is only lodging temporarily in the body — this is not our permanent home.

title: Let It Go, Kanna
hook: Kannan's Thatha makes him give away his precious marble collection — and he discovers something bigger.
story: Kannan had collected 108 marbles over three years — blue cats-eyes, green swirls, red fireballs. Thatha said, "Oru nalla visayam solla poren. Ithula oru half-a eduthu, inga temple la vara paasanga kuduthudu." Kannan cried that night. Next morning, he placed 54 marbles on the temple step. A barefoot boy's face lit up like Deepavali. Kannan's chest felt lighter than air. He ran back to Thatha. "Why you smiling?" Thatha asked. Kannan just hugged him.
tamil_dialogues: Kannan: 'Thatha, enna collection-a kuduthuta? Adhu en nenjula irukku!'
Thatha: 'Sollu da — adha kodutha pinna un manasu enna maadhiri irukku?'
Kannan: 'Kaila irundha pochu... aana nenjula nikkudhu Thatha.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple step, the 54 marbles arranged in a perfect circle around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannan, nee panna maadhiri — yaadhadha vittu kodutha, adhula irundha kashtam poidum. Atha vida periya sugangal varum."
thirukkural: யாதனின் யாதனின் நீங்கியான் நோதல் அதனின் அதனின் இலன்.
english_translation: From what from what a man is free, from that, from that his torments flee.
moral: Every attachment you release is a weight lifted from your heart.

title: Appa's Old Watch
theme: Renunciation / True Joy After Letting Go
hook: Maya watches Appa give away his prized vintage watch to a stranger and thinks he's gone mad.
story: Appa wore his father's old HMT watch every day — never took it off. A customer came, saw it, said he'd lost his own father's watch and was heartbroken. Without a pause, Appa unstrapped it. "Inga vangikko." Maya gasped. "Appa! Adhu thatha's watch!" That night, the customer returned with homemade payasam. "Un appa kodutha watch enakku korangala maadhiri irukku." Maya finally understood: giving made Appa richer.
tamil_dialogues: Maya: 'Appa, unga appa watch-a vera aalukku koduthuta!'
Appa: 'Adhu oru kai kattradhu ma. Adha vida valiya sonthama irundha watch vera edhum irukka?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar winds a vintage HMT watch, its ticking echoing through the shop.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Veendum adhai vida periya sugangal varum — adhu dhan thuravu. Adhai vittu thanni kuda anubavichu paaru."
thirukkural: வேண்டின்உண் டாகத் துறக்க துறந்தபின் ஈண்டுஇயற் பால பல.
english_translation: Give up all to gain the True and endless joys shall hence seek you.
moral: The most generous act is the seed of unexpected joy.

title: The Sweet Stall War
hook: Viji must walk past 14 sweet stalls without buying anything — and her tongue fights her brain.
story: Akka challenged Viji: cross Pudhu Mandapam without eating one sweet. "Mudiyum!" Viji said. Then the jalebi stall sizzled. Then the mysurpak cart called her name. Then the halwai waved hot gulab jamuns under her nose. Viji stopped. Her hand went to her pocket. Akka didn't say anything. Viji closed her eyes, counted to ten, and walked. At the end, Akka bought her one jalebi — she'd earned it.
tamil_dialogues: Viji: 'Akka, adha paathale venum pola irukku! Oru velai mattum?'
Akka: 'Oru velai dhaan... appo innoru velai. Adhu dhaan pudichirukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the jalebi stall, steam swirling around his white beard.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neeyum akka sonna maadhiri — adhu konjam, innu konjam nu kondu poidradhu dhaan asai. Adha nirutha vendum."
thirukkural: அடல்வேண்டும் ஐந்தன் புலத்தை விடல்வேண்டும் வேண்டிய வெல்லாம் ஒருங்கு.
english_translation: Curb the senses five and renounce the carving desires all at once.
moral: Winning against one desire makes you stronger against the next.

title: The Empty Room
hook: Selvam's family is moving to a smaller house — he must leave his toys, his bed, everything — then finds peace in nothing.
story: The moving truck was half empty. "We can't take everything," Amma said. Selvam's room was down to a plastic chair. He cried on it. That night in the empty room, his voice echoed. He shouted "Haaai!" and the room shouted back. He laughed. He rolled on the bare floor. The fan whirred above him and he felt... free. Without the clutter, the room was a playground. He told Amma, "Namma innum koraika vendiyadhu illa Amma."
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Amma, ivlo space irukku! Munnadi room full-a irundhadhu. Naan onnum paathadhe illa!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhaan Selvam — neraiya irundha, adha dhaan paathutu iruppom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar lies on the bare floor, watching the fan blade shadows dance.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porul irundha oru mayakkam. Adhu illadhadhu dhaan nijam. Veliya onnum illadhadhu manasukku nimmadhi."
thirukkural: இயல்பாகும் நோன்பிற்கொன்று இன்மை உடைமை மயலாகும் மற்றும் பெயர்த்து.
english_translation: To have nothing is law of vows; having the least deludes and snares.
moral: The less you own, the more you see.

title: The Birthday Gift That Weighed Too Much
theme: Renunciation / Body Itself is a Burden
hook: Priyanka gets a puppy for her birthday but learns the heaviest burden isn't the puppy — it's her own wants.
story: Periyamma gave Priyanka a golden retriever pup. She was overjoyed. Then Periyamma said, "Ithu un kaila irukka vendiyadhu. Aana, namma manasula irukka vendiyadha irundha, adhu dhaan kashtam." Priyanka didn't understand. That evening, she saw a stray dog with matted fur, ribs showing. Periyamma fed it. "Why him but not the puppy?" Priyanka asked. "I want nothing," Periyamma said. "That's why I can help everyone." Priyanka looked at the puppy, then at the stray, and understood — the burden wasn't having the puppy; it was wanting.
tamil_dialogues: Priyanka: 'Periyamma, ungalukku enna venum?'
Periyamma: 'Onnum venam. Adhu dhaan periya aaratru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits between the two dogs, scratching both their ears equally.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku edhu venumo adhu dhaan un kettu. Venam nu irundha, unakku thunai irukka vendiyadhu illa."
thirukkural: மற்றும் தொடர்ப்பாடு எவன்கொல் பிறப்பறுக்கல் உற்றார்க்கு உடம்பும் மிகை.
english_translation: Why add to bonds while this body is too much for saints to be birth-free.
moral: Wanting nothing is the greatest freedom.

title: The Trophy Wall
hook: Arvind has 23 trophies on his wall but loses one race — and learns winning isn't everything.
story: Arvind was the fastest runner in school. 23 gold trophies lined his shelf. Then at the district meet, a new boy beat him by two seconds. Arvind didn't talk for a day. Appa came to his room. "Unakku enna da? Neeyum odra payyan. Avanum odra payyan. Yaarum periyavan illa." Arvind stared at his trophies. They looked like metal now, not magic. He took the biggest one down. "Appa, idha vida mudiyuma?" "Mudiyum da." They gave it to the school library.
tamil_dialogues: Arvind: 'Appa, avan enna vittutu poittan. Naan loser!'
Appa: 'Loser-a? Avan odran. Nee odura. Rendume same da. Un periya aasai dhaan unna thookki pottadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar holds the trophy, turning it over in his hands, not impressed.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naan, enadhu nu oru perumai. Adha vida mudiyudhu. Adhu dhaan porul. Vaanavargalukum kedaiyaadha idam adhu."
thirukkural: யான்எனது என்னும் செருக்குஅறுப்பான் வானோர்க்கு உயர்ந்த உலகம் புகும்.
english_translation: Who curbs the pride of I and mine, gets a world rare for gods to gain.
moral: Pride is a cage; humility is the key.

title: The Sticky Fingers
hook: Meena grabs a hot murukku straight from the oil and won't let go — the pain teaches her about clinging.
story: Paati was frying murukku. Meena wanted one so bad she reached into the oil and grabbed it. Hot oil burned her fingers, but she held on. "Pakki! Vidu!" Paati yelled. Meena dropped it, crying. Paati put butter on her fingers. "Sonna dhaan puriyum — pidichu irundha valikkum, viduna vali pogum." Meena sniffled. "Paati, adhu pidichu irundhadhu dhaan kashtam." Paati nodded. "Adhu dhaan da — ponna, aasaiya, ellathaiyum pidichu irundha valikkum."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Paati, viduna dhaan vali pogudhu!'
Paati: 'Ama da. Pidichu irundhu, manasula irundha kashtam. Vida therinja dhaan aaraadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the kitchen threshold, a murukku cooling on a banana leaf.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pidichadhu dhaan kastam. Pidichadhu vida dhaan nimmadhi. Manasula pidichadhu vida, adhu vida periya kashtam."
thirukkural: பற்றி விடாஅ இடும்பைகள் பற்றினைப் பற்றி விடாஅ தவர் க்கு.
english_translation: Grief clings on and on to those who cling to bonds without release.
moral: The tighter you hold, the more it hurts — let go to heal.

title: The Net and the Starfish
theme: Renunciation / The Free vs The Entangled
hook: Karthik finds a starfish tangled in a fishing net — and his Appa shows him the difference between being caught and being free.
story: Karthik found a starfish wrapped in old fishing line. It was alive but trapped. "Appa, idha paaru!" Together they untangled it, strand by strand. The moment the last thread slipped off, the starfish didn't move. Then slowly, it inched toward the water. A wave carried it home. "Appa, idhu enna kathai?" Appa said, "Namma starfish mathiri dhaan. Namma manasula neraiya kayaru irukku. Adha potta dhaan namma free." Karthik thought about his anger over losing his pencil.
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Appa, starfish ku kayaru pidichu irundhadhu, adha viduna swim pannudhu.'
Appa: 'Ama. Namma manasum adhe maadhiri. Pidichu irundhadhu vida dhaan poidalam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels in the shallow water, the freed starfish on his palm, sliding it into the sea.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pidichadhu vida dhaan unmai oru nimmadhi. Vitta payal dhaan thalaipatta payal. Mathavan mayangi irupaanga."
thirukkural: தலைப்பட்டார் தீரத் துறந்தார் மயங்கி வலைப்பட்டார் மற்றை யவர்.
english_translation: Who renounce all are free from care; others suffer delusive snare.
moral: Freedom begins where attachment ends.

title: The Broken Cycle
hook: Bhavana's new cycle is stolen — and Anna shows her that everything passes, even pain.
story: Bhavana locked her new cycle outside the shop. Two minutes later — gone. Chain cut. She screamed, cried, kicked the pole. Anna hugged her. "Pochu da." "Anna, adhu puthu cycle!" "Morning irundhadhu. Ippo illa. Evening vera cycle vanthudum. Adhu pola dhaan — ellaam poidum." Bhavana looked at the empty pole. She thought about the bird that died last month. The rainbow after rain. Everything came, everything went. She blew her nose. "Saridhaan Anna. Poi saapdalaam."
tamil_dialogues: Bhavana: 'Anna, enna cycle! Enna pannuva?'
Anna: 'Azhuda dhaan mudiyum. Cycle pochu. Un kashtamum poidum. Adhu dhaan unmai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans against the pole where the cycle was tied, a small yellow flower in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulagam nilaikala. Adha therinja dhaan aaraadhu. Cycle pochu, kashtam varudhu — adhu pogum podhu, nimmadhi varum."
thirukkural: பற்றற்ற கண்ணே பிறப்பறுக்கும் மற்று நிலையாமை காணப் படும்.
english_translation: Bondage cut off, rebirth is off; the world then seems instable stuff.
moral: Everything changes — peace comes when you accept that.

title: The Man Who Had Nothing
hook: Chitra meets a man who owns nothing but radiates joy — and learns who to truly hold on to.
story: At Srirangam temple, a man with only a dhoti sat smiling at the pigeons. Babies crawled on him. He laughed. Chitra was fascinated. "Amma, ivar kita onnum illa. Appo enna santosha?" Amma said, "Avar kita onnum illa. Aana avar dhaan ellathaiyum pidichu irukkar." Chitra didn't get it. The man looked at her. "Kozhandha, unakku enna venum?" "Onnum venam," Chitra said without thinking. The man laughed so hard the pigeons flew. "Adhu dhaan da — onnum venam nu irukradhu dhaan periya selvam."
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'Unkalukku enna venum?'
Sanyasi: 'Enakku onnum venam. Adhu dhaan enakku irukku.'
Chitra: 'Onnum illadhadhu... selva maadhiri irukku?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits among the pigeons, the sanyasi's laughter echoing around them.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Endha porulaiyum pidikala — avar dhaan pidikradhu. Avarai pidichu, nammalum vida kathukalam. Adhu dhaan setthi."
thirukkural: பற்றுக பற்றற்றான் பற்றினை அப்பற்றைப் பற்றுக பற்று விடற்கு.
english_translation: Bind thyself to the unbound one, that binding breaks all bonds anon.
moral: Hold on to the one who holds onto nothing — that is the way to freedom.

title: The Fake Diamond Ring
theme: Realizing the Truth / Mistaking False for True
hook: Divya buys a sparkly "diamond" ring on the street — then Paati shows her what real value looks like.
story: Divya spent her pocket money on a shiny ring a street vendor sold as "real diamond." It sparkled like crazy. She showed Paati proudly. Paati took it to the window. "Nalla irukku." She scratched it against her gold bangle. The shine peeled off. Plastic. Divya's face fell. Paati said, "Adhu dhaan pazhagam — veliya nalla irundha, adhu nalladhu nu nenaikkudhu. Un kai la irukradhu dhaan sariya? Adha vida periya unmai un manasula irukku." Divya stopped crying. "Paati, idhu poi dhaana?" "Ama. Aana nee dhaan unmai."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Paati, adhu mummy diamond maadhiri dhaan irundhadhu!'
Paati: 'Ama, aana adhu poi. Un ullae irukka unmai adha vida shining.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar holds the faded plastic ring, light passing through its center, empty.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Veliya theriyum porulai unmai nu nenaikradhu dhaan mayakkam. Adhu dhaan namma kettu. Summa irukka vittu paaru."
thirukkural: பொருளல்ல வற்றைப் பொருளென்று உணரும் மருளானாம் மாணாப் பிறப்பு.
english_translation: That error entails ignoble birth which deems vain things as things of worth.
moral: Not everything that glitters is gold — the truest treasure is within.

title: Under the Bed
theme: Realizing the Truth / Light After Ignorance
hook: Kavin is terrified of the monster under his bed — until Appa makes him look with a flashlight.
story: Every night, Kavin heard scratching under his bed. He was sure a monster lived there. Appa came with a torch. "Nee paathiya?" "No, but I heard!" Appa lay on the floor and shone the light. A mouse. A tiny, trembling mouse. Kavin's fear vanished. He laughed nervously. "Appa, adhu dhaana monster?" Appa said, "Kathiradhu dhaan da. Nee bayanthu irundhadhu, athu dhaan irula. Velichcham potta, ellam therim. Manasula irukka mayakkamum adhe dhaan."
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Appa, adhu mouse-a? Naan monster nu nenachen!'
Appa: 'Irul la irundhadhu monster illa da. Irul dhaan matter. Velicham potta, unmai theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside the bed, the mouse sitting on his shoulder, Kavin laughing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Irul dhaan kettadhu. Velicham dhaan unmai. Manasula irukka irulai potta dhaan unmai theriyum. Appo dhaan sugangal varum."
thirukkural: இருள்நீங்கி இன்பம் பயக்கும் மருள்நீங்கி மாசறு காட்சி யவர்க்கு.
english_translation: Men of spotless pure insight enjoy delight devoid of night.
moral: Fear lives in darkness — shine the light of truth and it disappears.

title: The Swimming Test
theme: Realizing the Truth / Clarity Removes Doubt
hook: Rajesh is terrified of the deep end — until Anna teaches him that doubt is deeper than water.
story: Rajesh clung to the pool edge. The deep end looked like an ocean. "Anna, adha thandida mudiyadhu." Anna swam beside him. "Enakku theriyum? Nee bayapadradhu thanni illa. Nee bayapadradhu 'mudiyuma mudiyadha' nu doubt dhaan." Rajesh looked at the water. He took a breath. He pushed off the wall. For three seconds he panicked. Then his arms remembered. Stroke, stroke, breath. He reached the other side. He turned. Anna was grinning. "Pathiya? Athan munnae adhu dhaan doubt. Adha vittuta, ellam easy."
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Anna, deep end la kooda muthala nenachen mudiyadhu nu!'
Anna: 'Nee mudiyadha nu nenaicha dhaan mudiyadhu. Manasula clear iruntha, thanni kooda thadai illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar floats on his back in the middle of the pool, arms spread.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sandegam dhaan periya adaippu. Adhu illadha manasu vaanai vida nerungiya irukku. Un sandegatha vida."
thirukkural: ஐயத்தின் நீங்கித் தெளிந்தார்க்கு வையத்தின் வானம் நணிய துடைத்து.
english_translation: To doubtless minds whose heart is clear, more than earth heaven is near.
moral: Doubt is the only barrier — clarity makes everything possible.

title: The Five Senses Game
theme: Realizing the Truth / Beyond the Senses
hook: Ananya plays a blindfold game with Chithappa — and discovers there's more to knowing than seeing.
story: Chithappa blindfolded Ananya at the silk loft. "Ippo sollu — idhu enna?" He put a silk thread in her hand. "Thread." "Correct. Ippo?" A bell. "Bell." "Ippo?" He put nothing. Ananya waited. "Onnum illa." "Correct! Adhu dhaan — adha namma kaal, kai, mugam, sevi therinjikala. Aana adhu irukku." He removed the blindfold. "Namma anchu pulangalaala dhaan ellam therinjudha? Atha vida periya ariivu ullae irukku." Ananya looked at her own hand. She felt something — not touch, just knowing.
tamil_dialogues: Ananya: 'Chithappa, kaalaala theriyadha dhaan edhavadhu irukka?'
Chithappa: 'Nee innu nenaikra adhu dhaan — anchu pulangalaala theriyadhu. Adhu dhaan meiyan ariivu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the loom, blindfolded, weaving a silk saree without looking.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anchu porivaala dhaan ellaam theriyum nu nenaikradhu thappu. Athisaya ariivu manasula dhaan irukku. Adha kaandhu pidichu."
thirukkural: ஐயுணர்வு எய்தியக் கண்ணும் பயமின்றே மெய்யுணர்வு இல்லா தவர்க்கு.
english_translation: Knowledge of five senses is vain without knowing the Truth within.
moral: Your five senses only see the surface — truth lives deeper.

title: The Mango Seed Secret
theme: Realizing the Truth / Seeing Things As They Are
hook: Thatha gives Muthu a dried-up mango seed and says it's a tree — Muthu learns to see what's hidden.
story: Thatha handed Muthu a small, brown, wrinkly mango seed. "Idhu oru maram." Muthu laughed. "Thatha, idhu seed dhaan. Vera enna irukku?" Thatha made him plant it. Every day Muthu stared at the mud. Nothing. Week two. Nothing. Week three — a tiny green shoot. Muthu screamed. Thatha came running. "Paatha? Seed ullae maram irundhadhu. Unga kaal ungalukku adha kaatadha. Aana nijam adhu dhaan. Appo dhaan — porulai athu pola irukku."
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Thatha, seed la irundha maram, adhu njaan theriyala!'
Thatha: 'Ama da. Namma kaal ku theriyadha dhaan neraiya unmai irukku. Adha kaandhu pidikradhu dhaan ariivu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the mango sapling, its two tiny leaves glowing in the sun.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pazhaku, adha pola iruntha dhaan. Adhu pola irukka. Atha therinja dhaan ariivu. Nee sonnadhu pola — porulai pathu kaandhu pidu."
thirukkural: எப்பொருள் எத்தன்மைத் தாயினும் அப்பொருள் மெய்ப்பொருள் காண்பது அறிவு.
english_translation: Knowledge is truth of things to find in every case of every kind.
moral: True knowledge is seeing what something really is, not just what it looks like.

title: The Old Man's Pen
theme: Realizing the Truth / The Path of No Return
hook: Nandini visits an old age home and meets a man who has stopped wanting to be anywhere else — he's found the path.
story: Nandini went with Amma to distribute bananas. An old man sat by the window, not eating, not speaking. Just watching the sky. "Amma, avarukku enna achu?" Amma whispered, "Avar innoru varam veliya varadhu. Mela poga vendiyadhu na, Nandini." Nandini sat beside him. "Thatha, ungalukku enna aasai?" He turned slowly. "Aasai illa kozhandha. Naan poi serntha idathuku thirumba vara vendiyadhu. Adhu dhaan porum." Nandini didn't fully understand, but she felt a strange peace near him.
tamil_dialogues: Nandini: 'Thatha, ungalukku enna venum?'
Thatha: 'Onnum venam. Naan serntha idam dhaan porum. Thirumba varadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the old man, both looking at the same sunset through the window.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee ippo irukka idathula irundhu meiya ariivai adaintha payal, thirumba varadhu. Adhu dhaan kathai mudivu."
thirukkural: கற்றீண்டு மெய்ப்பொருள் கண்டார் தலைப்படுவர் மற்றீண்டு வாரா நெறி.
english_translation: Who learn and here the Truth discern, enter the path of non-return.
moral: Those who truly see the truth find a path they never need to leave.

title: The Broken Mirror
theme: Realizing the Truth / One Minded Vision
hook: Kathir breaks his sister's mirror in anger — and Amma shows him that the fault was never in the mirror.
story: Kathir and his sister fought. He threw her bag. It hit the mirror. Crack — the mirror split his face into a hundred angry pieces. He froze. Amma came. She didn't shout. She picked up a shard. "Idha paaru. Un mugam irukku. Rendaanathu shard la? Un mugam. Moonjaana shard la? Un mugam. Aana adhu dhaan da — athuverai. Nee kovam pannuna, adhu lachu lachu aai mudidhu." Kathir started crying. "Neeye dhaan sari panna vendum. Adhu marum. Aana nee maari dhaan aaganum."
tamil_dialogues: Kathir: 'Amma, naan dhaan kovam pannitten. Adhu dhaan thappu.'
Amma: 'Kovam dhaan kanna mooduthu. Manasula onna vaichu paakradhu dhaan ariivu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar looks into a single unbroken shard of mirror, seeing clearly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru ninaivu dhaan vendum. Iru ninaivu iruntha, mayakkam. Sariya paakradhu dhaan periya ariivu."
thirukkural: ஓர்த்துள்ளம் உள்ளது உணரின் ஒருதலையாப் பேர்த்துள்ள வேண்டா பிறப்பு.
english_translation: One-minded sage sees inner-truth; he is free from thoughts of rebirth.
moral: A single, focused mind sees the truth — scattered thoughts only create confusion.

title: The Pencil Box War
theme: Realizing the Truth / Knowledge Ends Folly
hook: Janani thinks Kavya stole her pencil box and gossips about her — then learns the truth and feels foolish.
story: Janani's pencil box was missing. She was sure Kavya took it — Kavya always looked at it. She told everyone. "Avala nambadha, ava thirudi!" For three days, Kavya sat alone. Day four, Janani found the pencil box under her own desk. It had fallen. She felt sick. She went to Kavya. "Kavya... naan dhaan thappu. Unakku theriyadha dhaan." Kavya looked up. "Sari. I don't hate you." Janani cried. Such a small thing, but she'd been so blind.
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Kavya, naan unmai therinjikkaama pesitten. Sorry.'
Kavya: 'Unakku therinjudha? Nee therinjikanum nu ninacha dhaan theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits between their desks, the pencil box between them, open.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pidha mayakkam dhaan namma kastam. Ama, therinja dhaan adha vida mudiyum. Athu dhaan periya ariivu."
thirukkural: பிறப்பென்னும் பேதைமை நீங்கச் சிறப்பென்னும் செம்பொருள் காண்பது அறிவு.
english_translation: It is knowledge to know Self-Truth and remove the folly of birth.
moral: True knowledge removes the folly of false judgments.

title: The Leaking Boat
theme: Realizing the Truth / Knowing the Refuge
hook: Kumaravel's thatha takes him fishing in a boat with a leak — and teaches him where true safety lies.
story: Thatha's old boat had a crack. Water seeped in. Kumaravel panicked. "Thatha, thanni! Boat moodiduchu!" Thatha kept rowing. "Adha paakadha. En kaila irukka oar-a paaru. Anga dhaan namma kai." Kumaravel didn't understand. Thatha said, "Boat sari illana, adha vida mudiyadhu. Aana namma dhaan boat-a nadathranom. Namma dhaan athukku kadhal." Kumaravel looked at the oar in his hands. He rowed. The boat moved. The water stopped rising when they reached shore. "Thatha, boat veena? Namma dhaan?" Thatha smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Kumaravel: 'Thatha, boat veena. Namma enna pannuvom?'
Thatha: 'Boat veenana kuda, namma veena illa. Nammala dhaan ellaam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the shore, holding the leaking boat's rope, dry and calm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaar munna nikkala? Unnoda baaram yaar eduppa? Oar un kaila irukku. Thunai ullae irukku."
thirukkural: சார்புணர்ந்து சார்பு கெடஒழுகின் மற்றழித்துச் சார்தரா சார்தரு நோய்.
english_translation: Know the Refuge; off with bondage; be free from ills of thraldom, O sage.
moral: When you know your true support, no external crack can sink you.

title: The Three Angry Words
theme: Realizing the Truth / Ending the Three Poisons
hook: Lathika gets angry, calls her friend a bad word, and Miss Renuka shows her how three things destroy peace.
story: Lathika's best friend Sneha accidentally spilled water on her homework. "Dei! Unakku onnum theriyadha da!" Sneha's eyes welled. Lathika sat alone at lunch. Miss Renuka sat beside her. "Moodu kovam, mathavanga mela poramai, enna theriyadha mayakkam — moonu vishayam. Idha un life la irundha eduthuta, kashtam ellam pogum." Lathika thought. She went to Sneha. "Sorry. Naan thappu. Kovam dhaan. Theriyama dhaan sonnen." Sneha hugged her. Just like that, the world felt lighter.
tamil_dialogues: Lathika: 'Miss, enaku kovam vanthudhu. Adha nirutha mudiyala.'
Miss: 'Kovam dhaan unmai. Aana atha unna vida. Venum na, aaraadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the empty lunch table, three fallen petals before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Moonu periya vishayam: kovam, poramai, mayakkam. Idha vittuta, baaki ellaam sari aagum. Kashtamum poidum."
thirukkural: காமம் வெகுளி மயக்கம் இவ்மூன்றன் நாமம் கெடக்கெடும் நோய்.
english_translation: Woes expire when lust, wrath, folly expire even to name, fully.
moral: Let go of anger, envy, and confusion — every other problem follows.

title: Nila's Red Bangle
theme: Curbing Desire / Root of All Births
hook: Nila wants a red bangle her friend has — Thatha shows her where that wanting comes from.
story: Nila's friend had a beautiful red glass bangle. Nila wanted it so bad her stomach hurt. "Thatha, avala kitta ore oru bangle irukku. Enakku adhe maadhiri venum!" Thatha took her to the bangle shop. Rows and rows of red bangles. "Unakku edhu venum?" Nila's eyes went wide. She couldn't choose. Then she saw green bangles. Then blue. Her want moved. Thatha said, "Paatha? Ippo red, aprom green, aprom blue. Adhaan aasai. Adhu mudiyadhadhu. Adhu dhaan piragavi."
tamil_dialogues: Nila: 'Thatha, aasai enna vida maatadhu pola irukku!'
Thatha: 'Aama da. Adhaan vithu. Adhu iruntha, piragavi varum. Adha vittutama nimmadhi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the bangle shop, one red bangle on his wrist, perfectly still.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aasai dhaan piragaviku vithai. Adha vittutama, jananamum nikkum. Un aasaiya vida kathu."
thirukkural: அவாஎன்ப எல்லா உயிர்க்கும் எஞ்ஞான்றும் தவாஅப் பிறப்பீனும் வித்து.
english_translation: Desire to all, always is seed from which ceaseless births proceed.
moral: Desire is the seed of every restless birth — let it go.

title: The Next Toy
hook: Venba wants every toy in the shop — but Amma asks her one question that stops her heart.
story: Venba's birthday. Amma took her to the toy shop. "Oru vilaiyattu porul vaangi kuduren." Venba grabbed a remote car. Then a doll. Then a puzzle. "Amma, idhum venum, idhum, idhum!" Amma knelt. "Venba, unakku ennana panna? Adhu vaanguna, unakku santhosama varuma? Illa adha vaanga dhaan venuma?" Venba stopped. She looked at the car in her hand. She already felt tired of it. She put it down. "Amma, oru book vaangi kudunga. Adhu pothum." Amma smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Venba: 'Amma, ivlo vilaiyaattu porul irukku, aana enakku onnum venama pola irukku.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhaan unmai aasai — venam nu irukradhu. Adhu dhaan periya selvam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the shop counter, a single book in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Venam, venam nu irukradhu dhaan kastam. Venam nu irundha, piragavi nikkum. Antha nilai dhaan periya vaaram."
thirukkural: வேண்டுங்கால் வேண்டும் பிறவாமை மற்றது வேண்டாமை வேண்ட வரும்.
english_translation: If long thou must, long for non-birth; it comes by longing no more for earth.
moral: The deepest longing should be for not longing at all.

title: The Queen's Seat
hook: Sofia fights her cousin for the window seat in the bus — then Appa shows her a wealth she never imagined.
story: Sofia and cousin Riya raced for the window seat. "Naan dhaan kathukuren!" "Naa dhaan munda!" They shoved. Appa watched. Finally, Riya got it. Sofia sulked the whole ride. At the stop, Appa pointed to a man sleeping on the pavement. "Avan kita onnum illa. Aana avan kashtapada villai. Enakku venum nu avanum solala." Sofia looked. The man was smiling in his sleep. "Appa, avanuku venumna aasai illa." "Adhu dhaan avana selvamaakudhu, ma."
tamil_dialogues: Sofia: 'Appa, avanuku edhavadhu venum pola irukka?'
Appa: 'Illa ma. Enakku venum nu avanuku illa. Adhu dhaan periya selvam — adha vida periya selvam inge illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the sleeping man, his shadow covering him like a blanket.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Venam nu irukradhu dhaan periya porul. Adha vida periya porul innoru idathula illa. Veetu kooda vida periyadhu."
thirukkural: வேண்டாமை அன்ன விழுச்செல்வம் ஈண்டில்லை ஆண்டும் அஃதொப்பது இல்.
english_translation: No such wealth is here and there as peerless wealth of non-desire.
moral: The greatest wealth is wanting nothing.

title: Soapy Hands Clean Heart
hook: Chinnu washes his hands ten times a day — but Paati says the real dirt is inside.
story: Chinnu loved washing his hands. After every little thing, he ran to the well. "Paati, kai velvetta clean!" Paati smiled. "Ama. Ullae irukka kuppai kazhuvura?" Chinnu looked at his chest. "Ulla? Enna irukku?" "Asai, kovam, poramai. Adhu dhaan kuppai. Kai thanni la clean aagum. Aana manasu aasai illadha podhu dhaan clean aagum." Chinnu thought. He looked at his clean hands. Then he shared his lollipop with a crying baby. "Paati! En manasu clean aachu?"
tamil_dialogues: Chinnu: 'Paati, kai kazhuvina clean. Aana manasu clean aaga enna pannanum?'
Paati: 'Aasai illadha irundha, manasu clean. Adhu dhaan periya snanam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar draws water from the well, pouring it over his own head, eyes closed.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ullae irukka kuppai dhaan kazhuvanum. Manasu aasai illama irundha, adhu dhaan thooiyamai."
thirukkural: தூஉய்மை என்பது அவாவின்மை மற்றது வாஅய்மை வேண்ட வரும்.
english_translation: To nothing crave is purity; that is the fruit of verity.
moral: True purity isn't clean hands — it's a heart free from desire.

title: The Empty Nest
hook: Keerthi visits her grandparents' empty house after they moved away — and feels a strange freedom.
story: Keerthi's grandparents had moved to Chennai. Their Ooty house stood empty. Keerthi walked through the silent rooms. Old photos, empty cupboards, a lone chair by the window. "Amma, paati illa, thatha illa. Veedu veenadha irukku." Amma hugged her. "Illa da. Veedu irukku. Aana adhu yaarukkum sontham illa. Adhu dhaan free." Keerthi lay on the floor. Dust motes danced in the sunlight. She could roll, shout, do anything. No rules. She laughed. "Amma, namma dhaan free!"
tamil_dialogues: Keerthi: 'Amma, veetu la ellam irukku aana yaarum illa. Adhu free maadhiri irukku.'
Amma: 'Ama da. Yaarukkum sontham illadha dhaan free. Aasai illama irukradhu dhaan free.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the lone chair by the window, sunlight falling on his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aasai vitta payal dhaan sutandhiran. Avanuku sutandhiran. Adhu dhaan visudha nilai."
thirukkural: அற்றவர் என்பார் அவாஅற்றார் மற்றையார் அற்றாக அற்றது இலர்.
english_translation: The free are those who desire not; the rest not free in bonds are caught.
moral: You are truly free only when nothing owns a piece of your heart.

title: The Sticky Candy Trap
theme: Curbing Desire / Desire is a Snare
hook: Ganesh takes a free candy from a stranger — then finds himself trapped in wanting more.
story: A man at the school gate gave Ganesh a free candy. It was the best candy he'd ever tasted. "Innum venuma? Naalaiku vaa." Next day, Ganesh went back. The man gave him two. "Naalaiku innum korren." Ganesh went every day. His pocket money went to the man. He stopped playing with friends. Anna noticed. "Ganesh, adhu enna?" Ganesh cried. "Anna, naan nirutha mudiyala. Adhu enna trap pola irukku." Anna hugged him. "Adhu dhaan da — aasai oru kanni. Unakku dhaan therinjudha. Adhu dhaan unmai."
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Anna, adhu oru candy dhaan, aana enala nirutha mudiyala!'
Anna: 'Adhu dhaan da aasai. Siriya aasai, periya kanni. Unakku therinjudha, adhu dhaan visudham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, a single candy in his open palm, untouched.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Asai oru kanni. Adha vida dhaan nimmadhi. Nee adha vittuta. Athu dhaan aram. Aparam veetu."
thirukkural: அஞ்சுவ தோரும் அறனே ஒருவனை வஞ்சிப்ப தோரும் அவா.
english_translation: Dread desire; Virtue is there; to every soul desire is snare!
moral: Desire is a trap that looks like a gift — fear it.

title: The Broken Video Game
theme: Curbing Desire / Destroy Desire, Get Freedom
hook: Suresh's gaming console breaks and he rages — then a week without it changes everything.
story: Suresh's PlayStation crashed in the middle of his final match. He screamed, kicked the table, threw the controller. It shattered. Appa didn't say anything. Just picked up the pieces. For three days Suresh was miserable. Day four, he was bored. Day five, he picked up his old cricket bat. He went to the street. His friends cheered. They played till dark. Suresh came home sweaty, laughing. "Appa, naan inniki cricket aadhen!" Appa smiled. "Un aasaiyai kondu pona, nee free aaiduva."
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Appa, athu pochu. Ippo naan enna pannuven?'
Appa: 'Edhavadhu pannu da. Cricket aadu, dost kita pesu. Aasai vittuta, neraiya vazhi irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the street, a cricket bat in his hand, the evening sun behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aasaiyai aravittuta, verayum adhu dhaan nidhi. Sollu da — appo dhaan unakku sutandhiran varum."
thirukkural: அவாவினை ஆற்ற அறுப்பின் தவாவினை தான்வேண்டு மாற்றான் வரும்.
english_translation: Destroy desire; deliverance comes as much as you aspire hence.
moral: When you destroy desire, freedom comes rushing in.

title: The Empty Lunchbox
theme: Curbing Desire / No Desire, No Sorrow
hook: Deepa drops her lunchbox and loses her favorite food — Paati teaches her that sorrow follows desire.
story: Deepa's lunchbox fell. Her favorite biryani spilled on the floor. She cried. "Paati, enna biryani! Naan saapda maatten!" Paati sat beside her. "Ada, athu dhaan problem. Unakku adhu venum nu aasai. Adhu illana, unakku kashtam irukkaadhu." Deepa sniffled. "Ippo enna pannuven?" Paati made her a simple curd rice. Deepa ate quietly. It was good. "Paati, curd rice nalla irukku." "Ama. Nee aasai vida kathuna, enna koduthalum santhosham."
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Paati, biryani illana dhaan enakku sorgam!'
Paati: 'Illa da. Biryani venum nu aasai dhaan sorgam. Adhu illana, curd rice um sugang.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the kitchen floor, eating curd rice with his hand, peaceful.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aasai irundha dhaan kashtam. Adhu illana, kashtamum illa. Adhu dhaan unmai aaraadhu."
thirukkural: அவாஇல்லார்க் கில்லாகுந் துன்பம் அஃதுண்டேல் தவாஅது மேன்மேல் வரும்.
english_translation: Desire extinct, no sorrow-taints; grief comes on grief where it pretends.
moral: No desire means no disappointment — sorrow only lives where wanting grew.

title: The Fairground Ring
theme: Curbing Desire / Joy is the Absence of Desire
hook: Arun wins a stuffed teddy at the fair but immediately wants a bigger one — and misses the joy right in front of him.
story: Arun threw rings at bottles. On his very last ring, he won a small teddy. "Appa! Paatha!" He hugged it. Then he saw a bigger one. "Appa, adha venum!" He threw more rings. Lost his money. Got nothing. He sat on the ground, angry. The small teddy sat beside him. Appa said, "Un kaila irukka teddy paathiya? Adhu unaku romba santosham koduthadhu. Aana nee adha vida periya adha patha udane, adhu pochu." Arun looked at the small teddy. He hugged it again. It felt warm.
tamil_dialogues: Arun: 'Appa, naan periya teddy eduthurpale! Ippo naan loser!'
Appa: 'Illada. Nee periya teddy venum nu aasai. Adhu dhaan un santhosham eduthudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the ring-toss booth, the small teddy in his arms, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periya aasai vittuta, sugangal aarambam. Un kaila irukka chinnadha paaru. Adhu dhaan siri sugangal."
thirukkural: இன்பம் இடையறா தீண்டும் அவாவென்னும் துன்பத்துள் துன்பங் கெடின்.
english_translation: Desire, the woe of woes destroy; joy of joys here you enjoy.
moral: When the pain of wanting dies, joy flows without end.

title: The Tired Little Swimmer
theme: Curbing Desire / The State Beyond All Wants
hook: Elango is exhausted in the middle of the pool — and his coach tells him to stop trying to reach the edge.
story: Elango was halfway across the pool. His legs burned. His arms ached. The other end looked miles away. "Mudiyala, mudiyala!" Coach called. "Nikkadha! Enna nadakradha paaru!" Elango stopped kicking. He let his body go limp. He floated. The water held him up. He didn't need the other end. He was fine right here. Coach said, "Adhu dhaan da — aasai vittuta, nimmadhi. Nee karaiku poganum nu nenaicha dhaan kashtam. Irukkara idathula nimmadha iru." Elango floated, looking at the sky through the glass roof.
tamil_dialogues: Elango: 'Coach, enaku mudiyala. Karaiku poga mudiyala!'
Coach: 'Karai poganum nu aasai vittuta. Thanni dhaan unna thookum. Nee nimmadhi iru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar floats beside Elango, both looking up at the blue ceiling, completely still.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aasai irukka idathula nimmadhi illa. Adha vitutu iru. Antha nilai dhaan periya vaaram. Varum adhu."
thirukkural: ஆரா இயற்கை அவாநீப்பின் அந்நிலையே பேரா இயற்கை தரும்.
english_translation: Off with desire insatiate; you gain the native blissful state.
moral: Let go of wanting and you arrive at the peace that was always waiting for you.

title: The Lost Bat
hook: Vikram's brand new cricket bat falls into the sea — and he blames his fate.
story: Vikram saved pocket money for months to buy a Kashmir willow bat. First match, he hit a six — but the bat slipped from his sweaty hands and splashed into the Bay of Bengal. "Enna oolu Appa! Naa enna pannuven?" He sat sulking on the sand. Appa fished out a floating wooden plank. "Ithu vachu aadalaam?" Vikram tried. The plank had a strange curve that hooked every ball. He scored 50 runs with that junk plank. "Appa, enna mayam?" Appa smiled. "Oozh un kaila irundha bat-a eduthuchu, aana un kaila vella vendra balathai koduthuchu."
tamil_dialogues: Vikram: 'Appa, en bat pochu! En oozh ketti irukku!'
Appa: 'Oozh onnu da — adha dhaan namma seyal maathikalaam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the shoreline, one foot in the waves, holding the curved plank like a bat.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oozh vandhu un kaila irundhadha edukalaam, aana adha vida nalla onna kodukalama. Nee ninaikira maadhiri ellaam nadakkum - aana adhu nalla maadhiri irundha, athu dhaan oozh."
thirukkural: ஆகூழால் தோன்றும் அசைவின்மை கைப்பொருள் போகூழால் தோன்றும் மடி.
english_translation: Efforts succeed by waxing star / Wealth-losing brings waning star.
moral: Fate can take what you have, but your effort decides what you become.

title: The Exam Paper
hook: Nandhini fails her maths exam despite studying — but a forgotten notebook changes everything.
story: Nandhini studied 15 days straight. Still, the exam paper felt like Chinese. She got 34 marks — failing. "Amma, naan padichen! Enakku 34 dhaan vandhuchu!" Amma hugged her. That night, cleaning her bag, they found an old notebook from 5th standard — Nandhini's late Thatha's handwriting. "Kochu ponnukku: oozh nalla irundha, theriyadhadhu theriyum." Inside were all her current chapter sums worked out in 5 different ways. In the retest, she scored 92 — using Thatha's methods. "Amma, appadiye aarambam dhaan purila. Ippo dhaan puraiyudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Nandhini: 'Amma, naan fail aayidachen! En oozh romba mosam!'
Amma: 'Oozh dhaan da kutty — aana adha thirumba sari panna oru vazhi irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at Nandhini's study table, turning the old notebook pages gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oozh unna mudhal feel panna vechichu, aana kaetadhu keta maadhiri dhaan nalla oozhum varum. Thatha solli kodutha aarvam dhaan unakku nalla oozh."
thirukkural: பேதைப் படுக்கும் இழவூழ் அறிவகற்றும் ஆகலூழ் உற்றக் கடை.
english_translation: Loss-fate makes a dull fool of us / Gain-fate makes us prosperous, wise!
moral: Bad fate can confuse you, but good fate reveals the wisdom already within.

title: The Auto Driver's Son
hook: Gowtham can solve complex puzzles without schooling — Selvam discovers his son's hidden talent.
story: Selvam dropped his son at school every day. Gowtham never went in. He sat at the auto stand solving crossword puzzles from old newspapers. One day, a college student couldn't solve a sudoku. Gowtham looked at it for 10 seconds. "Sir, 7 pottu, adha 3, aprom 9." The student solved it. "Un payyan genius-u da!" Selvam was confused. "Nee school pogaama epdi ipdi therinjikitta?" Gowtham shrugged. "Therinjikala. Pona janma kathadhu manasula irukku pola."
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Da, unakku epdi therium ithu ellam?'
Gowtham: 'Theriyadhu appa. Solli kudukka mudiyadhu. Aana therium.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the auto rickshaw, solving a crossword on his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee padaadhadhu onnum illa. Un ullae irukka arivu dhaan veliya varudhu. Adhu dhaan unmai — vaazhkaila kathadhu, pona janmala kathadhu."
thirukkural: நுண்ணிய நூல்பல கற்பினும் மற்றுந்தன் உண்மை யறிவே மிகும்.
english_translation: What matters subtle study deep? / Levels of innate wisdom-keep.
moral: You can study many books, but your innate wisdom will always surpass them.

title: Two Brothers, Two Paths
hook: Sangeetha's cousins get new phones while she gets old books — Paati explains the two fates.
story: Swathi and Swetha came from the US with iPhones, iPads, new clothes. Sangeetha got Paati's hand-me-down books. "Paati, avanga ellam nalla irukka. Naamathen kastama?" Paati opened an old trunk. Inside: 200-year-old palm leaf manuscripts. "Intha vedanga ellam un periya periyavar kai ezhuthu. Ithu unakku kedaikudhu — avanga vangi kuda mudiyadha selvan." Years later, Sangeetha became a Tamil scholar. Her cousins still scrolled Instagram. "Paati, neenga sonna maadhiri dhaan aachu."
tamil_dialogues: Sangeetha: 'Paati, avanga ellam nalla irukka, naan en ipdi?'
Paati: 'Oozh rendu vidham da. Onnu selvan, innonu arivu. Unakku irandavadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the trunk, a palm leaf manuscript unrolled on his lap.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oozh oruthanukku selvan tharum, innoruthanukku arivu tharum. Rendula ethu periyadhu? Kaalam dhaan sollum."
thirukkural: இருவேறு உலகத்து இயற்கை திருவேறு தெள்ளிய ராதலும் வேறு.
english_translation: Two natures in the world obtain / Some wealth and others wisdom gain.
moral: Fate distributes differently — some get wealth, some get wisdom. Both are gifts.

title: The Business That Failed
hook: Periyappa's textile shop burns down — but the fire reveals a hidden fortune.
story: Periyappa's shop caught fire at midnight. Everything burned — 50 lakhs worth of fabric. Karthik cried. "Periyappa, namma ellam pochu." But a week later, while clearing the debris, they found a steel trunk under the floorboards. Inside: 50 sovereigns of gold that Periyappa's father had hidden during emergency in 1965. "Naan ithu paththi kooda theriyama irundhen." Karthik's eyes widened. "Periyappa, kettadhu nalladha maari pochu!" Periyappa laughed. "Oozh — onna edukkum, innonnu tharum."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Periyappa, kade pochu. Nammala irundhadhu ellam pochu!'
Periyappa: 'Oozh dhaan da. Aana adhe oozh dhaan nammala kaapadhichum irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the burned shop, holding a single gold coin that glows.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oozh oru pakkam edukkum, innoru pakkam tharum. Kettadhu maari theriyum, aana athu nalladha maari mudiyum."
thirukkural: நல்லவை எல்லாஅந் தீயவாம் தீயவும் நல்லவாம் செல்வம் செயற்கு.
english_translation: In making wealth fate changes mood; / The good as bad and bad as good.
moral: When making wealth, what seems good may turn bad and what seems bad may turn good.

title: The Stolen Purse
hook: Meena's school fee purse gets stolen — but the thief returns it with extra money.
story: Meena clutched the purse with 2000 rupees — her school fees. The bus was packed. A hand slipped in. The purse was gone. Meena screamed. "Appa! En kai sella pochu!" Appa held her. "Pona pogaattum." Two hours later, a stranger came to their home. "Meena va?" He handed the purse. The money was untouched. Instead, there was a note: "Sorry. Naan thaan eduthen. Aana un appa sonna maadhiri, enakku saapadhu vanga kooda use panna mudiyala. Idhu unakku sonda." Appa smiled. "Sonda dhaan da varum. Adha edutha kooda athu odambu seradhu."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Appa, thirudan kuda atha thirumba vandhutaar!'
Appa: 'Oozh la illadhadhu yaar kaila nikkadhu da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stand, the purse in one hand, a bus ticket in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku sonda illadha porul, nee edutha kooda, adhu un kaila nikkadhu. Unakku sonda, adha yaaralum edukka mudiyadhu."
thirukkural: பரியினும் ஆகாவாம் பாலல்ல உய்த்துச் சொரியினும் போகா தம.
english_translation: Things not thine never remain / Things destined are surely thine.
moral: What is not meant for you won't stay; what is yours can never be taken away.

title: The Lottery Ticket
hook: Muthu's Thatha wins a 10-lakh lottery but gives it all away — and explains why.
story: Thatha bought a 50-rupee ticket on a whim. He won 10 lakhs. The whole street celebrated. Muthu was already planning a new bike. But Thatha went to the temple and donated all 10 lakhs to the annadhanam fund. "Thatha! Enna pannreenga? 10 lakhs!" Thatha sat him down. "Oozh namma kaila koduthadhu. Aana atha namma epdi use pannradhu — athu dhaan namma kaila. Enakku 10 lakhs kudutha, atha vida periya santhosham annadhanathula irukku."
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Thatha, 10 lakhs! Namma vangalaamaadha ellam vangalaam!'
Thatha: 'Oozh koduthadhu da. Atha enna pannradhu — adhu namma choice.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the temple annadhanam hall, serving rice to a long line of people.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oozh unakku kodutha porulai nee epdi anubavikiraai — athu dhaan un choice. Aana koduthavan kaella kodutha alavukku dhaan anubavikka mudiyum."
thirukkural: வகுத்தான் வகுத்த வகையல்லால் கோடி தொகுத்தார்க்கு துய்த்தல் அரிது.
english_translation: Who crores amass enjoy but what / The Dispenser's decrees allot.
moral: Even if you crores amass, you can only enjoy what fate's allotted.

title: The Rich Man's Lesson
hook: Divya sees a homeless man give his last roti to a dog and wonders who is truly poor.
story: At the signal, a homeless man sat with a steel bowl. Three rotis. A street dog limped near him. The man smiled, broke one roti and gave it to the dog. Divya tugged Amma's saree. "Amma, avanuku erandu dhaan roti. Aana naai kku kuduthaan." Amma watched. "Avana vida namma dhaan daridram da." Divya didn't understand. Amma continued, "Avan kaila irundhadhai avana pangu ittaan. Ivalo irundhum kooda namma pangu ittukka maatom. Avan dhaan periya chelvan."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Amma, avan kaila erandu roti dhaan. Aana pati vachaan.'
Amma: 'Avana vida namma kaila niraiya irukku. Namma eduthukka maatom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the homeless man, sharing the remaining roti with him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porulai potta mathiri theriyalaam, aana ullae porumai illadhavan dhaan daridran. Porulai kudutha uLLam dhaan periya selvan."
thirukkural: துறப்பார்மன் துப்புர வில்லார் உறற்பால ஊட்டா கழியு மெனின்.
english_translation: The destitute desire will quit / If fate with ills visit them not.
moral: Even the poor can let go of desire — if fate doesn't test them too harshly.

title: The Rainy Day Fund
hook: Ramesh's family loses everything in a flood — then finds an old well that saves them.
story: Three days of nonstop rain. Water entered their home. TV, fridge, clothes — everything ruined. Ramesh's mother cried. "Ippo enna pannradhu?" Ramesh felt hopeless. Appa walked to the backyard. An old well, dry for 20 years, was now overflowing. Inside: thousands of fish that swam in from the flooded river. Appa sold the fish. "Kovila irundha kaasu pottu. Namma kastam dhaan namma selvan aachu." The neighbors were amazed. Ramesh hugged Appa. "Kettadhu nalladha maari achu!"
tamil_dialogues: Ramesh: 'Appa, ellam pochu. Namma poraiyum pochu.'
Appa: 'Kaeta kaalathula dhaan nalla kaalam theriyum da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the overflowing well, fish jumping around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kettadhu maari dhaan theriyum, aana atha vida nalladhu vandhu kondirukkum. Kaeta kaalatula dhaan nalla kaalatula namma nambikkai irundha athu theriyum."
thirukkural: நன்றாங்கால் நல்லவாக் காண்பவர் அன்றாங்கால் அல்லற் படுவ தெவன்?
english_translation: Who good in time of good perceive / In evil time why should they grieve?
moral: If you recognize goodness in good times, why suffer when times are bad?

title: The Invisible Wire
hook: Mani tries to climb Rockfort 100 times and fails — the 101st time, something invisible holds him back.
story: Mani was stubborn. Every Sunday he tried to climb all 437 steps of Rockfort without stopping. 100 Sundays. 100 failures. His legs gave up at step 396 every time. "Chithappa, naan mudikkira maadhiri irukku. Aana 396 la niruthuraanga kaal!" Chithappa climbed with him. At step 396, he pointed to a small iron gate — locked. "Adhu dhaan da. Oozh. Nee mudikka vendiyadhu illa. Innum kaathiru." Next week, the gate was open. Mani climbed all 437 steps. "Chithappa! Oozh kooda kaathirukku!"
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Chithappa, naan mudichidalaam nu nenachaa, enna thadukkuradhu?'
Chithappa: 'Oozh dhaan. Nee mudichidalaam nu nenacha kooda, oozh sari vara varaikkum mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the top of Rockfort, the iron gate wide open behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oozh periya vali. Neeyadha jeyikka mudiyadhu. Aana oozh sari vanthale, nee mudipadhu dhaan. Kaathiru, aana kai vidalama iru."
thirukkural: ஊழிற் பெருவலி யாவுள மற்றொன்று சூழினுந் தான்முந் துறும்.
english_translation: What power surpasses fate? Its will / Persists against the human skill.
moral: No force is greater than fate — it prevails even against all human planning.

title: The School Captain Election
theme: Glory of Kingship / Six Elements of Leadership
hook: Arul wants to be school captain but loses — Appa shows him the six things a real leader needs.
story: Arul prepared a speech, wore a tie, waved at everyone. He lost by 2 votes. "Appa, naan jeyichidalaam nu nenachen!" Appa sat him down with a paper. "Venda. Oru leader kku venum: padai, kudumbam, porul, amaiyarchi, natpu, aran." Arul was confused. "So?" Appa explained: "Un friends dhaan padai. Un class dhaan kudumbam. Un marks dhaan porul. Un plan dhaan amaiyarchi. Un teachers dhaan natpu. Un confidence dhaan aran. Unakku ivai illa." Arul worked on all six. Next year, he won 48-2.
tamil_dialogues: Arul: 'Appa, naan speech-a nalla dhaan kuduthhen!'
Appa: 'Speech mattum pothaadhu. Leader aaganum na, aaru visayangalum venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the school assembly, six glowing orbs around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru leader aaganum na, padai, kudumbam, porul, amaiyarchi, natpu, aran — intha aarum venum. Adhu dhaan unmaiyaana aatchi."
thirukkural: படைகுடி கூழ்அமைச்சு நட்பரண் ஆறும் உடையான் அரசருள் ஏறு.
english_translation: People, troops, wealth, forts, council, friends / Who owns these six is lion of kings.
moral: A true leader needs six pillars: army, community, wealth, counsel, allies, and strength.

title: The Frightened Firefighter
theme: Glory of Kingship / Four Royal Virtues
hook: Bala dreams of being a firefighter but is scared of heights — Amma teaches him the four qualities.
story: Bala's father was a firefighter who died on duty. Bala wanted to follow him. But he froze on the monkey bars. "Amma, enakku bayama irukku. Naan avan mathiri aaga mudiyadha." The kids laughed. Amma held his face. "Nee dhaan avan magan. Unakku venum: anjama iru, kolai kudu, ariya, aarvama iru. Nee mudichuve." Bala trained every day — strength, knowledge, courage, generosity. One year later, he climbed the 50-foot training tower. "Amma, naan mudichen!"
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Amma, enakku bayama irukku. Naan mudiyadha mathiri irukku.'
Amma: 'Anjama iru, kolai kudu, ariya, aarvama iru. Adhu dhaan pothum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the training tower, a fire hose in one hand, his other hand raised.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvarukku aatchi venum na, anjamai, kolai kuduthal, aRivu, aarvam — intha nangu irundha dhaan mudiyum. Neeyum atha dhaan pannra."
thirukkural: அஞ்சாமை ஈகை அறிவூக்கம் இந்நான்கும் எஞ்சாமை வேந்தர்க் கியல்பு.
english_translation: Courage, giving, knowledge and zeal / Are four failless features royal.
moral: A true leader never lacks courage, generosity, knowledge, and enthusiasm.

title: The Sleepy Morning
theme: Glory of Kingship / Alertness and Learning
hook: Priya sleeps through her alarm and misses the scholarship test — Appa teaches her three kingly habits.
story: Priya's alarm clock broke. She woke up at 10 AM — the test was at 9. She wailed. "Appa! Scholarship pochu!" Appa didn't scold. "Neenga sari. Inime rendu alarm vachiko. Aana adhu mattum pothaadhu. Leader kku venum: thookam illama irukradhu, padikka vendradhu, dhairiyama irukradhu." Priya wrote it on her wall. Every day: 5 AM wake up, 1 hour study, 1 brave act. Next year, she topped the scholarship with full marks. "Appa, moonum irundha dhaan mudiyum!"
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Appa, naan thoongi poiten. Scholarship exam miss achu!'
Appa: 'Oruvar kku moonu visayam venum — thookamillamai, kalvi, thunivudaimai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by Priya's study table, pointing to the three rules on her wall.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvar kku intha moonum venum. Thoongamal irukradhu — kalvi — thunivu. Adhu dhaan vellum."
thirukkural: தூங்காமை கல்வி துணிவுடைமை இம்மூன்றும் நீங்கா நிலனான் பவர்க்கு.
english_translation: Alertness, learning bravery / Are adjuncts three of monarchy.
moral: Three things must never leave a ruler: sleepless alertness, learning, and courage.

title: The Fair Class Monitor
theme: Glory of Kingship / Justice and Honor
hook: Kavin becomes class monitor and must punish his best friend — or lose his honor.
story: Kavin got the monitor badge. His best friend Suresh whispered, "Nee monitor dhaan da. Namma class la namma dhaan!" But Suresh cheated on the test. The whole class saw. If Kavin reported him, their friendship would break. If he didn't, he'd be a fake monitor. Kavin stood up. "Miss, Suresh copy adichaan." Suresh stared in shock. After class, Suresh was about to leave. Kavin said, "Nee thappu pannina, naan solra dhaan. Aana naan un friend dhaan." Suresh smiled after a second. "Sari da. Nee nalla monitor dhaan."
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Monitor da! Un friend kuda report pannureeya?'
Kavin: 'Nee thappu pannina, solra dhaan. Adhu dhaan nijamaana natpu.'
