thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between their two desks, one hand on each boy's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Murai — adhu dhaan periyadhu. Nanbanai vida murai periyadhu. Murai seivaan dhaan unmaiyaana aatchiyaalar."
thirukkural: அறனிழுக்கா தல்லவை நீக்கி மறனிழுக்கா மானம் உடைய தரசு.
english_translation: A brave noble king refrains from vice / Full of virtue and enterprise.
moral: A true ruler never abandons virtue, removes evil, and holds honor firmly.

title: The Lunchbox King
theme: Glory of Kingship / Earn, Guard, Spend
hook: Anitha gets 100 rupees for school lunch but can't decide when to eat, save, or share.
story: Amma gave Anitha 100 rupees. "Intha kaasu vechi onnu vangu." At school, she saw idli for 20, chips for 10, a new pencil box for 50. She wanted everything. "Amma, naan enna pannanum?" Amma explained: "Mudhal sampadichu, aprom sethu vachu, kaathu, aprom pangu pottu kudu. Leader maadhiri." Anitha bought idli for 20, saved 30, put 20 in her piggy bank, and used 30 to buy a slate for her poor friend Rani. "Amma, naan leader maadhiri panniten!"
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Amma, 100 rupees! Enna vanguradhu?'
Amma: 'Sampadichu, sethu vachu, kaathu, pangu kudu — adhu dhaan leader.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the school lunch area, a steel plate with idli in one hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvar kku porulai sampadichu, sethu vachu, kaathu, aprom pangu kudutha dhaan periya aatchi."
thirukkural: இயற்றலும் ஈட்டலுங் காத்தலும் காத்த வகுத்தலும் வல்ல தரசு.
english_translation: The able king gets, stores and guards / And spends them for people's safeguards.
moral: A true leader earns, saves, protects, and shares wealth wisely with all.

title: The Principal's Door
hook: Siva is terrified of the principal — until he sees him buying samosa at the canteen.
story: Principal Joseph was like a god to Siva. Tall, serious, silent. "Principal room ku poga dhaan bayama irukku." One day, Siva's project was due and he forgot it at home. He had to meet the principal. Shaking, he went to the office. Empty. He found the principal at the canteen, eating a samosa, laughing with the cook. "Principal sir...?" The principal smiled. "Vaa da. Samosa venuma?" Siva felt the fear melt. "Sir, konjam nalla dhaan irukeenga." The principal laughed. "Naan kuda oru manushan dhaan da. Vanthu kattu."
tamil_dialogues: Siva: 'Principal sir kitta poga bayama irundhadhu...'
Principal: 'Bayapadadha da. Oruvar kitta poga, avaru epdi irukkaar nu therinjudha dhaan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the canteen table, sharing a samosa with Principal Joseph.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvar kitta poga bayam irukka koodadhu. Avare easy a irundha, avar kizhae irukka makkalum santhosama iruppaanga."
thirukkural: காட்சிக் கெளியன் கடுஞ்சொல்லன் அல்லனேல் மீக்கூறும் மன்னன் நிலம்
english_translation: That land prospers where the king is / Easy to see, not harsh of words.
moral: A kingdom prospers when the ruler is approachable and speaks gently.

title: The Sweet Words
theme: Glory of Kingship / Sweet Speech and Giving
hook: Pooja's neighbor shouts at everyone but gives generously — and Pooja learns the power of sweet words.
story: Uncle Kumar was rich. He donated 5000 to the temple fund. But he shouted at the priest. "Edhuvum sariyilla ungaluku!" Pooja was confused. "Amma, avaru 5000 kuduthaaru, aana romba kovama irukkaaru." Amma took her to the sweet shop. "Pooja, idhu rosa peda. Intha peda evalo nalla irukku. Aana avaru kovama irundha, atha saapdravar vayila inippa irukkaadhu." Pooja understood. "Parava illa. Intha pedava vida, intha pedava kudutha sirippu dhaan periyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Amma, avaru periya danam kuduthaaru. Aana kovam varaaru.'
Amma: 'Kodai mattum pothaadhu. Intha maadhiri kodai kudutha, adhu valikkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the sweet shop, holding a rose peda, offering it with a smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kodai mattum pothaadhu. Atha intha maadhiri kodutha dhaan adhu nalladhu. Intha maadhiri kudutha, adhu valikkum."
thirukkural: இன்சொலால் ஈத்தளிக்க வல்லார்க்குத் தன்சொலால் தான்கண் டனைத்திவ் வுலகு.
english_translation: The world commends and acts his phrase / Who sweetly speaks and gives with grace.
moral: The one who gives with sweet words owns the whole world through their speech.

title: The Missing Slate
theme: Glory of Kingship / Justice as Divinity
hook: Venba's slate goes missing and she accuses the wrong person — the teacher teaches about true justice.
story: Venba's new slate vanished. She saw Geetha with a similar slate. "Miss! Geetha en slate edutha!" Miss asked Geetha. "Illenga, idhu en slate." Venba snatched it. Scratch marks matched. But Miss examined closely — the scratch was on the LEFT side. Venba's slate had scratch on RIGHT. "Venba, idhu Geetha slate dhaan. Un slate un bag la irukku." Venba checked. Her slate was buried under books. She had forgotten. "Miss... naan thappa sollitten." Miss smiled. "Muraikku appuram dhaan venum. Munnadi thappu seidha, athu murai illa."
tamil_dialogues: Venba: 'Miss, ava dhaan eduthaaru!'
Miss: 'Rendum paaru. Unaku therinja varaikkum murai seiyakoodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the blackboard, chalk in hand, writing "Murai" on it.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Murai seiyanum. Aana adhu therinju dhaan seiyanum. Unakku theriyaadha varaikkum murai seiyakoodadhu."
thirukkural: முறைசெய்து காப்பாற்றும் மன்னவன் மக்கட்கு இறையென்று வைக்கப் படும்.
english_translation: He is the Lord of men who does / Sound justice and saves his race.
moral: A king who does justice and protects his people is considered a god among men.

title: The Harsh Critic
theme: Glory of Kingship / Tolerance of Criticism
hook: Dhanush's art teacher criticizes his painting harshly — Thatha shows him why a leader must bear bitter words.
story: Dhanush painted Meenakshi Temple for the competition. Teacher said, "Indha colour combination romba mosam. Perspective ellam wrong. Nee artist aaga mudiyadhu." Dhanush tore his painting. "Thatha, avaru romba mosama sonnaaru. Naan artist aaga mudiyadhu." Thatha picked up the torn pieces. "Kadhula kadicha maadhiri irundha. Aana adhu kathu kadicha maadhiri. Nee sari panna vendiyadhu." They fixed the painting together. Dhanush won third prize. "Thatha, avaru sonnadhu dhaan sari. Atha kettu dhaan naan improve pannen."
tamil_dialogues: Dhanush: 'Thatha, avaru romba kadumaiya sonnaaru!'
Thatha: 'Kadumaiya sonnadha kettu dhaan namma valarom. Atha thanganum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the temple painting, a brush in his hand, touching up a detail.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kadichu sonna kooda, athu sariya irundha, adhu kettu dhaan namma valarom. Athu dhaan unmai."
thirukkural: செவிகைப்பச் சொற்பொறுக்கும் பண்புடை வேந்தன் கவிகைக்கீழ்த் தங்கும் உலகு.
english_translation: Under his shelter thrives the world / Who bears remarks bitter and bold.
moral: The world finds shelter under a ruler who patiently endures bitter words.

title: The Four Gifts
theme: Glory of Kingship / Bounty, Justice, Care, Grace
hook: Hariharan gets four gifts for his birthday — Amma explains the four things a king must have.
story: Four gifts arrived. A cricket bat, a chess set, a plant sapling, and a photo frame. Hariharan grabbed the bat. "Adhu dhaan! Ithu enaku pothum!" Amma stopped him. "Oruvar kku nangu venum. Kodai, irakkiya, murai, kudumbam." Hariharan was confused. Amma explained: "Bat kodai. Chess — murai. Sapling — irakkiya (care). Photo frame — kudumbam. Nee leader aaganum na, nangu venum." Hariharan planted the sapling. "Amma, ippo dhaan puraiyudhu. Intha sapling ku thanni oothura maadhiri dhaan kudumbathai kaapadradhu."
tamil_dialogues: Hariharan: 'Amma, bat dhaan pothum! Motham pothum!'
Amma: 'Oruvar kku nangu venum. Kodai, irakkiya, murai, kudumbam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the garden, watering the newly planted sapling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kodai, irakkiya, murai, kudumbam — intha nangu irundha dhaan periya aatchi. Oruvar dhaan."
thirukkural: கொடையளி செங்கோல் குடியோம்பல் நான்கும் உடையானாம் வேந்தர்க் கொளி.
english_translation: He is the Light of Kings who has / Bounty, justice, care and grace.
moral: A king who has generosity, justice, care for people, and grace is the light of all rulers.

title: The Flawless Pot
hook: Vaishali thinks she learned pottery in one day — the potter shows her what flawless learning really means.
story: Vaishali sat at the wheel. In one hour, she made a crooked pot. "Uncle, naan kathukitten!" Uncle smiled. He took the pot, examined every angle, found a hairline crack. "Indha pot la vitthai irukku. Nee kathadhula crack irukku." He smashed it. "Mudhal crack podhaa. Aprom rendavadhu." Vaishali cried. Uncle gave her clay. "Sariya kathuko. Vitthai illama." She made 50 pots. Each time, Uncle found a flaw. On the 51st, Uncle nodded. "Idhu dhaan. Vitthai illa. Nee kathukitta."
tamil_dialogues: Vaishali: 'Uncle, enakku pottery therinjudhu!'
Uncle: 'Therinjirukku nu nenaikradhu dhaan periya theriyama irukradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the potter's wheel, his hands perfectly shaping a flawless pot.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Katra dhaan adha vitthai illama katra dhaan mudiyum. Aprom dhaan adha vida mudiyum."
thirukkural: கற்க கசடறக் கற்பவை கற்றபின் நிற்க அதற்குத் தக.
english_translation: Lore worth learning, learn flawlessly / Live by that learning thoroughly.
moral: Learn what is worth learning without flaw, then live in accordance with that learning.

title: The Blind Accountant
theme: Learning / Numbers and Letters as Eyes
hook: Mithran meets a blind man who calculates faster than him — and learns the real meaning of vision.
story: Mithran helped at his father's vegetable stall. A blind man came. "Rendru kilo thakkali, moonu kilo vengayam, edhai?" Mithran calculated on paper. Before he finished, the blind man said, "176 rupees." Mithran checked — correct. "Sir, unakku kan theriyadha? Appadi enna calculation?" The man smiled. "Enakku kannu theriyadhu. Aana kanakku therium. Ezhuthu therium. Athu dhaan kannu." Mithran realized: numbers and letters are the real eyes. "Sir, unakku kannu theriyalaya? Neenga dhaan enakku kannu kaatinaaru."
tamil_dialogues: Mithran: 'Sir, unakku kan theriyadhu? Appadi enna kanakku?'
Muthaiyya: 'Ezhuthum, kanakkum dhaan kannu. Adhu irundha, kannu theriyadhadhu onnum illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside Muthaiyya at the vegetable stall, touching tomatoes gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ezhuthum kanakkum dhaan manushanukku kannu. Adhu irundha, yaarum kuruvar illa."
thirukkural: எண்ணென்ப ஏனை எழுத்தென்ப இவ்விரண்டும் கண்ணென்ப வாழும் உயிர்க்கு.
english_translation: Letter, number, art and science / Of living kind both are the eyes.
moral: Numbers and letters are the eyes of all living beings — without them, we are blind.

title: The Two Sores
hook: Janani laughs at a classmate who can't read — the teacher shows her the two sores on her own face.
story: Kavi couldn't read. She pointed at words and guessed. Janani laughed. "Kavi, idhu 'palli' illa 'padi'! Unakku ezhutha kooda theriyadha?" The teacher heard. She brought a mirror. "Janani, un mookkula rendu pun irukku." Janani checked. Nothing. Teacher said, "Kalladhavar mookkula rendu pun irukka. Un munnadi Kavi. Neeyum Kavi maadhiri dhaan irundha — unakku ivalai kaatradha vida pun dhaan." Janani felt ashamed. "Kavi, sorry. Naan unakku ezhutha katradhena?" Kavi nodded. Janani taught her. A year later, Kavi could read the entire board.
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Kavi, idhu kooda theriyadha?'
Teacher: 'Kalladhavan mookkula pun irukku. Neeyum adhe maadhiri dhaan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between Janani and Kavi, holding an open book for both to read.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Katravar kannu irundhavar. Kalladhavan kannu illadhavar. Adhu dhaan vithiyasam."
thirukkural: கண்ணுடையர் என்பவர் கற்றோர் முகத்திரண்டு புண்ணுடையர் கல்லா தவர்.
english_translation: The learned alone have eyes on face / The ignorant two sores of disgrace.
moral: The learned are the only ones with eyes; the unlearned have two sores on their face.

title: The Teacher Who Left
hook: Aravind's favorite teacher is transferred — he learns that true learning lives beyond the classroom.
story: Rani Miss taught Tamil. She made every lesson a story. "Silapathigaram na oru cinema mathiri!" The whole class loved her. One day, she announced transfer. "Naan inga irundhu porena." The class cried. Aravind clutched her saree. "Miss, neenga illama naama epdi padipom?" Rani Miss opened Silapathigaram. "Kannagi padichirkka. Avanga kovama irundha. Aana adha vida namma padichadhu dhaan irukkum. Nee padicha adhu un kuda irukkum." Aravind read the book that night. He felt Miss was still there. "Miss, neenga illa. Aana neenga sonna kathai irukku."
tamil_dialogues: Aravind: 'Miss, neenga pona, naama epdi padipom?'
Rani Miss: 'Unakku naan sonnadhu dhaan irukkum. Atha yaaralum edukka mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, a copy of Silapathigaram in his hand, waving goodbye.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Katravarum katrapavarume santhosama sernthu, aarvama piridhal — adhu dhaan pulavar thozhil."
thirukkural: உவப்பத் தலைக்கூடி உள்ளப் பிரிதல் அனைத்தே புலவர் தொழில்.
english_translation: To meet with joy and part with thought / Of learned men this is the art.
moral: The scholar's art is to meet joyfully and part thoughtfully — knowledge remains.

title: The Rich Man's Son
hook: Karthik's rich friend flaunts a new laptop — Karthik feels poor until Paati reveals the one thing richer than money.
story: Karthik's friend Siddharth had a MacBook, iPhone, Kindle. "Nee en vitu vandhu padikira?" Karthik felt small. "Paati, avan room la ellaam irukku. Namma kaila oru puthagam kooda illa." Paati opened a trunk. Inside: 500 books — worn, dog-eared, loved. "Naan 40 varsham sethu vacha puthagangal. Un periya appa kuda padicha." Karthik picked a tamil novel. The smell of old paper. The joy of turning pages. "Paati, avan laptop life 5 years. Intha puthagam 40 varsham!" Paati nodded. "Padicha arivu — atha yaaralum vanga mudiyadhu. Aasa irundha dhaan kedaikkum."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Paati, avan room la ellaam irukku. Namma kaila omum illa.'
Paati: 'Padicha arivu dhaan periya selvan. Atha kaasu kuduthu vanga mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in Paati's old trunk room, surrounded by 500 books.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku kaasu illa, aana aarvam irundha, padichu dhaan mudiyum. Adhu than periya selvan."
thirukkural: உடையார்முன் இல்லார்போல் ஏக்கற்றுங் கற்றார் கடையரே கல்லா தவர்.
english_translation: Like poor before rich they yearn: / For knowledge: the low never learn.
moral: The learned yearn for knowledge like the poor before the rich — the ignorant remain low.

title: The Deep Well
hook: Pavi tries to draw water from a shallow well and gets mud — Thatha teaches that deeper knowledge gives purer results.
story: The summer well was dry. Pavi lowered the bucket — only mud came up. "Thatha, thanni illa!" Thatha took her to the next field. A deep borewell. Bucket down. Crystal clear water came gushing. "Rendum well dhaan. Aana aazham vithyasam." Pavi didn't understand. Thatha said, "Paadam padikra maadhiri dhaan. Konjam padicha — konjam therium. Aazhama padicha — niraiya therium." Pavi started reading one extra hour every day. Her Tamil grew from halting to fluent. "Thatha, neenga sonna maadhiri — aazhama padicha dhaan thanni suthama irukku!"
tamil_dialogues: Pavi: 'Thatha, well la thanni illa!'
Thatha: 'Aazhama irundha dhaan suthama varum. Padichalum adhe dhaan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the deep borewell, a bucket of crystal clear water beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Katra alavu dhaan arivu. Aazhama katra — aazhama therium. Konjam katra — adhu porum."
thirukkural: தொட்டனைத் தூறும் மணற்கேணி மாந்தர்க்குக் கற்றனைத் தூறும் அறிவு.
english_translation: As deep you dig the sand spring flows / As deep you learn the knowledge grows.
moral: Like a sand well, the deeper you dig, the more water flows — the more you learn, the more knowledge grows.

title: Worlds Without Borders
hook: Rahul asks why the train stops at every station — Amma tells him that learning has no stops.
story: At the railway station, a train roared past. "Amma, indha train Madurai varaikkum nikkada?" Amma said, "Adhu mail train. Nikkadhu." Another train stopped. "Idhu nikkudhu. Ella station laum." Rahul was curious. "Naan epdi Amma? Nikkama?" Amma laughed. "Kalvi dhaan indha express train mathiri. Nee padikka padikka — unakku yeadhum nikkadhu. Ulagam enga ponaalum, padicha arivu un kuda varum." Rahul thought. "Amma, naan innum padipaen. Saavu varaikkum padipaen." Amma hugged him. "Adhu dhaan da mudhal padam."
tamil_dialogues: Rahul: 'Amma, indha train nikkadhu. Aana adhu epdi?'
Amma: 'Athu dhaan kalvi. Adaikkalam illa. Enga ponaalum kuda dhaan varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the express train, a book open in his hand, looking out the window.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kalvi — adhukku yeadhum adaikkalam illa. Neeyum enga ponaalum, adhu un kuda dhaan varum. Saavu varaikkum kattu."
thirukkural: யாதானும் நாடாமால் ஊராமால் என்னொருவன் சாந்துணையுங் கல்லாத வாறு.
english_translation: All lands and towns are learner's own / Why not till death learning go on!
moral: All countries and towns belong to the learner — why stop learning until death?

title: The Seventh Life
hook: Nithya struggles in 7th standard — Paati reveals the secret that learning in this life carries across seven births.
story: Nithya was failing. "Paati, enakku onnum puraiyala. Naamatha ottai thala." Paati was grinding kozhukattai dough. "Nee intha kozhukattai mavu mathiri." Nithya frowned. "Paati, naan kozhukattai illa!" Paati laughed. "Oru kozhukattai panna mavu eduthu, adha azhaga seiya vendiyadhu. Appo adhu nalla varum. Nee padicha arivu — adhu ezhula 7 janmam varai un kuda varum." Nithya's eyes widened. "7 janmama?" Paati nodded. "Ezhula yen? Ama. Nee ippo padicha arivu, adhu un kuda varum." Nithya started studying with new energy. "Paati, naan 7 janmam dhaan padipa!"
tamil_dialogues: Nithya: 'Paati, enakku onnum puraiyala. Naan mudiyadhu.'
Paati: 'Nee padichadhu ezhula ezhula varum. Ippo padichu, aprom saanthirukka vaendaam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in Paati's kitchen, a kozhukattai in one hand, a book in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru maathira padicha arivu — adhu un kuda ezhula ezhula varum. Adhu dhaan periya aasthhi."
thirukkural: ஒருமைக்கண் தான்கற்ற கல்வி ஒருவற்கு எழுமையும் ஏமாப் புடைத்து.
english_translation: The joy of learning in one birth / Exalts man upto his seventh.
moral: The learning acquired in one birth protects and elevates one for seven births.

title: The Dancing Scholar
hook: Rangan sees his dance teacher learning a new language — and realizes the scholar's secret joy.
story: Rangan's nattuvanar spoke perfect Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Sanskrit. One day, Rangan found him learning Hindi with a children's book. "Sir! Unakku Hindi theriyum. Appadi enna padikreenga?" Teacher smiled. "Illa da. Indha okka vaartha theriyadhu." Rangan laughed. "Sir, unakku kooda padikka venama?" Teacher nodded. "Santhosham da. Padikradhula dhaan santhosham. Neeyum padikra unaku pidichirukka." Rangan understood: scholars don't learn for marks, not for job, not for fame. They learn for the joy of learning. "Sir, neenga dhaan periya pulavar."
tamil_dialogues: Rangan: 'Sir, unakku Hindi theriyum. Appadi enna kathukreenga?'
Teacher: 'Theriyadha oru vaartha irukku. Adha kathuka dhaan santhosham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the dance school, a Hindi children's book open in his lap.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Katravar — avaruku padikradhula niraiya sandhosham. Ulaga makkal padicha sandhoshatha paathu, aavala padipar."
thirukkural: தாமின் புறுவது உலகின் புறக்கண்டு காமுறுவர் கற்றறிந் தார்.
english_translation: The learned foster learning more / On seeing the world enjoy their lore.
moral: The learned desire to learn even more when they see the world enjoying knowledge.

title: The Library Fire
hook: Sruthi's beloved village library burns down — she discovers that true knowledge can't be burned.
story: The library was old, dusty, magical. Sruthi spent every evening there. Then one night — fire. Everything burned. 10,000 books. Gone. Sruthi cried. "Amma, ellam pochu. Inga irundha puthagangal, arivu, ellam pochu." Amma held her. "Puthagangal pochu. Aana nee padichadhu pocha?" Sruthi sniffed. "Nee padicha kathai, kavidhai, varalar — adhu enna? Un kaila, un manasula irukku." Sruthi realized: the books were ash. But the stories she had read — they were still inside her. "Amma, naan padicha ellam en kuda irukku!" She sat down and wrote down every story she remembered.
tamil_dialogues: Sruthi: 'Amma, puthagangal ellam erinjiruchu! En arivum erinjirucha?'
Amma: 'Puthagam erinjiruchu. Aana nee padichadhu un manasula irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the burned library, ash on his clothes, but a glowing book in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Puthagam erinjiruchu. Aana athula irundha arivu — adha yaalum erikka mudiyadhu, kadikka mudiyadhu. Adhu dhaan nijamana selvan."
thirukkural: கேடில் விழுச்செல்வம் கல்வி யொருவற்கு மாடல்ல மற்றை யவை.
english_translation: Learning is wealth none could destroy / Nothing else gives genuine joy.
moral: Learning is the supreme wealth that nothing can destroy — all other wealth is inferior.

title: Board Illaama Chess
theme: Lack of Learning / Learning Without Foundation
hook: Arjun memorised all chess moves from YouTube but doesn't know the board has 64 squares.
story: Arjun challenged the school champion. He placed pieces wrong — bishops on wrong colours, queen not on her colour. The champion laughed. "Unakku board teriyadha?" Arjun's face burned. Appa watched from the audience. After the match, Appa sat with him. "Mundhi adhula erangaadha, mavan. Adha purinjurukka vendum."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Appa, naan ellam move um padichu vechirundhen, aana board puriyala!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — adhaaram illaama kathradhu, manala maelu veedu katradhu maadhiri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the chessboard, rearranging the pieces correctly one by one.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arjuna, adhaaram illaama padichadhu ellam veLla pOi. Kaelvi illaama kathradhu — athu vERum kathradhu illa."
thirukkural: அரங்கின்றி வட்டாடி யற்றே நிரம்பிய நூலின்றிக் கோட்டி கொளல்.
english_translation: Like play of chess on squareless board Vain is imperfect loreless word
moral: Learning without foundation is like playing chess without a board.

title: English Pesa Theriyadhu
hook: Priya nods along when teacher reads English — until teacher asks her to read the next page.
story: Priya pretended she understood English. Every day she nodded and smiled. One day teacher called her. "Read aloud, Priya." Priya stared at the squiggles. Her lips moved but no sound came. The class snickered. At home, she cried. Amma held her. "Naanum adhe mathiri dhan irundhen, ma. Ippo kathukalam."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Amma, ellarum sirichutaanga. Enaku English padikka theriyadhu.'
Amma: 'Therinjirkadhadhu dhan periya kuzhappam illa. Sethukalaam adha.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the blackboard, writing Tamil and English side by side.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Theriyadhadhu therinja maadhiri nadikkardhavan — avan moochu vida koodadhu. Theriyanu therinja dhan, padikka aarambikalam."
thirukkural: கல்லாதான் சொற்கா முறுதல் முலையிரண்டும் இல்லாதாள் பெண்காமுற் றற்று.
english_translation: Unlearned man aspiring speech Is breastless lady's love-approach
moral: Pretending knowledge you don't have is empty and shameful.

title: Summa Irundha Nallavan
theme: Lack of Learning / Silence of the Ignorant
hook: Karthik never speaks in class because he doesn't know — but everyone thinks he's wise.
story: Karthik sat quietly every class. Never answered. Never raised hand. The teacher thought he was dull. One day, a visiting scholar asked a tough question. Brightest kids stammered. Karthik stayed silent. The scholar noticed. "Why don't you speak?" Karthik said quietly, "Enaku theriyadhu, athaan solala." The scholar smiled. "Idhu dhan nalla padicha sign, mavan."
tamil_dialogues: Scholar: 'Unakku theriyadha, illa bayama?'
Karthik: 'Theriyadhu. Athaan solradhu illa. Poia sonna adhu modhalla thappu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the scholar, nodding at Karthik with approval.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Theriyadhadhai theriyum nu sonna — adhu dhan aarambam. Summa irundha kuda kuraiya illa, Karthik."
thirukkural: கல்லா தவரும் நனிநல்லர் கற்றார்முன் சொல்லா திருக்கப் பெறின்.
english_translation: Ev'n unread men are good and wise If before the wise, they hold their peace
moral: Even the unlearned are wise if they stay silent before the wise.

title: Theriyadha Genius
theme: Lack of Learning / Unvalued Wit of the Unread
hook: Ramesh builds a brilliant water pump from scrap — but judges dismiss him because he can't explain in English.
story: Ramesh's pump worked perfectly — lifted water from 20 feet. Judges walked past. "Nice. Tell us the principle in English." Ramesh froze. He knew it in Tamil. He knew it in his hands. But English words escaped him. The judges moved on to a boring project with perfect English. Teacher found Ramesh crying behind the tent. "Nee build panni kaatina, avanga notes mattum dhan kaatinaanga. Yaar periya scientist?"
tamil_dialogues: Teacher: 'Ramesh, unakku theriyum, aana solra vaai illa, adhu dhan problem?'
Ramesh: 'Sir, en kaiyala pannuven, aana solla theriyala.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside Ramesh's water pump, letting water flow through his fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Therindhu pannradhu dhan arivu. Adha English la solradhu mattum dhan arivu illa. Ramesh, un kai dhan un veLakkam."
thirukkural: கல்லாதான் ஒட்பம் கழியநன் றாயினும் கொள்ளார் அறிவுடை யார்.
english_translation: The unread's wit though excellent Is not valued by the savant
moral: The world may not recognize your wit if you lack the language of the learned.

title: Periya Vartha, Sima Kelavi
theme: Lack of Learning / Speech Betrays the Untaught
hook: Deepa uses a big word she heard on TV — and the entire auditorium bursts out laughing.
story: Deepa stood on the debate stage. "The government's... uh... extrapolation policy is wrong." She didn't know what "extrapolation" meant. The opposing team giggled. The judge raised an eyebrow. "Can you explain extrapolation?" Deepa's mouth opened and closed like a fish. The hall erupted. She ran off stage. Appa found her behind the school. "Adha dhan solluvanga, pazhagadha vaarthai ellam adicha avanga kaiya kaatiduvanga."
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Appa, naan padicha irundha intha maadhiri aagirukkaadhu.'
Appa: 'Ama da. Theriyadha vaarthai vachu perusaa irukka ninaikradhu — adhu dhan theriyama irukradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the back of the empty auditorium, a book in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Theriyaadha vaarthai pottu pEsi, adha vida periya kelavi illa. Padichu dhan, therinju dhan pEsa vendum."
thirukkural: கல்லா ஒருவன் தகைமை தலைப்பெய்து சொல்லாடச் சோர்வு படும்.
english_translation: A man untaught when speech he vaunts Sadly fails before savants
moral: An untaught person who brags about knowledge will be exposed when they speak.

title: Kala Nilam
hook: Muthu hates school and wants to farm — until Paati shows him a field that yields nothing.
story: Muthu threw his school bag. "Paati, enakku padikkave pidikkala. Naan vayala uzhudhu pannuven!" Paati took him to a dry, cracked field. No water, no paddy. "Paaru da. Idhu dhan padikkaamal irukradhu. Vayal nalla irundha, nel varum. Illana — ithu maadhiri kaLar nilam." Muthu stared at the barren earth. "Idhu dhan naan?" Paati nodded sadly.
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Paati, vayal nalla irukku. Naan uzhuthu pannidalaam?'
Paati: 'Padicha dhan vayaluku nalla vilai. Padikadhadhu — ithu dhan. Kaathukkum satham, kaLar.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the dry field, a single seed in his palm, looking at the cracked earth.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Avan padicha dhan avanuku vilai. Padikkadhavan — paal illadha kaLar nilam maadhiri. Kaathukkum satham dhan migum."
thirukkural: உளரென்னும் மாத்திரையர் அல்லால் பயவாக் களரனையர் கல்லா தவர்.
english_translation: People speak of untaught minds "They just exist like barren lands"
moral: An unlearned person exists but yields nothing — like barren land.

title: Mann Bommai
theme: Lack of Learning / Beauty Without Knowledge
hook: Anitha buys the most beautiful clay doll — but it crumbles the moment she touches it.
story: At the pottery shop, a painted doll caught Anitha's eyes — golden crown, red lips, shining eyes. "Amma, idhu dhan venum!" She carried it carefully. But her little fingers squeezed — the doll crumbled into dust. It was raw clay inside, never baked. Anitha cried. Amma held her. "Paaru ma, inga veliya azhaga irukku, ullae omum illa. Padikkadhavanum apdi dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Amma, adhu enna ulLe kuppa irundhadhu!'
Amma: 'Veliya azhaga irundha dhan pothuma? Ullae gnanam irukka vendum. Illana paavam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the potter's wheel, shaping new clay with wet hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mann bommai veliya azhaga irundha pothum, ullae omum irundhadhu. Padikkadhavanum — irumbu kavacham potta bommai mathiri."
thirukkural: நுண்மாண் நுழைபுலம் இல்லான் எழில்நலம் மண்மாண் புனைபாவை யற்று.
english_translation: Like painted clay-doll is his show Grand subtle lore who fails to know
moral: Beauty without learning is just a painted clay doll — empty inside.

title: Lottari Selvam
theme: Lack of Learning / Wealth in Fools' Hands
hook: Selvam wins 5000 rupees in a lottery — and loses everything in one day.
story: Selvam bought a ticket with his lunch money. He won! 5000 rupees! He bought a phone, new clothes, treats for friends. By evening, the phone was snatched, friends vanished, money was gone. He sat on the bus-stop bench, empty-handed. Appa sat next to him. "Selva, un kaiyila nalla porula vandhadhu, aana adha kaapadra arivu illadha pochu — adha vida periya thappu illa."
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Appa, enakku ellam poiduchu. Kasu kooda illa.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — kasu kedaikkum, aana adha utilise panna theriyadhadha vida periya aluvai illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the empty bench, counting nothing on his fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Selva, padichavan kaiyila kasu iruntha, adhu valarum. Padikkadhavan kaiyila iruntha — adhu vERum pOkum. Kasu mattum porumaa?"
thirukkural: நல்லார்கண் பட்ட வறுமையின் இன்னாதே கல்லார்கண் பட் ட திரு.
english_translation: Wealth in the hand of fools is worse Than a learned man's empty purse
moral: Wealth in a fool's hands brings more suffering than poverty in a wise person's life.

title: Jathi Illa, Arivu Dhan
theme: Lack of Learning / Birth vs Learning
hook: Ganesh's Brahmin friend knows no slokas — but the Dalit boy who cleans the temple floor recites the entire Kural.
story: Ganesh's Thatha took him to the temple. The Brahmin priest's son wore new clothes but stumbled on basic verses. "Ra.. Raama... Raama..." Meanwhile, Kumar, who swept the temple floor, was reciting Thirukkural under his breath. Perfect pronunciation. Every word clear. Ganesh was stunned. "Thatha, Kumarukku romba arivu irukku, aana avan..." Thatha held his hand. "Ama da. Pathu janmam padicha dhan, avan mathiri aaganum. Manushanoda arivu avan kula pathi sonnadhu illa."
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Thatha, Kumarukku ootha vadai illa, aana avanuku arivu romba irukku!'
Thatha: 'Arivu dhan periya kudi, da. Pirappu alla.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans against the temple pillar, listening to Kumar's recitation with closed eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Uyara pirandhadhaala periyavana illa. Padichu therinjadhu dhan periyavan. Manushan avan arivaala dhan aLappaduvan."
thirukkural: மேற்பிறந்தா ராயினும் கல்லாதார் கீழ்ப்பிறந்தும் கற்றார் அனைத்திலர் பாடு.
english_translation: Lower are fools of higher birth Than low-born men of learning's worth
moral: A learned person of low birth outranks an unlearned person of high birth.

title: Kaattu Maadu
theme: Lack of Learning / Beasts vs Learned Men
hook: Vignesh thinks studies are useless — until Appa points out animals don't study either.
story: Vignesh hated homework. "Enakku padikkave pidikkala! Naan pasu mathiri irundha pothum!" He thought it was a joke. Appa took him to the cattle market. Row after row of cows, buffaloes, goats. "Paaru da. Ivanga padikkala. Aana ivangala vida konjam kooda nalla irukka, unakku padikkave vendum." Vignesh looked at the animals chewing cud. He didn't want to be like them. He picked up his bag.
tamil_dialogues: Vignesh: 'Appa, ivanga padikkala aana nalla dhan irukkaanga!'
Appa: 'Ama. Aana ivanga enna aagalaam, aana nee enna aagalaam. Adhu dhan vithiyasam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands among the cattle, a book in one hand, his other on a cow's back.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manushan pavam dhan. Padicha dhan manushan. Illana — ivai anaithum kaadu. Adhu dhan vithiyasam."
thirukkural: விலங்கொடு மக்கள் அனையர் இலங்குநூல் கற்றாரோடு ஏனை யவர்.
english_translation: Like beasts before men, dunces are Before scholars of shining lore
moral: The unlearned are like beasts compared to those who study the shining scriptures.

title: Paati's Treasure Box
hook: Nithya thinks Paati is poor until she hears Paati's stories — richer than any treasure.
story: Nithya felt sad that Paati lived in a small house with old things. "Paati, unakku kasu illa?" Paati laughed and took her to Marina Beach. As the sun set, Paati told stories — about demons and kings, about her childhood, about Thiruvalluvar. Fishermen gathered to listen. A stranger said, "Paati, un kadhai keta dhan, enga kasu irukku." Nithya understood: Paati was the richest person she knew.
tamil_dialogues: Nithya: 'Paati, un kitta kasu illa, aana evlo periya katha irukku!'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan ma — kElvi dhan periya selvam. Kasu pogum, aana adhu pOgaadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the Marina sands, the sunset behind him, listening to Paati's story.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Selvathula irukra periya selvam — kaadha kElvi. Atha vida periya selvam illa. Kaadhu kondu kElu."
thirukkural: செல்வத்துட் செல்வஞ் செவிச்செல்வம் அச்செல்வம் செல்வத்து ளெல்லாந் தலை.
english_translation: Wealth of wealths is listening's wealth It is the best of wealths on earth
moral: The wealth of listening is the greatest of all wealth.

title: Vayiru Poradhu
theme: Listening / Ear Food vs Stomach Food
hook: Kannan stuffs his face at dinner and ignores his father's story — until Amma stops the food.
story: Kannan loved eating. Every evening, Appa tried to tell a story from the newspaper. Kannan's ears were closed; his mouth was full. "Appa, konjam saapdata porum, solreenga!" One day Amma took his plate away. "Kanna, vayiruku mathum saapdu kudukka koodadhu. Kaadhukkum saapdu kudutha dhan." Kannan cried. But Amma held firm. "Uncle sonna madhiri — kaadha saapdradha vida vayira saapdradhu perisaa irukka koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Kannan: 'Amma, en plate kudu! Pasikkudhu!'
Amma: 'Vayirukku mathum kuduva — kaadhukku kudukka maata. Rendukkum samama.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the empty dinner table, a newspaper in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannan, vayiruku saapdu kudukradhu dhan aavasyam. Aana kaadhukku saapdu kudukradhu dhan periya aavasyam. Adhai marakka koodadhu."
thirukkural: செவிக்குண வில்லாத போழ்து சிறிது வயிற்றுக்கும் ஈயப் படும்.
english_translation: Some food for the stomach is brought When the ear gets no food for thought
moral: When the ear gets no wisdom-food, even the stomach's food is less worthy.

title: Kaadhal Kadhai
hook: Devi sees an old man listen to the temple bells for hours — and learns why listening is divine.
story: Every evening, a toothless old man sat at Marudamalai temple. He didn't pray. He didn't chant. He just listened — to bells, to wind, to birds. Devi asked, "Thatha, ivar enna pannuraar?" Thatha smiled. "Avan kElvin dhan thavam pannuraan. Yaar kElviya sodhikkalaa avanga — avanga theivam mathiri." That night, Devi sat beside him. She listened to the evening aarti. Her heart felt light. For the first time, she felt peace.
tamil_dialogues: Devi: 'Thatha, ivar enna pannitu irukkaar? Summa dhan irukkaar!'
Thatha: 'Summa illa da. Avan unarvodu kElkraan. Athai vida periya tavam illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the old man, both listening to the temple bells with closed eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "KElviya kaadha valaikku kudukradhu — adhu dhan dheivam aagradhu. Yaar nerama kElkraano, avanga theivathoda samam."
thirukkural: செவியுணவிற் கேள்வி யுடையார் அவியுணவின் ஆன்றாரோ டொப்பர் நிலத்து.
english_translation: Whose ears get lots of wisdom-food Equal gods on oblations fed
moral: Those who feast their ears on wisdom are equal to the gods.

title: Bus Stand Paadam
hook: Raj never went to school but learns everything by listening at the bus stand.
story: Raj sold water packets at Trichy bus stand. He couldn't read. One day, a teacher waited for a bus and read aloud from a book. Raj listened. Next day, same teacher, same book. Raj memorised the whole page. He asked, "Sir, naanum padikkalaama?" The teacher smiled. "Un kElvi dhan un padikkalam. Kaadhula dhan un padam." Raj became the best listener. He never went to school, but his mind was fuller than most.
tamil_dialogues: Raj: 'Sir, enakku ezutha varadhu, aana kEta porum padichidalaam?'
Teacher: 'Ama da. Kaadhukku saapdu kudutha — adhu dhan periya palli.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a bus stand bench, listening to Raj recite what he heard.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Padikala naalum, kElvi iruntha, adhu dhan kaappu. Theriyadhadhai kElvi kathukalam. Kaadhu dhan un palli."
thirukkural: கற்றில னாயினுங் கேட்க அஃதொருவற்கு ஒற்கத்தின் ஊற்றாந் துணை.
english_translation: Though not learned, hear and heed That serves a staff and stay in need
moral: Even if you haven't studied, listening becomes your staff in times of trouble.

title: Paati's Walking Stick
hook: Meena's parents fight every night — Paati's words become the stick that holds her up.
story: Meena hid under her blanket when Appa and Amma shouted. Broken plates. Crying. She ran to Paati's room. Paati pulled her close and whispered stories of brave girls. "Nee bayapadadha. Intha kastamum kadandhu pOgum." Meena held Paati's words like a walking stick. Every night she repeated them. One morning, the house was quiet. Appa made tea for Amma. Meena smiled. Paati's words had held her steady.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Paati, ellaam seri aaguma?'
Paati: 'Sari aagum ma. Nalla varthai kElu, adha pidichu kolu. Adhu un kOl.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at Paati's doorstep, a wooden staff in hand, watching the sunrise.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "KaLam sErndha pOdhu — nalla vaarthai dhan un kOl. Athai kElu. Athu dhan unai kaapadhradhu."
thirukkural: இழுக்கல் உடையுழி ஊற்றுக்கோல் அற்றே ஒழுக்க முடையார்வாய்ச் சொல்.
english_translation: Virtuous men's wisdom is found A strong staff on slippery ground
moral: The words of virtuous people are like a strong staff when you're on slippery ground.

title: Naanum Padikiren
hook: Bharath was ready to drop out — one sentence from his teacher changed everything.
story: Bharath's father said, "Padikka vendam, velai ku vaa." He packed his bag to leave. His teacher stopped him at the corridor. "Bharath, oe oru periya engineer aaga mudiyum. Un kai nalla irukku. Nee nambu." Just those words. Bharath stayed. He listened to teacher's advice every day. Ten years later, he came back as an engineer to thank her. "Miss, neenga sonna adhu naalu vaarthai — athu dhan ennai kappadhichadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Bharath: 'Miss, enakku padikara velai illa, poiduven.'
Teacher: 'Nee padichu mudichu, onna vida mudiyadhu. Oru vaarthai — naalu vaarthai — unai maathidalam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, watching Bharath walk in as an engineer.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Konja nalla vaarthai — adhu dhan oruvanai uyarthum. Kaadhukku kedachadhu — adhu dhan periya vazhi."
thirukkural: எனைத்தானும் நல்லவை கேட்க அனைத்தானும் ஆன்ற பெருமை தரும்.
english_translation: Lend ear to good words however few That much will highly exalt you
moral: Even a few good words, if heeded, will exalt you greatly.

title: Kathir's Mouth
hook: Kathir speaks without thinking — until Thatha teaches him to listen first.
story: Kathir always blurted things. "Amma, un mooku perisaa irukku!" Everyone winced. He couldn't understand why people got angry. Thatha took him to the market. "Nee pathu vaarthai solradhukku munnadi, pathu vaarthai kElu." A vendor shouted a price. Kathir almost said "Romba aagum!" But he paused. He listened. The real customer bargained calmly. Kathir learned: listen fully, then speak softly. He went home and told Amma, "Un mooku romba azhaga irukku." She smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Kathir: 'Thatha, enakku pesa romba aasai varudhu!'
Thatha: 'Adha adakku da. Muthalla kElu. Appo dhan un varthai sari aagum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by a market stall, a finger to his lips, listening to the crowd.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla kElvi irundha — avan vaai thappa pesaadhu. Kaadhu dhan vaaya adakkum. Nee adha kElu."
thirukkural: பிழைத்துணர்ந்தும் பேதைமை சொல்லா ரிழைத்துணர்ந் தீண்டிய கேள்வி யவர்.
english_translation: Who listen well and learn sharply Not ev'n by slip speak foolishly
moral: Those who listen well will never speak foolishly, even by accident.

title: Sangeetham Theriyadhu
theme: Listening / Ears Not Drilled by Wisdom
hook: Suresh hears the mridangam but can't feel the beat — his ears are closed to wisdom.
story: Suresh's parents forced him into music class. The teacher played a simple thaalam. "Suresh, thappu podu." Suresh's hands clapped randomly. Off beat. Wrong rhythm. The other kids laughed. Teacher stopped. "Suresh, nee kElra, aana kEkkala. Rendum vithiyasam." Suresh didn't understand. Teacher walked to him. "Nee saptam kElra, aana adhula irukra gnanathai kEkkala." Suresh finally closed his eyes. He felt the beat in his chest. His hands moved — perfectly.
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Sir, naan kElren dhan, aana varala!'
Teacher: 'Kaadhu irundha pothum. Gnanathoda kEkkanum. Adhu dhan padam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits cross-legged, a mridangam before him, beating the perfect thaalam.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaadhu iruntha pothum — adhu kEkkadha kaadhu mathiri. Gnanathoda kEkkadhadhu — adhu dhan kElvi illa."
thirukkural: கேட்பினுங் கேளாத் தகையவே கேள்வியால் தோட்கப் படாத செவி.
english_translation: That ear though hearing is dulled Which is not by wisdom drilled
moral: Ears that hear but don't understand are as good as deaf.

title: Azhaga Pesalam
hook: Vidya thinks she's the smartest girl in town — until she listens to a weaver's wisdom.
story: Vidya won every quiz. She walked with her chin high. "Enaku ellaam theriyum!" Thatha took her to a weaving workshop. An old weaver sat at the loom, threading silk. "Unakku ithu theriyuma?" Vidya shook her head. "Indha silk 6000 varusama irukku." She didn't know. He showed her how threads weaved. "Unakku veRai padichadhu theriyum. Vaazhkai theriyala." Vidya's pride melted. She sat quietly, listened to the weaver for two hours.
tamil_dialogues: Vidya: 'Thatha, avaruku evlo periya arivu irukku!'
Thatha: 'KEl amaichaa dhan theriyum. Enakku ellaam theriyum nu nenaikradhu — adhu dhan theriyama irukradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the loom, pulling threads with the old weaver.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla kElvi irundha dhan oruvan thala kuniyum. KElvi irundha — modesty varum. Adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: நுணங்கிய கேள்விய ரல்லார் வணங்கிய வாயின ராதல் அரிது.
english_translation: A modest mouth is hard for those Who care little to counsels wise
moral: Without deep listening, a humble mouth is hard to achieve.

title: Ruchi Theriyadha Naakku
theme: Listening / Taste by Ear vs Mouth
hook: Arun can taste every spice in food but can't taste the beauty in a story — Amma worries.
story: Arun was a foodie. "Amma, ithula konjam pulippu, konjam kaaram, romba nalla irukku!" But when Amma told stories, he yawned. "Kadhai la enna ruchi?" One day at Mahamaham, a storyteller narrated the Mahabharata. The crowd laughed, cried. Arun sat bored. "Amma, enakku onnum puriyala." Amma sighed. "Arun, vaai ku ruchi theriyum, kaadhukku theriyala. Idhu dhan periya problem." Arun tried. He closed his mouth. He opened his ears. Slowly, words began to taste like honey.
tamil_dialogues: Arun: 'Amma, kadhai la enaku ruchi illa. Saapdradhu saapduven!'
Amma: 'Kaadhukku ruchi illadhavan — avan vaayoda mathum saapdra avan. Athu dhan sAvadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the Mahamaham tank, cupping water to his ear.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arun, kaadhukku ruchi irundha dhan manushan. Illana, vaai dhan perisu. Kaadhu dhan gnanathin vaasal."
thirukkural: செவியிற் சுவையுணரா வாயுணர்வின் மாக்கள் அவியினும் வாழினும் என்?
english_translation: Who taste by mouth and not by ear What if they fare ill or well here?
moral: Those who taste only with the mouth and not with the ear — what difference whether they live or die?

title: Friends' Fort
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Wisdom as Weapon
hook: Bully gang surrounds Kathir — he uses one wise sentence and wins without a punch.
story: Four big boys cornered Kathir. "Pocket money kudu!" Kathir's heart pounded. Fight or flight? Then he remembered Amma's words. "Avanga kovathula irundha — nalla vaarthai kudukka dhan appo." He looked at the leader calmly. "Unakku enna venum? Kasu illa, aana unakku oru nalla vaarthai kodukka mudiyum. Nee en friend aaga?" The leader stopped. Nobody had ever spoken to him like that. He lowered his fist. "Nee... vera maadhiri dhan irukka."
tamil_dialogues: Kathir: 'Enakku un munnadi adi podradhukku bayam illa. Aana adhula enna aarvam? Nee en nanban aaga?'
Bully: 'Nee... romba clever dhan da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the boys, his arms open, facing the bully.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arivu dhan aayudham. Kathir, nee kaiya kaatala, aana varthaiya kaatina. Adhu dhan periya fort."
thirukkural: அறிவற்றங் காக்குங் கருவி செறுவார்க்கும் உள்ளழிக்க லாகா அரண்.
english_translation: Wisdom's weapon wards off all woes It is a fort defying foes
moral: Wisdom is a weapon that wards off all troubles — an impenetrable fort.

title: Market Loot
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Wisdom Guides Senses
hook: A vendor tries to cheat Pooja with a fake gold chain — her wisdom saves her.
story: The market was bright and loud. A vendor called Pooja. "Vaa ma, nalla chain — 100 ruva dhan!" Pooja's heart jumped. But she remembered Appa's rule: "Aasai vandha, kaadhu ketta dhan kai kuduka koodadhu." She examined the chain. Light weight. Faint green. "Idhu gold illa, copper." The vendor's face fell. Pooja walked away with a smile. Appa, watching from behind, almost burst with pride. "Unakku therinja boomi perisaa irukku, ma."
tamil_dialogues: Vendor: 'Vaa ma! 100 ruva gold chain!'
Pooja: 'Ithu gold illa, copper. Nalla kai kettam pannanum, aprom vaanganum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the jewelry stall, holding a real gold coin up to the light.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pooja, aasai vandhadhum kai kudukka koodadhu. Arivu irundha, kai adangum. Adhu dhan unmaiyaana porul."
thirukkural: சென்ற இடத்தால் செலவிடா தீதொரீஇ நன்றின்பால் உய்ப்ப தறிவு.
english_translation: Wisdom checks the straying senses Expels evils, impels goodness
moral: Wisdom controls the wandering senses, rejects evil, and guides toward good.

title: Thotti Kada Arivu
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Truth from Everyone
hook: Hari thinks only teachers know things — Thatha shows him a street sweeper who knows botany.
story: Hari laughed when Thatha stopped to talk to the park sweeper. "Thatha, avar enna pEsuraar?" But the old sweeper pointed to a tree. "Idhu iluppai. Indha marathula irundhu dhan soap pannuvaanga." Hari was shocked. The sweeper named every plant. Which leaf cures fever. Which bark makes rope. "Thatha, avaruku evlo theriyum!" Thatha smiled. "Arivu yaar kitta venum — adha yaar kitta keta dhan. Kaadhu irundha pothum."
tamil_dialogues: Hari: 'Thatha, thotti karanuku evlo arivu irukku!'
Thatha: 'Arivu yaar kitta venum, adha kEl da. Yaar kitta irundhu vandhaalum — adhu dhan arivu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sweeps leaves beside the sweeper, both smiling at Hari.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaar kitta irundhu sonnaalum, adha unmai nu therinja — adha sEridhu kOla vendum. Athu dhan arivu."
thirukkural: எப்பொருள் யார்யார்வாய்க் கேட்பினும் அப்பொருள் மெய்ப்பொருள் காண்ப தறிவு.
english_translation: To grasp the Truth from everywhere From everyone is wisdom fair
moral: True wisdom grasps the truth from anyone, anywhere.

title: Nalla Pesu, Nalla KElu
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Clear Speech, Subtle Understanding
hook: Anu's teacher speaks in riddles that no one understands — Anu discovers the key.
story: Teacher spoke complex English. "Quantum entanglement epistemological paradigm." The class stared blank. Anu raised her hand. "Sir, konja simple-a solla mudiyuma? Jodhi electrons maadhiri — onna vitta, innadhu feel pannum?" The teacher paused. "Yes! Exactly!" From that day, Anu became the class translator. She took complex ideas and made them simple. Teacher realized: true wisdom is not in big words but in clear understanding.
tamil_dialogues: Classmate: 'Anu, sir enna solra, onnum puriyala!'
Anu: 'Avanga perusa solraanga. Naan simple-a solren. Kaetu kOlu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the blackboard, writing a simple Tamil sentence.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periya vaarthai sollradhu dhan arivu illa. Sariya puriyadhadha, puriyara mathiri sollradhu dhan arivu."
thirukkural: எண்பொருள வாகச் செலச்சொல்லித் தான்பிறர்வாய் நுண்பொருள் காண்ப தறிவு.
english_translation: Speaking out thoughts in clear trends Wisdom subtle sense comprehends
moral: True wisdom speaks clearly and grasps the subtle truths from others.

title: Madhiri Varadhu
hook: Kavi's best friend scores higher — instead of jealousy, she smiles and hugs her.
story: Results came. Kavi got 472 out of 500. Her best friend Divya got 489. Other friends expected Kavi to be sad. Instead, Kavi hugged Divya. "Nee nalla dhan eduthurukka! Naanum next time try pannuven!" The class was confused. Kavi's Amma had taught her: "Malaika irundhadhum, santhosama iru. Mathavanga nalla irundha, adhuku santhosama iru." Kavi's mind was like still water — neither high nor low.
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Kavi, unakku kovama illa? Divya unna vida jaasthi eduthuta?'
Kavi: 'Venaam. Nanbana vida jaasthi endru ninaikka koodadhu. Avanga perusa sandhosam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the exam hall entrance, a flower in one hand, equal petals on both sides.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Malaika vandhadhu, kovama? Illa, santhosama? Arivu irundha, rendum onna dhan. Athisayama malaraadhu, kumbaadhu."
thirukkural: உலகம் தழீஇய தொட்பம் மலர்தலும் கூம்பலும் இல்ல தறிவு.
english_translation: The wise-world the wise befriend They bloom nor gloom, equal in mind
moral: True wisdom neither blooms in praise nor wilts in blame.

title: Nade Nadandhu
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Moving with the World
hook: Renu wants to quit Bharatanatyam after her guru changes the routine — Appa teaches her to flow.
story: Renu had practised the same dance for months. Then her guru changed the entire sequence. "Ithu puthu style." Renu wanted to walk out. "Appa, ivanga epdi maatha mudiyum? Naan dhaan yaerkanave padichuten!" Appa sat her down. "Renu, nadhi maadhiri iru. Maram veesuna — vaLai. Kal veesuna — suttu pO. Aana nadhi pOna nadakkudhu. Unmayaana arivu — ulagathoda nadakradhu dhan." Renu performed the new sequence. She didn't just dance — she flowed.
tamil_dialogues: Renu: 'Appa, naan pAthiya mathi pOttiduchu! Adhu dhaan padam!'
Appa: 'Ulagam maari kondae irukkum. Athoda nadandha dhan — arivu. Illana thadai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the stage, flowing his hand like a river.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Renu, nadhi maadhiri — edhirthu pOgadhadhu. Ulagam maari kondae irundhaalum, athoda nadandha dhan — adhu dhan arivu."
thirukkural: எவ்வ துறைவது உலகம் உலகத்தோடு அவ்வ துறைவ தறிவு.
english_translation: As moves the world so move the wise In tune with changing times and ways
moral: True wisdom moves with the world, adapting to change like a river.

title: Cycle Race Vision
hook: Mani sees the pothole before the race — everyone else crashes, he wins.
story: Race day. Everyone checked their cycle chains. Mani walked the track. He noticed a pothole near the final turn. "Thatha, adha paaru." Thatha nodded. "Adhu dhan un victory." The race started. Boys pedalled furiously. At the pothole, three boys crashed. Mani slowed, swerved, overtook them smoothly. He won. His friends asked, "Epdi da vechura?" Mani said, "Thatha sonnaar — 'munnadi paathu, pinnadi pO'. Mani looked forward, not down."
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Mani, unakku epdi theriyum?'
Mani: 'Thatha sonnaar — "munnadi paathu, pinnadi pO". Munnadi irukra kuzhi therinja, adha thavirkalam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the finish line, pointing at the pothole with a knowing smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mani, arivu irundhavan munnadi paarpavan. VarapOradhai mundhadhukka kaapaan. Athu dhan gnanam."
thirukkural: அறிவுடையார் ஆவ தறிவார் அறிவிலார் அஃதறி கல்லா தவர்.
english_translation: The wise foresee what is to come The unwise lack in that wisdom
moral: The wise foresee what is coming; the unwise do not.

title: Nalladha Bayam
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Fear the Right Things
hook: Bala is scared of the dark but not scared of swimming in deep river — Appa swaps his fears.
story: Bala cried if lights went out. "Bayama irukku!" But he jumped into the Thamirabarani without checking the current. Appa pulled him out. "Bala, irulukku bayapada, aana neeru ezuthi pOkradhukku bayapadala?" Bala was confused. Appa said, "Unakku bayapada vendiyadhu — irul illa. Athai vida periya bayam — adhu theriyama pOgum neeru. Arivu irundha, yaedha bayapada vendum nu theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Appa, irul la bayama irukku!'
Appa: 'Irul la enna irukku? Onnum illa. Aana neeru la enna irukku — aabathu. Adhu dhan bayapada vendiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands knee-deep in the river, a lantern in one hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bayapada vendiyadhai bayapadala, bayappada vendadhai bayapada vendum. Adhu dhan arivu. Arivu irundha — sariya bayapadalam."
thirukkural: அஞ்சுவ தஞ்சாமை பேதைமை அஞ்சுவது அஞ்சல் அறிவார் தொழில்.
english_translation: Fear the frightful and act wisely Not to fear the frightful's folly
moral: Not fearing what should be feared is folly; fearing what should be feared is wisdom.

title: Annai Veedu
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Guarding Against Evil
hook: Divya is home alone when a stranger knocks — her wisdom saves her from danger.
story: Amma went to the market. "Divya, kaala pathu." A knock came. "Amma office la irundhu vandhuten. Kadha vaai." Divya's heart raced. She remembered Amma's rule: "Arambama anumana iru." She asked, "Office address sollunga?" Pause. "Adhu... adhu..." Divya bolted the chain. "Ungaluku inga velai illa. Naan police ku phone pannuren." The man fled. Amma returned, Divya told her. Amma held her tight. "Un arivu dhan unnai kaapadhichadhu, ma."
tamil_dialogues: Stranger: 'Amma office la irundhu vandhen. Kadha vaai.'
Divya: 'Amma office address sollunga. Sollala na, nAn phone pannidalam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands behind the bolted door, one hand on Divya's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arambama anumana iru. VarapOra aabathai, arivu irundha mundhadhukka kaapadhikalam. Adhu dhan gnanam."
thirukkural: எதிரதாக் காக்கும் அறிவினார்க் கில்லை அதிர வருவதோர் நோய்.
english_translation: No frightful evil shocks the wise Who guard themselves against surprise
moral: The wise guard against evil before it arrives — no shock can shake them.

title: Selvi's Wealth
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Wisdom is All Wealth
hook: Selvi's rich classmate flaunts a new phone — Selvi feels poor until Amma shows her real wealth.
story: Selvi's classmate came with a new iPhone. "Selvi, paaru! 80,000 ruva!" Selvi looked at her worn chappals. At home, she cried. "Amma, namakku omum illa." Amma sat on the floor with her. "Selvi, unakku enna theriyum? Oru katha sollu. Naalu — aaru katha sollu." Selvi recited. From memory. Perfect Tamil. Amma smiled. "Adhu dhan porul, ma. Avanga kaiyila phone irukku. Un kaiyila ezhuthu illa. Eppovum un kooda irukku." Selvi understood: her wisdom weighed more than any phone.
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'Amma, avanga kitta 80,000 ruva phone irukku.'
Amma: 'Selvi, un kitta evlo katha irukku? Adhu dhan 80,000 ku mela. Arivu dhan periya porul.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in Selvi's small home, a single lamp lighting the room warmly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Selvi, arivu irundhavan — avan dhan ellam udaiyavan. Illadhavan, edhai udaiyalum — avan omum illadhavan. Arivu dhan unmai porul."
thirukkural: அறிவுடையார் எல்லா முடையார் அறிவிலார் என்னுடைய ரேனும் இலர்.
english_translation: Who have wisdom they are all full Whatev'r they own, misfits are nil
moral: The wise possess everything; the unwise, despite all possessions, have nothing.

title: The Little Fort
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Wisdom as a Weapon
hook: Anitha's friend says she's weak because she reads books instead of fighting.
story: At the fishing village, Kumar teased Anitha for reading under the palm tree. "Pusthagam padichu enna da laabam?" That evening, a storm warning came. While everyone panicked, Anitha remembered her science book and told them to drag the boats higher. Kumar stared as the waves crashed below. "Neeyum sonnadhu sari dhan, Anitha."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Pusthagam padichadhu dhan — enakku theriyum, kaatru evlo veghama varum nu.'
Kumar: 'Nee sonnadhu sari dhan. Naan dhan theriyama sirichen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the shore, arms crossed, watching the storm pass safely.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arivu dhan aayudham. Athu illadhavanuku, thannam periya kotta kooda sari illa. Neeyum Kумарkum rendu perukum inga therinjudhu."
thirukkural: அறிவற்றங் காக்குங் கருவி செறுவார்க்கும் உள்ளழிக்க லாகா அரண்.
english_translation: Wisdom's weapon wards off all woes It is a fort defying foes
moral: Wisdom is the greatest weapon — no enemy can destroy it.

title: The Auto Driver's Daughter
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Wisdom Guides Actions
hook: Deepika's Amma asks why she took a different route home — and learns the value of thinking.
story: Every day, Deepika walked home through the crowded bus stand. One day she went the long way around. Amma scolded her for being late. "Enna da late?" Deepika smiled. "Amma, inniki sandai naal, adha nera road la kooda katta pona, bus ellam mudiya mudichidhum. Naan nenaichen, namma veetu pakka poi sethukalam." Amma hugged her. "Unakku baalam dhan irukku da."
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Enna late, school mudinchu odane vaa nu sollilaya?'
Deepika: 'Amma, inniki sandai. Traffic la sikkuvom. Naan nenaichen, vera road la ponal sari nu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a bus-stop bench, smiling as Deepika passes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sariya yosippadhu dhan arivu. Adhu illadhavanuku, road kooda kudutha, thappu road la dhan povaanga."
thirukkural: சென்ற இடத்தால் செலவிடா தீதொரீஇ நன்றின்பால் உய்ப்ப தறிவு.
english_translation: Wisdom checks the straying senses Expels evils, impels goodness
moral: True wisdom guides you away from harm and toward goodness.

title: The Scrap Seller's Secret
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Learning from Everyone
hook: Vignesh thinks the old scrap seller is dirty and dumb — until he fixes his broken fan motor.
story: Thatha sent Vignesh to sell old newspapers. The scrap seller, Muthaiya, was bent and grey, fingers stained black. Vignesh whispered, "Ivaruku enna theriyum?" Muthaiya saw his broken fan. "Tharalaam, seri panniduven." He opened the motor, twisted two wires, and the fan whirred to life. Vignesh's jaw dropped. "Thatha, ivar romba clever-a irukkaaru."
tamil_dialogues: Vignesh: 'Thatha, Muthaiya uncle ku motor theriyuma?'
Thatha: 'Arivu ellarukkitayum irukku da. Yaaraiyum light-a eduka koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a pile of scrap, soldering a broken radio.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaar kitta irundhu venumnalum arivu kathukalam. Pusthagala mattum illa — badhil, vazhi, anubavam ellaam."
thirukkural: எப்பொருள் யார்யார்வாய்க் கேட்பினும் அப்பொருள் மெய்ப்பொருள் காண்ப தறிவு.
english_translation: To grasp the Truth from everywhere From everyone is wisdom fair
moral: Wisdom can come from anyone — learn from everyone.

title: The Complicated Drawing
hook: Pavithra's drawing teacher gives confusing instructions — Akka teaches her to simplify.
story: The teacher said, "Geometric perspective using orthogonal projection." Pavithra cried. She didn't understand a word. Akka came to pick her up. "Enna achu?" Pavithra showed the scribble. Akka laughed. "Adha sonna maadhiri — oru kubus varaya solraaru. Appuram easy dhan." She drew a simple cube in 3D. "Nee unakku theriyadhadha, easy-aa ketu kathuko."
tamil_dialogues: Pavithra: 'Akka, sir enna solraaru nu enakku puriyala!'
Akka: 'Kadai la kathukra vishayatha, simple-aa yosichu paaru. Puriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the blackboard, drawing a perfect circle with chalk.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee sollradhu mattum pothum, adhu mattum puriyanum. Simple-aa sollra dhan arivu. Periya vaartha ellam venam."
thirukkural: எண்பொருள வாகச் செலச்சொல்லித் தான்பிறர்வாய் நுண்பொருள் காண்ப தறிவு.
english_translation: Speaking out thoughts in clear trends Wisdom subtle sense comprehends
moral: True wisdom is speaking simply and understanding deeply.

title: The Ice Cream Girl
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Steadiness of Mind
hook: Divya drops her ice cream and expects to cry — but Amma shows her how the wise stay calm.
story: Divya's chocolate cone slipped and splattered on the hot road. She opened her mouth to wail. Amma knelt. "Paaru, ice cream karaichudhu. Adha namba nirutha mudiyadhu. Aana innoru vaangi kuduppen." Divya sniffled. Amma said, "Santhosham vandha sandhosham, kastam vandha kadupu — adhu dhan. Arivu irukravanuku rendum samam." Divya wiped her tears.
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Amma, adhu poiduchu! Enoda ice cream!'
Amma: 'Poitu dhan irukkum da. Aana adhuku ala koodadhu. Va innonu vaanguvom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the ice cream stall, holding two cones — one melting, one whole.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pona poradha pona poradhu. Manasu irundha pothum, atha maathikalam. Arivu irukravanuku sandhosamum kastamum onnu dhan."
thirukkural: உலகம் தழீஇய தொட்பம் மலர்தலும் கூம்பலும் இல்ல தறிவு.
english_translation: The wise-world the wise befriend They bloom nor gloom, equal in mind
moral: The wise are steady — they neither bloom with pride nor wilt with sorrow.

title: The School Timetable
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Adapting to the World
hook: Sathish memorizes his school timetable but can't figure out the bus schedule — Appa teaches him to adapt.
story: Sathish knew every class period by heart. But at the new bus stand, he froze. The buses didn't come on time. "Appa, bus timetable sari illa! Enna pannradhu?" Appa laughed. "Ulagam timetable sari illadha dhan irukkum. Athu pola nadandhukka kathukko. Adhu dhan arivu." Next day, Sathish watched the bus flow. At 8:15, the 8:10 bus came. He adapted.
tamil_dialogues: Sathish: 'Appa, sonna time ku bus varadha!'
Appa: 'Ulagam timetable sari illa da. Nee dhan adjust pannanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stand, boarding a bus that arrived late.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulagam epdi irundhalum, atha epdi face pannradhu nu therindhukradhu dhan arivu. Nee busuku adjust pannra maadhiri, ulagathukku adjust pannanum."
thirukkural: எவ்வ துறைவது உலகம் உலகத்தோடு அவ்வ துறைவ தறிவு.
english_translation: As moves the world so move the wise In tune with changing times and ways
moral: Wisdom is adapting to the world as it is, not as you wish it to be.

title: The Rain That Came
hook: Meena sees dark clouds and says nothing — Paati notices she'd already brought the clothes in.
story: Meena sat on the verandah. Dark clouds rolled in. Amma was at work. The clothes were drying. Meena got up and silently dragged the clothesline in. The rain exploded. Paati came out, saw the dry clothes. "Neeya edukkitta?" Meena nodded. "Paati, munnadi karu megham patha, adhu mazhai nu enakku theriyum." Paati kissed her head.
tamil_dialogues: Paati: 'Edukitta da thuni ellaam? Vera yaarum illa. Neeya?'
Meena: 'Ama paati, mazhai varadhu munnadiye pathutten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the verandah, dry, watching the rain pour.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Munnadi paarthu nadandhukradhu dhan arivu. Adha sonna dhan, aatchi kooda nilaikkum. Appuram panradhu kastam."
thirukkural: அறிவுடையார் ஆவ தறிவார் அறிவிலார் அஃதறி கல்லா தவர்.
english_translation: The wise foresee what is to come The unwise lack in that wisdom
moral: The wise see what's coming and prepare — the unwise only react.

title: The Dark Room
theme: Possession of Knowledge / True Fear vs False Fear
hook: Vikram screams at a shadow in the dark — Anna shows him it's just his school bag.
story: Power cut. Vikram screamed. "Anna! Andha room la oru saththam!" Anna held his hand. "Vaa, pakka po." Vikram hid behind. Anna flicked his lighter. The "shadow monster" was Vikram's own school bag hanging on the hook. "Paaru da. Bayapadradhuku dhan unakku arivu venum. Enna pathi bayapadradhu, enna venam nu therinjikanam."
tamil_dialogues: Vikram: 'Anna, room la edho irukku! Bayama irukku!'
Anna: 'Bayam dhan irukku. Aana adhu school bag dhan da. Un school bag kuda unakku theriyala.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the dark room, holding the lighter, calm as stone.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bayapadra dhan arivu. Aana enna pathi bayapadradhu nu therinjudha venum. Andha bag ku bayapadalaam, aana andha sathathukku?"
thirukkural: அஞ்சுவ தஞ்சாமை பேதைமை அஞ்சுவது அஞ்சல் அறிவார் தொழில்.
english_translation: Fear the frightful and act wisely Not to fear the frightful's folly
moral: Fear the right thing — not everything. That is wisdom.

title: The Fort Gate
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Guarding Against Surprises
hook: Mano's Appa tells him to look at every possible hiding spot before climbing the fort steps.
story: Before climbing Rockfort's 437 steps, Appa stopped. "Mano, iva irundha oru baagam therinjudha. Edhir pakkam, pina pakkam, maele." Mano was bored. "Appa, time waste." But when they reached the top, a monkey snatched a woman's bag. Mano had already seen the monkey at step 200. "Appa, andha kuranungala naan pathutten. Atha dhan neenga sonneenga." Appa smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Mano: 'Appa, enna patha kooda kattai?'
Appa: 'Nee pathadhu dhan, adhu arivu. Adhu irukravanuku edhuvum bayam illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the top of Rockfort, the monkey sitting quietly beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Munnadiye paathu kaapadradhu dhan arivu. Atha illadhavanuku edhuvum nikkadhu. Appuram vandhu than adikkum."
thirukkural: எதிரதாக் காக்கும் அறிவினார்க் கில்லை அதிர வருவதோர் நோய்.
english_translation: No frightful evil shocks the wise Who guard themselves against surprise
moral: The wise guard against trouble before it comes — no shock can shake them.

title: The Empty Pencil Box
theme: Possession of Knowledge / Wisdom is True Wealth
hook: Rajesh has the fanciest pencil box but can't solve a single sum — Sindhu has nothing but passes every test.
story: Rajesh's pencil box had three layers, a calculator, even a sharpener with a container. "Naan dhan rich." Sindhu had a half-chewed pencil and a torn eraser. But when the teacher wrote sums, Sindhu finished in five minutes. Rajesh stared blank. "Sindhu, un kitta oru pencil dhan irukku." Sindhu smiled. "Pencil mathiram pothum da. Arivu thanmele iruntha, edhu vena."
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Un kitta evlo saman irukku? Onnum illa!'
Sindhu: 'Pencil mathiram pothum da. Arivu ullae irukka venum, veliya illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the back of the class, an old slate in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arivu irukravanuku uyarvu. Athu illadhavanuku, thanga box kooda onnum pannadhu. Arivu dhan periya selvam."
thirukkural: அறிவுடையார் எல்லா முடையார் அறிவிலார் என்னுடைய ரேனும் இலர்.
english_translation: Who have wisdom they are all full Whatev'r they own, misfits are nil
moral: The wise have everything — without wisdom, nothing matters.

title: The Broken Trophy
theme: Correcting Faults / Removing Pride, Anger, Pettiness
hook: Karthik's little brother breaks his cricket trophy — and Karthik's reaction shocks the family.
story: Karthik practiced for a year to win that trophy. His little brother Mani swung a bat and smashed it. Karthik's face turned red. He raised his hand. Amma stepped in. "Karthik! Adhu vena pannadha!" Karthik's hand shook. He remembered his grandpa's words. He breathed. "Mani, poi book eduthu vaa. Namma vechu veppom." Amma hugged him. "Un chinam enna achu?"
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Karthik, kovama irukkiya?'
Karthik: 'Illa amma. Adhu pochu. Adhuku kovapadradhuku Mani thappu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the broken trophy, pieces glowing in light.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kovamum, perumaiyum, serumaiyum — adhu dhan periya kurai. Adha vittavara than peruval."
thirukkural: செருக்குஞ் சினமும் சிறுமையும் இல்லார் பெருக்கம் பெருமித நீர்த்து.
english_translation: Plenty is their prosperity Who're free from wrath pride lust petty
moral: True greatness belongs to those free from pride, anger, and pettiness.

title: The Prize Money
theme: Correcting Faults / Avarice and False Pride
hook: Priya wins essay prize money but hides it to buy herself a dress — Appa finds out.
story: Priya won a school prize of 500 rupees. She hid the envelope. Appa saw her secret smile. "Enna vishesham?" "Onnum illa." But that evening, Amma was crying — they needed money for Appa's medicine. Priya held the envelope. Her new dress or Appa's health? She handed the money. "Appa, intha eduthuko." Appa's eyes welled.
tamil_dialogues: Appa: 'Enna da kaiyila?'
Priya: 'School prize Appa. Naan dress vaangalaam nu irundhen. Aana unakku dhan venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the loom, threads of gold and silk around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kuduthu kodhai illadhadhu dhan periya kurai. Atha vittu kudutha dhan manasu perusa aagum."
thirukkural: இவறலும் மாண்பிறந்த மானமும் மாணா உவகையும் ஏதம் இறைக்கு.
english_translation: Mean pride, low pleasure, avarice These add blemishes to a prince
moral: Greed and false pride are stains on the soul — give freely.

title: The Stolen Pencil
hook: Selvi steals a pretty pencil from her classmate — then can't sleep all night.
story: Selvi saw a pink pencil with a butterfly topper on Anu's desk. She slipped it into her bag. At home, she couldn't eat. Couldn't sleep. Amma noticed. "Selvi, enna achu?" Selvi burst out crying. She showed the pencil. Amma held her. "Nee adha thirudiya? Adhu un kitta irukka vendiya dhan illa." Next day, Selvi returned it. Anu smiled. "Paravala, unakku venumna kaetu kuduppenla."
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'Amma, naan oru pencil thiruditten!'
Amma: 'Thirudradhu thappu. Aana nee sollradhu dhan nalladhu. Andha pencil kuda, adhu sari illa nu unakku theriyudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the school gate, holding a pink pencil.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thirudradhu thappu dhan. Aana adhu thappu nu unakku theriyudhu. Adha nikka vechodhu — adhu dhan periya vishesham."
thirukkural: தினைத்துணையாங் குற்றம் வரினும் பனைத்துணையாக் கொள்வர் பழிநாணு வார்.
english_translation: Though millet-small their faults might seem Men fearing disgrace, Palm-tall deem
moral: Those who fear shame see even a small fault as huge.

title: The Mirror Trick
hook: Bala always blames others — Ravi makes him look in the mirror.
story: Bala fought in cricket. "Sixer pota, pitch dhan sari illa!" Batting failure? "Light dhan problem!" Ravi got tired. He brought a small mirror. "Bala, paaruda." Bala looked at himself. "Enna?" "Neeyum dhan error irukka. Pitch, light, weather — ellam sari dhan. Nee dhan sari illa." Bala kept the mirror. That afternoon, he practiced his shots. He stopped complaining.
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Ravi, mirror vechi enna pannra?'
Ravi: 'Un mistake unakku theriyanum. Mirror la paaru — nee dhan mistake.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar holds the mirror, showing Bala his own reflection.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kurramm, atha sari pannanum. Adha paathu kaapadradhu dhan arivu. Illadhadhu, adhu dhan periya pakai."
thirukkural: குற்றமே காக்க பொருளாகக் குற்றமே அற்றந் த்ரூஉம் பகை.
english_translation: Watch like treasure freedom from fault Our fatal foe is that default
moral: Guard against your faults like your greatest treasure — a fault is your real enemy.

title: The Firecracker Lesson
theme: Correcting Faults / Faults Destroy Like Fire
hook: Ganesh secretly plays with matches near firecrackers — Periyappa catches him just in time.
story: Sivakasi sparkled with firecracker shops. Ganesh found a stray matchbox. "Oru suttaa pathudhalam." He struck a match near a pile of wrapped crackers. Periyappa's hand grabbed his wrist like iron. "Dei!" He dragged him away. "Unakku theriyuma? Oru suttaa, neeyum, veetum, street-um pogaum!" Ganesh trembled. Periyappa's voice softened. "Adhu dhan da — fault ah nee control pannala, adhu unna control pannum."
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Periyappa, naan oru suttaa mathiram dhan!'
Periyappa: 'Oru suttaa pathaiya? Adhula un vazhkai kaiyila irukku da. Fault-a kaapadra dhan nalla vazhkai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the factory, holding an unlit match, fire danger behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kurram edukkum, kadichaa. Adha munnadiye kaapadra dhan nalla vazhkai. Adhu pona, firecracker maadhiri."
thirukkural: வருமுன்னர்க் காவாதான் வாழ்க்கை எரிமுன்னர் வைத்தூறு போலக் கெடும்.
english_translation: Who fails to guard himself from flaw Loses his life like flame-lit straw
moral: A fault unchecked destroys like fire on dry straw.

title: The Complain King
hook: Senthil complains about everyone's faults — Akka writes a list of his own.
story: Senthil had a habit. "Akka, nee late, nee slow, nee romba pesra." Every day a new complaint. Akka silently wrote a list. Sunday morning, she handed him a paper. "Senthiloda kurrangal: kovam, alambal, thevai illadha pesradhu, mathavangala dosham podradhu." Senthil read. His face went red. "Naan ipdi dhana?" Akka nodded. "Mudhal un kurrangala sari pannu. Appuram enna sollu."
tamil_dialogues: Senthil: 'Akka, nee enna list podra?'
Akka: 'Un kurrangala. Munnadi unna sari pannu. Appuram mathavangala namba dosham podalam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between them, holding the list, nodding at Senthil.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mudhal un kurrangala sari pannu. Appuram mathavanga kurrangala pathi pesalam. Adhu dhan neri."
thirukkural: தன்குற்றம் நீக்கிப் பிறர்குற்றங் காண்கிற்பின் என்குற்ற மாகும் இறைக்கு?
english_translation: What fault can be the king's who cures First his faults, then scans others
moral: Fix your own faults first — then you have the right to speak of others'.

title: The Empty Piggy Bank
hook: Nithya saves every rupee but refuses to share — Thatha breaks his own bank to teach her.
story: Nithya's piggy bank was full. Heavy. She shook it proudly. But when her friend needed 10 rupees for a sketch pen, Nithya said no. "Enakku venum." Thatha watched. He brought his own old bank. Broke it. All the coins fell. "Eduthuko, un friend ku koduthu vai." Nithya stared. "Thatha, unoda savings?" "Selvam dhan irukku. Aana kodhai illana, adhu enna payan?"
tamil_dialogues: Nithya: 'Thatha, neenga enna pannreenga?'
Thatha: 'Kodukradhula dhan selvam irukku. Vaangi vechadhu mattum poradha.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the broken bank, coins scattered like stars.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kai pathi kaapaathu vechavan, avan selvam kooda ketta poradhu. Selvam kodupaavan dhan."
thirukkural: செயற்பால செய்யா திவறியான் செல்வம் உயற்பால தன்றிக் கெடும்.
english_translation: That miser's wealth shall waste and end Who would not for a good cause spend
moral: Wealth hoarded without giving is wealth wasted.

title: The Sticky Fingers
theme: Correcting Faults / Greed is the Worst Fault
hook: Kannan takes extra candy when the shopkeeper isn't looking — Amma makes him return it.
story: At the park shop, the keeper turned. Kannan slipped two extra candies into his pocket. His heart pounded. Amma saw everything. "Kannan, enna potta?" He froze. The candy burned in his pocket. "Amma, naan... naan..." Amma took his hand. "Nee thirudala da. Va, thiruppi kuduppom." Kannan cried. Amma said, "Asai dhan enna achu. Adha control pannradhu dhan aatchi."
tamil_dialogues: Kannan: 'Amma, naan extra edhutten. Ennaku bayama irukku.'
Amma: 'Asai dhan da. Adha vittudha vendum. Thiruppi kuduthu, manasu light aagum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the shop, holding a single candy, smiling gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Asai dhan periya pasi. Adha vittu kudutha dhan, manasukku nalla irukkum. Adhu dhan kurrangala venum."
thirukkural: பற்றுள்ளம் என்னும் இவறன்மை எற்றுள்ளும் எண்ணப் படுவதொன் றன்று.
english_translation: The gripping greed of miser's heart Is more than fault the worst apart
moral: Greed is the worst fault of all — it grips the heart tightest.

title: The Class Monitor Speech
theme: Correcting Faults / No Boasting, No Useless Deeds
hook: Usha becomes class monitor and brags all day — Teacher gives her a silent task.
story: Usha wore the badge like a queen. "Naan dhan monitor. Naan sollradhu dhan sari." She boasted at lunch, scolded everyone. Teacher smiled. "Usha, oru velai. Oru naal full-a pesama iru." Usha tried. It was hard. By evening, she understood. She didn't need to shout to be a leader. She just needed to do right. Teacher nodded. "Sari. Ippo dhan unakku monitor ku artham puriyudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Teacher: 'Usha, unakku badge venum? Aallena velai venum?'
Usha: 'Teacher, pesama irukradhu kashtam dhan. Aana dhan ungalukku artham puriyudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the teacher's desk, a badge glowing in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Perumai pada koodadhu. Nalla kariyam pannanum. Adhu dhan. Perumai sollradhu matham."
thirukkural: வியவற்க எஞ்ஞான்றும் தன்னை நயவற்க நன்றி பயவா வினை.
english_translation: Never boast yourself in any mood Nor do a deed that does no good
moral: Don't boast — and don't do things that bear no good fruit.

title: The Secret Diary
hook: Deepa secretly reads her brother's diary — Appa catches her and teaches about boundaries.
story: Deepa's brother kept a diary under his bed. She knew it was wrong but opened it. Appa's shadow fell. "Deepa. Adhu avan diary." She froze. "Appa... naan..." Appa sat beside her. "Unakku theriyum dhan, adhu thappu nu. Aana un aasai unna control pannudhu. Adhu dhan problem. Nee un aasaiya kaapaadra dhan arivu." Deepa closed the diary. Put it back. She never touched it again.
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Appa, adhu oru pusthagam dhan.'
Appa: 'Adhu avan manasu da. Kavala kaapaadra dhan arivu. Adha thiruda koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the window, a diary in his hands, closed.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Asaiya kontrol pannanum. Adha theriyama kavala vittudha koodadhu. Adha kaapaadradhu dhan nalla vazhkai."
thirukkural: காதல காதல் அறியாமை உய்க்கிற்பின் ஏதில ஏதிலார் நூல்.
english_translation: All designs of the foes shall fail If one his wishes guards in veil
moral: Guard your wishes wisely — secrecy is strength.

title: The Vegetable Vendor's Friend
