theme: Seeking the Aid of Great Men / Choose Wise Friends
hook: Sarathi's Appa lets him choose a friend — not by age or money, but by wisdom.
story: Sarathi played alone. "Appa, enaku friend venum." Appa took him to the market. "Paaru. Andha vegetable kaaran — avaru 90 vayasu, aana avaruku theriyadhadhu illa. Avara friend aakko." Sarathi approached the old man. "Uncle, naan un kooda pesalama?" The old man smiled. They talked about vegetables, life, rain. Sarathi learned more in one hour than a year in school. Appa smiled. "Sariyaana friend select pannradhu dhan periya arivu."
tamil_dialogues: Sarathi: 'Uncle, unakku evlo theriyum!'
Uncle: 'Anubavam dhan da. Nallavanga kooda irundha, adhu dhan vazhkai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the vegetable cart, weighing tomatoes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nallavanga kooda irukradhu dhan periya vishayam. Yaar kooda irukraan nu therinju dhan irukanum."
thirukkural: அறனறிந்து மூத்த அறிவுடையார் கேண்மை திறனறிந்து தேர்ந்து கொளல்.
english_translation: Weigh their worth and friendship gain Of men of virtue and mature brain
moral: Choose friends who are wise and virtuous — not just fun.

title: The Leaky Roof
theme: Seeking the Aid of Great Men / Help That Protects
hook: When rain floods their home, Raji's Amma calls the old mason who rebuilt their house before.
story: The rain poured through the roof. Raji was scared. "Amma, thanni varudhu!" Amma called an old man — Rajan, the mason who built their house. He came in the rain, fixed the holes, stayed until the water stopped. "Kavalai padaadha. Naan irukken." Raji watched him work. "Amma, ivar yaaru?" Amma said, "Namma kastathula kaapadra aalu. Avar mathiri aalu venum, da."
tamil_dialogues: Raji: 'Amma, Rajan uncle yaaru?'
Amma: 'Namma aalu da. Kastam varum bodhu, kaapadravan dhan nalla aalu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the rain, holding a tile, water flowing past him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kastathula kaapadravan than nalla aalu. Avanai kaapadra dhan, kastam varadha munnadiye."
thirukkural: உற்றநோய் நீக்கி உறாஅமை முற்காக்கும் பெற்றியார்ப் பேணிக் கொளல்.
english_translation: Cherish the help of men of skill Who ward and safe-guard you from ill
moral: Keep close those who protect you from trouble — before it comes.

title: The Gold Medallist Uncle
theme: Seeking the Aid of Great Men / Rare Treasure of Good Friends
hook: Vinoth envies his friend's rich uncle — until he sees how poor his own "great man" really is.
story: Vinoth's friend had an uncle who bought him a cycle. Vinoth felt bad. "Appa, nama friend uncle bike kuduthaaru. Namma uncle enna kuduthaaru?" Appa smiled. "Vaa, un uncle kitta po." Vinoth's uncle was a retired teacher — old shirt, simple house. But when Vinoth failed math, that uncle taught him for three months. No fee. Vinoth passed. "Appa, uncle bus fare kuda kedaiyaadha, aana..." Appa nodded. "Enna koduthaaru theriyudha? Arivu. Adhu bike vida periyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Vinoth: 'Appa, uncle kitta bus fare kuda illa. Aana avar kitta arivu irukku.'
Appa: 'Bike kudutha aalu vendaam. Arivu kudutha aalu dhan venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the uncle's simple house, a single book glowing in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periyavangala thunaiya podradhu romba aritha. Atha pannravanuku neriya payan."
thirukkural: அரியவற்று ளெல்லாம் அரிதே பெரியாரைப் பேணித் தமராக் கொளல்.
english_translation: Honour and have the great your own Is rarest of the rare things known
moral: Making the truly great your own is the rarest achievement of all.

title: The Kabaddi Captain
theme: Seeking the Aid of Great Men / The Power of Betters
hook: Murugan is the best kabaddi player — until Coach pairs him with an older player and he sees real power.
story: Murugan was king of the school team. Fast, strong, untouchable. Coach brought in a retired state player — thick glasses, limping. Murugan laughed. "Ivar-a?" The old man smiled. One match. The old man caught Murugan every single raid. Murugan couldn't move. "Epdi da pannra?" The old man said, "Anubavam da. Neeyum oru naal epdi panradhu nu kathukka vendiyadhu dhan." Murugan sat at his feet after that.
tamil_dialogues: Murugan: 'Uncle, enna ungalala enna panna mudila!'
Uncle: 'Anubavam dhan da. Nee innum valara vendiyavadhu irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the kabaddi court, whistle around his neck.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Namma vida periyavanga kooda irundha, namma aatchi valarudhu. Adhu dhan namma balam."
thirukkural: தம்மிற் பெரியார் தமரா ஒழுகுதல் வன்மையு ளெல்லாந் தலை.
english_translation: To have betters as intimates Power of all powers promotes
moral: Having those greater than you as your own is the greatest power of all.

title: The Lost Puppy
theme: Seeking the Aid of Great Men / Eyes That Guide
hook: Sandhya finds a lost puppy but doesn't know what to do — Paati shows her how to seek help wisely.
story: A puppy shivered under a car. Sandhya wanted to help but didn't know how. "Paati, enna pannradhu?" Paati didn't answer directly. "Andha kadai la Anna — avaru sandaiku ulla nadandha aalu. Ava kitta poi ketu." Sandhya ran to the shopkeeper. He recognized the puppy. "Ada, idhu Muthu's dog. Naan phone panniduven." Paati nodded. "Kaaryam exercise pannanu — sari aalu kitta dhan poganum."
tamil_dialogues: Sandhya: 'Paati, enakku theriyala enna pannradhu!'
Paati: 'Therinjavan kitta dhan poganum. Unakku theriyadhadha, sari aalu kitta ketu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the shop, the puppy licking his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vishayathai exercise pannanu — sari aalu solli kuduppaan. Kalla irukka aalu dhan."
thirukkural: சூழ்வார்கண் ணாக ஒழுகலான் மன்னவன் சூழ்வாரைக் சூழ்ந்து கொளல்.
english_translation: Ministers are the monarch's eyes Round him should be the right and wise
moral: The right advisors are like eyes — choose them wisely.

title: The Cycle Race
theme: Seeking the Aid of Great Men / Safety in Good Company
hook: Arasu wants to race his cycle on the dangerous highway — Selvam's wise friend stops them.
story: Arasu and his friends planned a cycle race on the main road. "Vazhi nalla irukku, speed adichidalam." Selvam's older cousin, a college cyclist, stopped them. "Dei, andha road la lorry varum. Nee speed edutha, kaatu kooda pathukka mudiyadhu." Arasu pouted. The cousin took them to a village path. Safe, curvy, fun. They raced and laughed. Arasu understood. "Nalla aalu sonnadhu kettadhu nalladhu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Arasu: 'Anna, highway la dhan race podlam!'
Cousin: 'Adhu dhan problem da. Un aatchi unakku theriyala. Nalla vazhi kaamippan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar cycles beside them on the village path, no hands on the handlebar.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nallavanga kooda irundha, adhu kaapadum. Yaar kooda irukraan nu theriyum."
thirukkural: தக்கா ரினத்தனாய்த் தானொழுக வல்லானைச் செற்றார் செயக்கிடந்த தில்.
english_translation: To move with worthy friends who knows Has none to fear from frightful foes
moral: With good company, no enemy can harm you.

title: The Broken Windows
theme: Seeking the Aid of Great Men / Friends Who Correct You
hook: Vimal throws a stone and breaks a window — Kathir stops him from running away and makes him confess.
story: Vimal's stone shattered the science room window. He turned to run. Kathir grabbed his shirt. "Dei! Nikkuda!" Vimal struggled. "Vidu! Ottuvar!" Kathir held tight. "Nee otta pona, un manasu dhan unna ottum. Va, principal kitta sollidalam." Vimal shook. But Kathir walked with him. Principal just said, "Parava illa. Iru posthagam vaangi kuduthutu." Vimal thanked Kathir. "Nee illana, naan otti iruppen."
tamil_dialogues: Vimal: 'Vidu da! Bayama irukku!'
Kathir: 'Ottuna, thappu perusum aagum. Va, naan kooda varaen. Nallavan dhan ninaikka veppaan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the broken window, pieces of glass like stars around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Idukura thunai irukravanuku, periya aatchi illa. Avan thappu pannina, avanuku ninaikka veppaan."
thirukkural: இடிக்குந் துணையாரை யாள்வரை யாரே கெடுக்குந் தகைமை யவர்.
english_translation: No foe can foil his powers whose friends reprove him when he errs
moral: A friend who corrects you is greater than any army.

title: The Lazy King
theme: Seeking the Aid of Great Men / Without Correctors, You Fall
hook: Ramesh ignores his homework, no one stops him — and he faces the consequence.
story: Ramesh's parents were busy. No one checked his homework. He got lazy. Watched TV. Slept in class. Teacher didn't notice. Month later, exams came. Zero. Ramesh failed. He cried. Amma finally saw the report. "Enna da ithu?" Ramesh wailed, "Amma, neenga enna check pannala!" Amma hugged him sadly. "Naan dhan thappu. Adhu dhan — namma kooda iduvar illana, namma kedudhom."
tamil_dialogues: Ramesh: 'Amma, neenga enna ninaikka vechurukka vendiyadhu!'
Amma: 'Ama da. Nallavanga ninaikka vechurka vendiyadhu. Adhu illadhadhu, kedudhom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at Ramesh's desk, the failed test paper in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Idukra aalu illadha mannan, avan vetri pada vaara? Adhu dhan — nallavanga thunai venum."
thirukkural: இடிப்பாரை இல்லாத ஏமரா மன்னன் கெடுப்பா ரிலானுங் கெடும்.
english_translation: The careless king whom none reproves Ruins himself sans harmful foes
moral: Without someone to correct you, you will ruin yourself — even without enemies.

title: The Stage Fright
theme: Seeking the Aid of Great Men / Support Gives Stability
hook: Latha forgets her dance steps on stage — her teacher's nod from the wings saves her.
story: Latha stood on stage. 500 people watched. Her mind went blank. Music started. Her legs froze. She looked to the wings. Her teacher — old, grey-haired — nodded. Just a nod. "Unakku mudiyum." Latha breathed. The steps came back. She danced like fire. Afterward, she hugged her teacher. "Miss, neenga illana naan stage la ninnu kuda irundhurukka maatten." Teacher smiled. "Namma dhan un thunai."
tamil_dialogues: Latha: 'Miss, unga mottu enna kaapadichidhu!'
Teacher: 'Nee dhan adichu. Naan thunai dhan. Aana thunai irukradhu dhan velikkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the wings, nodding the same nod.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thunai illadha aalu, nilaila varadhu. Thunai irukradha dhan, adhu nikkum."
thirukkural: முதலிலார்க ஊதிய மில்லை மதலையாஞ் சார்பிலார்க் கில்லை நிலை.
english_translation: No capital, no gain in trade No prop secure sans good comrade
moral: Without support, there is no stability — in business or in life.

title: The New School
theme: Seeking the Aid of Great Men / Never Abandon Good Friends
hook: Swetha moves to a new city and makes "cool" friends — but Paati warns her not to ditch her old best friend.
story: Swetha's family moved. New school, new friends. A group of popular girls invited her. "Nee nalla irukka, namma kooda iru." But they said, "Adhu un pazhaya friend-a? Avanga namma level illa." Swetha felt torn. Paati noticed. "Enna achu?" Swetha told her. Paati said, "Periya aalu solluvanga — nalla friend-ai vida dhan periya aatchi illava? Avanga kooda irundha nalla. Adhai vida koodadhu." Swetha called her old friend that night.
tamil_dialogues: New Friend: 'Ava namma level illa. Ava vittutu vaa.'
Swetha: 'Nalla friend-ai vida periya aatchi illa. Naan ava kooda dhan iruppen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the phone, the receiver glowing in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla friend-ai vida adhu periya aatchi. Adha vida dhan koodadhu. Adhu ten times worse."
thirukkural: பல்லார் பகைகொளலிற் பத்தடுத்த தீமைத்தே நல்லார் தொடர்கை விடல்.
english_translation: To give up good friends is ten times worse Than being hated by countless foes
moral: Losing a good friend is worse than making a hundred enemies.

title: The Mini Mart Disaster
theme: Foresight in Action / Weighing Loss and Gain
hook: Gowri opens a "business" selling lemonade but forgets to count the cost of lemons.
story: Gowri squeezed thirty lemons, added sugar, and sold cups for two rupees each. By evening she'd sold twenty cups — forty rupees! Appa asked, "Lemon, sugar, glass, neer — ithu ellam evalo aagum?" Gowri calculated. She'd spent sixty rupees on supplies. She earned forty. Loss twenty rupees. "Appa, naan lossthu poitten!" Appa smiled. "Adhu dhan business-nu solluradhu — loss-gain rendum paakka vendum."
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Appa, naan 40 rupees sample sambathichen!'
Appa: 'Lemon, sugar, glass ellam evalo aagum? Apdi paatha unaku loss dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at Gowri's lemonade stall, counting coins on his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Edhayum seiya munnadi — aavadhu, kedukkuradhu, aparam enna labham — moodhama yosippadhu dhan arivu. Gowri ippodhaan kathukitta."
thirukkural: அழிவதூஉம் ஆவதூஉம் ஆகி வழிபயக்கும் ஊதியமும் சூழ்ந்து செயல்.
english_translation: Weigh well output the loss and gain And proper action ascertain
moral: Before any action, think through the costs, benefits, and consequences.

title: The Science Fair Team
theme: Foresight in Action / Choosing Wise Counsel
hook: Harish picks the laziest boy for his science project team just because he's cool.
story: Harish built a volcano with Karthik, who did nothing. The project flopped. Akka asked, "Yaaru choose panna? Nalla smart-a irukkiravanai eduthurukalama?" Harish thought. Next time, he chose Priya — quiet but brilliant. They won second prize. "Akka, nalla friends-a choose pannadhu dhan ellaam."
tamil_dialogues: Akka: 'Cool-a irukkiravan mattum pothuma? Smart-a irukkirava choose pannu da.'
Harish: 'Sari akka. Priya dhan correct choice.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the winning project board, adjusting a paper volcano.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla aalungaloda serndhu, nalla yosichu seiya mudiyadhadhu onnum illa. Choose pannradhu dhan mukkiyam."
thirukkural: தெரிந்த இனத்தொடு தேர்ந்தெண்ணிச் செய்வார்க்கு அரும்பொருள் யாதொன்றும் இல்
english_translation: Nothing is hard for him who acts With worthy counsels weighing facts
moral: With good advisors and careful thought, nothing is impossible.

title: The Video Game Scheme
hook: Ravi wants to sell his cricket bat to buy a gaming console — Periyappa stops him.
story: Ravi's friend offered to sell his old PlayStation for five hundred rupees. Ravi had only two hundred. "Naan cricket bat vaanga vendam, adha vaangi appuram veetla sollidalam." Periyappa heard this. "Ravi, unakku edhukku adhu venum? Bat vechaa nalla vilayaada laam. Ithu time waste." Ravi thought. He kept his bat. Two months later, the PlayStation broke. Ravi still played cricket.
tamil_dialogues: Ravi: 'Periyappa, bat-a vittu console vaangalaam nu irukken.'
Periyappa: 'Mudhal-a kai vituttu aasa patta kedum da. Summa time waste.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans on a cricket bat, watching Ravi play in the sun.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sambathikka vendum nu paathu, irukkira muthal-ai izhakkradhu — adhu arivu illa. Nyayama yosichu nadakkradhu dhan nalladhu."
thirukkural: ஆக்கம் கருதி முதலிழக்கும் செய்வினை ஊக்கார் அறிவுடை யார்.
english_translation: The wise risk not their capital In doubtful gains and lose their all
moral: Never risk what you already have for a doubtful gain.

title: The Magic Trick
hook: Divya wants to try a magic trick she saw online — but Amma warns her it's dangerous.
story: Divya watched a video where a girl put a coin in a bottle using a burning paper. She tried it with a plastic bottle. Amma caught her. "Divya, idhu clear-a illa — enna aagum nu theriyadhu. Kai yetha venum." Divya stopped. Later she read in the comments: "Three kids got hurt doing this." She hugged Amma. "Nee sonnadhu sari amma."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Amma, video la panni kaatitaanga. Naanum pannalaama?'
Amma: 'Adhu safe-aa irukku nu clear-a theriyama pannadha da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the terrace, holding a bottle with a coin inside.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Edhuvum theliva theriyama seiya thonumaa? Appo adha seiyadha irukradhu dhan nalladhu. Kelvi kedu varadhu nu bayapada vendum."
thirukkural: தெளிவி லதனைத் தொடங்கார் இளிவென்னும் ஏதப்பாடு அஞ்சு பவர்.
english_translation: They who scornful reproach fear Commence no work not made clear
moral: Never start something unless you fully understand the risks.

title: The Fort of Pillows
theme: Foresight in Action / Planning Before Acting
hook: Mani builds a pillow fort without planning and it collapses — Thatha teaches him strategy.
story: Mani grabbed every pillow and blanket, stacked them randomly. The tower wobbled and crashed on his head. "Thatha, fort collapse aagiduchu!" Thatha laughed. "Unakku plan illa da. First frame podanum, base strong-a vechanum. Athu pola — vela seiya munnadi plan pannanum." They rebuilt together — sturdy, tall, perfect.
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Thatha, fort dhaan vilundhuduchu!'
Thatha: 'Plan illaama seiyradhu dhan vilundhudum. Plan irundha nikkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits inside the rebuilt pillow fort, cushions around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vela seiya munnadi vazhi yosichu dhan seiya vendum. Illana, etthirigal unakku edhirthu varuvaanga."
thirukkural: வகையறச் சூழா தெழுதல் பகைவரைப் பாத்திப் படுப்பதோ ராறு.
english_translation: Who marches without plans and ways His field is sure to foster foes
moral: Acting without a plan invites failure and creates enemies.

title: Right Job, Right Boy
theme: Foresight in Action / Choosing the Right Action
hook: Vijay fails his math test and fails his art competition — Amma shows him the middle path.
story: Vijay spent all week memorizing math formulas — he failed. For the art competition, he tried to paint like a master — he came last. "Amma, enaku edhuvum varala!" Amma held his hands. "Nee rendum extreme panna da. Unaku varadhadha panna koodadhu. Varadhadhu sariya pannu. Adhu podhum." Vijay found balance: studied math one hour, drew for one hour.
tamil_dialogues: Vijay: 'Amma, naan rendulaum thottu poitten!'
Amma: 'Overa pannadhu dhan thappu. Correct-a pannu da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at a table with a math book and a paintbrush side by side.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Seiya vendiya velaiya seiyadhadhu kedukkum. Seiya koodadha velaiya seiyradhum kedukkum. Sariya therinthu seiyanum."
thirukkural: செய்தக்க அல்ல செயக்கெடும் செய்தக்க செய்யாமை யானுங் கெடும்.
english_translation: Doing unfit action ruins Failing fit-act also ruins
moral: Doing what shouldn't be done ruins you — but not doing what should be done also ruins you.

title: The Dare That Backfired
theme: Foresight in Action / Think Then Act
hook: Kathir's friends dare him to jump from the roof into the pool — he almost says yes.
story: "Jump da! Bayapatra!" The pool looked deep. But Kathir remembered something. "One second." He checked the pool depth. Only four feet. Too shallow. "Naa jump pannala." His friends laughed at him. That evening, Appa heard the story. "Nalla yosicha da. Think, then act. Adhu dhan unmaiyaana dhairiyam."
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Jump da! Unga veettu pool dhan!'
Kathir: 'Munnadi yosichu paathutu varan. Depth check pannanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the pool edge, holding a measuring stick.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Munnadi nalla yosichu, appuram dhan seiyanum. Yosichu seiya vendiya velaiya, seidhu yosikka koodadhu."
thirukkural: எண்ணித் துணிக கருமம் துணிந்தபின் எண்ணுவம் என்பது இழுக்கு.
english_translation: Think and dare a proper deed Dare and think is bad in need
moral: Think first, then act. Never act first and think later.

title: The Broken Puppet Show
theme: Foresight in Action / Planning is Key
hook: Anjali wants to put on a puppet show for the temple festival but has no puppets, no stage, and no script.
story: "Chithi, naan puppet show pannanum!" Chithi smiled. "Puppet irukka? Stage irukka? Script irukka?" Anjali froze. She had nothing. She ran around frantically the day before. Nothing worked. Chithi sat her down. "Edaikulla mudiyadhu da. Adhaan plan illama seiyradhu kastam nu artham." Anjali learned to plan first.
tamil_dialogues: Anjali: 'Chithi, naan innikae show pannidalam nu irunthen.'
Chithi: 'Edaikulla mudiyadhu da. Plan seiya vendum — aprom dhan seiya vendum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the empty puppet stage, a single puppet on his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Plan illaama kashtapattu aalungal support pannalum, adhu vilundhudum. Nalla plan dhan vellai."
thirukkural: ஆற்றின் வருந்தா வருத்தம் பலர்நின்று போற்றினும் பொத்துப் படும்.
english_translation: Toil without a plan ahead Is doomed to fall though supported
moral: Hard work without a plan will collapse, even with many supporters.

title: The Homework Helper
theme: Foresight in Action / Knowing Each Person's Nature
hook: Suresh asks his talkative friend to keep a secret — and is shocked when the whole school knows.
story: Suresh trusted Kavin with a secret — that he wet the bed sometimes. Next day, the whole class giggled. Suresh was heartbroken. Amma said, "Kavin sonnavanuku nalla character illa nu unakku theriyumae? Avana nambi thappa dhan." Suresh understood: know someone's nature before trusting them. Even good actions need the right person.
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Amma, avana nambi sonnen. Avan solradhu dhan!'
Amma: 'Avan character theriyumae? Atha nambi dhan seiya vendum da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the school gate, a finger to his lips.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalladhu seiyanum naalum, yaar kooda seiyradhu nu avangoda nature-a pathu dhan seiyanum. Illana, nalladhum kettadha aagidum."
thirukkural: நன்றாற்ற லுள்ளுந் தவுறுண்டு அவரவர் பண்பறிந் தாற்றாக் கடை.
english_translation: Attune the deeds to habitude Or ev'n good leads to evil feud
moral: Even good actions fail when done with the wrong people — know their nature first.

title: The Show and Tell Lie
hook: Priya lies to impress her class by saying her father owns a car factory — then he visits with an auto.
story: "My Appa owns a BMW factory!" The class was impressed. Then Appa came for Parent's Day — on his auto rickshaw. Priya hid. Appa found her. "Priya, enna achu?" She cried. "Naa sollitaen — nee factory owner nu." Appa didn't scold. "Unmai solluradhu dhan periya vishesham. Nee adha kathukko." She apologized to the class.
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Appa, naa sollitaen neenga big businessman nu.'
Appa: 'Poi solli varadhu periya aal illa. Seiya koodadha vela.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the auto rickshaw, smiling at Priya.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulaga sirippu varadha maadhuri seiyanum. Adha vida periya aal yaarum illa. Mattavanga enna ninaipanga nu pathi kavalai padradhu koodadhu."
thirukkural: எள்ளாத எண்ணிச் செயல்வேண்டும் தம்மோடு கொள்ளாத கொள்ளாது உலகு.
english_translation: Do deeds above reproachfulness The world refutes uncomely mess
moral: Do things that invite no shame — the world will respect only what's honorable.

title: The Strongest Kid in Class
theme: Knowing One's Strength / Know Your Power
hook: Balu challenges the tallest boy to an arm wrestle and loses badly — Thatha asks why he chose that fight.
story: Balu thought he was strong because he could lift his school bag. He arm-wrestled Rajesh — lost in two seconds. "Thatha, avan dha jaichitan." Thatha said, "Avan unna vida height, weight ellam jaasthi. Un balam unna vida periyavan kooda mudi — nee un balam enna nu therinjukko. Speed-la nee vella laam." Balu won the running race instead.
tamil_dialogues: Balu: 'Thatha, avan romba strong! Unnakku theriyumaa?'
Thatha: 'Avandha periyavan. Aana nee speed-la jaippanu theriyumaa?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the finish line, stopwatch in hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un balam enna nu therinju, adhu dhaan vazhi. Nee yaroda venumna, yaaroda kooda mudi nu therinju seiya vendum."
thirukkural: வினைவலியும் தன்வலியும் மாற்றான் வலியும் துணைவலியும் தூக்கிச் செயல்.
english_translation: Judge act and might and foeman's strength The allies' strength and go at length
moral: Know your strength, your opponent's strength, and the task's demands before acting.

title: The Kitchen Experiment
theme: Knowing One's Strength / Knowing Your Limits
hook: Kannan tries to make dosa for the first time without Amma's help — it becomes a sticky mess.
story: Kannan poured batter, spread it — it became a thick, gooey blob. He tried again. Worse. Amma came. "Kannan, unakku dosa varada nu theriyumae? Adhaan un limit. Aana nee vendaamal try pannadhu dhan thappu." Kannan said, "Appo naan enna pannalaam?" Amma said, "Unakku dosa varadhu — aana tea poda varum. Adha sey."
tamil_dialogues: Kannan: 'Amma, dosa sticky aagiduchu!'
Amma: 'Unakku dosa varadhu. Aana unakku tea poda varum. Un limit therinju sey.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sips a cup of tea that Kannan made perfectly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku enna varum, enna varaadhu nu therinjukka vendum. Therinja pramadham, unakku varadhadha seiya try pannadha."
thirukkural: ஒல்வ தறிவது அறிந்ததன் கண்தங்கிச் செல்வார்க்குச் செல்லாதது இல்.
english_translation: Nothing hampers the firm who know What they can and how to go
moral: Know what you can do, stay within your limits, and nothing can stop you.

title: The Frog Jump Disaster
hook: Gopi brags he can jump across the widest puddle — and lands face-first in the mud.
story: "Naan ivalo dooram jump pannuven!" The puddle was six feet wide. Gopi ran, jumped — splat. Mid-air he knew he'd never make it. He landed in the water, mud all over. The whole class laughed. Teacher pulled him out. "Nee un limit therinjukkaama irundha da. Nee jump pannirukka koodaadha dooram adhu. Ippo puriyudha?"
tamil_dialogues: Gopi: 'Naan inga irundhu anga varai jump panniduven!'
Teacher: 'Un kannuku puriyadha dooram? Atha paathu dhan jump pannanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands dry on one side of the puddle, shaking his head with a smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thannoda balam therinjukkaama over-a try pannradhu, pallapaer thotta velai dhan. Munnadi therinju dhan seiyanum."
thirukkural: உடைத்தம் வலியறியார் ஊக்கத்தின் ஊக்கி இடைக்கண் முரிந்தார் பலர்.
english_translation: Many know not their meagre might Their pride breaks up in boastful fight
moral: Many fail because they don't know their limits and overestimate their strength.

title: The Copycat Artist
theme: Knowing One's Strength / Adapt Within Limits
hook: Shruthi tries to paint like her favorite Instagram artist and hates every stroke.
story: Shruthi admired Anu's hyper-realistic portraits. She tried copying it — but her lion looked like a cat. "Appa, enaku varadha!" Appa looked at her sketchbook. The lion was bad, but the background hills looked beautiful. "Un style adhu illa da. Nee landscape nalla varaya. Atha pannu." Shruthi painted a sunset — it won the school competition.
tamil_dialogues: Shruthi: 'Appa, enaku Anu mathiri varadha!'
Appa: 'Anu mathiri varanum na adha dhan pannanum. Unakku varadhadha pannu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands before a painting of a sunset, brush in hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mathavanga maadhiri aaganum nu nenachu, adhu un style illa. Un style enna nu therinju, adhe pannu. Athu dhan veli."
thirukkural: அமைந்தாங் கொழுகான் அளவறியான் தன்னை வியந்தான் விரைந்து கெடும்.
english_translation: Who adapts not, outsteps measure And brags himself-his fall is sure
moral: Those who don't adapt to their own nature and outstep their limits are destined to fail.

title: The Overloaded Cart
theme: Knowing One's Strength / Too Much Breaks
hook: Muthu tries to carry twenty kilos of potatoes in a tiny cart — the axle snaps.
story: Muthu's uncle gave him a small handcart. "Rendu sack vechuko." But Muthu thought, "Naan jaasthi load pannalam." He piled four sacks. The cart wobbled. Ten steps later — crack! The wheel broke. Potatoes rolled everywhere. Uncle came running. "Poadhu load pannitta da. Un cart-oda capacity theriyumae? Atha vida jaasthi potta, idhu dhan aagum."
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Uncle, cart oda poiduchu!'
Uncle: 'Aaama, jaasthi load podradhaala dhan. Therinju podanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside the broken cart, picking up a potato.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mayil irukai kooda, jaasthi load panna, adhu odaidhum. Namma pannura velaiyum apdi dhan — athigama panna kedum."
thirukkural: பீலிபெய் சாகாடும் அச்சிறும் அப்பண்டஞ் சால மிகுத்துப் பெயின்.
english_translation: Even the gentle peacock's plume Cart's axle breaks by gross volume
moral: Even a gentle feather breaks the cart if loaded beyond its capacity.

title: The Tree Climber
hook: Selvam climbs to the thinnest branch to prove he's brave — and it starts cracking.
story: "Paati, naan vera level climber!" Selvam went higher and higher. The branch grew thinner. He reached for the top. Crack! He froze. Paati looked up calmly. "Selvam, konnai vida jaasthi pona, vizhundhuduva. Mella irangiva." He climbed down shaking. Paati held him. "Un limit enna nu theriyum — adha vida poradhu dhan thappu."
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Paati, branch crack aagiduchu!'
Paati: 'Konnda vida munnadi pona, vizhundhuruva. Limit therinju iru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a sturdy lower branch, feet dangling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Konnda nera pona, uyir kooda poidum. Unakku ethana varai poga mudiyum nu therinju, adha vida munnadi poga koodadhu."
thirukkural: நுனிக்கொம்பர் ஏறினார் அஃதிறந் தூக்கின் உயிர்க்கிறுதி ஆகி விடும்.
english_translation: Beyond the branches' tip who skips Ends the life as his body rips
moral: Pushing beyond your absolute limit can be fatal.

title: The Pocket Money Plan
theme: Knowing One's Strength / Saving Within Limits
hook: Vasu gets his monthly pocket money and blows it all in one day — Appa teaches budgeting.
story: Vasu got three hundred rupees. He bought a video game CD, chips, a toy car. By evening, zero rupees. For twenty days, he had nothing. "Appa, kaasu illa." Appa said, "Nee limit therinjukaama selavu pannitta. Irupathu naal un vayiru pathi yosichu paaru. Atha dhan nigazhchi nu solluvom." Vasu learned to budget.
tamil_dialogues: Vasu: 'Appa, enaku kaasu illa. Vera edhum kuduka mudiyadha?'
Appa: 'Unakku limit theriyum. Adha vida jaasthi selavu pannina, idhu dhan aagum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits with a small money box, counting coins carefully.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aaluvudhal therinjirundha dhan porulam nikkum. Illana, evalo irundhalum kadisi varaikkum kedum."
thirukkural: ஆற்றின் அறவறிந்து ஈக அதுபொருள் போற்றி வழங்கு நெறி.
english_translation: Know the limit; grant with measure This way give and guard your treasure
moral: Know the limit, give and spend in moderation — that's the way to protect wealth.

title: The Little Lemonade Stand
theme: Knowing One's Strength / Outflow vs Inflow
hook: Anitha's lemonade stand earns only ten rupees while spending thirty — Akka helps fix the math.
story: Anitha sold lemonade for two rupees. She earned ten rupees. But she spent thirty on supplies. "Akka, naan business la nalla illa." Akka calculated. "Unakku varavu pathaam jaasthi selavu. Apdi sellama niruthanum." Anitha reduced price of lemon, used less sugar. Next day, profit — five rupees. Small, but profit. Akka smiled. "Varavu kammi aanalum selavu adha vida kammi irukanum."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Akka, naan business pannalaam nu nenachen, aana loss dhan.'
Akka: 'Selavu varavu vida jaasthi irukka koodadhu. Adhu dhan limit.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at Anitha's lemonade stand, checking a small account book.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Varavu kammi aanalum, selavu adha vida kammi irundha, kedai illa. Selavu athigama irundha dhan kedum."
thirukkural: ஆகாறு அளவிட்டி தாயினுங் கேடில்லை போகாறு அகலாக் கடை.
english_translation: The outflow must not be excess No matter how small income is
moral: No matter how small your income, never spend more than you earn.

title: The Free Jilebi Offer
theme: Knowing One's Strength / Living Within Means
hook: Karthik eats ten free jilebis at the fair and gets a terrible stomach ache.
story: "Free jilebi! Unlimited!" Karthik ate one, two, five, ten. His stomach bloated. He couldn't walk. Appa carried him home. "Enna achu?" Karthik groaned. "Jaasthi saapditta." Appa laughed. "Therinjirukkaama saapdra koodadhu da. Un vayiru nikkum idam theriyumae? Adha vida jaasthi panna, idhu dhan aagum."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Appa, vayiru valikkudhu!'
Appa: 'Unakku evalo saapdalaam nu theriyum. Atha vida jaasthi panna idhu dhan aagum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by a jilebi stall, holding a single piece.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Limit therinju dhan vaazha vendum. Therinjukaama irundha, vaazhkai kedum. Nalla irukka maadhiri theriyum, aana ullae kedum."
thirukkural: அளவறிந்து வாழாதான் வாழ்க்கை உளபோல இல்லாகித் தோன்றாக் கெடும்.
english_translation: Who does not know to live in bounds His life seems rich but thins and ends
moral: A life without limits seems rich at first but quietly destroys itself.

title: The Generosity Trap
theme: Knowing One's Strength / Charity Within Means
hook: Deepa gives all her savings to a beggar and then has no money for her own school project.
story: Deepa saw a poor old woman and gave her five hundred rupees — all her savings. Next day, school announced a project that cost two hundred. Deepa had nothing. Amma said, "Kodukradhu nalladhu dhan. Aana unakku irukkira alavula kodukka vendum. Muzhu porumaiya vittuta koodadhu. Nee limit therinju dhan kodukkanum."
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Amma, naan kudutha rate, ippo kaiyil onnum illa.'
Amma: 'Kodukradhu nalladhu dhan. Aana un porumaiya vida koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school entrance, holding a 200-rupee note.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kodukradhu nalladhu dhan. Aana, athigama koduthu, neeyum kasta padradhu thappu. Limit therinju dhan porumai."
thirukkural: உளவரை தூக்காத ஒப்புர வாண்மை வளவரை வல்லைக் கெடும்.
english_translation: Wealth amassed quickly vanishes Sans level if one lavishes
moral: Generosity without measuring your limits will quickly drain your resources.

title: The Night Owl and the Morning Lark
theme: Knowing the Right Time / Right Time for Everything
hook: Ramesh studies at midnight but fails his morning exam — Akka tells him about timing.
story: Ramesh stayed awake till 2 AM studying. But the exam was at 8 AM. He was sleepy, forgot everything. Failed. Akka said, "Nee raathirikku padikra, aana exam kaalaila! Un time-ku exam time match aagala." He switched to 5 AM study sessions. Cleared the next test. "Paathiya, time correct aana, vellai easy aagidum."
tamil_dialogues: Ramesh: 'Akka, padichittum fail aagitten!'
Akka: 'Time correct illa da. Right time la pannanum. Illana, adhu waste.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits with a clock, the sun rising behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaalai la aenda kaagam, raathiri la aenda koogai — rendum time therinju dhan irukku. Ungalukum apdi dhan."
thirukkural: பகல்வெல்லும் கூகையைக் காக்கை இகல்வெல்லும் வேந்தர்க்கு வேண்டும் பொழுது.
english_translation: By day the crow defeats the owl Kings need right time their foes to quell
moral: The crow defeats the owl by day — choose the right time for everything.

title: The Right Season for Mangoes
theme: Knowing the Right Time / Acting in Season
hook: Chitra wants to pluck raw mangoes in January — Thatha explains seasons and patience.
story: Chitra saw small green mangoes and wanted to pick them. Thatha stopped her. "Innum samayam aagala. Kaal varaikkum kaathiru." She waited. March came. The mangoes turned golden, sweet, juicy. Thatha handed her one. "Ippo dhan time. Kaalathoda pesa therinjirundha, nalla palan kedaikkum. Athu dhan visudha arivu."
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'Thatha, maanga pakkudhu! Erukalaama?'
Thatha: 'Innum time aagala da. Kaal varaikkum kaathiru. Appo dhan kedaikkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the mango tree, a golden mango in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaalathoda porundhi nadakradhu dhan — adhu dhan pazhagu. Appo dhan sevvai kedaikkum. Illana, ella velaiyum waste."
thirukkural: பருவத்தோடு ஒட்ட ஒழுகல் திருவினைத் தீராமை ஆர்க்குங் கயிறு.
english_translation: Well-ordered seasoned act is cord That fortune binds in bon accord
moral: Acting in harmony with the right season binds fortune to you forever.

title: The Pottery Master
theme: Knowing the Right Time / Tools and Timing
hook: Mani tries to make a pot without wetting the clay properly — it crumbles in his hands.
story: Mani sat at the wheel. "Uncle, naan pannidalam!" Dry clay. He shaped it. It cracked. He tried again — cracked again. Uncle laughed. "Da, first clay-a right-a ready pannanum. Proper water, proper time. Karanam illaama, enna pannalum waste." Uncle soaked the clay overnight. Next day, Mani made a perfect pot. "Time kudutha dhan, sevvai varum."
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Uncle, clay crack aagiduchu!'
Uncle: 'Time kudutha dhan sevvai varum. Karuvi, kaalam, kariyam — moonum venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the potter's wheel, a wet clay pot taking shape.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karuvi sari irundha, kaalam sari irundha, kariyam sari aagum. Moonum sari irundha, kastama onnum illa."
thirukkural: அருவினை யென்ப உளவோ கருவியான் காலம் அற஧ந்து செயின்.
english_translation: What is hard for him who acts With proper means and time and tacts?
moral: When you have the right tools, the right time, and the right method, nothing is difficult.

title: The School Play Audition
theme: Knowing the Right Time / Place and Time
hook: Lalitha auditions for the lead role on a noisy, chaotic day — and flubs every line.
story: Lalitha practiced for weeks. But audition day was during first period when everyone was noisy. She stumbled over words. She didn't get the role. Amma said, "Nalla time choose panni irukka vendum. Munnadi poyi, silent-a audition kettu irukka vendum." Next semester, she auditioned after school when it was quiet. She got the lead. "Time and place sari irundha dhan success."
tamil_dialogues: Lalitha: 'Amma, naan manda thotta. Audience disturbance.'
Amma: 'Right time choose panni irukka vendum da. Anga dhan un veli.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the empty auditorium, spotlight on him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaalam sari irundha, idam sari irundha, ulagam kooda kai koodum. Atha dhan choose pannanum."
thirukkural: ஞாலம் கருதினுங் கைகூடுங் காலம் கருதி இடத்தாற் செயின்.
english_translation: Choose proper time and act and place Even the world you win with ease
moral: With the right time and the right place, even conquering the world becomes easy.

title: The Patient Fisherman
theme: Knowing the Right Time / Waiting Without Panic
hook: Kumaresan throws his fishing line and pulls it up every thirty seconds — Thatha shows him patience.
story: "Thatha, meen vara maata nu paakudhu!" Pull. Check. Nothing. Pull. Check. Nothing. Thatha sat silently, line still. "Kaalathoda pesa therinja dhan meen kedaikkum. Kai kadaila meen pidikalaam nu nenacha, onnum kedaikaadhu." Kumaresan waited ten minutes. The line tugged — a big fish! He screamed with joy. Thatha smiled. "Pathiya? Kaalathukku kaathirundha dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Kumaresan: 'Thatha, meen enna varadha?'
Thatha: 'Kaalathoda pesa therinja dhan varum. Kadimaiya iru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the shore, fishing line still, sea behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaalam varum varaikkum kaathiru. Amaidhiya iru. Kavala pada vendam. Ulaga veeraangal kooda kaalam partha dhan iruppaanga."
thirukkural: காலம் கருதி இருப்பர் கலங்காது ஞாலம் கருது பவர்.
english_translation: Who want to win the world sublime Wait unruffled biding their time
moral: Those who seek greatness wait for the right time without panic or unrest.

title: The Game of Chess
theme: Knowing the Right Time / Retreat to Advance
hook: Arvind attacks recklessly in chess and loses all his pieces — Karthi teaches him defensive strategy.
story: Arvind moved his queen forward. Attack! Attack! Karthi calmly moved pieces back. "Retreat pannra da? Bayam dhaana?" Karthi smiled. "Kaalama paathu dhan. Ippo retreat pannitu, appuram attack pannalam." Karthi waited. Arvind's queen was trapped. Karthi struck. Checkmate. "Paathiya? Retreat pannitu dhan jaippom."
tamil_dialogues: Arvind: 'Enna da retreat pannitu? Attack pannu!'
Karthi: 'Kaalama paathu. Ippo amaidhiya irundha dhan, aprom jaippom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands over the chessboard, hand hovering over a knight.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Odukkam dhan balam. Kaalam varum varaikkum pinvangi irundha, adhu dhan nalla asthram. Aattu kutta kooda apdi dhan."
thirukkural: ஊக்க முடையான் ஒடுக்கம் பொருதகர் தாக்கற்குப் பேருந் தகைத்து.
english_translation: By self-restraint stalwarts keep fit Like rams retreating but to butt
moral: The strong practice restraint — like a ram that steps back to strike harder.

title: The Hidden Talent
theme: Knowing the Right Time / Concealing Power Until Ready
hook: Priyanka can sing beautifully but hides it from everyone — until the right moment.
story: Priyanka sang only in the bathroom. At school, she was quiet. "Edhu da un talent?" friends asked. "Onnum illa." Appa knew. "Enna kaathukka?" "Avanga enna pathi enna nenaipangalo nu bayam appa." Annual Day came. The main singer fell sick. Priyanka trembled. Then she walked up. She sang. The hall went silent, then cheered. Appa's eyes teared. "Right time varathukku kaathiruntha, adhu dhan veli."
tamil_dialogues: Priyanka: 'Appa, enaku stage la pada bayama irukku.'
Appa: 'Bayapadadha. Un time varum. Kaathiru. Appo dhan pada vendum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the back of the hall, clapping silently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un balathai ella edathulayum kaatradhu dhan nalladhu. Sariyaana kaalathila dhan, adhu veli aaganum. Andha kaalathukku kaathirundha dhan adhu balam."
thirukkural: பொள்ளென ஆங்கே புறம்வேரார் காலம்பார்த்து உள்வேர்ப்பர் ஒள்ளி யவர்.
english_translation: The wise jut not their vital fire They watch their time with hidden ire
moral: The wise don't reveal everything at once — they watch for the right time.

title: The Bully in the Playground
theme: Knowing the Right Time / Endure Then Act
hook: Sekar's bully teases him daily — Sekar wants to fight but Amma tells him to wait.
story: Rajesh took Sekar's tiffin, pushed him, called him names. Sekar's fists clenched. "Amma, avanuku adi podanum pola irukku!" Amma said, "Kaalathukku kaathiru. Un time varum." One day Rajesh broke the principal's window. Sekar was the only witness. He told the truth calmly. Rajesh got suspended. Amma said, "Paathiya? Sariyaana kaalathula dhan, sariyana vela seiya mudiyum."
tamil_dialogues: Sekar: 'Amma, avan enaku adi podraa! Naanum adi podalama?'
Amma: 'Kaalam parthu dhan. Nee correct-a nikka vendum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the broken window, looking at Sekar calmly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathrunga kaila kooda, kaalathukku kaathirukkanum. Avan kettavan — adhu avanuku sariyaana kaalathula dhan theriyum."
thirukkural: செறுநரைக் காணின் சுமக்க இறுவரை காணின் கிழக்காம் தலை.
english_translation: Bear with hostiles when you meet them Fell down their head in fateful time
moral: When you meet enemies, endure them — their downfall will come at the right time.

title: The Sudden Cricket Match
theme: Knowing the Right Time / Strike When Opportunity Comes
hook: The star player gets injured and Vinoth has to bat at a crucial moment — he must seize it.
story: Final match. Twenty runs needed. Star batsman sprained ankle. Vinoth was next. "Naa? Enaku varadha!" Captain said, "Time da idhu! Nee pannanum." Vinoth stepped up. First ball — four! Second — six! He won the match. The team lifted him. Captain hugged him. "Sariyaana time la, sariyaana velai pannanum. Nee adhu dhan panna."
tamil_dialogues: Vinoth: 'Captain, enaku bayama irukku!'
Captain: 'Time da idhu. Bayapadadha. Nee pannu!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the crease, cricket bat over his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kedacha kaalathula, adha sariya use pannanum. Andha nimidam thappa vittuta, aparam varadhu. Azhaga pannu."
thirukkural: எய்தற் கரியது இயைந்தக்கால் அந்நிலையே செய்தற் கரிய செயல்.
english_translation: When comes the season ripe and rare Dare and do hard things then and there
moral: When a rare opportunity comes, act immediately and do what needs to be done.

title: The Heron's Lesson
theme: Knowing the Right Time / Patience and Precision
hook: Janani watches a heron stand still for an hour — then strike a fish in one second.
story: Janani got bored watching the heron. "Thatha, adhu enna pannudhu? Summa nikkudhu!" Thatha whispered. "Kaathu irukku. Kaalam varum." The heron stood like a statue. A fish swam close. In a flash — stab! The heron caught it. Janani's jaw dropped. Thatha said, "Nee paathiya? Kaathirundha dhan, sariyaana nimidathula adichu pidikalam."
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Thatha, kokku enna summa nikkudhu?'
Thatha: 'Kaalam parthu irukku. Appo dhan oru nimidathula pidikkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the heron at the pond's edge, both perfectly still.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kokku maadhiri, kaalathukku kaathiru. Appo dhan oru kuthula meen pidikalam. Amaidhiya irundha dhan vellum."
thirukkural: கொக்கொக்க கூம்பும் பருவத்து மற்றதன் குத்தொக்க சீர்த்த இடத்து.
english_translation: In waiting time feign peace like stork In fighting time strike like its peck
moral: Wait like a heron in stillness — when the time comes, strike like its beak.

title: The Crocodile Lesson
theme: Knowing the Place / Choosing the Right Ground
hook: Prasanna thinks he can fight anyone anywhere until Thatha shows him a crocodile.
story: Prasanna bragged he could beat any boy in school. Thatha took him to the river where a crocodile floated like a log. "Nee adha munda adala?" Prasanna stepped back. "Illa Thatha... andha vaaya paaru." A frog jumped. In one flash the croc snapped it. Thatha patted him. "Adhu dhan — idam therinja, chinna frog kooda periya muthalaya jeykum."
tamil_dialogues: Prasanna: 'Thatha, adhu thanni la dhan jeykkudhu illa?'
Thatha: 'Ama da. Athu dhan — sariyana idathula ninnu dhan jeykka mudiyum. Nee un idam theriyuma?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands knee-deep in the river, a crocodile gliding peacefully past him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sariyana idam therinja, evanaiyum jeykalam. Thanni la muthalai, karai la naari — rendukkum avanga idam dhan. Nee un idam theriyumaa?"
thirukkural: தொடங்கற்க எவ்வினையும் எள்ளற்க முற்றும் இடங்கண்ட பின்அல் லது.
english_translation: No action take, no foe despise Until you have surveyed the place.
moral: Know your ground before you act — the right place turns weakness into strength.

title: Fort of Chairs
theme: Knowing the Place / The Power of Position
hook: Selvam and his friends face older bullies — Appa teaches them to use the terrain.
story: Older boys blocked Selvam near the fort ruins. "Ninga ellam inga dhan vara mudiyum!" Selvam remembered Appa's words. He led his gang behind a collapsed wall, through a narrow passage the big boys couldn't fit. The bullies got stuck. Selvam's team circled around and blocked the exit. "Appa, avangalukku inga theriyadhu — nammakku therium!"
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Appa, avanga perusa irukkaanga, namma jeykamaattena?'
Appa: 'Jeykalam. Aana idam therinja dhan. Unga chinna body inga dhan advantage.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits atop the Vellore Fort wall, looking down like a chess grandmaster.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru perusaa irundha kooda paravalla — sariyana idathula ninnu dhan poraada vendum. Kottaikulla ninnu poraadravanai yaaraalum vellum mudiyadhu."
thirukkural: முரண்சேர்ந்த மொய்ம்பி னவர்க்கும் அரண்சேர்ந்தாம் ஆக்கம் பலவுந் தரும்.
english_translation: Many are gains of fortresses Ev'n to kings of power and prowess.
moral: Choosing your ground gives you strength even against a stronger enemy.

title: Mud Princess
theme: Knowing the Place / Strategy Over Strength
hook: Gowri is the smallest in her team but Appa shows her how mud can beat muscle.
story: Gowri cried. "Appa, naan romba chinna. Ennaala oru velayum vella mudiyadha." Appa took her to the wet paddy field. "Nee anga po. Pakkathula Kanni ninnu adu." Kanni was twice her size but sank knee-deep in the mud. Gowri — light-footed — ran circles around her. Victory. "Appa, naan small dhan, aana man la naan dhan jeyichen!"
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Appa, ennaala avanga kitta porada mudiyadha.'
Appa: 'Sariyana idam therinja, un chinna kai kooda periya kai aagum. Man dhan inga un weapon.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the edge of the paddy field, one foot in the water.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Valimai mattum dhan pothum nu nenaikadha. Idam therinchal, chinna kaiyaalum periya kaiyai vellalaam. Adhu dhan ariyu."
thirukkural: ஆற்றாரும் ஆற்றி அடுப இடனறிந்து போற்றார்கண் போற்றிச் செயின்.
english_translation: Weaklings too withstand foe's offence In proper fields of strong defence.
moral: Even the weak can win when they choose the right ground.

title: Banyan Surprise
hook: Kannan's team always loses the temple festival game — Thatha shows him the hidden path.
story: The annual Thanjavur temple game — capture the flag. The older team always won. Thatha gathered Kannan under the giant banyan. "Avanga perusa iruntha, avanga nera varuvaanga. Nee pakkathula irukka small root passage la pona, avanga expect panna maataanga." Kannan led his team through the hidden roots. The older team stood stunned as Kannan grabbed the flag. "Thatha, avanga mudila kai vechittom!"
tamil_dialogues: Kannan: 'Thatha, avanga romba perusa irukkaanga.'
Thatha: 'Perusa irukkuravangala expect pannadha idathula dhan surprise pannalam. Ithu dhan strategy.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the banyan's roots, smiling with a temple lamp glowing behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru enna expect pannamaataano, anga dhan namma attack pannanum. Sariyana idam, sariyana neram — rendum serntha dhan vellum."
thirukkural: எண்ணியார் எண்ணம் இழப்பர் இடனறிந்து துன்னியார் துன்னிச் செயின்.
english_translation: If fighters fight in vantage field The plans of foes shall be baffled.
moral: Surprise and position can defeat even the strongest plan.

title: The Gate Bully
hook: Arasi is afraid of the new bully who waits for her at the gate — Amma teaches her the pond lesson.
story: Every day, Rani waited by the gate to snatch Arasi's ribbons. Arasi was terrified. Amma took her to the backyard pond. "Adula enna irukku?" "Meen... thavala..." "Adhu dhan. Meen thanni la irukurathu dhan adhu. Adha veliya eduthutta, athuku onnum panna mudiyadhu." Arasi understood. She stopped going through the gate. She climbed the back wall where Rani couldn't follow. "Amma, avalukku aval idam illadha pona, avalaala onnum panna mudiyala!"
tamil_dialogues: Arasi: 'Amma, ava gate la dhan nikkura. Naan bayapadren.'
Amma: 'Aval idathula ninnu poraada vendam. Nee un idam kandupidichiko.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the pond, feeding a fish with his fingertips.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Muthalai thanni la irukurathu dhan adhu. Adha veliya eduthutta, athuku velai illa. Nee un idathula ninnu poradu — adhu dhan jeyi."
thirukkural: நெடும்புனலுள் வெல்லும் முதலை அடும்புனலின் நீங்கின் அதனைப் பிற.
english_translation: In water crocodile prevails In land before others it fails.
moral: Even a crocodile is helpless on land — choose where you fight.

title: Boat on the Road
hook: Vetri compares himself to his cousin and feels useless — Appa shows the car and the boat.
story: Vetri's cousin Muthu was great at cycling. Vetri couldn't cycle well. "Appa, Muthu mathiri naan onnum illa." Appa took him to the harbour. "Kathula irukka periya car — adha kadal la oda mudiyadhu?" "Mudiyadhu." "Andha vanchi — adha road la oda mudiyadhu?" "Mudiyadhu." "Adhu dhan da — onakkum Muthukkum vithiyasam. Onakku oru idam, avanuku innoru idam." Vetri smiled. He was the best artist in class.
tamil_dialogues: Vetri: 'Appa, Muthu romba nalla cycle oduraan. Naan onnum illa.'
Appa: 'Vanchi road la oduma? Car kadal la oduma? Onakku un idam irukku — kandupidichuko.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between a fishing boat and a parked car, one hand on each.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru idathula onakku valimai illa na, adhu un valimai illadhadhu illa. Nee sariyana idathula irukka vendum. Athu dhan vellum."
thirukkural: கடலோடா கால்வல் நெடுந்தேர் கடலோடும் நாவாயும் ஓடா நிலத்து.
english_translation: Sea-going ship goes not on shore Nor on sea the strong-wheeled car.
moral: Everything has its place — don't compare your boat to someone's car.

title: The Dark Room
theme: Knowing the Place / Knowing Your Limits
hook: Mani brags he's not scared of anything — Appa blindfolds him and walks him through the market.
story: "Naan edhukum bayapadamaatten!" Appa tied a cloth over Mani's eyes. "Nee ippo poi, market la irundha kadala vaangi." Mani stumbled. He couldn't hear properly. A cow bell startled him. He tripped over a stone. "Appa, onnum theriyala!" Appa removed the blindfold. "Nee enna sonna? Idam theriyadha idathula, un balam ellam poi." Mani understood: courage needs knowledge of the ground.
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Appa, andha katti romba bayama irukku. Onnum theriyala.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — idam theriyadha idathula, un bayam dhan jeykum. Muthal idam theri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the market entrance, handing a bag of kadala to a blindfolded boy.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Idam theriyadha idathula pona, bayam varum. Muthal idam therinju, appo dhan veliye va."
thirukkural: அஞ்சாமை அல்லால் துணைவேண்டா எஞ்சாமை எண்ணி இடத்தால் செயின்.
english_translation: No aid but daring dash they need When field is chosen right for deed.
moral: Know your ground first — courage without knowledge is just blind luck.

title: The Tiny Army
theme: Knowing the Place / Small Force, Right Place
hook: Bala's team has only three players for the hill-climbing race — Thatha shows them the goat trail.
story: The Rockfort climbing race. Bala's team had only three kids. The other team had eight. "Thatha, namma jeykamaattena?" Thatha pointed to a narrow goat path winding behind the temple. "Avanga periya group periya step la varuvaanga. Nee chinna group — intha small path la po." Bala's team scrambled up the hidden trail. They reached the top while the big team was still midway. "Thatha, small group jeyichuduchi!"
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Thatha, avangalukku eight peru. Nammakku moonu peru dhan.'
Thatha: 'Avanga periya step la varuvaanga. Unga small group ku idhu dhan best path.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the top of the Rockfort, wind blowing his white beard.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sinnavan idam therinja, periyavanai jeykalam. Idam dhan mukiyam — alavu illa."
thirukkural: சிறுபடையான் செல்லிடம் சேரின் உறுபடையான் ஊக்கம் அழிந்து விடும்.
english_translation: Though force is small, if place is right One quells a foe of well-armed might.
moral: A small force in the right place defeats a large force in the wrong one.

title: Home Ground
theme: Knowing the Place / Defending Your Space
hook: A stray dog enters their yard and Devi panics — Amma shows her the power of familiar ground.
story: A big stray dog wandered into their courtyard. Devi screamed and ran. "Amma, naai!" Amma calmly picked up a stick and tapped the ground. The dog backed away. "Devi, nee bayapadra. Idhu namma veedu — nammakku inga theriyum. Adhukku theriyadhu." Devi picked up a small stick. She stepped forward. The dog ran out. "Amma, namma veetula nammakku dhan power irukku!"
tamil_dialogues: Devi: 'Amma, andha naai bayama irukku!'
Amma: 'Bayapada vendam. Ithu namma veedu. Nammakku inga theriyum, adhukku theriyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the courtyard, the stray dog sitting calmly at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Namma idathula namma dhan valimai. Sathru vandhalum, nammakku therinja idathula ninnu poraada vendum."
thirukkural: சிறைநலனும் சீரும் இலரெனினும் மாந்தர் உறைநிலத்தோடு ஒட்டல் அரிது.
english_translation: To face a foe at home is vain Though fort and status are not fine.
moral: Even without wealth or status, your home ground gives you strength.

title: The Quicksand Champion
theme: Knowing the Place / Using the Terrain
hook: Raju is chased by a stray bull — Paati tells him to run to the marsh.
story: A bull charged at Raju near the forest edge. Raju ran straight. The bull was faster. "Paati, adhu varudhu!" Paati shouted, "Marsh la po! Anga po!" Raju zigzagged into the soft marsh. The bull charged in — and sank knee-deep. It bellowed in frustration. Raju laughed. "Paati, athuku adhu theriyala — inga nadakka mudiyadhu nu!"
tamil_dialogues: Raju: 'Paati, adhu varudhu. Oda mudiyala!'
Paati: 'Nee marsh la odada. Avanuku adhu theriyadhu. Anga dhan nee safety.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the edge of the marsh, the bull stuck and calm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathruku theriyadha idathula ninnu poraadu. Avan valimai theriyum, aana avanuku idam theriyadhu."
thirukkural: காலாழ் களரில் நரியடும் கண்ணஞ்சா வேலாள் முகத்த களிறு.
english_translation: A fox can kill a war tusker Fearless with feet in deep quagmire.
moral: A clever fighter uses the terrain to defeat a stronger enemy.

title: The Four Tests
hook: A new friend offers Karthik free candy — Appa teaches him the four tests of trust.
story: A new boy at school, Gopi, gave Karthik free toffees. "Friends dhan!" Karthik was happy. Appa watched. "Karthik, avan enakku free-aa kodukkara? Avan enna expect pannraan?" Karthik thought. Gopi later asked for homework answers. Then asked Karthik to steal chalk. Then lied to the teacher. Appa said, "Paaru — oru manushanai arivu, porul, inbam, bayam — ivai nnu therinja dhan nambalaam."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Appa, avan first nalla dhan irundhaan.'
Appa: 'First nalla irukravan lam dhan dangerous. Therinju dhan nambanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at a T Nagar street corner, weighing four fruits on a scale.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvanai nambara munnadi, avan arivu, porul, inbam, bayam — ivai naalu pathi therinju dhan nambanum. Adhu dhan visudha therivedu."
thirukkural: அறம்பொருள் இன்பம் உயிரச்சம் நான்கின் திறந்தெரிந்து தேறப் படும்.
english_translation: Pleasure, gold, fear of life Virtue- Test by these four and trust the true.
moral: Test a person by virtue, wealth, pleasure, and fear before you trust them.

title: The Stolen Pencil
hook: Pavitra finds an expensive pen on the floor and everyone says keep it — but her family name says otherwise.
story: A gold pen lay on the classroom floor. "Yaro pottutu poittanga!" Her friends said, "Vechiko!" Pavitra picked it up. She thought of Amma's words: "Namma kudumbathula yarum poiy solla maatta — adhu namma per." She gave the pen to the teacher. The owner — the headmaster's daughter — cried with relief. Amma hugged her. "Un kudumbathukku née maanam saathicha."
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Pavitra, adha vechiko. Yarum keta maata.'
Pavitra: 'Mudiyadhu. Namma kudumbathula yarum thirudi irukka maatta.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the loom, weaving gold thread into a silk saree.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla kudumbathula porandhavanukku, thappu panna manasu varadhu. Athu dhan kudumaigal — adhu dhan per."
thirukkural: குடிப்பிறந்து குற்றத்தின் நீங்கி வடுப்பரியும் நாணுடையான் சுட்டே தெளிவு.
english_translation: Spotless name of noble birth Shamed of stain-that choice is worth.
moral: A person from a good family feels shame at doing wrong — that's who you trust.

title: The Wise Fool
theme: Selection and Confidence / Wisdom vs Education
hook: Dinesh has read 100 books and thinks he knows everything — Thatha asks a simple question.
story: Dinesh bragged to Thatha. "Naan 100 books padichiten. Enakku ellam therium." Thatha smiled. "Sari. Indha kudisai la irukka mathi — adhu yenna neerpathu? Enna kaayam?" Dinesh froze. He knew scientific names, water cycles — but the simple temple mathi? He didn't know. Thatha said, "Padichadhu dhan therinja pothum. Theriyaadhadhu irukka koodadhu." Dinesh realized: even 100 books can't teach everything.
tamil_dialogues: Dinesh: 'Thatha, naan 100 books padichiten. Enakku ellam therium.'
Thatha: 'Appo sollu — un paati vaikra kunkumam enna kaayam?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the library, holding a single palm-leaf manuscript.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Padichadhu dhan therinja pothum — adhu dhan theriyama irukka koodadhu. Theriyaadhadha therinja dhan arivu."
thirukkural: அரியகற்று ஆசற்றார் கண்ணும் தெரியுங்கால் இன்மை அரிதே வெளிறு.
english_translation: Though deep scholars of stainless sense Rare is freedom from ignorance.
moral: Even the most learned person has blind spots — true wisdom knows its limits.

title: The Good and Bad Box
hook: Mala's best friend copied her test — Mala must decide if she's still trustworthy.
story: Mala's best friend Vennila copied her answers. Teacher caught them. Mala was heartbroken. "Appa, ava en kitta poi sonna. Naan avanai nambalaama?" Appa said, "Oru thappu pannitana, ava nalla payyan illa nu artham illa. Ava evlo nalla gunam irukku, evlo kettu gunam irukku — rendum paathu dhan mudivu pannanum." Mala watched Vennila apologize sincerely. She gave Vennila another chance. Vennila never cheated again.
tamil_dialogues: Mala: 'Appa, ava copy adicha. Naan avanai nambalaama?'
Appa: 'Oru thappu pannitana, ava kettaval illa. Gunamum kuttramum rendum paathu dhan mudivu pannanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between two desks, holding a balance scale.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvan kuttram mattum paakadha. Avan gunamum paar. Ethu adhigamo, adha vechi dhan decision edukanum."
thirukkural: குணம்நாடிக் குற்றமும் நாடி அவற்றுள் மிகைநாடி மிக்க கொளல்.
english_translation: Good and evil in man weigh well Judge him by virtues which prevail.
moral: Judge people by weighing their good and bad — choose by what outweighs.

title: The Touchstone
theme: Selection and Confidence / Deeds Define Worth
hook: Srini is called "useless" by a classmate — Appa shows him the touchstone of actions.
story: A classmate sneered. "Unnaala onnum panna mudiyadhu. Neeyum useless." Srini came home crying. Appa took him to the loom. "Nee indha thread la knot pannu." Srini's small fingers tied perfect knots — his grandmother had taught him. The master weaver praised him. "Idhu un kai velai." Appa said, "Deed dhan touchstone. Mathavanga solradhu illa. Nee enna pannra — adhu dhan un worth."
tamil_dialogues: Srini: 'Appa, avan enna useless nu solraan.'
Appa: 'Avangala paakadha. Nee pannra velai dhan un touchstone. Idha paaru — evlo nalla knot podra.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the loom, examining a perfect knot.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periya manushan yarunnu therinjudha adhu avan seyalala dhan. Sollala illa. Karaikal pathi."
thirukkural: பெருமைக்கும் ஏனைச் சிறுமைக்கும் தத்தம் கருமமே கட்டளைக் கல்.
english_translation: By the touchstone of deeds is seen If any one is great or mean.
moral: Your actions are the touchstone of your true worth — not others' words.

title: The Friendless Boy
hook: A new girl is friendly but has no other friends — Amma warns Anjali to watch carefully.
story: Kavya was new and latched onto Anjali. "Nee dhan en frnd." Anjali liked her. But Kavya had no other friends. "Amma, avala ellarum yaen sutha maata?" Amma said, "Oruvalukku oruthan dhan friend na, apdi lam irukka koodadhu. Adhu red flag." Anjali watched. Kavya gossiped about everyone. When Anjali disagreed, Kavya got angry. Anjali understood: someone without any friends usually has a reason.
tamil_dialogues: Anjali: 'Amma, avala ellarum sutha maata. Aana ava nalla dhan irukka.'
Amma: 'Oruvalukku oruthan dhan friend na, adhula enna problem irukko? Yosichu paaru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the school corridor, watching children play in groups.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaarukum nanban illadhavan — avan kitta enna kuttiram irukkumo? Adha therinja dhan nambalaam."
thirukkural: அற்றாரைத் தேறுதல் ஓம்புக மற்றவர் பற்றிலர் நாணார் பழி.
english_translation: Choose not those men without kinsmen Without affine or shame of sin.
moral: Be careful trusting someone with no real friends — there's usually a reason.

title: The Favorite Student
hook: The teacher's favorite student is always praised — Venkat discovers he's been cheating.
story: Ramesh was the teacher's pet. "Ramesh dhan nalla padikraan." Venkat felt jealous. Then he noticed: Ramesh copied during exams. He hid notes in his sleeve. Venkat told Appa. "Appa, avar nallavadhu pole irukkaar, aana?" Appa said, "Adhu dhan problem. Nee avanoda arivu paathu dhan nambina — avan level la avan nalla dhan nadikraan. Aana deep ah paatha dhan theriyum." Venkat realized: don't trust based on appearances alone.
tamil_dialogues: Venkat: 'Appa, Ramesh teacher kitta nalla irukka mathiri nadikraan. Aana avan copy adikraan.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — pakka nalla irukravan lam dhan dangerous. Nee avan seyal pathi dhan nambanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the teacher's desk, holding a test paper.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvan pakka nalla irukkaan, avanai mathikkadha. Avan seyal pathi dhan nambanum. Appa solla maadhiri."
thirukkural: காதன்மை கந்தா அறிவறியார்த் தேறுதல் பேதைமை எல்லாந் தரும்.
english_translation: On favour leaning fools you choose; Folly in all its forms ensues.
moral: Trusting someone just because they seem nice leads to all kinds of trouble.

title: The Unknown Helper
theme: Selection and Confidence / Test Before Trust
hook: A stranger helps Geetha find her lost mother — Thatha teaches her to verify before trusting.
story: Geetha lost Amma in the crowded Kodaikanal market. A man smiled. "Vaa, un amma pakkathula dhan." Geetha remembered Thatha's rule. "Illa. Naan inga dhan nikkuren." She ran to a shopkeeper instead. The shopkeeper called Amma. Later Thatha said, "Avan unakku help panna vara pola irundhaan. Aana nee avanai therinjudha maadhiri pathukitta. That's correct. Muthal test pannu, appo dhan nambu."
tamil_dialogues: Geetha: 'Thatha, avan enakku help panna dhan vandhaan pola.'
Thatha: 'Aagalaam. Aana muthal therinjudha maadhiri pathu dhan nambanum. Adhu dhan safety.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the market fountain, watching over the children.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaraiyum muthal theriyama nambadha. Therinja appuram dhan nambanum. Adhu dhan budhi."
thirukkural: தேரான் பிறனைத் தெளிந்தான் வழிமுறை தீரா இடும்பை தரும்.
english_translation: To trust an untried stranger brings Endless troubles on all our kins.
moral: Trusting someone without testing them first brings endless trouble to your family.

title: The Lazy Watchman
hook: Appa hires a new watchman for the lock shop — Boopathy watches how Appa tests him.
story: A new watchman, Muthu, asked for the job. "Naan nalla kaapadhuvaren." Appa didn't say yes immediately. He left a one-rupee coin on the floor near the gate. Muthu picked it up and put it on the counter. Next day, Appa left the shop keys "accidentally". Muthu locked them in the drawer and waited. Appa smiled. "Ivan nallavan." Boopathy understood: "Theriyaama dhan Appa test pannittu irukkaar!"
tamil_dialogues: Boopathy: 'Appa, kaasa patha dhan kaadha vitutinga?'
Appa: 'Ama. Oru manushanai therinja dhan nambanum. Sollala, seyala dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the lock shop counter, holding a single key.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sollala, seyala dhan oru manushanai therinjudha mudiyum. Appa test pannra maadhiri — néeum pannu."
thirukkural: தேறற்க யாரையும் தேராது தேர்ந்தபின் தேறுக தேறும் பொருள்.
english_translation: Trust not without testing and then Find proper work for trusted men.
moral: Don't trust anyone without testing them first — then trust them fully.

title: The Suspicious Teacher
theme: Selection and Confidence / Balance in Trust
hook: Nithya's new teacher treats everyone as guilty until proven innocent — Amma explains both extremes.
story: New teacher, Mrs. Sarala. "Evanda pencil kalaichavan? Nee dhan!" She suspected everyone. Nithya was angry. "Amma, ava ellam suspicious-a dhan irukka. Nallavadhu, kettadhu — ellam ore maadhiri." Amma said, "Adhu dhan problem. Athigam nambradhum, athigam solla nambadhadhum — rendum dhan dangerous. Balance dhan venum." Nithya understood: neither blind trust nor blind suspicion is right.
tamil_dialogues: Nithya: 'Amma, teacher ellam cheat pannuravan maadhiri nenaikra. Yaaraiyum nambamaata?'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan thappu. Athigam nambradhum, athigam solla nambadhadhum — rendum kedukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the classroom door, a balanced scale in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Theriyaama nambana, therinja nambala na — rendum dhan kastam. Balance dhan venum."
thirukkural: தேரான் தெளிவும் தெளிந்தான்கண் ஐயுறவும் தீரா இடும்பை தரும்.
english_translation: Trust without test; The trusted doubt; Both entail troubles in and out.
moral: Trusting without testing and suspecting the trustworthy — both bring trouble.

title: The Right Hands
theme: Selection and Employment / Choosing the Right Person
hook: Chandru asks Amma why she assigns different tasks — and learns about choosing the right person.
story: Amma gave Chandru's sister the money counting, Chandru got the vegetable cutting. "Amma, yaen avanuku dhan cash counting, enakku vettu vettu?" Amma laughed. "Un akka romba careful — counting avaluku suit aagum. Nee knife handle panna nalla theriyum — vettu vettu unakku suit aagum. Sariyana aal, sariyana velai." Chandru proudly chopped perfectly.
tamil_dialogues: Chandru: 'Amma, avanuku dhan cash counting, enakku dhan vettu vettu?'
Amma: 'Avanga avanga velai dhan. Unakku vettu theriyum, avaluku count theriyum. Adhu dhan correct.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the kitchen, a knife in one hand and coins in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sariyana aalai sariyana velai la veikkanum. Appo dhan velai nalla nadakkum. Adhu dhan manam."
thirukkural: நன்மையும் தீமையும் நாடி நலம்புரிந்த தன்மையான் ஆளப் படும்.
english_translation: Employ the wise who will discern The good and bad and do good turn.
moral: Match the task to the person's nature — that's how work gets done well.

title: The Festival Manager
hook: Mano is in charge of Pongal arrangements — he must manage resources, not just tasks.
story: Appa put Mano in charge of Pongal festival prep. "Nee dhan ellam paathukanum." Mano was excited. But he bought too many bananas, forgot the sugarcane, and ran out of money. "Appa, enna pannradhu?" Appa said, "Nee resource manage pannala. Muthal yosichu dhan seyya vendum. Adhu dhan — porulai valarkka, aapatthai thiruppi." Mano learned to plan, save, and allocate. Next year, everything was perfect.
tamil_dialogues: Mano: 'Appa, bananas adhigam, aana kazhanga illa. Paisa mudinjudhu!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — resource therinja dhan manage pannalaam. Muthal yosichu seyya vendum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the Pongal pot, sugarcane in one hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porulai valarkkradhu, edhirkaalam paathu seyradhu — adhu dhan nalla aal. Appa sonna maadhiri."
thirukkural: வாரி பெருக்கி வளம்படுத்து உற்றவை ஆராய்வான் செய்க வினை.
english_translation: Let him act who resource swells; Fosters wealth and prevents ills.
moral: Choose the person who can grow resources and prevent problems, not just spend.

title: Four Qualities
theme: Selection and Employment / The Perfect Worker
hook: Thatha explains to Uma why the head cook is the best — he has four special qualities.
story: The temple kitchen served 500 people daily. "Thatha, avaru epdi ivlo perukku saapadu pannraaru?" Thatha said, "Paaru — avarukku naalu gunam irukku. Love — avan saapadai pasi pathi vechi ninaikraan. Wit — avan enna samayal pannanum nu therinjaan. Non-craving — avan sambalam pathi kavalai padadha. Clarity — avan ella velaiyum suthama pannraan." Uma watched. The cook worked silently, perfectly, cheerfully.
tamil_dialogues: Uma: 'Thatha, avaruku epdi ipdi suthama panna mudiyudhu?'
Thatha: 'Avarukku naalu gunam — anbu, arivu, aasai illadhadhu, clear mind. Athu dhan perfect aal.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the cook, stirring a giant pot with a smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anbu, arivu, asai illadhadhu, clear mind — ivai naalu gunam irundha dhan oru aalai nambalaam."
thirukkural: அன்பறிவு தேற்றம் அவாவின்மை இந்நான்கும் நன்குடையான் கட்டே தெளிவு.
english_translation: Trust him in whom these four you see: Love, wit, non-craving, clarity.
moral: Trust the person who has love, wisdom, contentment, and clarity.

title: The Changing Friend
theme: Selection and Employment / Actions Reveal Truth
hook: Saravanan's trusted friend acts differently when given responsibility — Appa explains.
story: Saravanan recommended his best friend Suresh for class monitor. "Avan nallavan. Avan nalla irukaan." But Suresh became bossy. He scolded everyone, played favorites. Saravanan was hurt. "Appa, avan yappa idha maadhiri? Naan avanai nambinen." Appa said, "Therinju dhan nambina, aana velai kodutha aprom avan vera maadhiri maari irukaan. Adhu dhan — people differ when given responsibility. Athuvum sari dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Saravanan: 'Appa, avan nallavan dhan nu ninaichen. Aana monitor aana aprom maari poitaan.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — velai kodutha aprom dhan oru manushan unmai theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the classroom door, watching Suresh with a calm face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Therinja approm kooda, velai kodutha aprom avan maari poidalaam. Athu dhan — adhukku dhan test pannanum."
thirukkural: எனைவகையான் தேறியக் கண்ணும் வினைவகையான் வேறாகும் மாந்தர் பலர்.
english_translation: Though tried and found fit, yet we see Many differ before duty.
moral: Even trusted people can change when given power — keep observing.

title: The Right Leader
theme: Selection and Employment / Choose by Ability
hook: Ranjith's class votes for the popular boy as cricket captain — Thatha says wait and watch.
story: "Kannan dhan captain!" Everyone voted for the popular boy. Ranjith was sad — he wanted his friend Bala, who was a better player. Kannan couldn't bowl, couldn't strategize. They lost three matches. Thatha said, "Nee paatha? Adhu dhan — nalla aal therinju dhan velai kodukkaanum. Popularity dhan pothum. Therinju dhan select pannanum." Next election, Bala became captain. They won the trophy.
tamil_dialogues: Ranjith: 'Thatha, Kannan dhan popular. Aana avanaala captain panna mudiyala.'
Thatha: 'Adhu dhan — popularity mattum pothum. Therinju dhan select pannanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the cricket pitch, holding a bat and a ballot paper.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla aal therinju dhan velai kodukkaanum. Popularity mattum paathu select panna koodadhu."
thirukkural: அறிந்தாற்றிச் செய்கிற்பாற்கு அல்லால் வினைதான் சிறந்தானென்று ஏவற்பாற் றன்று.
english_translation: Wise able men with power invest Not by fondness but by hard test.
moral: Give responsibility to the capable, not just the popular.

title: The Right Time
hook: Pooja has to choose a partner for the science fair — Amma teaches her to consider the timing too.
story: Science fair. Pooja needed a partner. She wanted to pick her best friend. "Amma, Seetha kooda sernthu pannalaama?" Amma said, "Nee avanoda character, avan velai, neram — moonum paathu dhan select pannanum. Seetha nalla iruka, aana avala time irukka?" Seetha had dance practice daily. Pooja chose Anu — who had free time and good ideas. They won second prize. "Amma, character, work, time — moonum match aana dhan nalla irukku!"
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Amma, Seetha nalla dhan irukka. Aana avala time illa.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan — aal, velai, neram — moonum match aaganum. Appo dhan success.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the science fair, looking at a clock and a project board.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sariyana aal, sariyana velai, sariyana neram — moonum match aagum pothu dhan success."
thirukkural: செய்வானை நாடி வினைநாடிக் காலத்தோடு எய்த உணர்ந்து செயல்.
english_translation: Discern the agent and the deed And just in proper time proceed.
moral: Match the right person, the right task, and the right time together.

title: The Piece That Fits
hook: Ganesh watches a carpenter choose the perfect piece of wood — and learns about matching skills.
story: The carpenter scanned the wood pile. He picked a curved piece, not a straight one. "Idha dhan use pannuvom." Ganesh was confused. "Uncle, idu vantai irukku!" The carpenter fitted it into a curved chair leg. Perfect match. Appa explained: "Idhu dhan — andha wood, andha work, andha aal — moonum match aagum pothu dhan perfect. Nee velai select pannra maadhiri." Ganesh understood: find the match, don't force the fit.
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Uncle, idu vantai irukku. Yaen idha eduthinga?'
Uncle: 'Ithu dhan correct. Nera irukradhu lam inga match aagadhu. Athu dhan — aal velai match aaganum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the carpenter shop, holding a curved piece of wood.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Andha wood, andha work, andha aal — moonum match aagum pothu dhan sari. Adhu dhan ariyu."
thirukkural: இதனை இதனால் இவன்முடிக்கும் என்றாய்ந்து அதனை அவன்கண் விடல்.
english_translation: This work, by this, this man can do Like this entrust the duty due.
moral: Find the exact person whose skill matches the exact task.

title: The Artist's Freedom
theme: Selection and Employment / Freedom to Work
hook: Lavanya's design is rejected by the master weaver until Amma says let her do it her way.
story: Lavanya drew a kolam design for the new silk saree. The weaver said, "Ippadi illa, appadi pannu." She tried his way. Ugly. She cried. Amma told the weaver, "Avala avala maadhiri panna vidunga. Avana dhan designer." The weaver stepped back. Lavanya's design — a free-flowing floral — became the season's bestseller. "Amma, enakku freedom kudutha, naan nalla panna mudiyudhu!"
tamil_dialogues: Lavanya: 'Amma, avaru enna correct pannu solraar. Aana enakku adhu sari illa.'
Amma: 'Nee un style la pannu. Aalukku freedom dhan venum. Appo dhan nalla work varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the loom, a finished saree glowing with Lavanya's design.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aalukku freedom kudutha dhan avan nalla velai pannuvaan. Athigam interfere panna koodadhu."
thirukkural: வினைக்குரிமை நாடிய பின்றை அவனை அதற்குரிய னாகச் செயல்.
english_translation: His fitness for the duty scan Leave him to do the best he can.
moral: Once you choose the right person, give them freedom to do their best.

title: The Suspicious Boss
theme: Selection and Employment / Trust Your Team
hook: Appa keeps double-checking Kathir's inventory count — Kathir feels insulted until he sees the bigger picture.
story: Appa gave Kathir the inventory job. But Appa counted again every time. "Appa, nee enakku count panna solra, aana pinna neeye count panni check pannra. Enakku nambikkai illaya?" Appa said, "Illada. Naan unna namburen. Aana née count pannradhu enaku theriyum. Atha naan check pannradhu — adhu dhan nambikkai. Nee sonnadhu correct dhan, aana ella thadavaium? Atha confirm pannalam." Kathir understood: trust but verify isn't distrust — it's teamwork.
tamil_dialogues: Kathir: 'Appa, nee count panna solra, aprom check panni paakura. Nambikkai illaya?'
Appa: 'Illa da. Confirm pannradhu dhan nambikkai. Namma team — rendum sernthu check pannom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the factory floor, counting bales of cloth with Appa.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvan velai pannra, adha check pannradhu dhan team work. Adhu nambikkai illadhadhu illa."
thirukkural: வினைக்கண் வினையுடையான் கேண்மைவே றாக நினைப்பானை நீங்கும் திரு.
english_translation: Who do duty for duty's sake Doubt them; and fortune departs quick.
moral: If you doubt a capable worker, fortune itself will leave you.

title: The King and the Weaver
theme: Selection and Employment / Leadership Sets the Tone
hook: The factory owner shouts at workers and production drops — Chezhiyan learns how the leader shapes everything.
story: The silk factory owner screamed daily. "Vela pannuveengala illa?" Workers were scared. Production fell. Chezhiyan asked Thatha, "Yaen avanga nalla vela panna maata?"
