Thatha said, "Mudalali kodu iruntha, velaikarar manasu koduma irukum. Mudalali nera iruntha, velaikararum nera iruppa." New owner came — calm, respectful. Production doubled. "Thatha, ippo avanga nalla vela pannra!" "Adhu dhan — leader dhan tone set pannraan."
tamil_dialogues: Chezhiyan: 'Thatha, yaen velaikaranga bayapadraanga?'
Thatha: 'Mudalali koduma irundha, velaikararum adhe maadhiri iruppa. Mudalali nallavadhu iruntha, avangalum nalla iruppa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the factory gate, workers walking in happily.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mudalali nera iruntha, velaikararum nera iruppa. Mudalali kodu iruntha — ulagamum koduma irukkum. Adhu dhan."
thirukkural: நாடோறும் நாடுக மன்னன் வினைசெய்வான் கோடாமை கோடா துலகு.
english_translation: Worker straight the world is straight The king must look to this aright.
moral: The leader's integrity shapes the integrity of everyone below them.

title: Paati's Old Phone
hook: Karthik thinks his Paati has gone senile because she keeps calling him by his father's name.
story: Karthik visited Paati after two years. She hugged him and said, "Sundaram, vandutiya?" Sundaram was his Appa's name. Karthik rolled his eyes. "Paati, naan Karthik da!" Later Amma explained: "Un Paati ava sonna maadhiri dhan — avalukku un appa vida periya pasam illa. Nee avan maadhiri dhan irukka." Karthik understood. Paati didn't forget him — she saw his father in him. That night he held her hand and said, "Paati, naan dhan da — Sundaram illa, aana un peran dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Paati, naan Karthik da, un peran!'
Paati: 'Theriyum da, theriyum. Nee avanu maadhiri dhan irukka.'
Amma: 'Paati unna marakala. Avanga unmaiyaana pasam kammi aagadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on Paati's old wooden swing, gently rocking.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Paati unna marakala da. Avanga nenapu kadala vida aazham. Sondham endradhu adhu dhan — porama irundhalum, nenappu maaraadhu."
thirukkural: பற்றற்ற கண்ணும் பழைமைபா ராட்டுதல் சுற்றத்தார் கண்ணே உள.
english_translation: Let fortunes go; yet kinsmen know The old accustomed love to show
moral: True kinship never forgets, even when fortune fades.

title: Cousin vs Cousin
hook: Harini hates her cousin Divya because she's better at everything — until a crisis at school changes everything.
story: Divya won every prize: first in drawing, first in running, first in oratory. Harini felt like a shadow. "Amma, Divya enakku pidikaadhu!" Then the school annual day came. Harini forgot her lines on stage. The whole hall stared. Divya slipped from the audience, ran to the wings, and whispered each line. Harini performed perfectly. Afterward she hugged Divya. "Unnale dhan da." Divya smiled. "Namma rendum ore kudumbam da."
tamil_dialogues: Harini: 'Divya enakku romba jealous. Avana vida naan onnum illa.'
Divya: 'Neeyum da pora pora varuva. Namma rendum ore blood.'
Amma: 'Sondham endradhu adhu dhan — adhuku mela onnum illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands backstage, holding the curtain rope.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudumbam endradhu periya balam. Atha vida periya selvan illa. Sondham irundha, yendha kastamum thedudhu."
thirukkural: விருப்பறாச் சுற்றம் இயையின் அருப்பறா ஆக்கம் பலவும் தரும்.
english_translation: The gift of loving Kins bestows Fadeless fortune's fresh flowers
moral: Loving kinship brings countless blessings that never fade.

title: The Empty Dining Table
hook: Vikram wonders why his family's big dining table always feels empty even when they're all sitting together.
story: Vikram's family had a huge teak dining table. But Appa ate in the hall watching news. Amma served and ate in the kitchen. Akka ate in her room studying. Vikram sat alone at the big table with his plate. One day his friend Sanjay came over. Sanjay's family of six squeezed around a tiny table, eating from each other's plates, laughing. "Amma, avanga table chinna dhan, aana avanga ellarum ore idathula dhan saapdraanga." Amma looked at the empty table. Next dinner, she switched off the TV.
tamil_dialogues: Vikram: 'Amma, namakku table perisu dhan, aana evanum ore idathula saapdradhu illa.'
Amma: 'Nee sonnadhu nijam da. Table perisirukradhala dhan samandham varadhu.'
Appa: 'Inniki TV off. Namma ellarum ore idathula.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the crowded little table with Sanjay's family, eating from a banana leaf.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudumbam endradhu idam serndadhu illa. Manasu serndadhu dhan. Periya veetla irundhu enna payan, ullam serndha saapadu illa?"
thirukkural: அளவளா வில்லாதான் வாழ்க்கை குளவளாக் கோடின்றி நீர்நிறைந் தற்று.
english_translation: A kinless wealth is like a tank Which overflows without a bank
moral: Wealth without sharing is like a tank without banks — overflowing but useless.

title: The Lost Pencil Box
hook: Manoj's new pencil box goes missing and he blames his cousin — but learns a hard lesson about judging kin.
story: Manoj's fancy new pencil box with a calculator disappeared. He saw it under Kanish's bag. "Thirudan! Nee dhan edutha!" Kanish cried. Amma intervened. "Manoj, adhu Kanish vaangi kuduthadhu dhan — unna surprise pannanum nu." Manoj froze. His cousin had bought him a gift. He had accused him instead. Manoj sat next to Kanish. "Sorry da. Naan wrong dhan." Kanish wiped his eyes. "Paravala da. Namma ore kudumbam."
tamil_dialogues: Manoj: 'Neeyen pencil box edutha? Thirudan!'
Kanish: 'Naan eduthukala da! Adhu unakku gift!'
Manoj: 'Sorry da. Naan dhan wrong.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits between the two boys, holding the pencil box like a treasure box.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Selvam endradhu vithiyasa kaatradhu illa. Atha sondhathoda pangu podradhu dhan. Adhu dhan selvathin periya payan."
thirukkural: சுற்றத்தால் சுற்றப் படஒழுகல் செல்வந்தான் பெற்றத்தால் பெற்ற பயன்.
english_translation: The fruit of growing wealth is gained When kith and kin are happy found
moral: The true fruit of wealth is being surrounded by happy kin.

title: The Box of Old Photos
theme: Cherishing Kinship / Generosity and Kind Words
hook: Ananya finds a dusty box of photos and discovers her grumpy uncle was once the most generous person in the family.
story: Periyappa was always strict, never smiled. "Ananya, adha thodaadhe!" But one day, cleaning his room, Ananya found a photo box. Black-and-white photos of Periyappa feeding street kids, giving blankets, building a small library. "Periyappa, neenga?" He looked, his eyes softened. "Adhu pazhaya kaalam da. Appo naan..." Ananya hugged him. "Periyappa, apdiye irundhurukalaam!" He smiled after years. "Unakka dhan da adha kaatunen."
tamil_dialogues: Ananya: 'Periyappa, neenga evlo periya manasu!'
Periyappa: 'Adhu pazhayadhu da. Ippo naan... mathiri.'
Ananya: 'Mathira vendam. Adhu dhan neenga!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sorts through old photos with Ananya, smiling at each one.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Koduthalum, inzholum — rendum sondhathai kaapadhum. Periyappa sonna maadhiri, pazhaya nalladhai marakka koodadhu."
thirukkural: கொடுத்தலும் இன்சொலும் ஆற்றின் அடுக்கிய சுற்றத்தால் சுற்றப் படும்.
english_translation: Loving words and liberal hand Encircle kith and kin around
moral: Giving and kind words keep family close forever.

title: The Angry Uncle
hook: Pavitra is terrified of her rich uncle who shouts at everyone — until she sees him secretly pay for a poor boy's school fees.
story: Periyappa was rich and always angry. "Enna da ithu! Waste!" Pavitra hid whenever he visited. One day she followed him to town. He stopped at a small shop, spoke to a boy in torn clothes, and handed an envelope. The boy's mother cried, touched his feet. Periyappa pushed her away gruffly. "Podi, podi!" But Pavitra saw his eyes. She ran to him. "Periyappa, unna paatha mathiri thappu nu nenachen." He grunted but held her hand.
tamil_dialogues: Pavitra: 'Periyappa, neenga kovam dhan, aana manasu nalladhu.'
Periyappa: 'Sss... yaarukkum sonnadha. Deal?'
Pavitra: 'Un secret en kitta safe.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands behind the shop, watching the boy's mother wipe her tears.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Koduthalum kovama irundha, athu selvam illa. Kova kedaiyamal koduthal dhan periya kodai. Periyappakku adhu innum padikka vendum."
thirukkural: பெருங்கொடையான் பேணான் வெகுளி அவனின் மருங்குடையார் மாநிலத்து இல்.
english_translation: Large giver and wrathless man Commands on earth countless kinsmen
moral: Give without anger — a generous, calm heart draws everyone close.

title: The Crying Baby
theme: Cherishing Kinship / Share Openly Like Crows
hook: Sruthi gets irritated by the cawing crows every morning — until Amma shows her why they call their family before eating.
story: Every morning, crows gathered on the neem tree. "Kaaa-kaaa-kaaa!" Sruthi threw a stone. "Amma, indha kaaakkalukum saadha vedhu!" Amma stopped her. "Paaru — adhu karaidhadhu dhan. Athu than kudumbathoda saapdum. Namma dhan orutharai saapdradhu." Amma put rice on the terrace. The big crow called. Smaller crows came. They all ate together. Sruthi watched. "Amma, kaaakkalum namakku nalla paadam solli kuduthudu."
tamil_dialogues: Sruthi: 'Amma, kaaakkaluku sound romba!'
Amma: 'Adhu karaidhadhu — avanga kudumbathuku call pannradhu. Namma dhan isolate aagidrom.'
Sruthi: 'Inniki irundhu, naanum kootama dhan saapdren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the terrace, feeding crows from his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaaakkal kooda karaichi kootama saapdudhu. Namma dhan kudumbatha vittu dhooram aagidrom. Adhu dhan unmai sondham."
thirukkural: காக்கை கரவா கரைந்துண்ணும் ஆக்கமும் அன்னநீ ரார்க்கே உள.
english_translation: The crows hide not; thy call and eat Welfare abides a man of heart
moral: Like crows that call their flock before eating, prosperity belongs to those who share openly.

title: The Teacher's Pet
theme: Cherishing Kinship / Fairness to All Kin
hook: Rohan's teacher always praises the same three students — until Rohan's mother explains what true fairness looks like.
story: Mrs. Meenakshi always said, "Arun, super! Varsha, brilliant!" Rohan felt invisible. "Amma, teacher avangaluku dhan preference kudutha." Amma smiled. "Neenga sonna maadhiri — rendu peruku dhan smile, unakku?" But next week, Rohan's drawing won a prize. Mrs. Meenakshi announced it to the whole class. "Everybody, Rohan's drawing is the best!" She looked at him with the same smile. Rohan realized: she was fair all along. He just hadn't earned the spotlight yet.
tamil_dialogues: Rohan: 'Amma, teacher ku mathavanga dhan pidikkum.'
Amma: 'Illada. Nee effort poda illa dhan. Teacher elloraiyum samama dhan pakkum.'
Rohan: 'Un sonna maadhiriye achu. Naan effort podala.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the last bench, a red pen in hand, marking a perfect star on Rohan's drawing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvarai mattum eduthu parppadhu mariyadhai illa. Aana arivukku thagunda mariyadhai koduthal dhan — athu dhan nyayam."
thirukkural: பொதுநோக்கான் வேந்தன் வரிசையா நோக்கின் அதுநோக்கி வாழ்வார் பலர்.
english_translation: From public gaze when kings perceive Each one's merits so many thrive
moral: A leader who sees each person's merit fairly will have many loyal hearts.

title: The Fight That Ended Itself
theme: Cherishing Kinship / Kin Return When Conflict Ends
hook: Divya's Athai stopped visiting after a silly fight over a saree — Divya wonders why grown-ups hold grudges so long.
story: Two years ago, Athai and Amma fought over a silk saree. Athai left and never came back. Divya missed her Athai's special fish curry. One day, she cycled to Athai's house alone. "Athai, amma kitta kovam venam. Un fish curry dhan venum." Athai's eyes filled. She packed a full meal. "Sollu ma — sareyala enna perusu? Namma sondham perusu." Divya brought it home. Amma ate and cried. "Avan sonna maadhiri dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Athai, indha sareyala naama pirinja? Athu waste!'
Athai: 'Nee sonnadhu nijam ma. Sarai vida nee perusu.'
Amma: 'Naan dhan thappu. Vara sollu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between Amma and Athai, holding the saree like a peace flag.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sanda irundhalum, sondham kedaiyaadhu. Piriya kaaranam pona odane, sondham serndhudum. Adhu dhan sondhathin perumai."
thirukkural: தமராகிக் தற்றுறந்தார் சுற்றம் அமராமைக் காரணம் இன்றி வரும்.
english_translation: Forsaken friends will come and stay When cause for discord goes away
moral: When the reason for conflict is gone, family finds its way back together.

title: The Boy Who Left
hook: Suresh's older brother ran away to Chennai and comes back broke — Appa must decide whether to trust him again.
story: Gopi anna ran away at 17. "Chennai la superstar aaiduven!" Three years later, he returned — thin, tired, empty-handed. Suresh saw Appa's face harden. "Appa, anna..." Appa turned away. "Poi sollu — vendaam." Gopi sat at the doorstep all night. At dawn, Suresh brought him coffee. "Anna, Appa thappu dhan, aana?" Appa came out, looked at Gopi, then at the shop. "Vaa. Irundhu paaththu pannu." Gopi wept.
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Appa, anna varattum. Avanum namma kudumbam dhan.'
Appa: 'Avana nambalaam? Poi sonnan.'
Gopi: 'Naan marippen Appa. Nambungal.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the shop counter, a measuring tape in hand like a welcome thread.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pona sondham thirumbi varum bodhu, nambika koduthal dhan perumai. Adhu dhan unmaiyaana kaaval."
thirukkural: உழைப்பிரிந்து காரணத்தின் வந்தானை வேந்தன் இழைத் திருந்து எண்ணிக் கொளல்.
english_translation: Who leaves and returns with motive The king should test him and receive
moral: When a lost family member returns, test them with love, not suspicion.

title: The Last Slice of Cake
hook: Kamala grabs the last cake slice without checking — and ruins her Chithi's surprise for her.
story: At Kamala's birthday, a big chocolate cake sat on the table. "Paaru, last piece naan dhan!" She grabbed it. Chithi screamed. "Stop! Adhu special!" Too late. Under the piece was a ring. Chithi had hidden a gift ring in that slice. Everyone stared. Kamala felt her face burn. "Chithi... naan..." Chithi sighed. "Sari da, forget pannitten." But Kamala never forgot. That day she learned: rushing without looking breaks beautiful moments.
tamil_dialogues: Kamala: 'Ithu last piece! Naan dhan!'
Chithi: 'Vendaam! Adhula ring irukku!'
Kamala: 'Oh no... naan paathukala. Sorry Chithi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the cake table, holding a slice with the ring still inside.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Avasara padradhu dhan — adhu periya edam. Munna yosichu seyal pannadhadhu dhan, sandhosham keduthidum."
thirukkural: இறந்த வெகுளியின் தீதே சிறந்த உவகை மகிழ்ச்சியிற் சோர்வு.
english_translation: Worse than wrath in excess is Forgetfulness in joy-excess
moral: Forgetting in a moment of joy can cause more harm than anger itself.

title: The Homework That Never Came
hook: Arun is the smartest boy in class but never submits homework — until his reputation shatters.
story: Arun aced every test. "Homework? Pannala. Aana naan dhan first." He was proud of his laziness. Then the annual grades came. Mr. Ravichandran announced: "Arun — incomplete homework 40 times. Grade C." Arun's jaw dropped. His class rank dropped from 1st to 15th. His friends who did homework passed him. He went to the teacher crying. "Sir, naan..." "Arun, therija dhan unna vellum. Un somberi dhan unna velladhu."
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Arun, homework pannala?'
Arun: 'Pannala. Aana naan dhan first varuven.'
Teacher: 'Arun, un arambam nalla dhan irukku, aana marandha dhan unna keduthudu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the notice board, pointing at the grade sheet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla arivu irundhum, adha kaapadradhu dhan perumai. Marandhu vida — adhu dhan un pugazhai kedukkum."
thirukkural: பொச்சாப்புக் கொல்லும் புகழை அறிவினை நிச்ச நிரப்புக் கொன் றாங்கு.
english_translation: Negligence kills renown just as Ceaseless want wisdom destroys
moral: Negligence destroys fame the way constant poverty destroys wisdom.

title: The Lost Grandmother's Recipe
hook: Meena thinks she can remember Paati's famous fish curry recipe without writing it down — she's wrong.
story: Paati made the best fish curry in Madurai. "Paati, recipe soli kudu!" Paati laughed. "Nee paathu dhan padichuko!" Meena watched, confident she'd remember. But by evening, she forgot the key ingredient — the special tamarind paste. She tried making it. Disaster. Paati tasted it and laughed softly. "Nenappu vekka maaten nu nenacha? Athu dhan un periya thappu. Ezhuthi vekka dhan." Meena wrote it down that night.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Paati, naan nenachu vekken. Recipe ezhutha vendam.'
Paati: 'Nenappu maaraadha? Nee appo dhan pugazh aduva.'
Meena: 'Illenga. Naan ezhuthi vekkaren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in Paati's kitchen, holding a palm-leaf manuscript of the recipe.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nenappu mattum poradhu. Sariyaana vazhi — adhai ezhuthi vai. Marandhadhu un pugazhai kedukkum."
thirukkural: பொச்சாப்பார்க் கில்லை புகழ்மை அதுஉலகத்து எப்பால்நூ லோர்க்கும் துணிவு.
english_translation: Forgetful nature fails of fame All schools of thinkers say the same
moral: A forgetful person can never earn lasting fame — all great minds agree.

title: The Open Gate
theme: Unforgetfulness / No Fortress for the Forgetful
hook: Bala leaves the farm gate open and a goat escapes — but blames his younger sister.
story: Bala was playing in the yard. Appa said, "Bala, gate close pannu." "Hmm." He forgot. Next morning, the baby goat was gone. "Amma, akka dhan gate close pannala!" His sister cried. Appa searched all day. Found the goat near the highway. Bala was hiding. Appa sat him down. "Nee dhan gate close pannala. Nee dhan poy sonna. Enna nadakkum?" Bala cried. "Sorry Appa." Appa said, "Sorry pota dhan porum? Innoruthar mela potta — adhu dhan periya thappu."
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Naan illa! Akka dhan!'
Appa: 'Bala, née dhan. Sollu — unakku theriyum.'
Bala: 'Sorry Appa. Naan dhan marandhuten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans on the farm gate, a goat beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Marandhadhu manushan. Aana adhai matha mela podradhu — adhu dhan periya thappu. Marandhalum bayam venaam. Nenachu sey."
thirukkural: அச்ச முடையார்க்கு அரணில்லை ஆங்கில்லை பொச்சாப் புடையார்க்கு நன்கு.
english_translation: The fearful find no fortress here The forgetful find good never
moral: A forgetful person has no protection — and loses all that is good.

title: The Forgotten Lunch Box
hook: Viji forgets her lunch box for the third time and a classmate shares food — teaching her to be mindful.
story: "Viji, lunch box?" Viji's face fell. She had forgotten again. Empty stomach, whole afternoon. Nandini quietly slid half her dosa onto Viji's plate. "Saapdu." Viji was ashamed. Third time this month. "Nandini, naan epdi marandhu...?" Nandini smiled. "Athuku dhan naan irukken. Aana naalai un mura — kaapadhu." That night, Viji kept her lunch box on her school shoes so she wouldn't forget. "Amma, naan maraka maaten."
tamil_dialogues: Viji: 'Nandini, unakku enna kudutha? Naan daily marakaren.'
Nandini: 'Ithu dhan dosa. Paravala. Aana naalai kaapadhu.'
Viji: 'Naalai irundhu maraka maaten. I promise.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the school corridor, holding two lunch boxes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Munna yosichu seyal pannadhadhu dhan. Marandhadhuku appuram thappu — adhu dhan varutham varudhu."
thirukkural: முன்னுறக் காவாது இழுக்கியான் தன்பிழை பின்னூறு இரங்கி விடும்.
english_translation: Failing foresight the guardless man Shall rue his folly later on
moral: Without foresight, a person will regret their mistakes far too late.

title: The Promise Keeper
hook: Iniyan promises his Thatha he'll water the garden for 30 days — every single day without fail.
story: Thatha had to go to Chennai for a month. "Iniyan, garden un kaiyila. Daily water pannuva?" "Pannuven Thatha." Day 1, 2, 3... easy. Day 10, rain came. "Amma, inniki rainy, water panna vendama?" "Nee promise panniyachu." Day 15, he was tired. Day 21, friends called him to play. He almost skipped. Then he remembered Thatha's face. He watered. Day 30, Thatha returned. The garden was blooming. Thatha hugged him. "Nee mudichutta da. Nenappu vachuta."
tamil_dialogues: Iniyan: 'Thatha, naan daily water pannuven.'
Friend: 'Vaa da play pannalam!'
Iniyan: 'Mudiyadhu da. Naan promise pannitten.'
Thatha: 'Nee mudichutta. Nee periya aalu da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the blooming garden, watering a single tulsi plant.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Solla mattu sollu. Adha sey. Nenappu vantha dhan — atha vida periya visesham illa."
thirukkural: இழுக்காமை யார்மாட்டும் என்றும் வழுக்காமை வாயின் அதுவொப்பது இல்.
english_translation: Forget none; watch with wakeful care Miss none; the gain is sans compare
moral: Never forget your duty to anyone — there is no greater virtue than unfailing mindfulness.

title: The Impossible Science Project
hook: Rishi's science project seems impossible until Amma teaches him to break it into small, unforgettable steps.
story: "Make a working volcano model!" Rishi panicked. "Amma, impossible! Mudiyadhu!" Amma sat with him. "Udane panna mudiyadhu. Aana daily oru step." Day 1: collect cardboard. Day 2: cut the base. Day 3: paint. Day 4: mix baking soda. Day 5: test. Day 15: the volcano erupted perfectly. Red lava flowed. Rishi shouted, "Amma! Erupted! Naan pannitten!" Amma hugged him. "Nenachu dhan panna — impossible-nu onnum illa."
tamil_dialogues: Rishi: 'Amma, mudiyadhu. Romba kashtam.'
Amma: 'Step by step pannu. Oru naal oru velai.'
Rishi: 'Amma! Erupted! Naan pannitten!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the erupting volcano model, a tiny paper flag stuck in the crater.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nenachu seyal pannina — impossible-nu onnum illa. Marandha velai illa. Nenachu sey."
thirukkural: அரியஎன்று ஆகாத இல்லைபொச் சாவாக் கருவியால் போற்றிச் செயின்.
english_translation: With cautious care pursue a thing Impossible there is nothing
moral: With careful, mindful effort, nothing in this world is impossible.

title: The Winning Silambam Move
theme: Unforgetfulness / Do What the Wise Endorse
hook: Saravanan tries a flashy move to impress everyone instead of the simple winning move his master taught him.
story: Saravanan was good at silambam. "Master, naan oru puthu move try pannuven!" Master warned. "Adhu risky da. Basic dhan jeyikkum." But at the tournament, Saravanan tried his flashy move. He slipped. The opponent struck. He lost. He sat alone. Master came. "Sonnen da. Pugazh poga vendam. Pugazhalikkapatta vazhi — adhu dhan vellum. Atha sariya sey." Next tournament, Saravanan did the simple, perfect block-and-strike. He won. He bowed to Master.
tamil_dialogues: Saravanan: 'Master, puthu move try pannuven.'
Master: 'Adhu velladhu. Pugazhalikkapatta move pannu.'
Saravanan: 'Master sonnadhu dhan sari. Naan thappu pannitten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the silambam ring, holding a bamboo staff perfectly straight.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pugazhalikkapattadhai sey. Therinjavar solli kuduthadhai sey. Adhu dhan vellum. Unakku dhan."
thirukkural: புகழ்ந்தவை போற்றிச் செயல்வேண்டும் செய்யாது இகழ்ந்தார்க்கு எழுமையும் இல்.
english_translation: Do what the wise commend as worth If not, for seven births no mirth
moral: Do what the wise praise; if you don't, sorrow follows for seven births.

title: The Captain Who Fell
hook: Kavin watches the star captain get benched for skipping practice — and learns to avoid the same fate.
story: Rajesh was the best footballer. "Pracice? Naan dhan best. Skip pannuven." Kavin was junior. He saw Rajesh coasting. Coach warned him thrice. Rajesh laughed. "Coach threat dhan." Match day: Rajesh was benched. He cried. Kavin played. They lost. Afterward, Rajesh sat alone. Kavin sat beside him. "Anna, unna paathu naan padichiten." Rajesh looked up. "Nee padichuko da. Naan dhan thappu pannitten." Kavin never skipped practice again.
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Anna, practice skip pannra?'
Rajesh: 'Skip pannuven. Naan dhan best.'
Coach: 'Rajesh — bench. Kavin, ready aagu.'
Kavin: 'Anna, unna paathu dhan naan padichiten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the bench beside Rajesh, both watching the game.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirar thappu paathu nee padichuko. Athu dhan arivu. Varuthapadradhuku appuram padikalaam."
thirukkural: இகழ்ச்சியின் கெட்டாரை உள்ளுக தாந்தம் மகிழ்ச்சியின் மைந்துறும் போழ்து.
english_translation: When joy deludes, their fate recall Whom negligence has made to fall
moral: When joy makes you careless, remember those who fell from the same pride.

title: The Marble Champion
hook: Chinnathambi wants to win the street marble championship but keeps losing because he can't focus.
story: Chinnathambi loved marbles. But he always lost. "Akka, enna naan epdi jeyikken?" Akka watched him play. "Nee konjam neram paathu dhan aadra, aana kai maathi aadura. Athu dhan thappu." Chinnathambi tried. Focus on one shot. Don't look at others. Take a deep breath. He aimed. Flick. The marble hit perfect. He won his first game. "Akka! Naan jeyitchen!" Akka smiled. "Mind set pannina dhan — ellam kedaikum."
tamil_dialogues: Chinnathambi: 'Akka, naan daily lose pannren!'
Akka: 'Nee mind set pannala. Manasu irundha, ellam kedaikum.'
Chinnathambi: 'Jeyitchen! Naan dhan champion!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels on the dusty street, a single marble between his fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manasu onnu iruntha dhan — ellam sathiyam. Nenachadhu kedaikum. Athu dhan gurus."
thirukkural: உள்ளியது எய்தல் எளிதுமன் மற்றுந்தான் உள்ளியது உள்ளப் பெறின்.
english_translation: Easy it is a thing to get When the mind on it is set
moral: When the mind is truly set on something, achieving it becomes easy.

title: The Stolen Lollipop
theme: Righteous Sceptre / Justice Must Be Impartial
hook: Priya sees the principal punish only the poor boy when both the rich and poor boy were fighting.
story: Two boys fought. Ravi (rich) and Murugan (poor). Principal Madam saw them. She caught Murugan alone. "Nee dhan adicha! Stand outside!" Ravi walked free. Priya was confused. "Madam, rendu perum dhan adichanga." Madam looked at her sharply. "Nee poi solla vendam!" That night Priya told Appa. Appa went to school the next day. "Madam, rendu perukum ore dhandanai. Athu dhan neethi." Madam apologized and punished both equally. Priya felt proud.
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Madam, rendu perum dhan adichanga!'
Madam: 'Nee poi solla vendam!'
Appa: 'Madam, rendu peraiyum ore maadhiri paarungal. Adhu dhan neethi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the principal's door, holding a scale that balances perfectly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neethi endradhu — elloraiyum ore aalai la kaala vendum. Pana dhanathai paathu illa. Paatham dhan."
thirukkural: ஓர்ந்துகண் ணோடாது இறைபுரிந்து யார்மாட்டும் தேர்ந்துசெய் வஃதே முறை.
english_translation: Test and attest impartially Consult and act the laws justly
moral: Justice must be blind to status — judge by action, not by wealth.

title: The Village That Thrived
theme: Righteous Sceptre / Justice Makes the World Thrive
hook: Mani notices that since the new panchayat leader took over, the village has become peaceful and prosperous.
story: Old leader was corrupt. "Kai kula kodutha dhan velai." Roads were broken, water tank empty. Then Mariamma became panchayat leader. She was strict but fair. "Yaarum kai kula kodukka koodadhu." Within months, the road was fixed, water came, the school reopened. Mani asked her, "Akka, evlo sariya achu!" Mariamma smiled. "Unakku oru visayam sollattuma? Namma sariya irundha dhan — namma oor sariya irukkum. Adhu dhan neethi."
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Akka, oor romba sariya achu. Road, water, ellam.'
Mariamma: 'Namma sariya irundha dhan, oor sariya irukkum.'
Villager: 'Mariamma dhan unmaiyaana leader.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the newly repaired village well, drinking fresh water.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru naattu mayiru dhan neethi. Neethi sariya irundha, oor vaazhum. Adhu dhan — nilam vanam paarkum."
thirukkural: வானோக்கி வாழும் உலகெல்லாம் மன்னவன் கோல் நோக்கி வாழுங் குடி.
english_translation: The earth looks up to sky and thrives And mankind to king's rod of justice
moral: As the earth depends on the sky, people depend on just leadership.

title: The Fair Referee
hook: Lingam's team loses because of a controversial referee call — but later he realizes the referee was right.
story: Final match. Lingam's team was tied 15-15. He crossed the line. The referee blew whistle. "Out!" "Ennada! Naan crossing dhan pannen!" The referee stood firm. "Nee line touch pannala." Lingam screamed. They lost by one point. Silver medal. He cried. Later, the referee showed him the video. "Nee line touch pannadhadhu puriyudha?" Lingam watched. The referee was right. He had been dishonest by an inch. "Sorry uncle. Naan thappu."
tamil_dialogues: Lingam: 'Uncle, naan crossing pannen! Out illa!'
Referee: 'Video paathu sollu. Nee line touch pannala.'
Lingam: 'Sorry uncle. Naan dhan thappu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the kabaddi court, a measuring tape marking the boundary.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neethi illaama, oruvi veettaiyum kattam mudiyadhu. Athu dhan aarambam. Neethi konjam vantadhum, ellaam seriyagum."
thirukkural: அந்தணர் நூற்கும் அறத்திற்கும் ஆதியாய் நின்றது மன்னவன் கோல்.
english_translation: The Sage's scripture and virtue spring From the sceptre of a stately king
moral: Justice is the foundation of all virtue and knowledge.

title: The Land Dispute
hook: Vasanth's family and their neighbor fight over a land boundary — until his father proposes a solution that shocks everyone.
story: For years, Vasanth's family and Kumar uncle fought over a 3-foot strip of land. "Adhu namma bhoomi!" "Illada, adhu namma!" They had taken each other to court. One day, Vasanth's Appa called Kumar uncle. "Kumar, rendu perukum oru aala. Atha namma amma perula koviluku kuduthuduvaam." Kumar stared. "Koviluku?" Appa nodded. "Ama. Aparam naama rendu perum ore aala la pandhai podalam." The fight ended. The village applauded. Vasanth felt proud.
tamil_dialogues: Vasanth: 'Appa, adhu namma bhoomi dhan!'
Appa: 'Vasanth, adhu oru koochu bhoomi. Namma rendu perum ore idathula ninnu dhan vazhalaam.'
Kumar: 'Un appa dhan periya manasu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the disputed land, now a small temple garden.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neethi sariya irundha dhan, makkal ore idathula serndhu vazhum. Nee paatha mathiri."
thirukkural: குடிதழீஇக் கோலோச்சும் மாநில மன்னன் அடிதழீஇ நிற்கும் உலகு.
english_translation: The world clings to the ruler's feet Whose sceptre clasps the people's heart
moral: When a leader unites people with justice, the world stands by them.

title: The Rain That Came Late
hook: Selvam's grandfather insists on following the right farming methods despite the drought — and is rewarded.
story: The monsoon was late. Every farmer around them used chemicals to force growth. "Selvam, adha potta dhan vilaichal varum!" But Selvam's Thatha refused. "Namma mura prakaram dhan pannuvom." They waited. The sun burned. Selvam lost hope. "Thatha, namma thappu dhan?" Then on the 40th day, the sky broke. Rain poured. Their organic paddy grew strong and golden. The chemical fields cracked and failed. "Thatha! Namma dhan vellum!" Thatha hugged him. "Mura sariya irundha — annamum sariya varum."
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Thatha, mathavanga ellam chemical podraanga. Namma?'
Thatha: 'Namma mura sari. Adhu dhan velikkum.'
Selvam: 'Thatha! Mazhai! Namma dhan vellum!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the golden paddy field, rain falling on his outstretched palms.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mura sariya irundha dhan — mazhai, vilai, ellam sariya varum. Murai dhan selvathin aarambam."
thirukkural: இயல்புளிக் கோலோச்சும் மன்னவன் நாட்ட பெயலும் விளையுளும் தொக்கு.
english_translation: Full rains and yields enrich the land Which is ruled by a righteous hand
moral: When a leader rules with justice, rain and harvest follow in abundance.

title: The Weakest Player
hook: Manikandan is the weakest in dodgeball, always targeted — until the PT teacher changes the rules to protect the weak.
story: Every game, the strong boys aimed at Manikandan first. "Weak-u da! Aim-u!" He always got out first. One day, Manikandan sat out crying. PT Sir stopped the game. "New rule. Strong students can only target strong students. Weak students get two lives." The strong boys protested. "Sir, unfair!" PT Sir said, "Unfair dhan inga nalla irundha? Adhu dhan nyayam." From that day, Manikandan enjoyed the game. He even caught a ball once.
tamil_dialogues: Strong Boy: 'Weak-u da! Aim-u!'
PT Sir: 'New rule. Strong vs strong. Weak get two lives.'
Manikandan: 'Sir, naan ball catch pannitten!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the center of the dodgeball court, a red ball in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vel endradhu vellum endradhu illa. Neethi dhan vellum. Valumai vida neethi perusu."
thirukkural: வேலன்று வென்றி தருவது மன்னவன் கோலதூஉங் கோடா தெனின்.
english_translation: Not the spear but the sceptre straight That brings success to monarch's might
moral: Victory comes not from force, but from the straight sceptre of justice.

title: The Honest Shopkeeper
theme: Righteous Sceptre / Justice Protects the Just
hook: Sangeetha watches Murugan uncle give extra change to a customer and later sees that customer protect his shop from thieves.
story: "Uncle, pencil vanganum." Murugan uncle was known for his honesty. When a customer paid 100 for a 50-rupee item, uncle said, "Naan change illa. Innoru kadaila change vaangi kudu." The customer came back and said, "Uncle, neenga sariya irukkeenga." One night, thieves tried to break into the shop. The same customer saw them and chased them away. Next day, Sangeetha asked, "Uncle, neenga sariya irundhadhu dhan avar kaapadhicha pla?" Uncle smiled. "Neethi dhan kaapadhum, da."
tamil_dialogues: Sangeetha: 'Uncle, neenga extra change kudutha?'
Uncle: 'Namma sariya irundha dhan — pira vazhi sariya nadakkum.'
Customer: 'Uncle sariya irupparu. Adhu dhan avan kaavalan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the shop counter, counting coins one by one.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neethi sariya nadathuravanai — neethi kaapadhum. Adhu dhan palan."
thirukkural: இறைகாக்கும் வையகம் எல்லாம் அவனை முறைகாக்கும் முட்டாச் செயின்.
english_translation: The king protects the entire earth And justice protects his royal worth
moral: When you protect others with justice, justice itself protects you.

title: The Principal Who Listened
hook: Deepak's principal punishes the whole class for one student's mistake — Deepak gathers the courage to speak up.
story: Someone broke a window in class 7. Principal sir announced: "Whole class standing punishment for one week." Deepak was furious. "Sir, adhu sari illa. Oruvan thappuku ellaarum dhandanai?" The principal glared. "Nee enna solra?" Deepak's voice shook. "Sir, thappu pannavanai kandu pidichu dhandichu. Ellaaraiyum alladhu." The class went silent. The principal stared for a long minute. Then he said, "Sari. Thappu pannavan munnadi vara sollu." Deepak felt his heart burst with relief.
tamil_dialogues: Principal: 'Whole class punishment — one week!'
Deepak: 'Sir, adhu sari illa. Oruvanuku ellaarum dhandanai?'
Principal: 'Nee sonnadhu sari. Thappu pannavan varattum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the back of the classroom, his hand raised in support.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ellaaraiyum ore aala la paarthu dhandichal — adhu dhan kodumai. Thappu pannavanai mattum dhandichu. Adhu dhan neethi."
thirukkural: எண்பதத்தான் ஓரா முறைசெய்யா மன்னவன் தண்பதத்தான் தானே கெடும்.
english_translation: Hard of access, the unjust king He shall himself his ruin bring
moral: An unjust leader who won't listen or act fairly brings ruin upon themselves.

title: The Gatekeeper's Daughter
theme: Righteous Sceptre / A Leader's True Duty
hook: Lakshmi sees her father, the headmaster, treat the rich parents warmly but ignore the poor gatekeeper — until one crisis changes everything.
story: Lakshmi's Appa was the school headmaster. He always smiled at the rich parents. "Vanakkam sir! Vanga!" But the school gatekeeper, Muthu, he barely acknowledged. "Muthu, gate open pannu." One day, a stray dog entered the school. Kids screamed. The rich parents ran. Muthu calmly walked, caught the dog, and led it out. Appa watched. He walked to Muthu. "Muthu, neenga dhan inga periya aalu. Sorry — unna naan correct-a paathukala." From that day, Appa greeted Muthu first every morning.
tamil_dialogues: Lakshmi: 'Appa, Muthu maama dhan dog-a pudicharu. Nee avara matter pannala.'
Appa: 'Nee sonnadhu nijam. Naan thappu pannitten.'
Muthu: 'Headmaster, nalla irukkeenga. Innum nalla iruppeenga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the school gate beside Muthu, petting the stray dog.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudigalai kaapadradhu dhan thalaimai kadan. Pana dhanam illa — ovvoru manushanum sama dhan."
thirukkural: குடிபுறங் காத்தோம்பிக் குற்றம் கடிதல் வடுவன்று வேந்தன் தொழில்.
english_translation: Save his subjects and chide the wrong Is flawless duty of a king
moral: A true leader's duty is to protect every citizen equally and correct their own faults.

title: The Bully's End
theme: Righteous Sceptre / Removing Evil From the Root
hook: Jeeva's school bully takes money from every junior — Jeeva finds a way to stop him without fighting.
story: Akash was the school bully. "Pocket money kodu, illa na adi!" Jeeva was terrified. Every week, he gave his lunch money. He told Appa. Appa said, "Neeyum avan maadhiri aagadha." Jeeva thought. Next day, he gathered all the juniors. "Namma ore aayudham — silence." When Akash demanded money, no one spoke. No one gave. They all stood together, silent. Akash mocked. "Nee dhan first!" Jeeva stood still. Akash pushed him. Jeeva didn't move. The teacher arrived. Akash was caught. The reign ended.
tamil_dialogues: Akash: 'Pocket money kodu!'
Jeeva: 'Engalukku onnum illa. Nee enna pannuva?'
Akash: 'Nee dhan first varuva!'
Jeeva: 'Illa. Naan illa. Yaarum illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the circle of silent children, a shield of light around them.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kodiyavanai othukala vendum. Adhu dhan — kalaigalai edukkum bodhu, vilaichal nalla varum."
thirukkural: கொலையிற் கொடியாரை வேந்தொறுத்தல் பைங்கூழ் களைகட் டதனொடு நேர்.
english_translation: Killing killers, the king, behold Weeds removes from cropful field
moral: Removing the cruel from society is like removing weeds from a field — it protects the harvest.

title: The Class Monitor Who Went Too Far
theme: Cruel Sceptre / Tyranny Is Worse Than Murder
hook: Class monitor Anand slaps a boy for talking — Harini realizes the monitor is worse than any troublemaker.
story: Anand was the most feared monitor in school. He carried a ruler, hit kids for whispering, made them kneel. One day he slapped Karthik for passing a note. Karthik's nose bled. Harini stood up. "Anand, nee oru rowdy kooda illa. Nee avan vida kodumai." The class gasped. Teacher walked in, saw the blood, removed Anand's badge that day.
tamil_dialogues: Harini: 'Anand, nee konnavan vida kodumai. Nee dhan periya thimiru.'
Anand: 'Naan dhan monitor! Naan dhan correct!'
Teacher: 'Monitor-na adikradhu illa da. Kaapadradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the classroom door, holding a broken ruler.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anand, nee monitor-a irundha, adhu unakku adikradhuku illa. Nee oru kolai pannravan vida kodumai — avan oruthana, nee pala pera."
thirukkural: கொலைமேற்கொண் டாரிற் கொடிதே அலைமேற்கொண்டு அல்லவை செய்தொழுகும் வேந்து.
english_translation: The unjust tyrant oppressor is worse than cruel murderer.
moral: A person who abuses power is worse than a criminal — power is for protection, not cruelty.

title: The Bus Conductor's Extra Coin
hook: Siddhu sees the bus conductor overcharge an old woman and Amma fights back with math.
story: The conductor demanded 50 rupees from a grandmother. "Rate board-la 15 dhan!" Siddhu pointed. The conductor snarled, "Nee saamathula vantha." Amma pulled out her phone, clicked the rate board. "Sari, conductor. Naan thani transport office ku call pannren. Ippo 15 vaangu." The conductor's face fell. He took 15 and walked away. Siddhu grinned. "Amma, neenga Superman!"
tamil_dialogues: Siddhu: 'Amma, avanga 50 rupees kekkaraan! Board-la 15 dhan!'
Amma: 'Sari, naan pethukkuren. Conductor, photo eduthuten. Ippo 15 vaangu.'
Conductor: 'Sari sari vangiko.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the bus stand, holding a fare chart like a scroll.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bayam kaatvi dhan ippadi pannuran. Ana un amma bayapadala. Adhu dhan power — thappu pannravan munnadi nikkradhu."
thirukkural: வேலொடு நின்றான் இடுவென் றதுபோலும் கோலொடு நின்றான் இரவு.
english_translation: Sceptered tyrant exacting gold is "give" of lanced robber bold.
moral: Forcing people to pay unfairly is no different from robbery.

title: The Stolen Lunch Spot
hook: A local bully claims the playground as "his" territory and takes kids' things — until Karthi's Appa steps in.
story: Every day, Raju anna stood at the playground gate. "Indha area enathu. Nee vaangina chocolate enakku." The kids were terrified. Karthi stopped going. Appa noticed. "Enna achu?" Karthi cried. Appa went to the playground. He stood before Raju. "Nee daily pannra. Kids ellam bayapadra. Nee dhan inga periya problem." Raju ran. Next week, a real community watch was formed.
tamil_dialogues: Karthi: 'Appa, avaru ellam eduthu kattikkaraaru. Chocolate, pencil, ellam.'
Appa: 'Daily dhan pannraana? Adhu dhan problem. Daily pannina, adhu dhan kettadhu.'
Raju: 'Nee yaaru da inga vanthu pesra?'
Appa: 'Naan dhan appa. Nee nadanthukko.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans on the playground gate, watching kids play freely.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Raju daily pannradhu dhan kettadhu. Oru naal pannina, theriyum. Daily pannina, nai kooda kedum."
thirukkural: நாடொறும் நாடி முறைசெய்யா மன்னவன் நாடொறும் நாடு கெடும்.
english_translation: Spy wrongs daily and do justice or day by day the realm decays.
moral: Small daily injustices destroy everything — whether a kingdom or a playground.

title: The Captain Without a Team
theme: Cruel Sceptre / Abuse Drives People Away
hook: Venba is team captain but shouts at everyone — soon no one wants to play with her.
story: Venba was the best player. She also screamed at everyone. "Oru ball kooda nera adika theriyadha! Poi veetla iru!" One by one, girls quit. Only Venba stood on the court alone. Coach said, "Nee best player, aana worst captain. Un kooda aada yaarukum pidikala." Venba sat down and cried. "Enaku theriyala coach." Coach said, "Ippo theriyudha?"
tamil_dialogues: Venba: 'Komal, unakku ball adika theriyadha!'
Komal: 'Sari captain, naan irukkuren.'
Coach: 'Paaru Venba, un kooda yaarum illa. Kuthi pesina dhan leader illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the bleachers, a volleyball at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee kuthi pesina, un kooda irukravanga pOi duvaanga. Athu dhan kodungal. Poi paaru — yaarum illa."
thirukkural: கூழுங் குடியும் ஒருங்கிழக்கும் கோல்கோடிச் சூழாது செய்யும் அரசு.
english_translation: The king shall wealth and subjects lose if his sceptre he dares abuse.
moral: A leader who abuses power loses both their team and their purpose.

title: The Tax That Made a Village Cry
hook: Gowri sees farmers crying because the new landlord doubled their rent — and finds a way to fight back.
story: The new landlord came in a big car. "Renda mada vali. Illa na, veli." Farmers cried. Gowri's Thatha had no money. Gowri saw tears rolling down his face. "Thatha, aludha?" Thatha hid his face. That night, Gowri wrote a letter to a local news channel. "Enga urla oru landlord..." The channel came, filmed. The landlord was shamed publicly. Rent went back to normal.
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Thatha, enna achu? Un kannula thanni.'
Thatha: 'Illamma, onnum illa.'
Gowri: 'Thatha, naan oru letter ezhudhen. Nallavadhu aagum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the paddy field, holding a handful of rice with tears in his eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thanniya vaanguradhukku bayama illa. Atha vaangi kuthi pesradhu dhan kettadhu. Aalungaloda kanni thuni ellam."
thirukkural: அல்லற்பட்டு ஆற்றாது அழுதகண் ணீரன்றே செல்வத்தைத் தேய்க்கும் படை.
english_translation: Groaning tears caused by tyrant's sway file the royal wealth away.
moral: Wealth built on others' tears never lasts — it melts away like rain.

title: The Fairness Trophy
theme: Cruel Sceptre / Justice Brings Lasting Glory
hook: Joshua's friend cheats and wins the trophy — but the principal's next words change everything.
story: Joshua studied for weeks. But his friend Ramesh copied in the exam and won the first prize. Joshua was crushed. At assembly, Principal said, "We have a special award — Perunthagai Thirumagan Award for Honesty." She called Joshua. "Joshua failed, but never cheated. That is true glory." The whole school clapped. Ramesh looked down. Joshua's Amma cried in the back.
tamil_dialogues: Joshua: 'Amma, Ramesh copy pannittu jeyichittan. Enaku enna?'
Amma: 'Paru da, unmaiuku ellam oru naal vethi kedikum.'
Principal: 'Joshua is our real winner. Honesty is greater than any trophy.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the principal, the Perunthagai trophy glowing in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Cheating-la jeyichadhu, adhu perumai illa. Nera vali-la jeyichadhu dhan nilaikum. Athu dhan seer."
thirukkural: மன்னர்க்கு மன்னுதல் செங்கோன்மை அஃதின்றேல் மன்னாவாம் மன்னர்க் கொளி.
english_translation: Glory endures by sceptre right without it wanes the royal light.
moral: True glory comes from fairness — without it, even a winner's crown fades.

title: The Empty Water Tank
theme: Cruel Sceptre / No Mercy Means Death
hook: Murugan's Paati waits 4 hours for water but the tanker driver skips their street to punish them.
story: The water tanker came every Tuesday. Today the driver drove straight past Paati's street. Paati chased with an empty pot. "Enna achu?" Driver shouted, "Nee pona varaikku kovam! Indha street ku innai adi illa!" Murugan saw Paati's face fall. That night, no water for dinner. Murugan asked, "Paati, avan ennamo namma kitta kovama? Aana namma ellam kettom." Paati hugged him.
tamil_dialogues: Murugan: 'Paati, avanuku kovam irundha, athuku namma ellam kettoma?'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan da — avanu kovam, aana adhu nammala aakkudhu.'
Murugan: 'Adhu nyayama illa Paati.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the empty water tank, his hand on the dry tap.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pasiya irukravanuku thanni illa — adhu dhan kodumai. Oruvaruku illama aduthavarku kodutha, adhu mazhai illadha bhoomi mathiri."
thirukkural: துளியின்மை ஞாலத்திற்கு எற்றற்றே வேந்தன் அளியின்மை வாழும் உயிர்க்கு.
english_translation: Dry like the earth without rainfall is graceless king to creatures all.
moral: A ruler without mercy is like a sky without rain — everything dies.

title: Rich Boy, Empty Heart
theme: Cruel Sceptre / Wealth Without Justice Is Worse Than Poverty
hook: Sanjana envies the rich neighbor boy with everything — until she sees how his father treats him.
story: The neighbor boy had a PS5, new shoes, a big room. Sanjana was jealous. Then one night she heard shouting. "Nee fail aana! Ennoda munda! Veliya poi!" The father was screaming. The boy sobbed. Sanjana's Appa made her tea. "Paaru ma, namma kuda nalla pattadhu. Athu dhan periya vishayam." Sanjana hugged her Appa. She never envied that boy again.
tamil_dialogues: Sanjana: 'Appa, avan kuda PS5 irukku, room irukku, enaku onnum illa.'
Appa: 'Kaathula kekkudha avan amma alugu? Athu dhan vila.'
Sanjana: 'Kekkudhu Appa. Namma nalla irukkom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on Sanjana's simple terrace, looking at the stars.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Selvam irundha dhan santhosham varadhu. Nyayam irundha dhan santhosham. Adhu illama selvam dhan kastam."
thirukkural: இன்மையின் இன்னாது உடைமை முறைசெய்யா மன்னவன் கோற்கீழ்ப் படின்.
english_translation: To have is worse than having not if ruler is unjust despot.
moral: Wealth under an unjust ruler is worse than poverty — money can't buy peace.

title: The Rain That Stopped
theme: Cruel Sceptre / Injustice Upsets Nature Itself
hook: Bhoomika's Thatha says the drought is because the panchayat president is corrupt — and nature agrees.
story: No rain for 40 days. Crops dying. Bhoomika asked Thatha, "Mazhai enna vara maata?" Thatha pointed at the president's new house. "Avan nammala dhongu pannitu irukkaan. Mazhai adha theriyudhu." Bhoomika didn't understand. That night, a storm came — but rain fell only on the village side, not the president's house. Thatha smiled. "Paaru, boomiyalum theriyudhu." Bhoomika believed.
tamil_dialogues: Bhoomika: 'Thatha, mazhai varaadha — avan dhaan karanama?'
Thatha: 'Ama. Nyayam kedaiyaadha idathula, mazhaiyum varaadhu.'
Bhoomika: 'Aana naathu vanthudhu!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the rain, his face lifted to the sky, water streaming down.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nyayama illa idathula, mazhai kooda varaadhu. Athanala dhan seer solluranga. Nyayam dhan mazhai."
thirukkural: முறைகோடி மன்னவன் செய்யின் உறைகோடி ஒல்லாது வானம் பெயல்.
english_translation: The sky withdraws season's shower if the king misuses his power.
moral: When rulers are unjust, even nature withholds its blessings.

title: The Lazy Watchman
hook: Divya notices the watchman sleeps all night and thieves steal the colony's plants — Appa teaches about duty.
story: Every night, the watchman slept at the gate. Thieves stole potted plants, a bicycle, even the colony's rose bushes. Divya woke Appa. "Appa, night watchman thookam!" Appa called a colony meeting. The watchman was fired. Next week, a new watchman came — awake all night. Divya brought him tea. "Appa, adhu dhan watchman-a?" Appa nodded. "Duty illadhavan, avan dhan periya thief."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Appa, avaru thookittu irukkaar. Kai edutha yaarum illa!'
Appa: 'Duty pannadhavan, avan dhan periya kalvan.'
Divya: 'Puthu watchman nalla irukkaar!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the gate, wide awake, a lantern in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaaval irundha dhan kaapadha mudiyum. Adhu illaama, ellam kedum. Oruvar duty illana, ellaarum keduvaanga."
thirukkural: ஆபயன் குன்றும் அறுதொழிலோர் நூல்மறப்பர் காவலன் காவாக்கெனின்.
english_translation: The six-functioned forget their lore cows give less if kings guard no more.
moral: When guardians neglect their duty, everything they were meant to protect falls apart.

title: Judge Mithra and the Missing Eraser
theme: Absence of Terrorism / Judge Before Punishing
hook: Mithra is accused of stealing an eraser — but the teacher investigates first and finds the truth.
story: Riya's glitter eraser was missing. "Mithra eduthutu poita!" Everyone looked at Mithra. Teacher said, "Stop. Ellaarum iranga." She checked each desk. The eraser had fallen behind Riya's own bag. Teacher held it up. "Mithra mele kuthri pesradhu thappu. Munnadi therinjitu dhan sollanum." Mithra cried. Riya said sorry. Teacher hugged both.
tamil_dialogues: Riya: 'Mithra dhan eduthutu poita!'
Teacher: 'Adha munnadi therinjiko. Aparam pesalam.'
Mithra: 'Naan edukala miss!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the classroom door, holding an eraser like a gavel.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Munnadi therinju, aparam sollu. Adhu dhan nyayam. Munnadi kuthri vittal, thappu. Nyayam dhan kaapadhum."
thirukkural: தக்காங்கு நாடித் தலைச்செல்லா வண்ணத்தால் ஒத்தாங்கு ஒறுப்பது வேந்து.
english_translation: A king enquires and gives sentence just to prevent future offence.
moral: Investigate first, judge later — a true leader never punishes without proof.

title: The Soft Catcher
theme: Absence of Terrorism / Firm but Gentle
hook: Vikram's father catches a hard-hit ball with bare hands — and shows how strength lies in gentleness.
story: Vikram's father taught him cricket. "Geda vaiku da!" Vikram bowled hard. Father caught it easily, gently. "Appa, valikala?" Father laughed. "Katukka edutha, kaai noValikum. Mella edutha, adhu kadakkum." He showed his open palm. "Adhu dhan da — katumaiya illa, mella dhan nikka mudiyum." Vikram understood: you don't need to be harsh to be strong.
tamil_dialogues: Vikram: 'Appa, katumaiya pidikanum illa?'
Appa: 'Illada. Mella dhan periya aakku varum. Kadi dhan ulladha kedukkum.'
Vikram: 'Appo soft-a dhan correct-a?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the backyard, catching a ball with one open palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Katumaiya vittal, adhu odindhu pOidum. Mella edutha, adhu nilaikkum. Un viLayattilum, vaazhkaiyilum adhu dhan."
thirukkural: கடிதோச்சி மெல்ல எறிக நெடிதாக்கம் நீங்காமை வேண்டு பவர்.
english_translation: Wield fast the rod but gently lay this strict mildness prolongs the sway.
moral: Be firm in intention but gentle in action — that is the way to lasting success.

title: The Screaming Captain
theme: Absence of Terrorism / Fear Destroys the Ruler
hook: Naveen shouts at his teammates every game — until they all walk off the court.
story: "What are you doing da! Pass the ball! Idiot!" Naveen screamed every match. His team played worse each time. One day, during a timeout, all five sat down and didn't get up. "Vera yaaru? Nee vechu aadu." Naveen stood alone. Coach said, "Paathiya. Un kovam dhan unna thOikkira." Naveen sat down. "Enaku venam coach. I understand."
tamil_dialogues: Naveen: 'Yov! Unakku aada theriyadha!'
Captain: 'Sari da, neeye aadu.'
Coach: 'Nee kathi mathiri irukka. Athanala dhan yaarum un kooda illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the basketball, the court empty around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bayam kaati aatradhu — adhu dhan kettadhu. Athanala dhan avan veetukku pOitaanga. Bayam dhan unna thOikkudhu."
thirukkural: வெருவந்த செய்தொழுகும் வெங்கோல னாயின் ஒருவந்தம் ஒல்லைக் கெடும்.
english_translation: His cruel rod of dreadful deed brings king's ruin quick indeed.
moral: If you lead through fear, your fall will come swiftly — and you'll fall alone.

title: The Lunch Lady's Smile
theme: Absence of Terrorism / Harsh Words Hasten Ruin
hook: Kavya snaps at the lunch lady for giving her cold food — and sees the hurt in her eyes.
story: "Idhu enna saapadu? KaRuppa pOchu! Unakku saapada theriyadha?" Kavya shouted. The lunch lady's smile froze. She silently took the plate, heated it, returned it. Her hands were shaking. Kavya's Amma was a cook too. That night, Kavya cried. Next day she brought a flower. "Sorry akka." The lady hugged her. "Parava illa ma."
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Idhu saapada koodadhu! Cold-a irukku!'
Lunch Lady: 'Sorry ma, naan change pannren.'
Kavya: 'Akka, sorry. Naan dhan thappu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the school kitchen, stirring a pot with a gentle hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Katumaiya pesina, adhu unnai kedukkum. Mella pesina, adhu unnai valakkum. Kavya katumaiya pesina, avalukum kedum."
thirukkural: இறைகடியன் என்றுரைக்கும் இன்னாச்சொல் வேந்தன் உறைகடுகி ஒல்லைக் கெடும்.
english_translation: As men the king a tyrant call his days dwindled, hasten his fall.
moral: Harsh words make people call you cruel — and your world will crumble fast.

title: The Hidden Prince
hook: Pooja learns that the old priest who never speaks to anyone was once a kind man — until power changed him.
story: The chief priest never smiled. He walked past devotees like they were invisible. "Swami, oru vakkum solladha?" Pooja asked. Grandfather whispered, "Avandha periya aacharya. Aana avaru nalla maadhiri illa." One day a little boy fell near him. The priest just stepped over him. Pooja ran to help. Later she told Grandfather, "Avaruku periya padavi, aana periya manasu illa."
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Thatha, avaru enna pesa maataara?'
Thatha: 'Padavi vanthuchu, aana manasu valarala.'
Pooja: 'Naan valarumbodhu, manasum valaranum Thatha.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the temple corridor, his hand extended to bless everyone equally.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Padavi irundha dhan periyavadhu illa. Kidaikiravangala santhosama paakradhu dhan periyavadhu. Athu illaama, padavi dhan paavam."
thirukkural: அருஞ்செவ்வி இன்னா முகத்தான் பெருஞ்செல்வம் பேஎய்கண் டன்னது உடைத்து.
english_translation: Whose sight is scarce, whose face is foul his wealth seems watched by a ghoul.
moral: Wealth and position mean nothing if you cannot greet others with a kind face.

title: The Flower Seller's Lesson
theme: Absence of Terrorism / Harsh Words Wipe Out Wealth
hook: Meenakshi's mother shouts at a poor flower seller — and a week later needs her help desperately.
story: "Enna pazhaya pOO! Idhu vaanga koodadhu!" Amma screamed at the flower seller at the temple market. The old woman lowered her head. A week later, Amma forgot her purse at home. The same seller handed her a jasmine garland. "Eduthukonga, nalla vishayam." Amma's face went red. She paid double the next day. Meenakshi said, "Amma, mella pesina nalla irundha?"
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Indha pOO romba mosam! Vera eduthutu vaa!'
Flower Seller: 'Sari amma, sorry.'
Meenakshi: 'Amma, ava nallavadhu pannala? Namma katumaiya pesinom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits among the flower baskets, a single jasmine in his hand, smelling it gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Katumaiya pesina, adhu unnai kedukkum. Mella pesina, adhu unnai vaLakkum. Amma sonna maadhiri dhan — adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: கடுஞ்சொல்லன் கண்ணிலன் ஆயின் நெடுஞ்செல்வம் நீடின்றி ஆங்கே கெடும்.
english_translation: Whose word is harsh, whose sight is rude his wealth and power quickly fade.
moral: Harsh words and a rude face can destroy even the greatest wealth.

title: The Principal's Red Pen
theme: Absence of Terrorism / Cruelty Eats Power
hook: The new principal punishes the whole class for one student's mistake — but Sundar speaks up.
story: New principal made them stand in the sun for hours if someone talked. One day, a boy sneezed. "Ellarum bayala nillungai!" Two hours in heat. Sundar fainted. After recovering, he went to the principal's office. "Sir, neenga pannradhu dhan periya thappu. Nanga onnum pannala." The principal stared. Next week, punishments changed. The whole class thanked Sundar.
tamil_dialogues: Sundar: 'Sir, oruthan thappuku ellaraiyum nikkavekkradhu nyayama?'
Principal: 'Nee enakku edhiri da?'
Sundar: 'Illasir. Nyayam ketten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands outside the principal's office, waiting patiently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kadumaiya pannina, adhu unnai dhan theikkum. Sundar sonna maadhiri, nyayam ketta dhan adhu maari. Adhu dhan seer."
thirukkural: கடுமொழியும் கையிகந்த தண்டமும் வேந்தன் அடுமுரண் தேய்க்கும் அரம்.
english_translation: Reproofs rough and punishments rude like files conquering power corrode.
moral: Cruel words and excessive punishments eat away at your authority like a file on metal.

title: The Elder Brother's Anger
theme: Absence of Terrorism / Anger Without Counsel Ruins
hook: Raghu hits his little brother for spilling milk — Amma shows him that anger never fixes anything.
story: Little Vicky spilled milk all over Raghu's notebook. Raghu slapped him. Vicky cried for an hour. Amma sat Raghu down. "Un kovatha katti, avana adicha. Note book kedum. Milk pochu. Vicky aludhan. Onnum correct aagala." Raghu looked at his wet notebook and crying brother. Nothing was fixed. He hugged Vicky. "Sorry da." Vicky hugged back. Milk was already cleaned.
tamil_dialogues: Raghu: 'Poi da! En notebook keduthuta!'
Amma: 'Kovatha katti adicha, onnum sari aagala. Ellam dhan kedum.'
Raghu: 'Sorry da Vicky.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the dining table, a spilled glass of milk before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kovam dhan seyal kedukkum. Nee kovama irundha, onnum sari aagadhu. Amaithiya irundha dhan sari aagum."
thirukkural: இனத்தாற்றி எண்ணாத வேந்தன் சினத்தாற்றிச் சீறிற் சிறுகும் திரு.
english_translation: The king who would not take counsels rages with wrath — his fortune fails.
moral: Anger without listening to reason shrinks everything good in your life.

title: The Wall That Saved Us
theme: Absence of Terrorism / Preparation Prevents Fear
hook: Priya's father builds a small wall around their hut — neighbors laugh until the cyclone comes.
story: Every year, cyclone flooded their street. Appa said, "Indha varaiku sEthu kattuvaam." He built a small wall. Neighbors laughed. "Nalla irukku, aana cyclone-la edhu nikkum?" Priya felt embarrassed. Then the cyclone came. Water rose. Neighbors' huts flooded. Priya's house stayed dry. Neighbors knocked. Appa let them in. "Appa, neenga correct dhan!" Priya hugged him. Appa said, "Munna seyal — athu dhan kaapadhum."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Appa, ellaarum namma sEthu paiyan solraanga!'
Appa: 'Munna seyal pannina dhan bayam illa.'
Neighbor: 'Nee dhan correct da! Enga veetuku vandhuduvaama?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the wall, his hand on the solid brickwork, dry and safe.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Munna seyal pannina dhan bayam irkadhu. Adhu dhan sEthu. Bayatha vittu valiya — adhu dhan veeram."
thirukkural: செருவந்த போழ்திற் சிறைசெய்யா வேந்தன் வெருவந்து வெய்து கெடும்.
english_translation: The king who builds not fort betimes fears his foes in wars and dies.
moral: Prepare before danger comes — those who wait until the crisis are already defeated.

title: The Bully's Burden
theme: Absence of Terrorism / Cruelty Is a Burden on Earth
hook: Rajesh realizes the school bully is more burdened than anyone — and pity replaces fear.
story: Guna was the meanest boy in school. He pulled chairs, stole lunch, called names. Everyone hated him. One day, Rajesh saw Guna's father at the school gate — drunk, screaming at Guna. Guna stood silent, taking it. Paati said, "Paaru da. Avan kastam dhan avana katumaiya aakudhu." Rajesh never feared Guna again. He felt sorry. He left an extra dosa in Guna's bag.
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Paati, avan romba katumaiya irukkaan.'
Paati: 'Avanukku kastam irukku. Adhu dhan avan bayam.'
Rajesh: 'Naan avanuku dosa vechuten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between Guna and his father, a shield of light around the boy.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kadumai dhan avan bayam. Adhu dhan avan uda sOru. Aana avanuku irakkiya vena — avan kastam perusu."
thirukkural: கல்லார்ப் பிணிக்கும் கடுங்கோல் அதுவல்லது இல்லை நிலக்குப் பொறை.
english_translation: The crushing burden borne by earth is tyrants bound to fools uncouth.
moral: A cruel person is the heaviest burden on this earth — but even they need compassion.

title: The Shopkeeper's Kind Eyes
theme: Benignity / The World Lives by Kind Eyes
hook: Ananya notices that the sweet shop owner with the kindest eyes has the longest queue — and learns why.
story: Two sweet shops, side by side. One had a grumpy owner. The other, Kumar mama, always smiled. "Vaa ma, enna vEnum?" Ananya asked Appa, "Enna Kumar mama kitta ellarum varanga?" Appa said, "Avan kanga dhan ma — avar paarpadhu la dhan anbudhu irukku." Ananya watched. Every customer walked out happier than they walked in. One smile made all the difference.
tamil_dialogues: Ananya: 'Appa, Kumar mama shop-la dhan ellarum varanga!'
Appa: 'Avan kannula dhan anbudhu irukku. Adhu dhan vendum.'
Kumar Mama: 'Vaa ma, unakku special laddu!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Kumar mama at the shop, both smiling at customers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannula anbudhu irundha dhan ulagam irukku. Adhu illama, ulamae kedum. Kumar mama kannula anbudhu irukku."
thirukkural: கண்ணோட்டம் என்னும் கழிபெருங் காரிகை உண்மையான் உண்டிவ் வுலகு.
english_translation: Living in the world implies the bounteous dame of benign eyes.
moral: The world exists because of kindness in people's eyes — without it, nothing survives.

title: The New Girl's First Day
theme: Benignity / Without Kindness, People Are Just Burdens
hook: Swathi joins a new school mid-term — no one speaks to her until one girl shares her lunch.
story: Swathi stood at the class door. Everyone stared. Nobody said "hi." The teacher put her at the last bench. Lunch time came. She sat alone. Then Meera came over. "Enna kondu vandha? Illa na, namma share pannikalam." She opened her box — half parotta, half chicken curry. Swathi's eyes welled. "Romba thanks." Meera smiled. "Sandaikkum naanum puthusa vandhadhu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Swathi: 'Enakku yaarum pesa mattaanga...'
Meera: 'Paravalla. Naan irukkena. Saapdu.'
Swathi: 'Un kannula nalladhu irukku Meera.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits between the two girls at the lunch table, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannula nalladhu illana, adhu sErkkumai illa. Meera kannula nalladhu irukku — athu dhan avalai sErkkudhu."
thirukkural: கண்ணோட்டத் துள்ளது உலகியல் அஃதிலார் உண்மை நிலக்குப் பொறை.
english_translation: World lives by looks of lovely worth who lack them are burdens of earth.
moral: A kind look connects people; without it, a person is just another weight on the earth.

title: The Eyes That Lost Their Song
theme: Benignity / Eyes Without Kindness Are Useless
hook: Vignesh asks why his blind music teacher plays so beautifully — and learns real sight is in the heart.
story: Vignesh's veena teacher was blind. But he played like a river flowing. "Sir, unakku kaN theriyadhu, aana epdi ipdi vasippenga?" The teacher smiled. "Kannula dhan paakradhu illa da. Kan moodittu paakradhu dhan unmai paarvai." Vignesh closed his eyes while playing. The music was deeper. He opened his eyes. The world looked different now — kinder, softer.
tamil_dialogues: Vignesh: 'Sir, unga kaNNu theriyadhu, aana epdi ipdi vaasipenga?'
Teacher: 'Kann moodittu dhan unmaiyaana paarvai varum.'
Vignesh: 'Ippo dhan puriyudhu — kaN illama irundha dhan romba theriyudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the teacher, eyes closed, fingers on the veena strings.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "KaN irundha pOthum, adhu anbudhu illaama irundha, adhu kaN illadhadhO kadumai. Manasu kaN dhan periyadhu."
thirukkural: பண்என்னாம் பாடற்கு இயைபின்றேல் கண்என்னாம் கண்ணோட்டம் இல்லாத கண்.
english_translation: Of tuneless song what is the use? Without gracious looks what are eyes?
moral: Eyes without kindness are like a veena without a song — beautiful but useless.

title: The Grandma at the Signal
theme: Benignity / Eyes Are Only Decoration Without Grace
hook: Deepak sees a grandmother begging at the signal every day — and questions whether his eyes are even useful.
story: Every day, the same grandmother knocked on car windows. Deepak's friend said, "Paathiya, poyaava?" Deepak went silent. That night he told Amma. "Amma, naan avala daily paakran. Oru naalum onnum pannala. En kannu enakku enna use?" Amma hugged him. Next day, Deepak bought her idli and gave her his old chappals. The grandma cried. Deepak felt like his eyes worked for the first time.
tamil_dialogues: Deepak: 'Amma, avala paathum, naan onnum pannala. En kannuku enna use?'
Amma: 'Kannu paakradhukku dhan. Aana adhu paathu seyal pannanum.'
Grandma: 'Un kannula nalladhu irukku da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the signal, holding the grandma's hand, looking at Deepak.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu irukku, aana anbudhu illana, adhu poovu mathiri — azhaga irukku, aana manam illa. Nee seyal pannina, adhu dhan kannuku aNi."
thirukkural: உளபோல் முகத்தெவன் செய்யும் அளவினால் கண்ணோட்டம் இல்லாத கண்.
english_translation: Except that they are on the face, what for are eyes sans measured grace.
moral: Eyes on a face mean nothing if they don't move you to kindness.

title: The Wounded Sparrow
hook: Sandhya finds a sparrow with a hurt wing — Paati says even the bird knows kind eyes when it sees them.
story: A sparrow lay on the road, wing twisted. Sandhya picked it up. It didn't fly. It looked at her. "Paati, ithu enna paakudhu?" Paati said, "Un kannula anbudhu irukka nu paakudhu." Sandhya kept it in a box, fed it water. Three days later, the sparrow flew. Before leaving, it sat on Sandhya's finger for a whole minute, looking into her eyes. Sandhya cried.
tamil_dialogues: Sandhya: 'Paati, ithu en kannala dhan paakudhu!'
Paati: 'Anbudha paakradhu dhan kannuku aNi. Adhu unna nambudhu.'
Sandhya: 'Ithu dhan en kannuku aNi Paati.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the terrace, the sparrow on his finger, looking into Sandhya's eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannuku aNi dhan anbudhu. Adhu illa na, kannu pun dhan. Nee anbudha paatha, ulame anbudha paakkum."
thirukkural: கண்ணிற்கு அணிகலம் கண்ணோட்டம் அஃதின்றேல் புண்ணென்று உணரப்படும்.
english_translation: Kind looks are jewels for eyes to wear without them they are felt as sore.
moral: Kindness is the jewel of the eyes — without it, eyes are just wounds that see.

title: The Girl Who Didn't Blink
theme: Benignity / Ungracious People Are Like Dead Trees
hook: Janani's neighbor Muthu mama never looks at anyone — Amma says he's like a dead tree in the grove.
story: Muthu mama had a beautiful garden. But when kids said "Vanakkam," he walked past like they didn't exist. Janani asked, "Amma, Muthu mama enna pesa maataara?" Amma pointed at a dry tree in the grove. "Adu paaru — vairum irukku, ilai illa. Adhu dhan Muthu mama." Weeks later, Janani still said "Vanakkam" every day. One day, Muthu mama nodded. Just once. Janani ran inside. "Amma! Muthu mama kalai vituchu!"
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Muthu mama manushan dhaana? Enna pesa maataara?'
Amma: 'Vairum irukku, aana uyir illa — adhu marathuku samam.'
Janani: 'Amma! Muthu mama sirichuttaaru! Marathuku ilai varudhu!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the dry tree, watering its roots patiently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannula anbudhu illa na, maram dhan. Aana Janani anbudha irundha, adhu dhan andha marathukku thanni — maathi vidum."
thirukkural: மண்ணோடு இயைந்த மரத்தனையர் கண்ணோடு இயைந்துகண் ணோடாதவர்.
english_translation: Like trees on inert earth they grow who don't eye to eye kindness show.
moral: A person without a kind look is like a tree rooted in dead soil — alive but not living.

title: The Mirror That Told the Truth
theme: Benignity / Kind Eyes Make the Man
hook: Surya asks Appa why some people feel invisible — Appa shows him his own reflection and asks a hard question.
story: Surya complained, "Appa, school-la oru teacher irukkaaru. Avara paatha, avar enna kaN patta madhiri paakka maataar." Appa took him to the mirror. "Nee unna paakura maadhiri, andha teacher unna paakuraana?" Surya looked at his own eyes. They were tired, unfriendly. Appa said, "Kannula dhan visaesham. Nee mathavanga kannula enna paakranu pathu paar." Surya understood.
tamil_dialogues: Surya: 'Appa, teacher enna paakka maataar!'
Appa: 'Nee un kannala enna paakra? Anga dhan problem.'
Surya: 'Adhu... naan nalla paakala.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands behind Surya in the mirror, reflecting both their faces.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannula anbudhu illadhavan, avan kannae kedum. Aana anbudha paakradhu dhan avan kannuku uyir. Nee anbudha paaru."
thirukkural: கண்ணோட்டம் இல்லவர் கண்ணிலர் கண்ணுடையார் கண்ணோட்டம் இன்மையும் இல்.
english_translation: Ungracious men lack real eyes; men of real eyes show benign grace.
moral: A person without kind eyes is truly blind — and a person with kind eyes can never be ungracious.

title: The Guard Who Cared
hook: The school guard is the strictest man in school — until Michael sees him feed a lost puppy.
story: Sekhar uncle was the strictest guard. No running, no shouting, no loitering. Kids were scared. One stormy evening, Michael found him under a tree, feeding a wet puppy from his lunch box. "Uncle... neenga?" Sekhar uncle looked up. "Intha mOlu thanni la nanaichu kitirkudhu." He wrapped it in his old shirt. Michael ran home. "Amma! Sekhar uncle puppy kaapaththittu irundhaaru!" Amma smiled. "Kadumai dhan kadavul, karunai dhan manasu."
tamil_dialogues: Michael: 'Uncle, neenga dhan kids-a adikreenga! Aana puppy-a kaapathreengale!'
Sekhar: 'Adhu dhan da — kadumai vElam, karunai veRai vEra.'
Michael: 'Adhu dhan nyayamaana kaaval.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Sekhar uncle under the tree, the puppy sleeping on his lap.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kadumaiya irundha pOthum, karunai irukka vENum. Adhu dhan seer. Kadumai kodal, karunai uyir."
thirukkural: கருமம் சிதையாமல் கண்ணோட வல்லார்க்கு உரிமை உடைத்திவ் வுலகு.
english_translation: Who gracious are but dutiful have right for this earth beautiful.
moral: Those who are both kind and responsible truly deserve this beautiful world.

title: The Enemy Who Helped
hook: Shruthi's biggest rival tears her project accidentally — Shruthi helps her fix it minutes before judging.
story: Nandini was Shruthi's enemy. Always competing, always fighting. Before the science fair, Nandini accidentally knocked over Shruthi's volcano model. It broke. Nandini froze, expecting a scream. Shruthi's eyes burned. Then she breathed. "Sari... sErkka vendum." She handed Nandini glue. Together, they fixed it. Both projects won first prize together. Teacher said, "Nee dhan winner Shruthi — avanai mathiradhu periya veeram."
tamil_dialogues: Nandini: 'I'm sorry! I didn't mean to!'
Shruthi: 'Sari, thappu dhaan. Aana pathikalam. SErkka vendum. Vaa.'
Teacher: 'Shatruvai mathiradhu dhan periya veeram.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the two girls, holding the repaired volcano model.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unnoda edhiri kuda unakku kaN irundha dhan — avan kooda sErkka mudiyum. Adhu dhan periya veeram. Karunai dhan vellum."
thirukkural: ஒறுத்தாற்றும் பண்பினார் கண்ணும்கண் ணோடிப் பொறுத்தாற்றும் பண்பே தலை.
english_translation: To be benign and bear with foes who vex us is true virtue's phase.
moral: Forgiving and helping even an enemy is the highest form of kindness.

title: The Bitter Medicine Smile
hook: Arvind sees a little girl smile while getting an injection — and learns the meaning of true grace.
story: Arvind was terrified of injections. He cried before the nurse even touched him. Next bed, a girl his age — bald, thin, but eyes shining. She got her injection without a tear. She even smiled at the nurse. "Valikala?" Arvind asked. The girl said, "Valikkudhu. Aana ava nalla dhaan pannra. Naan alutha, avaluku manasu valikkum." Arvind was ashamed. He took his injection without crying.
tamil_dialogues: Arvind: 'Valikkudhu illa?'
Girl: 'Valikkudhu. Aana avanga nalla dhaan pannraanga. Naan siricha, avanga relax aaguvaanga.'
Arvind: 'Un kannula romba nalladhu irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the girl's hospital bed, holding her hand, both smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vali irundha pOthum, munnala sirikka mudiyum. Athu dhan periya veeram. Nallavadhu mathavangalukkaaga irukradhu dhan nalla nAgarigam."
thirukkural: பெயக்கண்டும் நஞ்சுண் டமைவர் நயத்தக்க நாகரிகம் வேண்டு பவர்.
english_translation: Men of graceful courtesy take hemlock and look cheerfully.
moral: True grace is smiling even when it hurts — for the sake of others' peace.

title: The Secret Eye
hook: Vicky learns that his police officer Appa uses two weapons — the law and secret informers.
story: Appa came home late. Vicky asked, "Appa, unakku theriyadha crime epdi therinjudhu?" Appa smiled. "Rendu kannu irukku, da. Oru — sattam. Innoru — otti. Rendumm sari illana, onnum puriyaadhu." Next day, Appa took Vicky to the station. A man in a lungi whispered something. Appa caught a thief in two hours. Vicky's eyes went wide.
tamil_dialogues: Vicky: 'Appa, avaru yaaru? Otti vaanginaar pola?'
Appa: 'Ama da. Otti illaama, sattam kooda kurudhu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the station entrance, one hand on a law book, one shading his eyes like a lookout.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru raajavukku rendu kannu venum — oru sattam, innoru otti. Adhu dhan avanukku theriyudhu. Nee Vicky, un kannu rendum therinjuna mathiri irukkanum."
thirukkural: ஒற்றும் உரைசான்ற நூலும் இவையிரண்டும் தெற்றென்க மன்னவன் கண்.
english_translation: A king should treat these two as eyes — The code of laws and careful spies.
moral: A good leader needs both the law and intelligence to see the truth.

title: The Bus Stop Watch
hook: Swetha notices Thatha knows everyone's business at the bus stand — he's always watching.
story: Every evening at the bus stand, Thatha knew who was fighting, who was sick, who was lying. "Thatha, unakku epdi ellam therium?" Thatha winked. "Naan parkaren. Yaaru enna pannraanga nu." A man tried to pick a woman's purse. Thatha cleared his throat. The man froze. Swetha realized: watching is a superpower.
tamil_dialogues: Swetha: 'Thatha, unakku epdi avaru thudikkira nu therium?'
Thatha: 'Paathu dhan. Kannu irundha podhum. Nee kooda paaru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a bus stand bench, reading a newspaper with holes cut for his eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ellam therinja varaikkum — ennadhu nadakkudhu, yaar yaaru — athu dhan aatchi. Nee paathu dhan therinjudha vendum."
thirukkural: எல்லார்க்கும் எல்லாம் நிகழ்பவை எஞ்ஞான்றும் வல்லறிதல் வேந்தன் தொழில்.
english_translation: All that happens, always, to all — The king should know in full detail.
moral: A wise leader or child knows what's happening around them at all times.

title: The Missing Lunch Box
hook: Karthik's lunch keeps disappearing — until he sets a clever trap with his friend.
story: Three days in a row, Karthik's lunch was gone. Teacher did nothing. "Yaaro dhan eduthutu poraan." Karthik and Ramesh set a plan. Karthik put a note inside the empty box: "Naan unna paathuten." Next day, a scared boy returned the box with a chocolate. Karthik realized: without investigation, problems never solve themselves.
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Ramesh, avan yaaru nu therinjudha venum. Illana apdiye poidum.'
Ramesh: 'Sari. Naan otti maadhiri parkaren. Nee trap vaikkara.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the classroom door, one finger on his lips, one finger pointing at the lunch box.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un lunch box pOna kooda, aaru eduthaan nu therinjudha vendum. Therinja dhan odhurikka mudiyum. Theriyadhadhu dhan periya azhivu."
thirukkural: ஒற்றினான் ஒற்றிப் பொருள்தெரியா மன்னவன் கொற்றங் கொளக்கிடந்தது இல்.
english_translation: Conquests are not for the monarch who cares not for the spy's remark.
moral: Without investigation, you cannot solve any problem or win any battle.

title: The Auto Uncle's Secret
hook: Divya's Appa questions not just the stranger but also the friend who seems too helpful.
story: At the railway station, a smiling auto driver offered help. "Bus illa pa, naan drop pannren." Appa smiled but asked three others the same route price. Divya whispered, "Appa, avar nallavadhu mathiri irukkaare." Appa said, "Nallaavadhu mathiri irundha dhan sandhegam. Ellaraiyum oru maadhiri dhan paakkanum." Turned out the auto was planning double fare.
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Appa, avar nallavara illa kevalama nu epdi theriyum?'
Appa: 'Ellaraiyum sari paathu dhan. Nallavan, kettavan nu onnum illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans against a station pillar, counting auto fares on his fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ellaraiyum ore maadhiri paakkanum — un veetukkaaran, un sathru. Ellarukkum ore kannu. Athu dhan otti."
thirukkural: வினைசெய்வார் தம்சுற்றம் வேண்டாதார் என்றாங்கு அனைவரையும் ஆராய்வது ஒற்று.
english_translation: His officers, kinsmen and foes — Who watch keenly are worthy spies.
moral: A true investigator judges everyone equally — friend, family, or stranger.

title: The Fearless Girl
hook: Pooja follows a chain snatcher alone because she's not afraid — and she knows how to blend in.
story: A chain was snatched from a woman at the market. Everyone froze. Pooja didn't. She took off her ribbon, opened her hair, bought a flower jasmine, and walked behind the thief like any innocent girl. "Akka, naan pathukuren." She noted his hideout, his bike number. Police caught him in an hour. Her Akka hugged her tight.
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Akka, avan enna pathu bayapada maatten. Naan kaiyoda irundhen.'
Akka: 'Unakku bayam illadha irukku. Adhu dhan un aayudham.'
