thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the market entrance, a jasmine flower in his hand, smiling knowingly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bayam illadha manasu, maari maari poga therinjadhu — adhu dhan nalla otti. Un kannu avan pathu bayapadaadha."
thirukkural: கடாஅ உருவொடு கண்ணஞ்சாது யாண்டும் உகாஅமை வல்லதே ஒற்று.
english_translation: Fearless gaze, suspectless guise — Guarding secrets mark the spies.
moral: A good observer is fearless and can blend in anywhere to uncover the truth.

title: The Holy Disguise
hook: Balu sees a beggar near the temple who doesn't beg — he watches. Amma reveals he's a detective.
story: A beggar sat near the gopuram every day. He never asked for money. He just watched. "Amma, avar enna kaathukittu irukkaar?" Amma whispered. "Avar police otti, da. Uruttu paarkaraanga." That evening, the beggar caught two chain snatchers. Balu was amazed. "Amma, avar sanyasi maadhiri irundhaaru!" Amma nodded. "Adhu dhan avan aayudham."
tamil_dialogues: Balu: 'Amma, avar yachikkala! Summa dhan irundhaaru!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan — avan sanyasi maadhiri irundhu dhan uthavi pannraan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the "beggar," both in plain white cloth, neither looking at each other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sanyasi maadhiri irundhu, uruttu parkradhu — adhu dhan otti. Yarum sandhegamaata. Adhu dhan avangoda aayudham."
thirukkural: துறந்தார் படிவத்தராகி இறந்தாராய்ந்து என் செயினும் சோர்விலது ஒற்று.
english_translation: Guised as monks they gather secrets, They betray them not under threats.
moral: The best detectives hide in plain sight, never breaking their cover.

title: The Secret Under the Bed
hook: Sanjay's new video game controller keeps breaking — he must spy to find the real cause.
story: Three controllers broken. "Amma, quality illa!" Sanjay blamed the shop. Anjali was quiet. Sanjay decided to watch. He hid behind the curtain. At midnight — Anjali crept out of bed, picked up the controller, and started dancing with it wildly. She tripped, it snapped. Sanjay jumped out. "Nee dhan! Sandheham illa inime!" Anjali cried. "Sorry anna."
tamil_dialogues: Sanjay: 'Neeye dhan mudichirukka! Inniku dhan therinjudhu!'
Anjali: 'Anna, naan dhan. Kalyanama kaekalanna.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the curtain, a broken controller in one hand, nodding.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee sandhehama irundha, naerla paaru. Adhu dhan unmai. Sandheham illadha dhan unmai theriyum."
thirukkural: மறைந்தவை கேட்கவற்றாகி அறிந்தவை ஐயப்பாடு இல்லதே ஒற்று.
english_translation: A spy draws out other's secrets Beyond a doubt he clears his facts.
moral: A true detective removes all doubt — only certainty matters.

title: Double Check
hook: Murali's friend says he saw a snake in the garden — but Murali checks again to be sure.
story: "Murali! Terrace la paambu!" his friend shouted. Murali grabbed a stick. But something made him pause. "Enakku oru sandaheham." He went to the neighbor's house. "Uncle, unga terrace la paambu irundha?" Uncle laughed. "Adhu dhan, naan potta kaiyodu kambu, da!" Murali's extra check saved a panic. His Akka smiled. "Renduthadava paathu dhan nambanum."
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Paambu dhan paatharu! Nambu da!'
Murali: 'Oru thadava dhan paathen. Vera yaaravadhu paathacha nu paarkalam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the garden, one hand shading his eyes, looking up twice.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru otti sonna mathiyadha. Innoru ottiya kooda kaekanum. Appo dhan unmai theriyum."
thirukkural: ஒற்றொற்றித் தந்த பொருளையும் மற்றுமோர் ஒற்றினால் ஒற்றிக் கொளல்.
english_translation: The reports given by one spy by another spy verify.
moral: Always double-check information — one source is never enough.

title: Three Friends, One Truth
hook: Gowri hears a rumor about her best friend — she asks three people separately to find the truth.
story: "Selvi unna pathi ketta sollira!" Gowri's heart sank. She didn't react. She found Selvi alone. "Selvi, nee enna pathi enna solra?" Selvi said, "Onnum illa." Then Meena. "Selvi unna pathi nalla dhan solra." Then Anitha. "Selvi unakku gift vaangittu irukka." All three agreed. The rumor was a lie. Gowri hugged Selvi. "Moonu perum ore maadhiri sonna dhan nambunen."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Selvi unna pathi nalla dhan solra da. Naan ketaen.'
Gowri: 'Moonu perum ore maadhiri sonna, appo dhan nambalaam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between three trees in the playground, listening to each child separately.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru vishayatha moonu per kitta kaekanum. Moonu perum ore maadhiri sonna, appo dhan unmai nu therinjudhum."
thirukkural: ஒற்றெற்ருணராமை ஆள்க உடன்மூவர் சொற்றொக்க தேறப்படும்.
english_translation: Engage the spies alone, apart — when three agree confirm report.
moral: When three separate sources say the same thing, you can trust it's true.

title: The Pencil That Betrayed
hook: Manoj offers his friend a chocolate to tell secrets — but the friend gets caught because of the chocolate.
story: "Ramesh, unakku chocolate kudukkaen. Yaaru class la glass utacha nu sollu." Ramesh took the chocolate. Teacher saw the wrapper. "Ramesh, yaaru thantha?" Ramesh pointed. "Manoj." Teacher called Appa. That night, Appa said, "Manoj, otti vaangradhu dhan thappu illa — aana adha therchik kudutha, adhu dhan thappu. Raajiyam vechu otti vaanga koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Manoj: 'Appa, naan chocolate thantha koodadha?'
Appa: 'Unakku theriyama adha kudutha koodadhu. Adhu dhan raajiyam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the dining table, a chocolate wrapper folded into a tiny bird.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un otti enna solliraan nu therchik kudutha, avan otti illa — avan kooli kaaran. Raajiyam kodukka koodadhu."
thirukkural: சிறப்பறிய ஒற்றின்கண் செய்யற்க செய்யின் புறப்படுத்தான் ஆகும் மறை.
english_translation: Give not the spy open reward — It would divulge the secret heard!
moral: Never reward a spy openly — it exposes the secret and the source.

title: The Last Bench Boy
hook: Kabilan thinks his classmate is poor because he wears old clothes — but Amma says real wealth is inside.
story: "Amma, raju kita nalla dress illa. Avan poor." Amma stopped cooking. "Kabilan, avanoda marks paathiya? Avan first rank. Adhu dhan avanoda real wealth, da. Dress ella porumai." Kabilan felt small. Next day, Raju shared his lunch with him. Kabilan realized: Raju's heart was richer than anyone. Energy and kindness — that's real wealth.
tamil_dialogues: Kabilan: 'Amma, avan kita onnum illa pola.'
Amma: 'Avan kita irukku — oomai. Oomai irundha, ellaam irukka maadhiri dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Raju on the last bench, a golden glow around the boy's heart.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oomai dhan unmaiyaana selvam. Kaasum, porulum pOga pOgum. Aana oomai irundha, adhu dhan nilaikkum."
thirukkural: உடையர் எனப்படுவது ஊக்கம் அஃதில்லார் உடையது உடையரோ மற்று.
english_translation: To own is to own energy — All others own but lethargy.
moral: True wealth is not money or clothes — it's the energy and drive inside you.

title: The Lost Remote
hook: Meena cries over a broken toy — Paati teaches her that heart-power never breaks.
story: Meena's favorite remote-control car broke. She sobbed for hours. "Paati, athu pOchchu!" Paati sat beside her. "Vechadhu pOchchu. Aana un ulladhu pOgala?" Meena sniffled. "Yen ulladhu?" Paati touched her chest. "Un oomai. Nee thappu pannitta kooda, marubaadi try panna oomai irukku. Adhu dhan unmai selavu." Meena built a paper boat and raced it instead. And smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Paati, adhu pOchchu. Enakku adhu mattum dhan irundhadhu!'
Paati: 'Pona pOga dhan. Aana un ulladhu pOgadhu. Adhu dhan unmai selavu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the floor, building a paper boat alongside Meena.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porul pOna, aana oomai irundha, adhu pOgadha. Adhu dhan nijamana selavu. Oomai irundha, mathadhu ellam varum."
thirukkural: உள்ளம் உடைமை உடைமை பொருளுடைமை நில்லாது நீங்கி விடும்.
english_translation: Psychic heart is wealth indeed — Worldly wealth departs in speed.
moral: Your courage and spirit are the only wealth that never leaves you.

title: The Broken Cycle
hook: Venba's cycle chain breaks on a hill — her friend complains but Venba refuses to give up.
story: The hill was steep. Chain snapped. Friend Priya sat down. "Aiyo! Namma lucky illa! Veezhndhu pochu!" Venba got off, examined the chain. She tied her dupatta around it. "Priya, ezhunda. Lucky illadhadhu dhan vazhi." She pushed the cycle uphill. At the top, she fixed it properly. Priya followed, ashamed. "Venba, nee epdi ipdi?" Venba smiled. "Complaint pannina edhu vida poradhu?"
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Namma lucky illada! Ellam fail dhan!'
Venba: 'Lucky illadha, namma pOm. Athu dhan periyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the hilltop, holding the dupatta-tied chain, wiping sweat.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Azhnjudhen, setthudhen nu solradhavanuku onnum kedaikaadhu. Oomai irundha, vazhi kedaikkum. Complaint illadhadhu dhan vellum."
thirukkural: ஆக்கம் இழந்தேமென்று அல்லாவார் ஊக்கம் ஒருவந்தம் கைத்துடையார்.
english_translation: The strong in will do not complain — The loss of worldly wealth and gain.
moral: Those with true energy never complain about loss — they just push forward.

title: Fortune Knocks
theme: Energy / Where Zeal Lives, Fortune Follows
hook: Dinesh wants a job to help his family — but everyone says no. He keeps trying and fortune finds him.
story: Dinesh's Appa was sick. He needed work. "Please sir, enakku onna vela kodunga." Ten shops, ten no's. He didn't stop. At the tea stall, Annachi was overwhelmed. "Naan oru aalu vecha dhan." Dinesh grabbed a cloth. "Sir, freeya try pannuren." He cleaned tables, served tea, counted change. By evening, Annachi hired him. "Un oomai dhan enna jeichudu, da."
tamil_dialogues: Dinesh: 'Sir, freeya try pannuren. Venumna kooda pannuven.'
Annachi: 'Illa da — unakku oomai irukku. Adhu dhan pOrum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands behind the tea stall, pouring a perfect cup of tea, steam rising like a question mark.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Edhu try pannittu irukka, adhu dhan un pakkam varum. Oomai irundha, udane dhan lucky varum."
thirukkural: ஆக்கம் அதர்வினாய்ச் செல்லும் அசைவிலா ஊக்க முடையானுழை.
english_translation: Fortune enquires, enters with boom — Where tireless strivers have their home.
moral: Fortune searches for the one who never stops trying — and settles where energy lives.

title: The Lotus Boy
hook: Teacher shows Harish a lotus in the pond — the deeper the water, the taller it grows.
story: Harish was sad he was shorter than everyone. "Sir, enna naan lamba valara maaten." Teacher took him to the temple pond. "Paaru da. Antha thamarai. Neer etlana, adhu atla dhan valarudhu. Nee kooda — un manasu atlam lam valara vendum." Harish understood. Instead of height, he grew in knowledge. By year-end, he was class topper. Height didn't matter.
tamil_dialogues: Harish: 'Sir, naan lamba valara maaten. Enna pannradhu?'
Teacher: 'Thamarai paathiya? Neer atlam dhan adhu valarudhu. Un manasatha vaala vendum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the pond edge, a lotus blooming beside him, taller than his head.
thirukkural: வெள்ளத்தனைய மலர்நீட்டம் மாந்தர்தம் உள்ளத்தனையது உயர்வு.
english_translation: Water depth is lotus height — Mental strength is men's merit.
moral: Like the lotus, your growth depends on your inner depth, not your outer appearance.

title: The Sky's the Limit
hook: Latha wants to enter the district science fair but fears she'll fail — her uncle says aim for the stars anyway.
story: "Chithappa, naan first varadha. Show panni vetikeda mudiyadhu." Chithappa pointed at the valley below. "Antha maratha paaru. Adhu vaanatha nokka dhan valarudhu. Kidaikkaama pona kooda, adhu valarudhu. Nee kooda — periya aasai vekka vendum." Latha entered. She didn't win first place. But her project got selected for state level. Failure wasn't failure at all.
tamil_dialogues: Latha: 'Chithappa, naan jikka maaten. Pannalama?'
Chithappa: 'Periya ninaippu vekka vendum. Kedaikala na kooda ninaippu perisaa irukkanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans against a pine tree at the viewpoint, pointing at the sky.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee ninaikira alavuku dhan nee valaruvai. Chinna ninaippu vecha, chinna dhan iruppai. Periya ninaippu — periya valarchi."
thirukkural: உள்ளுவதெல்லாம் உயர்வுள்ளல் மற்றது தள்ளினும் தள்ளாமை நீர்த்து.
english_translation: Let thoughts be always great and grand — Though they fail their virtues stand.
moral: Always think big. Even if you fall short, your ambition lifts you higher than those who never dared.

title: The Elephant's Lesson
hook: Vetri loses the wrestling match badly — Teacher shows him an elephant that stands firm even under spears.
story: Vetri lost the school wrestling final. In front of everyone. He wanted to cry. "Sir, naan fail." Teacher took him to the temple elephant. "Antha yaanai paaru. Avan mela ethana ambu varudhu? Aana avan ulayaadhu, da. Nee veezhndhutta, aana ezhundha dhan periyavana aava." Vetri went back to practice the same evening.
tamil_dialogues: Vetri: 'Sir, veezhndhutten. Ellar munnadi.'
Teacher: 'Yaanai veezhdha, adhana periya yaanai dhan. Ezhundha pOrathu dhan mukkiyam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the temple elephant, one hand on its trunk, both facing Vetri.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaanai mela ethana ambu varudhu? Aana adhu nilkudhu. Appo dhan née veezhndha pOdhilum ezhundhu nilkanum."
thirukkural: சிதைவிடத்து ஒல்கார் உரவோர் புதையம்பிற் பட்டுப்பாடு ஊன்றும் களிறு.
english_translation: Elephants are firm when arrows hit — Great minds keep fit ev'n in defeat.
moral: True strength is not in never falling — but in standing firm even after the arrows hit.

title: The Empty Bounty
hook: Karthik boasts about being generous but never shares his snacks — Priya calls him out.
story: "Naan romba generous, da! Naa periya daanam pannuven!" Karthik said loudly. But when Seema asked for a bite of his chips, he said, "Saapittuten, illa." Priya stood up. "Karthik, un vayila dhan 'generous' irukku. Ulladhu illa." The class laughed. Karthik's face burned. He realized: talking about charity without doing it is empty noise.
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Née sollradhu ellam vayiru da. Seiyal illa.'
Karthik: 'Enaku... enaku theriyala. Naan dhan thappu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the classroom door, holding an empty bowl.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naan periya daanam pannuven nu sollitu, onnum pannadhadhu — adhu vayila maatdham. Seiyal illadhadhu, sollu kooda porumai."
thirukkural: உள்ளம் இலாதவர் எய்தார் உலகத்து வள்ளியம் என்னும் செருக்கு.
english_translation: Heartless persons cannot boast "We are liberal to our best."
moral: You cannot claim to be generous if your heart doesn't match your words.

title: The Tiger Heart
hook: Chinna is tiny but fights off a stray dog that threatens his sister — true strength isn't size.
story: A big stray dog growled at his little sister. She screamed. Chinna was half the dog's size. His legs shook. But he picked up a stick, stepped forward. "PO! PO DA!" The dog backed off. Amma ran out. "Chinna, nee yaanai dhan da. Siz illa — manasu dhan." Chinna hugged his sister. "Naan periyavan illa? Manasula dhan periyavan."
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Chinna, nee romba chinna, aana un manasu perisaa irundhadhu.'
Chinna: 'Size illa Amma. Ulladhu dhan matter.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a village stone, a tiger cub sitting beside him — both small but fierce.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaanai perisaa irundha pOdhum, puli munnadi bayapadudhu. Karanam — puli ku manasu perisaa irukku. Appadi dhan née."
thirukkural: பரியது கூர்ங்கோட்டது ஆயினும் யானை சிறியது புலிதாக்குறின்.
english_translation: Huge elephant sharp in tusk quails — When tiger, less in form, assails.
moral: Size doesn't matter. What matters is the courage in your heart.

title: The Wooden Man
hook: Appa shows Mahesh a wooden statue and a real man — and asks him to spot the difference.
story: "Mahesh, idhu yaaru?" Appa pointed at a wooden statue. "Marathula panna aalu." Then at a real man. "Idhu dhan manushan. Difference enna?" Mahesh thought. "Oru uyir...?" Appa nodded. "Aana un manasula oomai illadhadhu. Nee veyala panna maata, madiya irukka. Appo nee maram maadhiri dhan. Oomai irundhavan dhan manushan." Mahesh got up and started his homework.
tamil_dialogues: Mahesh: 'Appa, rendum ore maadhiri irukku?'
Appa: 'Illada — un ulladhu irukka, adhu dhan manushan. Illadhadhu dhan maram.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the wooden statue and Mahesh, touching the boy's chest where the heart beats.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oomai illadhavan dhan maram. Manusan aavadhadhu avan ulladhu. Adhu dhan manushanai manushan aakudhu."
thirukkural: உரமொருவற்கு உள்ள வெறுக்கை அஃதில்லார் மரம்மக்களாதலே வேறு.
english_translation: Mental courage is true manhood — Lacking that man is like a wood.
moral: Without inner energy and drive, a person is just a wooden statue — heart makes us human.

title: The Dying Lamp
theme: Unslothfulness / Sloth Kills the Family Name
hook: Siva sees Paati lighting the lamp and realizes their house has become dark because his father stopped trying.
story: Paati lit the lamp. Siva noticed — only one lamp now. "Paati, munnadi moonu vilakku irundhadhu." Paati's eyes filled. "Unga appa vela panna maata. Kudumbam irundha, adhu oLikka oomai venum. Oomai illadhadhu poidum." Siva felt something break inside. That night, he told Appa, "Appa, naan school la first varuven. Paati vilakku kaapathuven." Appa cried.
tamil_dialogues: Siva: 'Paati, moonu vilakkula onnu dhan eriyudhu?'
Paati: 'Kudumbam oLikka oomai venum pOla Siva. Madi ku oLikka mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the lamp, its flame flickering but growing as he cups his hands around it.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Madiya irundha, kudumbam oLikkaadhu. Oomai dhan adha oLikkum. Nee dhan oomai — kudumbathai oLikka vendum."
thirukkural: குடியென்னும் குன்றா விளக்கம் மடியென்னும் மாசூர மாய்ந்து கெடும்.
english_translation: Quenchless lamp of ancestry goes — When foul idleness encloses.
moral: A family's honor is a lamp that only hard work keeps burning — laziness extinguishes it.

title: The Lazy King's Crown
theme: Unslothfulness / Hate Sloth Like You Hate Poverty
hook: Rajiv sees the king's throne and asks why the great Chola kings never sat idle — Teacher explains.
story: "Sir, raaja dhan, avanuku vela enna? Nalla dhan irundhurukaalam?" Teacher laughed. "Raja irundha, adhu periya vela. Thoongina, naadu kedum. Madiya irundha, kudumbam poidum. Raja vanthu elaikkaranai vida kashtam." Rajiv looked at the empty throne. "Appo naanum?" Teacher nodded. "Nee un vida — nee un kudumbathin raja. Madi illadha iru."
tamil_dialogues: Rajiv: 'Sir, raajakku vela illa pola?'
Teacher: 'Raja dhan periya velai. Madiya irundha, raajyamum, kudumbamum kedum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the museum throne, a crown on his head, polishing the armrest with a cloth.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Madi — adhu dhan ethellam azhikkum. Adha vida vendum. Jeevana azhikkura madiya vida vendum."
thirukkural: மடியை மடியா ஒழுகல் குடியைக் குடியாக வேண்டுபவர்.
english_translation: To make your home an ideal home — Loath sloth as sloth; refuse it room.
moral: If you want your family to thrive, hate laziness as much as you would hate ruin.

title: The Fool Who Slept
hook: Anand keeps delaying his homework. Appa shows him a shopkeeper who slept through his own business dying.
story: Every evening: "Appa, aprom pannren." Anand never did homework. Appa took him to Muthu uncle's shop. Closed. Cobwebs. "Muthu uncle epdi?" Appa said, "Madi dhan avanai konjudhu. Saavadhu varadha munnadiye avanai mudichudhu." Anand felt cold fear. He ran home and finished his homework before dinner. "Appa, naan inime delay panna maatten."
tamil_dialogues: Anand: 'Uncle shop enna achu?'
Appa: 'Madi dhan da. Madi varadha munnadiye manushanai mudichidum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands before the closed shop, a spiderweb glistening across the shutter.
thirukkural: மடிமடிக் கொண்டொழுகும் பேதை பிறந்த குடிமடியும் தன்னினும் முந்து.
english_translation: The fool who fosters sluggishness — Before he dies ruins his house.
moral: Laziness destroys your family and your future even before death comes for you.

title: The Weeds of Sloth
hook: Nandini's beautiful garden grows weeds because she stopped watering it — her mother shows the parallel.
story: Nandini planted rose seeds. For a week, she watered. Then she forgot. "Amma, aprom paathukaren." Two weeks later — weeds, dried soil, no roses. Nandini cried. Amma said, "Nee mattum dhan illa. Vela vittudra ellorum kooda. Kudi kudumbam kooda inga dhan kedum. Nalla irukka vayai paathukka vendum." Nandini replanted that day.
tamil_dialogues: Nandini: 'Amma, en rose ella sethudhu! Naan marandhutten.'
Amma: 'Kudumbamum apdi dhan da. Paathukka vitudna, kedum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels in the garden, pulling out weeds with both hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Madiya irundha, bayangara aagum. Kudi kudumbam kedum. Nalla irukka, velai pannittu irukkanum."
thirukkural: குடிமடிந்து குற்றம் பெருகும் மடிமடிந்து மாண்ட உஞற்றிலவர்க்கு.
english_translation: Who strive not high, sunk deep in sloth — Ruin their house by evil growth.
moral: Neglect and laziness let weeds grow — in a garden, in a family, in a life.

title: The Four Thieves
hook: Paati tells Bala about four thieves who steal a person's future — and Bala realizes he has all four.
story: "Bala, naalaghu thirudan irukka. Unakku theriyumaa?" Bala shook his head. "Onnu — vela neekkradhu. Rend — marandhu podradhu. Moonu — madi. Nal — thoongal." Bala's face fell. He did all four. "Paati, naan dhan thirudana?" Paati hugged him. "Illada. Nee inime therinjudha. Thirudanai viratta." Bala made a chart: no delaying, no forgetting, no idle sitting, no oversleeping.
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Paati, naalaghu perum... avanga enna thiruduraanga?'
Paati: 'Un jeevan, da. Nee madiya irundha, avanga eduthutu pOguraanga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar draws four lines in the dust with a stick, then erases three.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vela neekkradhu, marakradhu, madi, thoongal — naalaghu thirudan. Avanga un aayusai thiruduraanga."
thirukkural: நெடுநீர் மறவி மடிதுயில் நான்கும் கெடுநீரார் காமக்கலன்.
english_translation: To lag, forget, idle and doze — These four are pleasure boats of loss.
moral: Delaying, forgetting, idleness, oversleeping — four thieves that steal your life.

title: The Rich Idler
hook: Suresh envies a rich boy who has everything — until he sees the boy can't even tie his own shoes.
story: Guna had the newest shoes, biggest house, latest phone. Suresh was jealous. One day, Guna's shoelace came undone. He stood helpless. "Anna, kattidu." Guna didn't know how. Suresh tied it for him. He realized: all that money, and Guna couldn't dress himself. That night, Suresh thanked Amma for teaching him to be independent. Rich isn't always capable.
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Unakku kattadha theriyadha?'
Guna: 'Amma dhan kattuvaanga. Naan pannadhadhu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the compound wall, both his own sandals perfectly tied.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porul irundha pOdhum, madi irundha, adhu pOrumai. Nee seyal panna therinjudha venum. Porul seyalai vaanga maatadhu."
thirukkural: படியுடையார் பற்றமைந்தக் கண்ணும் மடியுடையார் மாண்பயன் எய்தல் அரிது.
english_translation: With all the wealth of lords of earth — The slothful gain nothing of worth.
moral: All the riches in the world are useless if laziness has made you incapable.

title: The Laughing Boys
hook: Mani doesn't do his project — the whole class laughs when Teacher asks. Amma teaches him why.
story: "Mani, un project?" Mani's hands were empty. He'd watched TV instead. The class snickered. "Mani sleepy head!" "Mani lazy!" Mani wanted to disappear. At home, Amma said, "Avanga sirikka dhan pannuvaanga. Nee madiya irundhadhu nijam. Sirippa vitra, vela pannu." Mani stayed up all night. Next day, he submitted the best late project. The class stopped laughing.
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Amma, evanum sirikka pannittu irukka. Enaku kasta irukku.'
Amma: 'Adhuku kaaranam nee dhan. Inime sirikkama iruka, vela pannu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the back of the classroom, a completed project in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Madiya irundha, mathavanga siripanga. Adhu nijam. Anntha sirippa vitra, vela pannanum."
thirukkural: இடிபுரிந்து எள்ளுஞ்சொல் கேட்பர் மடிபுரிந்து மாண்ட உஞற்றிலவர்.
english_translation: The slothful lacking noble deeds — Subject themselves to scornful words.
moral: If you don't do your work, the world will laugh at you — and they'll be right.

title: The Slave Boy
theme: Unslothfulness / Laziness Makes You a Slave
hook: Appa tells Kumar that laziness makes you a slave to those who work hard — and shows him real slaves.
story: At Marina, Kumar watched coolies carry heavy loads. "Appa, avanga kashtapadraanga." Appa said, "Avanga velai pannittu dhan saapdraanga. Aana nee? Vela pannadhadhu na, un sathru unna adima aakidum." Kumar thought about Ramesh — the class bully who took his snacks. But Ramesh studied hard. Kumar realized: Ramesh studied, so he won. Laziness is true slavery.
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Appa, adimai na adhu oru kaalam dhan.'
Appa: 'Illada. Madiya irundha, yaaravadhu un mela aatchi pannuvaanga. Adhu dhan adimai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the Marina sand, drawing a line in the sand — on one side, "slave"; on the other, "free."
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee madiya irundha, un sathru un mela aatchi pannuvaan. Velai pannu — nee suthandhiran."
thirukkural: மடிமை குடிமைக்கண் தங்கின்தன் ஒன்னார்க்கு அடிமை புகுத்தி விடும்.
english_translation: If sloth invades a noble house — It will become a slave of foes.
moral: Laziness makes you a slave to those who work hard — stay active to stay free.

title: The Turning Point
hook: Ravi is the laziest boy in school until Headmaster gives him a chance to prove he can change.
story: "Ravi — homework? illa. Project? illa. Neenga enna pannuveenga?" Headmaster asked. Ravi looked down. "Sir, oru chance kodunga. Naan maari dhan." Headmaster made him the class attendance monitor. Every day, Ravi had to come on time, mark names. The responsibility changed him. By year end, he was completing homework. "Ravi, nee maari irukka." Ravi smiled. "Madiya vida dhan mariyadhai."
tamil_dialogues: Ravi: 'Sir, naan maari dhan. Nambungalen.'
Headmaster: 'Poradhu da. Madiya vitra, nee yen aaganum nu neeye pathuko.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, holding open the door for Ravi.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Madiya vida dhan maariyadhai. Nee unna maathitu. Adhu dhan kudumbathai maathum."
thirukkural: குடியாண்மை உள்வந்த குற்றம் ஒருவன் மடியாண்மை மாற்றக்கெடும்.
english_translation: The blots on race and rule shall cease — When one from sloth gets his release.
moral: All the faults in a family or a person begin to disappear the moment laziness is let go.

title: The Slothless King
theme: Unslothfulness / The Power of Hard Work
hook: Thatha tells Arul about the king who never slept more than four hours — Arul tries to be like him.
story: "Thatha, indha palace enna?" "Thirumalai Nayakkar palace. Avan oru periya raja. Avaru naaluku naladhu mani mathiram thoonguvaaraam. Madi — avarukku adhu oru sathru. Adhu dhan avanai perisaa aakichu." Arul tried it. Woke up at 5 AM. Studied. Played. Won the elocution prize. "Thatha, naan thoongala. Madiya vittuten." Thatha hugged him. "Nee dhan raja."
tamil_dialogues: Arul: 'Thatha, avan thoongama irundha, apdi dhan periyavana aanaara?'
Thatha: 'Ama da. Madi illadha dhan jeevikkalam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the palace courtyard, pointing at the sky at sunrise.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Madi illadha raja dhan periya aatchi seyvaan. Nee kooda — madi illadha dhan raaja aavuai. Un aatchi un jeevan."
thirukkural: மடியிலா மன்னவன் எய்தும் அடியளந்தான் தாயதெல்லாம் ஒருங்கு.
english_translation: The slothless king shall gain en masse — All regions trod by Lord apace.
moral: A ruler — or a child — who rejects laziness can achieve as much as the gods themselves.

title: Gopi's Broken Cycle
hook: Gopi's bicycle chain snaps on the way to school — Appa won't fix it for him.
story: Gopi was halfway down Mela Theru when his cycle chain jammed. He pushed it home, sweating. "Appa, chain poiduchu. Nee fix panni kudu!" Appa was reading the paper. "Enna da, un cycle nee dhan fix pannanum. Naan panna maaten." Gopi was angry. He threw his bag down. Then he found Appa's toolkit, watched a YouTube video, and spent two hours fixing it himself. Grease on his face, he finally got the chain on. He rode around the street, beaming. Appa watched from the window, smiling.
tamil_dialogues: Gopi: 'Appa, chain poiduchu. Nee fix panni kudu pls!'
Appa: 'Nee dhan pannanum da. Kashtam nu feel pannadha. Muyarchi pannu.'
Gopi: 'Sari Appa. Naan dhan pannren!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the compound wall, holding a rusty sprocket in his hand, watching Gopi work.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Gopi, 'kashtam' nu ninaichu vittuta koodadhu. Muyarchi pannitta dhan vellum varum. Un cycle chain maadhiri dhan vaazhkai — nee dhan sari pannanum."
thirukkural: அருமை உடைத்தென்று அசாவாமை வேண்டும் பெருமை முயற்சி தரும்.
english_translation: Feel not frustrate saying This hard Who tries attains striving's reward
moral: Don't give up when something is hard — effort itself brings greatness.

title: The Half-Painted Fence
theme: Manly Effort / Completing What You Start
hook: Kathir offers to paint the fence but leaves half of it unfinished — and the cows escape.
story: Thatha asked Kathir to paint the backyard fence. Kathir started enthusiastically. Halfway through, he got bored. "Seri, naaliku pannren." He left the brush in the can. That night, the unpainted part of the fence gave way. Three cows wandered onto the main road. Kathir and Thatha ran after them in the dark for an hour. After they caught them, Thatha said nothing. Just handed Kathir the brush. Kathir finished the whole fence that night — properly.
tamil_dialogues: Kathir: 'Thatha, bore adichiduchu. Naaliku pannren.'
Thatha: 'Vela naduvula niruthra koodadhu da. Adhu dhan periya alatchinam.'
Kathir: 'Seri Thatha... ippo dhan puriyudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans against the freshly painted fence, a brush in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vela mudiya varaikkum pannanum. Naduvula vittuta — adhu dhan thappu. Unakku theriyuma, ulagam avan vela mudiyaadhavanai vittu vidaadhu."
thirukkural: வினைக்கண் வினைகெடல் ஓம்பல் வினைக்குறை தீர்ந்தாரின் தீர்ந்தன்று உலகு.
english_translation: In doing work don't break and shirk The world will quit who quits his work
moral: If you leave work unfinished, the world will leave you behind too.

title: Pooja's Lemonade Flood
hook: Pooja starts a lemonade stall to buy a doll — but earns so much she can't keep it all.
story: Pooja wanted a big doll from the shop. Amma said, "Nee sambadichu vaangi kodu." So Pooja set up a lemonade stall. She squeezed lemons, added sugar, and sold. First day: 50 rupees. Second: 120. She was rich! But then she saw three street children watching her stall, thirsty. She poured them free glasses. Then bought them each a lemonade. By evening, she had only 20 rupees left. But the children hugged her. "Amma, ivanga romba santhosama irukanga!"
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Amma, 120 rupees sambadichen doll ku!'
Amma: 'Adha vera edhukku use panna pora?'
Pooja: 'Anga street pasanga ku lemonade kuduthen. Avanga kaetu kaetu irundhanga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under Pooja's stall, a half-empty lemonade glass in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un kaiyal dhan unakku porumai varum. Atha mathaavangalukku kudukkum podhu dhan adhu perusu aagum. Muyarchi pannravan dhan kodukkalaam."
thirukkural: தாளாண்மை என்னும் தகைமைக்கண் தங்கிற்றே வேளாண்மை என்னுஞ் செருக்கு.
english_translation: On excellence of industry depends magnanimous bounty
moral: Hard work is the foundation of generosity — only those who strive can truly give.

title: Bava The Bungler
hook: Bava wants to buy everyone gifts at the fair but has no money because he never works.
story: At the Kovilpatti fair, Periyappa bought murukku for everyone. Bava's cousins got bangles, balloons, toys. Bava had nothing to give. "Periyappa, naanum vaangi kudukanum!" Periyappa said, "Enna kaiyala? Un kaiyala sambadichadhae illaye?" Bava felt small. That summer, he started helping at the tea stall. By Deepavali, he had saved 500 rupees. He bought small gifts for everyone — including Periyappa. Periyappa's eyes welled up.
tamil_dialogues: Bava: 'Periyappa, enakku gift vaangi kuduka kasu illa!'
Periyappa: 'Muyarchi pannama, udhavi panna mudiyadhu da. Adhu dhan unmai.'
Bava: 'Irudha Periyappa, naan ippo sambadichen! Idhu unakku!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the fair, holding a bright balloon in one hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaiyala sambadichavanga dhan perusa kodukka mudiyum. Summa irundha, enna kudukka? Muyarchi dhan perusa aakkum."
thirukkural: தாளாண்மை இல்லாதான் வேளாண்மை பேடிகை வாளாண்மை போலக் கெடும்.
english_translation: Bounty of man who never strives Like sword in eunuch's hand it fails
moral: Generosity without effort is like a sword in a weak hand — useless.

title: The Pillar of Pallavan Colony
theme: Manly Effort / Hard Work Supports Others
hook: Muthu skips work to play cricket — and his little sister goes hungry.
story: Muthu's Amma worked double shifts. Muthu had to pick up vegetables on his way home. But one day, his friends called for cricket. He played. Forgot everything. When he reached home, his 5-year-old sister was crying of hunger. "Anna, pasiya irukku." Amma wasn't home yet. Muthu ran to the shop, bought idli batter, made dosa. He fed her. That night he told Amma, "Naan ippo dhan purinjichen. Naa velai pannala na, avalukku pasi. Naan dhan ella."
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Va da Muthu, cricket aada!'
Muthu: 'Illada, en thangachi ku saapadu vangi dhan aaganum.'
Friend: 'Approm paathuko.'
Muthu: 'Naa illana, avanuku pasi da. Naan dhan support.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the kitchen, watching Muthu make dosa for his sister.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Muthu, un kaiyal dhan un kudumbam nilkkudhu. Vela pannravan dhan mathavangalukku thunai. Nee dhan thoon."
thirukkural: இன்பம் விழையான் வினைவிழைவான் தன்கேளிர் துன்பம் துடைத்தூன்றும் தூண்.
english_translation: Work who likes and not pleasure Wipes grief of friends, pillar secure
moral: One who values work over pleasure becomes the pillar that holds up their family.

title: The Lazy King's Ants
hook: Selvi watches ants work all day while a grasshopper plays — Thatha ties it to human life.
story: Selvi lay on the grass, bored. "Thatha, enna pannradhu? Somberi-a irukku." Thatha pointed to ants carrying food. "Paaru, ithu dhan unakku school." Then a grasshopper jumped by. "Adha paaru Thatha! Aadudhu!" Thatha smiled. "Adhu aadalam. Aana mazhai vanthudum. Appo adhu enna pannum? Ants dhan ready." Selvi got up. She went inside, opened her textbook. Thatha smiled. "Unakku puriyudha?"
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'Thatha, erumbu romba kashtapadudhu. Aana vizhundhu vilayaadale.'
Thatha: 'Adhu dhan — erumbu dhan jama pannum. Vizhundhu azhivadhu.'
Selvi: 'Naanum erumbu maadhiri irukanum Thatha?'
Thatha: 'Ama. Adhu dhan nalla vaazhkai kanakkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on an anthill, ants crawling peacefully over his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Erumbu velai pannudhu. Vizhundhu vilayaadudhu. Aana mazhai vanthudum. Appo dhan theriyum yaar jama pannitanu. Muyarchi dhan selva aakkum."
thirukkural: முயற்சி திருவினை ஆக்கும் முயற்றின்மை இன்மை புகுத்தி விடும்.
english_translation: Industry adds prosperity Indolence brings but poverty
moral: Hard work brings wealth; laziness brings nothing but poverty.

title: Lakshmi's Gold Bangles
theme: Manly Effort / Fortune Favors the Hardworking
hook: Lakshmi envies the rich girl's gold bangles — but learns they came from her own mother's weaving.
story: Lakshmi's classmate came with shining gold bangles. "En amma vaangi kudutha!" Lakshmi felt sad. "Paati, enakku enna varaadhu?" Paati took her to the loom. "Un amma dhan adha weave panna." Lakshmi saw her mother's blistered fingers weaving silk 12 hours a day. "Amma kaalum valikkudhu, kaiyum valikkudhu. Aana ava nirutha maata." Lakshmi hugged her mother. "Naan kadaiku velai panna poren Amma — un kai eduthu vekren."
tamil_dialogues: Lakshmi: 'Paati, avaluku gold irukku. Enakku enna illa?'
Paati: 'Un amma gold kuda vaangi kudukalama? Aana ava silver unakku kodukka ennama irukka?'
Lakshmi: 'Unmai dhan Paati. Amma kai dhan en gold.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the loom, weaving a golden thread into the silk.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Lakshmi, un amma kai dhan gold. Muyarchi pannravan kaiyila dhan selva irukku. Somberi kitta pooja irunthalum, athu nilkkaadhu."
thirukkural: மடியுளாள் மாமுகடி என்ப மடியிலான் தாளுளான் தாமரையி னாள்.
english_translation: Illuck abides with sloth they say Laxmi's gifts with labourers stay
moral: Laziness attracts misfortune; hard work attracts wealth.

title: The Failed Test
theme: Manly Effort / Trying is What Matters
hook: Arjun fails his math test and feels like a failure — but Appa shows him the real failure is not trying.
story: Arjun got 23/100 in math. He hid the paper. When Appa found it, Arjun cried. "Appa, naan loser. Enna mudiyala." Appa sat beside him. "Un friend Kavi evlo vanguna?" "Hundred." "Avan try pannittu dhan eduthan. Nee try panniya?" Arjun was quiet. He hadn't studied. Appa said, "Tholadhu da. Try pannadhadhu dhan tholvi. Muyarchi pannu." The next test: Arjun studied two weeks straight. He got 67. Not a hundred. But Appa hugged him. "Pathiya? Muyarchi panna dhan vellum."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Appa, naan fail aitten. Enna mudiyala.'
Appa: 'Unna mudiyadhadhu thappu illa da. Muyarchi pannadhadhu dhan thappu.'
Arjun: 'Seri Appa. Naan try pannren!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the study table, an old Tamil book open before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Tholvi varum. Adhu bayam illa. Muyarchi pannama irukradhu dhan periya tholvi. Muyarchi pannitta — athu dhan vellum."
thirukkural: பொறியின்மை யார்க்கும் பழியன்று அறிவறிந்து ஆள்வினை இன்மை பழி.
english_translation: Misfortune is disgrace to none The shame is nothing learnt or done
moral: Failing is not shameful — not trying is the real disgrace.

title: The Rainy Day Cart
theme: Manly Effort / Effort Pays Despite Odds
hook: Kumar's Amma is sick and can't sell her flowers — so 12-year-old Kumar pushes the cart himself in pouring rain.
story: Amma lay in bed with fever. The flower cart sat loaded. "Naaliku vikkalaam." But Kumar knew — wilted flowers sell for nothing. He grabbed the cart handle. Rain poured. Mud splashed. He pushed two kilometers to the market. People laughed at the wet boy with drooping jasmine. But a wedding party needed garlands. They bought everything. Kumar came home with 800 rupees. Amma cried. "Unaku kashtam theriyala da?" Kumar grinned. "Kashtam vantha dhan sambadikalaam Amma."
tamil_dialogues: Neighbour: 'Mazhaila pova? Poi sethu varuva da.'
Kumar: 'Pona dhan theriyum. Naa try panniten. Adhu dhan porum.'
Amma: 'Deivam un kaiyila irukku da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the rain, a single jasmine garland around his neck, dripping wet but smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mazhai vantha, velai nirutha koodadhu. Kadavul seiya mudiyadhu nu ninaichalum, muyarchi dhan kooli tharum. Kumar pathiya?"
thirukkural: தெய்வத்தான் ஆகா தெனினும் முயற்சிதன் மெய்வருத்தக் கூலி தரும்.
english_translation: Though fate is against fulfilment Hard labour has ready payment
moral: Even if fate seems against you, hard work always pays its own wage.

title: The Fate-Breaker
hook: A fortune-teller says Jyothi will never be a doctor — she decides to prove the stars wrong.
story: The village fortune-teller looked at Jyothi's palm. "Unakku padipu illa. Kalyanam dhan." Jyothi's heart sank. But her teacher said, "Nambadha. Un kaiyala dhan un vidhi ezhudha mudiyum." Jyothi started studying at 5 AM every day. No breaks. No excuses. When she failed her 10th boards, the fortune-teller smirked. She rewrote — passed. When she got into college, the whole village cheered. She became a nurse. Not a doctor, but close enough. She went to the fortune-teller: "Paaru. Naan dhan en vidhiya maatiten."
tamil_dialogues: Fortune-teller: 'Un kaiyala padipu illa da.'
Jyothi: 'Naan maatitu kaatren.'
Teacher: 'Adhu dhan — kaiyala dhan vidhiyai jeyikalaam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the classroom, pointing at a chalkboard that reads "ஊழையும் உப்பக்கம் காண்பர்".
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vidhi irukku. Aana adha jeyikradhu dhan namma kaiyala irukku. Muyarchi pannravan, vidhiya kuda vilakka mudiyum."
thirukkural: ஊழையும் உப்பக்கம் காண்பர் உலைவின்றித் தாழாது உஞற்று பவர்.
english_translation: Tireless Toiler's striving hand Shall leave even the fate behind
moral: The tireless worker can leave even fate itself behind.

title: The Smashing Pongal Pot
theme: Not Yielding to Adversity / Laugh at Troubles
hook: Anbu's Pongal pot cracks and spills everywhere — Amma laughs instead of crying.
story: Pongal morning. The pot was boiling over. Anbu ran to lift it — slip! The pot crashed. Rice, milk, jaggery flooded the floor. Anbu's eyes filled with tears. "Amma, ellam kedaichiduchu!" Amma looked at the mess. She looked at Anbu's panicked face. And she laughed. Full-bellied laughter. "Paaru da, Pongal namakku sutha floor la vilayadudhu!" She scooped up the clean parts. "Nee kashtam paathu kara radhu illa da, sirichu eduthuko. Appo dhan adhu sariyagum." Anbu hesitantly smiled. Then laughed too.
tamil_dialogues: Anbu: 'Amma, Pongal kedaichiduchu! Naan enna pannadhu?'
Amma: 'Sirichu eduthuko da. Kashtam varum podhu adha eduthu sirikradhu dhan adha velikka vazhi.'
Anbu: 'Sarithan Amma. Nallavena floor Pongal?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the kitchen doorway, a cracked pot beside him, laughing silently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anbu, kashtam varum podhu karalaam. Aana sirichu edutha — adhu dhan velikkum. Nakkum. Athu dhan jeyikradhu."
thirukkural: இடுக்கண் வருங்கால் நகுக அதனை அடுத்தூர்வது அஃதொப்ப தில்.
english_translation: Laugh away troubles; there is No other way to conquer woes
moral: When trouble comes, laugh at it — there's no better way to conquer hardship.

title: The Cyclone School
theme: Not Yielding to Adversity / Wisdom Overcomes Sorrow
hook: A cyclone destroys Meena's school — but her Appa teaches her that knowledge lives in the mind, not walls.
story: The cyclone came at midnight. Meena huddled with her family. Morning: their school was a pile of sticks and tin sheets. Meena cried. "Appa, school illama enna pannradhu?" Her friends' families talked of moving away. But Appa sat her down under the surviving banyan tree. He took out a chalk piece. "Padikalaam da. School mattum dhan education illa. Nee karpadhu un manasula dhan." He drew Tamil letters on a slate. Every day, under that tree, Meena studied. Three months later, the school was rebuilt. But Meena still remembered the tree lessons.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Appa, school illama enna padikradhu?'
Appa: 'School irukka da. Nee dhan school. Un manasu dhan school.'
Meena: 'Aana blackboard illaye?'
Appa: 'Bhoomi dhan blackboard. Akasham dhan roof.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the banyan tree, a chalk piece in his hand, letters drawn on the ground.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Meena, vellam varum. Poyidum. Aana un manasila irukka arivu — adha eduthukka mudiyadhu. Athu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: வெள்ளத் தனைய இடும்பை அறிவுடையான் உள்ளத்தின் உள்ளக் கெடும்.
english_translation: Deluging sorrows come to nought When wise men face them with firm thought
moral: Floods of sorrow vanish when a wise person faces them with a calm mind.

title: The Empty Lassi Glass
theme: Not Yielding to Adversity / Don't Grieve the Grieved
hook: Chitra's pet parrot dies — but Paati shows her that crying doesn't bring it back, only love does.
story: Chitra's parrot Mithu was gone. The cage was silent. Chitra hadn't eaten in two days. "Paati, Mithu varadha? Naan kara kara dhan irukken." Paati held her face. "Karadhu dhan varum da. Aana Mithu vantha, un kannale thanni paathu adhu santhosama irukkuma?" Chitra stopped. "Chithi enna pannalaam?" Paati took her to the terrace. They planted a sunflower. "Ithu valarum. Mithu maadhiri. Nee karalaam. Aana adhu valara vidu."
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'Paati, Mithu enakku venum. Enna pannradhu?'
Paati: 'Mithu vantha, nee karadhadhu adhu paathu avaluku santhosama?'
Chitra: 'Illa Paati.'
Paati: 'Apdi dhan. Mithu ninaippula vaikkira dhan adhu jeyikradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the terrace, watering the sunflower, a small feather at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Chitra, karadhadhu konjam dhan. Mithu ninaippa eduthuko. Kashtam varum. Aana adhula mungi poi — adhu dhan kashtam."
thirukkural: இடும்பைக்கு இடும்பை படுப்பர் இடும்பைக்கு இடும்பை படாஅ தவர்.
english_translation: Grief they face and put to grief Who grieve not grief by mind's relief
moral: Those who refuse to grieve over grief — they put grief itself to grief.

title: The Bullock Cart Boy
theme: Not Yielding to Adversity / Patient Strength
hook: Velu's bullock cart gets stuck in mud — he wants to beat the bull, but Thatha shows patience wins.
story: The cart was axle-deep in mud. Velu shouted, whipped the air. "Poi! Poi da!" The bull strained, slipped, fell. Velu raised the stick. Thatha grabbed his hand. "Velu! Adhu pannadha." Thatha got down. He cleared the mud around the wheels. He spoke softly to the bull. "Aahaa... mella da." The bull relaxed. Thatha put stones under the wheels. "Ippo." The bull pulled gently. The cart came out. Velu stared. "Thatha, vela adichaa varadhu. Aana paithensa pannaa varudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Velu: 'Thatha, adikkalaama? Adhu mella dhan irukku!'
Thatha: 'Adicha adhu nikkum. Paaithensa pannu. Appo dhan velai varum.'
Velu: 'Kashtam eduthukittu po, appo dhan victory varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the cart, one hand on the bull's neck, the other raised in peace.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Velu, adikkradhu konjam dhan. Samaadhaana irundhu proceed panna — adhu dhan periya balam. Kashtatha patience-aa eduthukka dhan jeyikradhu."
thirukkural: மடுத்தவா யெல்லாம் பகடன்னான் உற்ற இடுக்கண் இடர்ப்பாடு உடைத்து.
english_translation: Who pulls like bulls patiently on Causes grief to grieve anon
moral: Like a bull that pulls steadily through mud, patience makes grief itself give up.

title: The Spilled Milk Magic
theme: Not Yielding to Adversity / Bravery in Crisis
hook: Ravi's Amma drops the milk can — 50 rupees wasted. Ravi does something brave that fixes everything.
story: Amma dropped the milk can. White flooded the floor. 50 rupees — gone. She sat down, head in hands. Ravi saw her shoulders shake. He wanted to cry too. But instead, he ran to the corner shop. "Kannan uncle, enakku milk venum. Kaadhu kuduka maaten — Amma kitta vaangi kuduka sonnanga." Kannan uncle gave milk. Ravi ran back. "Amma! Parunga!" Amma looked up. Tears and surprise. "Epdi da?" Ravi grinned. "Kannan uncle kitta pesitten. Amma, karaadha. Naan irukken."
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Enna mudiyala da. Kashtam dhan.'
Ravi: 'Amma, naan irukken. Naan dhan unakku balam.'
Amma: 'Kozhandha, nee dhan en kavalai eduthutta.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the shop entrance, a milk packet in his hand, watching Ravi.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ravi, bayam varum. Aana adha eduthu ninnu, eduthukkittu pona — adha dhan veeram. Un Amma kavalai neeye eduthutta."
thirukkural: அடுக்கி வரினும் அழிவிலான் உற்ற இடுக்கண் இடுக்கண் படும்.
english_translation: Before the brave grief grieves and goes Who dare a host of pressing woes
moral: Before a brave heart, grief itself becomes grieved and retreats.

title: The Lost Prize Money
theme: Not Yielding to Adversity / Remain Steady in Loss
hook: Sanjay wins 1000 rupees in a race — then loses the envelope on the bus.
story: Sanjay's legs burned. But he won. First place. 1000 rupees. His first prize ever. He clutched the envelope on the bus. When he got home — empty hands. His face went white. He searched the bus. Nothing. At home, he sat on the steps, silent. "Suresh, naan 1000 rupees lose pannitten." Suresh said, "Adu thappu. Aana nee jeyicha vishyam perusu." Sanjay thought. He could cry. Or he could run again. Next year, he won again. Held the envelope tighter. "Irudha evan edutha?"
tamil_dialogues: Sanjay: 'Suresh, naan 1000 lose pannitten! Enna pannradhu!'
Suresh: 'Tholvi varum. Aana nee tholkkala. Nee jeyicha murai irukke.'
Sanjay: 'Unmai dhan. Naan karala. Muyarchi pannuven.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the school steps, a gold medal in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sanjay, tholvi varum. Aana adha kara kara irundha — adhu dhan periya tholvi. Nee velai pannitta, porumai edukka — adhu dhan jeyi."
thirukkural: அற்றேமென்று அல்லற் படுபவோ பெற்றேமென்று ஓம்புதல் தேற்றா தவர்.
english_translation: The wise that never gloat in gain Do not fret in fateful ruin
moral: Those who don't cling to joy in gain don't despair in loss either.

title: Papa's Sore Throat
theme: Not Yielding to Adversity / Accepting Life's Pain
hook: Kutty has a fever and complains constantly — until Amma tells him the body is made for some pain.
story: Kutty had been in bed for three days. Fever, sore throat, runny nose. "Amma, valikkudhu. Enakku kashtama irukku. End aagala. Yenna kashtam?" Amma sat beside him. "Kutty, unakku theriyuma, namma udale kashtam vantha dhan valarum. Athu dhan." Kutty looked confused. "Enna Amma?" Amma said, "Odambu kashtam dhan da. Fever varum, vali varum. Atha dhan udambuna. Aana adha bayapdadha — adha eduthuko." Kutty nodded slowly. "Seri Amma. Naan innum konjam kashtam eduthutu santhosama irukren."
tamil_dialogues: Kutty: 'Amma, enna kashtam ithu? End aagala?'
Amma: 'Kutty, udambu irukka kashtam varum. Atha dhan. Adha eduthukko.'
Kutty: 'Seri Amma. Kashtatha eduthutu santhosam irupen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the bedside, a cool cloth on Kutty's forehead.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kutty, un udambu kashtam dhan. Fever, vali — ithu dhan udambu. Athai eduthukitta, bayam poidum. Bayam illadhadhu dhan penance."
thirukkural: இலக்கம் உடம்பிடும்பைக்கு என்று கலக்கத்தைக் கையாறாக் கொள்ளாதாம் மேல்.
english_translation: The wise worry no more of woes Knowing body's butt of sorrows
moral: The wise don't panic at pain — they know the body is made for some suffering.

title: The Sunshine Girl
theme: Not Yielding to Adversity / Accepting Both Joy and Sorrow
hook: Deepika's favorite doll breaks — Paati teaches her that life is both joy and sorrow equally.
story: Deepika's doll fell from the window. The ceramic face cracked in two. She sobbed. "Paati, adhu poiduchu." Paati picked up the pieces. "Deepika, unakku santhosam kudukkum. Aana kashtamum kudukkum. Athu dhan." Deepika wiped her nose. "Enna Paati?" Paati held her hand. "Pazhagittu po. Kashtam vantha unakku kavalai varum. Aana adha vida koodadhu. Athu dhan vaazhkai." Deepika kept the pieces. She didn't glue them. She kept them as a reminder. "Paati, idhu dhan vaazhkai."
tamil_dialogues: Deepika: 'Paati, boomla enakku santhosam mattum illa?'
Paati: 'Illada. Kashtamum varum. Adhuvum vaazhkai dhan.'
Deepika: 'Adha dhan eduthukitu munna poganum?'
Paati: 'Ama. Athu dhan. Kashtatha eduthukitu munna pova.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the hill, a cracked doll piece in each hand, balancing them.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Deepika, vaazhkai la santhosam mattum illa. Kashtamum irukku. Athai rendaiyum eduthukitta dhan nalla vaazhalaam."
thirukkural: இன்பம் விழையான் இடும்பை இயல்பென்பான் துன்பம் உறுதல் இலன்.
english_translation: Who seek not joy, deem grief norm By sorrows do not come to harm
moral: One who doesn't chase only joy, but accepts grief as normal — is never truly harmed.

title: The Winning Loser
theme: Not Yielding to Adversity / Detachment from Outcomes
hook: Bala's team loses the cricket final by 1 run — but Thatha says real loss is losing your cool.
story: One run. One stupid run. Bala threw his bat across the ground. His teammates cried. "Thatha, oru run-la thotuchu! Naan six adikalaam nu irundhen!" Thatha picked up the bat. "Bala, nee jeyikkanum nu ninaicha. Tholvi vantha. Atha dhan." Bala said, "Aana Thatha, valikkudhu!" Thatha said, "Valikkum da. Aana adha feel pannu. Atha dhan vaazhkai. Nee aada vendiyadhu adhu dhan. Jeyi illa, tholvi illa. Nee dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Thatha, thotutu poittaen! Oru run la!'
Thatha: 'Tholvi varum. Aana adha dhan eduthuko. Nee unna vida koodadhu.'
Bala: 'Vaazhkai la rendum irundha dhan nalla irukku?'
Thatha: 'Ama. Athu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the boundary line, a cricket bat balanced on his finger.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bala, jeyilum santhosam, tholvilum santhosam — adhu dhan unmai. Athu dhan vida mudiyadhadhu."
thirukkural: இன்பத்துள் இன்பம் விழையாதான் துன்பத்துள் துன்பம் உறுதல் இலன்.
english_translation: In joy to joy who is not bound In grief he grieves not dual round!
moral: One not attached to joy itself — does not feel grief in grief either.

title: The Lemonade Opportunity
theme: Not Yielding to Adversity / Finding Opportunity in Trouble
hook: A terrible heat wave hits Theni — everyone is suffering. Suriya turns it into a business.
story: The sun was brutal. 42 degrees. People lay on floors. Suriya's little sister fainted. "Amma, romba heat-aa irukku. Namma enna pannradhu?" Amma had no answer. Then Suriya had an idea. He squeezed all the lemons. Added water, sugar, ice. He set up a table on the street. "Lemonade! Rendoo rupee! Rendoo rupee!" People lined up. By evening, he had 250 rupees. He bought a fan for the house. Amma's eyes filled. "Suriya, nee kashtatha business a maatita."
tamil_dialogues: Suriya: 'Amma, heat romba adikku. Namma enna pannradhu?'
Amma: 'Enna pannradhu da. Sahikkanum.'
Suriya: 'Naan oru idea. Lemonade pottalam! Kashtatha dhan jeyikkanum!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at Suriya's lemonade stall, a glass in his hand, sweat on his brow.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Suriya, kashtam vantha appo dhan namma budhi work aagum. Adha dhan opportunity a maatradhu. Seiyalaam — athu dhan periya jeyi."
thirukkural: இன்னாமை இன்பம் எனக்கொளின் ஆகுந்தன் ஒன்னார் விழையுஞ் சிறப்பு.
english_translation: His glory is esteemed by foes Who sees weal in wanton woes!
moral: One who finds gain in seeming loss earns even their enemies' respect.

title: The Right Time Teacher
theme: Ministers of State / Choosing Right Means, Time, Mode
hook: Mani plants a sapling at noon in blazing sun — Appa teaches him the importance of right timing.
story: Mani got a mango sapling from school. He ran to plant it immediately. Noon sun. 40 degrees. He dug, planted, watered. By evening, the leaves were brown. "Appa, sapling sethuduchu!" Appa came out. "Enna nerathula natta?" Mani thought. "Noon." Appa smiled. "Veyilla natta, sethudum. Kaalaila illa malaiyila nattanum. Un vazhkai la — edhukku sariyaana neram therinjirukkanum." Mani nodded. The next sapling he planted at 5 PM. It grew into a tree.
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Appa, sapling sethuduchu!'
Appa: 'Enna nerathula natta?'
Mani: 'Noon la.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan. Neram therinja dhan velai varum. Kashtam dhan padam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the dead sapling, a fresh seed in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mani, sariyaana neram, sariyaana vazhi, sariyaana seyal — moonu dhan venum. Athu dhan amaithi."
thirukkural: கருவியும் காலமும் செய்கையும் செய்யும் அருவினையும் மாண்டது அமைச்சு.
english_translation: He is minister who chooses Right means, time, mode and rare ventures
moral: To succeed, choose the right tool, the right time, and the right method.

title: The Class Monitor's Burden
theme: Ministers of State / Leadership Requires Knowledge, Firmness, Diligence
hook: Leela becomes class monitor and struggles to control the class — Amma teaches her what true leadership needs.
story: Leela got the monitor badge. Day 1: kids laughed, threw paper. "Leela, nee enna panna mudiyum?" She shouted. They laughed louder. She cried. At home, "Amma, enna mudiyala. Avanga ketkamaata." Amma said, "Leela, unakku theriyuma? Nee first knowledge vaangi. Appo dhan authority varum." Leela studied harder. She became the topper. Then kids respected her. She was firm but kind. Day 30: the class was silent when she spoke. "Amma, ippo dhan class nyayama irukku."
tamil_dialogues: Leela: 'Amma, avanga en sonna ketkamaata?'
Amma: 'Nee avangaloda knowledge la vida vendum. Appo dhan authority.'
Leela: 'Ippo puriyudhu. Munnadi knowledge, appo dhan power.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the classroom door, a book in one hand, a firm gentle gaze.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Leela, arivu irundha dhan ulagam unnai eduthukku. Adhu dhan amaithi. Kaaval, arivu, seyal — moonum venum."
thirukkural: வன்கண் குடிகாத்தல் கற்றறிதல் ஆள்வினையோடு ஐந்துடன் மாண்டது அமைச்சு.
english_translation: With these he guards people, -by his Knowledge, firmness and manliness
moral: Leadership needs courage, knowledge, diligence — to truly care for those you lead.

title: The Broken Friendship
theme: Ministers of State / Unity and Reconciliation
hook: Viji's two best friends stop talking to each other — Viji gets them to reconcile.
story: Rani and Kavi were best friends. Then a fight over a pencil box. They stopped talking. The whole class took sides. Viji sat in the middle. "Unakku enna? Rendum sanda dhan." Viji couldn't study. At lunch, Rani sat with the girls. Kavi sat alone. Viji took Kavi's plate to Rani. "Listen. You both miss each other." She forced them to sit together. Silence. Then Kavi giggled. Rani smiled. Viji grinned. "Pathiya? Inga dhan dhosth."
tamil_dialogues: Viji: 'Rani, Kavi unga rendu perum sanda podradhu niruthunga.'
Rani: 'Avala dhan first.'
Kavi: 'Illa, ava dhan.'
Viji: 'Rendume sari. Ippo peace. Athu dhan periya amaithi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the playground bench, a pencil box between two children.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Viji, pirinjavana serkkradhu dhan periya vela. Atha dhan amaithi. Athu dhan sugam."
thirukkural: பிரித்தலும் பேணிக் கொளலும் பிரிந்தார்ப் பொருத்தலும் வல்ல தமைச்சு.
english_translation: A minister cherishes friends Divides foes and the parted blends
moral: A true leader knows how to bring separated people together.

title: The Thoughtful Gift
theme: Ministers of State / Think Before You Act
hook: Divya buys a gift for Amma without thinking — it's the wrong size. Thatha teaches her to think first.
story: Divya wanted to surprise Amma. She bought a beautiful saree — 2000 rupees. All her savings. She ran home, proud. "Amma, idhu unakku!" Amma opened it. It was a blouse piece. Only a blouse piece. Divya had grabbed the wrong thing. She cried. Thatha sat her down. "Edhukku munnadi think pannala?" Divya sniffled. "Naan surprise panna aasai." Thatha said, "Think first, act later. Edhai pannalum, moonu steps: think, select, do." Divya returned it. Got the right saree. Amma wore it for Pongal.
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Thatha, naan blouse vaangitten. Saree nu ninaichen!'
Thatha: 'Think pannama action panna — adhu dhan mistake.'
Divya: 'Munnadi aalocha pannanum Thatha?'
Thatha: 'Ama. Think, select, do. Adhu dhan formula.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands outside the textile shop, a measuring tape in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Divya, think pannama action pannradhu dhan thappu. Sariya aalocha pannitu, select pannitu, seyya vendum."
thirukkural: தெரிதலும் தேர்ந்து செயலும் ஒருதலையாச் சொல்லலும் வல்லது அமைச்சு.
english_translation: A minister must sift reflect Select and say surely one fact
moral: Think carefully, choose wisely, then act decisively — that is the way of wisdom.

title: The Wrong Friend
theme: Ministers of State / Choosing the Right Advisors
hook: Sekar trusts a classmate who always gives bad advice — until Appa shows him what a true helper looks like.
story: Sekar's friend Guna always said, "Adha pannada, teacher kitta solla vendam." When Sekar failed, Guna laughed. When Sekar needed help, Guna was busy. Appa noticed. "Sekar, un friend yaaru?" Sekar said Guna. Appa asked, "Avan unakku enna help pannittan?" Sekar thought. Nothing. "Appa, avan romba nalla pesuvaan." Appa said, "Nalla pesuradhu mathiri illa — nalla seyal dhan. Nee avana vittutu, nalla friend a thedi eduthuko." Sekar found a new friend, Murali, who actually taught him math.
tamil_dialogues: Sekar: 'Appa, Guna nalla dhan irukka.'
Appa: 'Seyal paaru. Pesradhu illa. Nalla seyal dhan unmai.'
Sekar: 'Murali dhan help pannuraan. Ippo puriyudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, pointing at two boys — one talking, one helping.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sekar, avalochana dhan mukiyam. Nalla aalochana — nalla seyal. Adha kudukkuravanai dhan friend a edukkanum."
thirukkural: அறனறிந்து ஆன்றமைந்த சொல்லான்எஞ் ஞான்றுந் திறனறிந்தான் தேர்ச்சித் துணை.
english_translation: Have him for help who virtue knows Right wisdom speaks, ever apt in acts
moral: Choose as your helper one who knows virtue, speaks wisely, and is always capable.

title: The Street Smart Girl
theme: Ministers of State / Wisdom Beats Cleverness
hook: Anitha is the class topper — but Sharada from the village solves a problem she can't.
story: Anitha had all the books. "Naan dhan intelligent." Sharada sat in the back, never spoke. One day, the teacher asked, "Enga oorla water shortage varum — enna pannuvanga?" Anitha quoted the textbook: "Rainwater harvesting." Sharada raised her hand. "Teacher, namma oorla, thanni ilana, kai vechikalam. Bore well. Kuththi." The teacher smiled. Anitha was silent. After class, she asked, "Sharada, unakku epdi therium?" Sharada said, "Entha pusthagathalum varadhu. Anubhavathala varum."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Sharada, unakku epdi adhu therium? Naan padichen.'
Sharada: 'Padichadhu dhan arivu. Parthadhu dhan budhi.'
Anitha: 'Ippo dhan puriyudhu — randum venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits between two desks, a book in one hand, a map in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anitha, pusthagathula irukradhu dhan arivu. Aana anubhavam dhan budhi. Rendum venum. Athu dhan nalla amaithi."
thirukkural: மதிநுட்பம் நூலோடு உடையார்க்கு அதிநுட்பம் யாவுள முன்நிற் பவை.
english_translation: Which subtler brain can stand before The keen in brain with learned lore?
moral: What cleverness can stand before one who has both book knowledge and real-world wisdom?

title: The Fish Market Lesson
theme: Ministers of State / Book Knowledge vs Practical Wisdom
hook: Ganesh reads all about fishing in books — but when he actually goes to the harbour, he knows nothing.
story: Ganesh read three books on fishing. Species, nets, seasons. He knew everything. Periyappa took him to Kasimedu. "Come. I'll show you real fishing." Ganesh pointed. "Periyappa, adhu skipjack tuna! Pusthagathula padichen." Periyappa handed him a net. "Catch it." Ganesh threw the net. It tangled. The fish swam away. The fishermen laughed. Periyappa untangled it. "Pusthagam dhan therium. Kai work theriyadhu. Irunda work — pom. Rendum venum."
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Periyappa, enakku ellam therium! Pusthagathula padichen!'
Periyappa: 'Pusthagam therium. Kai work theriyadhu. Rendum venum.'
Ganesh: 'Ippo dhan puriyudhu. Padichadhu podhum, anubhavam venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a fishing boat, untangling a net with patient hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ganesh, pusthagam dhan arambam. Aana ulagam epdi work aagudhu nu therinjukkanum. Adhu dhan amaithi."
thirukkural: செயற்கை அறிந்தக் கடைத்தும் உலகத்து இயற்கை அறிந்து செயல்.
english_translation: Albeit you know to act from books Act after knowing world's outlooks
moral: Even if you know theory from books, learn how the world actually works before acting.

title: The Boy Who Spoke Truth
theme: Ministers of State / Truth Over Power
hook: The Principal makes a wrong announcement — no one corrects him except Kathir.
story: The Principal announced, "Tomorrow is holiday!" The whole school cheered. Then his assistant whispered. The Principal's face fell. "Sorry, it's not a holiday." Everyone was disappointed. But no one said anything. Kathir stood up. "Sir, neenga ippo sonna maadhiri dhan — neenga poiyum solla koodadhu." The class gasped. The Principal stared. Then he smiled. "Nee sariya solra. Naan thappa sollitten. Idhu dhan neethi." He gave Kathir a special mention in assembly. "Ungala maadhiri thairiyama pesanum."
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Kathir, Principal kitta adha sonna? Unakku periya problem!'
Kathir: 'Sariya solradha vida periya edhuvum illa. Avanum eduthuppan.'
Principal: 'Nee dhan sari. Naanum edhuthukren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the assembly podium, a microphone before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kathir, unmai sollanum. Adhu mukiyam. Munnadi yaar irunthalum — unmai dhan jeyikkum. Nee adhu dhan pannitta."
thirukkural: அறிகொன்று அறியான் எனினும் உறுதி உழையிருந்தான் கூறல் கடன்.
english_translation: The man in place must tell the facts Though the ignorant king refutes
moral: One must speak the truth even when the person in power might not like it.

title: The Poisoned Advice
theme: Ministers of State / Bad Counsel is Worse Than Enemies
hook: Raja listens to his "friend" who tells him to skip homework — and disaster follows.
story: Raja's friend Muniyandi always gave advice. "Homework pannada. Sir check panna maata." Raja listened. Next day, Sir checked. Raja got a zero. "Raja, nee dhan pannala." Muniyandi laughed. Appa got called. "Raja, un friend Muniyandi unakku enna help pannittan?" Raja thought. "Appa, avan advice dhan enna ketkutum." Appa said, "Ketkum advice kuda ennemaa irukku. Avan ketkuravan. Avalo enemy um venam." Next week, Raja sat next to a good student.
tamil_dialogues: Raja: 'Appa, Muniyandi dhan sonnan homework pannada nu.'
Appa: 'Avana vida oru enemy kooda better. Ennemaa advice — athu dhan kedumai.'
Raja: 'Ippo dhan puriyudhu. Nalla advice dhan venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the school steps, a broken pencil beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Raja, ketkura aalochana — athu dhan periya enemy. Nalla aalochana illana, aayiram enemies um onnu seradhu."
thirukkural: பழுதெண்ணும் மந்திரியின் பக்கததுள் தெவ்வோர் எழுபது கோடி உறும்.
english_translation: Seventy crores of foes are better Than a minister with mind bitter
moral: A bad advisor is worse than seventy crore enemies — choose your counsel carefully.

title: The Indecisive Captain
theme: Ministers of State / Decisiveness is Key
hook: Vinoth is cricket team captain but can't decide on bowling changes — the match slips away.
story: The match was close. 10 runs needed. 2 overs left. Vinoth held the ball. Should he bowl himself or give it to Kumar? He stood there, thinking. "Venu? Kumar?" The crowd shouted. Time ran. The umpire said, "Hurry!" Vinoth panicked, tossed the ball randomly. Kumar bowled. Four, six, four. They lost. The coach said, "Vinoth, well thought out plan — but execution illa. Plan pannitu, decide pannitu, execute pannanum. Delay pannradhu dhan tholvi." Vinoth never forgot.
tamil_dialogues: Team: 'Vinoth! Quick! Decide pannu!'
Vinoth: 'Enakku theriyala! Yaaru bowl pannradhu?'
Coach: 'Aalochana dhan mukiyam. Aana decide pannama irukradhu dhan periya tholvi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bowler's mark, a cricket ball in his hand, ready.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vinoth, plan pannitu irukka koodadhu. Decide pannanum. Seiyanum. Athu dhan victory. Alochichittu irundha — opportunity poidum."
thirukkural: முறைப்படச் சூழ்ந்தும் முடிவிலவே செய்வர் திறப்பாடு இலாஅ தவர்.
english_translation: The unresolved, though well designed To fulfil an act they have no mind
moral: Even the best plan is useless without the decisiveness to execute it.

title: Tongue Like Honey
theme: Power of Speech / Goodness of Words
hook: Nandhu's teacher calls his mother to say he never fights — but his real power is in his words.
story: Nandhu was the smallest in class but everyone listened when he spoke. One day a new boy, Irfan, dropped his lunch. Everyone laughed. Nandhu said, "Adhu yaarukkum aagalam da, nee bayapadaadha." Irfan smiled. Teacher called Amma. "Un payyan romba nalla pesuraan." Amma hugged him. "Vaiyala nalladha pesina, adhu dhan periya nalanu."
tamil_dialogues: Nandhu: 'Irfan, adhu periya vishayam illa da, vaangi saapdu.'
Amma: 'Nee sollra vaartha dhan unakku periya aayudham da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the school bench, a honey bee on his finger.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nandhu, un vaartha dhan unakku periya nalan. Adhu matha nalam ellaam adhukku munnadi onnum illa."
thirukkural: நாநலம் என்னும் நலனுடைமை அந்நலம் யாநலத்து உள்ளதூஉம் அன்று.
english_translation: The goodness called goodness of speech Is goodness which nothing can reach.
moral: The goodness of good speech is a goodness like no other.

title: The Slip That Sank
theme: Power of Speech / Guard Against Slips
hook: Shakthi blurts out the temple treasure location in front of strangers — and learns the cost of a loose tongue.
story: Appa was showing Shakthi the secret tunnel behind the Brhadeeswarar temple. "Idhu romba secret da, yaarukkum solla koodadhu." Shakthi nodded. But at the gate, a tourist asked, "Enna paatheenga?" Shakthi said, "Enga Appa enakku secret tunnel kaatitaaru!" Appa's face fell. That night, Shakthi cried. "Appa, naan theriyama sollitten." Appa said, "Adhu dhan da — vaartha oru ambu maadhiri. Vittuta podhu, thirumba eduka mudiyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Shakthi: 'Appa, naan sonnadhu thappu dhan!'
Appa: 'Ama da. Sollradhukku munnadi yosichu sollu. Illa na kastam dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the temple gopuram, finger to his lips.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Shakthi, un vaarthala laabamum kedumum varum. Adha kaapadradhu dhan arivu. Sollradhukku munnadi oru nimisham yosichu paaru."
thirukkural: ஆக்கமுங் கேடும் அதனால் வருதலால் காத்தோம்பல் சொல்லின்கட் சோர்வு.
english_translation: Since gain or ruin speeches bring Guard against the slips of tongue.
moral: Gain and ruin both come from speech — guard every word.

title: The Grumpy Uncle
hook: Thenmozhi's grumpy uncle never talks to anyone — until she finds the right words.
story: Uncle was always angry. "Poi da, poi." Everyone avoided him. Thenmozhi asked Paati, "En ipdi irukkaaru?" Paati said, "Avarukku yaarum nalla pesa maataanga. Nee nalla pesi paaru." Next day, Thenmozhi said, "Uncle, unga coffee romba nalla irundhadhu." Uncle's eyes softened. He made her a paper boat. "Nee mattum dhan en kitta nalla pesura."
tamil_dialogues: Thenmozhi: 'Uncle, neenga enna pannitu irukkeenga?'
Uncle: 'Onnum illa... unakku vena paper boat panni tharen.'
Thenmozhi: 'Paati! Uncle pesuraaru!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the window, a paper boat floating in a bowl of water.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thenmozhi, nalla pesina, yaaraiyum un kooda vaikkalam. Vera solla thevai illa. Adhu dhan vaartha vali."
thirukkural: கேட்டார்ப் பிணிக்கும் தகையவாய்க் கேளாரும் வேட்ப மொழிவதாம் சொல்.
english_translation: A speech is speech that holds ears And attracts even those that are averse.
moral: True speech attracts even those who don't want to listen.

title: The Debate Star
theme: Power of Speech / Weigh Your Words
hook: Karthik stammers in the debate — but Appa's advice on weighing words wins him the trophy.
story: Karthik's hands shook at the podium. Words jumbled. He lost badly. That night, Appa said, "Sollradhukku munnadi, un vaarthaiyae edhai nizhai." Karthik practised: think, weigh, speak. Next debate, he paused, thought, then spoke slowly. Every word hit like a target. The judges gave him first prize. Teacher said, "Nee sollra maadhiri yaarukkum solla theriyathu."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Sir, naan pesra velaiyila yosichu dhan pesaren.'
Teacher: 'Adhu dhan periya kala. Un vaarthaikku edhai illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the back of the hall, arms crossed, nodding.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karthik, yosichu pesradhu dhan vaartha vali. Adhai vida periya aramum porulum illa."
thirukkural: திறனறிந்து சொல்லுக சொல்லை அறனும் பொருளும் அதனினூஉங்கு இல்.
english_translation: Weigh thy words and speak; because No wealth or virtue words surpass.
moral: There is no wealth or virtue greater than measured speech.

title: The Bully's Lesson
hook: A bully challenges Balu to a fight — Balu defeats him with words, not fists.
story: "Va da! Adi pannuva!" Raju the bully pushed Balu. Balu remembered Chithappa's words: "Nee adi podradha vida, un vaarthaikku adi poda mudiyadhadhu maadhiri pesu." Balu said calmly, "Raju, unakku kovam varadhu dhan sari. Aana adichaa rendu perukum kastam dhan." Raju stopped. The whole class stared. "Enna da sollra?" Balu smiled. "Namma friendship pannalaam da." Raju put his hand down.
tamil_dialogues: Balu: 'Adi podradha vida, pesi eduthukradhu dhan periya jeyam.'
Raju: 'Unakku adi pidikala?'
Balu: 'Pidikala. Aana un kooda friendship venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a banyan tree, two boys shaking hands beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Balu, un vaarthaikku edhir illa. Yaaralayum unna vella mudiyadhu. Adhu dhan vaartha valimai."
thirukkural: சொல்லுக சொல்லைப் பிறிதோர்சொல் அச்சொல்லை வெல்லுஞ்சொல் இன்மை அறிந்து.
english_translation: Speak out thy word so that no word can win it and say untoward.
moral: Speak so powerfully that no opposing word can defeat yours.

title: The Gossip Girls
theme: Power of Speech / Speak Sweet, Listen Well
hook: Varshini's friends gossip about a classmate — she learns to speak sweet and also catch the good in others' words.
story: Divya whispered about Meena's old dress. "Paathiya, avan romba comedy." Varshini laughed too. But Athai saw her sad face. "Enna achu?" Varshini said, "Naanum ippa Meena mathiri gossip kettu dhan irundhen." Athai said, "Nee nalla pesu, mathavadhu sollula nalladha eduthuko." Next day, Varshini told Divya, "Namma ipdi pesradhu thappu." Divya stopped. Meena smiled at her.
tamil_dialogues: Varshini: 'Namma ellaam adha pathi pesradhu nalla illa.'
Divya: 'Un sonnadhu sari dhan. Namma inniki ava kooda vilaada ponom.'
Athai: 'Nee sonnadhu dhan — nalla pesu, nalladha eduthuko.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans on the school gate, listening to the girls' conversation with a gentle smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla pesradhum, mathavadhu sollula nalladha edukradhum — adhu dhan visudha manushan kuri."
thirukkural: வேட்பத்தாஞ் சொல்லிப் பிறர்சொல் பயன்கோடல் மாட்சியின் மாசற்றார் கோள்.
english_translation: Spotless men speak what is sweet And grasp in others what is meet.
moral: Speak sweetly and extract the good from others' words — that is spotless conduct.

title: The Fearless Speaker
hook: Rajesh has to give a speech in front of the whole school but his enemy tries to stop him.
story: Kanna mocked Rajesh before his speech. "Unakku pesa varadhu da. Nikkatha." Rajesh's legs shook. But Appa's voice echoed: "Sollradhu unaku theriyum. Nee bayapada vendam." Rajesh stepped up. His voice was clear, strong, flawless. "Nanban, edhir — yaar irundhaalum, vaarthai munnadi ellam samam." The crowd cheered. Kanna sat down quietly. Appa clapped from the back.
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Vaarthai munnadi bayam illa. Adhu dhan porum.'
Appa: 'Nee sollittaiya — soarvu illa, bayam illa, adhu dhan veli.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the stage, one hand raised in blessing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sollradhula soarvu illa, bayam illa. Adhu dhan unmai. Ava udane yaaralayum vella mudiyadhu."
thirukkural: சொலல்வல்லன் சோர்விலன் அஞ்சான் அவனை இகல்வெல்லல் யார்க்கும் அரிது.
english_translation: No foe defies the speaker clear Flawless, puissant, and free from fear.
moral: A skilled speaker who is flawless and fearless — no enemy can defeat.

title: The Class Monitor
theme: Power of Speech / The World Listens
hook: Naveen becomes class monitor — but nobody listens until he learns to speak with order.
story: Teacher made Naveen monitor. He shouted. "Silence da!" Nobody cared. Amma said, "Adi podradhu podhum da. Nee nirantharam nalla pesu." Next day, Naveen said calmly, "Ellaarum unga place la okkarungalen." First, silence. Then everyone sat. Teacher smiled. "Naveen, nee sollradhu dhan work aagudhu." Naveen realized: ordered words move the world.
tamil_dialogues: Naveen: 'Amma, naan sollum podhu yaarum kettu maataangala.'
Amma: 'Nee nirantharam nalla pesu. Adhu dhan work aagum.'
Teacher: 'Naveen, un vaarthai dhan class ah niruthudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the empty classroom, a notebook open before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naveen, nirantharama nalla pesunna, ulagam adha kaekkum. Adhu dhan vaartha vali."
thirukkural: விரைந்து தொழில்கேட்கும் ஞாலம் நிரந்தினிது சொல்லுதல் வல்லார்ப் பெறின்.
english_translation: The world will quickly carry out The words of counsellors astute.
moral: The world swiftly obeys those who speak with order and sweetness.

title: The Big Mouth
theme: Power of Speech / Know When to Stop
hook: Deepan talks non-stop in class — Thatha teaches him the value of a few good words.
story: Deepan raised his hand for everything. "Miss! Miss! Naan!" Even when he had nothing to say. Kids rolled their eyes. Thatha said, "Deepan, adi potta saththam podhum da. Neraya pesradhu dhan nalladhu illa. Konjam pesu — aana adhu dhan nalladha pesu." Deepan tried. Next day, he spoke only once. Teacher stopped. "Deepan, un vaarthai romba nalla irukku." Quality over quantity.
tamil_dialogues: Thatha: 'Pesradhu dhan nalladhu, aana neraya pesradhu dhan nalladhu illa.'
Deepan: 'Appo naan konjam dhan pesanum?'
Thatha: 'Konjam aana nalladha pesu. Adhu dhan porum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the back of the assembly, counting on his fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neraya vaartha sollradhu dhan arivu illa. Konjam aana maasam illadha sollu. Adhu dhan vaartha thunai."
thirukkural: பலசொல்லக் காமுறுவர் மன்றமா சற்ற சிலசொல்லல் தேற்றா தவர்.
english_translation: They overspeak who do not seek A few and flawless words to speak.
moral: Those who don't know the power of few flawless words will always overspeak.

title: The Flower That Didn't Smell
theme: Power of Speech / Express Your Knowledge
hook: Lavanya knows all the answers but can't explain them — she's like a flower with no fragrance.
story: Lavanya studied hard. But when teacher asked, "Explain pannu," she froze. Words stuck. "Theriyum miss... aana solla theriyala." Amma said, "Un kitta neraya knowledge irukku, aana adha mattavadhu kitta share panna theriyala na, adhu vasanam illadha poo maadhiri dhan." Lavanya practised speaking in front of the mirror. Slowly, words flowed. Teacher said, "Ippo dhan unakku oru fragrance vandhurukku."
tamil_dialogues: Lavanya: 'Amma, enakku theriyum aana solla varala.'
Amma: 'Therinjadhu mattavanga kitta solla theriyala na, adhu vasanam illadha poo.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in a garden, a jasmine flower glowing in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Therinja vishayama irundha adha mattavanga kitta share panna theriyum. Adhu dhan arivu. Ilana vasanam illadha poo."
thirukkural: இணருழ்த்தும் நாறா மலரனையர் கற்றது உணர விரித்துரையா தார்.
english_translation: Who can't express what they have learnt Are bunch of flowers not fragrant.
moral: Knowledge without expression is like a flower with no fragrance.

title: The Clean Hands
theme: Purity in Action / Pure Deeds Bring All
hook: A vendor offers Arul extra change by mistake — returning it brings unexpected reward.
story: Arul bought vegetables. The vendor gave him 50 rupees extra. "Vaa da, kai niraiya kaasu!" But Arul saw the man's old hands, hardworking. "Saar, neenga 50 rupai extra pottuteenga."
