The vendor's face lit up. "Un kai dhan suthama irukku da." He gave Arul a free mango. Appa saw. "Nee nalladha pannitta. Adhu dhan unakku venum. Pure action dhan ellam tharum."
tamil_dialogues: Arul: 'Saar, 50 rupai extra!'
Vendor: 'Un kai dhan sutham da. Vera yaarum thiruppi kuduthuruka maatta.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the vegetable cart, weighing a single tomato.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arul, suthamaana seyal unakku ellam tharum. Adhai vida periya nalan illa."
thirukkural: துணைநலம் ஆக்கம் தரும் வினைநலம் வேண்டிய எல்லாந் தரும்.
english_translation: Friendship brings gain; but action pure Does every good thing we desire.
moral: Pure action gives you everything you truly need.

title: The Copycat
theme: Purity in Action / Avoid Worthless Acts
hook: Monisha copies her friend's homework — Akka shows her the difference between useful and useless acts.
story: Monisha was tired. "Akka, Sheela homework copy panniten. Naan panna mathiri." Akka closed her book. "Monisha, adhu dhan waste. Adhu unakku periya payanum tharaadhu. Nee pannra seyal dhan un vaazhkai." Monisha was quiet. "Adhu nalla seyal illa?" Akka said, "Nalla seyal illa, unakku nalla payanum tharaadhu." Monisha started doing her own homework. Next test, she got 90%. "Akka! Naan nalladha dhan panninen!"
tamil_dialogues: Monisha: 'Akka, copy pannradhu nalladhu illa?'
Akka: 'Illa da. Adhu unakku nalla payanum tharaadhu. Nee seyra seyal dhan un vaazhkai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the study desk, a book open under his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Monisha, pugazhum nanmaiyum tharaadha seyalai vittudu. Adhu dhan arivu."
thirukkural: என்றும் ஒருவுதல் வேண்டும் புகழொடு நன்றி பயவா வினை.
english_translation: Eschew always acts that do not Bring good nor glory on their part.
moral: Always avoid deeds that bring neither goodness nor glory.

title: The Stolen Pencil
theme: Purity in Action / Guard Your Reputation
hook: Manikandan takes a fancy pencil from his friend's bag — Amma's words ruin his sleep.
story: Karthik's new pencil case had a shiny blue pen. Manikandan wanted it. He slipped it into his pocket. "Enakku venum!" At home, he couldn't sleep. Amma saw. "Enna da, moodu sarakku?" Manikandan cried. "Amma, naan pen eduthen." Amma said softly, "Adhu un pugazha kedukka koodadhu da. Nee nallavan. Adha thiruppi kudu." Next day, he returned it. "Sorry da, unakku vachittu vandhuten." Karthik smiled. "Parava illa da."
tamil_dialogues: Manikandan: 'Amma, naan thiruditten. Enakku bayama irukku.'
Amma: 'Nee nallavan da. Thiruppi kudu. Un pugazh dhan mukkiyam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the doorway, the blue pen glowing in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manikandan, un pugazha kedukkira seyal panna koodadhu. Adhu dhan visudham."
thirukkural: ஒஓதல் வேண்டும் ஒளிமாழ்கும் செய்வினை ஆஅதும் என்னும் அவர்.
english_translation: Those in the world desire for fame Should shun the deed that dims their name.
moral: Those who value their name must shun deeds that dim it.

title: The Rich Lie
theme: Purity in Action / No Shame in Poverty
hook: Deva is tempted to lie about his family's poverty to impress rich friends — Paati stops him.
story: A rich classmate mocked Deva's old shoes. "Paathiya, un chappal kulla viral veliya irukku!" Deva wanted to lie. "Namma puthu chappal vanthiduchu..." But Paati's words stopped him. "Eda, namma visudha vaazhkai dhan periya chelva. Veroru kaaranam vendam." Deva stood tall. "Ithu dhan en chappal. Aana naan nallavan." The boy shut up. Teacher heard and clapped. "Nee romba nalla sollita da."
tamil_dialogues: Deva: 'Paati, avanga kitta namma sari illanu feel aagudhu.'
Paati: 'Deva, visudha vaazhkai dhan periya chelva. Yaar munnadiyum thala kuniya vendam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside Deva, one hand on his shoulder, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Eda, kastam vanthaalum maanam kedukka koodadhu. Adhu dhan unmai visudham."
thirukkural: இடுக்கண் படினும் இளிவந்த செய்யார் நடுக்கற்ற காட்சி யவர்.
english_translation: Though perils press the faultless wise Shun deeds of mean, shameful device.
moral: Even in hardship, the wise never do shameful deeds.

title: The Regretful Slap
theme: Purity in Action / Don't Repeat Wrongs
hook: Vetrivel slaps his younger brother in anger — cries all night and wants to take it back.
story: Kutti broke Vetri's paper rocket. Vetri slapped him. "Poi da!" Kutti cried. Vetri felt his own hand burn. "Enna pannitten?" He cried to Periyappa. "Periyappa, naan adichitten. Enakku feel pannudhu." Periyappa said, "Seidha thappai thiruppi seyya koodadhu. Athu dhan nalla visudham." Vetri hugged Kutti. "Sorry da." Kutti smiled. "Parava illa anna." Vetri never hit again.
tamil_dialogues: Vetri: 'Periyappa, naan avana adichen. Enakku kastama irukku.'
Periyappa: 'Seidha thappai thiruppi seyya koodadhu. Adhu dhan nalla visudham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits between the two brothers, one hand on each head.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vetri, thappu seidha udane mannichudu. Aana adhe thappai thiruppi seyya koodadhu. Adhu dhan visudham."
thirukkural: எற்றென்று இரங்குவ செய்யற்க செய்வானேல் மற்றன்ன செய்யாமை நன்று.
english_translation: Do not wrong act and grieve, "Alas" If done, do not repeat it twice.
moral: Don't do wrong and regret it; if you do, never repeat it.

title: The Hungry Mother
theme: Purity in Action / Even for Family, No Wrong
hook: Muthu's Amma is starving — a shopkeeper offers stolen fish cheap. Muthu says no.
story: Amma hadn't eaten in two days. Muthu was desperate. A shopkeeper whispered, "Vaa da, free fish. Yaarukkum theriyadhu." Muthu's hands reached. Then she stopped. "Amma enakku sollittu dhan nadandha — pizhai panna koodadhu." She went home. A neighbor brought food. "Enga Amma sonna, unga Amma romba kastama irukka. Vaanga." Muthu cried. "Naa nalladha dhan panninen." Amma hugged her.
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Amma, enakku pizhai panna maatan nu vachirundhen.'
Amma: 'Nee nalladha dhan pannitta da. Un seyal dhan ennoda pasiyai vida periyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the door, a bowl of rice in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Muthu, un amma pasiya irundhaalum, sariyilladha seyal panna koodadhu. Un seyal dhan unmai."
thirukkural: ஈன்றாள் பசிகாண்பான் ஆயினுஞ் செய்யற்க சான்றோர் பழிக்கும் வினை.
english_translation: Though she who begot thee hungers Shun acts denounced by ancient seers.
moral: Even if your mother starves, never do what the wise condemn.

title: The Empty Wallet
theme: Purity in Action / Honest Poverty vs Dirty Wealth
hook: Somu has no lunch money — a rich boy offers to pay if he cheats on a test. Somu refuses.
story: "Somu, un wallet empty-a irukku pole. Naan lunch pottaren — nee en kitta answer sollu." Ramesh smirked. Somu's stomach growled. But Chithi's voice came: "Kai kuppai aana manasu kuppai illa. Adhu dhan periya selva." Somu said, "Venda da. Pasikkum aana naan thappu panna maaten." Ramesh was shocked. Teacher heard. "Somu, unakku periya manasu. Vaa, en kooda lunch saapdalaam."
tamil_dialogues: Somu: 'Pasikkum aana naan thappu panna maaten.'
Chithi: 'Kai kuppai aana manasu kuppai illa. Adhu dhan periya chelva.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the canteen table, an empty plate before him but a smile on his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Somu, kai kuppai irundhaalum, manasu kuppai illadhadhu dhan unmai. Adhu dhan periya chelva."
thirukkural: பழிமலைந்து எய்திய ஆக்கத்தின் சான்றோர் கழிநல்குரவே தலை.
english_translation: Pinching poverty of the wise Is more than wealth hoarded by Vice.
moral: Honest poverty is greater than wealth gained through vice.

title: The Railway Race
hook: Senthil and friends jump the train gate to save time — Appa makes him watch the consequences.
story: "Va da! Gate dhanda da!" Senthil's friends jumped the railway gate. Senthil followed. Appa caught him. "Niruthu!" He dragged him to the platform. A train rushed past. A man who jumped last month — his leg was bandaged. "Pathiya? Adhu dhan kalaikkum. Ippo vandha laabam — aana adhu pilla pannum kashtam." Senthil understood. Some deeds look easy but bring lasting pain.
tamil_dialogues: Senthil: 'Appa, ellaarum pannaanga! Naanum panninen.'
Appa: 'Ellarum pannina adhu sari aagadhu da. Adhu forbidden seyal dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the platform as the train thunders by, his hand on Senthil's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Senthil, forbidden seyal — adhu work aanaalum kashtam tharum. Atha vida dhan nalladhu."
thirukkural: கடிந்த கடிந்தொரார் செய்தார்க்கு அவைதாம் முடிந்தாலும் பீழை தரும்.
english_translation: Those who dare a forbidden deed Suffer troubles though they succeed.
moral: Forbidden deeds bring pain even when they succeed.

title: The Lost Wallet
theme: Purity in Action / Tears vs Blessings
hook: Pooja finds a wallet with money — keeping it brings tears, returning it brings joy.
story: Pooja found a black wallet at the bus stand. 500 rupees inside! "Paati! Namma shop ku pogaalaam!" Paati looked sad. "Pooja, adhu yaaroda kashtama irukku. Adha vachaa, un kaila dhan irukkum. Aana adhu atha owner kitta poi aazhum." Pooja returned the wallet to the old woman who was crying. "Amma, unga wallet." The woman kissed her. "Deivam dhan unna anuppi irukku." Paati smiled. "Pathiya? Seidha nalladhu dhan un kooda varum."
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Paati, 500 rupai dhan. Vachukkalama?'
Paati: 'Adhu yaaroda kashtam da. Atha vachaa, adhu un kaiyila aazhum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside the crying old woman, the wallet in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pooja, aazha vandha porul aazha dhan pogum. Aana nalladha seidha porul un kooda dhan nirukkum."
thirukkural: அழக்கொண்ட எல்லாம் அழப்போம் இழப்பினும் பிற்பயக்கும் நற்பா லவை.
english_translation: Gains from weeping, weeping go Though lost, from good deeds blessings flow.
moral: Gains from tears will go with tears; honest gains stay and bless.

title: The Prize That Didn't Last
hook: Suriya cheats in the school quiz and wins the trophy — by evening, everything falls apart.
story: Suriya peeked at the teacher's answer sheet. He won the quiz trophy. "Appa! Paaru!" But Kavi knew. "Neeya donga da." Suriya's happiness lasted four hours. The trophy fell and broke. "Appa, adhu... adhu en kaila irundhu vizhundhuduchu." Appa said, "Suriya, adhu dhan — sallaththa vantha porul, sallaththa dhan pogum. Iru maadhiri." Suriya cried. "Naan thiruppi kuduthuten."
tamil_dialogues: Suriya: 'Appa, trophy udainjuduchu.'
Appa: 'Sallaththa vandha porul, saththam podhum. Adhu dhan unmai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside the broken trophy, picking up a piece.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Suriya, sallaththa vandha porul — pasu man paanaiyila thanni maadhiri. Adhu nikkadhu. Nijamaana seyal dhan nikkum."
thirukkural: சலத்தால் பொருள்செய்தே மார்த்தல் பசுமண் கலத்துள்நீர் பெய்திரீஇ யற்று.
english_translation: The wealth gathered in guilty ways Is water poured in wet clay vase.
moral: Wealth gained by fraud is like water in a wet clay pot — it cannot hold.

title: The Iron Mind
hook: Anitha loses every race — Coach says the real strength is in her mind, not her legs.
story: Anitha came last again. "Coach, enakku mudiyadhu. Na oru loser." Coach sat her down. "Anitha, un kaal la thappu illa. Un manasula dhan thappu. Vinai thitpam na un manasu dhan, un kaal illa." Anitha started practising her mind — imagining the finish line. Next race, her legs moved like wind. She won bronze. "Coach! Naan mudichitten!" Coach said, "Nee un manasula dhan jeyichitta. Adhu dhan periya vinai thitpam."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Coach, enakku race la jeyikkanum!'
Coach: 'Un kaal dhan work aagum, aana un manasu dhan vellum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the finish line, a stopwatch in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anitha, seyal thitpam na oruvan manathu thitpam dhan. Matha ellam poi."
thirukkural: வினைத்திட்பம் என்பது ஒருவன் மனத்திட்பம் மற்றைய எல்லாம் பிற.
english_translation: A powerful mind does powerful act And all the rest are imperfect.
moral: Firmness in action is firmness of mind — all else is secondary.

title: Don't Cry Over Spilled Milk
theme: Vigour in Action / Don't Fuss, Don't Waver
hook: Jeeva breaks a glass of milk — Amma teaches him not to create fuss and not to give up.
story: Jeeva was pouring milk for his sister. The glass slipped. Crash. Milk everywhere. "Amma! Udaichitten! Enna pannradhu!" Jeeva started crying. Amma came calmly. "Kozhappam vera, thappu vera. Nee kovapada vendam. Indha kozhappatha sari pannu." Jeeva wiped. "Ippo rendavathu try pannu." Jeeva poured again. This time it didn't fall. Amma said, "Pathiya? Kozhappathula panick aavadha, thirumba try pannu."
tamil_dialogues: Jeeva: 'Amma! Pal udainjuduchu! Enna pannradhu!'
Amma: 'Kozhappam vera. Nee kovapada vendam. Thirumba pannu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the kitchen door, a fresh glass of milk in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Jeeva, munnadi kozhappatha panna vendam; panna aparam olkka koodadhu. Adhu dhan vinai thitpam."
thirukkural: ஊறொரால் உற்றபின் ஒல்காமை இவ்விரண்டின் ஆறென்பர் ஆய்ந்தவர் கோள்.
english_translation: Shun failing fuss; fail not purpose These two are maxims of the wise.
moral: Don't create fuss before starting; don't waver after starting.

title: The Secret Art Project
theme: Vigour in Action / Show Only When Done
hook: Harini shows her half-finished painting to her rival — the unfinished work gets mocked and ruined.
story: Harini was painting a Thanjavur doll. Half done. Her rival Divya came. "Enna da, adhu nalla illa. Naa vida nalla pannuven." Harini felt small. She tried to paint over. The whole thing became messy. Teacher said, "Harini, mudikkadhadha yaarukkum kaata koodadhu. Muthal mudichitu, aparam kaatu." Harini started fresh in secret. On competition day, her doll painting won. Divya said nothing. Teacher smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Harini: 'Miss, ava enna pathu fun pannita.'
Teacher: 'Seiyaradhai muthal mudichitu kaatu. Idaiye kaatina kastam dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands before the winning painting, studying it closely.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Harini, seyal mudichadhadha idaiye kaatradhu kastam tharum. Mudichadha kaatu. Adhu dhan valimai."
thirukkural: கடைக்கொட்கச் செய்தக்க தாண்மை இடைக்கொட்கின் எற்றா விழுமந் தரும்.
english_translation: The strong achieve and then display Woe unto work displayed midway.
moral: Complete your work first, then show it — showing mid-way brings ruin.

title: The To-Do Champion
theme: Vigour in Action / Do What You Say
hook: Kiran tells everyone he'll win the spelling bee — but when the time comes, he hasn't practised.
story: Kiran boasted. "Naa dhan winner! Spelling ellam en life!" Appa said, "Sollradhu sulabam. Pannradhu dhan kashtam." Kiran forgot to study. He lost in the first round. "Appa... naan lose pannitten." Appa said, "Sollradhu dhan easy, pannradhu dhan kastam. Ippo unakku puriyudha." Kiran started studying quietly. Next year, he still didn't win — but he came second. "Appa, naan try panniten. Matter dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Kiran: 'Appa, sollradhu easy aana pannradhu kastam nu unarchi varudhu.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — sollradhu ellaralayum mudiyum. Pannradhu dhan kastam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the dining table, a spelling list in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kiran, sollradhu ellarukkum sulabam. Aana solliyadhu maadhiri seyradhu dhan arumai."
thirukkural: சொல்லுதல் யார்க்கும் எளிய அரியவாம் சொல்லிய வண்ணம் செயல்.
english_translation: Easy it is to tell a fact But hard it is to know and act.
moral: Speaking is easy for all; doing what you say is the real challenge.

title: The Cycle Repair
hook: Ganesh wants to learn cycle repair but elders laugh — his determination wins everyone's respect.
story: Ganesh watched the cycle shop. "Thatha, naan cycle sari panna kathukka venum." Thatha laughed. "Un kai la weight illa da." Ganesh didn't give up. Every day he went. "Bhaiyya, enakku kathukudu." He failed. Chain broke. Tyre punctured. But he kept going. One day, a customer's cycle broke. Ganesh fixed it in 10 minutes. The customer said, "Intha payyan periya mechanic da!" Thatha beamed. "Vinai thitpam dhan unakku intha perumaiya thanthichu."
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Thatha, naan kathukitten! Paaru!'
Thatha: 'Nee vida mudiyadha paiyan dhan da. Un vinai thitpam dhan unna jeyika vachichu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on an upturned bucket, watching Ganesh fix a tyre.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ganesh, un seyal thitpam dhan unakku perumaiya kondu vandhadhu. Gandhan kooda unna purinjuka vechidum."
thirukkural: வீறெய்தி மாண்டார் வினைத்திட்பம் வேந்தன்கண் ஊறெய்தி உள்ளப் படும்.
english_translation: Dynamic deeds of a doughty soul Shall win the praise of king and all.
moral: Determined action will win praise and recognition from everyone.

title: The Math Wall
hook: Ranjani hates math — Amma shows her that strong belief is stronger than any formula.
story: "Amma, enakku maths varadhu! Naa fail!" Ranjani threw the book. Amma said, "Nee yaaru amma? Nee dhan ninaikira maadhiri dhan aaguvai. Unakku varum nu ninaichaa varum." Ranjani stared. "Ulladha?" "Ulladha." Ranjani started with one sum. Ten minutes. She got it right. "Amma! Vandhuduchu!" Amma smiled. "Nee ninaichadhu kedaikum. Adhu dhan vinai thitpam." Ranjani passed with a B. She never said "mudiyadhu" again.
tamil_dialogues: Ranjani: 'Amma, enakku maths varadhu.'
Amma: 'Nee dhan ninaikira maadhiri dhan aaguvai. Un manasula dhan baaram.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the study table, a math problem solved in front of him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ranjani, ninaipadhu dhan nadakkum. Un manasu valumai irundha, ellam kedaikum. Adhu dhan vinai thitpam."
thirukkural: எண்ணிய எண்ணியாங்கு எய்துவர் எண்ணியார் திண்ணியர் ஆகப் பெறின்.
english_translation: The will-to-do achieves the deed When mind that wills is strong indeed.
moral: What you firmly think, you will achieve — if your will is strong enough.

title: The Tiny Hero
theme: Vigour in Action / Don't Judge by Size
hook: Everyone thinks Chinna is too small to help — but when the bus breaks down, the smallest boy saves the day.
story: "Chinna, un kai la weight illa. Thalli nillu." At the bus stand, the driver couldn't find the fallen bolt. Everyone searched. "Enga irukku?" Chinna crawled under the bus. His small hand fit between the gears. He pulled out the bolt. "Saar, intha dhan!" The driver's jaw dropped. "Un kai dhan pani aachu da!" Appa stood proud. "Paaru, uruva pathi yaarum sonna, udan elangam pannadha. Chinna irundhaalum adhu periya vinai dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Chinna: 'Saar, naan eduthutta!'
Driver: 'Un kai dhan machine! Nee chinna aana periya paiyan dhan!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the bus beside Chinna, the bolt gleaming in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Chinna, uruvai pathi sonna udane elakkanam koodadhu. Periya vantha achu dhan pola."
thirukkural: உருவுகண்டு எள்ளாமை வேண்டும் உருள்பெருந்தேர்க்கு அச்சாணி அன்னார் உடைத்து.
english_translation: Scorn not the form: for men there are Like linchpin of big rolling car.
moral: Don't despise by appearance — the smallest part often holds the biggest together.

title: The Festival Float
hook: The temple car wheel gets stuck during the festival — everyone panics until Kumar takes charge.
story: The giant temple car stopped. The wheel was stuck in mud. People screamed. "Pudunga! Thallunga!" Nothing worked. Kumar climbed up, saw the wooden block jammed under. "Periyappa! Indha marathai edukkanum!" Periyappa said, "Over! Nee illa mudiyadhu!" Kumar didn't listen. He jumped down, pulled a lever. The wheel rolled free. The crowd cheered. Periyappa hugged him. "Nee dhan da hero. Thunjama illadha seyal dhan vellum."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Periyappa, idhu dhan problem!'
Periyappa: 'Unakku theriyudha. Nee thuninjha paiyan dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the temple car wheel, his hand on the freed axle.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kumar, thunjama illadha manasu dhan vinaiyai nikka vaikkum. Thunjuttu sey — adhu dhan vinai thitpam."
thirukkural: கலங்காது கண்ட வினைக்கண் துளங்காது தூக்கங் கடிந்து செயல்.
english_translation: Waver not; do wakefully The deed resolved purposefully.
moral: In the work you've chosen, don't waver — act without delay or fear.

title: The Burning Class
theme: Vigour in Action / Endure Pain for Gain
hook: Divakar wants to master the fire breath — but holding it burns. Teacher says pain is the path.
story: "Kanna, agni praanayama romba kastam. Un mula la eri varum." Divakar tried. His lungs burned. "Saar, eri varudhu! Mudiya mathiri illa!" Teacher said, "Kashtam varum — adha sahadhikradhu dhan vinai thitpam. Ippa adhu eri varudhu. Aana mudila na — adhu velikka varadhu." Divakar breathed through the burn. Day 10, he held it 2 minutes. Teacher smiled. "Kashtam irundhadhu dhan unakku veli thanthadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Divakar: 'Saar, eri varudhu! Mudiya mathiri illa!'
Teacher: 'Kashtam dhan vazhi. Atha sahadhikradhu dhan vinai thitpam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in lotus position, a small flame hovering over his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Divakar, kashtam vanthaalum vinai thitpam koodadhu. Kashtam dhan veli tharum."
thirukkural: துன்பம் உறவரினும் செய்க துணிவாற்றி இன்பம் பயக்கும் வினை.
english_translation: Do with firm will though pains beset The deed that brings delight at last.
moral: Though pain comes, do the work with firm will — the end brings joy.

title: The Last Lap
theme: Vigour in Action / Willpower is Everything
hook: Murali collapses 100m before finish — Coach screams "Un manasu dhan work aagum!" and he crawls to victory.
story: The annual Marina marathon. Murali was leading. Then his leg seized. Cramp. He fell. The second runner passed. Third. "Mudiyadhu saar... en kaal work aagala." Coach ran beside him. "Murali! Un kaal dhan work aagala. Aana un manasu work aagum. Thitpam dhan venum. Athu dhan ulagam ketkum!" Murali crawled. Ten meters. Five. He crossed the line. Last. But the whole crowd stood up. Coach cried. "Nee dhan winner. Un vinai thitpam dhan unmai."
tamil_dialogues: Murali: 'Saar, en kaal work aagala! Enakku mudiyaadhu!'
Coach: 'Un manasu dhan work aagum! Athu dhan ulagam ketkum!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the finish line, arms open wide, the sunrise behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Murali, vinai thitpam dhan ulagam virumbudhu. Veru onnum illa. Neeyadha vida, nill."
thirukkural: எனைத்திட்பம் எய்தியக் கண்ணும் வினைத்திட்பம் வேண்டாரை வேண்டாது உலகு.
english_translation: The world merits no other strength But strength of will-to-do at length.
moral: The world values no other strength as much as the strength of will.

title: தயக்கம் வேணாம்
hook: Mano wants to buy milk but keeps changing which coin to use — Appa shows him why hesitation ruins everything.
story: Mano stood at the Aavin booth, three coins in his palm. Ten rupees, five rupees, two rupees. He kept switching. "Appa, idhu dhan... illa idhu dhan." The queue got long. The booth uncle sighed. Appa took the ten-rupee coin and slapped it on the counter. "Idha kuduthutu vaa. Alukka vendiya vishayathula alundha, rendum poidum." Mano got the milk, but the five and two were still in his hand — wasted time.
tamil_dialogues: Mano: 'Appa, yedhula kudutha correct-nu theriyala!'
Appa: 'Mudivu eduthutu thayangina, adhu dhan periya thappu. Mudivu edutha odane seyyanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the milk booth, holding a single coin up to the sun.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee mudivu edutha pinna, adha seyyanum. Thayangina, onnum nadakkadhu. Thayakkam dhan sathru."
thirukkural: சூழ்ச்சி முடிவு துணிவெய்தல் அத்துணிவு தாழ்ச்சியுள் தங்குதல் தீது.
english_translation: When counsel takes a resolve strong Weak delay of action is wrong
moral: Once you decide, act fast — hesitation destroys everything.

title: Wait For It
hook: Vicky wants to pluck raw bananas from Thatha's farm — Thatha teaches him some things need waiting, some need speed.
story: Vicky saw a bunch of green bananas. "Thatha, ippove parichu podalam!" Thatha laughed. "Adha vida, andha thenkai vettu da — adhu vantha kaiyudan seyyanum." Vicky cut the coconut fast. "Ippadhi dhan — edha wait pannanum, edha odane seyyanum-nu therinjudha venum." Next week, the bananas ripened. Vicky understood: timing is everything.
tamil_dialogues: Vicky: 'Thatha, rendum ore maadhiri dhan — pattadhu, paachadhu!'
Thatha: 'Illada. Ethuku wait pannanum, ethuku odane seyyanum-nu theriya venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the banana tree, a ripe bunch in his lap, a green one in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thaamadham seyya vendiya vishayathula thaamadham seyyanum. Odane seyya vendiya vishayathula thaamadham panna koodadhu. Therinjudha dhan arivu."
thirukkural: தூங்குக தூங்கிச் செயற்பால தூங்கற்க தூங்காது செய்யும் வினை.
english_translation: Delay such acts as need delay Delay not acts that need display
moral: Know what to delay and what to do at once — that is wisdom.

title: School Fete Plan
hook: Keerthi's class wants a stall at the school fete but has no money — Akka shows the clever way.
story: "Akka, class-la ₹200 dhan irukku. Fete-la enna stall podradhu?" Akka was mixing kolam powder. "Unna irukka vazhi paaru da." She grabbed old bottles, painted them, filled with colored water. "Bottle throw game! Athukku romba porumai illa, aana nalla irukkum." On fete day, their stall earned ₹2000. Just using what they had.
tamil_dialogues: Keerthi: 'Akka, kasu illama enna pannradhu?'
Akka: 'Yaarum sollala da — irukka vazhi dhan nalla vazhi. Irukkadhadha nenacha enna payan?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the fete, holding a painted bottle, smiling at the children's game.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mudiyadhadha nenachi kovama irukka koodadhu. Mudiyadhadhula irundhu mudiyaradha paakradhu dhan arivu."
thirukkural: ஒல்லும்வா யெல்லாம் வினைநன்றே ஒல்லாக்கால் செல்லும்வாய் நோக்கிச் செயல்.
english_translation: It's best to act when feasible If not see what is possible
moral: Do what you can with what you have — find a way through every wall.

title: Half-Built Birdhouse
hook: Gowtham leaves his birdhouse half-finished — and a sparrow family suffers because of it.
story: Gowtham started building a birdhouse. Day one — excited. Day two — bored. He left it with no roof. Rain came. Next morning, a wet baby sparrow sat shivering inside that roofless box. "Amma! Adhu nanaichudu pochu!" Amma pointed. "Un vela mudiyadhadhala dhan adhu kashtam. Vela mudicha irundha, adhu irundhurukkum." Gowtham fixed the roof that evening.
tamil_dialogues: Gowtham: 'Amma, naan mudikkanumna adhu kashtama pochu nu ennakku theriyadhu!'
Amma: 'Vela mudiyadha vida periya kedumai illa da. Etti vittadhu neruppu maadhiri — suttu podum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside the fixed birdhouse, a sparrow perched on his finger.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee etti vitta vela dhan suttu podum. Mudiya varaikkum seyyanum. Illana, adhu thirumbi unnai suttu podum."
thirukkural: வினைபகை என்றிரண்டின் எச்சம் நினையுங்கால் தீயெச்சம் போலத் தெறும்.
english_translation: Work or foe left unfinished Flare up like fire unextinguished
moral: Unfinished work comes back to burn you like a smoldering fire.

title: Five-Point Plan
theme: Methods of Action / Planning Before Acting
hook: Dharani's uncle asks him to make 100 gulab jamuns for a wedding — and he fails because he didn't plan.
story: Periyappa's phone rang. "Darling, 100 gulab jamun venum, 2 hours la!" Dharani got excited. He grabbed dough, oil, sugar — but forgot to check if gas was full. Halfway, the cylinder ran out. Guests waited. Periyappa sighed. "Mundhe yosichirukka vendiyadhu — porul, karuvi, kaalam, idam, seyal-nu aindhu. Nee onnum yosichadhu illa." Dharani never forgot that lesson.
tamil_dialogues: Dharani: 'Periyappa, avasu dhan! Yosichadhu illa!'
Periyappa: 'Yosicha dhan seyyanum. Porul, karuvi, kaalam, idam, seyal — aindhum paathu dhan aarambikkanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the sweet stall, five ingredients laid before him in perfect order.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aindhum yosicha dhan velai nalla nadakkum. Porul irukka, karuvi irukka, kaalam sariya irukka, idam sariya irukka — appo dhan seyal nalla nadakkum."
thirukkural: பொருள்கருவி காலம் வினையிடனொடு ஐந்தும் இருள்தீர எண்ணிச் செயல்.
english_translation: Money and means, time, place and deed Decide these five and then proceed
moral: Plan five things before any task — resources, tools, time, place, and method.

title: River Crossing
theme: Methods of Action / Foresight and Risk
hook: Bala wants to swim across the Cauvery after rain — Thatha makes him count the risks first.
story: Cauvery was full. "Thatha, naan ninnikkaren! Nalla suvar irukku!" Thatha sat him down. "Mudivu enna? Iduvum enna? Payan enna?" Bala was confused. Thatha said, "Mundhe yosi da — nee naduvula saltha enna aagum? Nee kara serntha enna payan? Adhu yosichu dhan velai seyyanum." Bala watched a log float past — fast and dangerous. He chose not to swim.
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Thatha, naan ninnikalam-nu nenachen!'
Thatha: 'Mudivu, iduvu, payan — moonum yosichu dhan seyal nadakkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands ankle-deep in the Cauvery, watching the current with calm eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee seyra velai mudivula enna nadakkum, naduvula enna iduvu varum, kadasila enna payan kedaikkum — adhu yosichu dhan seyyanum."
thirukkural: முடிவும் இடையூறும் முற்றியாங்கு எய்தும் படுபயனும் பார்த்துச் செயல்.
english_translation: Weigh well the end, hindrance, profit  And then pursue a fitting act
moral: Before any task, think of the end, the obstacles, and the gain.

title: The Idli Master
theme: Methods of Action / Learning From Experts
hook: Srini thinks he can make perfect idlis without Paati's help — and ends up with stone-hard lumps.
story: "Paati, naan dhan pannuven! Nee pakaadha!" Srini mixed the batter, added salt, poured into molds. Ten minutes later — hard, flat, inedible lumps. Paati laughed softly. "Srini, unakku theriyum-nu nenaikra. Aana unakku innum theriyala. Muthalla nalla payal kitta kathukko." She showed him how much water, how much salt, how long to steam. Perfect idlis.
tamil_dialogues: Srini: 'Paati, naan pannalaam-nu nenachen! Adhu dhan fail!'
Paati: 'Mudiyadhadha muthalla nalla payal kitta kathukko. Appo dhan seyya mudiyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the kitchen table, a perfect idli on a banana leaf before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Seyra velai pattri nalla payal kitta kathukkradhu dhan arivu. Adhu illama seyya pona, ellam kettudum."
thirukkural: செய்வினை செய்வான் செயன்முறை அவ்வினை உள்ளறிவான் உள்ளம் கொளல்.
english_translation: Know first the secret from experts That is the way of fruitful acts
moral: Before you act, learn the craft from those who know it best.

title: Elephant and Mouse
theme: Methods of Action / Leverage and Strategy
hook: Chitra sees how mahouts use one elephant to catch another — and learns the power of smart strategy.
story: At the camp, a wild elephant had strayed into the farm. The mahout didn't chase it. He sent their trained elephant, Raja. Raja walked calmly, trumpeted softly, and the wild one followed him back. Chitra was amazed. "Appa, avana adichu kondu varaama... inbathu yanaiya vechu dhan!" Appa smiled. "Adhu dhan da — oru velai kondu innoru velai seyradhu. Vaal serukki pannalam."
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'Appa, nera adichu kondu varala!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan cleverness da. Velai kondu velai seyradhu — yaanai kondu yaanai pidikkradhu mathiri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between two elephants, one hand on each trunk, perfectly calm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru velai kondu innoru velai seyradhu dhan budhi. Nera nadakkama, velai kondu velai seyya koodum. Adhu dhan arivu."
thirukkural: வினையான் வினையாக்கிக் கோடல் நனைகவுள் யானையால் யானையாத் தற்று.
english_translation: Lure a tusker by a tusker Achieve a deed by deed better
moral: Use one task to accomplish another — work smart, not just hard.

title: The Bully's Pencil
hook: Sanjay wants to fight the class bully — Appa suggests a smarter way: make him a friend.
story: Kathir bullied Sanjay every day. "Appa, avan kitta sandai podren!" Appa said, "Sandai podradha vida, avan kitta nambikkai vaangidu. Enna solla solra?" Sanjay thought. Next day, Kathir had no pencil for the exam. Sanjay gave him one. Kathir stared. "Enakku...?" Sanjay nodded. After that, Kathir never bullied him again.
tamil_dialogues: Appa: 'Sandai podradha vida, avan kitta nambikkai vaangi. Athu dhan periya velai.'
Sanjay: 'Appa, naan avanuku pencil kudutha, avan shock aagi poittan!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the classroom door, a single pencil in his hand, dividing two boys no more.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathruvukku nanri seyradhu dhan nalla payan. Poi adichadhavida, avan kitta nambikkai vaangradhu periya velai."
thirukkural: நட்டார்க்கு நல்ல செயலின் விரைந்ததே ஒட்டாரை ஒட்டிக் கொளல்.
english_translation: Than doing good to friends it is More urgent to befriend the foes
moral: Winning over an enemy is more urgent than helping a friend.

title: The Tall Boy's Lesson
hook: Guna is the tallest in class and bullies shorter kids — until his mother shows him the power of bending.
story: Guna thought he was king because he was tall. He grabbed shorter kids' snacks, pushed them. Amma watched from the gate. That evening, she handed him a big box. "Idha thera vaikkira varaikkum kaappiyum thick-a iruntha, nee dhaan adjust pannanum." Guna couldn't fit the box through the door standing straight. He had to tilt it. "Unaku puriyudha? Valiya irundha dhan periya manushan illa. Kurai iruntha adha therinju adjust pannradhu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Guna: 'Amma, naan height dhan matter-nu nenachen!'
Amma: 'Valiya irundha kooda irakkiya venum. Irakkiya illadhavan dhan chinna.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the door, the tilted box in his hands, showing how humility enters.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Valiya irundhadhu dhan periya manushan illa. Kurai iruntha adhu therinj, adjust pannradhu dhan periya manushan."
thirukkural: உறைசிறியார் உள்நடுங்கல் அஞ்சிக் குறைபெறின் கொள்வர் பெரியார்ப் பணிந்து.
english_translation: Small statesmen fearing people's fear Submit to foes superior
moral: True strength is knowing when to bend — humility is not weakness.

title: The Class Monitor
hook: Kavya is chosen as class monitor but doesn't know how to lead — Appa lists the three must-haves.
story: "Appa, monitor aagitten! Aana enna pannradhu-nu theriyala!" Appa sat her down. "Munnadi moonu vishayam — Anbu, Arivu, Vaaimai. Unga class-la ellaarayum anba paaru. Unga velai ennanu therinju vaa. Vaaima sollu." Kavya tried it. She loved her classmates, listened to teacher, was honest about attendance. By term end, even the naughty kids respected her.
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Appa, monitor dhan aana enna pannradhu?'
Appa: 'Anbu, arivu, vaaimai — moonum irundha dhan nalla monitor aaga mudiyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the classroom door, a monitor badge glowing in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvar kooda velai seyra alavukku anbu venum, therinjudha alavukku arivu venum, nalla pecha vaaimai venum."
thirukkural: அன்புடைமை ஆன்ற குடிப்பிறத்தல் வேந்தவாம் பண்புடைமை தூதுரைப்பான் பண்பு.
english_translation: Love, noble birth, good courtesy Pleasing kings mark true embassy
moral: A good representative needs three things: love, knowledge, and honesty.

title: The Lost Puppy
hook: Pavi finds a lost puppy but can't convince anyone to help — Paati teaches the three secrets of persuasion.
story: A tiny puppy shivered near the silk stalls. "Paati, idha yaaru eduthukka mudiyum?" Paati said, "Sonna dhan aagum. Munnadi moonu — anbu irukka venum, nalla therinju pesanum, vaaimai irukka venum." Pavi went to the shopkeeper. "Uncle, unga kitta idha vaikka mudiyuma? Naan afternoon vandhu eduthukren." Her honest, loving words won him over.
tamil_dialogues: Paati: 'Pesa theriyumnu dhan ellaam nadakkum. Anbu, arivu, vaaimai — moonum.'
Pavi: 'Paati, naan nalla pesina, uncle idha vaikka mudiyum-nu sonnaaru!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside the puppy, one hand on its head, listening to Pavi's words.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pesa therinjavala solli dhan velai nadakkum. Anbu irundha, arivu irundha, vaaimai irundha, yaaraiyum convince pannalam."
thirukkural: அன்பறிவு ஆராய்ந்த சொல்வன்மை தூதுரைப்பார்க்கு இன்றி யமையாத மூன்று.
english_translation: Envoys must bear love for their prince Knowledge and learned eloquence
moral: Love, knowledge, and eloquence are the three keys to persuasion.

title: The Quiz Champion
hook: Hari is chosen for the inter-school quiz team but feels he's the weakest — Amma shows him the power of deep knowledge.
story: Hari was smart but nervous. "Amma, enakku varadha mathiri irukku. Munnadi school-oda champion irukkaanga." Amma handed him a book. "Veliya paakadha da. Ulla paaru. Unakku therinja vishayatha nalla therinju vekka vendiyadhu. Athu dhan vedi." Hari studied three topics deeply. On stage, when the champion fumbled, Hari answered every question clearly. His team won.
tamil_dialogues: Hari: 'Amma, avangalam romba therinjavanga maadhiri irukkaanga!'
Amma: 'Nee unakku therinjadha nalla therinju vechuko. Athu dhan vedi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the audience at the quiz, nodding as Hari answers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla kathukittu, nalla pesa therinja dhan vedi. Kathukkadhadhavan pesina, adhu veeram illa."
thirukkural: நூலாருள் நூல்வல்லன் ஆகுதல் வேலாருள் வென்றி வினையுரைப்பான் பண்பு.
english_translation: Savant among savants, he pleads  Before lanced king, triumphant words
moral: Be the most knowledgeable among the knowledgeable — that is true expertise.

title: The School Play
hook: Nandan wants to lead the school play but has no dialogue delivery — Teacher shows the three things he needs.
story: Nandan got the lead role. He froze on stage. Teacher took him aside. "Nee aindhu vishayam therinjirukka venum — munnadi moonu: azhagu, arivu, kalvi. Azhaga irukka venum stage-la. Arivu venum dialogue puriyadhukku. Kalvi venum adha nalla padikka." Nandan practiced posture, learned his lines deeply, studied the play's meaning. On finals night, he nailed it.
tamil_dialogues: Nandan: 'Sir, stage-la ninnadhum kaal vichukudhu!'
Teacher: 'Azhagu, arivu, kalvi — moonum irundha, stage unnadhu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands backstage, a mask in one hand, a book in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru periya velai seyya munnadi, moonu vishayam therinjirukka venum — good form, sense, lore."
thirukkural: அறிவுரு வாராய்ந்த கல்விஇம் மூன்றன் செறிவுடையான் செல்க வினைக்கு.
english_translation: Who has these three: good form, sense, lore Can go as bold ambassador
moral: Before a big task, prepare three things — appearance, intelligence, and learning.

title: The Compliment Box
hook: Ananya's friend stops talking to her because she speaks too bluntly — Appa teaches the art of sweet words.
story: Ananya told her best friend, "Un drawing nalla illa." Friend stopped talking. Ananya cried. "Appa, naan unmai dhan sonnen!" Appa smiled. "Unmai sonna pothum da. Nalla sollanum. Un drawing improve aagum — aana unaku pakka oru nalla touch irukku. Apdi sollanum." Ananya went back. "Sorry da. Unaku line-work nalla irukku. Ithula konjam work pannalam." They made up.
tamil_dialogues: Ananya: 'Appa, unmai sonna dhan thappa?'
Appa: 'Unmai sonna pothum, aana nalla sollanum. Kadiradha vida, sari seyra maadhiri sollanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the drawing table, a half-finished sketch of two smiling girls beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kodumaiya solla koodadhu. Nalla pesi, nalla vaai solli dhan velai nadakkum. Kadiradha vida sirichu sollu."
thirukkural: தொகச்சொல்லித் தூவாத நீக்கி நகச்சொல்லி நன்றி பயப்பதாந் தூது.
english_translation: Not harsh, the envoy's winsome ways Does good by pleasant words concise
moral: Speak sweetly, skip the hurtful parts, and bring good through cheerful words.

title: The Teacher's Request
hook: Rajiv must ask the strict principal for a holiday extension — Amma tells him to be fearless and choose the right moment.
story: Rajiv's grandmother was sick. He needed three more days leave. But the principal was a lion. "Amma, avan munnadi enakku bayama irukku!" Amma said, "Kathukko. Thairiyama iru. Kaalam paathu pesu." Rajiv waited till after the principal had his coffee — everyone said morning coffee made him calm. He walked in, spoke clearly without fear. Principal signed it.
tamil_dialogues: Rajiv: 'Amma, principal sir munnadi enna sollradhu-nu theriyala!'
Amma: 'Kettu kathukko. Pesa bayapadaadha. Kaalam paathu pesu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands outside the principal's office, a leave letter in his calm hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kathukko. Bayapadaadha. Kaalam paathu pesu. Nee pesradhu un velai — adha dhaeriyama pesanum."
thirukkural: கற்றுக்கண் அஞ்சான் செலச்சொல்லிக் காலத்தால் தக்கது அறிவதாம் தூது.
english_translation: Learned, fearless, the envoy tends Convincing words which time demands
moral: Be learned, be fearless, speak to convince, and know the right time.

title: The Flower Delivery
hook: Sangeetha delivers flowers to the wrong house because she didn't check the address properly — Paati teaches the lesson.
story: "Paati, ivalo flowers! Veedu vidu-nu kudukkalaam!" Sangeetha ran to the first pink house. Wrong house. Second pink house — also wrong. She was tired and the flowers wilted. Paati sat her down. "Kaadam therinju seyyanum. Idam therinju seyyanum. Yosichu seyyanum. Avasarapadradhu dhan thappu." Next time, Sangeetha checked the address, the time, and counted twice.
tamil_dialogues: Sangeetha: 'Paati, avasa dhan odi poitten! Thappu veedu!'
Paati: 'Kaadam paaru, idam paaru, yosichu sey. Avasaram dhan sathru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the correct doorstep, a single fresh flower in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaadam therinju, idam therinju, yosichu dhan seyyanum. Avasaram irundha thappu aagum."
thirukkural: கடனறிந்து காலங் கருதி இடனறிந்து எண்ணி உரைப்பான் தலை.
english_translation: Knowing duty time and place The envoy employs mature phrase
moral: Know your duty, choose the right time and place, then speak wisely.

title: The Three Pots
hook: Vetri's Thatha gives him three clay pots and asks which one is strong enough to carry water — teaching the three virtues.
story: Thatha placed three pots. "Yedhu nalla irukku?" Vetri picked the shiny one. Thatha filled it with water — it leaked. "Ithukku suttham irukku, aana balam illa." Second pot was plain but thick — it held water. "Ithukku balam irukku. Aana adha mattum pothuma?" Third pot was clean, thick, and had a handle. "Ithu dhan. Suttham, balam, thunivu — moonum."
tamil_dialogues: Vetri: 'Thatha, moonum iruntha dhan nalladha?'
Thatha: 'Ama da. Suttham, thunai, thunivu — moonum iruntha dhan yaaru munnadiyum nikkalam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the potter's wheel, three perfect pots beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Suttham, thunai, thunivu — moonum dhan venum. Oru velai sonnavanukku ithu moonum irukka venum."
thirukkural: தூய்மை துணைமை துணிவுடைமை இம்மூன்றின் வாய்மை வழியுரைப்பான் பண்பு.
english_translation: The true envoy of three virtues Is pure helpful and bold in views
moral: Three things make a true messenger — purity, helpfulness, and courage.

title: The Exact Message
hook: Divya is sent to tell the fish vendor "ammakku vendam" but is tempted to add her own drama.
story: Amma sent Divya to the market. "Selvi kitta poi sollu — innikki vendam-nu." Divya reached. Selvi akka was busy. Divya thought, "Nee munnadi kozhuppa kudutha, adhu nalla irukkathu-nu solren-nu sonna, romba nalla irukkum!" But she remembered. "Selvi akka, amma innikki vendam-nu sonnaanga." Simple. Direct. Selvi nodded. Amma later heard and hugged her. "Nee sariya solli irukka. Adhu dhan nambikkai."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Amma, avanga kitta nalla solla-nu nenachen! Aana neenga sonna maadhiri dhan sonnen!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da nambikkai. Naa sonnadha sariya sollanum. Adhu dhan viswasam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the fish market, holding a single word in his palm like a gem.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sonna sandhesam sariya sollanum. Athula onnum serkka koodadhu, eduthukka koodadhu. Adhu dhan nambikkai."
thirukkural: விடுமாற்றம் வேந்தர்க்கு உரைப்பான் வடுமாற்றம் வாய்சேரா வன்கணவன்.
english_translation: The envoy who ports the king's message Has flawless words and heart's courage
moral: Deliver the message exactly as given — nothing more, nothing less.

title: The Brave Note
hook: Karthik's best friend asks him to lie to save him from punishment — Karthik chooses the harder path.
story: Friends copied in the exam. The teacher asked Karthik, "Yaar copy panninaanga?" Karthik knew. His best friend, Ganesh. And Ganesh had begged, "Solladha da!" Karthik stood there. His heart raced. He remembered Appa's words: "Sariya iruntha, saavadhu vantha kooda, sariya dhan iru." He told the truth. Ganesh was punished, but later thanked him. "Neenga dhan unmaiyaana friend."
tamil_dialogues: Appa: 'Saavadhu vantha kooda, sariya dhan irukkanum. Athu dhan periya dhaeriyam.'
Karthik: 'Appa, avan en friend dhan — aana unmai periyadhu. Sonnen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between Karthik and Ganesh, his hands on both their shoulders.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sariya irukradhukku saavadhu vantha kooda, adhu dhan veeram. Sariya solli ninnadhu dhan periyadhu."
thirukkural: இறுதி பயப்பினும் எஞ்சாது இறைவற்கு உறுதி பயப்பதாம் தூது.
english_translation: Braving death the bold envoy Assures his king's safety and joy
moral: Stand by the truth even when it costs you — that is true courage.

title: The Fireplace Lesson
theme: Conduct Before a King / Appropriate Distance
hook: Murali sits too close to the firewood stove and burns his arm — Periyappa teaches the golden rule of distance.
story: Cold morning. Murali wanted to get warm. He sat right next to the stove. Too close. "Ai-yo!" His sleeve caught a spark. Periyappa pulled him back. "Paatha da? Neruppu kitta nerunga koodadhu. Anna vida thola irundha, kudi kudi kaayudhu. Romba thola pona, kaayadhu. Sariyaana thola virumbanum." Murali sat at the right distance — warm, not burned.
tamil_dialogues: Murali: 'Periyappa, saisam! Neruppu kitta dhan nikkalaama?'
Periyappa: 'Neruppu kitta nerungina suttudum. Thola pona kaayadhu. Sariyaana thola dhan venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the perfect distance from the stove, neither too close nor too far.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neruppu kitta nerungina suttudum. Thola pona kaayadhu. Sariyaana thola dhan — athu dhan budhi."
thirukkural: அகலாது அணுகாது தீக்காய்வார் போல்க இகல்வேந்தர்ச் சேர்ந்தொழுகு வார்.
english_translation: Move with hostile kings as with fire  Not coming close nor going far
moral: Keep the right distance — too close burns, too far leaves you cold.

title: The Birthday Gift
theme: Conduct Before a King / Not Coveting
hook: Shweta's rich cousin gets a new bicycle for her birthday — Shweta desperately wants one too but Amma teaches restraint.
story: Cousin Priya got a shiny blue bicycle. Shweta ran home. "Amma! Enakkum venum! Priya kitta oru cycle irukku!" Amma was washing clothes. "Enakku venum-nu nenacha, adhu unakku varadhu da. Priya cycle pathi yosichu, un cycle pathi yosikkara?" Shweta was quiet. She had an older cycle — not shiny, but it worked. She went and rode it. It still ran fine.
tamil_dialogues: Shweta: 'Amma, Priya kitta nalla cycle irukku! Enakkum venum!'
Amma: 'Mathavanga kitta irukradha paathu aasai padradhu nalladhu illa. Unakku irukradhu pothum da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside Shweta's old cycle, his hand on the rusty handlebar.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mathavanga kitta irukradha paathu aasai padradhu nalladhu illa. Unakku irukradhu pothum-nu nenaikradhu dhan santhosham."
thirukkural: மன்னர் விழைப விழையாமை மன்னரால் மன்னிய ஆக்கந் தரும்.
english_translation: Crave not for things which kings desire  This brings thee their fruitful favour
moral: Don't covet what powerful people have — contentment brings its own reward.

title: The Extra Chocolate
hook: Arul takes an extra prasadam packet when no one is looking — his own guilt catches him before anyone else does.
story: The temple distributed prasadam. One each. When the volunteer turned, Arul slipped a second packet into his pocket. His hand shook. All through the temple, he couldn't enjoy the first one. The second packet burned in his pocket. Paati noticed. "Arul, kaichila enna?" Arul broke down. "Paati, naan rendu eduthuten!" Paati took him back. "Kuduthutu vaa. Pettiyana edukkala vendiyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Paati: 'Un kaichila enna? Theriyudhu da.'
Arul: 'Paati, rendu eduthuten. Manasu sariya illa!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple prasadam counter, one packet in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pettiyana edukkala vendiyadhu. Sirappa irundha kooda adha pathuko. Illana, adhu dhan unna pathukkum."
thirukkural: போற்றின் அரியவை போற்றல் கடுத்தபின் தேற்றுதல் யார்க்கும் அரிது.
english_translation: Guard thyself from petty excess Suspected least, there's no redress
moral: Guard yourself from small temptations — once accused, clearing your name is hard.

title: The Library Whisper
theme: Conduct Before a King / Proper Behavior
hook: Sneha and her friend giggle and whisper loudly in the library — Appa's silent disappointment teaches more than words.
story: In the library, Sneha and Kavitha giggled. "Maga! Avan hairstyle paaru!" The librarian glared. They kept whispering. Appa, reading nearby, didn't say a word. He just looked at Sneha — a calm, disappointed look. Sneha felt worse than if he'd shouted. Walking home, Appa said, "Periya manushangalukku munna nadandha maadhiri nadakkanum. Kitta irundha sirichadhum, kaadha kitta sollradhum illa."
tamil_dialogues: Sneha: 'Appa, onnum sollala... aana un mosam paathadhum enakku kashtama irundhudhu.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan. Naan solla vendam. Unakku theriyum thappunu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the library, a book open, one finger to his lips gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periya manushangalukku munna, kaadha kitta sollradhum kitta sirichadhum pannakoodadhu. Adhu dhan mariyadhai."
thirukkural: செவிச்சொல்லும் சேர்ந்த நகையும் அவித்தொழுகல் ஆன்ற பெரியா ரகத்து.
english_translation: Whisper not, nor smile exchange Amidst august men's assemblage
moral: In the presence of elders, control your whispers and laughter — that is respect.

title: The Locked Diary
theme: Conduct Before a King / Respecting Privacy
hook: Nithya finds Akka's diary unlocked and is desperate to read it — until she remembers some doors should stay closed.
story: Akka's diary sat on the table. Not locked. Nithya's hand reached out. "Oru glimpse dhan." Her fingers touched the cover. She opened one page. Then another. Akka walked in. "Enna pannra?" Nithya froze. "Naan... tha... Akka, sorry." Akka took the diary, eyes wet. "Idhu en personal thing. Nee adha padikkradhu enakku kashtam." Nithya never touched it again.
tamil_dialogues: Nithya: 'Akka, naan... padichuten. Sorry.'
Akka: 'Adhu en personal space da. Nee adha matta vendiyadhu. Vera yaaravadhu sonna dhan unakku theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at a desk, a closed diary under his hand, respecting its secrets.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Personal vishayam. Yaaravadhu sonna dhan kekkanum. Illana, adhu dhan kalavu. Kadavai adachu dhan vaikkalam."
thirukkural: எப்பொருளும் ஓரார் தொடரார்மற் றப்பொருளை விட்டக்கால் கேட்க மறை.
english_translation: Hear not, ask not the king's secret Hear only when he lets it out
moral: Don't pry into others' secrets — wait until they choose to share.

title: Dad's Mood Meter
theme: Conduct Before a King / Reading the Room
hook: Vikram wants to ask Appa for a new cricket bat but Appa is in a bad mood — Amma teaches him to read the room.
story: Appa came home tired, frowning. Vikram bounced in. "Appa! Bat venum! Nalla bat!" Appa snapped. "Ippo illa!" Vikram ran to Amma. Amma said, "Kaalam paathu kelada. Appa mood paaru. Appa coffee kudichu, relax aana piragu kel." Vikram waited. After dinner, Appa was smiling, watching TV. "Appa, bat...?" Appa nodded. "Naalaikku vangidalam."
tamil_dialogues: Vikram: 'Amma, avara mood nalla illadha dhan ketten!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da. Mood paathu, kaalam paathu kel. Appo dhan kedaikkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the living room sofa, watching the family dynamics with a knowing smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Odarambula enna mood-nu paathu dhan pesanum. Vendaadha nerathula kettadhu thappu. Mood therinju pesu."
thirukkural: குறிப்பறிந்து காலங் கருதி வெறுப்பில வேண்டுப வேட்பச் சொலல்.
english_translation: Discern his mood and time and tell No dislikes but what king likes well
moral: Read the mood, choose the right time, and say what pleases — not what irritates.

title: The Exam Result
theme: Conduct Before a King / Speaking Wisely
hook: Harini failed math and has to tell her strict Thatha — Thatha teaches what to say and what not to.
story: Harini's math mark: 32 out of 100. She was terrified. Thatha was a strict man. She thought of lying. "Thatha... 52... illa 62..." But she remembered: Thatha always said "Poina solradha vida, nijatha sollu. Athu dhan periya dhaeriyam." She walked in. "Thatha, 32 dhan. Aana next time improve pannren." Thatha was silent. Then he nodded. "Adhu dhan da nijam. Nee solla vendiyadhu adhu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Thatha: 'Enna mark?'
Harini: '32 Thatha. Aana naan improve pannren. Nija maadhiri solliten.'
Thatha: 'Nee nijatha sonnadhu dhan periya mark. Adhu dhan venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the loom, a report card in his hand, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla vishayam dhan sollanum. Aana venum-na nijatha sollanum. Poina solli payan illa."
thirukkural: வேட்பன சொல்லி வினையில எஞ்ஞான்றும் கேட்பினும் சொல்லா விடல்.
english_translation: Tell pleasing things and never tell Even if pressed what is futile
moral: Speak what is good and true — even when pressed, never say what is useless.

title: The Youngest Cousin
theme: Conduct Before a King / Respect Regardless of Relationship
hook: Shiva thinks he can talk rudely to the young new bride because she's "just a cousin's wife" — Periyappa shows him rank.
story: At the wedding, the new bride was young — just 19. Shiva called her "Dei! Idha eduthuttu vaa!" Periyappa pulled him aside, face hard. "Unakku ava peru theriyuma? Avar un periyamma. Avala 'dei' nu sollradhu unakku thaguma?" Shiva tried, "Ava young dhan..." Periyappa cut him off. "Young-a iruntha enna? Avoda padavi dhan matter. Mariyadhai kodutha dhan unakku mariyadhai varum."
tamil_dialogues: Shiva: 'Periyappa, ava young dhan! Naan enna thappu sonnen?'
Periyappa: 'Ava un periyamma. Vayasu paakka koodadhu. Padavi paakkanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the wedding mandapam, one hand raised in blessing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vayasula kalavasa illa. Padavi dhan matter. Chinna payyan-a iruntha kooda, mariyadhai kodutha dhan avanukum mariyadhai."
thirukkural: இளையர் இனமுறையர் என்றிகழார் நின்ற ஒளியோடு ஒழுகப் படும்.
english_translation: As young and kinsman do not slight; Look with awe king's light and might
moral: Don't disrespect anyone because they are young or related — respect the position.

title: The Monitor's Mistake
theme: Conduct Before a King / Integrity Despite Privilege
hook: Rohan is class monitor and could easily mark his best friend present even though he's absent — he chooses integrity.
story: Rohan was monitor. His best friend, Sam, was absent. "Rohan, en peru present pottudu. No one will know. Nee dhan monitor." Rohan's hand hovered over the attendance sheet. He could do it. He was monitor — who would check? But his father's voice echoed: "Unakku adhigaram irundha, adha dharmama use pannu." Rohan marked Sam absent. Sam was angry. Rohan said, "Sorry da. Unaku nalla friend-a irukka, naan dharmama irukanum."
tamil_dialogues: Sam: 'Nee dhan monitor! Oru peru present podradhu periya vishayama?'
Rohan: 'Adhigaram irukradhu dhan. Adha dharmama use pannradhu dhan periyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the teacher's desk, the attendance register open before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku adhigaram irundha, adha dharmama use pannu. Illana, adhu dhan thappu. Yaar paathaalum sari nu irukka vendum."
thirukkural: கொளப்பட்டேம் என்றெண்ணிக் கொள்ளாத செய்யார் துளக்கற்ற காட்சி யவர்.
english_translation: The clear-visioned do nothing base Deeming they have the monarch's grace
moral: Those with clear vision never abuse their position — integrity is everything.

title: The Old Friend's Lie
theme: Conduct Before a King / Not Abusing Familiarity
hook: Deepak asks his best friend's father (the headmaster) for special favours because "he's like an uncle" — Amma stops him.
story: Deepak's best friend was the headmaster's son. Deepak thought, "Uncle dhan — naan ask pannalam! Nalla bench, free period-la leave..." He went to the headmaster. "Uncle, naan front bench la..." Headmaster looked at him coldly. "Idhu school. Illa veedu illa. Nee enna uncle nu kooda school la kekka koodadhu. Pathukko." Deepak ran back, ashamed. Amma said, "Old friendship irukradha vechu privilege edukkala koodadhu da. Athu dhan kedumai."
tamil_dialogues: Deepak: 'Amma, avar en friend appa dhan! Konjam favour keta thappa?'
Amma: 'Pazhaya nanbara vechu privilege kekkradhu dhan periya thappu. Adhu dhan unakku kedumaiyai tharum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, a line drawn between friendship and duty.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pazhaya nanbara vechu, privilege edukkala koodadhu. Adhu dhan kedumaiyai tharum. Unmaiyaana nanban adha seyya maatan."
thirukkural: பழையம் எனக்கருதிப் பண்பல்ல செய்யும் கெழுதகைமை கேடு தரும்.
english_translation: Worthless acts based on friendship old  Shall spell ruin and woe untold
moral: Never use old friendships to gain unfair favours — it brings nothing but ruin.

title: Appa's Silent Eyes
theme: Discerning by Signs / Reading Unspoken Thoughts
hook: Ananya realizes Appa is sad even though he's smiling — and learns to read faces.
story: Appa smiled all evening, but his eyes stayed empty. He didn't laugh at Ananya's jokes. "Appa, unakku enna achu?" Appa said, "Onnum illa ma." But Ananya watched his eyebrows — they were droopy like sad clouds. She hugged him. "Appa, neenga sonna maadhiri irukka, aana un kannu vera maadhiri solludhu." Appa's eyes welled up. "Unakku theriyudha? Nee dhan en kannu."
tamil_dialogues: Ananya: 'Appa, neenga sollale, aana un kannu solludhu. Unakku kashtama irukku.'
Appa: 'Nee sollidiche ma. Unakku dhan ellam theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands under a streetlamp, his shadow stretching toward them like a silent companion.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sollama irundha kooda, manasu mukathula theriyum. Atha pathu purinjukradhu dhan nijamana arivu. Nee sonnadhu dhan kural."
thirukkural: கூறாமை நோக்கக் குறிப்பறிவான் எஞ்ஞான்றும் மாறாநீர் வையக் கணி.
english_translation: Who reads the mind by look, untold Adorns the changeless sea-girt world.
moral: The one who reads thoughts without words is the jewel of this world.

title: The God Who Knew
hook: Vikram thinks temple priests are magic because they know what he's thinking.
story: Vikram walked the girivalam path with Thatha. A priest smiled at him. "Neeyum un akka vecchu sandai potiya?" Vikram froze. How did he know? He had fought with Akka that morning. "Thatha, ivar en manasula enna nadakkudhu nu kettutaara!" Thatha laughed. "Illada. Avan un mughatha paathu dhan sonnaan." Vikram understood: the face is a window.
tamil_dialogues: Vikram: 'Thatha, ivar kooda vandhu en manasula irukradha sollitaaru!'
Thatha: 'Mugham dhan kannadi da. Adha padikka therinjavan dhan kadal.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar walks barefoot beside them, his eyes gentle and knowing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mughathula dhan unmai irukku. Adha padikka therinjavanga dhan god mathiri. Nee adha kathukko."
thirukkural: ஐயப் படாஅது அகத்தது உணர்வானைத் தெய்வத்தோ டொப்பக் கொளல்.
english_translation: Take him as God who reads the thought of another man without a doubt.
moral: One who reads another's heart without doubt is like God himself.

title: The Friend Who Didn't Speak
theme: Discerning by Signs / Reading Silent Friends
hook: Saravanan's best friend suddenly stops talking — but a single glance tells him everything.
story: Muthu sat on the bench, not running, not laughing. "Muthu, enna achu?" Muthu shrugged. But Saravanan saw his thumb rubbing his own knuckles — Muthu always did that when worried. "Un akka health sari illaya?" Muthu's head snapped up. "Epdi da therinjudhu?" Saravanan smiled. "Un kai dhan sonnadhu." Muthu hugged him.
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Naan onnum sollala da. Apdi epdi therinjudhu?'
Saravanan: 'Nee solla vendam da. Un kai, un mugham, un kannu — ellam solludhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the slide, watching the boys with a warm smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sollama irundha kooda, un body dhan sollum. Adha padikka therinjavanga dhan nalla nanbar."
thirukkural: குறிப்பிற் குறிப்புணர் வாரை உறுப்பினுள் யாது கொடுத்தும் கொளல்.
english_translation: By sign who scans the sign admit at any cost in cabinet.
moral: Those who read signs from signs are worth keeping at any cost.

title: Amma's Fake Smile
hook: Deepika knows Amma is hiding something even though she's acting normal.
story: Amma hummed while cooking, but her shoulders were tight, her jaw clenched when she stirred. "Amma, innikki office la enna achu?" Amma stiffened. "Onnum illa." Deepika put her hand on Amma's arm. "Amma, un kaikatti irukku. Nee bayama irukka?" Amma sat down. "En boss sonnaan — enakku salary cut panniduvaan nu." Deepika said, "Namma pathukalam."
tamil_dialogues: Deepika: 'Amma, neenga sollala, aana un body dhan solludhu.'
Amma: 'Unakku dhan ellam theriyudhu ma. Nee en kannu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the stove, a ladle in his hand, steam rising peacefully.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mugham ore maadhiri irundha kooda, udambu unmaiya sollum. Adha purinjavan dhan arivaali."
thirukkural: குறித்தது கூறாமைக் கொள்வாரோ டேனை உறுப்போ ரனையரால் வேறு.
english_translation: Untold, he who divines the thought Though same in form is quite apart.
moral: The one who knows what's unsaid is different from others, though same in form.

title: What Good Are Eyes?
theme: Discerning by Signs / Eyes That Don't See
hook: Karthik says Priya is "dumb" — but later realizes he was the blind one.
story: "Priya, onakku onnum puriyadha? Na sollradhu kooda puriyala?" Karthik yelled. Priya's face went blank. Later, Karthik saw Priya feeding a stray pup. The pup was limping. Priya just looked at its leg once, then gently wrapped it with her handkerchief. She didn't ask. She just saw. Karthik felt small. "Priya... sorry. Naan dhan onnum paakala."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Priya, un kannu ellam ennaiku veerum? Nalla paakudhu!'
Priya: 'Kannu paakradhu dhan. Manasu dhan padikkudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a tree, eyes closed, a pup sleeping at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu irundha podhum, paakradhu dhan theriyum. Aana manasu irundha dhan padikkudhu. Adhu dhan vali."
thirukkural: குறிப்பிற் குறிப்புணரா வாயின் உறுப்பினுள் என்ன பயத்தவோ கண்?
english_translation: Among senses what for is eye if thought by thought one can't descry?
moral: What use are eyes if they cannot see what the heart feels?

title: The Mirror Face
theme: Discerning by Signs / Face Reveals All
hook: Meenakshi tries to hide her disappointment about not getting a new dress, but her face betrays her.
story: "Adutha varusham vaangi kuduren," Chithi said, putting the silk saree back. "Sari Chithi." Meenakshi tried to smile. But her eyebrows dropped, her lips curved down. Chithi turned. "Un mughathula dhan ellam theriyudhu." Meenakshi felt caught. "Sorry Chithi." Chithi laughed. "Sari sari, ithu vaangi kudukren. Aana nee unmaiya iru. Mugathula poi solladha."
tamil_dialogues: Chithi: 'Un mughathula irundhu dhan unmai theriyudhu. Unakku kashtama irukku.'
Meenakshi: 'Chithi, naan nalla irukka mathiri try pannena, aana unakku theriyudhu!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside a silk rack, his reflection clear in a polished brass vessel.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mugham dhan kannadi. Adha mooda mudiyadhu. Un manasula enna irukko, adhu mughathula theriyum. Adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: அடுத்தது காட்டும் பளிங்குபோல் நெஞ்சம் கடுத்தது காட்டும் முகம்.
english_translation: What throbs in mind the face reflects just as mirror nearby objects.
moral: The face is a mirror — it reflects whatever the heart feels.

title: The Teacher's Mood
hook: Venkatesh knows exactly when the maths teacher is angry — before she even speaks.
story: Teacher walked in. Her heels clicked slower than usual. Her lips were pressed thin. Venkatesh whispered, "Inniki avanga mood off. Summa irunga." Kumar didn't listen. "Miss, home work..." Teacher's eyes shot. "30 sums — ellarum pannittu vaa!" Everyone groaned. After class, Kumar asked, "Venkatesh, epdi da therinjudhu?" Venkatesh said, "Avala mughatha paathu dhan. Neraya visayam sollama theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Miss onnum sollala, aana unakku epdi therinjudhu?'
Venkatesh: 'Mugham, nadai, kannu — ellaam solludhu da. Kathukko.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans against the school gate, observing children with a knowing smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mughathula irundhu dhan unmai theriyum — kopama, santhosama, bayama. Adha padikka therinjavan dhan jnaani."
thirukkural: முகத்தின் முதுக்குறைந்தது உண்டோ உவப்பினும் காயினும் தான்முந் துறும்.
english_translation: Than face what is subtler to tell First if the mind feels well or ill.
moral: Nothing is quicker than the face to tell whether the mind is happy or angry.

title: Just Looking Is Enough
theme: Discerning by Signs / Knowing Without Words
hook: Janani sees Paati understand a beggar's need without a single word exchanged.
story: At the temple, a beggar sat silently. He didn't stretch his hand. He just sat, eyes down. Paati walked past him, stopped, went back, and placed money in his palm. Janani was confused. "Paati, avar enna ketka?" Paati said, "Kettukitte dhan irukkaan. Avala kannu solludhu." Janani looked at the beggar's face again — the hollow cheeks, the tired eyelids. "Ippo dhan puriyudhu Paati."
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Paati, avar ketkala. Apdi epdi unakku theriyum?'
Paati: 'Kannu dhan solludhu da. Nee avana mughatha paaru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple entrance, his hand extended — not to ask, but to give.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sollama irundha kooda, mugham sollum. Atha padikka therinjavanga kitta romba neram edukka vendam."
thirukkural: முகம்நோக்கி நிற்க அமையும் அகம்நோக்கி உற்ற துணர்வார்ப் பெறின்.
english_translation: Just standing in front would suffice for those who read the mind on face.
moral: For one who can read the mind from the face, just looking is enough.

title: Eyes That Spoke
theme: Discerning by Signs / Friendship and Enmity in Eyes
hook: Pavithra knows Nandini is angry even though she's saying "fine" — because the eyes never lie.
story: "Fine, naan fine dhan," Nandini said. But her eyes were narrow, like slits, and she looked away. Pavithra knew. "Nandini, un kannu sonnadhu sari. Neenga en munnadi poi sonna, aana un kannu unmaiya solludhu." Nandini's eyes welled. "Sorry Pavithra. Naan dhan unna pathi wrong-a sonnen." They hugged. Eyes don't lie.
tamil_dialogues: Pavithra: 'Nee "fine" nu solra, aana un kannu "fine illa" nu solludhu.'
Nandini: 'Un kannukku epdi da theriyudhu?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a stone bench, a small bird perched on his finger, looking into its eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu dhan unmaiya sollum. Bayam, kopam, pasam — ellam kannula irundhu dhan theriyum. Adha padikka therinjavan dhan nallavan."
thirukkural: பகைமையும் கேண்மையும் கண்ணுரைக்கும் கண்ணின் வகைமை உணர்வார்ப் பெறின்.
english_translation: Friend or foe the eyes will show to those who changing outlooks know.
moral: The eyes reveal both friendship and enmity — learn to read them.

title: The Keen-Eyed Girl
theme: Discerning by Signs / Eyes as Tools
hook: Nalini's uncle says she has the sharpest eyes on the street — and she proves it.
story: Uncle held up two gold chains — one real, one fake. "Indha rendula enna difference?" Nalini leaned close. She noticed the fake one had a tiny unevenness near the clasp. "Uncle, ithu poi. Ithula thappu irukku." Uncle's jaw dropped. "Un kannu romba keen da. Nee peruya pannuva." Nalini realized: eyes are the sharpest tool.
tamil_dialogues: Uncle: 'Un kannu dhan kadaiyula irukka periya weapon da. Adha kondu dhan vela paakka mudiyum.'
Nalini: 'Kannu dhan ellam aramba Uncle.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar holds up a single gold coin, his eyes reflecting its gleam.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu dhan namma ukandha kOl. Adha kondu dhan namma ulagathai alakkalam. Kannu therinjavanga dhan vishesham."
thirukkural: நுண்ணியம் என்பார் அளக்குங்கோல் காணுங்கால் கண்ணல்லது இல்லை பிற.
english_translation: The scale of keen discerning minds is eye and eye that secrets finds.
moral: The sharpest measuring tool for a keen mind is the eye — nothing else compares.

title: Akka's School Speech
theme: Discerning in Council / Words Suited to Audience
hook: Senthil watches Akka practice her speech differently for different people — and wins the prize.
story: Akka had to give a speech for the school competition. Senthil watched her practice. For Amma, she explained in simple Tamil. For Appa, she used big English words. "Akka, nee yenda matha mathiri pesura?" Akka smiled. "Kettaavanukku therinja maadhiri sollanum da. Adhu dhan jeyikka vendi." The next day, Akka won first prize. Senthil understood: talk the listener's language.
tamil_dialogues: Senthil: 'Akka, nee engalukku vera maadhiri, un friend ku vera maadhiri pesusra?'
Akka: 'Yaar kitta pesraano, avarukku therinja maadhiri pesanum da. Adhu dhan winning.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the back of the school hall, nodding as Akka speaks.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Solluvaraya sollanum. Aana yaar munnadi sollradhu nu therinju sollanum. Adhu dhan arivu."
thirukkural: அவையறிந்து ஆராய்ந்து சொல்லுக சொல்லின் தொகையறிந்த தூய்மை யவர்.
english_translation: The pure in thought and eloquence adapt their words to audience.
moral: Speak after understanding your audience — that is pure eloquence.

title: The Right Word at the Right Time
theme: Discerning in Council / Timing in Speech
hook: Arul wants to ask for a new cricket bat but keeps messing up the timing.
story: Arul wanted a new bat. First, he asked when Amma was rushing to work. "Poi!" Then when she was tired. "Summa iru!" Then Amma sat on the verandah with tea, looking peaceful. Arul sat beside her. "Amma, nalla irukka? Ennoda bat... odainjudhu." Amma sipped tea. "Sari, vaangi kudukren." Arul learned: time and place matter.
tamil_dialogues: Arul: 'Amma, innikki neenga relax-a irukkeenga. Atha dhan naan kaeten.'
Amma: 'Neraya peruku theriyadhu da — eppo, epdi, yaar kitta kaekkanum nu. Unakku theriyudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the verandah steps, a clay cup of tea cooling beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Eppo pesanum, eppo summa irukkanum nu therinjavan dhan aRivaali. Adhu dhan velikkum pathai."
thirukkural: இடைதெரிந்து நன்குணர்ந்து சொல்லுக சொல்லின் நடைதெரிந்த நன்மை யவர்.
english_translation: Who know the art of speech shall suit their chosen words to time in fact.
moral: Choose your words and your timing wisely — that is the art of speech.

title: The Boy Who Talked Too Much
theme: Discerning in Council / Speech Without Understanding
hook: Gowtham keeps interrupting elders at a village meeting — and learns why silence is better.
story: The village elders gathered. Gowtham kept jumping in. "Nanbu, indha vishayam..." "Thatha, naan solla..." Thatha pulled him aside. "Nee innum valara payyan. Avanga ellam 60 varusham paathavanga. Nee enna solla pora?" Gowtham felt small. He sat silently and listened. After the meeting, Thatha patted him. "Nee summa irundhadhu dhan periya visayam da."
tamil_dialogues: Thatha: 'Nee vaai moodittu irundha dhan nalla irukkum. Kathukku. Appo dhan pesa vendum.'
Gowtham: 'Thatha, naan romba pesiditen. Sorry.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the edge of the meeting, finger to his lips, eyes smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mudiyavanga munnadi pesradhu dhan theriyama irukradhu. Mounam dhan periya arivu."
thirukkural: அவையறியார் சொல்லல்மேற் கொள்பவர் சொல்லின் வகையறியார் வல்லதூஉம் இல்.
english_translation: They speak in vain at length who talk words unversed which ears don't take.
moral: Speaking without understanding the audience is like talking to the wind.

title: The Two Faces
theme: Discerning in Council / Matching the Room
hook: Lavanya sees Appa talk like a scholar in the hall and like a friend outside — and learns versatility.
story: Inside the book launch, Appa spoke in perfect English, using big words. "Post-modernism... dialectical..." Lavanya was bored. Outside, a tea shop uncle asked, "Enna saar, book eppadi irukku?" Appa said in simple Tamil, "Nalla dhan irukku. Vaangitu vanga." Lavanya laughed. "Appa, neenga rendu maadhiri pesureenga!" Appa smiled. "Audience ku therinja maadhiri dhan pesanum."
tamil_dialogues: Lavanya: 'Appa, neenga andha hall la vera maadhiri, inga vera maadhiri pesusreenga!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan arivu da. Yaar munnadi irundhaalum, avanga level ku pesanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the hall and the tea shop, comfortable in both worlds.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ella idathula ore maadhiri pesa koodadhu. PEsradhu yaar munnadi nu therinju pesanum."
thirukkural: ஒளியார்முன் ஒள்ளிய ராதல் வெளியார்முன் வான்சுதை வண்ணம் கொளல்.
english_translation: Before the bright be brilliant light before the muff be mortar white.
moral: Shine before the wise; be simple before the simple — adapt to your audience.

title: The Quiet One Wins
theme: Discerning in Council / Restraint Before Elders
hook: Kumaresan wants to show off his knowledge but his uncle tells him to stay quiet — and it pays off.
story: At the wedding, elders discussed politics. Kumaresan knew all the answers. "Periyappa, naan solla..." Periyappa squeezed his hand — stop. He stayed quiet all night. At the end, an old man said, "Indha payyan romba nallavan. Amaithiya irundhaan." The whole family praised him. On the way back, Periyappa said, "Pathiya? Solladha irundhadhu dhan unakku peruma saetichu."
tamil_dialogues: Kumaresan: 'Periyappa, naan dhan correct answer therinjudhu! Aana summa irundhen.'
Periyappa: 'Adhu dhan da jeyippu. Mudiyavanga munnadi summa irukradhu dhan periya arivu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits among the wedding guests, silent, observing with a gentle smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mudiyavanga munnadi summa irukradhu dhan nalladhu. Adhu dhan unakku mariyaadhai tharum."
thirukkural: நன்றென்ற வற்றுள்ளும் நன்றே முதுவருள் முந்து கிளவாச் செறிவு.
english_translation: Modest restraint all good excels which argues not before elders.
moral: Restraint before elders is better than all other good deeds.

title: The Slip That Cost
hook: Mani makes a silly joke in front of the chief guest — and learns about tongue-slips.
story: Chief guest was speaking about discipline. Mani whispered loudly, "Ithu bore-a irukku." Everyone heard. The guest paused. Teacher's face went red. Mani wanted to disappear. After assembly, Teacher said, "Nee oru periya mansula irukka aRivaali munnadi adhe maadhiri sola koodadhu. Athu dhan un vaazhkaile periya loss." Mani never forgot.
tamil_dialogues: Teacher: 'Nee solradhu dhan thappu. Oru periya manushan munnadi apdi sola koodadhu.'
Mani: 'Sorry ma'am. Naan inniku dhan kathukitten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the back of the assembly, his finger gently touching his lips.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ariya munnadi sola koodadha varthai sollradhu dhan nila thadaRRa madhiri. Adhu unakku kedumai tharum."
thirukkural: ஆற்றின் நிலைதளர்ந் தற்றே வியன்புலம் ஏற்றுணர்வார் முன்னர் இழுக்கு.
english_translation: Tongue-slip before the talented wise is like slipping from righteous ways.
moral: A careless word before the wise is like losing your footing on the path of virtue.

title: The Scholar's Shine
theme: Discerning in Council / True Knowledge Shines
hook: Nithya doubts her father's knowledge until she sees him debate with a professor.
story: Nithya thought Appa was just a regular office worker. Then at the library, a professor challenged him on Tamil literature. Appa calmly quoted verses from Thirukkural, explained Sangam poetry. The professor's jaw dropped. Later, Nithya said, "Appa, unakku ivlo theriyuma?" Appa laughed. "Adhu dhan da — karpadhu enna solladha vidhu, adha mathavanga munnadi kattradhu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Nithya: 'Appa, neenga ivlo deep-a padicheengala? Enakku theriyame poiduchu!'
Appa: 'Kathukittu irundha dhan, adha nalla palagatha munnadi kaatalam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at a reading table, a palm-leaf manuscript open before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karpadhu dhan visesham. Aana adha arivanga munnadi kattradhu dhan adhu oLikkum. Appo dhan adhuku vilai."
thirukkural: கற்றறிந்தார் கல்வி விளங்கும் கசடறச் சொல்தெரிதல் வல்லார் அகத்து.
english_translation: The learning of the learned shines valued by flawless scholar-minds.
moral: The knowledge of the learned shines brightest among those who can appreciate it.

title: Watering the Right Bed
theme: Discerning in Council / Speaking to the Wise
hook: Divya learns a difficult raga from her teacher — because the teacher knew exactly how to teach her.
story: Divya struggled with the raga. "Miss, enakku varala." Teacher didn't scold. She broke it down into tiny pieces, used a story. "Raga means color. Imagine sunset." Suddenly, Divya got it. She sang perfectly. "Miss, unga teaching style dhan enakku set achu." Teacher smiled. "Nee kathukka ready-a irukka — adha naan pathutu dhan sonnen. Unakku set aavadhu dhan naanum set pannen."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Miss, unakku epdi theriyudhu naan eppadi kathukkuven nu?'
Teacher: 'Ketta kELvi ketkra munnadi dhan naan unnoda katchi padichiten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at a tanpura, fingers gently plucking the strings.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arivulla munnadi solradhu dhan thanni poothula vittal maadhiri. Adhu dhan valarchi tharum."
thirukkural: உணர்வ துடையார்முன் சொல்லல் வளர்வதன் பாத்தியுள் நீர்சொரிந் தற்று.
english_translation: To address understanding ones is to water beds of growing grains.
moral: Speaking to those who understand is like watering a garden — it makes knowledge grow.

title: Don't Waste Pearls
theme: Discerning in Council / Choosing Your Audience
hook: Prabhu tries to explain science to a drunk man at the bus stand — his uncle stops him.
story: A drunk man was shouting nonsense. Prabhu tried to explain, "Illenga, moon dhan earth suththudhu." The man got angry. Chithappa pulled Prabhu away. "Enna da pannra? Avanuku puriyaadhu. Avan kitta née vEda vachchu aLanthaa, adhu dhan waste." Prabhu understood. Some people aren't ready to listen. "Chithappa, adha dhan naan ippo purinjiten."
tamil_dialogues: Chithappa: 'Unakku theriyum, avanukku theriyadhu. Adhu dhan thevai. Nee adha set pannita.'
Prabhu: 'Aana adhu nijam dhan Chithappa!'
Chithappa: 'Nijam dhan. Aana avanukku adhu kaekka manasu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stand, watching the drunk man, not speaking a word.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ella munnadiyum ore maadhiri pesa koodadhu. Yaar munnadi, eppo — adhu dhan therinju pesanum."
thirukkural: புல்லவையுள் பொச்சாந்தும் சொல்லற்க நல்லவையுள் நன்குசலச் சொல்லு வார்.
english_translation: O ye who speak before the keen forgetful, address not the mean.
moral: Never waste wise words on those who cannot understand — save them for those who can.

title: Sweet Words Sour Audience
