theme: Discerning in Council / Right Words Wrong People
hook: Saroja gives a beautiful explanation about kindness — but her cousin just laughs at her.
story: Saroja was reading a book about kindness. She excitedly told her cousin, "Namma ellarukum nallavadhu pannanum." Cousin laughed. "Enna da, bore-a pesura?" Saroja felt crushed. She went to Amma. "Amma, naan nallavadhu solla pona, avan sirichaan." Amma hugged her. "Adhu dhan da — avanukku adhu puriyaadhu. Sariyaana audience kitta dhan nalla vishayam sollanum."
tamil_dialogues: Saroja: 'Amma, naan nijam dhan sonnen. Aana avan sirichaan.'
Amma: 'Avanukku adhu puriyala da. Atha vida. Unakku therinjavanga kitta sollu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the kitchen, a bowl of sweet porridge before him, steam rising.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla vishayam, aana thappana idathula solradhu dhan amirthathai kuppai la vittal mathiri. Vetam."
thirukkural: அங்கணத்துள் உக்க அமிழ்தற்றால் தங்கணத்தார் அல்லார்முன் கோட்டி கொளல்.
english_translation: To hostiles who wise words utters pours ambrosia into gutters.
moral: Speaking wisdom to those who won't value it is like pouring nectar into a drain.

title: The Brave Speaker
theme: Not Fearing the Council / Confidence in Front of Experts
hook: Vasanth freezes in front of the judges — but remembers his father's words and speaks anyway.
story: Vasanth's science project was perfect. But when he stood before five judges, his throat dried. "I... I..." He wanted to run. Then he remembered Appa's voice: "Avanga dhan periyavanga, aana unakku dhan theriyum. Nee sollu." Vasanth breathed. "This project shows solar energy can..." He spoke. The judges nodded. At the end, they clapped. Appa was in the back, smiling.
tamil_dialogues: Vasanth: 'Appa, avanunga munnadi pesradhuku romba bayama irundhuchu.'
Appa: 'Bayam varum da. Aana adha vida, unakku therinjadhu solradhu dhan important.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands among the judges, his white beard gleaming, nodding at Vasanth.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periyavanga munnadi pesradhuku bayam varadhu. Unakku therinjadhu sollu. Adhu dhan veeram."
thirukkural: வகையறிந்து வல்லவை வாய்சோரார் சொல்லின் தொகையறிந்த தூய்மை யவர்.
english_translation: The pure fail not in power of words knowing grand council's moods and modes.
moral: Those who know their subject and their audience never falter before any council.

title: The Student Who Taught the Teacher
theme: Not Fearing the Council / True Scholar Speaks
hook: Rajesh, a village boy, corrects the chief guest — and earns everyone's respect.
story: The chief guest said, "Thiruvalluvar wrote 133 kurals." Rajesh's hand shot up. "Sir, 1330 kurals." The hall went silent. The guest frowned. "Neenga sonnadhu sari dhan." Everyone stared. Rajesh's heart pounded. After the event, the guest called him. "Unakku theriyum da. Nee nalla padichuruka. Unga mathiri pasanga dhan namakku venum." Rajesh felt ten feet tall.
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Rajesh, unakku bayama illa? Chief guest munnadi apdi sonna?'
Rajesh: 'Irundhadhu. Aana unmai dhan periyadhu. Appo dhan adhu sollanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the front row, counting on his fingers, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karpavan dhan karpavan. Unakku therinjadhu, unakku theriyum. Adha solla bayapada koodadhu."
thirukkural: கற்றாருள் கற்றார் எனப்படுவர் கற்றார்முன் கற்ற செலச்சொல்லு வார்.
english_translation: Among scholars he is scholar who holds scholars with learned lore.
moral: The true scholar is one who can speak his knowledge confidently before other scholars.

title: The Lion's Heart
theme: Not Fearing the Council / Fearless in Assembly
hook: Mahesh is afraid to speak at the Pongal function — Thatha tells him about warriors and words.
story: "Thatha, enakku stage la pesa bayama irukku." Thatha pointed to the Vellore Fort. "Indha fort ah paaru. Engayo veeran yudhathila sethaanga. Aana nee stage munnadi oru speech kuda pesa bayapadra?" Mahesh felt silly. Thatha continued. "Pesradhu dhan veeram. Nee unmai sonna, unna yaalum velkka mudiyadhu." Mahesh went on stage. His voice shook, but he finished. The crowd clapped.
tamil_dialogues: Mahesh: 'Thatha, stage munnadi nikkradhuku kooda bayama irukku.'
Thatha: 'Bayapadradhu dhan veeram illa. Adha overcome pannradhu dhan veeram.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the fort walls, looking out at the horizon.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sabha munnadi nikkradhu dhan veeram. Anga bayapadradhu dhan periya veeram illa. Sabhaya edhirnikkradhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: பகையகத்துச் சாவார் எளியர் அரியர் அவையகத்து அஞ்சா தவர்.
english_translation: Many brave foes and die in fields; the fearless few face wise councils.
moral: Many die in battle; the truly brave are those who fear no council.

title: Learn and Teach
theme: Not Fearing the Council / Humble Growth
hook: Chitra goes to a scholars' meeting expecting to be humiliated — but instead learns the secret of growth.
story: Chitra was invited to a scholars' gathering. "Enakku oru payyan library la enna theriyum?" She sat quietly. The scholars debated. She listened. At the end, an old scholar said, "Nee enna solla varala?" Chitra said, "Naan kathukka dhan vandhen." The scholar smiled. "Adhu dhan unmai. Therinjavanga munnadi kathukka varadhu dhan periya arivu." He gave her a book. "Nalla padichu, appo née sollu."
tamil_dialogues: Scholar: 'Unakku enna theriyum? Enna solra?'
Chitra: 'Enakku onnum theriyaadhu. Atha dhan kathukka vandhen.'
Scholar: 'Adhu dhan unakku irukka periya arivu. Therinjavanga munnadi kathukka varadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits among the scholars, a stack of books beside him, listening more than speaking.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Therinjavanga munnadi kathukka varadhu dhan unmai. Atha vida periya visayam illa."
thirukkural: கற்றார்முன் கற்ற செலச்சொல்லித் தாம்கற்ற மிக்காருள் மிக்க கொளல்.
english_translation: Impress the learned with your lore from greater savants learn still more.
moral: Share what you know with the learned, but always learn more from those who know more.

title: The Debate Champion
theme: Not Fearing the Council / Prepared for Any Question
hook: Bhuvana prepares for every possible question and wins the debate because of her preparation.
story: "Unakku oru question — thamizhagam inniku enna?" The coach threw random questions. Bhuvana answered each. But one question stumped her. "What if AI replaces Tamil teachers?" She didn't know. "I... I'll find out and come back." Coach smiled. "Sariyaana answer dhan. Adhu dhan — ninna idathula, theriyadha na therinjudu varuven nu sollanum." On stage, she used that line. The audience loved her honesty. She won.
tamil_dialogues: Judge: 'Neenga correct answer sollala. Aana un honesty dhan unakku jeyi thanthuchu.'
Bhuvana: 'Theriyadha na theriyadhu nu solradhu dhan periya arivu nu nenaikiren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the podium, a book open in his hands, looking confident.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arivu dhan aayudham. Atha kondu dhan sabha munnadi nikkalam. Nee dhan jeyicha."
thirukkural: ஆற்றின் அளவறிந்து கற்க அவையஞ்சா மாற்றங் கொடுத்தற் பொருட்டு.
english_translation: Grammar and logic learn so that foes you can boldly retort.
moral: Learn thoroughly so you can face any council and answer any challenge.

title: Books vs. Courage
theme: Not Fearing the Council / Knowledge Without Courage
hook: Dinesh has the highest marks but can't speak in public — Priya shows him the truth.
story: Dinesh always scored 99%. But when the teacher asked him to explain, his words tangled. "I... um... the answer is..." Priya, who scored 70%, confidently explained the same concept. Dinesh felt humiliated. "Priya, unakku epdi da idhu easy-a varudhu?" Priya said, "Nee padichadhuku enna use? Nee solla mudiyala. Padichadhula dhan use illa." Dinesh realized: knowledge without expression is useless.
tamil_dialogues: Dinesh: 'Priya, enakku dhan theriyum, aana solla mudiyala.'
Priya: 'Therinja dhan enna? Solla mudiyadhadhu dhan useless.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school entrance, a book in one hand, pointing to the stage with the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Padichadhuku enna use? Adha sabha munnadi solla mudiyadha na, adhu dhan vedam."
thirukkural: வாளொடென் வன்கண்ணர் அல்லார்க்கு நூலொடென் நுண்ணவை அஞ்சு பவர்க்கு.
english_translation: To cowards what can sword avail and books to those who councils fail?
moral: What use is a sword to a coward, or books to one who fears the council?

title: The Eunuch's Sword
theme: Not Fearing the Council / Useless Knowledge
hook: Mala reads every book in the library but hides when asked to present — Kavitha calls her out.
story: Mala had read 50 books. But when the teacher said "Mala, presentation," she hid in the library. Kavitha found her behind the shelf. "Enna da pannra? Nee book la moolichittu irukka, aana solla mudiyadha?" Mala whispered, "Enakku bayam." Kavitha said, "Nee padichadhu unakku oru aayudham maadhiri. Aana adha eduthu kaatamal irundha, adhu dhan vettri illa. Poi solra."
tamil_dialogues: Kavitha: 'Mala, un kitta weapon irukku. Aana nee adha eduthu kaatala.'
Mala: 'Adha dhan naan bayapadren.'
Kavitha: 'Adha vida, adha eduthu kaataama irukradhu dhan periya vedam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the library, a book open, his eyes meeting Mala's with encouragement.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Padichadhu dhan aayudham. Aana adha eduthu kaatama irundha, adhu dhan waste. Sabha munnadi nillu."
thirukkural: பகையகத்துப் பேடிகை ஒள்வாள் அவையகத்து அஞ்சு மவன்கற்ற நூல்.
english_translation: Like eunuch's sword in field, is vain his lore who fears men of brain.
moral: The knowledge of one who fears the council is like a sword in a eunuch's hand — useless.

title: The Silent Scholar
theme: Not Fearing the Council / Speak or Be Forgotten
hook: Eswaran knows more than anyone but never speaks — until his mother forces him to.
story: Eswaran knew everything. But in class, he never raised his hand. "Avana keta, avanuku dhan theriyum. Aana avan solla maatan." One day, Amma came to school. "En payyan ge nalla mark irukku. Aana avan yak kai ya korai?" Teacher said. Amma went home and told Eswaran: "Nee solla mudiyadhadhu dhan unaku periya problem. Padichadhu ellam use illa." Next day, Eswaran forced himself to answer. The class was shocked. Teacher smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Enaku un mark veerum, aana solladhadhu dhan problem. Padichadhu solla dhan.'
Eswaran: 'Amma, naan try pannuren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a temple stone, a young boy beside him speaking confidently to a crowd.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Therinjavanga munnadi pesa mudiyadhadhu dhan periya vedam. Padichadhula enna use? Sollradhu dhan use."
thirukkural: பல்லவை கற்றும் பயமிலரே நல்லவையுள் நன்கு செலச்சொல்லா தார்.
english_translation: Though learned much his lore is dead who says no good before the good.
moral: However much you learn, if you cannot speak before the wise, your knowledge is dead.

title: The Learned Fool
theme: Not Fearing the Council / Knowledge Needs Courage
hook: Harini is called a "learned fool" by her Thatha — and decides to prove him wrong.
story: "Thatha, naan class la first dhan." Thatha was silent. "Enna Thatha, santhosham illa?" Thatha said, "Nee padichadhuku enna use? Nee solla mudiyala. Therinjavanga munnadi nikka mudiyala. Adhu dhan 'kalla aRivaali'." Harini was hurt. But that night, she wrote a speech and practiced in front of the mirror. Next day, she volunteered for the oration. She stuttered, but she finished. Thatha hugged her. "Ippo dhan nee padiche."
tamil_dialogues: Harini: 'Thatha, naan innikki speech kuduthen. Bayama irundhuchu, aana pannen.'
Thatha: 'Adhu dhan da padichadhu. Solladha irundha, adhu dhan waste.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside Harini's mirror, his reflection smiling back at her.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Padichadhu dhan porum. Aana adha sabha munnadi solla theriyadha na, adhu dhan karpadhu."
thirukkural: கல்லா தவரின் கடையென்ப கற்றறிந்தும் நல்லா ரவையஞ்சு வார்.
english_translation: Who fear to face good assembly are learned idiots, certainly.
moral: Those who fear the learned assembly, though learned, are worse than the unlearned.

title: Breathing Dead
theme: Not Fearing the Council / Speaking or Death
hook: Manikandan is terrified of the village council — but his uncle says silence is a kind of death.
story: "Periyappa, avanga periyavanga, naan enna solla?" Manikandan shook. Periyappa grabbed his shoulders. "Unakku theriyum da. Solladha irundha, nee undu dhan, aana setha mathiri dhan. Nee sollu." Manikandan stood at the council. "Periyavanga... enakku oru suggestion irukku." He spoke about a library for kids. When he finished, the oldest member said, "Nee solladha irundha, namma kaekkama poidurukkom." Manikandan smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Manikandan: 'Periyappa, avanga munnadi pesradhu romba kashtam.'
Periyappa: 'Kashtam dhan. Aana adha vida kashtam — onnum sollama irukradhu. Adhu dhan saavadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the center of the council, one hand raised, speaking with calm authority.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sabha munnadi pesa mudiyadhadhu dhan periya vedam. Nee undu dhan, aana saavadhu mathiri. Pesu."
thirukkural: உளரெனினும் இல்லாரொடு ஒப்பர் களன்அஞ்சிக் கற்ற செலச்சொல்லா தார்.
english_translation: They are breathing dead who dare not express before the wise their art.
moral: Though alive, those who fear to speak before the council are counted among the dead.

title: The Village That Grew Together
theme: The Country / Unfailing Harvests and Worthy People
hook: Kumar's Thatha shows him why their village never goes hungry even in drought — it's not just the soil.
story: A dry spell hit Thanjavur. Kumar watched the paddy wither. "Thatha, veLLam varadhu. Namma enna pannuvoam?" Thatha took him to the temple tank. Every family dug a channel from their own well to the common field. The rich shared water pumps, the strong dug, the old cooked. Within a week, the fields turned green again. Kumar's eyes widened. "Thatha, namma ooruku edhavadhu sIrappu irukku." Thatha smiled. "Oru ooru nu sonnadhu, mannu mattum illa da — manushan dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Thatha, enga ooruku mattum dhan veLLam varala?'
Thatha: 'Illada. Aana namma oorla ullavanga thaan matter. Adhu dhan periya velai.'
Kumar: 'Ellaarum serna kaapathittaanga!'
Thatha: 'Adhu dhan nalla naadu — ellaarum ninaikkaadhadhu dhan nadakkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands knee-deep in a lush green paddy field, holding a single grain of rice.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un thaatha sonna maadhiri — nalla naadu nu sonnadhu, vilai mattum illa. Anga ullavanga thaan naadu. Avar seyal dhan naadu."
thirukkural: தள்ளா விளையுளும் தக்காரும் தாழ்விலாச் செல்வரும் சேர்வது நாடு.
english_translation: It's country which has souls of worth, unfailing yields and ample wealth.
moral: A true land is not just fertile soil — it's worthy people who share and sustain.

title: Pappu's Pest-Free Plan
hook: Pappu's vegetable garden is destroyed by pests — Amma teaches him about natural balance.
story: Pappu planted brinjal, tomato, chillies. He watered daily, sang to them. But bugs came. Leaves curled. Fruits rotted. "Amma, ennoda garden ellam kettu pochu!" Amma brought ladybugs from the corner field. "Paaru da, ithu dhan un garden ku guard." The ladybugs ate the pests. Within a week, the garden bloomed. Pappu's brinjal grew fat and purple. "Amma, naan kondu vanthen, aana neenga dhan kaapathicheenga." Amma laughed. "Illada, prakruthi dhan. Nalla vilaindha edathula edhuvum kettadhu irukkaadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Pappu: 'Amma, poochi ellam en garden a kettu poduthu!'
Amma: 'Adhuku dhan ithu. Ithu prakruthi police.'
Pappu: 'Inimae naan marundu poda maaten!'
Amma: 'Ama da. Nalla vilaindha naadula kettadhu irukkaadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in Pappu's garden, a ladybug on his fingertip.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un thottam vilaindhadhu — kettadha marikka therinja dhan. Naadhum adhu dhan. Kettadha illadhadhu dhan nalla naadu."
thirukkural: பெரும்பொருளால் பெட்டக்க தாகி அருங்கேட்டால் ஆற்ற விளைவது நாடு.
english_translation: The land has large luring treasure where pests are nil and yields are sure.
moral: A rich land isn't just abundant — it's free from what destroys.

title: The Tax That Built a School
theme: The Country / Bearing Burdens for the King
hook: Vennila is upset when Appa pays taxes — until she sees her new classroom built with that money.
story: Vennila's Appa gave a big bag of rice to the village officer. "Appa, enna kudukreenga? Namma padikkara school ku kodukalaama?" Appa explained. "Idhu dhan variththu ma. Intha variththula dhan school, road, hospital ellam varum." Vennila sulked. Next month, her school got a new building — bright, with fans and a library. Her classroom had windows. "Appa, namma kudutha rice dhan ivai ellama?" Appa nodded. "Variththu ilana, naadu nadakkadhu da."
tamil_dialogues: Vennila: 'Appa, namma kastama sampaichadha kudukradhu enna?'
Appa: 'Variththu dhan naadu. Namma kudutha dhan nammakku thirupi varum.'
Vennila: 'School kuda adhula dhan vandhuchu?'
Appa: 'Ama. Adhu dhan naadu — thaaṅgi dhan mēlpādhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the new school entrance, a single brick in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vennila, variththu kodukradhu kashtam dhan. Aana adhu naattai kaapadhikkum. Thalai mela viLaiyadhadhu dhan nadai."
thirukkural: பொறையொருங்கு மேல்வருங்கால் தாங்கி இறைவற்கு இறையொருங்கு நேர்வது நாடு.
english_translation: It's land that bears pressing burdens and pays its tax which king demands.
moral: A true land shoulders its burdens — taxes build the nation.

title: The Hungry Summer That Never Came
theme: The Country / Free from Famine and Disease
hook: Somu hears old stories of famine — and realizes his village has something special.
story: Paati told Somu about the "karuv kalam" — the famine year when people ate cactus. "Paati, unmaiya? Aama saapdadha kedaiyadha?" Somu was scared. But when he looked around — his street had bananas, coconuts, mangoes falling from trees. The hospital was clean. No one was sick. "Paati, namma ooruku enna kashtam varadhu?" Paati smiled. "Namma naadu nalla irukku da. Pasiyum, piniyum, pagaiyum illadhadhu dhan nalla naadu." Somu hugged her tight.
tamil_dialogues: Somu: 'Paati, munnadi mazhai illama pasi vanthuchu nu sonneenga?'
Paati: 'Ama da. Aana namma ooruku adhu varadhu. Adhu dhan periya visesham.'
Somu: 'Adhu enna namma ooruku varaadhu?'
Paati: 'Namma naadu kaapadhuthu da. Oru naadu kaapadhuna, adhu dhan periya naadu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a mango tree heavy with fruit, sharing with village children.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pasi illadhadhu, pini illadhadhu, pagai illadhadhu — adhu dhan nalla naadu. Unakku ulagathula irukka nalla idham."
thirukkural: உறுபசியும் ஓவாப் பிணியும் செறுபகையும் சேரா தியல்வது நாடு.
english_translation: It is country which is free from fierce famine, plague and foemen's harm.
moral: A great land is one where hunger, disease, and enemies stay away.

title: The Broken Faction
hook: Anitha's street is divided by a silly fight between two families — she decides to fix it.
story: Two families on Anitha's street stopped talking over a chicken that crossed the wrong yard. No kids played together. No one shared food. Anitha felt sick. "Appa, ivanga rendu perum sanda pottukitu, namma street a kettu podraanga." Appa sighed. "Antha kozhi dhan karanam." Anitha baked a cake, wrote "Idhu dhan namma street ku samadhaanam," and took it to both houses. The families looked at each other and laughed. That evening, the street had a party.
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Appa, ivanga rendu perum sanda pottutu oor a kettu podraanga!'
Appa: 'Kuzhu pirakkum podhu naadu kettudum da.'
Anitha: 'Naan avanga rendu perukum cake kudukaren.'
Appa: 'Nee dhan naadu kaapadhapora da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the street party, eating a slice of cake.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kuzhu piriyum podhu naadu kettudum. Sanda illadhadhu dhan nalla naadu. Nee sonnadhu sari dhan — onna serdhadhu dhan namma balam."
thirukkural: பல்குழுவும் பாழ்செய்யும் உட்பகையும் வேந்தலைக்கும் கொல்குறும்பும் இல்லது நாடு.
english_translation: Sects and ruinous foes are nil, no traitors in a land tranquil.
moral: A great land has no internal fights — unity is its strength.

title: The Shop That Never Closed
theme: The Country / Wealth That Does Not Decline
hook: Manju's family shop faces a mega-mall opening across the street — but their regulars stay loyal.
story: A shiny new mall opened opposite Manju's small grocery. "Amma, ivanga ivalo periya shop, nammala velliduvanga." Manju was sure they'd lose everything. But that evening, old Mr. Krishnan came, bought his usual 100g tea. Then came Mrs. Lakshmi for her brand of oil. Then the auto driver for his daily beedi. Manju counted — more customers than ever. "Amma, avanga namma kitta dhan varanga!" Amma hugged him. "Silai illadhadhu dhan naadu. Namma sila nammakku dhan — athai yaalum edukka mudiyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Manjunath: 'Amma, avalO periya shop vanthuduchu. Namma enna pannuvoam?'
Amma: 'Namma sila nammakku dhan da. Athai avanga edukka mudiyadhu.'
Manjunath: 'Aana avanga rate kammi da!'
Amma: 'Nambikkai ku rate illa da. Adhu dhan periya silai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits behind the shop counter, counting coins in an old brass box.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ketadha edathula kuda silai kundriyadhadhu dhan naadu. Un amma sonna maadhiri — nambikkai dhan periya silai."
thirukkural: கேடறியாக் கெட்ட இடத்தும் வளங்குன்றா நாடென்ப நாட்டின் தலை.
english_translation: The land of lands no ruin knows — even in grief its wealth yet grows.
moral: The greatest land is one whose wealth doesn't crumble, even in hard times.

title: The Four Guardians
theme: The Country / Waters, Hills, Forests, Forts
hook: Chitra's Thatha shows her how mountains, rivers, forests, and a watchtower are like a person's limbs.
story: Thatha took Chitra on a hike. First, they climbed the hill — "Idhu dhan namma kaal." Then they crossed a stream — "Idhu namma kai." Then a dense forest — "Idhu namma udal." Finally, an old watchtower — "Idhu namma thalai." Chitra laughed. "Thatha, idhu ellam namma ooruku uruppu!" Thatha nodded. "Ama da. Mala, neer, kaadu, aran — naaduku ivai dhan uruppu. Idhu ellam illadhadhu naadu mudiyadhu." Chitra looked at the valley and felt safe.
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'Thatha, yaarum idha kaapadhara?'
Thatha: 'Amma, idhan dhan naadu. Mala, neer, kaadu, aran — ellam serna dhan naadu.'
Chitra: 'Appo namma ooruku ellaam irukku!'
Thatha: 'Ama. Adhu dhan namma kashtam varadha kaapadhum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the watchtower, one hand on the stone, looking across the valley.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naaduku uruppu irukka vendum: malai, neer, kaadu, aran. Adhu pola dhan manushanukum kaal, kai, thol, thalai."
thirukkural: இருபுனலும் வாய்ந்த மலையும் வருபுனலும் வல்லரணும் நாட்டிற்கு உறுப்பு.
english_translation: Waters up and down, hills and streams with strong forts as limbs country beams.
moral: A strong land has four limbs — hills, waters, forests, and forts.

title: The Five Jewels
theme: The Country / Five Jewels of a Nation
hook: Gowri's Appa asks her to name the five most precious things in their town — her answer surprises him.
story: Appa took Gowri to the top of the hill. "Paaru, namma oorukku enna enna nalla irukku nu sol." Gowri thought. "Pasi illa — namma oorla yaarum pasiyoda irukka maataanga." Appa nodded. "Appuram?" "Ella vithaium nalla vilaiyudhu — nel, thengu, vaazhai." "Appuram?" "Namma oorla sandhosama irukkaanga — sanda illa." "Innum?" "Oru edathula bayam illadha irukka mudiyudhu." Appa smiled. "Naadukku anju aabaranam: pini illadhadhu, selvu, vilai, sandhosam, kaappu. Adhu dhan naam."
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Appa, namma oorla yaarum aaspathirikku poga maataanga.'
Appa: 'Pini illadhadhu dhan mudhala aabaranam.'
Gowri: 'Maranam dhaan selvu!'
Appa: 'Ama. Adhu dhan aindhaavadhu — kaappu. Unakku adhu puriyudha?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the hilltop, five flowers in his hand, tossing them one by one into the wind.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pini illadhadhu, selvu, vilai, sandhosam, kaappu — naadukku anju aabaranam. Unakku ithu purindha, naadu purindha maadhiri."
thirukkural: பிணியின்மை செல்வம் விளைவின்பம் ஏமம் அணியென்ப நாட்டிவ் வைந்து.
english_translation: Rich yield, delight, defence and wealth are jewels of lands with blooming health.
moral: Health, wealth, harvest, happiness, safety — these five are a land's true gems.

title: The Idle Mango Tree
theme: The Country / Land That Yields Without Toil
hook: Velu thinks land is only valuable if you work hard on it — until a mango tree feeds him without effort.
story: Velu was lazy. "Enna pannitu irukka? Naadhaan vilacha dhan vilaiyum." Rajan took him to an old mango grove. They sat under a tree. A mango fell. Then another. They ate, juicy sweet. Velu looked up. "Idha yaarum vilacha maata?" Rajan laughed. "Adhu dhan — yaarum vilachaadhadhu dhan vilaiyudhu!" Velu understood. "Adha dhan oru nalla naadu. Yarum vilachaadhadhu vilaindha dhan naadu." He felt small for thinking hard work was everything.
tamil_dialogues: Velu: 'Idha yaarum vilachaadhadhu dhan vilaiyudhu?'
Rajan: 'Ama. Adhu dhan naadu — yarum kettadhu illadhadhu vilaiyum.'
Velu: 'Appo namma vilacha dhan vilaiyuma?'
Rajan: 'Vilachadhum vilaiyum, vilachaadhadhum vilaiyum. Adhu dhan nalla naadu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the mango tree, catching a falling fruit in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Velu, vilaiya vilaiyaadhadha vilaiyadhu dhan naadu. Adhu dhan prakruthi. Nee vilachadhum vilaiyum — adhu dhan periya naadu."
thirukkural: நாடென்ப நாடா வளத்தன நாடல்ல நாட வளந்தரு நாடு.
english_translation: A land is land which yields unsought; needing hard work the land is nought.
moral: A true land gives of its own accord — one that only yields through labor is no land at all.

title: The Kingless Village
theme: The Country / A Land Needs a Leader
hook: Selvam's village panchayat leader resigns — and the village falls into chaos without a head.
story: The panchayat president resigned. Suddenly, no one collected trash. The streetlight stayed broken. The water pump wasn't fixed. People fought over borewell water. Selvam was confused. "Amma, munnadi ellam nalla irundhadhu. Ippo enna achu?" Amma sighed. "Thalaivan illadhadhu naadu kettudum da. Ethaiyum silai vachaalum, oru thalai illana, adhu vEru." Selvam started a petition. The whole village signed. A new leader was elected. The next week, the streetlight glowed again.
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Amma, enga ooruku thaalaivan illadhadhu enna aagum?'
Amma: 'Naadu kettudum da. Ethaiyum oruvar nirvaahikkanum.'
Selvam: 'Naan petition kudukaren. Ellaarum sign pannanum!'
Amma: 'Nee sonnadhu sari dhan — thalai illana naadu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands under the newly-lit streetlight, a petition in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Edhaiyum silai vachaalum, oru thalai illana naadu mudiyadhu. Oruvar nirvaahirpadhu dhan naadu."
thirukkural: ஆங்கமை வெய்தியக் கண்ணும் பயமின்றே வேந்தமை வில்லாத நாடு.
english_translation: Though a land has thus every thing, it is worthless without a king.
moral: Even the richest land is useless without a good leader to guide it.

title: The Treehouse Lesson
hook: Ramesh builds a treehouse fort — but learns it must protect what's inside and withstand what's outside.
story: Ramesh built a treehouse with Appa. It had a rope ladder, walls, a lookout. His cousin Vicky came. "Naan attack!" He shook the tree. The mangoes fell. The treehouse wobbled. Ramesh was scared. "Appa, adhu kettudume?" Appa climbed up. "Ramesh, aran na sonnadhu, por la ninnu kaapadhuradhum, sathru thaduppadhum. Neeyum vellanum, kaapadhuvanum." Ramesh reinforced the branches. Next attack, Vicky couldn't shake it. Ramesh stood tall. "Enoda aran dhaan velluchu!"
tamil_dialogues: Ramesh: 'Appa, andha veedu oonjalam aadudhu!'
Appa: 'Aran rendum pannanum. Kai kaapadhanum, sathru thalaiyum mathanum.'
Ramesh: 'Naan balamana aran kattanum.'
Appa: 'Ama. Adhu dhan oru nalla aran. Vellavadhu kaapadradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a branch of the treehouse, looking out like a sentinel.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aran nu sonnadhu, vellava irundha mathava kaapadhadhu. Athai panna therinjavandhan veeran. Un odambum aran dhan."
thirukkural: ஆற்றுபவர்க்கும் அரண்பொருள் அஞ்சித்தற் போற்றுபவர்க்கும் பொருள்.
english_translation: The fort is vital for offence; who fear the foes have its defence.
moral: A good fort both defends its own and helps in battle — strength has two faces.

title: Kuttu's Fort of Four
hook: Thatha challenges Kuttu to build a "real fort" using only what nature provides — water, mud, rocks, and sticks.
story: Thatha gave Kuttu a challenge. "Oru aran kattu — thanni, mannu, kal, katthai mathum vachi." Kuttu dug a moat (water). Piled mud walls. Stacked rocks. Planted thorny branches around. His fort was small but strong. Thatha tested it — pushed, kicked. It held. "Thatha, idhu dhan aran!" Thatha lifted him. "Ama da. Mani neer, mannu, malai, kaadu — naalum irundha aran. Adhu dhan periya aran."
tamil_dialogues: Kuttu: 'Thatha, mannula aran kaatta mudiyuma?'
Thatha: 'Mani neer, mannu, malai, nilam — andha naalum irundha aran mudiyum.'
Kuttu: 'Naan kattiten! Paaru!'
Thatha: 'Ithu dhan periya aran. Kaadhum mannum neerum serna aran aagum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside Kuttu's tiny fort, pouring water into the moat carefully.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neer, mannu, kal, kaadu — andha naalu uruppum serna aran. Atha vachu dhan pagaiyara thadukka mudiyum."
thirukkural: மணிநீரும் மண்ணும் மலையும் அணிநிழற் காடும் உடைய தரண்.
english_translation: A crystal fount, a space, a mount, thick woods form a fort paramount.
moral: The best fortress uses nature's four elements: water, land, hill, and forest.

title: The Three Tests
theme: Fortresses / High, Broad, Strong, Hard to Access
hook: Vasanth's PT teacher sets up an obstacle course that tests height, width, strength, and reach.
story: PT Master announced: "Nalum aran. Uyaram, agalam, uRuthi, aRumai." Vasanth had to jump a high wall (height), cross a wide pit (breadth), carry a heavy rock (strength), and squeeze through a narrow tunnel (hard access). He failed the first three times. "Sir, mudiyala!" The teacher stood on the Rockfort steps. "Aran pola da — uyarama iruntha, agalama iruntha, uRuthiya iruntha, sElradhuku kashtama iruntha dhan aran." Vasanth tried again. He made it. The class cheered.
tamil_dialogues: Vasanth: 'Sir, idhu romba kashtam!'
Teacher: 'Aran kashtama dhan irukkanum. Illana, sathru ella sErndhuduvan.'
Vasanth: 'Naan mudiCare!'
Teacher: 'Uyaram, agalam, uRuthi, aRumai — naalum irundha dhan nalla aran.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the top of Rockfort, looking down the 600 steps.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aran nu sonnadhu, uyarama irukka vendum, agalama irukka vendum, uRuthiya irukka vendum, sElradhuku kashtama irukka vendum."
thirukkural: உயர்வகலம் திண்மை அருமைஇந் நான்கின் அமைவரண் என்றுரைக்கும் நூல்.
english_translation: An ideal fort's so says science: high, broad, strong and hard for access.
moral: A strong fortress must be high, wide, strong, and hard to reach — the four pillars of defense.

title: The Guard Who Fell Asleep
hook: Balu explores an old palace fort and discovers a hidden tunnel — then learns why a smart defense beats a big army.
story: At Thanjavur palace, Balu found a secret tunnel under the old fort. "Appa, idhu enge pOgudhu?" Appa explained. "PaeraNi varum podhu, avanga periya padaiya kootitu varuvaanga. Aana namma aran perisaa irundha, siriya kaval kooda pOdhum — avanga namma aranukkuLLE varadha." Balu imagined: a vast fort with just a few sharp eyes. "Chinna gumastu irundhaalum, aran perisaa irundha pOdhum?" Appa nodded. "Ama. Periya idathula chinna kaval — adhu dhan thaththuvam."
tamil_dialogues: Balu: 'Appa, idhu romba periya aran, aana yaarum kaval illa!'
Appa: 'Perisa irundhadhu dhan kaval. Sathrukku sElradhu kashtam.'
Balu: 'Appo siriya kaval kooda pOdhum?'
Appa: 'Ama. Periya aranuku siriya kaval pOdhum. Adhu dhan thaththuvam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the secret tunnel entrance, a lamp in hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periya aran, siriya kaval. Adhu dhan saadhanam. Sathru sErradhuku kashtama irukka vendum."
thirukkural: சிறுகாப்பிற் பேரிடத்த தாகி உறுபகை ஊக்கம் அழிப்ப தரண்.
english_translation: Ample in space, easy to hold — the fort foils enemies bold.
moral: A vast fortress with minimal guard defeats the enemy's spirit by its sheer scale.

title: The Well-Stocked Kitchen
theme: Fortresses / Impregnable with Stores of Food
hook: A cyclone traps Pooja's family indoors for three days — Amma's stocked kitchen saves them.
story: The cyclone warning came. Pooja was scared. "Amma, enna pannuvoam? Kadai ellaam moodiduchu." Amma smiled. She opened the kitchen shelf — rice, dal, oil, pickles, candles, matches, batteries. Three days of food. The storm raged outside. The family sat together, eating warm rice and pickle. Pooja felt safe. "Amma, neenga ellam vachirukkeenga." Amma nodded. "Aran pola da — ullae saapadu iruntha, sathru enna panna mudiyum?"
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Amma, kadai ellaam moodiduchu! Enna pannuvoam?'
Amma: 'Paaru da, namma veetula ellam irukku. Ithu dhan namma aran.'
Pooja: 'Ullae saapadu iruntha bayam illa!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan aran — ullae anna iruntha, sathru vellamudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at Pooja's dining table, a bowl of hot rice before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "AranukkuLLe saapadu iruntha, athai yaalum vella mudiyadhu. Un amma sonna maadhiri — ullae niraiva iruntha, bayam illa."
thirukkural: கொளற்கரிதாய்க் கொண்டகூழ்த் தாகி அகத்தார் நிலைக்கெளிதாம் நீரது அரண்.
english_translation: Impregnable with stores of food, cosy to live — that fort is good.
moral: A fortress that cannot be captured and has food inside — that's a true shelter.

title: The Librarian's Shield
hook: Shwetha thinks the old library building is weak — until she meets the librarian who guards knowledge like a fort.
story: The library building was old, cracked walls, rusted gate. "Idhu dhan aran?" Shwetha laughed. But inside, the librarian Uncle sat tall. He knew every book. He'd memorized the collection. When a rowdy boy tried to tear a page, Uncle stood like a mountain. The boy ran. Shwetha was amazed. "Uncle, neenga dhan ippadi irukkeenga!" Uncle smiled. "Aran nu sonnadhu, kaatukal mathum illa. Maththavarkalai kootitu irukradhu dhan periya aran."
tamil_dialogues: Shwetha: 'Uncle, idhu romba pazhaiya kattidam dhan!'
Uncle: 'Kattidam pazhaya dhan irundhalum, ullae nalla aal iruntha adhu aran.'
Shwetha: 'Neenga dhan periya kaaval kaari!'
Uncle: 'Ama. Aran irukka, nalla porul iruntha, nalla aal iruntha dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the library, a book open in his lap, watching over the quiet readers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kattidam mathum pOdhadhu — aranukku nalla porul venum, nalla aal venum. Adhu dhan aran."
thirukkural: எல்லாப் பொருளும் உடைத்தாய் இடத்துதவும் நல்லாள் உடையது அரண்.
english_translation: A fort is full of stores and arms and brave heroes to meet alarms.
moral: A fortress is more than walls — it needs resources and the right people inside.

title: The Sandcastle Siege
hook: Junior builds a sandcastle and his cousin keeps destroying it — until he learns to build a real defense.
story: At Marina Beach, Junior built a beautiful sandcastle. His cousin Ravi kicked it. Junior cried. Built again. Ravi kicked again. Akka sat beside him. "Nee epdi katta, avan epdi alikka? Aran apdi irukka koodadhu." Akka taught him: deep moat, thick walls, a second wall inside. Ravi tried. The moat stopped him. The outer wall held. He couldn't reach the inner castle. Junior cheered. "Akka, avanukku sElradhu kashtama irukku!"
tamil_dialogues: Junior: 'Akka, avan en mana veedu kettu podran!'
Akka: 'Adha thadukka dhan aran. Munna thanni, appuram suvar, ullae innum oru suvar.'
Junior: 'Ippo avanukku sElradhu kashtam!'
Akka: 'Adhu dhan aran. Sathru enna pannalum, aran adha thanginum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on Marina Beach, building a tiny sandcastle with perfect moats and walls.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru suttralum, thallinalum, vettinalum — aran thanginum. Adhu dhan aran. Nee katta sand castle maadhiri."
thirukkural: முற்றியும் முற்றா தெறிந்தும் அறைப்படுத்தும் பற்றற் கரியது அரண்.
english_translation: Besieging foes a fort withstands — darts and mines of treacherous hands.
moral: A true fortress withstands every attack — siege, assault, and tunnel alike.

title: The Last Lemon Tree
hook: Meena's lemon farm is surrounded by a forest fire — she and Appa must protect their last tree.
story: Fire crept up the hill. Neighbors fled. Meena's farm was in the path. "Appa, odiduvoam!" But Appa pointed to the last lemon tree — where she was born, under its shade. "Namma thangi nikkalaam." They dug a trench, poured water, wrapped the trunk in wet cloth. The fire came close — heat, smoke, crackling. Meena held the tree. The fire stopped at the trench. The tree survived. Meena wept. "Appa, thangitten!"
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Appa, neruppu varudhu! Odiduvoam!'
Appa: 'Namma aran irukku. Thangittalum, sathru thadukka mudiyum.'
Meena: 'Namma thangittom!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan aran — thangi ninnu, sathru thaduppadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the lemon tree, his hand on its burnt bark, green leaves still growing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru suttralum, aran thangi nikkum. Nee thangitta. Adhan dhan veeram. Adhu dhan periya aran."
thirukkural: முற்றாற்றி முற்றி யவரையும் பற்றாற்றிப் பற்றியார் வெல்வது அரண்.
english_translation: A fort holds itself and defies the attacks of encircling foes.
moral: A true fort holds its ground and defies every enemy that surrounds it.

title: The Battle of the Playground
hook: Irfan's kabaddi team is losing badly — he must become a fort that protects and attacks.
story: The inter-school kabaddi final. Irfan's team was down 10 points. The opponent was huge. "Captain, avanga romba power!" Irfan remembered Thatha's words: "Aran na, kaapadradhum, vellradhum." He changed strategy. Instead of attacking blindly, he formed a defensive ring. Let the opponent tire themselves. Then, one by one, Irfan's team struck. The giant opponents fell. The crowd roared. Irfan's school won. Thatha watched from the stands, tears in his eyes.
tamil_dialogues: Irfan: 'Avanga perisaa irukkaanga. Enna pannuvoam?'
Teammate: 'Aran maadhiri nikka vendum da.'
Irfan: 'Kaapadhuvoam! Appuram velluvoam!'
Teammate: 'Adhu dhan! Aran vellum!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the center of the kabaddi court, arms crossed, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aran na kaapadradhu dhan illa — vellradhum dhan. Munai muga kaapadhu, atha vella vendum. Adhu dhan periya aran."
thirukkural: முனைமுகத்து மாற்றலர் சாய வினைமுகத்து வீறெய்தி மாண்ட தரண்.
english_translation: A fort it is that fells the foes and gains by deeds a name glorious.
moral: The greatest fort doesn't just defend — it defeats the enemy and wins glory.

title: The Empty Palace
hook: Deepan visits a grand palace with high walls and deep moats — but feels empty without people inside.
story: The palace was huge — massive pillars, echoing halls, deep moats. Deepan ran through empty corridors. "Amma, evalo periya aran! Aana yaarum illa." Amma sat on a stone bench. "Sollu da, idhu aran dhaana?" Deepan thought. "Aran nnu sonna... ullae aal iruntha dhan." Amma nodded. "Adhu dhan. Ethaiyum perisa katta mudiyum. Aana ullae aal illana, adhu veRum kattu dhan. Nalla aal iruntha dhan kattidamum aran aagum."
tamil_dialogues: Deepan: 'Amma, idhu romba perisa irukku! Aana yaarum illa!'
Amma: 'Aran na nalla aal irukka vendum. Illana adhu veRum suthu.'
Deepan: 'Appo kattidam mattum pOdhadhu?'
Amma: 'Ama. Aal dhan aran. Kattidam veRum kattu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the middle of the empty palace hall, his footsteps echoing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kattidam evalo perisa irundhalum, ullae aal illana adhu aran illa. Nalla aal irundha dhan aran."
thirukkural: எனைமாட்சித் தாகியக் கண்ணும் வினைமாட்சி இல்லார்கண் இல்லது அரண்.
english_translation: But a fort however grand is nil if heroes do not stand.
moral: No fortress is worth its walls without capable people inside to defend it.

title: The Worthless Coconut
theme: Ways of Accumulating Wealth / Wealth Makes Worth
hook: Devi finds a dried, ugly coconut that everyone ignores — until it yields the sweetest milk.
story: At the coconut farm, a dark, shriveled coconut lay in the corner. Buyers passed it. "Ithu useless." Devi felt bad for it. "Appa, idhukku oru value illaya?" Appa cracked it open. Inside, the water was sweetest, the kernel soft. "Paaru da — porul iruntha, yaalum azhaga kaapanga. Illana, yaalum karuthuvaanga." Devi sold that coconut for more than the pretty ones. The buyer came back looking for more ugly coconuts.
tamil_dialogues: Devi: 'Appa, idhu karuppa irukku. Yaarum vaanga maataanga.'
Appa: 'VeRum paarvai. Porul iruntha, adhu azhaga theriyum.'
Devi: 'Andha thenga pazham nalla saapdadhu irundhadhu!'
Appa: 'Ama. Porul dhan edhaiyum azhaga kattum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a coconut tree, cracking open a dark coconut with his bare hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porul iruntha, yaalum aLavum. Porul illa na, yaalum suttu virala kaatum. Athu dhan porulin sirappu."
thirukkural: பொருளல்லவரைப் பொருளாகச் செய்யும் பொருளல்லது இல்லை பொருள்.
english_translation: Naught exists that can, save wealth, make the worthless as men of worth.
moral: Wealth has the power to give value where none is seen.

title: The Empty Lunchbox
theme: Ways of Accumulating Wealth / Poverty Despised, Wealth Honored
hook: Kannan's empty lunchbox makes him a target — until Priya shares hers and changes everything.
story: Kannan's father lost his job. Lunchbox was empty. Kids whispered. "Kannan ku saapadu illa." Girls laughed. Kannan hid in the library during lunch. Priya noticed. She sat beside him, opened her box, and shared. "Saapdu da." Kannan cried. "Priya, enaku edhuum illa." Priya said, "Porul illa na, yaarum suttu virala kaatraanga. Aana, namba nallavanga. Porul iruntha dhan mana mariyadhai." Kannan never forgot that kindness.
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Kannan, un lunchbox la enna?'
Kannan: '...illada.'
Priya: 'Va, naan kudhukiren. Porul illadhadhu kashtam dhan.'
Kannan: 'Yaarum en kooda saapda maataanga.'
Priya: 'Adhu dhan problem — porul iruntha dhan yaalum uyarama paapanga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the school lunch table, breaking a piece of idli in half.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porul iruntha uyaram, illa na thazhvu. Nee priya mathiri iru — porul iruntha dhan manam irukum."
thirukkural: இல்லாரை எல்லாரும் எள்ளுவர் செல்வரை எல்லாரும் செய்வர் சிறப்பு.
english_translation: The have-nothing poor all despise; the men of wealth all raise and praise.
moral: The world scorns the poor and honors the rich — but kindness can bridge that gap.

title: The Lantern in the Dark Street
theme: Ways of Accumulating Wealth / Wealth as Light
hook: Ganesh's street has no streetlight — a new shop owner installs one, and the whole street changes.
story: Ganesh's street was dark. No light. Kids couldn't play at night. Amma was scared to walk. One day, a new shop opened. The owner, Muthu Anna, installed a bright light outside. That night, the whole street glowed. Kids played cricket. Amma chatted with neighbors. Ganesh ran to the shop. "Anna, un veLichcham dhan engalukku veLichcham!" Muthu Anna smiled. "Porul nu sonnadhu, iruttha nikka vendadhu. Athu veLichcham maadhiri irukka vendum."
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Anna, un veLichchathuku namma street full a veLichcham aaiduchu!'
Muthu Anna: 'Ama da. Porul iruttai nikkum. Adhu dhan periya porul.'
Ganesh: 'Nee enna panna, andha veLichcham vanthuchu?'
Muthu Anna: 'Porul dhan da. Andha porul kondu dhan veLichcham vaikka mudiyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands under the new streetlight, his shadow long and warm on the road.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porul nu sonnadhu, irutta kaLaiyum veLichcham. Adhu dhan porul — enga pOnalum, iruttai nikkum."
thirukkural: பொருளென்னும் பொய்யா விளக்கம் இருளறுக்கும் எண்ணிய தேயத்துச் சென்று.
english_translation: Waneless wealth is light that goes to every land and gloom removes.
moral: Wealth is a light that never fades — it removes darkness wherever it goes.

title: The Honest Auto Driver
theme: Ways of Accumulating Wealth / Blameless Wealth Brings Virtue
hook: Raji's auto-driver Appa finds a wallet full of cash — returning it is the hardest and best decision.
story: Appa found a wallet on his auto seat — 50,000 rupees. Raji's eyes went wide. "Appa, idhu namma kastam theerkum. Amma operation ku idhu venum." Appa held the wallet. Hands shaking. He looked at Raji. "Da, porul nalla vaazhkai kudukum. Aana, nalla vaazhala vantha porul dhan atha tharum. Kalla vantha porul azhichidum." He returned it. The owner was a rich businessman. He paid Appa's Amma's hospital bill as thanks. Raji hugged Appa. "Nee sonnadhu unmai Appa."
tamil_dialogues: Raji: 'Appa, idhu namma veetla vaanga! Amma operation ku idhu dhan venum!'
Appa: 'Namma kastama irukalam. Aana kalla vantha porul adu azhichidum.'
Raji: 'Appo enna pannuvoam?'
Appa: 'Sarvam kodutha dhan athu nalladhaakum. Nalla vaazhala vantha porul dhan nalladhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the hospital reception, the businessman paying the bill beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porul nalla vaazhala varanum. Kalla vantha porul, atha azhichidum. Un appa sonna maadhiri — nalla vaazhkai dhan periya porul."
thirukkural: அறன்ஈனும் இன்பமும் ஈனும் திறனறிந்து தீதின்றி வந்த பொருள்.
english_translation: The blameless wealth from fairest means brings good virtue and also bliss.
moral: Wealth earned honestly brings both virtue and happiness.

title: The Crooked Money Bag
theme: Ways of Accumulating Wealth / Wealth Without Love is Disgrace
hook: Shankar's uncle becomes rich by cheating customers — but his family is the poorest in love.
story: Uncle's textile shop grew. He under-weighed, overcharged, sold old stock as new. Money flowed. But at home, Amma cried. Appa didn't talk. Shankar's cousin stopped smiling. Dinner was silent. "Paati, en appa porul vachirukkaar, aana enga veetla sandhosam illa." Paati held him. "Porul dhan venum. Aana anbu illadha porul — adhu kuppai. Anbodu vantha porul dhan periya porul." Shankar understood: money without love is just dirty paper.
tamil_dialogues: Shankar: 'Paati, en appa niraiya porul sampaichirukkaar, aana amma azhara?'
Paati: 'Porulukku anbu illana adhu kuppai. Anbodu iruntha dhan porulukku value.'
Shankar: 'Appo porul mattum pOdhadhu?'
Paati: 'Illada. Anbum, irakkiyum iruntha dhan porul nalladhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the silent textile shop, a single coin in his hand, weighing it.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porul irundhalum, anbu illana adhu veRum kasa. Anbodu iruntha porul dhan nijamana porul."
thirukkural: அருளொடும் அன்பொடும் வாராப் பொருளாக்கம் புல்லார் புரள விடல்.
english_translation: Riches devoid of love and grace — off with it, it is disgrace!
moral: Wealth without love and compassion is not wealth at all — it's a disgrace.

title: The King's Three Bags
theme: Ways of Accumulating Wealth / Sources of King's Revenue
hook: Vimal explores an old palace treasury and finds three bags — tax, tribute, and war spoils.
story: In Chettinad's old palace, Vimal found three stone chests. "Thatha, idhu enna?" Thatha explained. "Mudhal bag: vari — makkal kudutha porul. Rendavadu: ulku — porul vilaiyadha edutha porul. Moonavadhu: pagaiyara vida vanga porul." Vimal imagined the king counting. "Thatha, idhu ellam serna dhan porul aaguma?" Thatha nodded. "Ama. Aana ivai ellam nalla vaazhkai panna dhan. Porul dhan ellaathukum aadharam."
tamil_dialogues: Vimal: 'Thatha, moonu bag irukku. Idhu enna?'
Thatha: 'Vari, ulku, pagai — moonu vidha porul. Ellam serna dhan porul.'
Vimal: 'Appo raajavuku eppadi porul varum?'
Thatha: 'Makkal kuduthadhu, silai vilaiyadhadhu, sathru vida vanginadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the three stone chests, measuring coins on an old scale.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vari, ulku, pagai — moonu vidha porul. Ivai serna dhan raajavuku porul. Adhu dhan naadu nadakka."
thirukkural: உறுபொருளும் உல்குபொருளும்தன் ஒன்னார்த் தெறுபொருளும் வேந்தன் பொருள்.
english_translation: Escheats, derelicts; spoils of war, taxes, duties are king's treasure.
moral: A king's wealth comes from many sources — taxes, trade, and tribute.

title: The Wet Nurse and the Baby
theme: Ways of Accumulating Wealth / Compassion Nourished by Wealth
hook: Lalitha sees her Amma feed a poor woman's baby — and learns how wealth feeds compassion.
story: At the village well, a crying baby. The mother had no milk, no money for food. Lalitha's Amma took the baby, fed it from her own bottle. Every day, she bought milk for that baby. "Amma, namma porul irundha dhan namma irakkiya kaatta mudiyudhu?" Amma nodded. "Ama da. Irakkiyum anbuma irundha, porul dhan adha valarkkum. Porul illa na, namma ninaikkalaam, aana seiya mudiyadhu." Lalitha gave her piggy bank money for the baby's milk. The mother wept in gratitude.
tamil_dialogues: Lalitha: 'Amma, andha paasanga kulla enna kudukreenga?'
Amma: 'Paal da. Adhukku porul venum. Porul iruntha dhan irakkiya seiya mudiyum.'
Lalitha: 'Naan en kudhirai kaas kondu varava?'
Amma: 'Ama da. Porul dhan irakkiyai valarkkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the well, holding a bottle of milk, watching the baby sleep peacefully.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anbu dhan irakkiya valarkkum. Aana anba valarkka porul venum. Porul iruntha dhan anbu seyal aagum."
thirukkural: அருளென்னும் அன்பீன் குழவி பொருளென்னும் செல்வச்செவிலியால் உண்டு.
english_translation: Grace the child of love is nourished by the wet-nurse of wealth cherished.
moral: Compassion is the child of love — and wealth is the wet nurse that feeds it.

title: The Elephant on the Hill
theme: Ways of Accumulating Wealth / Wealth Makes Everything Possible
hook: Murali wants to build a mini library for his village — everyone says it's impossible until Appa shows how wealth changes the game.
story: Murali's dream: a library in his small tea estate village. "Unakku epdi mudiyum? Porul venum da." Murali was sad. Appa said, "Kunju anaikku yaanai por kaana maadhiri — kaiyila porul iruntha, enna vendiyadhu venum nu yosikka vendam. Adhu aagum." Murali sold his goat, saved every rupee, collected old books. The village donated. Six months later, the library opened. It was small — just a shelf under a tree. But kids came from far away to read. Murali was a hero.
tamil_dialogues: Murali: 'Appa, enakku nalla library venum. Aana porul illa.'
Appa: 'Kaiyila porul iruntha dhan. Adhu dhan ellaathaiyum mudikum.'
Murali: 'Naan goats a vitharen. Porul sampaicharen.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan. Porul iruntha, kunju anaikkum yaanai por maddiri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the tree-library, a children's book in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaiyila porul iruntha, adhu madiri dhan — enna vendiyadhu venum nu yosikka vendam. Adhu aagum."
thirukkural: குன்றேறி யானைப்போர் கண்டற்றால் தன்கைத்தொன்று உண்டாகச் செய்வான் வினை.
english_translation: Treasures in hand fulfil all things like hill-tuskers the wars of kings.
moral: With wealth in hand, even impossible things become possible — like elephants fighting on hills.

title: The Steel That Cuts Pride
theme: Ways of Accumulating Wealth / Wealth Sharper Than Steel
hook: A rich bully mocks Jeeva's poor family — Jeeva uses hard work and enterprise to cut his pride.
story: The landlord's son, Rathnam, mocked Jeeva daily. "Un appa factory la velai pannuraan. Nee enna pannuva?" Jeeva's blood boiled. Thatha stopped him. "Santhosham da. Adha kaatradhu dhan periya palam. Porul sampaikkara, adhavida kooriya aayudham illa." Jeeva started a small fireworks stall with Thatha. He worked hard, saved, grew. By Deepavali, his stall earned more than Rathnam's father's month's rent. Rathnam stood silent. Jeeva smiled. "Thatha sonna maadhiri — porul dhan kooriya aayudham."
tamil_dialogues: Jeeva: 'Thatha, avan ennai keLvi ketkiran. En Appa velai patru.'
Thatha: 'Porul dhan kooriya aayudham da. Nee sampaichu avana vida uyarama vaa.'
Jeeva: 'Naan stall pottuten!'
Thatha: 'Adhu dhan. Porul avanoda perumaiyai vettu podum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at Jeeva's fireworks stall, sparklers glowing in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porul dhan kooriya aayudham. Sathru perumaiyai athu vettu podum. Sampaithu kaatradhu dhan periya palam."
thirukkural: செய்க பொருளைச் செறுநர் செருக்கறுக்கும் எஃகதனிற் கூரிய தில்.
english_translation: Make wealth; there is no sharper steel the insolence of foes to quell.
moral: There is no sharper weapon than wealth — it cuts the pride of enemies.

title: The Twins of Happiness
theme: Ways of Accumulating Wealth / Wealth Brings Virtue and Joy
hook: Kaveri's Amma saves money for years to buy a sewing machine — the day it arrives, their whole life changes.
story: Amma worked in a garment factory, stitching 12 hours a day. She saved every rupee in a clay pot. "Amma, idhu enna?" "Sewing machine da. Athu vantha, naan veetla irunthu velai pannalaam." Two years. The pot filled. The day came. A shiny new sewing machine arrived. Amma stitched her first dress — for Kaveri. Then neighbors ordered. Soon, Amma earned more from home. Kaveri could study. Amma smiled, always. "Porul dhan da — athu aramum tharum, sandhosamum tharum."
tamil_dialogues: Kaveri: 'Amma, idhu romba azhaga irukku! Ippo enna pannuva?'
Amma: 'Ippo naan veetla irunthu velai pannalaam. Porul iruntha, aramum, sandhosamum.'
Kaveri: 'Amma unakku inga dhan sandhosama irukku?'
Amma: 'Ama da. Porul nalla vaazha kudutha, adhu dhan periya sandhosam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the new sewing machine, a spool of thread in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Porul iruntha, aramum sandhosamum irukkum. Un amma sonna maadhiri — porul nalla vaazhkai tharum."
thirukkural: ஒண்பொருள் காழ்ப்ப இயற்றியார்க்கு எண்பொருள் ஏனை இரண்டும் ஒருங்கு.
english_translation: They have joy and virtue at hand who acquire treasures abundant.
moral: Those who earn abundant wealth through honest means gain both virtue and happiness together.

title: Velu's Army of One
theme: Excellence of an Army / Well-Organised Force
hook: Velu's Thatha shows him an ant army devouring a grasshopper and explains what makes a real army.
story: Velu watched a column of army ants carry a giant grasshopper piece by piece. "Thatha, ithu onnu sitham dhan! Athu periyadhu!" Thatha laughed. "Sari, aana ithu pathi alavukku mela pathukku — ore iyakkam, ore edham. Athu dhan padai." Velu thought about his school's sports day march-past — everyone out of step. "Thatha, nanga kuda apdi dhan irukkanum?" Thatha nodded. "Unakku therinjudha, Velu."
tamil_dialogues: Velu: 'Thatha, ithu onnu sithama irundhum periya veetaiyya saapdudhu!'
Thatha: 'Sariyaana padai-kku angam ellam onna iyakkam — adhu dhan vellum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the ant column, one finger tracing their path.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Velu, oru padai-kku thevai ellaam — nalla kozhuppu, thairiyama — adhu dhan thalaivana selvam. Nee school-la kooda apdi dhan iru."
thirukkural: உறுப்பமைந்து ஊறஞ்சா வெல்படை வேந்தன் வெறுக்கையுள் எல்லாம் தலை.
english_translation: The daring well-armed winning force Is king's treasure and main resource.
moral: A well-organised, fearless army is a king's greatest wealth.

title: The Unshakeable Guard
hook: Kannan's Periyappa tells him how he stood guard through a cyclone without flinching.
story: The rain hammered the roof. Lightning split the sky. Kannan hid under the blanket. Periyappa sat calmly on the veranda, sipping tea. "Periyappa, bayama irukka?" Periyappa smiled. "Naan oru murai cyclone vechcha ninthen da. Oru nimisham kooda adangala. Padai-la nikkravan bayapda koodadhu." Kannan crawled out, sat beside him. He still shook but didn't run inside. Periyappa patted his head.
tamil_dialogues: Kannan: 'Periyappa, ungalukku bayam-e illaya?'
Periyappa: 'Padai-la nikkravanukku — kadalala, illa pulla — ore nikkai dhan. Athu dhan vellum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the rain beside Periyappa, water streaming past him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee nikkra idathula nillanum. Bayanthu odina — adhu dhan apajeayam. Thoarvadhu theriyama nikkradhu dhan vellum."
thirukkural: உலைவிடத்து ஊறஞ்சா வன்கண் தொலைவிடத்துத் தொல்படைக் கல்லால் அரிது.
english_translation: Through shots and wounds brave heroes hold Quailing not in fall, the field.
moral: An army that does not waver even in crisis — that is true strength.

title: The Cobra and the Rat Army
hook: Chinnu watches a swarm of rats flee from a single cobra and learns why numbers don't matter.
story: The paddy field was flooded with rats — hundreds of them. Chinnu gasped. "Uncle, ithu romba periya padaiya!" Then a cobra slithered in. One hiss. The rat army scattered like water. "Uncle, ava periya padai! Oru pambu munna oda poiduchu!" Uncle laughed. "Perusa irundha podhuma? Manasu irukanum. Adhu dhan vellum."
tamil_dialogues: Chinnu: 'Ava periya padai, aana odi poiduchu!'
Uncle: 'Etti perusa irundha enna? Thairiyama illana adhu padai illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a palm tree, the cobra coiled peacefully at a distance.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Perusa irundha podhum nu illa. Oru aana oru singama irundha — adhu dhan padai. Nee kooda apdi dhan."
thirukkural: ஒலித்தக்கால் என்னாம் உவரி எலிப்பகை நாகம் உயிர்ப்பக் கெடும்.
english_translation: Sea-like ratfoes roar What if? They perish at a cobra's whiff.
moral: A fearless few defeat a terrified multitude.

title: The Unbroken Flag
hook: Pooja's father shows her his regiment's old war flag — torn but never captured.
story: Appa unrolled a faded, bullet-ridden flag. "Appa, ithu enna katteri?" Appa's eyes went distant. "Nam padai kodhi. 1971 la Sialkot-la partchu. Aana oru murai kooda sathru kai-la adhu vizhala." Pooja touched the cloth. A bullet hole. "Appa, ithu ungalukku romba mukkiyama?" "Ama ma. Oru padai-kku adhu dhan uyir."
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Appa, ithu katteri aana ungalukku pidichcha kodhi?'
Appa: 'Idhu tholaya maradha kodhi. Nammoda munnorga kai-la irundhu vandhadhu. Indha kodhi vizhuntha dhan padai tholachadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at attention, saluting the torn flag.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pooja, oru padai-kku perumai adhu tholaiyadhadhu dhan. Unakku kooda — padicha kastam vandhalum vizhanum, adhu dhan vellum."
thirukkural: அழிவின்றி அறைபோகா தாகி வழிவந்த வன்க ணதுவே படை.
english_translation: The army guards its genial flame Not crushed, routed nor marred in name.
moral: An army that endures without being destroyed or routed is a true army.

title: Death's Door Team
hook: Kumar's basketball team is losing 0–12 when Coach tells them a war story that changes everything.
story: Kumar's team was getting crushed. Heads down. Coach called time-out. "Naan oru murai Kargil-la 20 per edhira 200 per-a ninthen. Saavu munna irundhadhu. Aana naanga oda mudiyadhu." Kumar looked up. "Coach, epdi?" "Oruvanukka oruvan ninnom. Adhu dhan. Ippo nenga kooda, ore aai-la nillunga. Adhu dhan padai." They did. They lost 18–20, but they fought till the buzzer.
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Coach, ava romba strong-a irukka!'
Coach: 'Saavu kooda munna ninnadhan da. Nee un teammate-kaga nillu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the basketball hoop, the net swaying.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kumar, saavu varum pothu kooda koodi nikkradhu dhan padai. Tholvadhu theriyama ninnadhu dhan vellum."
thirukkural: கூற்றுடன்று மேல்வரினும் கூடி எதிர்நிற்கும் ஆற்ற லதுவே படை.
english_translation: The real army with rallied force Resists even Death-God fierce.
moral: An army that stands united even against death itself — that is true might.

title: Four Pillars of Valour
theme: Army's Spirit / Four Virtues of an Army
hook: Mani's Thatha teaches him the four things every good army must have.
story: Thatha drew four lines in the sand. "Mani, padai-kku naalu karuvigal: maram, maanam, maandu vazhi, thettam." Mani frowned. "Thatha, maram na?" "Thairiyama. Adhu dhan padai-kku kadamai. Maanam — adhu dhan veththam, tholvadha ninaikama irukradhu. Maandu vazhi — sariyaana vazhi-la nadakradhu. Thettam — nambikkai." Mani traced the lines. "Thatha, naan school-la kooda apdi dhan irukanuma?" "Ama da."
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Thatha, padai-kku ithu mathum podhuma?'
Thatha: 'Indha naalu irundha, ulagathula yentha padai-um vellum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the four lines, touching each one.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mani, oru padai-kku indha naalu dhan kaaval. Nee padicha pothum indha naalu irundha — vellum."
thirukkural: மறமானம் மாண்ட வழிச்செலவு தேற்றம் எனநான்கே ஏமம் படைக்கு.
english_translation: Manly army has merits four:- Stately-march, faith, honour, valour.
moral: Courage, honour, discipline, and faith — these four protect an army.

title: The Drummer's Beat
theme: Army's Spirit / Knowing When to Advance
hook: Selvam watches the regimental drummer and learns that knowing when to move is everything.
story: The army band marched up the Rock Fort steps. The drummer — a lean old man — led the beat. Boom. Boom. Every step landed on the beat. Selvam ran alongside. "Anna, oru dhadava thappa adicha?" The drummer winked. "Thappa adicha, padaiye thappa nadakkum. Naan dhan kaalathai kaatranum." Selvam realized: the drummer decided the army's rhythm. Timing was everything.
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Anna, unga beat-la dhan ellaam nadakkudha?'
Drummer: 'Ama da. Naan dhan padai-kku neram kaatranum. Quick-aa adicha, avanga odranum. Mell-aa adicha, mell-aa nadakkanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar walks beside the drummer, matching his step to the beat.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Selvam, neram therinju nadakradhu dhan padai. Yenga podhunu therinja dhan vellum."
thirukkural: தார்தாங்கிச் செல்வது தானை தலைவந்த போர்தாங்கும் தன்மை அறிந்து.
english_translation: Army sets on to face to foes Knowing how the trend of war goes.
moral: An army advances knowing the tide of battle — timing is victory.

title: The Grand March
hook: Revathi thinks the school march-past is boring until she sees the difference between a crowd and an army.
story: Revathi's class marched raggedly. Legs out of sync, giggles everywhere. The principal called them aside. "Nenga padai maadhiri theriyala — koottam maadhiri therireenga." Then the NCC cadets marched. Crisp. Sharp. One line. Revathi's mouth hung open. "Ma'am, avanga... avanga kaal kooda ore maadhiri viludhu!" The principal nodded. "Oru padai-kku adhu dhan — aparicham irundha kooda, alagu irukanum."
tamil_dialogues: Revathi: 'Ma'am, avanga kaal ore maadhiri viludhu!'
Principal: 'Ama. Padai-kku pori illa — aana alagu irundha, adhu dhan bayam. Angam achu, uyir achu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands as the NCC cadets pass, his hand over his heart.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Revathi, oru padai-kku alagu dhan perumai. Nee kooda — sariyaana uniform podu, nimirnthu nadannu. Adhu dhan manasu."
thirukkural: அடல்தகையும் ஆற்றலும் இல்லெனினும் தானை படைத்தகையால் பாடு பெறும்.
english_translation: Army gains force by grand array Lacking in stay or dash in fray.
moral: Even without strength, an army gains glory by its grand appearance.

title: No Weakness, No War
hook: Saravanan's NCC captain says their troop lost the drill competition because of three things — and they are inside them.
story: The NCC troop came second. Saravanan was happy. Captain wasn't. "Nenga yethukku second-kku sandhosham? Mudalavanuku dhan treatment." He gathered them. "Moodu weakness, vendiya kobam, illaama irukradhu — indha moonu irundha, padai tholkkum." Saravanan thought: I didn't practice the rifle-turn enough. That was weakness. He nodded. "Captain, naan improve pannikren." Captain smiled. "Adhu dhan da unmaiyaana padai."
tamil_dialogues: Saravanan: 'Captain, second-kku kooda sandhoshama irukkalaamla?'
Captain: 'Padai-kku rendaam idam illa. Ondraam idam dhan. Pazhagu, kobatha vittutu, varumai illama iru — appo dhan vellum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the trophy, a single finger raised.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Saravanan, chithama irukradhu, kobam, varumai — indha moonu illaama irundha dhan oru padai vellum. Nee kooda apdi dhan."
thirukkural: சிறுமையும் செல்லாத் துனியும் வறுமையும் இல்லாயின் வெல்லும் படை.
english_translation: Army shall win if it is free From weakness, aversion, poverty.
moral: An army free from weakness, anger, and poverty will always win.

title: The General's Shadow
hook: Ganesh asks why an army needs generals when soldiers do the fighting.
story: Ganesh visited the Officers' Mess with his father. A retired colonel sat alone, medals gleaming. "Sir, ungalukku enna velai? Soldiers dhan sanda podranga?" The colonel laughed softly. "Naan illa na, avanga enge nikkradhu, eppo sutta podradhu, eppo nikkradhu — yaarum solla maata." Ganesh stared at the medals. "Oh. Nenga dhan direction kudukkreengala?" The colonel nodded. "Oru padai-kku thalaivan illana, adhu koottam dhan. Padai illa."
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Sir, soldiers dhan fight pannranga, aana generals enna pannranga?'
Colonel: 'Oru padai oru aai maadhiri. Thalaivan illana, kai kaal yellam onna iyakkama — adhu dhan padai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the colonel, his hand on the medals.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ganesh, ethanai per irundha enna? Thalaivan illana adhu padai illa. Nee kooda — un leader correct-a irundha dhan, nee vellum."
thirukkural: நிலைமக்கள் சால உடைத்தெனினும் தானை தலைமக்கள் இல்வழி இல்.
english_translation: With troops in large numbers on rolls Army can't march missing gen'rals.
moral: An army without a leader is just a crowd — leadership defines victory.

title: The Stone Sentinels
theme: Army's Spirit / Stand Before the Chief
hook: Murugan's Chinnathatha shows him statues of soldiers who never moved — even in death.
story: At the war memorial, stone soldiers stood forever at attention. Murugan touched one. "Chinnathatha, ivanga yenga?" "Ennoda thozhar da. Nanga oru murai sathrunga munna ninom. Ava sollanga — 'Odi ponga' — aana naanga odala." Murugan hugged the statue's leg. "Chinnathatha, ungalukku bayama irundhudha?" "Irundhadhu. Aana namma commander munna ninnadhu vittal, adhu dhan unmai."
tamil_dialogues: Murugan: 'Chinnathatha, ivanga setha piniyuma?'
Chinnathatha: 'Setha piniyum, aana odala. Commander munna ninna, saavu kooda onnu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the stone soldier, his hand on Murugan's head.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Murugan, oruvar munna ninna — adhu dhan padai. Un life kooda adhu vida perusa irukka koodadhu."
thirukkural: என்னைமுன் நில்லன்மின் தெவ்விர் பலரென்னை முன்நின்று கல்நின் றவர்.
english_translation: Stand not before my chief, O foes! Many who stood, in stones repose.
moral: A true soldier would rather turn to stone than retreat before their leader.

title: The Missed Lance
hook: Raju shoots an arrow and hits the target — but the coach scolds him for aiming at the easy ring.
story: Raju's arrow hit the outer ring. His friends clapped. Coach walked up. "Raju, nee sithathuku sutta? Athuku eppavume adikalam. Periya target-a vechu suttu thappu pannu — adhu dhan perumai." Raju was confused. "Sir, adicha sari dhanla?" "Sari dhan. Aana periya target-a miss pannradhu — adhu dhan unakku perumai tharum. Life-la kooda apdi dhan — easy-a edutha valara maata."
tamil_dialogues: Raju: 'Sir, naan adichen!'
Coach: 'Sithathuku adicha perumai illa. Yaanai-kku suttu thappu pannu — adhu dhan oru veeran.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the archery range, holding a longbow.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Raju, chinna target-a adichu perumai illa. Yaanai-kku sutta ambu thappinaalum — adhu dhan veeranuku aayudham. Nee life-la kooda periya aasaikaga poraadu."
thirukkural: கான முயலெய்த அம்பினில் யானை பிழைத்தவேல் ஏந்தல் இனிது.
english_translation: To lift a lance that missed a tusker Is prouder than shaft that hit a hare.
moral: It is better to aim high and miss than to aim low and hit.

title: Mercy's Edge
hook: Veera pins his opponent in a wrestling match but refuses to hurt him — and the teacher calls it true valour.
story: Veera had his classmate Karthi pinned. Everyone screamed, "Adi da! Mutti podu!" Veera's fist was raised. Karthi's eyes were wet. Veera stopped. He let go. "Nee ezhundhuko." The crowd booed. The PT teacher clapped. "Veera, nee dhan veeran. Porla jeyichavan dhan veeran nu — adhu dhan unmai." Veera helped Karthi up.
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Veera, adi da! Jeyichuta!'
Veera: 'Avan adi patta dhan jeyichadhuna? Avana vida vendam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the playground, his hand on Karthi's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Veera, periya veeram — edhirai adichu vilthradhu illa. Vilundhavanai eduthu vida — athu dhan adhoda vaal. Nee dhan periya veeran."
thirukkural: பேராண்மை என்ப தறுகண்ஒன் றுற்றக்கால் ஊராண்மை மற்றதன் எஃகு.
english_translation: Valour is fight with fierce courage Mercy to the fallen is its edge.
moral: True valour fights fiercely but its edge is mercy for the fallen.

title: The Smiling Survivor
hook: Balu's uncle laughs about the time a wild boar charged him in the jungle — and Balu learns why warriors smile at danger.
story: Balu tripped on the beach and scraped his knee. He cried loudly. Uncle sat beside him, laughing softly. "Sithathuku aala? Naan oru murai kaad-la oru kattu-panni kitta sethuten. Adhu enna vida perusa. Oda mudiyadhu. Naan sirichen." Balu stopped crying. "Sirichingala? Bayama irundhudha?" "Irundhadhu. Aana sirichadhu dhan jeyichadhu. Adhu dhan veeram."
tamil_dialogues: Balu: 'Uncle, sirikkradhu eppadi bayathula?'
Uncle: 'Bayam varum. Aana atha sirichu vida, adhu dhan vellum. Kattu-panni kooda sirippa pathchu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the beach, waves crashing behind him, a gentle smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Balu, bayam varum — adha sirichu vida, adhu dhan veeram. Bayapdradhula perumai illa. Atha jeyikradhu dhan."
thirukkural: கைவேல் களிற்றொடு போக்கி வருபவன் மெய்வேல் பறியா நகும்.
english_translation: At the tusker he flings his lance One in body smiles another chance.
moral: The brave throw their lance at the elephant and smile for another chance.

title: The Winking Warrior
theme: Army's Spirit / Steady Eye in Battle
hook: Mahesh's Thatha challenges him to climb the hill without blinking during a scary part.
story: The path to Arunachala was steep. One side was a drop. Mahesh's eyes kept closing in fear. Thatha stopped. "Kann moodaama nikka mudiyuma? Idhu oru sothanai. Sathru munna kann moodina — adhu dhan tholvi." Mahesh stared at the valley. His eyes watered. They wanted to close. He kept them open. Ten seconds. Twenty. "Thatha, mudichuten!" Thatha smiled. "Nee dhan veeran."
tamil_dialogues: Mahesh: 'Thatha, kann moodina sari varuma?'
Thatha: 'Sathru munna kann moodina — adhu dhan apajeayam. Nimirnthu paaru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the hilltop, the morning sun behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mahesh, bayathula kann moodama paakradhu dhan veeram. Un kann moodina, sathru vanthuduvan."
thirukkural: விழித்தகண் வேல்கொண டெறிய அழித்திமைப்பின் ஒட்டன்றோ வன்க ணவர்க்கு.
english_translation: When lances dart if heroes wink "It is a rout" the world will think.
moral: If a warrior blinks when the spear comes, the world calls it a retreat.

title: Scar Day
hook: Divya hides a scar from a fall — but her Akka shows her own scars from a bike accident and tells her what they mean.
story: Divya fell off her bicycle. A nasty scrape on her knee. She wore long skirts to hide it. Her Akka noticed. "Enna ma, kattai vechirukka?" Divya pulled her skirt down. "Onnum illa." Akka lifted her own sleeve. A long jagged scar ran down her arm. "Idhu paaru. Naan bike-la vizhunda pothu vandhadhu." Divya stared. "Akka, idhu unakku bayama irukka?" "Illada. Idhu dhan naan veeram-nu nenaikren. Kaayam pidichavan dhan veeran."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Akka, un kai-la enna achu?'
Akka: 'Bike-la vizhunthen. Kaayam pidichadhu dhan veeram, marachu vekradhu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the terrace, the evening sun touching his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Divya, kaayam pidichadhu dhan veeram. Adhai kaatradhu dhan perumai. Nee vizhundha pothum, kaayam pidichadhu dhan nee vandha vazhi."
thirukkural: விழுப்புண் படாதநாள் எல்லாம் வழுக்கினுள் வைக்கும்தன் நாளை எடுத்து.
english_translation: The brave shall deem the days as vain Which did not battle-wounds sustain.
moral: The brave count only the days they earned their scars.

title: The Anklets of Fame
hook: Anitha sees the dancers' anklets and learns they are like a soldier's leg-guards — both make noise for fame.
story: The temple dancer's anklets jingled with every step. Anitha was mesmerized. "Miss, ava kaal-la enna katti irukka?" "Adhu dhan salangai. Aana oru padai veeran kooda kaal-la kattuvaan — adhu dhan padai kaalam. Adhu sathru kitta bayathai undakkum." Anitha thought. "Miss, rendum ore maadhiri dhana?" "Ama. Oru veeranuku perumai dhan uyira vida perusunu nenaikuvaan."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Miss, avanga rendum ore maadhiri kaal katturaanga?'
Teacher: 'Ama. Oruvar alagukku, innoruvar bayathukku. Aana rendum perumai dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple entrance, anklets in one hand, a warrior's leg-guard in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anitha, veeranukku perumai dhan uyira vida perusu. Adhu dhan avanukku aayudham."
thirukkural: சுழலும் இசைவேண்டி வேண்டா உயிரார் கழல்யாப்புக் காரிகை நீர்த்து.
english_translation: Their anklets aloud jingle their name Who sacrifice their life for fame.
moral: Warriors who sacrifice life for fame — their very anklets sing their glory.

title: The King's Rebuke
hook: Suresh's friend dares him to swim past the breakers — Suresh's Amma tells a story about a soldier who faced the king's anger.
story: The waves were huge. Suresh's friend laughed. "Bayama irukka?" Suresh wanted to back down. Amma said, "Oru murai oru veeran, raja munnadi kooda ninnan. Raja kobama kettan, aana avan vilayaadha." Suresh looked at the sea. "Amma, avan enna panjaan?" "Avan ninnan. Athu dhan. Raja kooda bayapdaama." Suresh walked into the waves. He didn't go far — but he didn't retreat.
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Amma, avan sirikraan. Naan pona thappu?'
Amma: 'Veeranuku raja kooda bayam kedaiyadhu. Kaadala? Nee un bayatha saadhi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the beach, the wind blowing his white robe.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Suresh, bayapdaama irukradhu dhan veeram. Raja munna, kaadal munna, yethu munna — nimirnthu nillu."
thirukkural: உறின்உயிர் அஞ்சா மறவர் இறைவன் செறினும் சீர்குன்றல் இலர்.
english_translation: The king may chide, they pursue strife; They fear loss of glory; not life.
moral: True warriors do not fear even the king's anger — they fear only losing honour.

title: The Broken Promise
theme: Army's Spirit / Die for Your Word
location: Karur · Old Well in the Farm
hook: Murali's friend asks him to lie about stealing a mango — Murali remembers a soldier's promise.
story: Murali's friend Ramesh plucked a mango from the neighbour's tree. The neighbour came shouting. Ramesh whispered, "Nee sollu, naan illa nu." Murali's mouth opened. Then he thought of Thatha's words: "Oru murai vaagu kodutha, adhukaga saakka kooda thayiyaga irukanum." Murali shook his head. "Naan solva maataen. Nee dhan edutha." Ramesh got scolded. Later, Ramesh said, "Unakku periya manasu da." Murali smiled sadly.
tamil_dialogues: Ramesh: 'Murali, oru sollu sollu! Enna kaapadra.'
Murali: 'Naan unmai sollava. Thatha sonnaan — vaagu kodutha, athukaga saava kooda thayiyaga iru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands under the mango tree, a ripe fruit in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Murali, vaagu kodutha pinna adhukaga nillanum. Uyir poinalum. Adhu dhan veeram."
thirukkural: இழைத்தது இகவாமைச் சாவாரை யாரே பிழைத்தது ஒறுக்கிற் பவர்.
english_translation: Who will blame the heroes that lose Their lives in war to keep their vows?
moral: Who can blame those who die keeping their word? That is the highest honour.

title: The King's Tears
theme: Army's Spirit / A Death Worth Dying
hook: Lakshmi sees a statue of a soldier and asks why some deaths make people cry with pride.
story: Lakshmi's Appa took her to the cenotaph. Names carved in stone. Candles flickered. "Appa, ivanga yaaru?" "Namma oorum kaapadra pora setha veeranga." Lakshmi touched a name. "Appa, ivanga setha pini appa aluvaar?" Appa's eyes were wet. "Setha pini appa aluvadhu illa ma. Perumai-la kooda aluvom. Adhu dhan intha maadhiri saavu — periyavanga kooda ala mudiyadha maadhiri."
tamil_dialogues: Lakshmi: 'Appa, unga kannula thanni irukku. Vasanama?'
Appa: 'Peruma thanni da. Adhu dhan intha mari saavu — yaaraiyum ala vechidum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands before the cenotaph, his hand over his heart.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Lakshmi, saavu varudhu — aana adhu perumai koda varum pothu, adhu dhan sadhigaram. Nee kooda apdi vaalu."
thirukkural: புரந்தார்கண் நீர்மல்கச் சாகிற்பின் சாக்காடு இரந்துகோள் தக்கது உடைத்து.
english_translation: Such a death shall be prayed for Which draws the the tears of the ruler.
moral: A death that makes even the king weep — that is a death worth praying for.

title: The Secret Guard
hook: Priya doesn't understand why her Amma is so warm with an old schoolmate — until she learns what real friendship is.
story: At the bus stop, a woman called Amma's name. They hugged like old friends. Priya tugged Amma's saree. "Amma, avar yaaru?" "Ennoda school friend, Priya. Naan kastama irundha pothu, ava dhan enna kaapadra madhiri irundhaa." Priya didn't understand. "Amma, ava enna panna?" "Ava ennoda secret-a kaapadra madhiri irundha. Yaarukkum sollala. Athu dhan nijamaana friendship."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Amma, ava enna panna ungalukku?'
Amma: 'Ava ennoda secret-a kaathu. Adhu dhan friendship — sathru kooda poga vidaama kaapadradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between Amma and her friend, holding their joined hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Priya, nijamaana friendship oru kaaval madhiri. Sathru-kooda poga vidaadha oru kaaval. Adhu dhan nalla friendship."
thirukkural: செயற்கரிய யாவுள நட்பின் அதுபோல் வினைக்கரிய யாவுள காப்பு.
english_translation: Like friendship what's so hard to gain? That guards one against acts villain?
moral: What is as hard to gain as friendship? What protects like it against all wrongs?

title: Waxing and Waning
hook: Harini's friend stopped talking to her after she lost the class monitor election — Paati shows her the moon.
story: Harini cried on Paati's lap. "Paati, Sindhu ennoda pesradhe illa. Naan monitor illa-na, avalukku naan venam." Paati pointed at the sky. "Nila paaru. Valarudhu, theerudhu. Un friend Sindhu, apdi dhan. Nalla irukka varum, kastama irukka poidum. Aana nijamaana friend — nila maadhiri illa. Avalukku un mela anbu iruntha, ava eppavum iruppa."
tamil_dialogues: Harini: 'Paati, Sindhu monitor time-la dhan pesuva. Ippo illa.'
Paati: 'Nila maadhiri dhan — valaravanga, theiyavanga. Aana nijamaana ninaippu maaraadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the moonlight, his face lit silver.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Harini, nambikkaiyaana friend — nila maadhiri valarndhukonde iruppa. Nambikkai illa friend — theinjiduva. Nee adha therinjukko."
thirukkural: நிறைநீர நீரவர் கேண்மை பிறைமதிப் பின்னீர பேதையார் நட்பு.
english_translation: Good friendship shines like waxing moon, The bad withers like waning moon.
moral: True friendship grows like a waxing moon; false friendship fades like a waning one.

title: The Book That Grew
