hook: Vignesh borrows a book from his neighbour and finds notes in the margins — then realises the book is like friendship.
story: Uncle lent Vignesh a worn book. Every page had pencil notes. "Uncle, ithu yaaru ezhudhinadhu?" "Naan. First time padicha pothu, ithu nalla book-nu nenaichen. Ippo padikra pothu, innum nalla irukku." Vignesh read a page. The notes made him think deeper. "Uncle, ithu maadhiri dhana friendship-um?" Uncle smiled wide. "Ama da. Nalla friendship kooda apdi dhan — therinjathe therinjatha pola, innum nalla theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Vignesh: 'Uncle, book notes pottu padikradhu enna special?'
Uncle: 'Nalla friendship maadhiri da — ethanai velai padichaalum bor adikadhu. Innum pudhusa irukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the library, an open book on his lap.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vignesh, nalla friendship — nalla book maadhiri. Ethanai velai padichaalum, pudhusa theriyum. Adhu dhan perumai."
thirukkural: நவில்தொறும் நூல்நயம் போலும் பயில்தொறும் பண்புடை யாளர் தொடர்பு.
english_translation: Like taste in books good friendship grows The more one moves the more he knows.
moral: Good friendship, like a good book, grows richer the more you revisit it.

title: The Friend Who Said No
hook: Karthik copied in an exam — his best friend told the teacher instead of keeping quiet.
story: Karthik was furious. "Rajesh, nee dhaan ennoda best friend. Enna teacher-kitta solra?" Rajesh stood his ground. "Unakku poruthu dhaan solli kuduthen. Aana nee kettadhu panna, naan paakka mudiyadhu." The teacher reduced Karthik's marks but didn't suspend him. Later, Karthik understood. "Rajesh, nee sonnadhu sari dhan. Naan copy pannirundha, enakku edhum theriyadhu." Rajesh smiled. "Adhu dhan da friendship."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Rajesh, nee ennoda friend. Enna kola pannita?'
Rajesh: 'Unakku nalladhu dhan venum. Adhu dhan nijamaana friendship.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the two boys, one hand on each shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karthik, friendship-na sirippu mattum illa. Thappa panna theriyum pothu adicha kooda sollanum. Adhu dhan nijamaana friendship."
thirukkural: நகுதற் பொருட்டன்று நட்டல் மிகுதிக்கண் மேற்செனறு இடித்தற் பொருட்டு.
english_translation: Not to laugh is friendship made But to hit when faults exceed.
moral: Friendship is not for laughter alone — it must correct when faults go too far.

title: The Silent Understanding
hook: Ananya moves to a new city and meets a boy who doesn't say much — but understands everything.
story: Ananya sat alone at the lake. She missed her old friends. A boy sat beside her. He didn't talk. He just threw pebbles. After ten minutes, he said, "Unakku phone pannanum-na, en phone use panniko." Ananya smiled. No questions. No advice. Just help. "Nee enna per?" "Kathir." "Naan Ananya." "Apdi dhan friendship aagum." He was right. No big drama. Just two people. One moment.
tamil_dialogues: Ananya: 'Kathir, nee enna therinju irukka? Enna vandhu pesa?'
Kathir: 'Onnum therinja venam. Ulla viluntha dhan, friendship aagum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a lakeside rock, watching the ripples.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ananya, nijamaana friendship-kku romba pesa venam. Ullam onna irundha pothum. Adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: புணர்ச்சி பழகுதல் வேண்டா உணர்ச்சிதான் நட்பாங் கிழமை தரும்.
english_translation: No close living nor clasping grip Friendship's feeling heart's fellowship.
moral: Friendship needs no close contact nor constant company — just a meeting of hearts.

title: The Smile Behind the Smile
hook: Deepa's friend always smiles at her but talks behind her back — her Amma teaches her the difference.
story: Deepa came home quiet. "Amma, Rekha ennoda munnadi sirikka — aana pinnadi vera maadhiri pesura." Amma was cooking. "Mukathula siricha pothum, ullasa sirikala-na, adhu friendship illa." Deepa thought about it. Rekha smiled beautifully. But her words hurt. "Appo Amma, nijamaana friendship-na?" "Mukam sirikala — ullam sirikanum. Adhu dhan nijamaana friendship."
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Amma, Rekha ennoda sirika — aana pinnadi vera pesura.'
Amma: 'Mukathula sirichadhu friendship illa. Ullasa sirikradhu dhan friendship.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, holding a small mirror.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Deepa, mukathula sirichavan padhila, ullam sirikiravanai paaru. Adhu dhan nijamaana friend."
thirukkural: முகநக நட்பது நட்பன்று நெஞ்சத்து அகநக நட்பது நட்பு.
english_translation: Friendship is not more smile on face It is the smiling heart's embrace.
moral: Friendship is not the smile on the face — it is the smile in the heart.

title: The Ruin Rescuer
hook: Ravi almost follows bad company to smuggle goods — his friend Selvam stops him at the last moment.
story: Some older boys asked Ravi to carry a package from the harbour. "Simple da. Easy money." Ravi was tempted. Selvam grabbed his arm. "Vendaam da. Indha aalu nalla illa." Ravi shook him off. "Nee enna da, ennoda Appa-a?" Selvam didn't leave. He stood there. "Sari, naan kooda varan. Nee pona, naanum." Ravi stopped. Selvam was willing to go down with him. That shook him. "Sari da. Vaa."
tamil_dialogues: Ravi: 'Selvam, nee enna da naalu kaasu therinja maadhiri? Easy money!'
Selvam: 'Easy-aa vandhadhu easy-aa poidum da. Un nalladhu dhan enakku venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the harbour, the evening sun casting long shadows.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ravi, nijamaana friend apdi dhan — unna kedukka vida maatan. Kaal vizhuntha pinna kai koduppan. Adhu dhan nal friend."
thirukkural: அழிவி னவைநீக்கி ஆறுய்த்து அழிவின்கண் அல்லல் உழப்பதாம் நட்பு.
english_translation: From ruin friendship saves and shares The load of pain and right path shows.
moral: True friendship removes you from ruin, shows the right path, and shares your pain.

title: The Falling Dress
hook: Meena's saree blouse slips during the temple festival — Kavya fixes it instantly without anyone noticing.
story: Meena's blouse knot came loose. She froze. The crowd pushed. She couldn't fix it. Kavya noticed. Without a word, she stepped behind Meena, retied the knot in two seconds, and smiled. "Mudichuten. Nee pooka." Meena's eyes welled. "Enna pannirukka Kavya?" "Nee kastama irundha, naan paakka mudiyadhu. Athu dhan da." Meena hugged her right there. A friend who fixes your falling world without making a scene — that's true friendship.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Kavya, enna pannradhu? Ellam poidum!'
Kavya: 'Naan irukken. Nee kavalai padaadha.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands near the temple, oil lamps flickering around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Meena, nijamaana friendship — uduppu vizhuntha, kai maadhiri dhan. Utane eduthu vida. Adhu dhan persia ninaippu."
thirukkural: உடுக்கை இழந்தவன் கைபோல ஆங்கே இடுக்கண் களைவதாம் நட்பு.
english_translation: Friendship hastens help in mishaps Like hands picking up dress that slips.
moral: True friendship is like your own hands — instantly there when you're falling.

title: The Steady Pillar
hook: Janani fails her maths test — her friend Muthu doesn't say "it's okay", she just sits with her.
story: Janani stared at the red marks. 34 out of 100. Tears dropped. Muthu sat beside her. She didn't say "don't cry" or "next time". She just sat. When Janani leaned, Muthu's shoulder was there. After ten minutes, Muthu said, "Naan unakku maths soli kudukka?" Janani nodded. Muthu opened her notebook. "Paaru, indha sum maadhiri dhan. Nee pannuva." She taught her for an hour. Not because she had to. Because that's what friends do.
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Muthu, naan fail aagiten. Enna pannradhu?'
Muthu: 'Adha pathi ippo nenaikkadha. Naan irukken. Nalla padikalam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside their desk, the afternoon sun warming them.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Janani, nijamaana friend — un bayatha therindhum, un kooda nikkum. Adhu dhan friendship-kku periya aram."
thirukkural: நட்பிற்கு வீற்றிருக்கை யாதெனின் கொட்பின்றி ஒல்லும்வாய் ஊன்றும் நிலை.
english_translation: Friendship is enthroned on the strength That always helps with utmost warmth.
moral: The throne of friendship is built on unwavering, warm support.

title: The Friendship of Equality
hook: Bhuvana tells everyone she is best friends with the rich girl — but Sumathi shows her what she's losing.
story: Bhuvana boasted to everyone: "Sumathi ennoda best friend. Avaloda veetu periya car, swimming pool yellam irukku." Sumathi heard. She pulled Bhuvana aside. "Bhuvana, unakku en kitte friendship venum-na, en car kaaga? Illa enna kaaga?" Bhuvana's face fell. "Sumathi, naan..." "Naan unna friend-a nenaikren. Aana nee enna car maadhiri treat panna naan vara maaten." Bhuvana cried. "Sorry da. Nee dhan enakku venum."
tamil_dialogues: Sumathi: 'Bhuvana, unakku venum-na enna, illa en car-a?'
Bhuvana: 'Sumathi, naan thappa sonnen. Nee dhan enakku venum. Car illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the school garden, a single flower in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bhuvana, nijamaana friendship-kku perumaikaga solla venam. Athu dhan friendship-a kedukum. Adhu ullukulla dhan."
thirukkural: இனையர் இவரெமக்கு இன்னம்யாம் என்று புனையினும் புல்லென்னும் நட்பு.
english_translation: "Such we are and such they are!" Ev'n this boast will friendship mar.
moral: Even saying "this is who we are" can spoil a friendship — let it speak for itself.

title: The New Boy Test
hook: Karthi befriends a new student on day one — but his old friends warn him to wait.
story: Gowtham was new, funny, shared his lunch. Karthi thought, "Ennoda best friend!" But his friends said, "Adha paathu da, naaliku enna pannuvaaro." Karthi waited. Next week, Gowtham stole a pencil from the teacher's desk. Karthi saw and felt sad. "Naan friend aaguratha niruthiten."
tamil_dialogues: Karthi: 'Nee nalla friend maadhiri irundha, aana thirudura!'
Gowtham: 'Naan friend aaganum, adha dhan naa ninaichen.'
Karthi: 'Test pannama friend aagakoodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the school gate, holding a broken pencil.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee test pannama friend aana, aprom kashtam dhan. Munnadiye therinjudha venum. Athu dhan nalla friend."
thirukkural: நாடாது நட்டலிற் கேடில்லை நட்டபின் வீடில்லை நட்பாள் பவர்க்கு.
english_translation: Than testless friendship nought is worse For contacts formed will scarcely cease
moral: Don't rush into friendship — test first, or you'll carry the burden forever.

title: The Secret Betrayal
hook: Priya trusted Divya with her diary secret — next day the whole class knew.
story: Priya told Divya her favorite color, her crush on the kite shop boy, everything. "Nee nalla friend dhan." Next morning, the class bully shouted, "Priya love-u! Priya love-u!" Divya was laughing. Priya's heart broke. She never spoke to Divya again. "Naan test pannama friend aanaen. Adhu dhan thappu."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Unakku dhan naan ellam sonnen. Nee epdi ipdi pannalaam?'
Divya: 'Adhu joke dhan pa.'
Priya: 'Secret-a joke pannalaam? Illa da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the bus stop bench, a torn diary page in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aramba la friend aavadhadhu sulabam. Aana aprom varum kashtam dhan periyadhu. Nalla test panni dhan friend aaganum."
thirukkural: ஆய்ந்தாய்ந்து கொள்ளாதான் கேண்மை கடைமுறை தான்சாம் துயரம் தரும்.
english_translation: Friendship made without frequent test Shall end in grief and death at last
moral: A friend accepted without testing will bring you sorrow in the end.

title: The Three Questions
theme: Testing Friendship / Know Before You Trust
hook: Vignesh's Appa gives him three questions to ask before calling anyone a friend.
story: Vignesh wanted to befriend the cool new kid. Appa said, "Mundiya munnadi avana test pannu. 1) Avan kovam vara epdi? 2) Avan unmai solluvaano? 3) Avan kashtam la iruppaano?" Vignesh watched. The new kid lied to the teacher, got angry at a small thing, and left when a friend failed. "Appa, test fail."
tamil_dialogues: Vignesh: 'Appa, avan theriyadha mathiri irukka. Aana avan nalla friend illa.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — munnadi therinjudha venum. Guna, kudumbam, kuttram ellaam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the verandah steps, holding a small slate with three marks.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Gunam, kudimai, kurram, inam — inga ellaam paathu dhan friend aaganum. Illana aprom varuthapaduvae."
thirukkural: குணமும் குடிமையும் குற்றமும் குன்றா இனனும் அறிந்தியாக்க நட்பு.
english_translation: Temper, descent, defects and kins Trace well and take companions
moral: Know a person's character, family, faults, and friends before making them your friend.

title: The Honest Auto Ride
hook: Muthu sees an auto driver return a lost wallet and decides he's the right friend.
story: A wallet fell from a passenger's pocket. Muthu's Appa said, "Paaru, ithu test time." The driver picked it up. Muthu thought he'd keep it. But the driver ran after the passenger, gave it back. Muthu was shocked. "Appa, avar nallavar dhan!" Appa smiled. "Nee un friend aavangalayum apdi dhan therinju."
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Uncle, neenga edutthundu irukka laam!'
Auto Uncle: 'Illada. Adhu mathavanga porulai. Naai maadhiri irukka koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the auto, counting the returned notes with a gentle smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruvan kudi pirahndhavan, avanu manasa veliya katturavan, avanu nalla friend. Adha test pannradhu dhan arivu."
thirukkural: குடிப்பிறந்து தன்கண் பழிநாணு வானைக் கொடுத்தும் கொளல்வேண்டும் நட்பு.
english_translation: Take as good friend at any price The nobly born who shun disgrace
moral: A person of good family who fears disgrace is worth any price as a friend.

title: The Friend Who Made Me Cry
theme: Testing Friendship / True Friends Correct You
hook: Selvi copies homework and her best friend reports her to the teacher.
story: Selvi was tired. She copied the science homework. Ananya saw and said nothing. After class, Ananya went to the teacher. Selvi got detention. She was furious. "Unnala dhan!" But that night, Selvi thought. Ananya was right. Cheating would ruin her. Next day, "Sorry da. Nee dhan nalla friend."
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'Enna friend da nee! Teacher kitta solra!'
Ananya: 'Unakku nalladhu dhan da naa nenaichen. Thappu pannradhuku friend aaga maaten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the library, a book open between two girls.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Azhach solli, thappu nu kaati, nalla vaazhkai la kondu pova — adhu dhan nalla friend. Adhu kashtam dhan, aana nalladhu."
thirukkural: அழச்சொல்லி அல்லது இடித்து வழக்கறிய வல்லார்நடபு ஆய்ந்து கொளல்.
english_translation: Who make you weep and chide wrong trends And lead you right are worthy friends
moral: A true friend is one who makes you cry today to save you tomorrow.

title: The Ruined Birthday
hook: Arjun's birthday cake falls — the ones who laugh and leave were never friends.
story: Arjun blew the candles. His little cousin tripped. The cake smashed on the floor. Some friends laughed, "Ahahaha!" Others left saying, "Birthday waste." Only two stayed. Ravi helped clean. Keerthi said, "Adhu parava illa, innoru cake vaangalaam!" Arjun understood: misfortune is the true test.
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Poi da, cake pochu nu adichikara?'
Ravi: 'Cake vida friend dhan important da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the ruined cake, holding a single unlit candle.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kashtam varum pothu dhan nalla friend theriyum. Kashtam oru aaladhi pole. Adha vechi dhan alakkanum."
thirukkural: கேட்டினும் உண்டோர் உறுதி கிளைஞரை நீட்டி அளப்பதோர் கோல்.
english_translation: Is there a test like misfortune A rod to measure out kinsmen?
moral: Misfortune is the measuring rod that shows who your real friends are.

title: Fool's Company
hook: Kavin's friend keeps getting him in trouble — he learns leaving is a gain.
story: Suresh was fun. He dared Kavin to throw stones, jump walls, skip class. Kavin got punished. Teacher said, "Nee nalla payyan, aana avan unna kedukuraan." Kavin felt sad. But he stopped sitting with Suresh. After a week, his marks improved. "Suresa vida, adha vittadhu dhan periya aatchel."
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Vaa da, class skip pannalaam!'
Kavin: 'Illada. Nee enna thappa dhan edukkura. Na poren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the field, one hand raised in farewell.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "PEdhaiya vida, avanai vittadhu dhan periya aatchel. Athu dhan unmai. Nee avanai vittadhu dhan unakku nanmay."
thirukkural: ஊதியம் என்பது ஒருவற்குப் பேதையார் கேண்மை ஒரீஇ விடல்.
english_translation: Keep off contacts with fools; that is The greatest gain so say the wise
moral: The greatest profit is leaving the company of fools.

title: The Circus Friend
theme: Testing Friendship / Friends Who Leave in Hard Times
hook: Deepa's best friend is with her when playing but vanishes when she falls sick.
story: Meena and Deepa were inseparable. They played, ate, walked together. Then Deepa broke her leg. For one month at home, Meena never called, never visited. Deepa returned to school on crutches. Meena acted like normal. "Epdi irukka?" Deepa felt cold. "Unna patha, enakku manasu valikkudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Enakku kashtam na, nee vara illa.'
Meena: 'Naa... busy da.'
Deepa: 'Illada née. Nalla friend apdi panna maata.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside Deepa's crutches, leaning against the school wall.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku manasu chinnadha aakura, kashtam la unna vida — avanga friend dhan illa. Atha nee vittu dhan aaganum."
thirukkural: உள்ளற்க உள்ளம் சிறுகுவ கொள்ளற்க அல்லற்கண் ஆற்றறுப்பார் நட்பு.
english_translation: Off with thoughts that depress the heart Off with friends that in woe depart
moral: Don't keep friends who leave you in hard times — they only shrink your heart.

title: The Burning Memory
hook: Rajesh lends his favorite cricket bat to Gopi — Gopi sells it and says it's lost.
story: Rajesh's Appa bought him a MRF bat for his birthday. Gopi borrowed it. "Nanna pathukiren." Next week, "Bat-a? Lost da, sorry." Rajesh's heart burned. He saw Gopi playing with new shoes. Someone whispered, "Adha vitanu dhan shoes vanginaan." Rajesh never trusted him again. Even now, thinking of it burns.
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Nee bat-a vitta?'
Gopi: 'Illada lost.'
Friend: 'Namba pathom — Gopi bat-a vitanu shoe vaanginaan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a culvert, the ghost of a cricket bat in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kashtam la kai vida friend — avanai ninaichalum manasu eriyum. Adha dhan solradhu. Adhai vida koodadhu."
thirukkural: கெடுங்காலைக் கைவிடுவார் கேண்மை அடுங்காலை உள்ளினும் உள்ளஞ் சுடும்.
english_translation: Friends who betray at ruin's brink Burn our mind ev'n at death to think
moral: Friends who abandon you in trouble burn your heart even just to remember.

title: The Blameless Boy
hook: Hema is quiet, doesn't gossip, doesn't cheat — Pavithra's Amma tells her to keep her.
story: Hema was new in the colony. Other kids said, "Ava romba serious, ava kooda vilayaada vendam." Pavithra watched. Hema returned a lost 50 rupee note. She didn't laugh at others. She shared her snacks. Pavithra told Amma. Amma said, "Avala dhan friend aaganum. Maravadhe."
tamil_dialogues: Pavithra: 'Amma, Hemaku full serious.'
Amma: 'Serious a irundhadhu nalladhu da. Ava manasu suthama irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at a silk loom, weaving a pure white thread.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Masa illadhavana dhan friend aaganum. Kashtam koduthalum, avanai vittu vida koodadhu. Athu dhan nalla friend."
thirukkural: மருவுக மாசற்றார் கேண்மைஒன் றீத்தும் ஒருவுக ஒப்பிலார் நட்பு.
english_translation: The blameless ones as friends embrace; Give something and give up the base
moral: Embrace the pure-hearted as friends even at a cost; abandon those who are base.

title: The Let-Go Laugh
hook: Senthil keeps teasing Ravi about his stutter — Ravi gets angry until he realizes it's love.
story: Senthil always mimicked Ravi's stutter. "R-R-Ravi, ep-di ir-rukka?" Ravi hated it. One day he shouted, "Poda!" Senthil went silent. Next day, Senthil didn't speak. Ravi missed his stupid jokes. "Senthil, nee sonna sari dhan. Nee en friend." Senthil grinned. "Adhaan solla varen da."
tamil_dialogues: Ravi: 'Nee enna kekkura maadhiri pesuna enakku kovam vara.'
Senthil: 'Nee dhan friend da. Atha kaatradhuku dhan apdi pesuven.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between them, one hand on each shoulder, laughing softly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pazhaiya friend na, avano oru vidu. Avan solladhadhu, seyyadhadhu — adhu dhan pazhaiya friend. Adha vittu vida koodadhu."
thirukkural: பழைமை எனப்படுவது யாதெனின் யாதும் கிழமையைக் கீழ்ந்திடா நட்பு.
english_translation: That friendship is good amity Which restrains not one's liberty
moral: True old friendship never restricts your freedom — it lets you be yourself.

title: The Nosy Uncle
hook: Janani's uncle meddles in everything — she learns it's his way of caring.
story: Uncle came every Sunday. "Padi da, saapdu da, inga poi da." Janani got annoyed. "Appa, avar en ellam sonna maadhiri solluraar?" Appa laughed. "Avan nalla friend da. Namma kashtam avanuku theriyum. Adhaan solraan." Janani understood: old friends have the right to guide.
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Appa, avar enakku sollama irukka mudiyadha?'
Appa: 'Avan nalla friend, avanuku un mela anbu. Atha vida korai illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the dining table, a cup of tea between Uncle and Appa.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pazhaiya friend na, avanuku un mela urimai irukku. Avan sollradhu dhan nalladhu. Atha thiruthi vida koodadhu."
thirukkural: நட்பிற் குறுப்புக் கெழுதகைமை மற்றதற்கு உப்பாதல் சான்றோர் கடன்.
english_translation: Friendship's heart is freedom close; Wise men's duty is such to please
moral: The wise know that old friendship means both closeness and freedom.

title: The Dictator Friend
hook: Anbu's best friend tells him what to wear, eat, and do — Anbu finally rebels.
story: Kathir controlled everything. "Inga vaada, idha saapdu, ithu dhan nalla." Anbu obeyed. One day, Kathir said, "Nee indha shirt potta naa kooda pesa maaten." Anbu had enough. "Naan enna pottalum, naan dhan. Nalla friend na, adha accept pannanum." Kathir left. Anbu felt free, not sad.
tamil_dialogues: Kathir: 'Indha shirt dress pannaa? Naa friend illa!'
Anbu: 'Apdi na, nee friend illa. Nalla friend enna aaga vidaanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the hill path, wearing a simple white shirt.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pazhaiya friend avana aaga vidaanum. Illana Athu dhan friend illa. Avan unna aaga vidaanum."
thirukkural: பழகிய நட்பெவன் செய்யுங் கெழுதகைமை செய்தாங்கு அமையாக் கடை.
english_translation: Of long friendship what is the use Righteous freedom if men refuse?
moral: What use is an old friend who doesn't give you the freedom to be yourself?

title: The Surprise Tiffin
hook: Bala forgets his lunch — Murugan silently shares his without being asked.
story: Bala's Amma was sick. No lunch box. He sat hungry, watching others eat. Murugan, his benchmate for 3 years, silently pushed his tiffin box. "Saapdu da." Bala whispered, "Unakku illa?" Murugan shrugged. "Naa saapditen." Bala knew he was lying. But the warmth of that lie filled his stomach more than food.
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Murugan, nee saapdalaye!'
Murugan: 'Naa full da. Nee saapdu. Namma friendship ku adhu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits between them, an empty tiffin box in his hand, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ketta sollama, ketka vida — adhu dhan pazhaiya friend. Unakku theriyum avan enna nenaikuraanu. Adhu dhan periya friend."
thirukkural: விழைதகையான் வேண்டி இருப்பர் கெழுதகையாற் கேளாது நட்டார் செயின்.
english_translation: Things done unasked by loving friends Please the wise as familiar trends!
moral: The sweetest things in friendship are done without being asked.

title: The Broken Scooter
hook: Mani accidentally breaks Sekar's new scooter — Sekar wants to scream but remembers their years together.
story: Mani took Sekar's new bicycle for a spin. He crashed into a wall. The front wheel bent. Sekar ran out, furious. "Enna panna da!" Mani stood silent. Sekar wanted to shout. But he saw Mani's shaking hands. Eight years of friendship. He breathed. "Parava illa da. Sari pannalaam."
tamil_dialogues: Sekar: 'Enna da ithu! Puthu cycle!'
Mani: 'Sorry da... naa...'
Sekar: 'Sorry podhadhu. Aana nee dhan friend.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the bent cycle, examining the wheel.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla friend seyyum thappu — adha pedai thappu nu nenaikkanum. Illa avan avanoda periya urimai nu nenaikkanum. Adhu dhan pazhaiya friend."
thirukkural: பேதைமை ஒன்றோ பெருங்கிழமை என்றுணர்க நோதக்க நட்டார் செயின்.
english_translation: Offence of friends feel it easy As folly or close intimacy
moral: When old friends hurt you, treat it as a small mistake or a sign of closeness.

title: The Flood That Stayed
hook: Chinnu's house floods and his old friend Kumar wades through neck-deep water to save him.
story: The river overflowed. Chinnu and Thatha were trapped on the roof. Water rose. Then through the rain, a voice. "Chinnu! Chinnu!" Kumar swam with a plastic tub. "Vaa da!" He pulled Chinnu into the tub. They floated to the temple. Chinnu hugged him. "Nee vida maata nu enakku theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Chinnu: 'Kumar, nee vandhuta da! Bayama irundhadhu.'
Kumar: 'Nee irukka bayam epdi da varum. Na varen dhan irundhen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the temple steps, water up to his waist, a tub beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pazhaiya friend — kashtam vanthalum, avan vida maatan. Avan nila la ninnu dhan iruppan. Athu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: எல்லைக்கண் நின்றார் துறவார் தொலைவிடத்தும் தொல்லைக்கண் நின்றார் தொடர்பு.
english_translation: They forsake not but continue In friendship's bounds though loss ensue
moral: Old friends stay within the bounds of friendship even when everything is lost.

title: The Broken Pot
hook: Pooja's mother accidentally breaks Lakshmi's favorite pot — Pooja takes the blame.
story: Lakshmi's Amma gave her a clay pot from the temple. Pooja's Amma dropped it. It shattered. Lakshmi's eyes filled. Pooja stepped forward. "Naa dhan potten." Lakshmi knew Pooja didn't. "Neeyaa?" Pooja nodded. Later, "Enna da poi solra?" Pooja said, "Namma friendship ku adhu dhan. Adhu dhan pot."
tamil_dialogues: Lakshmi: 'Pooja, nee pottu uta — illa. Un Amma dhan.'
Pooja: 'Theriyum. Aana namma friendship la yaar thappu nu pArka vendam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels, gathering the clay shards, fitting them together.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pazhaiya friend na — edhu vanthalum anbu maradha. Kashtam vanthalum, anbu dhan nilaiyum. Athu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: அழிவந்த செய்யினும் அன்பறார் அன்பின் வழிவந்த கேண்மை யவர்.
english_translation: Comrades established in firm love Though ruin comes waive not their vow
moral: Friends rooted in true love never lose affection even when ruin comes.

title: The Liar's Whisper
hook: Someone tells Mani that Karthik said bad things about him — Mani refuses to believe.
story: A boy whispered, "Karthik unna pathi ketta sollu." Mani's blood boiled. He ran to confront. But he stopped. Eight years of friendship. "Naa avan kitta kekkalaam." Karthik said, "Unna pathi solla maaten da." Mani believed him. The liar was exposed. "Nalla friend irundha, ketta solla kaadhula vaikka koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Boy: 'Karthik unna pathi ketta sollinaan!'
Mani: 'Naa avan kitta kekkalam. Enakku avan mela nambikkai irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between Mani and the gossiper, one hand raised in stop.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pazhaiya friend-na, avan mela nambikkai irukkanum. Yaaro sollita nu avanai vida koodadhu. Adhu dhan nalla friend."
thirukkural: கேளிழுக்கம் கேளாக் கெழுதகைமை வல்லார்க்கு நாளிழுக்கம் நட்டார் செயின்.
english_translation: Fast friends who list not tales of ill Though wronged they say "that day is well"
moral: True friends don't listen to gossip about each other — they trust even when wronged.

title: The Generations Bond
hook: Meera sees her Paati meet her 70-year-old friend — they laugh like schoolgirls.
story: Paati's friend came from Chennai. They hugged, cried, laughed. "70 years!" They held hands, walked to the temple, bought flowers. Meera watched, amazed. "Paati, epdi ipdi irukkeenga?" Paati smiled. "Nanga school la dhan friend. 60 years achu. Kashtam, santhosham — ellam ore maadhiri." Meera wished she'd have a friend like that.
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Paati, 60 years! Epdi da mudiyudhu?'
Paati: 'Anbu iruntha, varsham kanakka illa da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the temple entrance, watching the two old women laugh.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kedaatha vazhi vantha friend — avanai vida koodadhu. Adha dhan ulagam virumbudhu. Nee apdi dhan irukkanum."
thirukkural: கெடாஅ வழிவந்த கேண்மையார் கேண்மை விடாஅர் விழையும் உலகு.
english_translation: To love such friends the world desires Whose friendship has unbroken ties
moral: The world cherishes those who never break a friendship that has lasted through generations.

title: The Enemy's Praise
theme: Old Friendship / Even Enemies Respect It
hook: The school bully says Kumar is okay — because Kumar never ditched his old friend.
story: Kumar's old friend Ramesh was slow, poor, not cool. But Kumar stayed with him — lunch, play, walk. The bully Siva watched. One day, Siva said, "Kumar nallavan da. Avan avan friend a vida maatan." The class was shocked. The bully praised Kumar! Kumar realized: loyalty earns even enemies' respect.
tamil_dialogues: Siva: 'Kumar, nee nallavan. Un friend a vida maata nu theriyudhu.'
Kumar: 'Enaku ithu periya compliment da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the field, a hand on Siva's head, a hand on Kumar's.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pazhaiya frienda vidaadhavanai — avanai kaetta vanungalum virumbuvaanga. Athu dhan unmai. Adhu dhan periya manam."
thirukkural: விழையார் விழையப் படுப பழையார்கண் பண்பின் தலைப்பிரியா தார்.
english_translation: Even foes love for better ends Those who leave not long-standing friends
moral: Even enemies come to love those who never abandon old friends.

title: The Honey Trap
hook: Nalini is so sweet, so generous — Paru's Amma notices something wrong.
story: Nalini gave Paru chocolates, complimented her, said "Ennoda best friend!" Paru was thrilled. Amma watched. "Avalloda friend aaga mudiyatha?" Paru asked. Amma said, "Paru, romba sweet-a irukradhu dhan sandhegam. Nalla friend na avasarama vara maata." Next week, Nalini asked Paru to steal her mother's earring. Paru said no.
tamil_dialogues: Nalini: 'Un Amma kai la oru earring irukku. Kondu vaa, vilaiyaadalaam!'
Paru: 'Illada! Nee dhan thief!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the apartment gate, a chocolate wrapper at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pani la niraiya theney polla — veliya inidha irundhalum ullae kottum. Appadi dhan keda friend. Adha vida vida vendum."
thirukkural: பருகுவார் போலினும் பண்பிலார் கேண்மை பெருகலிற் குன்றல் இனிது.
english_translation: Swallowing love of soulless men Had better wane than wax anon
moral: Friendship with those who lack goodness — better it shrinks than grows.

title: The Fair-Weather Friend
theme: Evil Friendship / Present in Wealth, Gone in Need
hook: Sanjay is Vicky's best friend when Vicky has snacks and money — when Vicky's father loses his job, Sanjay vanishes.
story: Vicky was the rich kid. Sanjay always sat with him, shared his chips, laughed. Then Vicky's Appa lost his job. No more snacks, no new pencils. Sanjay slowly moved to another bench. "Sanjay, vaa canteen ku." "Illada, na busy." Vicky understood. Sanjay didn't love him — he loved his money.
tamil_dialogues: Vicky: 'Sanjay, unakku enna? En odane pesra illa?'
Sanjay: 'Naa... vandhaen da.'
Vicky: 'Po da. Unakku naa venam, en kai la irundhadhu dhan venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits alone at the canteen table, a single plate before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla irukapom friend, kashtam na vida — avan friend illa. Avanukku un porulai dhan venum. Atha vida vidradhu nalladhu."
thirukkural: உறின்நட்டு அறினொருஉம் ஒப்பிலார் கேண்மை பெறினும் இழப்பினும் என்?
english_translation: Who fawn in wealth and fail in dearth Gain or lose; such friends have no worth
moral: Friends who are with you in wealth and leave in hardship — gaining or losing them makes no difference.

title: The Calculator Friend
theme: Evil Friendship / Cunning Friends Are Thieves
hook: Shalini always calculates — she's only friends with those who give her something.
story: Shalini was friendly. But Rani noticed: Shalini only talked to kids who had new toys, good snacks, or copied homework for her. One day, Rani had no snacks. Shalini ignored her. Next day, Rani brought cake. "Rani, enna da ithu? Nalla irukku!" Rani understood. Shalini wasn't a friend — she was a calculator.
tamil_dialogues: Rani: 'Enaku cake illadha naa unakku friend illa?'
Shalini: 'Illada... apdi illa...'
Rani: 'Ama, apdi dhan. Nee calculator friend.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, holding an abacus.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Avalukku un kitta irukka porulai dhan venum. Atha aval kettu dhan kudutha — adhu dhan thirudan mathiri. Avan kai la porulai vendum."
thirukkural: உறுவது சீர்தூக்கும் நட்பும் பெறுவது கொள்வாரும் கள்வரும் நேர்.
english_translation: Cunning friends who calculate Are like thieves and whores wicked
moral: A friend who weighs what they can get from you is no better than a thief.

title: The Battle Field
hook: Gowtham falls in the relay race — his friend Dhruv steps over him and keeps running.
story: Sports day. 4x100 relay. Gowtham stumbled. Dhruv was next. Instead of helping, Dhruv stepped over Gowtham's hand and ran. Everyone gasped. Gowtham's knee bled. He sat up. Dhruv didn't look back. After the race, Dhruv said, "Namba jeyicha da!" Gowtham thought: "Ivandha vida thanimai dhan nalladhu."
tamil_dialogues: Gowtham: 'Nee enna kai kooda kudukala?'
Dhruv: 'Race dhan important da.'
Gowtham: 'Unakku friend important illa. Race dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the track, helping Gowtham to his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kashtam la kai vida friend — avanoda irukradha vida, thaniya irukradhu dhan nalladhu. Adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: அமரகத்து ஆற்றறுக்கும் கல்லாமா அன்னார் தமரின் தனிமை தலை.
english_translation: Better be alone than trust in those That throw in field like faithless horse
moral: Better to be alone than to have friends who abandon you on the battlefield.

title: The Useless Helper
theme: Evil Friendship / Friends Who Don't Help
hook: Divya asks Rekha for help moving a heavy box — Rekha says she's busy every time.
story: Divya's Amma asked her to move a heavy box. "Rekha, konjam help pannu." "Busy da, aprom varen." 10 minutes. "Rekha, vaa." "Wait da." 30 minutes. Divya moved it herself, sweating. Next week, Rekha asked for help with homework. Divya said no. Rekha got angry. "Nee friend illa!" Divya smiled. "Nee dhan enakku help panna maate."
tamil_dialogues: Rekha: 'Divya, help pannu da.'
Divya: 'Nee dhan enakku help panna maate. Apdi irundha nee en friend?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the heavy box, lifting one corner.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku help panna maata friend — avanai vida vidradhu dhan nalladhu. Avan unakku oru payanum illa. Adha vida vida vendum."
thirukkural: செய்தேமஞ் சாராச் சிறியவர் புன்கேண்மை எய்தலின் எய்தாமை நன்று.
english_translation: Friends low and mean that give no help- Leave them is better than to keep
moral: Better to not have such friends at all than to have friends who never help.

title: The Wise Enemy
theme: Evil Friendship / Wise Enemy > Foolish Friend
hook: Kanna's friend Balu always drags him down — his enemy Ravi actually teaches him.
story: Balu laughed when Kanna failed. "Ahaha, zero!" Ravi, his rival, gave him a book. "Padichu paru." Kanna studied. Passed. Balu got jealous. "Nee padikradhu waste." Kanna understood: Ravi, his "enemy," helped him grow. Balu, his "friend," wanted him to fail. "Balu vida, Ravin kovam dhan nalladhu."
tamil_dialogues: Kanna: 'Ravi, nee enaku help pannra. Balu en friend dhan sokka aakuraan.'
Ravi: 'Sariyaana friend na unna mela konda povaan. Thappu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between Balu and Ravi, pointing at Ravi.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "PEdhaiya vida, arivulla satru — kodi mura nalladhu. PEdhai unna keduka, arivulla satru unna mela kondu pova."
thirukkural: பேதை பெருங்கெழீஇ நட்பின் அறிவுடையார் ஏதின்மை கோடி உறும்.
english_translation: Million times the wise man's hate Is better than a fool intimate
moral: A wise man's enmity is a million times better than a fool's friendship.

title: The Laughing Traitor
theme: Evil Friendship / Laughing Friends vs Serious Enemies
hook: Arul's funny friends laugh at his jokes but leave him to face the punishment alone.
story: Arul cracked a joke about the teacher. The whole group laughed. Teacher came. "Who said that?" Everyone pointed at Arul. The funny friends who laughed stepped back. "Naa illa sir." Arul got detention. He sat alone, understanding. They laughed with him, but they weren't with him. Enemies would have been more honest.
tamil_dialogues: Arul: 'Nee yellam sirichinga. Aana naan dhan adi vaangunen.'
Friend: 'Sorry da...'
Arul: 'Poi da. Enemies irundha nalladhu, unga maadhiri friend irukradhu vida.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the detention room, a book open on Arul's desk.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sirikkura friend vida, sanda podura satru — pathu mura nalladhu. Avan unna kedukama iruppan. Iva kedukuraan."
thirukkural: நகைவகைய ராகிய நட்பின் பகைவரால் பத்தடுத்த கோடி உறும்.
english_translation: Ten-fold crore you gain from foes Than from friends who are vain laughers
moral: Ten crore times better are enemies than friends who only laugh with you but abandon you.

title: The Sabotage
theme: Evil Friendship / Friends Who Spoil Your Work
hook: Meena's friend offers to help with her clay model — then secretly smashes it.
story: Meena made a beautiful Ganesha clay model for the competition. Sharadha said, "Naa help pannurean." Meena trusted her. While Meena got water, Sharadha pressed too hard — the trunk broke. "Sorry da, accident." Meena cried. Teacher saw. Another girl whispered, "Sharadha pOla pOna maadhiri... avanuku dhan first prize venum." Meena understood: saboteur.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Unakku first prize venum, adhaan da enna mudichiya?'
Sharadha: 'Illada... accident...'
Meena: 'Poi da. Nee en friend illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the broken clay Ganesha, holding the trunk piece.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un velaikku kedu pannuravan — avanai oru vaarthai illama vida vendum. Avan solli pannadha, aana avan seyal dhan unmai."
thirukkural: ஒல்லும் கருமம் உடற்று பவர்கேண்மை சொல்லாடார் சோர விடல்.
english_translation: Without a word those friends eschew Who spoil deeds which they can do
moral: Without a word, abandon friends who ruin the work they could have helped with.

title: The Two-Faced Liar
theme: Evil Friendship / Deed and Word Don't Match
hook: Swapna says "I'll come to your house" but never comes; says "I'll help" but never does.
story: Swapna always promised. "Naale un veetuku varen." She never came. "Homework help pannuven." She never helped. "Nalla friend dhan naa." Her actions said the opposite. Kavya felt used. "Swapna, nee sollradhu veraa, seyyradhu veraa." Swapna laughed it off. Kavya stopped waiting. "Sollum seyalum ore maadhiri illadhavan — avana vida vida vendum."
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Nee sollradhu veraa, pannradhu veraa.'
Swapna: 'Adhu dhan da, naa apdi dhan.'
Kavya: 'Apdi irundha nee en friend illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands with a mirror in one hand, showing Swapna's reflection.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sollum seyalum veru irundha — avanai vida vida vendum. Kanavula kuda adhu nalladhu illa. Avan sollum seyalum ore maadhiri irukkanum."
thirukkural: கனவினும் இன்னாது மன்னோ வினைவேறு சொல்வேறு பட்டார் தொடர்பு.
english_translation: Even in dreams the tie is bad With those whose deed is far from word
moral: Friendship with those whose words and deeds don't match is painful even in dreams.

title: The Snake at Home
hook: Uncle Ramesh smiles, brings gifts, praises everyone — then speaks behind their backs.
story: Uncle Ramesh came with toys. "Enna azhaga irukka! Nalla payyan dhan!" Senthil loved him. But at night, Senthil heard Uncle on the phone. "Ivan ellam useless da. Avan appa loser." Senthil's heart froze. Next day, Uncle smiled again. "Vaa da kutty!" Senthil stepped back. "Appa, avar veetla sirikiraan, veliya ketta solluraan."
tamil_dialogues: Senthil: 'Appa, Uncle veetla nalla maadhiri irukkaar, aana phone la ketta solluraar.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — veetla sirichu, veliya kedukuravan. Avana vida vida vendum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the doorway, half in the house, half outside.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Veetla sirichu veliya kedukuravan — avanoda irukradhu dhan periya apayam. Adha vida mulukkitte vida vendum."
thirukkural: எனைத்தும் குறுகுதல் ஓம்பல் மனைக்கெழீஇ மன்றில் பழிப்பார் தொடர்பு.
english_translation: Keep aloof from those that smile At home and in public revile
moral: Stay far away from those who smile at home but speak ill of you in public.

title: The Smiling Snake
hook: Karthik's new friend brings him snacks every day — but Appa notices something wrong.
story: A new boy named Ramesh started giving Karthik chips and chocolate every day. "Namba best friends dhan!" Karthik was thrilled. Appa watched silently. One day, Ramesh asked Karthik to steal Appa's phone for "a game." Karthik froze. "Appa, avan enakku chocolate kuduthaan, aana avan donga." Appa hugged him. "Adhu dhan — ellam kudukiravan namba friend illa da."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Appa, avan romba nalla irundhaan, aana avan donga!'
Appa: 'Santhosham da nee therinjutta. Silar veliya nallavadhu maadhiri iruppa, aana ullae aadra kaththi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple entrance, a coiled stone serpent beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla maadhiri irundhu kettadhu pannradhu — adhu dhan sathruvin kaiyila irukka aayudham. Unakku theriyama avanukku adi padradhu dhan periya apayam."
thirukkural: சீரிடம் காணின் எறிதற்குப் பட்டடை நேரா நிரந்தவர் நட்பு.
english_translation: The friendship by an enemy shown is anvil in time, to strike you down.
moral: An enemy's friendship is just a waiting hammer — beware sweet words from unknown hearts.

title: Chithi's Sweet Poison
hook: Anjali's Chithi brings her a new dress but whispers bad things about Amma — and Anjali doesn't know who to believe.
story: Chithi came from the city with a frilly frock. "Un amma unakku ethum vaangi kudukka maata, paaru naan mattum dhan unna love pannraen!" Anjali was confused. Amma never bought frilly frocks but she made dosa every morning. "Amma, Chithi sonna — nee enakku eduvum pannamaata?" Amma sat down. "Anjali, ava sonnadhu ellaam poi. Nee avan kaiyila erangadha."
tamil_dialogues: Anjali: 'Amma, Chithi enakku dress vaangi kuduthaanga!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan problem da — dress kudukiravanga ellam nallavanga illa. Ullae enna irukku nu paathu dhan nambi aaganum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the colony well, drawing water calmly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Namma kulathula thanni theliva irukka, pala irukka theriyum. Manushanai avanga seyal la dhan therinjukka mudiyum. Sollala."
thirukkural: இனம்போன்று இனமல்லார் கேண்மை மகளிர் மனம்போல வேறுபடும்.
english_translation: Who pretend kinship but are not their friendship's fickle like woman's heart.
moral: Those who pretend to be your own can change faster than the wind.

title: The Topper's Secret
hook: Divya admires the class topper who teaches everyone — until she sees her kick a street dog.
story: Swathi was first rank, helped everyone with math. Divya wanted to be like her. Then during lunch, Swathi kicked a puppy that came near her feet. Divya's heart broke. "Paati, ava nalla padikra, aana naaya adichaanga!" Paati said, "Padichadhala dhan manushan aagamaaten. Manasu nalladhu irukka vendum. Swathi padichirukka, aana avanuku anbu illa."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Paati, romba padichirukka, aana avanuku manasu illa pole irukku!'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan da — padichavan ellam nallavan illa. Manasu irukka dhan aaganum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the school gate, the puppy licking his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Evlo padichalum manasu illana adhu vaima dhan. Nee patha maadhiri — padichu dhan kedaiyaadhu. Anbu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: பலநல்ல கற்றக் கடைத்து மனநல்லர் ஆகுதல் மாணார்க்கு அரிது.
english_translation: They may be vast in good studies but heartfelt-love is hard for foes.
moral: Learning fills the mind but doesn't fill the heart — kindness matters more than grades.

title: The Fake Facebook Uncle
theme: Foe's Friendship / Sweet Face, Bitter Heart
hook: A friendly uncle offers Mukesh a free phone — but Akka's instincts kick in.
story: At Hosur market, a smiling uncle called Mukesh. "Enna da, phone venuma? Free-a kudukiraen!" Mukesh was excited. His Akka pulled him back. "Paaru, ivar kitta poi." The man insisted. "Naan un periyappa mathiri dhan!" Akka held Mukesh tight and walked away. Later they heard that same man had stolen kids' phones. Mukesh shivered. "Akka, nee illana naan..." Akka said, "Mukesh, nalla maadhiri irundhavan ellam nallavan illa da."
tamil_dialogues: Mukesh: 'Akka, avar free phone kuduthaar! Nalla aagidhu pole irukku!'
Akka: 'Adhu dhan apayam da — silar veliya sirichitu ullae kaththi vaichiruppa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the market entrance, a cracked phone at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mukam sirichalum manasu sirikkanum nu artham illa. Sathruvin sirippula bayam irundha, adhai vida mudiyum."
thirukkural: முகத்தின் இனிய நகாஅ அகத்தின்னா வஞ்சரை அஞ்சப்படும்.
english_translation: Fear foes whose face has winning smiles whose heart is full of cunning guiles.
moral: A smiling face can hide a cruel heart — trust your gut, not their grin.

title: The Friendly Coach
hook: A rival team's coach praises Sathish and offers to train him — but Thatha senses something off.
story: The rival team's coach came after the match. "Un bowling romba nalla irukku da. Naa unna train pannuraen!" Sathish was over the moon. But Thatha said, "Avan sonna ellaam nambada. Avan manasu namma team-a odacha aaganum nu paarpaan." Next practice, the coach taught Sathish a wrong grip. "Thatha sonnadhu sari dhan!" Sathish fixed his grip himself.
tamil_dialogues: Sathish: 'Thatha, avar enakku nalla grip soli kuduthaar!'
Thatha: 'Adhu thappa da. Avan kaiya erangaadha. Ellam poi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the cricket pitch, holding a worn-out bat.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee vantha pakkam irukravanga nallavadhu dhan solluva. Aana, avangaloda manasu un kooda illana, avanga solla koodadhu."
thirukkural: மனத்தின் அமையாதவரை எனைத்தொன்றும் சொல்லினால் தேறற்பாற்று அன்று.
english_translation: Do not trust in what they tell whose mind with your mind goes ill.
moral: Never trust the words of someone whose heart doesn't beat with yours.

title: The Compliment Trap
hook: A woman at the beach keeps praising Pooja's dress and asks her to come for photos.
story: A stylish auntie smiled at Pooja. "Un dress romba azhaga irukku da! Photo edukkalaama?" Pooja felt special. Then the auntie asked, "Un amma kitta sollaadha, naan unna oru shoot ku kootitu poren." Pooja's inner alarm rang. "Amma!" she screamed. The woman vanished. Amma held her tight. "Nee thappu pannala da. Sollalaam sollaama kootitu poga sonnavanga ellam dangerous."
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Amma, ava enakku photo edukkalaama nu sonnaanga!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da sathru — nallavadhu pole irundhu kettadhu pannuva.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the Marina shore, waves washing his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla vaartha solli kettadhu pannravan, adhu dhan sathru. Avan vaartha dhan aayudham. Nee avan solla maatika koodadhu."
thirukkural: நட்டார்போல் நல்லவை சொல்லினும் ஒட்டார்சொல் ஒல்லை உணரப்படும்.
english_translation: The words of foes is quickly seen though they speak like friends in fine.
moral: Enemy words wrapped in kindness are still poison — listen to your inner warning.

title: The Bowing Bully
theme: Foe's Friendship / Humble Words, Hidden Danger
hook: The school bully suddenly bows and says sorry — and Venkat's dad knows it's a trick.
story: Kathir, who once tore Venkat's bag, came smiling. "Sorry da, namba friend aagalaam." Venkat was shocked. Appa saw it. "Avanum sathru dhan. Atha vida dangerous." Venkat didn't understand. Appa said, "Vill mathiri — adhu kumbidum bodhu dhan adi podum. Nee avan kitta jaagrathaa iru." Next week, Kathir tried to make Venkat steal exam papers.
tamil_dialogues: Venkat: 'Appa, avan sorry sonnaan! Namba friend aaga vendiya?'
Appa: 'Illada! Vill kumbidradhu dhan adikkara. Athu dhan avan mathiri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands near a tannery, a bent bow in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru thala kumbiduvan. Aana avan kumbidradhu avan aayudhattai mukkam pannradhu dhan. Nee avan kaiyula erangakoodadhu."
thirukkural: சொல்வணக்கம் ஒன்னார்கண் கொள்ளற்க வில்வணக்கம் தீங்கு குறித்தமையான்.
english_translation: Trust not the humble words of foes danger darts from bending bows.
moral: When an enemy bows, he's aiming — a bent bow is not a greeting, it's a threat.

title: The Crying Crocodile
hook: A fisherman cries about his lost catch and tries to take Selvi's puppy — Thatha stops him.
story: At the harbor, a man sobbed loudly. "En valai poiduchu! Enakkum oru kozhandhai maadhiri veedu irukku!" He pointed at Selvi's puppy. "Ithu enakku koduthudu." Selvi felt pity. But Thatha stepped in. "Azhuvadhu ellam nijam illa da." The man's tears stopped instantly. He glared and walked away. "Thatha, avan poi azhudhaan?" Thatha nodded. "Ama. Kanner kuda aayudham dhan sathrukkukku."
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'Thatha, avan azhudhaan. Nijama dhan irukkum!'
Thatha: 'Kannera ellam nijam irukka dhan aaganum illa. Sathruvin kanner polama?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the harbor wall, a fishing net drying beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannir kuda sathru aayudham. Oru sathru azhuvadhu — avan kannir kuda avan kaiyila irukka vel. Nee aacharam ah irukkanum."
thirukkural: தொழுதகையுள்ளும் படையொடுங்கும் ஒன்னார் அழுதகண்ணீரும் அனைத்து.
english_translation: Adoring hands of foes hide arms their sobbing tears have lurking harms.
moral: Even an enemy's tears can be a weapon — don't let crying fool your heart.

title: The Praising Traitor
theme: Foe's Friendship / Praise as a Weapon
hook: Gowri's neighbor keeps praising her in front of everyone — but says the worst behind her back.
story: Neighbor Saradha akka always said loudly, "Un maga romba talent! Super da!" Gowri beamed. But Appa's friend told him: Saradha tells everyone "Avaluku talent omum illa, naanum veliya sonna madhiri." Gowri was crushed. "Appa, munnadi nalla sonnaanga, pinnadi kettadha solluraanga." Appa said, "Adhu dhan dangerous da — munnadi nalla solravan pinnadi kettadhu solvaan."
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Appa, ava enna pathi kettadhu sollura! Munnadi nalla sonnaanga!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — munnadi nalla solravan pinnadi nallavadhu solla maatan. Jaagrathaa iru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the rock fort, a parrot on his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Munnadi unna pugazhuva, aana pinnadi unna izhivaan. Adhu sathruvin velai. Unna pugazhvadhum, unna kevala pannradhum avan kaiyila irukka aayudham."
thirukkural: மிகச்செய்து தம்மெள்ளுவாரை நகச்செய்து நட்பினுள் சாப்புல்லற்பாற்று.
english_translation: In open who praise, at heart despise cajole and crush them in friendly guise.
moral: Those who praise you to your face and stab you behind are the worst kind of enemy.

title: The Overnight Bestie
hook: A girl moves to a new city and a friendly neighbor becomes her "best friend" overnight — with a hidden price.
story: New in Dindigul, Rajesh was lonely. Then Kavin came. "Namba best friends!" They played, shared lunch. Kavin bought him snacks. Then Kavin said, "Un amma gold chain eduthutu vaa, namba oru game aadalaam." Rajesh was confused. He told Amma. Amma's face turned pale. "Rajesh, avan romba close vantha vazhi dhan ithu. Avan namba friend illa." Rajesh cried that night — but learned a lesson.
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Amma, avan enakku ellam kuduthaan, aana avan gold chain kaekraan!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da — sila payyan nalla irundhu, unna trap pannuva. Nee bayapada vendam, therinjutta.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the colony park, a broken gold chain in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru un kooda nalla mathiri iruppaan. Aana avan kaalam paarpan. Nee avan kaiyila erangakoodadhu. Nallavadhu ellaam nalladhu illa."
thirukkural: பகைநட்பாம் காலம் வருங்கால் முகநட்டு அகநட்பு ஒரீஇ விடல்.
english_translation: When foes, in time, play friendship's part feign love on face but not in heart.
moral: An enemy's friendship is a mask — they'll smile with you today and trap you tomorrow.

title: The Free Candy Loss
hook: A vendor offers Mani a free giant candy — but it costs him his new pencil box.
story: At the bus stand, a man held out a giant lollipop. "Free da! Eduthuko!" Mani was thrilled. He handed over his new pencil box to hold it. The man ran. Mani cried. Paati hugged him. "Intha lollipop ivalavu periya nashtam pannichu da." Mani sniffled. "Free nu solradhula dhan andha trap irukku." Paati smiled. "Nee ippavdhu therinjutta. Romba costly candy da idhu."
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Paati, free candy nu sonnaan, aana en pencil box eduthutu odaidhaan!'
Paati: 'Free nu solravan kittam jaagrathaa irukanum da. Free la dhan periya nashtam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stand, a single coin in his wrinkled hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru nalaadhula dhan periya nashtam irukkum. Silar free nu solli velaiya edukkuva. Nee aasai pattu erangakoodadhu."
thirukkural: பேதைமை என்பதொன்று யாதெனின் ஏதங்கொண்டு ஊதியம் போகவிடல்.
english_translation: This is folly's prominent vein to favour loss and forego gain.
moral: Folly is taking a loss and calling it a deal — not everything free is a gift.

title: The YouTube Dare
hook: Balu's friend dares him to cycle down a steep hill without brakes — for 100 YouTube likes.
story: Muthu pulled out his phone. "Nee brakela cycle vidu na, indha video ku 100 likes varum da!" Balu's heart raced. He wanted fame. He pedaled down the Hairpin Bend. The cycle went crazy. Balu crashed into a bush. Bloody knees. Akka ran to him. "Unakku piththu pudichirukka da? 100 likes ku un uyira kudukka?" Balu cried. Likes didn't matter now.
tamil_dialogues: Balu: 'Akka, likes ku yellam naan enna panna matten inime!'
Akka: 'Nalla vishayam da — unaku theriyadha vishayathula erangadha. Adhu dhan unmai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a silver oak tree in Yercaud, a broken cycle wheel beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru nala vittu, unakku theriyadha kettadhu pannradhu — adhu dhan peththai. Un uyir ku mela likes illa."
thirukkural: பேதைமையுள் எல்லாம் பேதைமை காதன்மை கையல்ல தன்கட் செயல்.
english_translation: Folly of follies is to lead a lewd and lawless life so bad.
moral: The worst kind of foolishness is chasing cheap thrills at the cost of your own safety.

title: The Lost Boy
hook: Kanna wanders off at the station because he doesn't listen — and nearly gets on the wrong train.
story: Amma told Kanna, "En kooda iru. Pogadha." But Kanna was bored. He followed a balloon. Then a dog. Then he saw a train and climbed in. The doors closed. Kanna screamed. A kind uncle pulled him out just in time. Amma was in tears. "Unakku naan solli kodutha mattum pothuma? Unakku nana illaya?" Kanna hugged her tight. "Amma, inime naan kettu dhan pannuven."
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Unakku naan solli kodutha mattum potha, nee un vazhiye pova!'
Kanna: 'Amma, enakku bayama irukku. Inime naan un koodave iruppen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the railway platform, a lantern in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naanam illa, nadai illa, aasai illa — adhu dhan peththaiyin ettu. Kettu dhan nadakkanum, illana vali thappidum."
thirukkural: நாணாமை நாடாமை நாரின்மை யாதொன்றும் பேணாமை பேதை தொழில்.
english_translation: Shameless, aimless, callous, listless such are the marks of foolishness.
moral: Foolishness is wandering without aim, shame, or care — a lost life is a lost train.

title: The Smart Fool
hook: Kavya's cousin reads philosophy books but screams at his grandmother over food — and Kavya is shocked.
story: Cousin Siva came from Chennai. He talked about Einstein, Buddha, universe. Kavya was impressed. Then Paati served sambar rice. Siva pushed the plate. "Sambar la enna podra? Intha food romba boring!" Paati's eyes welled. Kavya couldn't believe it. "Nee Buddha, Einstein ellam pesra, aana unakku vaayai adakka theriyadha?" Siva went silent. Thatha whispered, "Padichadhula dhan aaganum, sollala."
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Siva, nalla padichirukka, aana un vaai adakka theriyadha?'
Siva: 'Enakku... enakku kovam dhan vanthuchu.'
Kavya: 'Adhu dhan problem — padichadhu manasula poga maatum pola irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the temple kolam, a philosophy book open but face down.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Evlo padichalum un manasu mattama irukka, adhu dhan periya peththai. Padichadhu un nadaiyila theriyanum. Illana, vazhakkam dhan."
thirukkural: ஓதி உணர்ந்தும் பிறர்க்குரைத்தும் தானடங்காப் பேதையின் பேதையார் இல்.
english_translation: No fool equals the fool who learns knows, teaches, but self-control spurns.
moral: The biggest fool is one who learns, teaches, but never tames their own temper.

title: The One-Birth Hell
hook: Arul's friend lies, cheats, and steals small things — Amma says he's building his own hell.
story: Kumar was Arul's friend. He stole Amma's bangle and blamed the maid. He lied about homework. Small things. One day, Kumar's own mother cried at school. "En payyan evanume nambamaata." Amma told Arul, "Kumar oru naragatha kattittu irukkaan — adhu avanukku oru piravi la dhan." Arul didn't understand. "Naragam?" Amma said, "Avan seyra thappu dhan avan naragam. Avan poitu dhan therinjukuraan."
tamil_dialogues: Arul: 'Amma, Kumar nalla irukka mathiri irukkaan!'
Amma: 'Ada, avan seyra thappu dhan avanukku naragam. Intha piravi yil dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the beach, the ocean roaring behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Peththai oru piravi la dhan naragatha kaapaan. Avan seyra thappu dhan avanukku vali. Atha vida mudiyadhu."
thirukkural: ஒருமைச் செயலாற்றும் பேதை எழுமையும் தான்புக் கழுந்தும் அளறு.
english_translation: The fool suffers seven fold hells in single birth of hellish ills.
moral: A fool builds their own hell in this very life — bad choices burn you here and now.

title: The Kitchen Disaster
hook: Viji has never cooked but tells Paati she can make the family dinner — and nearly burns the house.
story: Paati was sick. Viji said, "Paati, nee odu. Naan dhan saapadu pannuraen!" Paati smiled weakly. Viji poured oil — half a bottle. She threw in onions. The pan caught fire. Smoke everywhere. Neighbors ran in. Viji sat crying. "Paati, enakku theriyaama sonnen." Paati laughed gently. "Yaar kitta kettadhu? Nee keta va? Poi solli pannradhu dhan peththai, Viji."
tamil_dialogues: Viji: 'Paati, enakku theriyum nu sonnen, aana enna theriyala!'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan da — theriyama theriyum nu solli pannradhu periya peththai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the smoke-filled kitchen, a burnt ladle in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Theriyadhadhu theriyum nu sonnadhu dhan andha peththai. Kaila eranga mun kettu dhan pannanum."
thirukkural: பொய்படும் ஒன்றோ புனைபூணும் கையறியாப் பேதை வினைமேற் கொளின்.
english_translation: A know-nothing fool daring a deed not only fails but feels fettered.
moral: A fool who attempts what they don't know is heading straight for disaster, not glory.

title: The Lucky Winner
hook: Sekar wins a TV in a lottery but loses his family's savings trying to win a car.
story: Sekar's scratch card won a TV! He was thrilled. Then the man said, "Innum oru card eduthu — car varum!" Sekar spent his whole piggy bank. Nothing. Then Appa's 2000 rupees from the drawer. Nothing. Then Amma's gold chain. Nothing. Appa found out. Sekar sobbed. "Appa, enakku car venum nu aasai patten." Appa's face was heavy. "Un aasai dhan namma ellam nashtam pannichu."
tamil_dialogues: Sekar: 'Appa, enakku oru TV vanthuchu! Appo car um varum nu nenachen!'
Appa: 'Athu dhan peththai da. Oru nala kidaichadhula ninacha, adhu periya nala. Aana nee adhai vita travel panna.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the market, a broken scratch card at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Silaruku porumai illa — kidaicha porulai kaapadhradhuku badhila, adhai vida periya aasai pattu kaiyila irundhadhai izhthuva. Adhu dhan peththai."
thirukkural: ஏதிலார் ஆரத்தமர்பசிப்பர் பேதை பெருஞ்செல்வம் உற்றக்கடை.
english_translation: Strangers feast and kinsmen fast when fools mishandle fortunes vast.
moral: A fool with fortune feeds strangers while starving his own — greed wastes everything.

title: The Cycle King
hook: Ganesh gets a new cycle and acts like the king of the road — until he crashes into the principal.
story: Ganesh's Appa bought him a shiny new cycle. Ganesh felt like a king. He cycled like a maniac — no hands, eyes closed, loud shouting. "Naan dhan road king!" His friends cheered. Then he crashed into the school principal's scooter. The principal picked him up. "Unakku cycle kedaichadhum thala suthudha? Adha vida periya aal illa nu nenaikra, aana vazhi la dhan irukkanum."
tamil_dialogues: Teacher: 'Cycle kedaichadhum unakku bayam illama pochu?'
Ganesh: 'Sir... enakku bayam illa. Naan dhan king nu nenachen.'
Teacher: 'Bayam illadhadhu dhan unna kola pannichu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, a bicycle wheel spinning beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaiyila edhavadhu kedaichadhum, sila peththaigaluku thala suthum. Adhu dhan — kedaichadhula santhoshama irukka theriyadhadhu."
thirukkural: மையல் ஒருவன் களித்தற்றால் பேதைதன் கையொன்று உடைமை பெறின்.
english_translation: Fools possessing something on hand like dazed and drunken stupids stand.
moral: A fool with a little gets dizzy with pride — true winners stay grounded.

title: The Easy Friend
hook: Priya makes a "fun friend" who laughs at everything — but vanishes when Priya needs help.
story: Priya met Komal at the beach. Komal was super fun — laughed at every joke, shared ice cream, clicked photos. "Namba best friends forever!" Next day, Priya fell and twisted her ankle. She called Komal. "Komal, naan vizhundhuten, enakku help pannu!" Komal looked, then walked away with another group. Priya sat crying. Amma helped her up. "Priya, adhu dhan poi friend. Adhu dhan peththai — avanai nambadha."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Amma, ava enakku best friend nu sonnaanga, aana enna vittutu pOiduchu!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan — silar kastam varum bodhu dhan yaar nu theriyum. Adhunala dhan jaagrathaa irukanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the Promenade wall, the sea breeze in his white hair.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Peththaiyin nanbargal vilayaata nallavadhu dhan iruppa. Aana kastam varum bodhu avanga odiduva. Adhu dhan peththaiyin natpu."
thirukkural: பெரிதினிது பேதையார் கேண்மை பிரிவின்கண் பீழை தருவதொன்று இல்.
english_translation: Friendship with fools is highly sweet for without a groan we part.
moral: A fool's friendship is sweet candy — fun to eat but leaves you hungry when it matters.

title: The Wise Crowd
theme: Folly / The Fool Among the Wise
hook: Dinesh's uncle takes him to a scholarly gathering — where Dinesh realizes how much he doesn't know.
story: Periyappa took Dinesh to a gathering of poets and scholars. Everyone spoke in deep Tamil, quoted sangam literature. Dinesh tried to show off. "Naan dhan class la first rank!" An old scholar smiled. "Enna pathi solra? Unakku theriyadhadhu evlo periyadhu nu theriyuma?" Dinesh went red. Periyappa laughed gently. "Dinesh, nalla irukravangala saarnthu irundha, unakku theriyum. Athu dhan vala."
tamil_dialogues: Dinesh: 'Periyappa, avanga pesradhu onnum enakku puriyala.'
Periyappa: 'Adhu dhan nalla vishayam — unakku theriyadhadhu theriyudhu. Peththaiukku adhu kooda theriyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits among the scholars, a palm-leaf manuscript open on his lap.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Peththai nalla irukravangala saarnthu irundhum avan padikira maadhiri dhan iruppan. Aana avan ullathan adhai therinjuka maatan."
thirukkural: கழாஅக்கால் பள்ளியுள் வைத்தற்றால் சான்றோர் குழாஅத்துப் பேதை புகல்.
english_translation: Entrance of fools where Savants meet looks like couch trod by unclean feet.
moral: A fool among the wise is like an unwashed foot on a clean bed — out of place and unaware.

title: The Blind Scholar
hook: Vanitha's friend knows every fact in every book — but can't solve a single real-life problem.
story: Priya could recite all capital cities, all rivers, all mountains. Vanitha admired her. But at the library, the fan stopped. Priya panicked. "Enna pannradhu!" Vanitha found the switch. Priya couldn't boil rice, couldn't tie a saree, couldn't even cross the road. Vanitha asked Amma, "Priya neraya padichirukka, aana avanuku vazhka theriyadha?" Amma said, "Adhu dhan — padichadhala mathum vazhka mudiyadhu da."
tamil_dialogues: Vanitha: 'Amma, Priya ku ellam theriyum, aana enna vazhka theriyadha?'
Amma: 'Padichadhu dhan porum. Aana, vazhka therinjukanum. Adhu dhan unmai arivu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the library shelf, a closed book in one hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Padichadhala mathum pothum. Arivu dhan venum. Puththagam padichadhala dhan vali theriyadhu. Vazhkai padikkum bodhu dhan arivu varum."
thirukkural: அறிவின்மை இன்மையுள் இன்மை பிறிதின்மை இன்மையா வையாது உலகு.
english_translation: Want of wisdom is want of wants want of aught else the world nev'r counts.
moral: The worst poverty is not lack of money but lack of wisdom — books aren't life.

title: The Rich Fool's Gift
hook: A rich man in Muthu's street gives him a gold coin — but Thatha explains the real giver is Muthu's character.
story: The rich man gave Muthu a real gold coin. "Eduthuko da, unakku!" Muthu ran to Thatha. "Thatha, paaru! Gold coin!" Thatha examined it. "Avanuku idhu periya vishayam illa. Aana nee evlo nallavadhu irukka? Adhu dhan — avan kuduthadhala nee nallavan aagamaata. Un nallavadhu dhan kidaichadhuku kaaranam." Muthu didn't fully understand. Years later, he realized: the gold was worthless, but his own honesty was priceless.
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Thatha, avar nallavadhu dhan kuduthaar!'
Thatha: 'Illada. Avan koduthadhala avan nallavan aagamaatan. Nee kidaichadhuku karanam un nallavadhu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in Sivakasi's dusty street, a single sparkler glowing in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Peththai koduthaalum, adhu vaasi illa. Aana, nalla manasu irukravan kitta kodutha dhan adhu vaasi. Kudukiravan nallavadhu illana, adhu poochi."
thirukkural: அறிவிலான் நெஞ்சுவந்து ஈதல் பிறிதியாதும் இல்லை பெறுவான் தவம்.
english_translation: When fool bestows with glee a gift it comes but by getter's merit.
moral: A fool's gift doesn't make him kind — it's the receiver's own merit that draws goodness.

title: The Self-Harm Hero
hook: Suriya's friend pierces his own ear with a safety pin to look "cool" — and ends up in hospital.
story: Murugan wanted to be like movie stars. "Paaru, naan cool aaguraen!" He took a safety pin and pushed it through his own ear. Blood everywhere. Suriya screamed. Murugan fainted. At the hospital, his mother cried. "En payyan enna da pannittaan?" Suriya told Amma. Amma sighed. "Avan nallavadhu mathiri irukka ninaikiraan, aana avan thanne kadichikiraan. Adhu dhan kastam."
tamil_dialogues: Suriya: 'Amma, Murugan cool aaguraen nu ear la pin pannittaan!'
Amma: 'Ada pavam — avan nalla irukka ninaikiraan, aana avan thanne kettadhu pannikiraan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the village clinic, a cotton swab in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Peththai thanne thanakku ketta dhan seiva. Avan nallavadhu mathiri ninaikiraan, aana avan thalaivan avanai kolluraan."
thirukkural: அறிவிலார் தாந்தம்மைப் பீழிக்கும் பீழை செறுவார்க்கும் செய்தல் அரிது.
english_translation: The self-torments of fools exceed ev'n tortures of their foes indeed.
moral: No enemy can hurt a fool as much as the fool hurts himself trying to be "cool."

title: The Proud Rooster
hook: Vasu tells everyone he's the smartest in school — until the new girl humbles him with three questions.
story: Vasu walked around saying, "Naan dhan unga ellarukum maele." New girl Soumya asked, "Unna pathi sollu. Payan sollu. Irandu vaithiyam sollu." Vasu went blank. "Enna? Idhu enga school lesson la varadha?" Soumya smiled, "Adhu dhan — unakku theriyadha vishayam. Nee evlo periya aal." The whole class laughed. Vasu's face burned. Teacher smiled. "Nambikkai nalla dhan, aana adhu ellai kadandha peththai aagidum."
tamil_dialogues: Vasu: 'Naan dhan ellaam therinjavana!'
Soumya: 'Apdi na, en kannu enna color? Nee sollu.'
Vasu: 'Adhu... enakku theriyadha.'
Soumya: 'Nee ellam theriyadha dhan theriyudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the school ground, a peacock feather fallen at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naan dhan periya arivu aalanu ninaikravan, avanukku adhu dhan periya peththai. Unakku theriyadhadhu theriyala na, adhu dhan problem."
thirukkural: வெண்மை எனப்படுவது யாதெனின் ஒண்மை உடையம்யாம் என்னும் செருக்கு.
english_translation: Stupidity is vanity that cries "We have sagacity."
moral: The loudest "I know everything" is the clearest sign you know nothing.

title: The Fake Doctor
hook: A man in a white coat offers free checkups at the bus stand — but Meena's father catches him in a lie.
story: A man in a white coat sat at the bus stand. "Free blood test! Naan dhan doctor!" Many people lined up. Meena's Appa asked, "Enga padichinga doctor?" The man stuttered. "Chennai la... Madras Medical..." Appa asked, "Dean yaaru?" The man ran. Appa told the crowd, "Ithu doctor illa. Intha maadhiri aal ellam nammala mosam pannuva." Meena learned: people who pretend to know are more dangerous than those who admit they don't.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Appa, avar doctor maadhiri irundhaar!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — silar theriyama doctor maadhiri nadichu, nammala mosam pannuva.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stand, a stethoscope hanging from his neck, unused.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Theriyadhadhu theriyum nu solli pannradhu — adhu dhan peththai. Adhu mathavangaluku apayam. Unakku theriyadhadhu theriyala nu solla bayapadadha."
thirukkural: கல்லாத மேற்கொண்டொழுகல் கசடற வல்லதூஉம் ஐயம் தரும்.
english_translation: Feigning knowledge that one has not leads to doubt ev'n that he has got.
moral: Faking what you don't know makes people doubt even what you truly know.

title: The Naked King
hook: Kumar hides his torn shirt under a shiny new jacket — but his torn pocket shows everything.
story: Kumar wore a new jacket over his old torn shirt. "Akka, paaru, naan evlo smart!" But his jacket pocket was torn. His marbles fell out, then his lunch money, then his handkerchief. People pointed and laughed. Akka said, "Nee veliya nallavadhu mathiri kattra, aana ullae dhaan theriyudhu." Kumar cried. Akka hugged him. "Maranikka vendam Kumar. Adhu dhan — namma namma fault ah cover pannradhula dhan adhu dhan theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Akka, naan smarta irukka nenaichen!'
Akka: 'Java javanu cover pannra, aana un kurai veliya dhan theriyudhu. Adhu dhan periya peththai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the bazaar, a torn shirt neatly folded in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Peththai avan kuraiya cover pannuvaan. Aana avan seyala dhan avan kurai theriyum. Kuraiyai cover pannadhadhu dhan nalladhu."
thirukkural: அற்றம் மறைத்தலோ புல்லறிவு தம்வயின் குற்றம் மறையாவழி.
english_translation: Fools their nakedness conceal and yet their glaring faults reveal.
moral: Covering one fault only exposes another — honesty is the only real cover.

title: The Wise Advice That Wasn't
hook: Rajiv ignores Thatha's warning about the slippery cliff edge — and almost falls 500 feet.
story: At Doddabetta, Thatha said, "Rajiv, anga erangaadha. Adhu sambada." Rajiv rolled his eyes. "Thatha, unakku bayam. Naan dhan hero." He ran to the edge. The mud gave way. Rajiv slipped. Thatha grabbed his shirt just in time. Rajiv clung to Thatha, shaking. "Thatha... un sonnadhu keta irukka?" Thatha held him tight. "Nee keta irundha, ithu nadakkaadha. Peththai dhan avan aala therinja maadhiri irundhu, avan kettavan solla keta maatran."
tamil_dialogues: Rajiv: 'Thatha, naan un sonnadhu kettu irukka vendiyadhu!'
Thatha: 'Adhu dhan da — muzhakki vandha pin dhan theriyum. Nee kettavan solla keta maatta.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the cliff edge, mist swirling around his white veshti.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Peththai avanai kaapadhra solla keta maatan. Avanukku ellaam theriyum nu ninaipaan. Aana adhu dhan avanai kola pannum."
thirukkural: அருமறை சோரும் அறிவிலான் செய்யும் பெருமிறை தானே தனக்கு.
english_translation: The fool that slights sacred counsels upon himself great harm entails.
moral: The fool who ignores good advice builds his own disaster with his own hands.

title: The Stubborn Mule
hook: Eswari refuses to learn how to cross the road — until she almost gets hit by a bus.
story: Amma tried to teach Eswari: "Paakka, left paaru, right paaru, aprom dhan cross pannu." Eswari said, "Enakku theriyum da! Naan dhan munnadi pOguraen!" She ran across. A bus screeched. The driver shouted. Eswari froze mid-road. Amma pulled her. "Unakku theriyadha na solli kudutha kettu dhan aaganum." Eswari sobbed. "Amma, inime naan kettu dhan pannuven. Unakku theriyadha dhan enakku theriyala."
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Naan solli kuduthadhu kettu dhan aaganum.'
Eswari: 'Amma, inime naan kettu dhan pannuven. Unakku theriyadha dhan enakku theriyala.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the crosswalk, his hand stopping traffic.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Peththai avanukku edhu nalla theriyadhu. Sonna kettu dhan aaganum. Illana avan aala kettadhu nadakkum. Avan uyir dhan andha vidha."
thirukkural: ஏவவும் செய்கலான் தான்தேறான் அவ்வுயிர் போஒம் அளவுமோர் நோய்.
english_translation: He listens not nor himself knows plague is his life until it goes.
moral: A fool who won't listen and won't learn carries a plague until life itself is lost.

title: The Blind Leading the Blind
hook: Chitra's friend keeps "teaching" her the wrong math — and Appa shows her how the blind lead the blind.
story: Deepa said she'd teach Chitra math. "Naane solli kuduraen!" But Deepa didn't know it herself. She taught Chitra 2+2=5, 4+4=7. Chitra failed the test. Appa sat with her. "Deepa ku theriyum nu nenaikira? Avanukkum theriyadhu." Appa showed her the real method. "Appa, Deepa enakku thappa dhan solli kuduthaanga!" Appa nodded. "Peththai dhan — theriyama theriyum nu solli mathavangalukku thappa solli kudukiravan."
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'Appa, Deepa enakku thappa dhan solli kuduthaanga!'
Appa: 'Avanukku dhan theriyala. Theriyadhavan theriyamavanukku solli kudukiraan — adhu dhan kastam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the junction, a chalkboard with wrong sums beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaanadhavan kanadhavanukku vazhi kaami. Adhu dhan peththai. Unakku theriyadhadhu theriyala na, adha mathavangalukku solradhu dhan apayam."
thirukkural: காணாதான் காட்டுவான் தான்காணான் காணாதான் கண்டானாம் தான்கண்டவாறு.
english_translation: Sans Self-sight in vain one opens sight to the blind who bet their sight as right.
moral: The blind who guides the blind leads both into a pit — know yourself before teaching others.

title: The Boy Who Said No To Everything
hook: Pandi refuses to believe the well is dry even after seeing the empty bucket — he keeps lowering it anyway.
story: The village well had run dry. Everyone knew. But Pandi said, "Illada! Thanney irukku! Ellarum poi solra!" Every morning he lowered the bucket. Every time it came up empty. "Nallaaa paaru!" Amma brought him to the well. "Pandi, thanney illa. Adhu nijam." Pandi covered his ears. "Illada! Illada!" Then one day, his bucket brought up mud. He sat and cried. "Amma, thanney illa. Nijam dhan." Amma hugged him. "Unnala nijatha ethuka mudiyala. Aana adhu nijam."
tamil_dialogues: Pandi: 'Amma, thanney irukku nu naan solraen!'
Amma: 'Enna da solla? Nee kaetta, adhu dhan unmai. Nee unmaiya ethuka mudiyala nu artham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the dry well, an empty bucket beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Peththai nijatha ethuka maatan. Avan nijam theriyum, aana avan adha ethuka maatan. Avan sonnadhu dhan avanukku nijam. Adhu dhan avanai kola pannum."
thirukkural: உலகத்தார் உண்டென்பது இல்லென்பான் வையத்து அலகையா வைக்கப்படும்.
english_translation: To people's "Yes" who proffer "No" deemed as ghouls on earth they go.
moral: Denying what the whole world knows is not bravery — it's burying yourself in your own delusion.

title: Broken Cycle
hook: Harini slaps Karthi for tearing her notebook — then sees the same anger in her own reflection.
story: Karthi ripped Harini's project by mistake. She slapped him hard. He cried silently. That night, Harini saw Amma yelling at Appa over the phone — same vein popping on the forehead, same harsh voice. She looked in the mirror. Her own face still carried the anger. She went to Karthi's bed and hugged him. "Sorry da."
tamil_dialogues: Harini: 'Karthi, un mela enakku kovama irundhadhu. Appa adhu thappu.'
Karthi: 'Akka, nee enna adicha, valikkala. Aana unga mooka paatha dhan bayama irundhadhu.'
Harini: 'Naan ippo purinjiken. Kovam romba kettadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the stairs between the two siblings, a hand on each head.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Harini, un kovam onnu mattum illa — adhu oru noy. Ellaa uyirukkum naduvula adhu verupai kudukkum. Atha vittuta."
thirukkural: இகலென்ப எல்லா உயிர்க்கும் பகலென்னும் பண்பின்மை பார஧க்கும் நோய்.
english_translation: Hatred is a plague that divides And rouses illwill on all sides.
moral: Hostility is a disease that turns everyone into enemies — cure it at the root.

title: The Spilled Paint
hook: Rohan deliberately spills paint on Sneha's new dress — she wants to scream but remembers Appa's words.
story: Rohan knocked over the paint can. Sneha's white dress turned blue. She clenched her fists. "Nee!" But Appa's voice echoed — "Kovama irundha, adhu avana vida née ketta aaiduva." She took a breath, walked away. Rohan looked confused — he expected a fight. Next day, Sneha gave him half her lunch. Rohan never troubled her again.
tamil_dialogues: Sneha: 'Appa, avan enna dress kulla paint pottan! Naan adicha kooda thappa illa!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — avan unna kovam varathaan paakuran. Nee kovama irundha, avan jeichu mudinjadhu.'
Sneha: 'Appo... kovama irundha avanukku velai kudukkura maadhiri?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the colony gate, watching the children play together.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku kovam vantha, avan jeichuduran. Nee amaichi irundha, dhan nee jeipaa. Athu dhan periya vellum."
thirukkural: பகல்கருதிப் பற்றா செயினும் இகல்கருதி இன்னாசெய் யாமை தலை.
english_translation: Rouse not hatred and confusion Though foes provoke disunion.
moral: Even when others provoke you, refusing to hate is the greatest victory.

title: The Glory of Letting Go
hook: Vikram's rival trips him during the relay — but instead of fighting back, he does the unthinkable.
story: During the school relay, Karthik stuck out his foot. Vikram fell, scraped his knee. The crowd gasped. Vikram got up — not to punch Karthik, but to finish the race. He came last. But the whole school cheered for him. Coach Suresh lifted him. "Neeyae dhan winner, da." Karthik came later, ashamed. "Sorry da."
