tamil_dialogues: Coach: 'Vikram, avan unna vizhuthitan. Nee adicha kooda seri dhan!'
Vikram: 'Sir, avan vida naan nallavan nu kattanum. Athuku adi podradhu dhan vazhi illa.'
Karthik: 'Sorry da. Nee periyavan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the finish line, holding the torn ribbon.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku kovama irundha, adha vittutu po. Appo dhan unakku nijamana perumai vanthu sernthu."
thirukkural: இகலென்னும் எவ்வநோய் நீக்கின் தவலில்லாத் தாவில் விளக்கம் தரும்.
english_translation: Shun the plague of enmity And win everlasting glory.
moral: Let go of hostility, and you gain a glory that never fades.

title: The Rotten Mango
theme: Hostility / Joy Comes When Hate Goes
hook: Anitha hates the neighbour boy who broke her doll — Paati shows her a rotten mango and asks why.
story: Anitha sat fuming under the mango tree. "Paati, avan en bommai odachutan. Naan avanuku kudutha vidalamaatten." Paati picked up a rotten mango. "Indha maanga kaani, romba azhaga irundhadhu. Aana ullae poochi pidichu, kedudhu." Anitha looked. "Romba mosam." Paati smiled. "Nee vachirukra verupu adhe maadhiri dhan. Un ullae keduthudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Paati, avan bommai odachutan, naan avanuku enna pannuven?'
Paati: 'Un ullae irukka verupu dhan unna kedukkurathu. Avana vida."
Anitha: 'Aana Paati... athu romba kashtam.'
Paati: 'Kashtam dhan. Aana adha vittuta. Appo dhan un ullae sandhoshame varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the mango tree, catching a falling ripe mango.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Verupu unda eduthukittu irundha, sandhosham varaadhu. Verupai vittutu sandhosha padu."
thirukkural: இன்பத்துள் இன்பம் பயக்கும் இகலென்னும் துன்பத்துள் துன்பங் கெடின்.
english_translation: Hate-the woe of woes destroy; Then joy of joys you can enjoy.
moral: When hatred — the worst sorrow — is destroyed, the greatest joy dawns.

title: The Peace Post
hook: Yuvan watches a boy who never fights, never argues — and sees the gang leader unable to touch him.
story: Guna's gang ruled the bus stand. They picked fights, stole change. But one boy, Sathish, walked through them every day without a word. He didn't fight, didn't flinch. Guna tried everything — shoving, taunting. Sathish just smiled and walked. One day Guna gave up. "Unna vida veera illa da." Yuvan learned: peace is stronger than any fist.
tamil_dialogues: Yuvan: 'Appa, Sathish avanuku bayama illaya? Guna romba dangerous.'
Appa: 'Bayam illa da. Adhu dhan avanoda aayudham. Yaalum avana velkka mudiyadhu.'
Sathish: 'Enakku yaar melayum verupu illa. Athu dhan en protection.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a bus stop bench, reading a palm leaf.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaar kitta verupu illayo, avana yaalum velkka mudiyadhu. Adhu dhan unmaiyana veeram."
thirukkural: இகலெதிர் சாய்ந்தொழுக வல்லாரை யாரே மிக்லூக்கும் தன்மை யவர்.
english_translation: Who can overcome them in glory That are free from enmity?
moral: Those who live without hostility are invincible — no one can conquer them.

title: The Quarrel King
hook: Dinesh loves arguing and fighting — until he finds himself completely alone at lunch.
story: Dinesh argued with everyone. "Nee thappu" "Nee sonnadhu sari illa" "Adhu enakku pidikalai." One day, at lunch, every bench was full. No one sat with him. He walked around — every group turned away. He sat alone. Teacher saw. "Dinesh, un kooda yaarum illa?" Dinesh's eyes welled. He finally understood: his own hostility had eaten all his friendships.
tamil_dialogues: Teacher: 'Dinesh, yaarum un kooda saapda varala? Enna achu?'
Dinesh: 'Sir... naan dhan ellar kooda sandai poduven. Adhu dhan inga yaarum illa.'
Teacher: 'Pakka. Un verupu dhan unna thaniya aakichu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the empty spot beside Dinesh, sharing his own food.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Verupu irukka, sandhosham illa. Nee vera yaar kitta iruntha, avanga kuda unna vittutu poiduvanga. Athu dhan un kadhai."
thirukkural: இகலின் மிகலினிது என்பவன் வாழ்க்கை தவலும் கெடலும் நணித்து.
english_translation: His fall and ruin are quite near Who holds enmity sweet and dear.
moral: If you cherish hostility, your ruin is never far behind.

title: The Blind Spot
hook: Kavya hates the new girl from the "other side of town" — until she sees her reading the same book.
story: Riya transferred from the other side of town. Kavya's friends whispered. "Avanga area lam romba mosam." Kavya decided she didn't like Riya. But in the library, Riya was reading Kavya's favourite book. "Unakku dhan ishtama?" Riya smiled. Kavya sat down. They talked for an hour. Kavya realized: the hate was built on nothing.
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Amma, avanga area vera maadhiri, avanga nammala life vera.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan problem da — nee area paathu manushanai judge pannura.'
Riya: 'Nee enna en mela verupu vachirukka? Naan enna pannitten?'
Kavya: 'Onnum illa Riya. Naan dhan thappu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the school library, reading a book over their shoulders.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Verupu irukka, unakku unmai theriyadhu. Nee avanai pathi enna ninaikkera, adhu unmai illa. Nee avanai therinjapram dhan puriyum."
thirukkural: மிகல்மேவல் மெய்ப்பொருள் காணார் இகல்மேவல் இன்னா அறிவி னவர்.
english_translation: They cannot see the supreme Truth Who hate and injure without ruth.
moral: Hatred blinds you to the truth — you cannot see clearly when your heart is full of enmity.

title: The Broken Bat
hook: Murali breaks Karthik's new cricket bat — Karthik wants revenge but Thatha teaches a different way.
story: Murali swung and the bat cracked. "Nee!" Karthik's face went red. He pushed Murali. Murali pushed back. Thatha separated them. "Rendu perum cabula vitutu vanga." Karthik fumed all night. Next morning, Thatha took him to the shop. "Puthu bat vaangu." But Thatha bought two — one for Murali. "Appa, avan?" Murali came that evening, ashamed, with money. Karthik gave him the new bat. They played till sunset.
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Thatha, avan bat odachutan! Avanuku kuda bat vaangikita?'
Thatha: 'Avane un kitta vandhu sorry sonnan. Adhu periya vishayam. Nee avanuku bat kodutha, avan un best friend aaiduvaan.'
Murali: 'Karthik, naan vangi tharen. Unakku kovama irukuma?'
Karthik: 'Illada. Vaa vilaadalaam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple steps, watching the boys play cricket.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Verupai vittutu nalla irukradhu dhan periya aadhaayam. Nee vittukodutha, athu dhan un vellum."
thirukkural: இகலிற்கு எதிர்சாய்தல் ஆக்கம் அதனை மிக்லூக்கின் ஊக்குமாம் கேடு.
english_translation: To turn from enmity is gain Fomenting it brings fast ruin.
moral: Turning away from hostility brings prosperity; feeding it invites destruction.

title: The Fortune That Followed
hook: Divya hates the rickshaw uncle who overcharged them — until she stops hating and fortune turns.
story: Rickshaw uncle charged double. Divya hated him. "Appa, avan kalla." Every time she saw him, she stared angrily. Weeks later, she saw him struggling to push his rickshaw up a slope. "Appa, avan." Appa smiled. "Poi thooki vidu." Divya helped push. Uncle's eyes teared. "Unakku romba nandri." Next day, he came home with free vegetables from the market. Divya learned: let hate go, good things come.
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Appa, avan dhan namala mosam pannavan. Enna avana help pannanum?'
Appa: 'Adha vittutu. Nee help panna, athu unakku nalladhu.'
Uncle: 'Nee help panna romba santhosham. Ithu unakku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the rickshaw, holding the vegetable bag.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Verupai vittutu nalla irundha, unakku nalladhu nadakkum. Adhu dhan vaazhkai."
thirukkural: இகல்காணான் ஆக்கம் வருங்கால் அதனை மிகல்காணும் கேடு தரற்கு.
english_translation: Fortune favours when hate recedes Hatred exceeding ruin breeds.
moral: When hatred fades, fortune follows. When hatred grows, ruin comes.

title: The Weaving Wall
hook: Meena's mother and Selvi's mother are rivals — but the girls discover they weave the same pattern.
story: Meena's Amma and Selvi's Amma competed for the same silk contract. The families stopped talking. Meena was told not to play with Selvi. But one day, Meena saw Selvi drawing a silk pattern on the road — the same one Meena had drawn at home. "Neeyum?" They looked at each other. They started drawing together. Their mothers saw them laughing. The rivalry melted.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Selvi, namma rendu perum ore pattern vaairadhu!'
Selvi: 'Ama! Namma Amma rendu perum sandai podraanga, aana namma rendu perum ore maadhiri!'
Meena's Amma: 'Paaru... pasangaluku santhosham irukku. Namakku verupu venama.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at a silk loom, weaving the two girls' patterns together.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Verupu iruntha ellam kettadhu dhan. Sandhosham iruntha ellam nalladhu dhan. Adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: இகலானாம் இன்னாத எல்லாம் நகலானாம் நன்னயம் என்னும் செருக்கு.
english_translation: All evils come from enmity All goodness flow from amity.
moral: All bad things come from hatred; all good things come from friendship and love.

title: The Bully and the Tiny Kid
theme: Might of Hatred / Choosing Your Battles
hook: Ezhil sees a senior beating up first-graders — he wants to fight but a teacher shows him a better way.
story: Senior Gopi took first-graders' lunch. Ezhil's blood boiled. "Naan avanuku padi kodukaren." Teacher stopped him. "Gopi periyavan. Nee adicha, nee dhan thappu." Instead, Teacher gathered all first-graders, made them sit together. When Gopi came, forty kids stared silently. Gopi froze. He couldn't fight forty. Teacher smiled at Ezhil. "Paatha? Veliya kattu dhan periya aayudham."
tamil_dialogues: Ezhil: 'Sir, avan chinna pasanga kita lunch eduthukuran! Naan avan kitta pesanum?'
Teacher: 'Adi podradhu dhan vazhi illa. Aana namma rendu perum avan vida periya aayudham eduthukalam.'
Ezhil: 'Edhu sir?'
Teacher: 'Oththai. Namma rendu perum onna serndha, avan yaarai adippan?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands with the forty children, their tiny hands joined together.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periya aal kita sandai podradhu theriyama irukka koodadhu. Aana chinna aal kita sandai podradhu thappu. Unakku therinja vazhi dhan nalla vazhi."
thirukkural: வலியார்க்கு மாறேற்றல் ஓம்புக ஓம்பா மெலியார்மேல் மேக பகை.
english_translation: Turn from strife with foes too strong With the feeble for battle long.
moral: Don't fight the strong recklessly, and never bully the weak — know your battles.

title: The Boy With No Friends
hook: Kavin brags he needs no one — until he falls off his bike and no one helps.
story: Kavin always said, "Enakku yaarum venam. Naan thane nallavan." He had no love for anyone, no friends. One day, he fell off his bike on the main road. His leg bled. People walked past. "Yaarum illa..." He sat there, helpless. Appa came later. "Enna achu?" Kavin cried. "Appa, enakku yaarum illa... Help panna kuda yaarum vara maatanga." Appa hugged him. "Adhu dhan da — anbu illa na, thunai illa."
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Appa, yaarum enna help panna varala. Ellarum poiduchu.'
Appa: 'Anbu illa na thunai illa da. Nee yaar kitta anbu vacha, avanga dhan un kitta varuvanga.'
Kavin: 'Appa... naan maari aaguren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside Kavin, cleaning his wound with a cloth.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anbu illa na, un kitta yaarum varuvangala. Adhu dhan — anbu dhan periya balam."
thirukkural: அன்பிலன் ஆன்ற துணையிலன் தான்துவ்வான் என்பரியும் ஏதிலான் துப்பு.
english_translation: Loveless, aidless, powerless king Can he withstand an enemy strong?
moral: Without love, without support, without strength — you cannot stand against any challenge.

title: The Easy Target
theme: Might of Hatred / Weakness Attracts Trouble
hook: Manoj is scared of everything — Amma tells him bullies smell fear like sharks smell blood.
story: Manoj was terrified of Senthil, the harbour bully. He gave him his lunch every day. He never complained. One day, Senthil took his new chappals. Manoj came home barefoot. Amma was furious. "Manoj, nee bayapdata, avanuku velai kudukkura maadhiri. Unna paatha avanuku innum aasai varum." Next day, Manoj stood straight. "Illai. Indha chappal enadhu." Senthil was shocked. He backed off.
tamil_dialogues: Manoj: 'Amma, avan periyavan. Naan avan kita onnum panna mudiyadhu.'
Amma: 'Nee bayapdala, avan un velai dhan paakuran. Avana kitta ninnu sol. Illai nu sol.'
Manoj: 'Aana...'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da. Oru vaati ninnu sollu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the harbour, a fishing net in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bayapdata, unna ellarum easy target nu nenaipanga. Unakku bayam illa na, yaarum unna thodamatten."
thirukkural: அஞ்சும் அறியான் அமைவிலன் ஈகலான் தஞ்சம் எளியன் பகைக்கு.
english_translation: Unskilled, timid, miser, misfit He is easy for foes to hit.
moral: Fear, ignorance, and meanness make you an easy target — courage protects you.

title: The Exploding Bottle
theme: Might of Hatred / Anger Makes You Weak
hook: Ganesh breaks a chair in anger — the yoga teacher shows him how anger makes him easy to defeat.
story: Ganesh lost a game. He flipped his desk. Crack! The chair leg broke. Everyone stared. Yoga teacher called him. "Vaa." He made Ganesh stand on one leg. "Nee kovama irundha, un balance poidum." Ganesh wobbled, fell. Teacher smiled. "Paatha? Oru chinna thallu kuda unna vizhuthidum." Ganesh understood: anger was his weakness, not his strength.
tamil_dialogues: Teacher: 'Ganesh, nee kovama irundha, unna yaalum thalli vizhuthalama?'
Ganesh: 'Illai sir... aana enaku kovam varudhu.'
Teacher: 'Adhu dhan unoda periya weakness. Kovathala nee un control-a izhkkura.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on one leg on a stone, perfectly still, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kovam irukka, née easy target. Unna yaalum velkka mudiyum. Amaidhi irundha, yaalum unna velkka mudiyadhu."
thirukkural: நீங்கான் வெகுளி நிறையிலன் எஞ்ஞான்றும் யாங்கணும் யார்க்கும் எளிது.
english_translation: The wrathful restive man is prey To any, anywhere any day.
moral: The angry person is weak and easily defeated — self-control is true strength.

title: The Twisted Path
theme: Might of Hatred / Character Invites Trouble
hook: Padma watches the neighbourhood boy lie, cheat, and steal — and sees everyone abandon him.
story: Kumar was clever — but in a crooked way. He lied, cheated in exams, stole marbles. "Naan smart, da." One by one, friends left. Even his own team didn't want him. Padma asked Thatha. "Yaarum avan kooda ille." Thatha said, "Née paathiya? Avan seyal dhan avanoda enemies-a saikum. Yaarum avanai nambamaata."
tamil_dialogues: Padma: 'Thatha, Kumar romba smart. Aana yaarum avan kooda ille.'
Thatha: 'Smart irundha dhan porum? Seyal nalla irukka vendum. Illa na, yaarum avan kooda irukka maata.'
Kumar: 'Enakku yaarum venum. Naan dhan periyavan.'
Padma: 'Unakku dhan theriyala — nee dhan ellaraiyum odichikittu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the corner of the Chettinad street, leaning on a pillar.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nadathai sari illa na, yaarum un kooda irukka maata. Ellarum unna vittutu poiduvanga."
thirukkural: வழிநோக்கான் வாய்ப்பன செய்யான் பழிநோக்கான் பண்பிலன் பற்றார்க்கு இனிது.
english_translation: Crooked, cruel, tactless and base Any foe can fell him with ease.
moral: A crooked, cruel, and tactless person makes enemies effortlessly — character protects you.

title: The Red Eyes
theme: Might of Hatred / Blind Rage Destroys
hook: Bala becomes obsessed with revenge against the neighbour who killed his dog — Appa shows him the cost.
story: Neighbour's visitors ran over Bala's dog, Shadow. Bala's world went dark. "Naan avan veetai erippen." He collected kerosene. Appa caught him. "Bala! Nee enna panna poren?" Bala cried. "Appa, avanga dhan Shadow-a kondu ponaanga!" Appa sat him down. "Nee avana ericha, unaku enna kedaikum? Shadow thirumba varaadhu. Aana nee jailukku pova." Bala broke down. His rage had almost destroyed his own life.
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Appa, avanga dhan Shadow-a kondu ponaanga! Naan avanuku vidamaatten.'
Appa: 'Nee avana ericha, Shadow thirumba varaadhu. Aana un life kedudum. Adhu dhan avanuku velai.'
Bala: 'Appo naan enna pannuven?'
Appa: 'Vittutu po. Adhu dhan periya dheram.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels where Shadow used to sleep, one hand on the ground.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kovathula née enna pannura nu theriyala. Athu unna kedukum. Adha vittutu po."
thirukkural: காணாச் சினத்தான் கழிபெருங் காமத்தான் பேணாமை பேணப் படும்.
english_translation: Blind in rage and mad in lust To have his hatred is but just.
moral: Blind rage and uncontrolled desire destroy everything — hatred is its own punishment.

title: The Trouble Merchant
theme: Might of Hatred / Buying Off the Troublemaker
hook: Rajesh's Appa pays the local troublemaker to stop causing trouble — and it works.
story: Murugan always caused problems in the market. He argued, cheated, made scenes. Rajesh's Appa was losing customers. "Appa, avanuku police la complaint pannalam." Appa shook his head. Instead, he gave Murugan a small job. "Indha kadaila veesu podu. Sampalam tharen." Murugan stopped troubling. Rajesh was confused. "Appa, avanuku job kuduthuta?" Appa smiled. "Avana etthira adichadhavida, avanuku velai koduthu serntha mathiri vaikka dhan nalla vazhi."
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Appa, avan dhan namala mosam pannitu irundhavan. Avanuku job kuduthuta?'
Appa: 'Avana othai kodutha, avan innum mosam pannuvaan. Aana avanuku velai kodutha, avan serndu poraan.'
Murugan: 'Rajesh, un Appa romba nallavan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the shop counter, weighing vegetables on an old scale.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Saththuva kita sandai podradhavida, avanai serthukradhu dhan nalla vazhi. Adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: கொடுத்தும் கொளல்வேண்டும் மன்ற அடுத்திருந்து மாணாத செய்வான் பகை.
english_translation: Pay and buy his enmity Who muddles chance with oddity.
moral: Sometimes it's better to win over a troublemaker than to fight them — even if it costs something.

title: The Empty Chair
theme: Might of Hatred / Vice Loses Friends
hook: Siva cheats, bullies, and lies — he thinks he's smart until his birthday party has zero guests.
story: Siva planned a big birthday. Cake, balloons. He invited 20 friends. The day came. He waited. And waited. No one came. His mother called parents. "Sorry, Siva romba mosam pannitaaru." Siva sat alone. Teacher came. "Siva, enna achu?" Siva cried. "Sir, yaarum varala. Naane thappu. Naan cheat pannen, lie pannen, adichiten." Teacher sat beside him. "Adhu dhan da — nalla payyan irundha, ellarum varuvaanga."
tamil_dialogues: Siva: 'Sir, yaarum varala. Ellarum enna vittutu poiduchu.'
Teacher: 'Siva, nee yaar kitta nalla irundha, avanga dhan un kitta varuvanga. Nee kettavana irundha, yaarum varamaata.'
Siva: 'Naan maari aaguren sir. Irundhum... romba late aagiduchu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the birthday table, eating a piece of cake alone.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Gunam illa na, nanbar illa. Nee yaar kitta nalla irukka, avanga dhan un kitta irukka."
thirukkural: குணனிலனாய்க் குற்றம் பலவாயின் மாற்றார்க்கு இனனிலனாம் ஏமாப் புடைத்து.
english_translation: With no virtue but full of vice He loses friends and delights foes.
moral: Without virtue and full of faults, you lose all friends and become a joy to your enemies.

title: The Easy Win
theme: Might of Hatred / Joy Against the Weak
hook: Vetri's team faces the weakest team in the tournament — but the coach refuses to let them play full strength.
story: The rival team was awful. They dropped catches, missed goals. Vetri's team laughed. "Namakku easy win dhan." Coach stopped them. "Namma full strength aadradhu thappu. Avanga mela namma poradradhu periya vellai illa." He made them play one-handed. Vetri was angry. "Sir, trophy venama?" Coach said, "Venum. Aana oru kozhappana aal kita sandai podradhu periya vellai illa."
tamil_dialogues: Vetri: 'Sir, avanga romba weak. Namma easy-a jeikalam!'
Coach: 'Adhu dhan da — avanga mela namma poradradhu periya vellai illa. Unakku sandhosham varuma?'
Vetri: 'Illa sir... santhosham illa.'
Coach: 'Adhu dhan. Jei venum, aana nalla aal kita jeika vendum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the boundary line, arms crossed, watching.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Chinna aal kita sandai podradhu periya vellai illa. Unakku sandhosham vaikadhu. Periya aal kita poradu."
thirukkural: செறுவார்க்குச் சேணிகவா இன்பம் அறிவிலா அஞ்சும் பகைவர்ப் பெறின்.
english_translation: The joy of heroes knows no bounds When timid fools are opponents.
moral: Winning against the weak brings no real joy. True victory is against a worthy opponent.

title: The Silent Boy
theme: Might of Hatred / Must Stand Up
hook: Jeeva never fights — but Appa tells him there's a difference between peace and cowardice.
story: The gang took Jeeva's money every week. He gave silently. "Enakku sandai venam." Appa noticed. "Jeeva, un kai enna achu?" Jeeva broke down. "Appa, avanga en kai la irundhu paisa eduthukuraanga." Appa's face hardened. "Nee sandai podradhu dhan vazhi illa. Aana ninnu solla theriyama irukradhu dhan thappu." Next day, Jeeva stood. "Illai. Inime enakku kuduthukamaaten." The gang laughed — but they never came again.
tamil_dialogues: Jeeva: 'Appa, naan avanga kita sandai pannenna, adhu thappu dhan?'
Appa: 'Sandai podradhu dhan vazhi illa. Aana ninnu solla theriyama irukradhu dhan kettadhu. Oru vaati ninnu sollu.'
Jeeva: 'Illai nu solren. Athu pothuma?'
Appa: 'Pothum da. Athu dhan aarambam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside Jeeva, his hand on the boy's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Peace nu sonnadhu ellam saanthama irukradhu illa. Unakku vendiya idathula ninnu solla theriyama irukradhu thappu."
thirukkural: கல்லான் வெகுளும் சிறுபொருள் எஞ்ஞான்றும் ஒல்லானை ஒல்லா தொளி.
english_translation: Glory's light he will not gain Who fails to fight a fool and win.
moral: If you never stand up for yourself, you will never earn the light of glory or self-respect.

title: The Game Gone Wrong
theme: Knowing Enmity / Even Sport Can Become Enmity
hook: A friendly game of carrom turns into a screaming match — Amma shows them how silly it is.
story: Gowri and Sandhya played carrom. A strike went wrong. "Nee adha tappu aadina!" "Illai, nee dhan!" In five minutes, they were screaming. Amma came. "Enna achu?" Both pointed fingers. Amma picked up the carrom board. "Indha aatam ungaluku sandai kudukka dhan vaanginen? Nalla irukka vechirundha, innum aadalam irundhuchu." They looked down. Sandhya started laughing. Gowri laughed too. They hugged.
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Amma, ava tappu aadina, naan correct pannen!'
Sandhya: 'Nee dhan tappu! Unaku sandai dhan venum!'
Amma: 'Rendu perukum sandai dhan venumna, aattam vittutu sandai podu. Illa, nalla aadi sandhosama iru.'
Gowri: 'Sorry Sandhya. Aadalaam?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the carrom board, flicking the striker perfectly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vilayaaduvadhu dhan nalladhu. Aana adhe vilayattu dhan sandai aakidradhu thappu. Nalla iru, sandai venam."
thirukkural: பகைஎன்னும் பண்பி லதனை ஒருவன் நகையேயும் வேண்டற்பாற்று அன்று.
english_translation: Let not one even as a sport The ill-natured enmity court.
moral: Don't turn even a game into a fight — enmity should never be courted, not even for fun.

title: Words That Bleed
hook: Prem makes fun of a quiet girl's speech — but her one sentence reply makes the whole class go silent.
story: New girl Malini stammered during her oration. The class giggled. Prem mimicked her. "Pa... pa... pa..." Malini stopped. She looked at Prem. Calmly she said, "Un adutha varthai dhan. Aana adhu unna vida periya aayudham." The class went dead silent. Prem's face burned. After class, Divya scolded him. "Nee sonna adutha word la adichita. Sollala kooda periya aayudham illa da."
tamil_dialogues: Prem: 'Akka, ava oru varthai la enna adichita. Full class shock.'
Divya: 'Adhu dhan — words dhan periya aayudham. Kai vechadhavida solla vecha dhan periya vali.'
Malini: 'Nee enna fun pannalam. Aana unna vida venumna, solla dhan periya aayudham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school podium, a single palm leaf in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kai vechu aduradhu periya aayudham illa. Solla vechu aduravan dhan romba dangerous. Vaarthai dhan periya aayudham."
thirukkural: வில்லேர் உழவர் பகைகொளினும் கொள்ளற்க சொல்லேர் உழவர் பகை.
english_translation: Incur the hate of bow-ploughers But not the hate of word-ploughers.
moral: Better to face a physical fighter than a master of words — words cut deeper and last longer.

title: The Lonely Wolf
theme: Knowing Enmity / Making Many Foes Is Folly
hook: Mahesh picks fights with everyone — Amma warns him, but he doesn't listen until he has no one left.
story: Mahesh fought everyone. Classmates, neighbours, even the tea shop uncle. "Enakku yaarum bayama illa." Amma warned. "Mahesh, nee ellar kooda sandai podra. Oru naal yaarum illa." He laughed. Then his birthday came. He invited the whole class. Only two came — and they left early. Mahesh sat alone. Amma sat beside him. "Paatha? Palla per kooda sandai panna, nee thaniya aiduva."
tamil_dialogues: Mahesh: 'Amma, avan unna aduppavana vida periya aal! Adha dhan...'
Amma: 'Mahesh, nee romba periya aal mathiri sandai podra. Aana ore vaati yaarum illa na, nee thaniya aiduva.'
Mahesh: 'Ippo dhan puriyudhu Amma... naan dhan thappu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the empty birthday table, a single candle burning.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Palla per kooda sandai podradhu unna thaniya aakum. Adhu dhan periya kettadhu."
thirukkural: ஏமுற் றவரினும் ஏழை தமியனாய்ப் பல்லார் பகைகொள் பவன்.
english_translation: Forlorn, who rouses many foes The worst insanity betrays.
moral: Making enemies of everyone is the worst kind of foolishness — it leaves you completely alone.

title: The Enemy Ally
theme: Knowing Enmity / Turn Enemies Into Friends
hook: Kathir's rival Arul always fights him — until they're forced to share an umbrella in the rain.
story: Kathir and Arul hated each other. Every day, a fight. One day, the rain poured. Both waited under the same bus stop shelter. Then a bigger bully came and pushed both. "Poi da." For the first time, Kathir and Arul looked at each other — and together, they pushed back. The bully ran. They stood in the rain, laughing. Teacher saw. "Paatha? Sathruvum nanban aagalaam."
tamil_dialogues: Kathir: 'Arul, avan rendu peraiyum othai kuduthan!'
Arul: 'Ama da. Ippo naan un kooda nikkaren.'
Kathir: 'Nee en sathru dhan!'
Arul: 'Illa da. Naalai irundhu nanban.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the rain, holding an umbrella over both boys.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathruvum nanban aagalaam. Unakku vendiyadhu avanai serthukradhu dhan. Adhu dhan periya thiran."
thirukkural: பகைநட்பாக் கொண்டொழுகும் பண்புடை யாளன் தகைமைக்கண் தங்கிற்று உலகு.
english_translation: This world goes safely in his grace Whose heart makes friends even of foes.
moral: The person who can turn enemies into friends carries the world's respect and safety.

title: The Triangle War
theme: Knowing Enmity / Ally One Enemy Against Another
hook: Sekar is caught between two warring friends — Thatha shows him how to break the deadlock.
story: Sekar's two best friends, Velu and Mani, now hated each other. Sekar was stuck in the middle. "Nee yaar kooda?" Both demanded. Thatha saw Sekar stressed. "Enna da achu?" Sekar explained. Thatha said, "Oru thadava, Velu kita sollu, naan Mani kooda dhan. Mani kita sollu, naan Velu kooda dhan. Appo rendu perum unna thedi varuvaanga." Sekar tried it. Both came to him, wanting his side. They ended up talking — through him.
tamil_dialogues: Sekar: 'Thatha, rendu perum enna valaiyila iluthukittu irukkaanga.'
Thatha: 'Unakku rendu perum venumna, avanga rendu perukum nee venum nu kaatu. Athu dhan vazhi.'
Velu: 'Sekar, nee yaar kooda?'
Sekar: 'Rendu per kooda dhan. Nee enna pannuva?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple car, watching the three boys figure it out.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Rendu per sathru aa irundha, onna serthu vidu. Adhu dhan nalla vazhi. Unakku rendum venumna, rendaiyum serthu vaika."
thirukkural: தன்துணை இன்றால் பகையிரண்டால் தான்ஒருவன் இன்துணையாக் கொள்கவற்றின் ஒன்று.
english_translation: Alone, if two foes you oppose Make one of them your ally close.
moral: When you face two enemies alone, turn one into a friend — divide and conquer.

title: The Fake Friend
theme: Knowing Enmity / Keep Distance From Foes in Trouble
hook: The neighbour who always fought with Amma suddenly becomes sweet — Deepa is confused but Amma stays cautious.
story: Neighbour Sarala always fought with Amma. Boundary disputes, loud arguments. Then one day, she came with sweets. "Naama nalla irukalam." Deepa was happy. "Amma, paaru! Avanga ipdi varanga!" But Amma was careful. "Santhosham dhan. Aana konja naal paathutu iru." That week, Sarala asked for money. Amma politely refused. Sarala went back to fighting. Deepa understood: enemies don't change overnight.
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Amma, avanga nalla irukka varanga. Enna doubt?'
Amma: 'Santhosham dhan. Aana kaalam therinjuttu. Ippo namma nalla irukkom — avanga yenna venum?'
Sarala: 'Kadan venum. Kuduthuta?'
Amma: 'Illanga. Nalla iruklam, aana kadan vendam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the colony gate, watching the two women with a knowing look.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru nalla irukka varum podhu, sandhosha padalam. Aana jaggada irukanum. Avan unna enna ninaikiran nu therinjutte iru."
thirukkural: தேறினும் தேறா விடினும் அழிவின்கண் தேறான் பகாஅன் விடல்.
english_translation: Trust or distrust; during distress Keep aloof; don't mix with foes.
moral: Whether you trust or not, in times of trouble keep your distance from known enemies.

title: The Hidden Tear
theme: Knowing Enmity / Don't Show Weakness to Foes
hook: Saravanan's father died — and the bully uses it against him. Appa's ghost teaches him one last lesson.
story: Saravanan's Appa passed away. He was broken. The bully, Kutti, taunted him. "Appa illa payyan. Unna yaarum kaapadra maata." Saravanan wanted to cry — but he remembered Appa's words. "Sathru kita un vulnurability kaaturadhu thappu." He held his tears. He stood tall. "En Appa illa. Aana naan nallavan. Nee enna sonna enakku valikala." The bully was shocked. He walked away.
tamil_dialogues: Kutti: 'Appa illa payyan. Unna yaarum kaapadra maata.'
Saravanan: 'En Appa illa dhan. Aana athu enakku weakness illa. Adhu en strength.'
Kutti: 'Unakku valikala?'
Saravanan: 'Valikkum. Aana un kita kaatamaaten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside Saravanan, in the shape of his father, one hand on his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru kita un bayatha kaaturadhu thappu. Avan atha paathu unna adippan. Nee balama iru."
thirukkural: நோவற்க நொந்தது அறியார்க்கு மேவற்க மென்மை பகைவர் அகத்து.
english_translation: To those who know not, tell not your pain Nor your weakness to foes explain.
moral: Never show your weakness to an enemy — they will use it against you. Be strong.

title: The Fort Builder
hook: Raja and Selvam build a secret fort against the senior gang — but discover the best defence is unity.
story: Senior gang demanded "rent" from all juniors. "Ellam sernthu oru fort kattuvom." Raja and Selvam built a treehouse fort. They stored stones, water. Two weeks, they prepared. The gang came. "Kuduthuta!" Raja stood. "Illai." The gang laughed — but then all the juniors came and stood together. 30 kids against 5 seniors. The seniors ran. Raja learned: defence is knowing, acting, and uniting.
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Raja, avanga romba periya aal. Namma enna pannuvom?'
Raja: 'First, namma strength teriyanum. Avanga weakness teriyanum. Appo plan poduvom.'
Raja: 'Ellarum onna nillunga. Avanga namala onnum panna mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the treehouse, looking down at the united children.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru varum podhu, bayapdala. Therinja vazhi sollu. Kai kappu. Athu dhan periya kappu."
thirukkural: வகையறிந்து தற்செய்து தற்காப்ப மாயும் பகைவர்கண் பட்ட செருக்கு.
english_translation: Know how and act and defend well The pride of enemies shall fall.
moral: Understand the situation, prepare yourself, defend wisely — the enemy's pride will crumble.

title: The Thorn Seed
hook: Rohini ignores small teasing from a neighbour boy — but Appa shows her the thorn tree parable.
story: Neighbour boy Kajha started small. Hid her chappal. Laughed. "Adhu onnum illa." Rohini ignored. Weeks later, he broke her cycle. Then stole her project. Rohini cried. Appa took her outside. A thorn bush had grown from a small seed. "Nee adha mudhalla kaatha irundha, adhu siriya illa? Ippo perusa aaiduchu." Appa said. "Problems lam adhe maadhiri dhan. Siriya irukapodha kaathuko."
tamil_dialogues: Rohini: 'Appa, avan mudhalla chappal matum dhan maraichan. Ippo project eduthuta.'
Appa: 'Indha mulla chedi paaru. Siriya irundhapodha kaatha irundha, ippo perusaa irundhurukkaadhu.'
Appa: 'Sathruvum adhe maadhiri. Siriya irukapodha dhan kaathukanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the thorn bush, carefully cutting it with a sickle.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru siriya irukapodha dhan kaathukanum. Perusa aana pram, kaathukradhu kastam. Mudhala iruntha kaatha."
thirukkural: இளைதாக முள்மரம் கொல்க களையுநர் கைகொல்லும் காழ்த்த இடத்து.
english_translation: Cut off thorn-trees when young they are; Grown hard, they cut your hands beware.
moral: Cut off problems and enmity when they are small — if they grow, they will hurt you.

title: The Defeated Champion
theme: Knowing Enmity / Must Defeat or Be Defeated
hook: Muthu is terrified of the champion boxer — but the coach says the only way out is through.
story: Muthu's first boxing match was against Rajan — undefeated champion. "Enakku bayama irukku." Coach looked at him. "Muthu, nee avana jeichu aaganum. Illa na, avane unna jeichu aaga veppan. Adhu dhan boxing. Athu dhan life." Muthu stepped into the ring. First round, he got hit. Second round, he got up. Third round, he landed one punch — and another. He didn't win the match. But he didn't run. The crowd cheered. Coach hugged him.
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Sir, avana enna mudiyum? Avan champion!'
Coach: 'Mudiyum illa nu nee ninaikra, adhu dhan un defeat. Ring la ninnu poradu.'
Muthu: 'Naan odamaaten. Naan nikkuren.'
Coach: 'Adhu dhan da vellum. Nee nindra — adhu dhan jei.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the ringside, wiping Muthu's sweat.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru kita bayapdala. Avana ethirthu nillanum. Oda ponal, nee dhan thota. Ninnu poradha, athu dhan jei."
thirukkural: உயிர்ப்ப உளரல்லர் மன்ற செயிர்ப்பவர் செம்மல் சிதைக்கலா தார்.
english_translation: To breathe on earth they are not fit Defying foes who don't defeat.
moral: Those who cannot face and overcome their enemies have no place in this world — you must stand and fight.

title: Paati's Broken Pot
hook: Pooja's cousin pours dirty water into their well — and Pooja can't understand why family would hurt family.
story: Pooja loved when Murali anna came to visit. But one day she saw him empty a bucket of soapy water into Paati's well. "Anna, enna pannra?" He shushed her. That night, Paati's stomach hurt from the water. Pooja cried. "Paati, anna dhan panninaar!" Paati closed her eyes. "Sontham dhan da, aana manasula kaadhal illama irundha, nelalum neerum kooda kedu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Paati, anna namakku dhan sontham illa? En anga?'
Paati: 'Sontham endral kaadhal varanum da. Illana nizhal mathiri — irundhum illadha maadhiri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the well, dipping a single pot into clean water.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kinsmen dhan, aana manasula bayam irundha, adhu nizhal maadhiri — irundhum kedaiyaadhu. Nee paadham karkka koodadhu."
thirukkural: நிழல்நீரும் இன்னாத இன்னா தமர்நீரும் இன்னாவாம் இன்னா செயின்.
english_translation: Traitorous kinsmen will make you sad as water and shade do harm when bad.
moral: A hurtful relative is worse than poison — even shade and water can harm when meant to.

title: The Smiling Snake
hook: Dhruv's uncle hugs him warmly but Appa warns — the tightest hug can hide the sharpest knife.
story: Uncle Ramesh came from Chennai with chocolates. "Vaa da Dhruv, unakku romba pudikkum!" He hugged Dhruv tight. Later, Appa saw Uncle measuring the land boundary stones. "Appa, chithappa nalla dhan irukkar!" Appa held Dhruv's shoulder. "Kathi maadhiri irukkum da sontham. Aana kathi kooda theriyudhu yaarudhadhu nu. Sotta sontham theriyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Dhruv: 'Appa, chithappakku namma mela kaadhal dhan illa?'
Appa: 'Kaadhal maadhiri irukkum da. Aana ullae bayam. Kathi mathiri bayapada vendiya sontham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the boundary stones, one hand raised in warning.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kathi maadhiri irukkira sontham — adhai vida, katti irukkira sontham dhan periya bayam. Adhu enna seyyum nu theriyadhu."
thirukkural: வாள்போல பகைவரை அஞ்சற்க அஞ்சுக கேள்போல் பகைவர் தொடர்பு.
english_translation: You need not sword-like kinsmen fear — fear foes who feign as kinsmen dear.
moral: A sword in plain sight is less dangerous than a knife hidden in a brother's smile.

title: The Potter's Secret
hook: Karthik's best friend since kindergarten has been secretly breaking his clay pots every night.
story: Every morning, one of Karthik's pots was cracked. He thought it was the cat. Thatha watched silently. One night, they hid. Karthik's own friend, Selvam, crept in and smashed a pot with a stone. "Selvam! Neeyaa?" Selvam ran. Karthik wept. "Thatha, avan dhan." Thatha held him. "Ullae irukkira bagai dhan kashtam da. Solli theriyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Thatha, avan en friend dhan! En ipdi pannuraan?'
Thatha: 'Friend illada. Ullae bagai. Adhu dhan potter ooda mannu maadhiri — ullae irundhu varudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the potter's wheel, a single cracked pot in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulbakai dhan periya aayudham. Adhu ullae irundhu dhan varum. Nee bayapada vendiyadhu adhu dhan."
thirukkural: உட்பகை அஞ்சித்தற் காக்க உலைவிடத்து மட்பகையின் மாணத் தெறும்.
english_translation: The secret foe in days evil will cut you, beware, like potters' steel.
moral: Hidden enemies among friends cut deeper than any open sword — guard yourself.

title: The Gossip Well
hook: Janani hears her own aunt spreading lies about her mother — and the entire lane believes it.
story: Janani was fetching water when she heard Athai's voice. "Avala nambidadha, ava dhan thappu." Janani's pot slipped. "Amma, athai unna pathi mosama solra!" Amma didn't flinch. "Theriyum da. Aval manasu sari illa. Adhu dhan avala kedukudhu." Janani hugged her mother tight. "Amma, ella sonthamum apdi dhan illa?"
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Amma, athai namma veetula nalla dhan varra. Aana?'
Amma: 'Naa irukka dhan da. Ullae bagai irundha, adhu sonthathayum kedukkum. Avanuku dhan kedu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the well, a broken pot at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manasula bagai vantha, adhu sontha bandhamum keduthudum. Avaruku bayam dhan. Un ammaki kaadhal."
thirukkural: மனமாணா உட்பகை தோன்றின் இனமாணா ஏதம் பலவும் தரும்.
english_translation: The evil-minded foe within foments trouble, spoils kinsmen!
moral: A poisoned mind destroys not just the person but the entire family.

title: The Broken Swing
hook: Mani's older cousin loosens the swing rope — and Mani falls hard, breaking his arm.
story: The swing was Mani's favorite. Every evening Appa pushed him. But cousin Guna had loosened the knot. Mid-swing, the rope snapped. Mani hit the ground. His arm bent wrong. At the hospital, Guna stood crying. "Appa, guna anna dhan seidhaan." Appa's face turned dark. "Pona janmathula bagai. Ithu dhan uyira kedukkum."
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Appa, anna en life-a keduka pathaan.'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan ulbagai da. Uyira kedukkum. Nee jamaika iru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the broken swing, holding the snapped rope.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulbakai uyirukku aapathu. Sonthama irundhu bagai pannradhu uyira kedukum. Nee pathadhukkaga."
thirukkural: உறல்முறையான் உட்பகை தோன்றின் இறல்முறையான் ஏதம் பலவும் தரும்.
english_translation: A traitor among kinsmen will bring life-endangering evil.
moral: Hidden enmity in a relative can cost you your life — never ignore the signs.

title: The Split Kingdom
hook: Srini's two favorite teachers stop speaking to each other — and the whole school suffers.
story: Mrs. Geetha and Mr. Rajan were the best teachers. Then a petty fight over exam duty. They stopped talking. Students took sides. The school split. Srini's class failed the test. "Sir, neengala rendu perum pesa maatengala. Nanga dhan kashtapadrom." The Headmaster sighed. "Ullae ottumai illana, bayam dhan baaki."
tamil_dialogues: Srini: 'Sir, ungalala dhan nanga fail aachom.'
Headmaster: 'Ottumai irundha dhan school irukkum. Illana kedum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between two empty desks, one hand on each.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ondramai illadhadhu dhan periya kedu. Rendum ore idathula iruntha — aana ottumai illama dhan iruntha — adhu periya aapathu."
thirukkural: ஒன்றாமை ஒன்றியார் கட்படின் எஞ்ஞான்றும் பொன்றாமை ஒன்றல் அரிது.
english_translation: Discord in kings' circle entails life-destroying deadly evils.
moral: When leaders are divided, everyone under them suffers. Unity is survival.

title: The Lid That Lies
hook: Anjali's family seems happy together but Paati shows her a pot with a lid that doesn't fit.
story: Everyone smiled at the family dinner. Uncle served food. Aunt laughed. But Paati whispered, "Paaru, adha." The pot lid sat crooked — it didn't match the vessel. "Paati, adhu enna?" Paati said, "Namma veetula ellam sari maadhiri irukku, aana ullae ottumai illa. Adhu dhan idhu — mella potta mathiri, aana ullae otta irukku."
tamil_dialogues: Anjali: 'Paati, namma veetula ellam sari dhan pole irukku?'
Paati: 'Illada. Mella potta mathiri dhan. Ullae vettu. Paaru paanam mella vaithu potta maadhiri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the kitchen table, placing the lid perfectly on its pot.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sembu potta mathiri veliya sariya irukku, aana ullae ottumai irukka. Veliya mattum pothaadhu — ullae sari irukka vendum."
thirukkural: செப்பின் புணர்ச்சிபோல் கூடினும் கூடாதே உட்பகை உற்ற குடி.
english_translation: A house hiding hostiles in core just seems on like the lid in jar.
moral: A family that seems united but hides hatred is like a jar with a mismatched lid.

title: The Crumbling Gold
hook: Meena watches her uncle file gold into dust — and understands how her family is thinning away.
story: Uncle held a gold chain against the file. Shiny dust fell. "Uncle, gold soft dhan pole!" Uncle nodded. "Rombha soft. File adicha, dhusu dhusu nu poradhu." Meena thought about her family. Every week, a new fight. Every month, someone left. "Uncle, namma family um apdi dhan irukku. Poradhu, poradhu, karaichu podhu." Uncle stopped filing.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Uncle, gold apdi poradhu. Namma veetula um adhu dhan nadakudhu.'
Uncle: 'Ama da. Ulbagai dhan file. Solli solli karai kidudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the goldsmith's anvil, holding the gold dust in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ullae bagai irundha, adhu gold ah file mathiri arichu podum. Solli solli dhan kudumbam kedum."
thirukkural: அரம்பொருத பொன்போலத் தேயும் உரம்பொருது உட்பகை உற்ற குடி.
english_translation: By secret spite the house wears out like gold crumbling by file's contact.
moral: Internal hatred erodes a family silently, like a file wearing down gold.

title: The Sesame Slit
hook: Balu thinks a tiny slit in a sesame seed is nothing — until Amma shows him the whole bag is ruined.
story: Balu saw a small black dot on a sesame seed. "Amma, ithu onnum illa." Amma held up the bag. "Paaru, idhu ellam kettupochu." Every seed had the same tiny slit — weevils had entered and hollowed them all. Balu's eyes widened. "Sesame grew big, aana ullae kuppai." Amma said, "Adhu dhan ulbagai. Chinna dhan, aana kedu periyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Balu: 'Amma, chinna vettu dhan. Onnum perusa illa?'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan bayam da. Chinna dhan, aana motham kedukum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the kitchen counter, a single sesame seed between his fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ellu vettu maadhiri dhan. Chinna bagai, aana periya kedu. Ulbagaiyum adhu maadhiri dhan."
thirukkural: எட்பக வன்ன சிறுமைத்தே ஆயினும் உட்பகை உள்ளதாங் கேடு.
english_translation: Ruin lurks in enmity as slit in sesame though it be.
moral: Even a tiny crack of hatred can ruin everything — like a weevil in a sesame seed.

title: The Snake in the Hut
hook: Vilasini's family shares a house with a relative who secretly steals — and Appa says it's like living with a snake.
story: Vilasini's Periyappa lived with them. He was kind, but things kept disappearing. Amma's earrings. Appa's watch. Vilasini's piggy bank. One night, she saw him take the cash box. "Appa, periyappa dhan!" Appa didn't seem surprised. "Theriyum da. Aana enna pannradhu. Avaroda vaazradhu, paamboda ore kudanga irukradhu maadhiri dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Vilasini: 'Appa, avar nammaloda dhan irukkar, aana donga!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan kastam da. Kudanga ullae paambu — irukku dhan, aana jamaika dhan irukka mudiyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands outside the old home, holding a lamp, a snake slithering away from the door.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ullae bagai irundha, adhu uyira kedukkum. Kudanga ullae paambu maadhiri. Veliya dhan vazhi."
thirukkural: உடம்பாடு இலாதவர் வாழ்க்கை குடங்கருள் பாம்போடு உடனுறைந் தற்று.
english_translation: Dwell with traitors that hate in heart is dwelling with snake in selfsame hut.
moral: Living with someone who hates you inside is like sharing a pot with a cobra.

title: The Bully's Comeback
theme: Not Offending the Great / Respecting Strength
hook: Vinoth mocks the school's strongest bowler — and gets a lesson in respecting power.
story: Ganesh was the fastest bowler in school. Vinoth laughed when he missed a catch. "Enna bowling da! Naa kooda nalla poduvane!" Ganesh said nothing. Next game, Ganesh bowled a bouncer. Vinoth ducked, fell. The ball hit his shoulder. Ganesh helped him up. "Nee sanda vedam. Aana un mayiruku dhan kashtam." Vinoth never mocked anyone again.
tamil_dialogues: Vinoth: 'Ganesh, nee perusa onnum illa da!'
Ganesh: 'Sari da. Aana nalla irukradhu dhan nandri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the cricket pitch, ball in hand, watching calmly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Valimai irukaravara igazha koodadhu. Avanga porumai dhan periya balam. Nee adha purinjukka vendum."
thirukkural: ஆற்றுவார் ஆற்றல் இகழாமை போற்றுவார் போற்றலுள் எல்லாம் தலை.
english_translation: Not to spite the mighty ones — safest safeguard to living brings.
moral: Never mock someone with power — respect is the safest protection you can have.

title: The Silent Principal
theme: Not Offending the Great / Avoiding Trouble
hook: Kanna ignores the principal's warning about skipping class — and lands in serious trouble.
story: The principal saw Kanna sneaking out. "Maga, pOna vazhiyE pOda vendam." Kanna laughed. "Avar enna pannuvaar?" Next week, police came. Kanna and Babu were caught near the bus stand. The principal stood at the gate. "SonnEn da." Kanna cried. "Sir, kekkaama pOitten." The principal let them back, but Kanna never forgot: great people's words are warnings.
tamil_dialogues: Kanna: 'Babu, principal sir namma enna pannuvaaru?'
Babu: 'Avar dhan big. Nee solli kettu dhan pannanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, holding a cane, not to hit but to guide.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periyavanga solradhu dhan sari. Nee kekkama pOna, kashtam dhan varum. Adhu dhan avanga solradhu."
thirukkural: பெரியாரைப் பேணாது ஒழுகிற் பெரியாரால் பேரா இடும்பை தரும்.
english_translation: To walk unmindful of the great shall great troubles ceaseless create.
moral: Ignoring the wise brings endless trouble — their words are your shield.

title: The Broken Fence
theme: Not Offending the Great / Ruin Through Disrespect
hook: Suresh throws stones at the neighbor's coconut trees on a dare — and the neighbor turns out to be the village chief.
story: "Intha marathula kai thattuda, nee veera dhan!" Suresh threw a stone. A coconut fell. The owner came out — it was the village chief, Kumar. "Nee dhan da?" Suresh froze. Uncle Kumar didn't shout. "Nee avan vEra, naan vEra nu ninaika vendam. Aana intha marathula ninnu kai thattuna, ninnu ninaichi dhan." He walked away. Suresh never threw a stone again.
tamil_dialogues: Friend: 'Adichu da! Bayama irukka?'
Suresh: 'Avalo periya aalu. Naa avan kai thatta koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands under the coconut tree, one fallen coconut in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Evano periyavarunu theriyadha edathula kai thattina, adhu kedu. Periyavangala kanam."
thirukkural: கெடல்வேண்டின் கேளாது செய்க அடல்வேண்டின் ஆற்று பவர்கண் இழுக்கு.
english_translation: Heed not and do, if ruin you want — offence against the mighty great.
moral: If you want to ruin yourself, offend someone powerful. Respect prevents downfall.

title: Death by Invitation
hook: Raja sees a tiny dog bark at a sleeping elephant — and understands what happens when the weak provoke the strong.
story: A tiny stray dog stood before a sleeping temple elephant. It barked, snapped. "Amma, adhu paaru! Nai dhan!" Amma grabbed his hand. The elephant's eye opened. Slowly, it stood. The dog kept barking. The elephant just walked away. "Amma, yaanai odiduchu!" Amma said, "Yaanai odidha, aana nai ah konna irukalam. Athu dhan periyavar — porumai."
tamil_dialogues: Raja: 'Amma, nai ah yaanai munnadi kathudhu!'
Amma: 'Yaanai adha konna irukkum. Aana porumaiya vittudhu. Nee periyavangala mathiri iru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple steps, the elephant standing behind him peacefully.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periyavar munnadi seri illama pesradhu, saavadhai anai mathiri. Avanga porumai dhan unakku kaaval."
thirukkural: கூற்றத்தைக் கையால் விளித்தற்றால் ஆற்றுவார்க்கு ஆற்றாதார் இன்னா செயல்.
english_translation: The weak who insult men of might death with their own hands invite.
moral: Insulting someone stronger is like inviting death yourself — respect saves.

title: The Exiled Boy
theme: Not Offending the Great / No Escape
hook: Gowtham insults the village elder and runs away — but finds no one will take him in.
story: Gowtham called the village elder "pOna kizhavan" at the temple. The elder said nothing. Gowtham's Appa was furious. "Nee inga irukka koodadhu." Gowtham ran to the next village. But nobody gave him water. "Neeya, enga oorula kizhavana kai thattina paiyana?" He walked back, defeated. Appa was waiting. "Yenga pOna nee? Periyavana vida vEra idam illa da."
tamil_dialogues: Shopkeeper: 'Nee dhan antha paiyana? Ingayum unaku idam illa.'
Gowtham: 'Enna pannradhu? Yengalum pOgalaam?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the village border, a staff blocking the path.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periyavana kai thattina, pOga idam kedaiyaadhu. Enga pOnalum adhu dhan varum. Manichi kettu dhan aaganum."
thirukkural: யாண்டுச்சென்று யாண்டும் உளராகார் வெந்துப்பின் வேந்து செறப்பட் டவர்.
english_translation: Where can they go and thrive where pursued by powerful monarch's ire?
moral: When you offend the great, no place is safe — there's no escape from their reach.

title: The Fire Escape
theme: Not Offending the Great / Inescapable Consequence
hook: Chitra watches a man jump unhurt from a fire — and learns that some burns are not from flames.
story: At the market, a fire dancer jumped through flames. People clapped. He wasn't burned. "Thatha, avarukku enna aagala?" Thatha said, "Neruppu dhan — aana avan adha therinjavan." A rich man pushed through and insulted the dancer. The dancer smiled. Within a week, the rich man's business closed. Everyone knew. "Thatha, avan neruppula dhan irundhaan?" Thatha nodded.
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'Thatha, avan neruppulayum bayapadala?'
Thatha: 'Neruppula irundhu pOnaalam. Aana periyavar kai vittu pOga mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the fire, untouched, a single flame on his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neruppula irundhu pOna mudiyum. Aana periyavara vittu pOga mudiyadhu. Adhu dhan sattam."
thirukkural: எரியால் சுடப்படினும் உய்வுண்டாம் உய்யார் பெரியார்ப் பிழைத்தொழுகு வார்.
english_translation: One can escape in fire caught — the great who offends escapes not.
moral: You can survive fire, but not the consequences of offending the mighty.

title: The Sage's Shadow
theme: Not Offending the Great / Loss Through Offence
hook: Divya's rich uncle boasts about his wealth in front of a sage — and his money vanishes overnight.
story: Uncle Kumar was the richest man in Chidambaram. He saw a sage in torn clothes. "Paaru, ivaruku enna irukku? Enakku akkarai veedu, car, ellam irukku." The sage smiled. That night, Uncle's shop caught fire. Everything burned. "Amma, uncle dhan periya aalu, aana?" Amma held her. "Periyavar munadi perumai panna koodadhu. Adhu dhan kedu."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Amma, uncle romba periya aalu maadhiri irundhaaru.'
Amma: 'Periya aalu avaru amma. Namma uncle dhan perumaiya irundhaaru. Adhu dhan kedu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits with the sage, both in simple white, under the temple tree.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periyavargal munnadi perumai koodadhu. Avanga aatchi irukku. Nee adha purinjula, kedu dhan."
thirukkural: வகைமாண்ட வாழ்க்கையும் வான்பொருளும் என்னாம் தகைமாண்ட தக்கார் செறின்.
english_translation: If holy mighty sages frown, stately gifts and stores who can own?
moral: When you offend the wise, no amount of wealth can save you — humility is the only treasure.

title: The Mountain Mouse
theme: Not Offending the Great / The Weight of Disrespect
hook: Velu calls the school's oldest teacher "koondu" — and the whole village turns against his family.
story: Teacher Muthukrishnan was 60, with a bent back. Velu shouted, "Koondu sir, blackboard enna? nalla theriyala!" The class laughed. Teacher didn't react. But by evening, every shop in Ooty refused to serve Velu's mother. "Un magan nalla paadam karkka vendum." Velu cried. "Sir, enna mannichudu." Teacher said, "Mala saadharanam. Aana adha igazhvadhu dhan kedu."
tamil_dialogues: Velu: 'Sir, naan thappu pannitten.'
Teacher: 'Mala igazhadha da. Adhu periyadhu. Nee adha karkka vendum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on a hill in Ooty, the mist swirling around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mala maadhiri irukaravana igazha koodadhu. Adhu dhan kedu. Chinna aalu periya aalu illa — maratthai igazhadha."
thirukkural: குன்றன்னார் குன்ற மதிப்பின் குடியொடு நின்றன்னார் மாய்வர் நிலத்து.
english_translation: When hill-like sages are held small, the firm on earth lose home and all.
moral: Disrespecting someone as steady as a mountain will bring down your whole world.

title: The King's Fall
theme: Not Offending the Great / Power of the Wise
hook: Murugan hears that a sage cursed a king and the kingdom fell — and realizes words have power.
story: An old story, but Murugan's Appa told it on the palace street. "Oru naal, raja oru munivar kitta sirichaan. Munivar enna sollaama poittaan. Rathiri, raja veedu keduchudhu." Murugan shivered. "Appa, munivar enna seidhaar?" Appa lifted a stone. "Onnum seiyala. Avan sirikkradhu dhan avan kedu. Periyavanga maunam dhan aayudham."
tamil_dialogues: Murugan: 'Appa, avarapdi dhaana keduka mudiyum?'
Appa: 'Mudiyum da. Periyavanga varthaiku balam irukku. Adha igazha koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands where the palace once stood, now a flower garden.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periyavar seeradhu dhan aayudham. Nee adha igazha koodadhu. Raja aanalum, periyavar munadi kedum."
thirukkural: ஏந்திய கொள்கையார் சீறின் இடைமுரிந்து வேந்தனும் வேந்து கெடும்.
english_translation: Before the holy sage's rage, e'en Indra's empire meets damage.
moral: Even a king can fall if he offends the wise — power can't protect you from consequences.

title: The Mighty Fall
theme: Not Offending the Great / Even the Strong Can't Escape
hook: Pandian's friend boasts his father is the most powerful man — until he insults the temple priest.
story: Pandian's friend Selvan bragged. "En Appa dhan inga periya aalu. Yaarum avan mathiri illa." At the temple, Selvan pushed the priest aside. "Oda da." The priest said nothing. Within a month, Selvan's Appa lost the election, his business closed, his friends left. "Thatha, avar appa romba periya aalu dhan!" Thatha said, "Periyavar munadi yarum periya aalu illa da."
tamil_dialogues: Selvan: 'En Appa dhan periya aalu! Inga yaarum illa.'
Thatha: 'Periyavar munadi, appa kooda small dhan paiya.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the broken fort wall, a crow on his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periya aalu nu ninaichu, periyavara igazha koodadhu. Unakku support irundhalum, adhu kedum."
thirukkural: இறந்தமைந்த சார்புடையர் ஆயினும் உய்யார் சிறந்தமைந்த சீரார் செறின்.
english_translation: Even mighty aided men shall quail if the enraged holy seers will.
moral: No amount of power or connections can save you when you offend the truly great.

title: Appa's Lost Lorry
theme: Being Led by Women / Wasting Ambition
hook: Sekar's father had a chance to buy a lorry but his wife said no — now he's a helper in someone else's lorry.
story: Five years ago, Appa had saved enough for a lorry. Amma said, "Vendaam. Kashtam. Bayama irukku." Appa listened. Now he loaded other people's lorries. "Appa, unakku lorry vaanga dhan aasai." Appa wiped his face. "Amma solla kettu dhan nadandhen da. Naa solla vendiyadhu." Sekar hugged him. "Appa, un aasai dhan mukiyam."
tamil_dialogues: Sekar: 'Appa, amma solla kettu dhan lorry vaangala?'
Appa: 'Ama da. Ava solla kettu. Ippo matha aalu lorry la velai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside a parked lorry, looking at the open road.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sollu ketkradhu thappu illa. Aana un aasaiya vida koodadhu. Payam dhan un vazhiya adukkum."
thirukkural: மனைவிழைவார் மாண்பயன் எய்தார் வினைவிழையார் வேண்டாப் பொருளும் அது.
english_translation: Who dote on wives lose mighty gain that lust, dynamic men disdain.
moral: Letting fear in someone else's voice bury your dream is the surest way to lose it.

title: The Business That Closed
theme: Being Led by Women / Shame from Weakness
hook: Mahesh's uncle had a thriving garment factory — until his wife made him fire all the workers.
story: Uncle's factory employed 50 women. Business was good. Then Aunty got jealous. "Enna da antha ponnunga? Unnala mattum dhan?" Uncle fired them all. Machines stopped. Orders cancelled. "Ravi, en periyappa dhan big. Aana aunty solla kettaaru." Ravi said, "Theriyum da. Namma veetlayum adhu dhan. Periya aalu maadhiri, aana manasukku balam illa."
tamil_dialogues: Mahesh: 'Ravi, en periyappa periya aalu dhan!'
Ravi: 'Ama, aana avanuku manasu balam illa. Adhu dhan nana.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the silent factory gate, a single thread in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ponnunga sGnama irundha, athu nana. Aana avan solla kettu dhan velai pogum. Adhu dhan kedu."
thirukkural: பேணாது பெண்விழைவான் ஆக்கம் பெரியதோர் நாணாக நாணுத் தரும்.
english_translation: Who dotes, unmanly, on his dame — his wealth to him and all is shame.
moral: Letting someone control your decisions through jealousy brings shame, not success.

title: The Tailor's Secret
theme: Being Led by Women / Loss of Dignity
hook: Lalitha sees the famous tailor Raman bow to his wife's every demand — and loses respect for him.
story: Tailor Raman made the best silk blouses. Everyone respected him. But when his wife came, "Raman, coffee kondu vaa!" He ran. "Raman, ithu sari illa!" He changed it. After she left, he sighed. Lalitha whispered, "Amma, avaruku enna? Periya tailor dhan." Amma said, "Veliya periya aalu. Aana manasila siriya aalu. Avare therinjupaan."
tamil_dialogues: Lalitha: 'Amma, avaruku bayama irukku maadhiri!'
Amma: 'Ama da. Avaluku bayapadradhu dhan avaroda periya velai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the sewing machine, stitching a straight line.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manaiyali bayapadradhu dhan periya nana. Nalla aalukal munadi dhan adhu nana."
thirukkural: இல்லாள்கண் தாழ்ந்த இயல்பின்மை எஞ்ஞான்றும் நல்லாருள் நாணுத் தரும்.
english_translation: Who's servile to his wife always — shy he feels before the wise.
moral: Being ruled by fear at home makes you lose the respect of everyone outside.

title: The Fearful Potter
theme: Being Led by Women / Weakness in Action
hook: Kumar's father was the best potter but stopped working because his wife called his pots ugly.
story: Kumar's Appa made beautiful pots. But Amma said, "Indha kudam romba mosam. Unakku velai sari illa." Appa stopped. The wheel sat silent. "Paati, appa enna pot pannadha?" Paati's eyes teared. "Amma solla kettu dhan. Avana pathi avaluku peruma illa. Appo avan velai pannuvana?" Kumar took clay. "Appa, naan katren. Nee katta?"
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Appa, unakku pot romba nalla varum!'
Appa: 'Un ammakku pidikala da. Adhu dhan niruthitten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the potter's wheel, spinning clay perfectly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manaiyal solla kettu oru aalu niruthradhu — adhu dhan periya kedu. Avanu velai dhan mukiyam."
thirukkural: மனையாளை அஞ்சும் மறுமையி லாளன் வினையாண்மை வீறெய்த லின்று.
english_translation: Fearing his wife salvationless — the weaklings' action has no grace.
moral: When fear rules you, your work suffers too. Courage is the first step to greatness.

title: The Cycle That Never Came
theme: Being Led by Women / Fear Blocks Goodness
hook: Santhosh's father promised him a bicycle but his mother said no — and the lesson destroys more than a gift.
story: Appa had saved money for a blue bicycle. Santhosh was thrilled. But Amma said, "Vendaam. Kashtam. Oru cycle ku ivalo kasu?" Appa put the money back. "Appa, cycle?" Appa looked down. "Amma sonna maadhiri da." Santhosh cried that night. Not for the cycle — but because Appa said no to his own yes. Next day, Appa's friend asked for a loan. Appa said no. Fear spread everywhere.
tamil_dialogues: Santhosh: 'Appa, neenga dhan cycle vaangi kudupenu sonneenga!'
Appa: 'Amma sonna maadhiri dhan da, sari?'
Santhosh: 'Appa, amma sonnadhu dhan sariya?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the blue cycle, its bell ringing softly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru edathula bayapadravan, ella edathula bayam dhan. Adhu dhan avana aatkum."
thirukkural: இல்லாளை அஞ்சுவான் அஞ்சுமற் றெஞ்ஞான்றும் நல்லார்க்கு நல்ல செயல்.
english_translation: Who fears his wife fears always — good to do to the good and wise.
moral: Fear is like a weed — if you let it grow in one place, it spreads everywhere and chokes all goodness.

title: The God Who Ran Home
theme: Being Led by Women / Image Without Strength
hook: Bharath watches a man who acts like a god outside — but runs home when his wife calls.
story: At Rameswaram, a man in white gave big speeches. "Naan dhan inga dharmam pannradhu. Naan dhan periyavan." A woman's voice cut through. "VELU! SAAPDU READY!" The man dropped his holy tone. "Vandhuten!" and ran. People laughed. "Thatha, avar dhan periya aalu maadhiri?" Thatha chuckled. "Veliya periya aalu. Aana manasukku balam illadhavan. Aval kurala dhan avar oda vazhi."
tamil_dialogues: Bharath: 'Thatha, avar dhan periyavar dhan! En odraaru?'
Thatha: 'Periyavar illada. Aval kural kettu odraavan periyavan illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the shore, the sea behind him, calm and steady.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Veliya periyava maadhiri, aana ullae bayam — adhu dhan nana. Unmaiyaana balam ullae irukka vendum."
thirukkural: இமையாரின் வாழினும் பாடிலரே இல்லாள் அமையார்தோள் அஞ்சு பவர்.
english_translation: Who fear douce arms of their wives — look petty even with god-like lives.
moral: You can look like a king to the world, but if fear rules you at home, you are small.

title: The Shy Stone
theme: Being Led by Women / True Shame
hook: Renuka's father works 12 hours in the salt pans but her mother orders him around — and she realizes who deserves real respect.
story: Appa carried salt bags under the hot sun. His back burned. His hands bled. Renuka brought him water. But at home, Amma shouted, "Kaal kazhuva maataya nee? Evalo naal sollradhu!" Appa silently washed. Renuka couldn't take it. "Amma, appa unna vida nallavar! Avar naalu maelu velai pannraar! Nee avana adikkara maadhiri pesradhu sari illa!" Amma froze. Appa's eyes filled.
tamil_dialogues: Renuka: 'Amma, en appa unna vida mechana! Avanuku perumai kodunga!'
Amma: 'Nee sonnadhu sari dhan. Naa dhan thappu.'
Renuka: 'Appa nalla velai pannraar. Adhu dhan periya aal.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the salt pan, a bag of salt on his shoulder, looking at the sky.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Veliya azhagula irundhadhu dhan perumai illa. Ullae irukka veeram dhan periya aalu aakum."
thirukkural: பெண்ணேவல் செய்தொழுகும் ஆண்மையின் நாணுடைப் பெண்ணே பெருமை உடைத்து.
english_translation: Esteemed more is women bashful than man servile unto her will.
moral: A hardworking person deserves respect — not the person who bosses them around.

title: The Empty Chair
theme: Being Led by Women / Neglecting Friends
hook: Naveen's father used to sit with friends every evening — until his wife stopped him, and he became a lonely man.
story: Every evening, Naveen's Appa sat at the tea stall with friends. Laughing, talking. Then Amma said, "Un friends dhan unakku periya aalu? Naan illaya?" Appa stopped going. Friends called. He didn't answer. Months later, Naveen saw Appa sitting alone in the dark. "Appa, friend kitta pesa maatengala?" Appa said, "Amma sonna dhan." Vetri's father still came. "Naveen, un appa va pathu kooda pesa mudiyadha?"
tamil_dialogues: Vetri: 'Naveen, un appa va friend kooda paaka mudiyadhu?'
Naveen: 'Amma solla kettaaru. Ippo avaruku yaarum illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the tea stall, two empty cups before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nanthargalai vittudu, oru matha solla ketta. Adhu dhan periya thappu. Kaadhal illama irundha, adhu dhan kedu."
thirukkural: நட்டார் குறைமுடியார் நன்றாற்றார் நன்னுதலாள் பெட்டாங்கு ஒழுகு பவர்.
english_translation: By fair-browed wives who are governed — help no friends nor goodness tend.
moral: When one person rules your world, you lose everyone who truly cares for you.

title: The Artist Who Stopped
theme: Being Led by Women / Loss of All Virtues
hook: Mano discovers his art teacher was once a famous painter — but stopped because his wife called it a waste of time.
story: Teacher Shankar's paintings hung in the art school — beautiful, award-winning. But now he only taught kids. "Sir, enna neenga painting pannadha?" Teacher's brush stopped. "Un aunty sonnaanga — painting la velai illa, kasu illa nu." Mano was stunned. "Sir, ivalo periya artist! Neenga niruthitta!" Teacher looked at his empty canvas. "Ama da. Naa niruthitten. Avar sonnadhu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Mano: 'Sir, ungaluku periya talent irukku! En niruthineenga?'
Teacher: 'Amma solla kettu dhan. Ippo yosikradhu — thappu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands before the empty canvas, a brush in his hand, paint dripping.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru matha solla kettu, un thirai, un porul, un santhosam — mothathaya vida koodadhu. Adhu dhan kedu."
thirukkural: அறவினையும் ஆன்ற பொருளும் பிறவினையும் பெண்ஏவல் செய்வார்கண் இல்.
english_translation: No virtue, riches nor joy is seen in those who submit to women.
moral: When you let someone else silence your talent, you lose joy, wealth, and purpose together.

title: The Wise Boy's Choice
theme: Being Led by Women / Wisdom Prevails
hook: Arivu's friend pressures him to listen to his crush over his own judgment — but Arivu refuses.
story: Gokul had a crush on Latha. Latha said, "Nee antha science exhibition la participate pannadha. Waste of time." Gokul listened. He quit the project. Arivu was shocked. "Gokul, nee dhan best project! En niruthure?" Gokul hung his head. "Latha sonna." Arivu said, "Nee un manasukku kettu dhan pannanum. Matha aalu solla kettu un talent ah vida koodadhu." Gokul stayed in. He won first place. Latha came to congratulate. Gokul smiled at Arivu.
tamil_dialogues: Gokul: 'Arivu, Latha dhan niruthu nu sonna.'
Arivu: 'Gokul, nee un decision eduthuko. Yaar solla kettalum un manasu dhan mukiyam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the science exhibition trophy, a hand on Gokul's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yosichu dhan seiyanum. Yaar solla kettu matha aalu maadhiri aaga koodadhu. Un manasukku balam irukka vendum."
thirukkural: எண்சேர்ந்த நெஞ்சத் திடனுடையார்க்கு எஞ்ஞான்றும் பெண்சேர்ந்தாம் பேதைமை இல்.
english_translation: Thinkers strong and broad of heart by folly on fair sex do not dote.
moral: A wise heart listens to reason, not pressure. True strength is thinking for yourself.

title: Sweet Talk Saree Shop
hook: Vennila loves the lady who calls her "golden doll" — until Appa shows her the trick.
story: Every Saturday Vennila went to the saree shop with Appa. The shop lady always said, "Enna golden doll, unakku ithu romba azhaga irukkum!" Vennila smiled. She felt special. One day, the lady said the same thing to another girl. Vennila's smile dropped. "Appa, ava enakku maadhiriye solra?" Appa whispered, "Adhu dhan velai da. Poi sonna vaarthai." Vennila felt smart for figuring it out.
tamil_dialogues: Vennila: 'Appa, avan enakku maadhiriye solra! Unnaku nijamava pudikkuma?'
Appa: 'Poi dhan da. Nijama pudichaa — ella pasangalayum ore maadhiri solvaanga?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands outside the saree shop, holding a single silk thread.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Innisai solli dhan pala perum monguraanga. Vaarthai dhan sulabam — aana adhula nijam irukka? Nijama pakka kathukko."
thirukkural: அன்பின் விழையார் பொருள்விழையும் ஆய்தொடியார் இன்சொல் இழுக்குத் தரும்.
english_translation: For gold, not love their tongue cajoles Men are ruined by bangled belles
moral: Sweet words without true love are just bait — don't be fooled.

title: The Fake Friend
hook: A new girl at school is super sweet to Meena but only when she has snacks.
story: Kavi was the new girl. She held Meena's hand, shared secrets, called her best friend. But only on snack days. On empty-tiffin days, Kavi played with someone else. Meena cried to Akka. "Akka, ava en friend dhan, aana?" Akka said, "Nee pakka da — ava unakku friend a venum, illa un tiffin a venumnu." Meena watched. She understood.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Akka, kavi en friend thaana?'
Akka: 'Avaluku unna pidikkuma illa un fooda pidikkumanu paaru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the school bench, a closed tiffin box beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Payana pakka vendum, vaarthai mattum pakka koodadhu. Yaarukku enna venumnu therinjudhaal, fool aagamaattinga."
thirukkural: பயன்தூக்கிப் பண்புரைக்கும் பண்பின் மகளிர் நயன்தூக்கி நள்ளா விடல்.
english_translation: Avoid ill-natured whores who feign Love only for their selfish gain
moral: Judge people by their intentions, not their sweet words.

title: The Dark Room Hug
hook: Ananya's friend hugs her in the dark during a power cut — but it feels wrong.
story: During a power cut, Ananya's "best friend" hugged her tight in the dark room. But the hug felt cold, like a statue. Ananya felt scared, not warm. She told Amma. Amma said, "Nee comfortable illana, adhu dhan answer. True love feels like sunshine, not like a ghost hug." Ananya understood: real love never feels wrong.
tamil_dialogues: Ananya: 'Amma, ava katti pannudhu. Aana enakku bayama irukku.'
Amma: 'Nee bayapdra, adhu dhan unmai. Love la bayam irukka koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in a sunlit doorway, arms open in a warm embrace.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Chumma katti pannradhu ellam love illa. Adhu bayam illa, adhu verum show. Unnaku comfortable illana odi poidu."
thirukkural: பொருட்பெண்டிர் பொய்ம்மை முயக்கம் இருட்டறையில் ஏத஧ல் பிணந்தழீஇ அற்று.
english_translation: The false embrace of whores is like That of a damned corpse in the dark
moral: A hug without love is hollow — trust your feelings.

title: The Wise Boy's Choice
theme: Choosing Real Wealth Over Fake Charm
hook: Bhuvan's older cousin takes him to meet "cool" girls — but Bhuvan remembers Paati's lesson.
story: Cousin Ravi said, "Vaa da, nalla ponnungale pathu vaanga!" Bhuvan was excited. But at the corner, a group of girls laughed at everything Ravi said — fake loud laughs. Bhuvan felt empty. He remembered Paati: "Ullae nalladhu irundha dhan azhagu." He told Ravi, "Naa poraen. Inga enaku eduvum illa." Ravi laughed, but Bhuvan felt stronger.
tamil_dialogues: Ravi: 'Vaa da, ava nalla irukka!'
Bhuvan: 'Illada. Ullae nalladhu illana, veliyum verum mayam dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on Paati's veranda, reading an old palm-leaf manuscript.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee enna thedranu therinjukka. Ullae arivu irundha, veliya mayam unna fool aakadhu."
thirukkural: பொருட்பொருளார் புன்னலந் தோயார் அருட்பொருள் ஆயும் அறிவினவர்.
english_translation: The wise who seek the wealth of grace Look not for harlots' low embrace
moral: Those with real wisdom seek grace, not empty charm.

title: The Park Crowd
hook: The cool kids invite Dhruv to their "exclusive group" — but there's a price.
story: The park's popular group called Dhruv. "Vaa da, ungala dhan waiting!" Dhruv felt special. But the price: make fun of a smaller boy. Dhruv's stomach twisted. He said no. They laughed. "Sari da nalla payyane." Dhruv walked to the empty bench. Appa brought him ice cream. "Nee dhan jeyichuta, da. Unga manasu dhan unna nalla valiya kattum."
tamil_dialogues: Group leader: 'Adha pannu da. Illa na unaku entry illa.'
Dhruv: 'Entry venam. En manasu dhan mukiyam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the park bench, sharing the ice cream with Dhruv.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pala perum koottam irundha dhan nalladhu illa. Sariyaana neram sariyaana thunai irundha dhan porum."
thirukkural: பொதுநலத்தார் புன்னலம் தோயார் மதிநலத்தின் மாண்ட அறிவினவர்.
english_translation: The lofty wise will never covet The open charms of a vile harlot
moral: True wisdom doesn't trade self-respect for popularity.

title: The Selfie Trap
hook: A stranger wants to take Nithya's photo saying she's "beautiful" — but Chithi stops her.
story: At Vellore Fort, a young woman smiled at Nithya. "Unakku photo eduthu kattava? Romba cute ah irukka!" Nithya was about to pose. Chithi grabbed her hand. "Sorry, venam." Later Chithi explained: "Nalla maadhiri irundha dhan — aana theriyaadhavanga kitta photo kodukka koodadhu. Un body, un safety — adhu dhan important." Nithya hugged Chithi tight.
tamil_dialogues: Nithya: 'Chithi, ava nallavanga dhan pole irundhanga!'
Chithi: 'Theriyaadhavanga nallavangala kettavangala nu theriyadhu. Unna nee dhan kaapadthu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands behind Nithya, one hand gently on her shoulder, nodding at Chithi.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un peyarai, un kaulai, un manathai — ithu ellaam kaapadthu. Adhu dhan nija pathukappu."
thirukkural: தந்நலம் பாரிப்பார் தோயார் தகைசெருக்கிப் புன்னலம் பாரிப்பார் தோள்.
english_translation: Those who guard their worthy fame Shun the wanton's vaunting charm
moral: Guard your reputation and your safety — don't be lured by flattery.

title: The Double Heart
hook: Karthik's best friend starts being nice to someone else exactly the same way.
story: Arun was Karthik's best friend. Same lunch, same bench, same jokes. Then one day, Arun said the exact same jokes to the new boy, sat with him, shared his lunch. Karthik felt like a used toy. Amma said, "Avan rendu perukku ore maadhiri irukkaan. Adhu friendship illa da. Athu habit." Karthik learned: true friends make you feel special — not like an option.
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Amma, avan en friend dhan, aana avane maadhiri mathavan kitta irukkaan.'
Amma: 'Unaku mathavu mathiri feel panna vendam. Neeya special.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between two benches, one hand raised in a gentle stop gesture.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Iru manasu irundha, unakku oru manasu kedaikaadhu. True love means one heart, one friend."
thirukkural: நிறைநெஞ்சம் இல்லவர் தோய்வார் பிறநெஞ்சிற் பேணிப் புணர்பவர் தோள்.
english_translation: Hollow hearts alone desire The arms of whores with hearts elsewere
moral: A divided heart cannot give true friendship.

title: The Magic Spell
hook: Priya sees a woman offering "magic" bangles that make everyone love you — but Thatha knows better.
story: At the temple car street, a woman sold "magic bangles." "Idha potta, yaarume unna love pannuvaanga!" Priya wanted it badly. Thatha pulled her away. "Priya, love nu onnu kaiyala varaadhu. Atha vangittaa — adhu poi. Nijamana love kedaikaadhu." Later, a girl who bought it cried — no one loved her more. Priya hugged Thatha. "Unaku bangle venam. Unaku Thatha dhan porum."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Thatha, adhu magic dhan — yaarum enna love pannuvaanga!'
Thatha: 'Love nu onnu shop la kedaikaadhu da. Adhu ullae irundhu varanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the temple car, weaving a simple cotton thread.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Maya maadhiri irundha dhan irukum. Aana adhu nijamana vaazhkai illa. Nijamana anbu dhan vaazhkai."
thirukkural: ஆயும் அறிவினர் அல்லார்க்கு அணங்கென்ப மாய மகளிர் முயக்கு.
english_translation: Senseless fools are lured away By arms of sirens who lead astray
moral: Only the unwise fall for magic promises — true love needs no spell.

title: The Deep Hole
hook: Mani's friend dares him to jump into an abandoned well — like the "cool" guys do.
story: Selvam pointed at the old well. "Cool boys ellam inga jump panni dhan prove pannuvaanga!" Mani's heart raced. He looked down — dark, deep. "Illada, vera edhavadhu pannalam." Selvam laughed. "Bayam dha da!" Mani walked away. Next week, a boy broke his leg jumping. Mani visited him in hospital. "Unakku dhan bayam, illa enakku bayam nu therinjudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Jump da! Prove pannu!'
Mani: 'Prove pannadhadhu dhan courage. Bayapdi irundhu nirpadhu dhan veeram.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the hospital entrance, holding a single crutch.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ethukku prove pannanum? Unakku enna venumnu therinjuthu. Athu dhan periya vellam."
thirukkural: வரைவிலா மாணிழையார் மென்தோள் புரையிலாப் பூரியர்கள் ஆழும் அளறு.
english_translation: The soft jewelled arms of whores are hell Into which the degraded fall
moral: Following the wrong crowd leads to a deep fall — think before you jump.

title: Three Bad Things
hook: Sathya sees a man drunk, another gambling, another chasing girls — Appa connects the dots.
story: At Thanjavur market, Sathya saw three men: one stumbled drunk, one lost money at dice, one followed girls whistling. "Appa, iva ellaam enna pannraanga?" Appa said, "Ore problem da — avanga manasu irukku, aana adha control panna theriyala. Nee iva maadhiri irukka koodadhu. Unna nee control pannu. Athu dhan manushan."
tamil_dialogues: Sathya: 'Appa, ivanga enna pannittu varaanga?'
Appa: 'Avanga manasukku adimai da. Nee adimai illama iru — adhu dhan nijam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the market center, a young boy holding his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Iru manasam, kal, saaru — ivai moonum ore vithai. Idha vittu odi irundha dhan vaazhkai."
thirukkural: இருமனப் பெண்டிரும் கள்ளும் கவறும் திருநீக்கப் பட்டார் தொடர்பு.
english_translation: Double-minded whores, wine and dice Are lures of those whom fortune flies
moral: Bad company, bad drinks, and bad luck — avoid all three to stay strong.

title: The Cloudy Head
hook: Gowri's favorite uncle used to be funny — now he smells strange and falls asleep anywhere.
story: Uncle Ramesh was the funniest. He did magic tricks, told jokes, carried Gowri on his shoulders. Then he started drinking. Now he came home smelling funny, couldn't walk straight.
