Gowri asked Amma: "En uncle ku enna achu?" Amma said, "Adhu oru disease da. Atha vittutu vantha, namma support pannanum." Gowri wrote Uncle a letter: "Please come back, Uncle. I miss the old you."
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Amma, uncle enna saapdraar? Enakku adhu pudikkala.'
Amma: 'Athu nalla habit illa da. Aana naama avara love pannradhu nirutha koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Uncle on the terrace, looking at the stars.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kal irundha oru manushanoda oli poi dhan. Adha vittutu varadhu dhan avanoda nijama oLikkum."
thirukkural: உட்கப் படாஅர் ஒளியிழப்பர் எஞ்ஞான்றும் கட்காதல் கொண்டொழுகு வார்.
english_translation: Foes fear not who for toddy craze The addicts daily their glory lose
moral: Drinking steals your brightness — your true light comes from within.

title: The Respect Test
hook: The senior boys offer Vasanth a "special drink" to celebrate winning the race.
story: Vasanth won the 100m race. Seniors patted his back. "Special drink vangittom! Iru da!" They brought a bottle. Vasanth knew what it was. "Illada, naan saapdra maaten." The seniors laughed. "Chinna payyan dha!" Vasanth's coach came over. "He's not small. He's smart. Winners don't need that. Winners are already high on winning." The seniors shut up. Vasanth felt taller than his medal.
tamil_dialogues: Senior: 'Kudichu paaru da, pudikkum!'
Vasanth: 'Naa winner ah irundhu dhan happy ah irukken. Enakku adhu venam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the finish line, a gold medal glowing in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudi pannravanai nallavan ninaika maatan. Nee adha kudi pannama irundhadhu dhan unna nallavan aakum."
thirukkural: உண்ணற்க கள்ளை உணில்உண்க சான்றோரான் எண்ணப்படவேண்டா தார்.
english_translation: Drink not liquor; but let them drink Whom with esteem the wise won't think
moral: Real winners don't need alcohol — their victory is enough.

title: The Sour Face
theme: Alcohol Hurts the Ones Who Love You
hook: Jaya's father comes home drunk and can't even recognize her face — Paati holds Jaya close.
story: Appa stumbled in. His eyes were red, his smile crooked. "Jaya... nee enna da... yen azhuva?" He couldn't even hold her. Paati pulled Jaya onto her lap. "Appa ku romba mayakam da. Nee azhaadha." Jaya buried her face in Paati's sari. She whispered, "Paati, en Appa enakku sonna adhe vaarthai naalaikku theriyadha?" Paati's eyes filled. "Theriyum da. Theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Jaya: 'Paati, Appa enakku sonna adhe vaarthai naalaikku nyabagam irukka?'
Paati: 'Appa ku mayakam dhan da. Amaidhiya iru — avan thirumbuvaan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside Jaya, his hand on Paati's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kal irundha manushanoda mugham kuda saadharanam illa. Antha mayakam avanoda amma mughathai kuda kettadhu aakidum."
thirukkural: ஈன்றாள் முகத்தேயும் இன்னாதால் என்மற்றுச் சான்றோர் முகத்துக் களி.
english_translation: The drunkard's joy pains ev'n mother's face How vile must it look for the wise?
moral: A drunkard's face pains even his mother — alcohol steals your loved ones' smiles.

title: Shame Walks Away
hook: Rajesh's uncle was a respected teacher who lost his job because of drink — Rajesh tells the whole school.
story: In assembly, the headmaster asked about "bad habits." Rajesh raised his hand. "En uncle oru teacher dhan. Aana avar ku kudi aasa. School la avara respectful ah paaththaanga. Aana ippo, yaarum respect pannamaatan." The school went silent. "Ella kettavangalayum respect vittutum ponga. En uncle respect pochu." The headmaster nodded slowly. "Unga uncle ku naama help pannanum."
tamil_dialogues: Teacher: 'Rajesh, unga uncle enna pannraar?'
Rajesh: 'Respect panna mudiyadha alavuku kudichu poittaaru, sir.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the headmaster, a single tear on his cheek.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naanu nu oru nalla ponnu — ava kudi pannravan kitta irundhu poi dhan odiduva. Nee unna collect pannu."
thirukkural: நாண்என்னும் நல்லாள் புறங்கொடுக்கும் கள்ளென்னும் பேணாப் பெருங்குற்றத்தார்க்கு.
english_translation: Good shame turns back from him ashamed Who is guilty of wine condemned
moral: Shame leaves when alcohol enters — you lose the good name you've built.

title: The Fool's Trade
hook: Kumar watches a man pay 500 rupees for "special drink" and then can't walk straight.
story: At the weekly market, a man handed over 500 rupees for a small bottle. "Indha special dhan!" He drank it in one gulp. Two minutes later, he couldn't stand. His friends dragged him away. Kumar stared. "Appa, avar 500 rupees kuduthu nila kedakura alavuku kudichuttaar." Appa said, "Adhu dhan da — kudikiravan, kaasu koduthu, buddhi kulla dhan adhan." Kumar laughed. "Full fool business."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Appa, kaasu koduthu, nila kedaka, ivalo periya aasaiya?'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — mayam irundha kaasu koduthu mayakam vaanguraan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at a vegetable stall, holding a fresh coconut.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaasu koduthu mayakam vaanga — ithu dhan periya ariyaamai. Unakku terinja, adha panna maata."
thirukkural: கையறியாமை உடைத்தே பொருள்கொடுத்து மெய்யறியாமை கொளல்.
english_translation: To pay and drink and lose the sense Is nothing but rank ignorance
moral: Paying money to lose your senses is the worst trade you can make.

title: Walking Dead
hook: Divya sees a drunk man brought to the hospital — he looks just like a dead person.
story: Divya was at the hospital for Paati's checkup. The emergency doors burst open. A man lay motionless. Smell of alcohol filled the corridor. "Amma, avan sethutaana?" Amma held her. "Illada. Kudi pannittu mayangi poittan." Divya stared. The man's face was white, lips blue, eyes blank. "Amma, avan sethavana mathiri dhan irukkaan." Amma said, "Adhu dhan da. Kudi pannravan sethavana vida bedham illa."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Amma, avar sethavara pole irukkaaru.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan problem da — uyiroda irundhu setha maadhiri irukkaan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands outside the emergency room, a lamp in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudichu mayangi ponda — sethavana vida vera illa. Uyir irundha polum, adhu uyir illa."
thirukkural: துஞ்சினார் செத்தாரின் வேறல்லர் எஞ்ஞான்றும் நஞ்சுண்பார் கள்ளுண்பவர்.
english_translation: They take poison who take toddy And doze ev'n like a dead body
moral: A drunkard lying senseless is no different from a dead body.

title: The Hidden Laugh
hook: Shankar's friend Kanna hides his drinking from everyone — but the whole school finds out.
story: Kanna thought he was clever. He drank in secret, used perfume to hide the smell. But one day, he came to class swaying. He laughed at nothing. Dropped his books. The teacher knew. The students giggled. "Kanna ku enna achu?" Kanna couldn't answer. Later, Shankar told him: "Yen friend, unna ellarum pathuttu sirikka. Nee getha dhan. Drink is not cool." Kanna cried.
tamil_dialogues: Shankar: 'Kanna, unna paathu ellaam sirikka. Unakku theriyuma?'
Kanna: 'Naa... naan kudichadhu yaarukkum theriyadhu nu nenachen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the classroom door, a student giggling behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Odugu odugunu ninachaa — aana ulagathukku therinjudhum. Kudi pannradhu dhan periya siggu."
thirukkural: உள்ளொற்றி உள்ளூர் நகப்படுவர் எஞ்ஞான்றும் கள்ளொற்றிக் கண்சாய்பவர்.
english_translation: The secret drunkards' senses off Make the prying public laugh
moral: You can't hide drinking forever — the whole town will laugh at you.

title: The Hidden Bottle
hook: Kavya finds a hidden bottle in her father's cupboard — and asks him directly.
story: Kavya was looking for her lost earring. She opened Appa's cupboard. A bottle fell out. She knew what it was. At dinner, she asked: "Appa, cupboard la edho irukku." Appa froze. Amma stopped eating. "Adhu... adhu..." Kavya said, "Nee kudikara? Nee solradhu dhan unmai. Nee nijam sonna, naan help pannuven." Appa cried. "Sorry da." That night, he emptied the bottle in the sink.
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Appa, nijam sonna dhan sari aagum. Adha vittutu vandhudunga.'
Appa: 'Sari da. Naa try pannuraen. Unakkaaga try pannuraen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the kitchen sink, watching the liquid drain away.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Marachu vechadhu dhan periya problem. Nijam solli, adha vittutu varadhu dhan solution."
thirukkural: களித்தறியேன் என்பது கைவிடுக நெஞ்சத்து ஒளித்ததூஉம் ஆங்கே மிகும்.
english_translation: Don't say I'm not a drunkard hard The hidden fraud is known abroad
moral: Denial doesn't hide the truth — honesty is the only way forward.

title: Talking to a Wall
theme: You Can't Reason with a Drunk
hook: Sruthi tries to explain something to her drunk uncle — Thatha tells her it's useless.
story: Uncle was drunk again. Sruthi had a school play at 6pm. "Uncle, naalaikku 6 manikku school la drama! Varama?" Uncle nodded, swaying. "Ama da... ama... varan." The next day, he didn't come. Sruthi cried. Thatha hugged her. "Kudi pannravan kitta edha sonna, thanni mela ezhudha maadhiri dhan. Avanuku theriyadhu — avan ku manasu mayakam. Nee azhaadha."
tamil_dialogues: Sruthi: 'Thatha, avar vandhuruppaaru nu ninaichen.'
Thatha: 'Kudichittu irukravan solladhu edhavadhu eduthukku. Nee ozhunga iru, avan paathu nalla aaguvaan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the empty school play ground, a single chair waiting.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudichavana kitta karanam solli edhavadhu edukkalam nu ninaicha — nee thanni la munnula poyi muthukulla irukra alavu aasanam."
thirukkural: களித்தானைக் காரணம் காட்டுதல் கீழ்நீர்க் குளித்தானைத் தீத்துரீஇ அற்று.
english_translation: Can torch search one in water sunk? Can reason reach the raving drunk?
moral: You can't reason with a drunk person — it's like trying to light a fire underwater.

title: The Mirror Question
hook: Sanjay sees a drunk man on the street and asks: "Does he know how sad he looks?"
story: Coming back from school, Sanjay saw a man stumble near the Meenakshi temple. His shirt was torn, his face was dirty. Kids laughed. Sanjay didn't laugh. "Amma, avan feel pannuvaano? Avanuku theriyudha evlo kettadha irukkunu?" Amma was surprised. "Illada. Kudi pannravanuku adhu theriyadhu. Athu dhan periya problem." Sanjay said, "Naan valandha pinna kudi pannamaaten." Amma smiled through tears.
tamil_dialogues: Sanjay: 'Amma, avanuku theriyudha avan evlo kettadha irukkunu?'
Amma: 'Theriyaadhu da. Athu dhan adhoda periya aapu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple steps, the drunk man sleeping far behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudi pannravanoda avastha pakkum podhu, unakku feel panna mudiyanum. Adhu dhan arivu. Adha vida koodadha nu therinjudhum."
thirukkural: கள்ளுண்ணாப் போழ்திற் களித்தானைக் காணுங்கால் உள்ளான்கொல் உண்டதன் சோர்வு.
english_translation: The sober seeing the drunkard's plight On selves can't they feel same effect?
moral: When you see a drunkard's misery, ask yourself — do you want that for you?

title: The Golden Fish
theme: Winning at Gambling is Still Losing
hook: Balu catches a fish with a golden hook in its mouth — Thatha explains the trap.
story: Balu pulled a fish from the sea. Something shiny glinted — a hook swallowed deep. "Thatha, idha paaru — gold hook!" Thatha took it gently. "Adhu dhan da. Meenukku adhu ruchi. Aana adha vittutu dhan meen valaiyila sikkudhu. Gambling um adhe dhan. Neeye jeyicha maadhiri irukum, aana neeye lose pannitu iruppa." Balu threw the hook back into the sea.
tamil_dialogues: Balu: 'Thatha, gold hook irukku! Athu jeyichudaanu artham illa?'
Thatha: 'Illada. Gold hook dhan meenuku trap. Gambling um adhe dhan — vellum pola irukum, aana neeye lose aaguva.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands knee-deep in the sea, a golden fish in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Gambling la jeyichadhu dhan trap. Meenuku adhu ruchi dhan. Aana adhu dhan avanoda aapu."
thirukkural: வேண்டற்க வென்றிடினும் சூதினை வென்றதூஉம் தூண்டிற்பொன் மீன்விழுங்கி அற்று.
english_translation: Avoid gambling, albeit you win Gulping bait-hook what does fish gain?
moral: Winning in gambling is still losing — like a fish that swallows the golden hook.

title: The One Rupee Trap
hook: Ravi sees a man win 10 rupees in a dice game — then lose his whole salary.
story: At the bus stand, a crowd gathered. "Vaa da! Jeyichu 10 rupee eduthutu!" A man rolled dice. He won 10 rupees. The crowd cheered. Then he played again. Lost 50. Played again. Lost 200. One hour later, he had lost 5000 rupees — his whole month's salary. He sat on the ground, head in hands. Ravi tugged Appa's shirt. "Appa, avan 10 rupee jeyichaan. Aana 5000 kettu poittaan." Appa nodded. "Adhu dhan gambling."
tamil_dialogues: Ravi: 'Appa, 10 rupee jeyichutu 5000 enna kettu poittaan?'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — chinna vellum, periya loss. Oru vithai vittu nilam aaganum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stand, a single die crushed under his foot.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Gambling la jeyichadhu illa. Neeye odane lose aaguvanu dhan adhu. Chillai vellum appo polum periya loss than dhan."
thirukkural: ஒன்றெய்தி நூறிழக்கும் சூதர்க்கும் உண்டாங்கொல் நன்றெய்தி வாழ்வதோர் ஆறு.
english_translation: Can gamblers in life good obtain Who lose a hundred one to gain?
moral: Gamblers lose a hundred to gain one — there's no real winning.

title: The King's Coins
hook: The museum guide shows a king who lost his entire kingdom through dice.
story: At the Thanjavur Palace, the guide showed a painting: a king playing dice. "This king gambled every single day. One day, he lost his palace. His crown. His kingdom." Arivazhagan raised his hand. "Anna, mothama? Oru game la?" The guide nodded. "One game. One roll. Everything gone." Arivazhagan looked at the painting. The king looked happy in the picture — but the guide said he died poor. "Gambling is the kingdom's enemy."
tamil_dialogues: Ariv: 'Anna, oru game la ellam poiduchu?'
Guide: 'Ama da. Ulagathula periya aalungalum gambling la moodi poitanga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the king's portrait, a single die broken in half.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Rajavum gambling la moodi poitaan. Adhu enna aatchi? Adhu enna perumai? Ellam pochu — ore game la."
thirukkural: உருளாயம் ஓவாது கூறின் பொருளாயம் போஒய்ப் புறமே படும்.
english_translation: If kings indulge in casting dice All their fortune will flow to foes
moral: Even kings lose everything to gambling — it respects no one.

title: Papa's Empty Pocket
hook: Jennifer's father wins big one night — but they're still poor the next morning.
story: Jennifer's Appa came home smiling. "Naan jeyichen! 1000 rupee!" He showed the money. Jennifer was happy. New dress, maybe. But the next night, Appa lost everything — plus his fishing net. Amma cried. "Adhu ivalo velai seithu vangina valai." Jennifer looked at Appa. His face was gray. "Appa, adhu dhan gambling ah?" Appa nodded without looking up. That year, there was no Pongal new dress.
tamil_dialogues: Jennifer: 'Appa, neenga jeyichutu, aprom kettu poitinga. En Appa?'
Appa: 'Gambling apdi da. Jeyichadhu mattum nyabagam irukkum. Loss marandhudum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in their empty kitchen, a dry leaf in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Soodu rendu avamaanam tharum. Porulai kettathu mattum illa — manasaiyum kedaikkum."
thirukkural: சிறுமை பலசெய்து சீரழக்கும் சூதின் வறுமை தருவதொன்று இல்.
english_translation: Nothing will make you poor like game Which adds to woes and ruins fame
moral: Nothing brings poverty faster than gambling — it drains everything.

title: The Empty Chair
hook: Selvam visits an old age home and meets a man who was once a rich gambler.
story: Paati volunteered at the old age home. Selvam went with her. An old man sat alone, staring at the wall. "Avanga yaarum pakka varamaata?" Paati said: "Avanga oru kala la romba periya businessman. Aana gambling la ellam pochu. Family um vittutu pochu." Selvam asked the old man: "Thatha, unakku yen gambling pudichadhu?" The old man whispered: "Enna aalana... naan yosichadhu illa."
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Thatha, ungala yaarum pakka varamaata?'
Thatha: 'Illada. Gambling la saami, porulai mattum illa — anbaiyum kettu poittan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the old man, holding his empty hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Soodun kaasarum, sangamum, kaiyum — ivai ellam oru naal kaali aagidum. Gambler oda finale always empty."
thirukkural: கவறும் கழகமும் கையும் தருக்கி இவறியார் இல்லாகி யார்.
english_translation: The game, game-hall and gambler's art Who sought with glee have come to nought
moral: Gamblers who once had everything end up with nothing.

title: The Ogress Gambling
hook: Muthulakshmi sees men playing dice — their faces look like they're being eaten alive.
story: Muthulakshmi peeked through the crowd. Four men sat around a board, dice rolling. Their eyes were red, wide, hungry. One man's hand shook as he threw the dice. Lost. He screamed. Another man won, laughed like a demon. Muthulakshmi felt cold. "Appa, avanga mugham paatha — adha! Paiyanoda mughathula bayam, avanaoda mughathula pisaasu." Appa pulled her away. "Gambling pannravanoda manasula dheivam irukkaadhu. Pasi dhan irukkum."
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Appa, avanga mugham romba bayama irukku!'
Appa: 'Gambling pannravanoda manasula pasi dhan. Atha nirapa mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between Muthulakshmi and the gamblers, shielding her eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Soothu ennum soodhi — adhu oru pisaasu mathiri. Pidicha kai vidaadhu. Kaasu, manasu, uyir — ellaam adhu thinnum."
thirukkural: அகடாரார் அல்லல் உழப்பர்சூதென்னும் முகடியான் மூடப்பட்டார்.
english_translation: Men swallowed by the ogress, dice Suffer grief and want by that vice
moral: Gambling is a monster that devours money, mind, and soul.

title: The Lost Manners
hook: Ganesh's kind neighbor becomes angry and greedy after he starts gambling daily.
story: Uncle Suresh was the kindest. He fixed Ganesh's bicycle, told jokes, brought sweets. Then he discovered gambling. Now he shouted at his wife, ignored his kids, and once yelled at Ganesh for touching his bicycle. "Poi poi! Inga vena vendaam!" Ganesh ran home crying. "Amma, uncle enna achu?" Amma said, "Gambling avana maathittu pochu da. Nee paaru — oru habit enna maathiralam." Ganesh promised himself: never touch dice.
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Amma, uncle dhan, aana enna katharaaru?'
Amma: 'Gambling da. Nalla manushana kooda kettu aakidum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands outside Uncle Suresh's house, a broken bicycle beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Gambling pannravanoda nalla gunam ellam poidum. Anbu, porumai, panbu — ellam adhu saapdudhu."
thirukkural: பழகிய செல்வமும் பண்பும் கெடுக்கும் கழகத்துக் காலை புகின்.
english_translation: If men their time in game-den spend Ancestral wealth and virtues end
moral: Spending time in gambling dens destroys both wealth and good character.

title: The Three Lies
theme: Gambling Leads to Lies and Cruelty
hook: Padma's best friend starts stealing money and lying — all because his father gambles.
story: Rajesh was Padma's benchmate. He used to share lunch. Then he stopped. He started stealing classmates' money. Padma caught him. "Rajesh, yen?" Rajesh cried. "En Appa gambling la pottaaru. Vittula saapda kasu illa. Amma azhara. Naan enna pannuven?" Padma didn't know what to say. She told the teacher. The teacher helped Rajesh's family. "Gambling oru kudumbathaiye kedukku da. Nee nalla iru."
tamil_dialogues: Padma: 'Rajesh, yen kasu thirudra?'
Rajesh: 'En Appa gambling la mothama kettu poittaaru. Vittula saapda kasu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels beside Rajesh, an empty lunch box in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Gambling pannravan kudumbam mothama kaduvadhu. Kasu pochu, aana athoda anbum porumaiyum pochu."
thirukkural: பொருள்கெடுத்துப் பொய்மேற் கொளீஇ அருள்கெடுத்து அல்லல் உழப்பிக்கும் சூது.
english_translation: Game ruins wealth and spoils grace Leads to lies and wretched woes
moral: Gambling destroys wealth, truth, and kindness — leaving only misery.

title: Five Things
hook: The counselor shows Kani five picture cards — the five things gambling steals.
story: Counselor ma'am showed five cards: a shirt (dress), coins (wealth), a plate (food), a medal (fame), a book (learning). "Gambling pannravanukku ivai ellaam kedaikadhu." Kani was confused. "Ma'am, oru manushan gambling pannitu kooda dress podraan?" Ma'am smiled. "Podraan. Aana nalla dress illa. Avanuku saapadum irukkum, aana manasu niraiyaadhu. Avanuku perumai kedaikaadhu. Gambling — ellam kettadhu." Kani decided: no gambling, ever.
tamil_dialogues: Kani: 'Ma'am, oru manushan gambling pannitu kooda nalla irukka mudiyadha?'
Ma'am: 'Mudiyadhu. Adhu oru soodhi — unna mothama kedaikkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the school office, the five picture cards in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Andu — saamangalum, saapadum, oliyum, kalviyum — ivai ellaam gambling pannravanukku kedaikaadhu. Adhu dhan soothin andam."
thirukkural: உடைசெல்வம் ஊண்ஒளி கல்விஎன்று ஐந்தும் அடையாவாம் ஆயங் கொளின்.
english_translation: Dress, wealth, food, fame, learning-these five In gambler's hand will never thrive
moral: Dress, money, food, fame, and learning — gambling steals all five.

title: The Strange Love
theme: Life Clings to Suffering Like Gamblers to Dice
hook: Aadhavan watches a gambler lose again and again — yet come back for more.
story: At Srirangam temple gate, a man played dice. He lost. Lost again. Lost his watch. Lost his ring. Then he borrowed money and lost that too. "Amma, avan yen adha nirutha maata? Avanuku theriyudhu adhu kettu ponnum!" Amma said, "Gambling apdi da. Jeyichadhu oru nimidam. Aana adha vida mudiyadhu. Oru vithai pola — adha vida oru aasai. Athu dhan life um — kashta padra, kashta padra, aana vida mudiyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Aadhavan: 'Amma, avan yen adha vida maata?'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da — oru mayakam. Kashta paduvom nu therinjum, manasuku adhu venum pola irukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple entrance, a single lamp burning bright.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Soothu pannravanuku therium — adhu kettu ponum. Aana avan vida mudiyadhu. Athu pola dhan uyirkum — kashtam irundha polum, uyira vida aasai."
thirukkural: இழத்தொறூஉம் காதலிக்கும் சூதேபோல் துன்பம் உழத்தொறூஉம் காதற்று உயிர்.
english_translation: Love for game grows with every loss As love for life with sorrows grows
moral: Gamblers love the game more with every loss — just as life clings to existence through every pain.

title: Mootha Paiyan
hook: Selvam gets sick eating too many mangoes and Athai knows exactly why without a doctor.
story: Selvam ate seven raw mangoes from Athai's tree. By evening his stomach was a knot. "Athai, vaikuthu!" Athai plucked neem leaves, boiled them, made him drink. "Neeya munnadi oru kai panna maadhiri, innum oru kai pannale. Miga panna, athu dhan uyira kettudhu. Motham balance-la irukkanum, nee polapola pannale." Selvam sipped the bitter water and felt better by night.
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Athai, manga dhan saapitten, enakka innikki valikkudhu!'
Athai: 'Miginum kuraiyinum noyseyyum da — nadu nadu dhan nalladhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the mango tree, a single ripe fruit in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vaatham, pitham, kabam — moonu. Miginadhu noyai tharum, kuraindhadhum noyai tharum. Adha sariya pathukala dhan udambu nalla irukkum."
thirukkural: மிகினும் குறையினும் நோய்செய்யும் நூலோர் வளிமுதலா எண்ணிய மூன்று.
english_translation: Wind, bile and phlegm three cause disease So doctors deem it more or less.
moral: Balance in all things is the key to health.

title: Amma's Kitchen Rule
hook: Mani wants to eat again right after lunch but Amma has a firm rule about waiting.
story: Mani finished his lunch in five minutes. "Amma, innum pasikkudhu!" Amma pointed to the clock. "Oru mani neram wait pannu. Un vayithula irukka sapadu jeri aaganum. Adhuku munnadi saapdradhu dhan romba periya rogam varum." Mani waited — and after an hour, his hunger came back naturally. He realised Amma's rule was medicine itself.
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Amma, innum sapadlam? Pasikkudhu!'
Amma: 'Jeri aaga vitru da. Aprom dhan unakku marunthu venam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the kitchen table, a clock ticking beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Jeri aagadha sapadu udambula noya aakkum. Atha jeri aaga vitru, appo dhan udambukku nalladhu."
thirukkural: மருந்தென வேண்டாவாம் யாக்கைக்கு அருந்தியது அற்றது போற்றி உணின்.
english_translation: After digestion one who feeds His body no medicine needs.
moral: Eat only after your last meal digests — no medicine needed.

title: The Half-Plate Secret
hook: Vennila orders a huge meal but Appa makes her stop halfway through.
story: Appa took Vennila to the restaurant. She ordered a full meals — rice, sambar, kootu, payasam. She ate fast. Midway, Appa stopped her. "Pothum da." "Appa, innum irukku!" "Adhu dhan — innum irukku. Atha nalaiku saapdu. Ippave mudichuta, vaikuthu." She didn't listen, ate more. By night her stomach hurt. Appa gave her warm water. "Naan sonnadhu dhan. Arandhu arandhu saapdu."
tamil_dialogues: Vennila: 'Appa, innum saapda venum!'
Appa: 'Araal arandhu saapdu da. Adhu dhan udambukku nalladhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the next table, eating from a small steel plate.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Araal arindhu saapdradhu dhan udambuku nalladhu. Udambu jeri adha alandhukittu dhan saapda venum."
thirukkural: அற்றால் அறவறிந்து உண்க அஃதுடம்பு பெற்றான் நெடிதுய்க்கும் ஆறு.
english_translation: Eat food to digestive measure Life in body lasts with pleasure.
moral: Eat according to your digestion — that's the way to long life.

title: The Empty Stomach Race
theme: Medicine / Hunger is the Best Sauce
hook: Siva refuses to eat before a race and wins — Periyappa explains why.
story: Periyappa said, "Oda munna sapadlaam, nalla energy varum." But Siva said no. He ran on empty stomach. He won first place. "Periyappa, unaku theriyuma — naan saapdala, full stomach-la odina vaikudhu!" Periyappa laughed. "Ama da. Pasikkaradhu dhan udambuku signal. Nee adha correct-a follow panna, udambu nalla irukkum."
tamil_dialogues: Siva: 'Periyappa, sapadla odina velikkudhu!'
Periyappa: 'Adhu dhan — jeri aana sapadu dhan udambukku fuel.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the finish line, a steaming cup of water in hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Jeri aana piragu dhan saapdradhu. Udambuku bayangara vilakkam irundha, noy varadhu."
thirukkural: அற்றது அறிந்து கடைப்பிடித்து மாறல்ல துய்க்க துவரப் பசித்து.
english_translation: Know digestion; with keen appetite Eat what is suitable and right.
moral: Eat only when truly hungry — let appetite be your guide.

title: The Fasting Champion
hook: Muthu's friend fasts on Mondays and is the strongest in class — he learns why.
story: Manoj never ate on Mondays. "Unakku pal varadha?" Manoj smiled. "Illada, oru naal sappadla vittadhu, en udambu rest edukkum." Muthu tried it. Monday morning, water only. By afternoon he felt light, clear-headed. "Amma, oru naal sappadla vittadha udambu feel aagudhu!" "Adhu dhan da — oru naal udambuku nalla rest."
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Amma, enakku innikki light-a irukku!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan — sapadla vittadhu udambuku oru break.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the school bench, a steel glass of water beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Adikkadi sappadla vitradhu udambuku nalladhu — athu dhan udambu eduthukura rest."
thirukkural: மாறுபாடு இல்லாத உண்டி மறுத்துண்ணின் ஊறுபாடு இல்லை உயிர்க்கு.
english_translation: With fasting adjusted food right Cures ills of life and makes you bright.
moral: With measured food, properly timed, no disease can touch you.

title: Empty Vessel
hook: Lakshmi sees a street vendor selling fried snacks but Paati says only on empty stomach.
story: Lakshmi's stomach was still full from lunch. But the vadai smell was strong. "Paati, oru vadai venum!" Paati held her hand. "Vayithu niraiya irukka, bayangara harm da. Adha jeri aaga vitru. Appo dhan unakku nalla ruchi irukkum, udambukum nalladhu." Lakshmi waited two hours. That vadai was the best she ever ate.
tamil_dialogues: Lakshmi: 'Paati, vayithu niraiya iruntha enna aagum?'
Paati: 'Niraiya iruntha, adhu udambula poison maadhiri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the vadai stall, holding a single piece up to the sun.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vayithu khaaliya irukkradhu dhan nalla iruku. Athu pola udambu feel pannum, noyayum thadukkum."
thirukkural: இழிவறிந்து உண்பான்கண் இன்பம்போல் நிற்கும் கழிபேர் இரையான்கண் நோய்.
english_translation: Who eats with clean stomach gets health With greedy glutton abides ill-health.
moral: Eat with a clean stomach and health stays; eat as a glutton and disease stays.

title: The Festive Trap
hook: Gowri eats 14 festival sweets and learns the hard way why limits matter.
story: The temple festival had unlimited sweets. Gowri ate laddu, jangiri, mysorepak, badusha — 14 pieces. By evening she was vomiting. "Appa, enakku valikkudhu!" Appa carried her home. "Nee theraama saapdradhu dhan noiyai koottittu vanthichu. Udambu oru alavu dhan eduthukku." She never overate again.
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Appa, konjam kooda saapdalam nu thonuchu...'
Appa: 'Alavu theriyama saapdradhu dhan rogiyam varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple counter, holding a single laddu.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Noy alavu irundha udambula irundha, bayama irukka vendiya visayam. Adha nee therinjutu dhan irukkanum."
thirukkural: தீயள வன்றித் தெரியான் பெரிதுண்ணின் நோயள வின்றிப் படும்.
english_translation: Who glut beyond the hunger's fire Suffer from untold diseases here.
moral: Eat beyond hunger's measure and disease comes without measure.

title: The Smart Doctor's Secret
hook: Harini watches Dr. Ramesh treat patients — he never guesses, he always asks why.
story: A woman came with headache. Dr. Ramesh asked, "Sapadla? Thookkam? Kavalai?" She confessed she hadn't slept in three days. He gave her sleep medicine, not painkillers. Harini was amazed. "Uncle, thalai vali ku manju kuduthurukalaame?" "Illada — noyoda reason dhan important. Thalai vali symptom. Ana reason vera. Mothatha paakanum."
tamil_dialogues: Harini: 'Uncle, enakku doctor aaganum — epdi noya kandu pudikradhu?'
Doctor: 'Noy mattum illa da — adhu ennaala vanthuchu nu terinja dhan sari.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the doctor, a stethoscope around his neck.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Noyoda karanam therinjudha dhan marunthu kudutha aaganum. Illana poyya dhan vazhi pokum."
thirukkural: நோய்நாடி நோய்முதல் நாடி அதுதணிக்கும் வாய்நாடி வாய்ப்பச் செயல்.
english_translation: Test disease, its cause and cure And apply remedy that is sure.
moral: Diagnose the disease, its cause, and the cure — then act rightly.

title: The Village Doctor
hook: Karuppu's fever won't go down — the village doctor checks everything before giving medicine.
story: Karuppu had high fever. The village doctor came. He checked his pulse, tongue, eyes, felt his stomach. "Nerama panni, epdi? Etta neram? Moodu?" He noted the season, the time of day. "Rathri dhan joram adhigam." He gave medicine only after all this. Next day Karuppu was fine. "Amma, doctor valiya epdi therinjaru?" Amma smiled. "Padichirukaan da."
tamil_dialogues: Karuppu: 'Doctor, enakka joram innum poga maata?'
Doctor: 'Podhum da, innikki iravu sari aagum — samayam dhan important.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the clinic entrance, a palm leaf manuscript in hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pada sariya therinja doctor dhan noya sariya theripaan. Noy, noyoda balam, kaalam — moonum therinja dhan sari."
thirukkural: உற்றான் அளவும் பிணியளவும் காலமும் கற்றான் கருதிச் செயல்.
english_translation: Let the skilful doctor note The sickmen, sickness, season and treat.
moral: A skilled doctor considers the patient, the disease, and the season.

title: Four Pillars
hook: Priya breaks her arm and learns that healing takes a team — not just medicine.
story: Priya fell off the swing. At CMC Hospital, Dr. Thomas set her bone. Nurse Mary held her hand. "Bayama irukka? Naan irukken." Amma brought her food, wiped her face. The pharmacist gave the right syrup. Priya realised — each one played a part. "Amma, ellarume doctor dhan!" Amma smiled. "Ama da — rogi, doctor, marundhu, nurse — naalu perum dhan oru team."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Nurse Mary, ungalala dhan bayam pogudhu!'
Nurse Mary: 'Adhu dhan enga velai da — unakku theriyama oru support.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the hospital corridor, a healing light around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Rogi, doctor, marundhu, nurse — naalu per. Naalum onna iruntha dhan noy pola mudiyum."
thirukkural: உற்றவன் தீர்ப்பான் மருந்துழைச் செல்வானென்று அப்பால் நாற் கூற்றே மருந்து.
english_translation: Patient, doctor, medicine and nurse Are four-fold codes of treating course.
moral: Healing requires four: the patient, the doctor, the medicine, and the caregiver.

title: The Honest Auto Boy
hook: Kathir's auto driver finds a bag of money and returns it — Appa says that's true nobility.
story: Auto uncle found a bag with 50,000 rupees in his auto. He took it to the police station. Kathir saw the whole thing. "Appa, avaru enna kastama irukar, aana kaasa vittutu police station ku ponaar!" Appa said, "Adhu dhan kudi maanam da. Paisa irundha dhan nallavadhu illa — seyal dhan. Ivar periya kudumbathula dhan pirandhu irukar."
tamil_dialogues: Kathir: 'Appa, ivarukku kaasu venama?'
Appa: 'Venum da, aana adha vida uyaram dhan — nalladhu seyradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the auto, the bag of money on the seat beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unmaiyaana kudi maanam — athu irundha dhan manushanukku nalla peru varum. Sariya nadandhukradhu dhan athuku athayam."
thirukkural: இற்பிறந்தார் கண்அல்லது இல்லை இயல்பாகச் செப்பமும் நாணும் ஒருங்கு.
english_translation: Right-sense and bashfulness adorn By nature only the noble-born.
moral: Righteousness and modesty are the natural marks of true nobility.

title: The Three Promises
hook: Bala's Thatha teaches him the three marks of a noble-born: conduct, truth, modesty.
story: Bala found a 100 rupee note on the loom floor. "Thatha, yaaro potta maadhiri!" Thatha said, "Oru kudumbathula pirandhavan — moonu visayam therinjudha irukanum: nalla nadandhukradhu, unmai solradhu, naanam irukradhu." Bala gave the note to the weaver who dropped it. Thatha hugged him. "Nee inniki moonu ummum maintain pannita."
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Thatha, andha moonum enna?'
Thatha: 'Ozhukkam, vaaimai, naanam — moonum irundha dhan nalla kudi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the silk loom, weaving the three virtues into the fabric.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudi maanathula pirandhavan — ozhukkam, vaaimai, naanam — moonaiyum kai vittu vida maatan."
thirukkural: ஒழுக்கமும் வாய்மையும் நாணும்இம் மூன்றும் இழுக்கார் குடிப்பிறந் தார்.
english_translation: The noble-born lack not these three: Good conduct, truth and modesty.
moral: The truly noble never lack conduct, truthfulness, and modesty.

title: The Rich Girl's Smile
hook: Anitha from a rich family treats a poor girl badly — but Divya's kindness shames her.
story: Anitha came from a big house. She laughed at Divya's torn uniform. "Unakku uniform kooda vanga mudiyala?" Divya smiled at her. "Parava illa, nalaiku puthu uniform varum." At lunch, Divya shared her food. Anitha sat alone — no one shared with her. She went to Divya. "Nee en mela kaayam illama irukka?" Divya said, "Kaayam pottadhu dhan kuduthal."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Divya, nee en mela kaayam illaama irukka?'
Divya: 'Nakai, eegai, insol, ikazhamai — naanum dhan padichirukken.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the two girls, a hand on each shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nakai, eegai, insol, ikazhamai — naanu. Siri, kodu, nalla sollu, yaarayum ikazhaadhe. Athu dhan kudimaanam."
thirukkural: நகைஈகை இன்சொல் இகழாமை நான்கும் வகையென்ப வாய்மைக் குடிக்கு.
english_translation: Smile, gift, sweet words and courtesy These four mark true nobility.
moral: Smile, give, speak sweetly, and never scorn — these four define true nobility.

title: The Bribe That Failed
hook: A man offers Senthil's Appa a bribe to skip the queue — Appa refuses even for crores.
story: The bus was crowded. A man with a suitcase whispered to Senthil's Appa. "1000 rupees kudhuren, enna munnadi vitru." Appa looked at him. "Kodi kuduthalum enna vida maaten." The man got angry. Senthil held Appa's hand. "Appa, avaru kovama irukkar!" Appa said, "Kudipirandhavan — kovam vanthalum saariya irukanum. Adhu dhan namma maanam."
tamil_dialogues: Senthil: 'Appa, niraya kaasu dhan, eduthurukalaame?'
Appa: 'Kudipirandhavan kodi kidaichaalum, saariya nadakkanum da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus queue, holding a single coin.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kodi kidaichaalum, kudimaanam keezha poga koodadhu. Adhu dhan periya manushan."
thirukkural: அடுக்கிய கோடி பெறினும் குடிப்பிறந்தார் குன்றுவ செய்தல் இலர்.
english_translation: Even for crores, the noble mood Cannot bend to degrading deed.
moral: Even for millions, the noble-born will never stoop to a degrading act.

title: Empty Hands, Full Heart
hook: Roja's family has no money but she still offers water to a thirsty stranger.
story: Roja's Appa lost his job. There was little to eat. A beggar came. "Amma, thanni." Roja had only one glass of water. She gave it to him. Appa watched. "Roja, namakku thanni kooda illa." Roja said, "Appa, avarukum dhan pasikkudhu." Appa cried. "Neeyum en kudi maanam. Vazhangradhu kuraindhaalum, nalla seyal maaraadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Roja: 'Appa, avarukum dhan thanni venum.'
Appa: 'Namma kudumbathula dhan nee pirandha, athu dhan periya kudimaanam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the temple entrance, a brass pot of water beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaiyil onnum illaama irundhaalum, kudimaanam kuraiyaadhu. Vazhangra manasu irukkradhu dhan periyadhu."
thirukkural: வழங்குவ துள்வீழ்ந்தக் கண்ணும் பழங்குடி பண்பில் தலைப்பிரிதல் இன்று.
english_translation: The means of gift may dwindle; yet Ancient homes guard their noble trait.
moral: Even when wealth fades, the noble-born never lose their generous nature.

title: The Copy Cheat
hook: Ganesh is tempted to cheat in the exam but Venba's one look stops him.
story: Ganesh didn't study for the social exam. He wrote answers on his palm. Venba saw. She just stared. Ganesh froze. He washed his hands in the middle of the exam. The teacher was confused. After, Venba asked, "Enna panna?" Ganesh said, "Onnum pannala. Un paruppu dhan enakku vekkam kuduthuchu." Venba smiled. "Kudi maanathula pirandhavan, sariya nadandhukuvan."
tamil_dialogues: Venba: 'Kai la ezhuthi irundha? Nee apdi pannala?'
Ganesh: 'Panna maaten. Naan apdi pannadha family.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the blackboard, chalk in hand.
thirukkural: சலம்பற்றிச் சால்பில செய்யார்மா சற்ற குலம்பற்றி வாழ்தும் என் பார்.
english_translation: Who guard their family prestige pure Stoop not to acts of cunning lure.
moral: True nobles never stoop to cunning — they guard their family's name.

title: The Moon Spot
theme: Nobility of Birth / Faults Are Visible
hook: Deva lies once about eating chocolate — Amma says his fault shows like a spot on the moon.
story: Deva ate his sister's chocolate and said, "Naan saapdala." Amma just looked at him. "Deva, moon-la irukka karaigal ungalukku theriyuma?" "Theriyum." "Nee kuda apdi dhan — un kudumbathula pirandhavan, un kuttam ellarukkum theriyum. Moon maadhiri." Deva confessed.
tamil_dialogues: Deva: 'Amma, moon-la karaigal theriyudhu, aana naan...'
Amma: 'Kudipirandhavanuku kuttam maaraadhu. Andha karaigal maadhiri ellarukkum theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the balcony, moonlight falling on his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Andha karaigal moon-la theriyudhu. Adhu pola, nalla kudumbathula pirandhavanuku kuttam vegam theriyum."
thirukkural: குடிப்பிறந்தார் கண்விளங்கும் குற்றம் விசும்பின் மத஧க்கண் மறுப்போல் உயர்ந்து.
english_translation: The faults of nobly-born are seen Like on the sky the spots of moon.
moral: The faults of the noble stand out clearly, like spots on the moon.

title: The Rude Good Boy
theme: Nobility of Birth / Conduct Reveals Birth
hook: Vimal's friend from a "good family" is rude to a priest — Thatha explains everyone suspects his birth.
story: Vimal's friend Parthi came from a rich family. At the temple, Parthi yelled at the priest. "Enna manda, thiraka maata?" Vimal was shocked. Thatha pulled Vimal aside. "Paatha? Avan nalla kudumbathula dhan pirandhan. Aana avan seyal romba mosam. Ippo yaarum avan family-a nambamaata." Vimal understood: actions speak louder than family name.
tamil_dialogues: Vimal: 'Thatha, avan valya kudumbathula dhan pirandhan!'
Thatha: 'Seyal dhan theriyum da — seyal mosama irundha, kudumbam kuda appeal aagum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple gopuram, a finger to his lips.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla kudumbathula pirandhavan naama — aana seyal mosama irundha, kudumbathaiyum sandhegam aakidum."
thirukkural: நலத்தின்கண் நாரின்மை தோன்றின் அவனைக் குலத்தின்கண் ஐயப் படும்.
english_translation: If manners of the good are rude People deem their pedigree crude.
moral: If a well-born person behaves badly, even their good birth is doubted.

title: The Seed and the Sprout
hook: Chitra speaks so politely that a stranger asks which family she's from.
story: At the Erode market, Chitra asked a vendor, "Saar, ivai evlo?" The vendor smiled. "Pasai, unakku valya veedu epdi?" Chitra said, "Yaaru kitta keluradhu, adhu dhan theriyum." Periyappa heard this and beamed. The vendor said, "Iva periya kudumbathula dhan valandha pasai maadhiri irukka." Periyappa said, "Ama. Vithai maadhiri — aval seyal dhan kattum."
tamil_dialogues: Vendor: 'Unakku nalla veedu dhan pole?'
Chitra: 'Illanga, aana enakku nalla nadandhukradhu theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the market, weighing vegetables on an old scale.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nila thila irundha vithai maadhiri — kudumbam theriyadhu, aana seyal theriyum. Adhu pola."
thirukkural: நிலத்தில் கிடந்தமை கால்காட்டும் காட்டும் குலத்தில் பிறந்தார்வாய்ச் சொல்.
english_translation: Soil's nature is seen in sprout The worth of birth from words flow out.
moral: As the soil shows in the sprout, so does noble birth show in one's words.

title: The Bowing King
theme: Nobility of Birth / Humility is Greatness
hook: The richest boy in school bullies Kumar — but Kumar's Appa teaches that true nobility bows down.
story: Rajesh, the richest boy, bullied Kumar. "Unakku bike illa, nalla veedu illa. Nee enna kudimaanam?" Kumar came home crying. Appa laughed. "Kumar, unakku theriyuma — periya kudumbathula pirandhavan evan avan? Avan mattum dhan yaarukum adakki nikka mudiyum." Next day, Kumar greeted Rajesh with a smile. Rajesh was confused. "Enna unakku putthu theriyala?" Kumar said, "Periyavan adakki nikanum. Athu dhan kudimaanam."
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Nee valya kudumbam illa da!'
Kumar: 'Kudumbam perusala? Athu illa — naan adakki nikkradhu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the two boys, bowing slightly with a smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla kudumbathula pirandhavan — avan mattum dhan adakki nikka mudiyum. Athu dhan periya visayam."
thirukkural: நலம்வேண்டின் நாணுடைமை வேண்டும் குலம்வேண்டின் வேண்டுக யார்க்கும் பணிவு.
english_translation: All gain good name by modesty Nobility by humility.
moral: If you seek goodness, have modesty; if you seek nobility, have humility.

title: The Laughing Class
hook: Vasu's friends dare him to sing like a dog for money — he refuses even though he needs it.
story: Suresh pulled out a 50 rupee note. "Vasu, naai maadhiri kuraichu kaatu — idhu unakku." Vasu's stomach was empty. But he walked away. "Vasu, unakku thuni venama?" Vasu turned. "Venum dhan. Aana idhu maadhiri thazhndhu panna, en maanam poidum." The class went silent. The teacher heard. She bought him lunch. "Nalla panna da. Maanam irukradhu dhan periya selavu."
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Kashtama iruntha adha pannu da!'
Vasu: 'Kashtam dhan, aana adha vida kuduthal — maanam poidum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, a lunch box in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Udambuku venumnaalum, maanam po ga koodadhu. Adha vida kastam vera edhum illa."
thirukkural: இன்றி அமையாச் சிறப்பின ஆயினும் குன்ற வருப விடல்.
english_translation: Though needed for your life in main, From mean degrading acts refrain.
moral: Even for things essential to life, never do a degrading act.

title: The Stolen Medal
hook: Jagan finds a gold medal on the track — wearing it would win him fame, but he returns it.
story: Jagan found a gold medal near the finish line. No one saw. "Coach kitta kudutha nalla peru varum." But it belonged to someone else. "Ithu enakku illa." He returned it to the organizer. The owner, a blind runner, wept. "Nee en uyira thirumbi kuduthuta." Coach hugged Jagan. "Nee inniki 100 gold medal mathiri jeichuta."
tamil_dialogues: Coach: 'Yaarum paakala, eduthutu po irukalame.'
Jagan: 'Adhu dhan illa Coach — nalla peru venumna, sariya nadakkanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the track, the gold medal glowing in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla peru venumna, seeral seyal panna koodadhu. Atha vida periya visayam vera onnum illa."
thirukkural: சீரினும் சீரல்ல செய்யாரே சீரொடு பேராண்மை வேண்டு பவர்.
english_translation: Who seek honour and manly fame Don't do mean deeds even for name.
moral: Those who seek true honour never do dishonourable acts even for fame.

title: The Fallen Rich Man
hook: Anand's once-rich neighbour loses everything — but still walks with his head high.
story: Ramasamy Ayya was the richest man in the street. He lost everything. Now he walked barefoot. But his back was straight. "Ayya, unakku kastama irukka?" Anand asked. Ayya smiled. "Kaiyila onnum illa da. Aana thalaila maanam irukku. Athu pona piragu dhan kastam." Anand's Thatha said, "Pathiya? Perusa irundha adakki nikanum. Sirusa irundha uyarama nikanum."
tamil_dialogues: Anand: 'Ayya, unakku bayama irukka?'
Ayya: 'Bayam illada. Kaiyila illadhadhu dhan pona, thalaila irukka maanam poga koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar walks beside Ayya, both barefoot, backs straight.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aalavula perusa iruntha adakki nikanum, aana sirusa iruntha uyarama nikanum. Adhu dhan maanam."
thirukkural: பெருக்கத்து வேண்டும் பணிதல் சிறிய சுருக்கத்து வேண்டும் உயர்வு.
english_translation: Be humble in prosperity In decline uphold dignity.
moral: In prosperity be humble; in adversity hold your head high.

title: The Fallen Hair
hook: Dhanam's classmate was the school topper — now she steals lunch boxes. Dhanam asks why.
story: Nandini was the class topper. After her father died, she stole food. "Amma, Nandhini ippo thirudura!" Amma said, "Thalaila irundha mudi udhundha maadhiri da. Avalukku nee help pannu, kaalam sariyaagum." Dhanam shared her lunch. Nandini cried. "Enakku onnum theriyala." Dhanam said, "Udhuna mudi maadhiri dhan — aana mudi thirumba valarum."
tamil_dialogues: Dhanam: 'Amma, ava munna romba nalla irundha.'
Amma: 'Mudi udhundha maadhiri da — udhuna pramadhum, andha nilaiku varala, adhu dhan kastam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the school corridor, a lock of hair in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thalaila irundha mudi udhundha maadhiri — oru nilaiku vanthavan keezha poga, adhu periya kedumai."
thirukkural: தலையின் இழிந்த மயிரனையர் மாந்தர் நிலையின் இழிந்தக் கடை.
english_translation: Like hair fallen from head are those Who fall down from their high status.
moral: Those who fall from their high station are like hair fallen from the head.

title: The Little Crack
hook: Murugan lies about a small crack in the window — Headmaster shows how one crack destroyed a wall.
story: Murugan broke a window playing cricket. He blamed the wind. Headmaster took him to the old wall. One crack ran from top to bottom. "Paaru, oru siriya vettu — aana adhu dhan motha suvarai keezha pottudhu. Nee sonna oru siriya poi kooda — athum un maanatha kedukkum." Murugan confessed. "Headmaster, naan dhan udachen."
tamil_dialogues: Headmaster: 'Oru siriya kuttam — aana adhu dhan mothatha kedukkum.'
Murugan: 'Naan dhan udachen, Headmaster. Inime poy solla maaten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the cracked wall, touching the fissure.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Siriya dosham dhan periya manushanaiyum keezha kottidum. Malai maadhiri irunthalum — oru kunjani maadhiri vettu."
thirukkural: குன்றின் அனையாரும் குன்றுவர் குன்றுவ குன்றி அனைய செயின்.
english_translation: Even hill-like men will sink to nought With abrus-grain-like small default.
moral: Even mountain-like people fall from grace over a tiny mistake.

title: The Hanging Head
hook: Chezhiyan begs his rich friend for a job — his sister stops him and teaches him dignity.
story: Chezhiyan's friend Ravi laughed at him. "Unakku velai venuma? Munda pottu vanthu oru naal work pannu, naan solren." Chezhiyan agreed, head down. His Akka pulled him back. "Enna pannra? Avan unnai ikazhuran! Nee avan pinnaal pona dhan avan sirippan. Idhu periya maanam thappu." Chezhiyan woke up. "Sari Akka. Naan vera vazhi pathukiren."
tamil_dialogues: Chezhiyan: 'Akka, velai venum, avan kitta keluven.'
Akka: 'Unnai ikazhuravan pinnaal pona dhan avan sirippan. Nee apdi panna koodadhu!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the factory gate, head high, eyes steady.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unnai evan ikazhuran, avan pinnaal pona dhan avan sirippan. Athala vena dhan, avanukku un mayir kooda illai."
thirukkural: புகழ்இன்றால் புத்தேள்நாட்டு உய்யாதால் என்மற்று இகழ்வார்பின் சென்று நிலை.
english_translation: Why fawn on men that scorn you here It yields no fame, heaven's bliss neither.
moral: Why stand behind those who scorn you? It brings neither fame nor salvation.

title: Standing Tall at the Bottom
hook: Pandi loses everything in a landslide but refuses to beg from those who mocked him.
story: The landslide took Pandi's hut. Neighbors mocked. "Pandi kitte edachu therinjudhu namma vita." Rich uncle came. "Vaa da enga veetla thengu thinnu." Pandi wanted to go. Amma held him. "Avanga unna ikazhuranga. Saavadhu vanthalum, avan kitta poi nikka koodadhu." They built a small thatched roof themselves. "Amma, namma sariya dhan irukkom." Amma said, "Ama da — saavum vera, avan kitta poga koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Pandi: 'Amma, namma kastama dhan irukkom.'
Amma: 'Kastam dhan — aana andha mathiri avan munnadi nikkradhu, atha vida kastam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the thatched roof, hammer in hand, building alongside them.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Edhiriyin munnal pona vida, saavadhu nalladhu? Illa. Saavadhu varum aana maanam vida koodadhu."
thirukkural: ஒட்டார்பின் சென்றொருவன் வாழ்தலின் அந்நிலையே கெட்டான் எனப்படுதல் நன்று.
english_translation: Better it is to die forlorn Than live as slaves of those who scorn.
moral: Better to be called dead and fallen than to live as a slave to those who scorn you.

title: The Broken Watch
theme: Honour / What is Life Without Honour?
hook: Kumar steals his father's watch to sell — but Appa asks: "Is this how you want to live?"
story: Kumar wanted a new phone. He took Appa's old watch. "Vittu, puthu phone vaangalam." Appa caught him. He didn't shout. "Kumar, un soda valikudha?" Kumar cried. "Appa, enakku phone venum." Appa sat down. "Udambu valkkudhu dhan, maanam kedachu dhan irukanum. Adhukku phone venum? Udambu pathukalam — aana maanam illadha valkkai, athu dhan periya rogam."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Appa, enakku phone romba venum.'
Appa: 'Udambu valkradhadhu dhan podhuma? Maanam kedachu irukka venama?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the doorway, the watch in one hand, a phone in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Maanam illadha valkkai — adhu dhan periya rogiyam. Adha vida udambu patikradhu periyadhu illa."
thirukkural: மருந்தோமற்று ஊன்ஓம்பும் வாழ்க்கை பெருந்தகைமை பீடழிய வந்த இடத்து.
english_translation: Is nursing body nectar sweet Even when one's honour is lost?
moral: What use is a healthy body when honour is destroyed?

title: The Yak's Hair
hook: Veera's friend asks him to lie to save them both — Veera chooses truth over friendship.
story: Maran broke the chemistry equipment. "Veera, teacher kitta nee udachenu sollu. Naan pona, veetla adi." Veera loved Maran like a brother. "Maran, un mela anbu irukku. Aana unmai solla maatena?" Maran got caught. He stopped talking to Veera. Veera cried to Amma. "Amma, avan en kooda pesa maata." Amma said, "Yak maadhiri da — athukku mudi pona, uyir poga vendiyadhu dhan. Adhu dhan nee panna."
tamil_dialogues: Veera: 'Maran, enakku poy sonna uyir poga maatum pola irukku.'
Maran: 'Nee en friend illada!'
Veera: 'Friend dhan — atha dhan unmai solren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside a yak, whose hair falls in the breeze.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yak maadhiri — athukku mudi pona uyir pogum. Adhu pola maanam pona, uyir poga vendiyadhu dhan."
thirukkural: மயிர்நீப்பின் வாழாக் கவரிமா அன்னார் உயிர்நீப்பர் மானம் வரின்.
english_translation: Honour lost, the noble expire Like a yak that loses its hair.
moral: The noble would rather die than lose their honour — like a yak without its hair.

title: The Broken Pot
hook: Selvi accidentally breaks a prized pot but her lie is caught — Thatha shows her how the whole village respects truth.
story: Selvi was playing in the pottery shed. She knocked over Thatha's prize pot. It shattered. "Thatha, poonai dhan udacha." Thatha looked at her. "Selvi, andha velai karedukku naama perusa irukkom. Nee poy sonna — namma maanam pogum." The neighbors gathered. Thatha said loudly, "En pethi pot udainjadhuku, adha sari pannuva." Everyone clapped. Selvi cried. "Thatha, naan dhan udachen." Thatha hugged her. "Ippo dhan namma maanam theriyudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'Thatha, nalla peru illadha iruntha enna?'
Thatha: 'Nalla peru illadha iruntha, namma olimaiyum illa. Ulagu nammala vanangadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the potter's wheel, shaping a new pot.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Maanam irukravanai ulagu vanangum. Sariya irukradhu dhan periya visayam. Adhu dhan ullagam adukkum."
thirukkural: இளிவரின் வாழாத மானம் உடையார் ஒளிதொழுது ஏத்தும் உலகு.
english_translation: Their light the world adores and hails Who will not live when honour fails.
moral: Those who cannot bear dishonour are worshipped by the entire world.

title: Lunchbox Laugh
hook: A richer boy laughs at Karthi's small lunchbox — but Appa teaches him real greatness.
story: Karthi opened his lunchbox — two chapatis, no side dish. Gowtham from the next bench laughed loud. "Idha saapdra? Naa biriyani vachirukken!" Karthi's ears burned. He came home sulking. Appa was weaving palm baskets. "Karthi, avan saapdra porulai paathu chirikka — adhu avan perumai illa. Nee enna pandradhu, adhu dhan un perumai. Nee nalla padikkura, adhu un veetu perumai."
tamil_dialogues: Karthi: 'Appa, avan en lunchbox paathu chirichaan. Naa avana adikka venum pola irukku.'
Appa: 'Vidu da. Chirichavan chirichu povaane — aana un perumai un kooda irukkum. Manasu irundha podhum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a palm tree, weaving a basket beside Karthi's Appa.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karthi, perumai na sollala. Un veetla unakku kudutha saapadu, appa seiyra velai — ava perumai adhu. Unna chirikka oruthan — adhu avan azhivu."
thirukkural: ஒளிஒருவற்கு உள்ள வெறுக்கை இளிஒருவற்கு அஃதிறந்து வாழ்தும் எனல்.
english_translation: A heart of courage lives in light; devoid of that one's life is night.
moral: True greatness is courage in the heart, not what's in your lunchbox.

title: Painter's Pride
hook: Muthu is ashamed his Thatha is a mud potter — until he sees what his hands create.
story: In school, they asked "What's your father's job?" Muthu's Thatha was a potter. His friend sneered, "Mannu vachu panam sambaadhikara." Muthu felt small. That evening, Thatha was at the wheel, mud spinning, taking shape. A beautiful kalasham emerged. "Thatha, inga ellam unga kai velai dhan!" Thatha smiled. "Edhu velai illa — namma kai dhan. Atha mathi paathavaru periyavan. Avan kaala dhadichavan illa."
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Thatha, avanga namma velai pathi fun pannanga.'
Thatha: 'Velaiya paathu chirippavan — avan sirippu avan kaiyala dhan. Namma velai namma perumai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the potter's wheel, hands covered in wet clay.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirappu ellam ore maadhiri. Aana velai dhan perumaiya therivikkum. Un thatha kai maadiri — adhu dhan perumai."
thirukkural: பிறப்பொக்கும் எல்லா உயிர்க்கும் சிறப்பொவ்வா செய்தொழில் வேற்றுமை யான்.
english_translation: All beings are the same in birth; but work decides their varied worth.
moral: All are born equal; it's the work you do that determines your greatness.

title: The Boy Who Read to the Servant
hook: Pavithra's classmate ignores the servant, but another boy shares his snack and book with her.
story: Pavithra noticed two things at her new apartment complex. Vishal shouted at the maid "Dei, inga vaa!" Karthik, the colony topper, sat beside the same maid, sharing his murukku and reading a comic aloud. "Chinnatha, idha paaru — kalyanaraman!" Pavithra was confused. "Karthik, ava velaiya paaru, avaloda padikkira?" Karthik smiled. "Avalum namma maadhiri dhan pa. Velai mattum dhan vithiyasam."
tamil_dialogues: Pavithra: 'Avala koottama padikkira? School la ellaam chirippanga?'
Karthik: 'Chirichavanga dhan kevalam. Avalukku theriyadhadhu nama solli kuduka vendiyadhu. Adhu dhan perumai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the colony swing, a book open on his lap.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mela irundha mathiri theriyalam, aana ullae nallavadhu illa. Keezha irundhavar dhan periyavar. Adhu dhan sila perukku mattum varum."
thirukkural: மேலிருந்தும் மேலல்லார் மேலல்லர் கீழிருந்தும் கீழல்லார் கீழல்லவர்.
english_translation: Ignoble high not high they are; the noble low not low they fare.
moral: Those in high places may not be great; those in low places may be the noblest.

title: The Marble Champion
hook: Surya wins a marble game by cheating — his sister makes him return the prize.
story: Surya was the street marble champion. But in the final round, he flicked his opponent's marble with his nail — no one saw. He won 20 marbles. At home, Akka noticed his guilty face. "Enna achu? Kai yenna vachirukka?" Surya showed the marbles. "Naa... naa mosam senjen akka." Akka took his hand. "Perumai na, venradhu un kai kattai la illa. Nee andha goli ah thirumbi kudu. Unna periyavana aakradhu — adhu dhan perumai."
tamil_dialogues: Surya: 'Akka, avaru paathudla. Naa gothu senjen.'
Akka: 'Unakku theriyudhu illa — adhu dhan perumai. Po ippo thirumbi kudu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the marble circle, holding a single glass marble up to the light.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periya manasu na — nammale namma kappadradhu. Surya mosam seidhadhu theriyudhu — adhu perumai illa. Nera solli thappa senjadha thirumbi kudukradhu — adhu dhan perumai."
thirukkural: ஒருமை மகளிரே போலப் பெருமையும் தன்னைத்தான் கொண்டொழுகின் உண்டு.
english_translation: Greatness like woman's chastity is guarded by self-veracity.
moral: True greatness is like a woman's honor — guarded only by yourself, for yourself.

title: The Fallen Puppy
hook: A puppy falls into the deep fort moat — everyone says it's impossible to save.
story: The little pup yelped from the dry moat — 15 feet deep. A crowd gathered. "Saadhikkaadha vishayam. Po vaaya." Deepa tugged Appa's shirt. "Appa, athu sethudum." Appa looked around. He found an old gunny sack and a rope. "Nee kai kattu Deepa. Naan varren." He tied the sack to the rope, climbed down carefully, wrapped the trembling pup. The crowd cheered. "Enakku theriyum appa, unnal mudiyum nu!"
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Appa, yellam avana vida sollranga. Aana unnal mudiyuma?'
Appa: 'Mudiyadhadhu na sollama, senju paarkradhu dhan perumai Deepa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the moat edge, the wet gunny sack over his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periyavanga dhan kastamaana kaaryatha senju kaatraanga. Deepa appa maadiri — adhu dhan perumai. Yaaralum mudiyum nu nenaikradhu."
thirukkural: பெருமையுடையவர் ஆற்றுவார் ஆற்றின் அருமையுடைய செயல்.
english_translation: Great souls when their will is active do mighty deeds rare to achieve.
moral: Great people can do the impossible when they put their mind to it.

title: The Friendship Test
hook: Naveen wants to be friends with the "cool guys" until his uncle shows him real greatness.
story: The cool group wore fancy clothes, had phones. Naveen wanted in. "Avanunga kooda serntha dhan nalla irukkum." One day, the cool group teased a deaf boy. Naveen laughed along — inside it pinched. Periyappa saw everything. "Naveen, indha pasanga periyavanga illa. Periyavar yaaru theriyuma? Andha sevadhi payyan — avanoda amma kai pidichu kathula solli kudutha. Avan avan ammava periyavan nu nenaikuraan."
tamil_dialogues: Naveen: 'Periyappa, avanga dhan cool-aa irukanga.'
Periyappa: 'Cool vera perumai vera. Oruvanukku ullae enna irukku — adhu dhan perumai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the temple tank, watching the deaf boy smile at his mother.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sithaiya irukravanga periyavanga kooda sera nenaika maataanga. Periyavangala thedi seravanga. Adhu dhan perumai."
thirukkural: சிறியார் உணர்ச்சியுள் இல்லை பெரியாரைப் பேணிக்கொள்வேம் என்னும் நோக்கு.
english_translation: The petty-natured ones have not the mind to seek and befriend the great.
moral: Small minds never seek out great people — greatness knows greatness.

title: The New Bicycle Bully
hook: A rich boy flaunts his new cycle and bullies others — until his mother discovers how he treats the watchman's son.
story: The new boy Aarav had a shiny blue cycle with gears. He cycled past Arun and splashed muddy water. "Road la irundhu maari da!" Arun's clothes were ruined. That evening, Aarav's mother saw him kicking the watchman's son's cycle. "Aarav! Vaa inga." She made him call Arun. "Nee inna senjaaru?" Aarav looked down. "Sorry." Mother said: "Nee cycle vangi periyavana aagala. Nee solla kudukradhu dhan un perumai. Nalla iru."
tamil_dialogues: Aarav: 'Amma, avan cycle kooda illa.'
Amma: 'Adhu avan azhivu illa, un azhivu. Cycle la perumai illa — manasula dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans against the colony wall, watching the muddy cycle tracks dry.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Perumai thisai mariyadhai kuduthu oruvarukku kuduka vendiyadhu. Aarav valaiyadha perumai adhu perumai illa."
thirukkural: இறப்பே புரிந்த தொழிற்றாம் சிறப்புந்தான் சீரல்லவர்கண் படின்.
english_translation: The base with power and opulence wax with deeds of insolence.
moral: Small people with power only become arrogant — true greatness is humble.

title: The Winner Who Apologized
hook: Priya wins the running race unfairly, and the coach's silence makes her confess.
story: Priya touched the finish line first. But she had cut the corner while the teacher wasn't looking. The medal felt heavy. Coach came to her. "Azhaga odina Priya." He didn't say more. Priya couldn't sleep. Next morning, she went to Coach. "Sir, naa short-cut la odinen. Enaku medal vendam." Coach smiled. "Ippo dhan nee jeichu saamthiya. Unna vida periya vishayam — un azhivu sammathichadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Coach sir, medal vaangi periyavana aagala na.'
Coach: 'Periyavan yaaru theriyuma? Thappu senjadha oppukravan. Nee ippo dhan jeichu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the school bench, holding the returned medal in his open palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Perumai na — thannai thaan thalai kattikiradu. Jeyichu thaan nikka vendam. Thappu senju, adha oppu kuda venaama. Priya ippo nijama periyaval."
thirukkural: பணியுமாம் என்றும் பெருமை சிறுமை அணியுமாம் தன்னை வியந்து.
english_translation: Greatness bends with modesty; meanness vaunts with vanity.
moral: Greatness bows down in humility; smallness puffs itself up with pride.

title: The Project That Failed
hook: Yogesh's science project fails — his rival's succeeds, and his mother teaches him how to lose.
story: Yogesh spent a month on his solar water heater model. On fair day, his tube burst. Everyone laughed. His rival Vikram's model won first place. Yogesh wanted to break Vikram's model. Mother pulled him aside. "Yogesh, thottavanukku perumai illa — aana tholviya thanga therinja dhan periya manasu. Po, vikram kai kulu." Yogesh cried. But he walked over. "Congrats da." Vikram was shocked. "Thanks da, naan unakku help pannurean next time."
tamil_dialogues: Yogesh: 'Amma, avan munnadi kai kudukka? Naa thottuta.'
Amma: 'Tholviya thanga theriyadhadhu — adhu dhan periya tholvi. Po kai kudu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the two boys, their hands clasped in a handshake.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periyavarukku perumai illadha dhaiva seiyya. Avaruku ullae perumai irukka koodadhu. Yogesh tholviya thanginaan — adhu dhan perumai."
thirukkural: பெருமை பெருமிதம் இன்மை சிறுமை பெருமிதம் ஊர்ந்துவிடல்.
english_translation: Greatness is free from insolence; littleness swells with that offence.
moral: True greatness has no arrogance; only small minds boast.

title: The Torn Notebook
hook: A jealous girl tears Meenakshi's notebook — but Chithi tells her to hide the fault, not fight.
story: Meenakshi found her science notebook torn — page 7 missing. She knew who: Kokila, who always came second. Meenakshi wanted to slap her. Chithi was selling fish at the market. "Enna mukkanjam?" Meenakshi showed the torn book. "Kokila dhan seidha." Chithi didn't get angry. "Avala pakka solla vendam. Nee adha cover panni, oru puthu page potu. Aval siripu unna periyavala aakum. Adha cover panni vaithu dhan perumai."
tamil_dialogues: Meenakshi: 'Chithi, avala sirikka veikkanum. Enna pannanum?'
Chithi: 'Onnum panna vendam. Nee cover panni vei. Appo dhan avanga sirichavanga avanga aaguvanga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the fish market, neatly taping a torn page back into a notebook.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Periyavanga matha kurai maraikka pappanga. Sithaiya irukravanga dhan kuraiya thedi solvanga. Meenakshi, nee cover panni vei. Adhu dhan perumai."
thirukkural: அற்றம் மறைக்கும் பெருமை சிறுமைதான் குற்றமே கூறிவிடும்.
english_translation: Weakness of others greatness screens; smallness defects alone proclaims.
moral: Greatness covers the faults of others; smallness broadcasts them.

title: The Auto Fare
theme: Perfect Excellence / All Goodness Is Duty
hook: Divya's father pays the auto driver extra even when he overcharged — and the driver returns it.
story: The auto driver charged 50 rupees for a 30-rupee ride. Appa paid 50 without arguing. Divya was furious. "Appa! avan mosam pannuraan! Enna neenga kudukkareenga?" Appa smiled. "Oru nalla kaaryam pannradhu namaku kadamai Divya. Avan odhadha vendiyadhu illa. Aana naan nallavana irukka vendiyadhu." Next day, the auto driver came home. "Saar, nethiku kudutha 50, 30 dhan — idhu 20." He returned it. Divya's jaw dropped.
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Appa, avan thirudi vaangina mathiri. Neenga yen siricha?'
Appa: 'Nalladhu pannradhu namaku kadamai. Avan eduthukitta — avan kadamai avan kitta.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stop, the returned 20-rupee note in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla kaaryatha kadamai nu nenaikradhu dhan saandraanmai. Intha Appa maadiri — avan nalladhu panninaan, eadhirpaaru illa."
thirukkural: கடன்என்ப நல்லவை எல்லாம் கடன்அறிந்து சான்றாண்மை மேற்கொள்பவர்க்கு.
english_translation: All goodness is duty to them who are dutiful and sublime.
moral: For the truly excellent, every good deed is simply their duty — no reward needed.

title: The Lost Wallet
theme: Perfect Excellence / Character Is the Only Good
hook: Bala finds a wallet full of money and his friend pressures him to keep it.
story: A brown wallet lay on the sand. Bala opened it — 2000 rupees, an ID card. Santhanam's eyes lit up. "Dei, kaathal dhan! Nama ice-cream saapidalaam!" Bala held the wallet tight. "Adhu avan veetu saapaadu da." Santhanam argued. "Yaarum paakala! Eduthuko!" Bala remembered what his mentor said — a good name is the only wealth. He found the address on the ID — a widow's home. He returned it. She cried. "Daivam maadiri vandha da."
tamil_dialogues: Santhanam: 'Unakku piththu da? Eduthuko! Yaarum theriyadhu!'
Bala: 'Enakku theriyum. Adhu dhan pothum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the beach, the returned wallet beside a grateful old woman.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Saandraanmai na — pandradhu dhan. Bala avan ullae nallavana irukkuraan. Adhu dhan avanoda nijamana selavu."
thirukkural: குணநலம் சான்றோர் நலனே பிறநலம் எந்நலத்துள்ளதூஉம் அன்று.
english_translation: Good in the great is character; than that there is nothing better.
moral: For the excellent, character is the only good — nothing else compares.

title: Five Pillars
theme: Perfect Excellence / Five Pillars of Excellence
hook: Anitha's amma says five things make a great person — Anitha tests herself.
story: Amma was drawing kolam at the temple entrance. "Saandraanmakku anja thoon irukku — anbu, naanam, oppuravu, kannottam, vaaimai." Anitha counted on her fingers. "Anbu — love; naanam — shy; oppuravu — sharing; kannottam — kindness; vaaimai — truth." She looked at her reflection. "Amma, enakku ithu ellam irukka?" Amma laughed. "Unakku theriyala? Nee naai ku roti potu kodukka — adhu oppuravu. Nee siriya payyana adikkaama vida — adhu kannottam."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Amma, enakku ithu ellam illa pola.'
Amma: 'Iruku — unna pathi unakku theriyadhu. Nee seiyradhu dhan saandraanmai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the kolam, sprinkling rice flour in a perfect line.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anja thoon — anbu, naanam, oppuravu, kannottam, vaaimai. Ithu vachirukavan dhan saandraanmai. Nee seiya veikkira — adhu dhan."
thirukkural: அன்புநாண் ஒப்புரவு கண்ணோட்டம் வாய்மையொடு ஐந்துசால் ஊன்றிய தூண்.
english_translation: Love, truth, regard, modesty, grace — these five are virtue's resting place.
moral: Love, modesty, generosity, kindness, truthfulness — these five are the pillars of excellence.

title: The Teasing Uncle
theme: Perfect Excellence / Not Killing, Not Slandering
hook: A shopkeeper teases Sanjay for being skinny — his mother teaches him not to retaliate.
story: "Dei, enna saapdara? Ezhumbu kaati?!" The lock shop uncle laughed. Sanjay's face burned. He wanted to shout back — "Un ponnunga ellam..." but Mother stopped his mouth. "Vaa Sanjay." At home, Sanjay sulked. "Amma, avan enna kozhuppu sirichaan! Naan avan...!" Mother put her hand on his head. "Sanjay, avan kuraiya solradhu — adhu avandha perumai illa. Nee adha sirichu vida — adhu dhan un perumai. Vera yaara kuraiya solla vendam, adhu dhan saandraanmai."
tamil_dialogues: Sanjay: 'Amma, avan enna kalaichaan! Naa avan...'
Amma: 'Avan kalaicha — avan azhivu. Nee adha thangina — un perumai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the lock shop, running his finger along a rusty key.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kolladhadhu dhan nandra. Vera kuraiya solladhadhu dhan saandraanmai. Sanjay, nee kettadhe illa. Nee dhan periyavan."
thirukkural: கொல்லா நலத்தது நோன்மை பிறர்தீமை சொல்லா நலத்தது சால்பு.
english_translation: Not to kill is penance pure; not to slander virtue sure.
moral: Not killing is goodness; not speaking ill of others is true excellence.

title: The Strongest Apology
hook: Velu accidentally breaks a cricket bat and the owner attacks him — but Velu's apology stops a fight.
story: Velu's straight drive cracked the bat. Guna roared and pushed him. "En thaaiyoda vangi kudu!" The whole ground went silent. Velu's fists clenched. Everyone expected a fight. But Velu took a breath, walked to Guna, folded his hands. "Sorry da. Naan veetla solli, appa kitta vaangi kuduthuruven." Guna froze. The anger drained. "Sorry da... naan dhan over-aa pesitten." The teacher watched from the window. "Periya manasu, Velu."
tamil_dialogues: Guna: 'Nee kettu pona bat ah thiruppi kudukka mudiyuma?'
Velu: 'Sorry da. Naa thinama padikkala. Aana innikki naa kai kudutha — adhe pothum nu nenakara.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the cricket pitch, holding a cracked bat like a trophy.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Saandraanmai na — avan kai kudukuraan. Poradradhu illa. Velu avanoda bala adhu. Avan thaan periyavan."
thirukkural: ஆற்றுவார் ஆற்றல் பணிதல்; அதுசான்றோர் மாற்றாரை மாற்றும் படை.
english_translation: Humility is valour's strength; a force that averts foes at length.
moral: True strength lies in bowing down — humility is the weapon that disarms enemies.

title: The Defeat That Won
hook: Lakshmi loses a game to a younger girl and cries — Paati says losing well is the real touchstone.
story: Lakshmi always won at pallanguzhi. But the new girl, 6-year-old Pooja, beat her. Lakshmi threw the stones. "Naa odala! Inga irundhu poren." She ran to the beach. Paati followed slowly, sat beside her. "Lakshmi, tholvi enna theriyuma? Adhu oru proof. Yaar periyavan nu theriyradhu tholvi la dhan. Nee thotta nalla iru. Adhu dhan saandraanmai."
tamil_dialogues: Lakshmi: 'Paati, avanakitta thotta! Avan enna vida chinna.'
Paati: 'Tholvi la dhan yaaru periyavaru nu theriyum. Nee nalla thotta — adhu periya visayam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the beach sand, drawing a pallanguzhi board with his finger.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Saandraanmaikku kanakku — tholvi thanga theriyadha ille. Apdi thanginavan dhan periyavan. Lakshmi adhe pannala."
thirukkural: சால்பிற்குக் கட்டளை யாதெனின் தோல்வி துலையல்லார் கண்ணும் கொளல்.
english_translation: To bear repulse e'en from the mean is the touchstone of worthy men.
moral: The test of true excellence is accepting defeat — even from someone lesser than you.

title: The Hated Teacher
theme: Perfect Excellence / Returning Good for Evil
hook: Kumar hates his strict teacher who beat him — mother asks him to give kindness back.
story: Teacher slapped Kumar for not doing homework. Kumar hated her. "Avanga dhan enna kizhichanga. Ava kitta naan oru nalla vaartha solla maaten." Mother listened. "Kumar, innikki school la nee advanced dhan — saapadu edukara avanukku kudu." Kumar was confused. "Enna amma? Ava enna adicha!" Mother smiled. "Unna adicha — adha marandhu, nee avan dhan nallavana iru. Adhu dhan saandraanmai palagudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Amma, ava mattum dhan enakku kastama irukku!'
Amma: 'Avanukku nalladhu pannu. Appo dhan nee periyavan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, a small tiffin box in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Saandraanmai na — avan dhan iniyadha seiyradhu. Kumar, avan kitta avanukku iniyadha pannu. Appo kannottam vantudum."
thirukkural: இன்னாசெய்தார்க்கும் இனியவே செய்யாக்கால் என்ன பயத்ததோ சால்பு.
english_translation: Of perfection what is the gain if it returns not joy for pain?
moral: What use is excellence if you cannot return good to those who hurt you?

title: The Old School Bag
theme: Perfect Excellence / Poverty Is No Shame
hook: Jeeva's friends mock his old torn bag — his father tells him true shame is losing character.
story: The bag was torn at the corner — Jeeva's father stitched it with thick white thread. Friends laughed. "Enna da, un bag-a pathiye theriyala! Teri enna da adhu?" Jeeva hid his face. That evening, Father came from work, hands rough. "Enna da, bag sari illaya?" Jeeva couldn't lie. "Appa, avanga sirikkranga." Father sat down. "Jeeva, un kaiyila irukka paisa illa — adhu vehkam illa. Nee nallavan illa — adhu dhan vehkam. Un bag pathi sirikkavan sirichu pova."
tamil_dialogues: Jeeva: 'Appa, ella pasanga new bag vachirukanga.'
Appa: 'Nee nallavan illa nu yaarum sonna dhan vehkam. Bag illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stand, the old stitched bag over his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Saandraanmaikku ezhimai vehkam illa. Jeeva nallavan — adhu dhan saandraanmai. Bag illa."
thirukkural: இன்மை ஒருவற்கு இளிவன்று சால்பென்னும் திண்மை உண்டாகப் பெறின்.
english_translation: No shame there is in poverty to one strong in good quality.
moral: Poverty is no shame for one who possesses the strength of true excellence.

title: The Festival That Never Changed
hook: Janani sees her uncle give alms to the same beggar for ten years — never missing a day.
story: The same old beggar sat at the same temple gate. Every morning, Periyappa gave him 10 rupees. Janani asked, "Periyappa, ivarukku daily kudukkareenga? Adhe aalu, adhe idam?" Periyappa smiled. "Aamam janani. Naalu vayasu irundhu. Ippadi 10 varsham achu." Janani was amazed. "Oru naalum thappula?" "Thappala. Oru nalla kaaryatha vidama pannanum. Adhu dhan saandraanmai. Ullam vidama irukkuravan."
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Periyappa, avar veetla iruppaara? Yen daily kudukkareenga?'
Periyappa: 'Adhu periyavaroda urudhi. Nalladhu seiyradhula maatama irukanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the old beggar at the temple gate, a coin in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaalam maaralam — aana saandraanmai maaraadhu. Periyappa maadiri — oru nalla kaaryatha uruthiya pannina dhan periyavar."
thirukkural: ஊழி பெயரினும் தாம்பெயரார் சான்றாண்மைக்கு ஆழி எனப்படுவார்.
english_translation: Aeons may change but not the seer who is a sea of virtue pure.
moral: True excellence never changes — even as ages pass, the great remain steadfast.

title: The Missing Temple Donation
theme: Perfect Excellence / The World Rests on the Good
hook: The temple donation box is found empty and everyone blames the poor boy — until the truth comes out.
story: The hundial was empty. The crowd pointed at Kannan, the tea-shop boy. "Avandha dhan! Ezhai payyan!" Kannan was dragged. Ganesh watched, heart pounding. He knew Kannan — never stole a single tea powder packet. Ganesh stepped forward. "Samidhaarayya, Kannan seiyala. Naan paathen — naalu per veera vizha la kolla vandha aalu eduthaanga." The priest investigated — it was true. The crowd fell silent. "Oru nallavan irundha dhan ulagam nilkudhu Ganesh."
tamil_dialogues: Ganesh: 'Kannan seiyala, enakku theriyum. Naan solren.'
Priest: 'Oru nallavan unmai sollumbodhu — adhu dhan ulagatha kaakudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the empty hundial, his hand on Kannan's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Saandraanmai ulagatha thaan suthikiradu. Kannan saandraanmaiya irundhaan — Ganesh adha kaapadraan. Ithu dhan ulaga kadamai."
thirukkural: சான்றவர் சான்றாண்மை குன்றின் இருநிலந்தான் தாங்காது மன்னோ பொறை.
english_translation: The world will not more bear its weight if from high virtue fall the great.
moral: If the excellent lose their virtue, the world itself cannot bear its weight.

title: The New Girl's Bench
hook: A new girl sits alone at lunch because she doesn't speak Tamil well — Kavin shares his food with gestures.
story: Nithya had come from Mumbai. She knew no Tamil. Lunch break, everyone chatted in groups. She sat alone with her lunchbox. Kavin noticed. He walked over, pointed at her box, then his. "Saapdu... sama... epdi?" She smiled. "I don't understand." Kavin used hand gestures — eat, okay, good. She laughed and opened her box. Soon the whole table laughed without words. The teacher smiled. "Kavin, nee nallavan. Aramba pannikodukradhu dhan nalla panbu."
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Unakku Tamil theriyadha? Parava illa. Idha paaru — (gestures) — saapdu!'
Nithya: (laughing) 'You're funny. Thank you.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the lunch table, hands gesturing a friendly "come eat."
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Panbu — adhu mudhal yeppavum. Aramba pannikodukradhu dhan panbu. Kavin avan kai yaathu kaatinaan. Adhu dhan nalla panbu."
thirukkural: எண்பதத்தால் எய்தல் எளிதென்ப யார்மாட்டும் பண்புடைமை என்னும் வழக்கு.
english_translation: To the polite free of access easily comes courteousness.
moral: Courtesy makes you approachable to anyone — it's the easiest path to connect.
