title: The Watchman's Grandson
hook: Swathi is told not to play with the watchman's grandson — but mother disagrees.
story: Swathi's friends warned her. "Avan kai ellam kara. Avan appa dhan kaaval kaaran." Swathi avoided him. The boy, Mani, sat alone. Mother visited. "Swathi, avan kooda vilayaada?" Swathi looked down. "Avanga sonnanga... avan dhan kaaval kaaran payyan." Mother's face changed. "Swathi, intha panbu nalladhu illa. Anbu iruntha dhan panbu. Nee ovvoru manushanaiyum mathika. Adhu dhan periya kudumbathula pirandhadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Swathi: 'Amma, avanga sollranga — avan kooda vilayaada vendam nu.'
Amma: 'Un manasula enna irukku — adhunna panbu. Avan matha maadhiri dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the compound wall between Swathi and Mani, swinging his legs.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Panbu na — anbu, nalla kudumbam. Swathi amma sonna maadiri — ella manushanukkum mariyadhai. Adhu dhan panbu."
thirukkural: அன்புடைமை ஆன்ற குடிப்பிறத்தல் இவ்விரண்டும் பண்புடைமை என்னும் வழக்கு.
english_translation: Humanity and noble birth develop courtesy and moral worth.
moral: Love and being born into a good family — these two create true courtesy.

title: The Rich Uncle's Gift
hook: Rajesh's rich uncle gives him a cheap toy while his cousin gets an expensive one — uncle teaches him a lesson.
story: The big box came from Dubai. Cousin Vikram got a Playstation. Rajesh got a small car that rolled on the floor. He tried to hide his disappointment. Uncle sat beside him. "Rajesh, un mukhathula enna theriyudhu? Unakku idhu pothaadha?" Rajesh stayed silent. Uncle smiled. "Un cousin kitta adhu irukku — aana un kitta enna irukku? Nee sirichu avan kooda vilayaada. Adhu dhan periya panbu. Porulai paakadha — manasa paaru."
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Uncle, avanukku playstation — enakku idhu mattum?'
Uncle: 'Rajesh, un panbu adhe maadhiri irukka vendam. Nee kuduthadhai vaangi, athoda santhosha iru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the living room, the small toy car on his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Udal ore maadhiri iruka vendam — aana panbu ore maadhiri irukanum. Rajesh, avanapadi panbu nalla.
thirukkural: உறுப்பொத்தல் மக்களொப்பு அன்றால் வெறுத்தக்க பண்பொத்தல் ஒப்பதாம் ஒப்பு.
english_translation: Likeness in limbs is not likeness; it's likeness in kind courteousness.
moral: Looking alike doesn't make you alike; it's courtesy that makes two people truly similar.

title: The Jilebi Lady
hook: Vignesh sees an old jilebi vendor treat a beggar kindly — one act changes his worldview.
story: The old lady made jilebis at the beach. A beggar came, hand stretched. "Amma, pasi." People shooed him away. But Amma took a fresh jilebi, cooled it with her breath, and gave it. "Saapdu ma." No money. Vignesh watched. "Saravanan, avanga kaiya vida mattanga illa, jilebi kudukranga." Saravanan shrugged. Vignesh went close. "Amma, yen avarukku kuduthinga?" Amma smiled. "Pasi iruntha dhan koduka vendiyadhu. Adhu dhan namma panbu. Nee padicha aparam nalla aalu aaviya?"
tamil_dialogues: Vignesh: 'Amma, avar kitta paisaa illa. Yen jilebi kuduthinga?'
Amma: 'Pasi paathu kodukradhu dhan panbu, ma. Porula paathu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the beach wall, eating a piece of jilebi, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla panbu — porula iruka vendam. Pasi iruntha koduka vendiyadhu. Jilebi amma maadiri — adhu dhan nalla panbu."
thirukkural: நயனொடு நன்றி புரிந்த பயனுடையார் பண்புபாராட்டும் உலகு.
english_translation: The world applauds those helpful men whose actions are just and benign.
moral: The world praises those whose courtesy comes from kindness and a helpful heart.

title: The Joke That Hurt
hook: Dinesh makes a joke about his friend's father's job — it's not funny, it's cruel.
story: "Dei, un appa theril otha kudhirai maadiri!" Everyone laughed. But Kumar's face fell. His father was a cycle rickshaw puller. Dinesh laughed too — but Sister saw the whole thing. "Dinesh, namma appa enna pannuraaru?" Dinesh froze. Appa was also a coolie. "Nee andha joke potta — adhu namma appa va sirikka maadhiri dhan." Dinesh ran to Kumar. "Sorry da. Naa apdi sollirkka koodadhu." Kumar hugged him. "Podhum da."
tamil_dialogues: Sister: 'Dinesh, un appa enna pannuraaru? Andha joke — un appavai sirikka maadhiri irundha.'
Dinesh: 'Sorry da Kumar. Naa... enakku theriyama...'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by a cycle rickshaw, his hand on the handlebar.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sirichalum panbu irukanum. Vera aalu kastama irundha — adhe sirikkradhu thappu. Dinesh ippo puriya kuduthadhu dhan nalla panbu."
thirukkural: நகையுள்ளும் இன்னா திகழ்ச்சி பகையுள்ளும் பண்புள பாடறிவார் மாட்டு.
english_translation: The courteous don't even foes detest; for contempt offends even in jest.
moral: A courteous person won't hurt even in jest — true courtesy never leaves you, even in laughter.

title: The Collapsing Wall
theme: Courtesy / The World Rests on Courtesy
hook: A storm knocks down the compound wall — but the neighbor rebuilds it without being asked.
story: The cyclone had passed. Maha's compound wall had fallen. They had no money to fix it. That afternoon, Uncle from next door came with cement and bricks. "Seri, namma saadikalaam." Whole day he worked. Maha's mother cried. "Uncle, unakku enna kuduka?" Uncle wiped his sweat. "Enna kuduka? Pakkathu veetukku saadichaa dhan namma veetukkum saadikkum. Panbu illa naa ulagam veedhum. Adhu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Maha: 'Uncle, yen enga veetuku saadikringa?'
Uncle: 'Panbu dhan ulagatha saadikkudhu ma. Panbu illama namma vala mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the repaired wall, a trowel in one hand, brick in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Panbu dhan ulagatha kappadradhu. Uncle maadiri — pakkathu veetukku saadicha dhan namma veetukkum kaapadu. Adhu dhan."
thirukkural: பண்புடையார்ப் பட்டுண்டு உலகம்; அதுஇன்றேல் மண்புக்கு மாய்வது மன்.
english_translation: The world rests with the mannered best; or it crumbles and falls to dust.
moral: The world stands only because of courteous people; without them, everything falls apart.

title: The Sharp Boy Who Had No Friends
theme: Courtesy / Sharpness Without Manners Is Useless
hook: Prasanna is the class topper but no one wants to be his partner — teacher tells him why.
story: Prasanna always came first. But group projects — no one picked him. He sat alone. "Sir, enna teacher? Naan dhan topper!" Teacher smiled. "Prasanna, unakku theriyuma? Nee oru arivaal maadiri — koora. Aana andha arivaal kaiya vecha dhan cut aagum. Nee oru maram maadiri irukanum — ella sernthu valanum. Unakku panbu illa. Adhu dhan unakku friend illa."
tamil_dialogues: Prasanna: 'Teacher, naan dhan topper! Avan enna kooda sera maatikiraan?'
Teacher: 'Topper irundha mattum pothum. Panbu iruntha dhan ella un kooda varaanga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the classroom, a sharp pencil in one hand and a blunt one in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Arivaal mathiri koorama irukalam — aana athu cut pannum. Maram mathiri valaindha dhan — athu nizhal tharum. Panbu illa na — yaar un kooda illa."
thirukkural: அரம்போலும் கூர்மையரேனும் மரம்போல்வர் மக்கட்பண்பு இல்லாதவர்.
english_translation: The mannerless though sharp like file are like wooden blocks indocile.
moral: Even the sharpest mind is useless without courtesy — you're like a wooden log, not a human.

title: The Boy Who Spit
theme: Courtesy / Courtesy Even to the Rude
hook: A man spits near Kokila and shouts at her — mother says courtesy is needed most with rude people.
story: The man chewed tobacco, spat near Kokila's foot. "Dei, pakkam nillu!" Kokila's blood boiled. "Nee dhan...!" Mother's hand covered her mouth. "Vaanga, bus varudhu." On the bus, Kokila fumed. "Amma, avan enna kozhuppa sirichaan, kai kaati! Avan kitta naan oru nalla vaartha solla...!" Mother said calmly. "Kokila, avanai vida nee nallavana iru. Avan kitta kooda nalla irukanum. Adhu dhan panbu."
tamil_dialogues: Kokila: 'Amma, avan unmaiyana kozhuppu! Naa avan kitta...!'
Amma: 'Avan unmaiyana kozhuppu — aana nee unmaiyana nallaval. Atha vida vendam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stop, offering the rude man a cup of tea.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Panbu — athu nallavanga kitta mattum ille. Kettavanga kitta kooda panbu irukanum. Apdi irundha dhan nijamana panbu."
thirukkural: நண்பாற்றார் ஆகி நயமில செய்வார்க்கும் பண்பாற்றார் ஆதல் கடை.
english_translation: Discourtesy is mean indeed e'en to a base unfriendly breed.
moral: Being discourteous even to rude people is the lowest behavior — courtesy must never be lost.

title: The Dark Room
theme: Courtesy / A Smile Lights the World
hook: A power cut darkens the entire colony — Valli's smile lights up her mother's world.
story: The lights went out. Everyone groaned. Valli's mother was stressed — no money for the bill either. Valli felt the darkness. She found a candle, lit it. Then she went to mother, hugged her tight. "Amma, kavalai padaadha. Naan irukkena?" Mother's eyes welled. "Un siripu dhan enakku veLichcham, ma." The candle flickered but Valli's smile burned brighter. The neighbor later brought food — "Valli sonna, unga veetla light illa nu."
tamil_dialogues: Valli: 'Amma, light illa na enna? Naan irukkena?'
Amma: 'Un siripu dhan enakku periya veLichcham ma.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the dark room, a single candle between him and Valli.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Panbu na — andha siripu dhan. Valli sirichaa — andha veetuku veLichcham. Apdi irukka vendiyadhu. Siripu dhan periya veLichcham."
thirukkural: நகல்வல்லர் அல்லார்க்கு மாயிரு ஞாலம் பகலும்பாற்பட்டன்று இருள்.
english_translation: To those bereft of smiling light even in day the earth is night.
moral: For those who cannot smile, even the wide world is dark — a smile is the true light.

title: The Golden Spoon That Soured
hook: Ponni's rich cousin has everything but is rude — Thatha explains why her chappati tastes better.
story: Cousin Anand had a golden spoon, a big room, video games. Ponni had a small hut, mud floor, no toys. But when they ate together, Anand threw his food. "Sapadu sari illa!" Ponni's thatha made simple chappatis. Anand's mother asked, "Ponni, enna vishesham?" Ponni said, "En thoda saapda — adhu dhan vishesham." Thatha laughed. "Anand ku ellam irukku — aana thanthiram irukku. Ponni ku onnum illa — aana panbu irukku. Adhu dhan sapadai sugama aakudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Anand: 'Un saapadlam onnum illa! Naan biriyani saapduven!'
Ponni: 'Naan saapdradhu en amma kaadhal. Adhu dhan periya...'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the Chettinad dining table, breaking a simple chappati with his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Panbu iruntha dhan porulukku mathippu. Panbu illa na — porul ellam kettudhu. Thatha sonna maadiri — Ponni kaiyila panbu irukku. Adhu dhan periya selavu."
thirukkural: பண்பிலான் பெற்ற பெருஞ்செல்வம் நன்பால் கலந்தீமையால் திரிந்தற்று.
english_translation: The wealth heaped by the churlish base is pure milk soured by impure vase.
moral: Wealth in the hands of a discourteous person is like pure milk spoiled by a sour pot.

title: Appa's Locked Box
hook: Karthi finds a steel box full of gold coins his Appa never uses — not even for Amma's hospital bill.
story: Karthi's Appa had a box. Heavy. Locked. "Appa, amma hospital la irukkara, atha vaika vendama?" Appa looked down. "Adhu dhan apdi irukkanum ma." Later, the neighbor's daughter needed school fees. When Appa didn't help, Karthi broke the lock himself, took one coin, paid the fee. Appa was furious, then silent. "Nee dhan correct, ma."
tamil_dialogues: Karthi: 'Appa, intha gold enna pannum? Ada vaichaa dhan irukum?'
Appa: 'Ama da... adhu dhan problem. Naan dhan thappu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the steel box, legs crossed, looking at the rusted lock.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vaichaa vecha selva porula enna payan? Athai saapda vendum, mathavangalukum kuduka vendum. Illa naa setha maadhiri dhan."
thirukkural: வைத்தான்வாய் சான்ற பெரும்பொருள் அஃதுண்ணான் செத்தான் செயக்கிடந்தது இல்.
english_translation: Dead is he with wealth in pile Unenjoyed, it is futile
moral: Wealth you hoard but never use is the same as having nothing.

title: Uncle Miser's Funeral
hook: Meena's rich Periyappa dies — and they find his money rotting in gunny bags under his cot.
story: Periyappa lived alone. Never gave to anyone. "Setha pona enaku enna?" Meena tugged Thatha's veshti. "Thatha, periyappa ennikum saapda maatar?" After he died, they found bags of cash, moldy, eaten by rats. Thatha wept. "Selvam irundha, athai vazhkaikulla dhan anubavika vendum. Illa na, adhu kaasu illa — adhu kuppai."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Thatha, periyappa ennikum enna vituchu?'
Thatha: 'Moththam kaasu. Aana vida mudiyadha kaasu. Adhu than kastam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the rat-eaten room, a single currency note crumbling in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaasu irukku, adha anubavichaa dhan kaasu. Illana, adhu kagidha kasa maadhiri. Periyappa kuda kuda eduthutu poitaan."
thirukkural: பொருளானாம் எல்லாமென்று ஈயாது இவறும் மருளானாம் மாணாப் பிறப்பு.
english_translation: The niggard miser thinks wealth is all He hoards, gives not is born devil
moral: Money is only money when you use it; hoarding it wastes both wealth and life.

title: Bhoomi's Burden
hook: Bhoomi's rich neighbor owns three cars but sits on the porch all day, sad.
story: The mansion had a white ambassador car, never driven. "Paati, avaruku car irukku, aana drive pannra maatar." Paati whispered. "Avaruku periya veedu, periya car, aana santhosham illa. Enna payan?" Bhoomi walked to the gate. "Uncle, unga car la naan en school ku ponalama?" The old man's face lit up for the first time in years.
tamil_dialogues: Bhoomi: 'Uncle, car vaichaa dhan irukku, odraadhu?'
Uncle: 'Adhu dhan — naan yarukum kudukka vendam nu ninaichen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the back seat of the ambassador, rolling down the window.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Selvam irundhum adha anubavichaa dhan, adhu perumai. Illa na, adhu bhoomi mela oru bojjam dhan."
thirukkural: ஈட்டம் இவறி இசைவேண்டா ஆடவர் தோற்றம் நிலக்குப் பொறை.
english_translation: A burden he is to earth indeed Who hoards without a worthy deed
moral: A person who accumulates without sharing is just a burden on the earth.

title: The Lonely Mango Orchard
hook: Venba's grandfather planted a hundred mango trees but never let anyone eat the fruit.
story: "Appa, thatha nattu mango maram, aana ennikum naan saapdradhukku kuduthadhu illa!" The guard chased kids away. After Thatha died, the orchard was sold. The new owner opened the gates: "Ellarum vanga! Manga free!" Venba ate seven mangoes and cried for her Thatha. Appa hugged her. "Santhosham kuduka theriyadha selvam — adhu poi."
tamil_dialogues: Venba: 'Appa, thoattam irukku aana namakku manga kedaikadha?'
Appa: 'Ippo dhan theriyudhu — kuduthu santhosha padradhu dhan periya selvam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a mango tree, a ripe fruit falling into his open palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manga maram kai kudutha, athai mathavangalukum kuduka vendum. Illa na, adhu oru payanum illa."
thirukkural: எச்சமென்று என்எண்ணுங் கொல்லோ ஒருவரால் நச்சப் படாஅ தவன்.
english_translation: What legacy can he leave behind Who is for approach too unkind
moral: What legacy can you leave if no one even wants to come near you?

title: Muthu's Crores
hook: Muthu's father counts his money every night but won't spend a rupee on repairs.
story: The shop roof leaked. Customers dripped on. "Appa, roof pota vendama?" "Illada, kaasu mitham poganum." One night, rain destroyed all the cloth. Muthu watched his father howling at the sky. "Amma, appa kaasu eduthu vaichaaru, aana roof pota illa, ippo moththam pochu." Amma said nothing. Muthu learned: money not used destroys itself.
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Appa, ennikum roof pota vendam nu solreenga?'
Appa: 'Nee enna velakku! Kaasu mitham poganum!'
Muthu: 'Mitham... ippo moththam pochu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the ruined shop, water dripping on his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaasu vaipadhum kaasu dhan. Aana adha podhum pothu kaasu dhan. Vaichadhu dhan, adha kaapadradhu pola, adha paiyadhadhu dhan periya ariivu."
thirukkural: கொடுப்பதூஉம் துய்ப்பதூஉம் இல்லார்க்கு அடுக்கிய கோடியுண் டாயினும் இல்.
english_translation: What is the good of crores they hoard To give and enjoy whose heart is hard
moral: Crores are worthless if you can neither give nor enjoy them.

title: The Dry Well
hook: Priyan's uncle has a deep well but won't share water during summer — Priyan watches the irony.
story: Summer. All wells dried. Except uncle's. "Uncle, konjam thanni kudunga!" Uncle shut the gate. "En well, en thanni." Priyan's Thatha took the kids to the river. "Thaathaa, avaruku thanni irukku aana kudukka maatara?" Thatha filled a pot. "Selvam dhan irukku, aana irukku. Adhu dhan kashtam. Kudukkadhavanu selvam illa."
tamil_dialogues: Priyan: 'Uncle, kids ku thanni kudukkama irukkeenga!'
Uncle: 'En well. Ennaku dhan.'
Priyan: 'Appo en well irundhum payan illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the dry well's edge, lowering an empty pot on a rope.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thanni irukku, kudukka manasu illana, adhu kula dhan. Aana kudukka manasu irundha, adhu thanni dhan."
thirukkural: ஏதம் பெருஞ்செல்வம் தான்துவ்வான் தக்கார்க்கொன்று ஈதல் இயல்பிலா தான்.
english_translation: Great wealth unused for oneself nor To worthy men is but a slur
moral: Having wealth only for yourself, without giving, makes it worthless.

title: The Spinster's Gold
hook: Anjali's paati talks about a woman who saved gold for 50 years — never wore a single piece.
story: "Paati, pakkathu veetla yaaru irukka?" "Adhu Bhagyam paati. Avuku 50 varshama gold save, aana pottukka maata." Anjali peeked. An old woman sat surrounded by locked trunks. "Paati, enna pannitu irukka?" "Setha pona, baakki gold yarukko?" Bhagyam died. The gold was stolen that night. Paati sighed. "Ivalavu varsham save panni, orey naalum anubavichadhu illa."
tamil_dialogues: Anjali: 'Paati, avuku gold irukku, aana enna pannum?'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan — anubavikka theriyadha gold, adhu kal mannu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the empty trunks, a single bangle in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "50 varsham gold save panni, orey naalum pottukama irukradhu — adhu pottadhama irundha maadhiri dhan. Selvatha anubavichaa dhan athu namma selvam."
thirukkural: அற்றார்க்கொன்று ஆற்றாதான் செல்வம் மிகநலம் பெற்றாள் தமியள்மூத் தற்று.
english_translation: Who loaths to help have-nots, his gold Is like a spinster-belle grown old
moral: Wealth not shared is like a beautiful woman who ages alone — wasted.

title: Poison Fruit Tree
hook: Sekar's rich uncle's wealth attracts thieves, fights, and destruction — like a poison tree.
story: Uncle built a huge house. "Naan dhan periya aalu." But thieves came. Fights started. Cousins fought over property. The house became a war zone. "Appa, en uncle veetla ella time-um sanda dhan?" Appa picked a poisonous fruit from a tree in the backyard. "Paaru, nalla irukku pola irukka? Aana adhu nanju. Uncle selvamum adhu maadhiri dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Sekar: 'Appa, uncle house vera mathiri irukku, aana santhosham illa?'
Appa: 'Selvam aana adhu nanju maadhiri irukku. Adhu dhan problem.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands under a nux vomica tree, its fruit glowing red and deadly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla irukka maadhiri irundha, aana ullae nanju. Adhu dhan nandra illadha selvam. Etha vida adhu periya abayam."
thirukkural: நச்சப் படாதவன் செல்வம் நடுவூருள் நச்சு மரம்பழுத் தற்று.
english_translation: The idle wealth of unsought men Is poison-fruit-tree amidst a town
moral: Wealth that nobody wants or benefits from is like a poison tree in the middle of town.

title: The Looted Locker
hook: Rohini's father dies of stress from hoarding money — and the relatives take everything the same night.
story: Rohini's father was a miser. "Kaasu kaasu" was his only word. He died of a heart attack at his shop. That night, relatives came like vultures. "En appa setha odane, ella kaasu eduthutu poitaanga." Rohini's friend Kamala held her hand. "Yenna achu?" "En appa vaazhkaikulla kaasu eduthu vaicha Aana athai anubavichadhu illa. Ippo mathavanga eduthutaanga."
tamil_dialogues: Kamala: 'Uncle setha pona odane ellam eduthutaangala?'
Rohini: 'Ama. Oru kaasu kooda enakku kedaikala. Appa vaazhkaikulla anubavichadhu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the empty shop counter, a lone coin spinning on the wood.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anbu illama, aram illama, kaasaha save pannina, adhu avan anubavikala mathavanga eduthutu poiduvanga. Adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: அன்பொரீஇத் தற்செற்று அறநோக்காது ஈட்டிய ஒண்பொருள் கொள்வார் பிறர்.
english_translation: Others usurp the shining gold In loveless, stingy, vicious hold
moral: Wealth earned without love, virtue, or generosity will be taken by others.

title: Rain After Drought
hook: Pavithra watches her poor neighbor share his last rice — and learns who is truly rich.
story: The drought hit hard. Everyone was hungry. Pavithra's Amma had no rice left. The neighbor, a daily wage worker, came with a small bag. "Amma, ithu vaichuko." "Unakku enna irukku?" "Kuzhambu pottu dhan saapdraen. Aana unga payyanuku saapda venum." Pavithra cried. "Amma, avaru dhan periya selvam." Amma nodded. "Selvam kaasula illa, manasula dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Pavithra: 'Amma, avaruku kaasu illa aana kuduthaaru!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan unmaiyaana selvam ma. Kai kudutha dhan selvam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the neighbor's empty kitchen, a single pot of rice on the stove.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaasu irundha dhan selvam illa. Kai kudutha dhan selvam. Adhu dhan porul. Nandra illadha selvam vantaa mara maadhiri — nalla selvam vantaa mazhai."
thirukkural: சீருடைச் செல்வர் சிறுதுனி மாரி வறங்கூர்ந் தனையது உடைத்து.
english_translation: The brief want of the rich benign Is like rainclouds growing thin
moral: The poverty of a generous rich person is only momentary, like passing clouds.

title: The Stolen Chikki
hook: Gowtham steals a chikki from the shop — and his own face shames him more than any punishment.
story: Gowtham's fingers itched. The chikki packet called him. He slipped it into his pocket. The shopkeeper didn't see. But on the way, Gowtham felt heavy. His own reflection in the shop glass looked like a thief. "Paati, naan chikki eduthuten." Paati didn't scold. "Un manasula dhan paaru. Athu dhan unakku vethanai." They went back. Gowtham paid.
tamil_dialogues: Gowtham: 'Paati, naan eduthuten. En manasu sari illa.'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan naanam da. Naanam irukka, nee nalla payyan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the shop's glass window, Gowtham's reflection beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thirudu pannra pothu un manasula naanam varudhu. Adhu dhan unakku nalla visesham. Naanam irundha, thappu panna maata."
thirukkural: கருமத்தால் நாணுதல் நாணுந் திருநுதல் நல்லவர் நாணுப் பிற.
english_translation: To shrink from evil deed is shame The rest is blush of fair-faced dame
moral: True shame is about feeling bad for wrong deeds, not just blushing prettily.

title: Not Like the Others
hook: Latha's teacher explains why modesty makes humans special — not food or clothes.
story: "Animals um food saapdum. Dress pannadhu dhan us special?" Teacher asked. Latha thought. "Illamiss, naai dress pannadhu, naanum dress pannaren. Same dhan." The class laughed. Teacher smiled. "Nama special enna na — naanam. Enga seyala naanam varudhu. Adhu dhan nama manushana kattudhu." Latha got it. Modesty isn't about covering — it's about conscience.
tamil_dialogues: Latha: 'Miss, naaiyum naanum same maadhiri irukkom, aana nama special?'
Teacher: 'Naanam dhan latha. Athu dhan manushana kattudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the classroom door, a dog sitting quietly beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ella uyirukum saapdu, dress — adhu common. Aana manushana kattudhu naanam dhan. Atha irundha dhan nalla manushan."
thirukkural: ஊணுடை எச்சம் உயிர்க்கெல்லாம் வேறல்ல நாணுடைமை மாந்தர் சிறப்பு.
english_translation: Food, dress and such are one for all Modesty marks the higher soul
moral: All beings eat and wear — modesty is what makes humans truly special.

title: The Blushing Boy
hook: Harish accidentally breaks a vase — but instead of lying, his blush tells the truth.
story: Thatha's favorite vase fell. Crack. Harish's face went red. Amma walked in. "Yen da?" Harish couldn't speak. His cheeks burned. Amma looked at the pieces, then at his face. "Nee dhan panniya?" Harish nodded, tears welling. Amma hugged him. "Un mooku sonnadhu dhan unmai. Athu dhan naanam. Athu dhan un azhagu."
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Yar pannadhu?'
Harish: 'Mmm... naan dhan...'
Amma: 'Un mooku dhan unmai sonnachu — naanam irukku nu. Athu dhan periya visesham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels by the broken vase, picking up a painted shard.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naanam irundha dhan unmai. Amma sonna maadhiri, mooku suthama sonnachu. Athu dhan azhagu."
thirukkural: ஊனைக் குறித்த உயிரெல்லாம் நாண்என்னும் நன்மை குறித்தது சால்பு.
english_translation: All lives have their lodge in flesh Perfection has its home in blush
moral: Every being has a body; true grace lives in the feeling of shame.

title: The Swaggering Fool
hook: Vasu's cousin wears gold chains and struts like a king — but everyone laughs behind his back.
story: Raja came from the city. Gold chain, sunglasses. "Naan dhan super, da." He walked like a peacock. At the wedding, he slipped on the wet floor. The chain broke. Everyone saw. Vasu helped him up. "Raja, chain dhan pona, aana un perumai pona maadhiri irukku." Raja's face fell. "Naan dhan... adhu." Vasu smiled. "Naanam irundha dhan nalla irukkum da."
tamil_dialogues: Vasu: 'Raja, golusu vittutu pochu. Aana un swagger um vittutu pochu.'
Raja: 'Ama da... naan dhan over ah irundhen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the wedding dais, the broken chain in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thangam chain dhan nalla irukku. Aana atha vida naanam periya ornament. Athu vida periya ornament illa."
thirukkural: அணிஅன்றோ நாணுடைமை சான்றோர்க்கு அஃதின்றேல் பிணிஅன்றோ பீடு நடை.
english_translation: Shame is the jewel of dignity Shameless swagger is vanity
moral: Modesty is the ornament of the worthy — without it, even a grand walk is a disease.

title: The Mirror Girl
hook: Deepa watches a boy get bullied for his torn shirt — and she blushes for him.
story: A boy in torn shirt walked past. Rich kids laughed. "Thiruttu paiyan!" Deepa's face burned — not for herself, but for him. "Amma, avanuku naanam aagudhu. En manasum valikkudhu." Amma held her. "Adhu dhan periya naanam Deepa. Mathavanga naanatha nee feel pannra. Athu dhan nalla manasu." Deepa gave the boy her brother's old shirt.
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Amma, avanuku naanam aagudhu. Naan pathu feel pannaren.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan periya visesham. Adhu dhan sutha manasu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the rich kids and the boy, holding the old shirt.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku naanam varala, aana avanuku naanam vandhadhu — adha nee feel panna. Athu dhan periya naanam."
thirukkural: பிறர்பழியும் தம்பழியும் நாணுவார் நாணுக்கு உறைபதி என்னும் உலகு.
english_translation: In them resides the sense of shame Who blush for their and other's blame
moral: Those who feel shame for others' faults as well as their own — that is the true home of modesty.

title: No Fence, No Field
hook: Mano's Thatha builds a fence around his field — then says shame is the fence of the soul.
story: Thatha spent three days building a bamboo fence. "Thatha, intha fence enna?" "Namma field kaapadudhu." Mano pointed to the next field — no fence, cows ate everything. "Paaru. Athu mathiri dhan manasukum." Mano didn't get it. "Naanam nu oru fence irukku. Adhu irundha, thappu panna mudiyadhu. Adhu dhan manasa kaapadudhu." Mano understood: shame protects like a fence.
tamil_dialogues: Mano: 'Thatha, antha veetla fence illa — atha paadu maadugalum saapdudhu!'
Thatha: 'Adhu dhan — naanam irundha, thappu varaadhu. Fence maadhiri dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans on the bamboo fence, a calf licking his hand through it.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naanam irundha dhan manasu kaapadum. Fence illa na field kettu poidum. Naanam illa na manasu kettu poidum."
thirukkural: நாண்வேலி கொள்ளாது மன்னோ வியன்ஞாலம் பேணலர் மேலா யவர்.
english_translation: The great refuse the wonder-world Without modesty's hedge and shield
moral: Great people would rather die than live without the fence of modesty.

title: Anbu's Jump
hook: Anbu hears the story of a girl who jumped into the falls rather than face false shame.
story: The waterfalls roared. Amma pointed to the edge. "Anga dhan, oru ponnu setha." Anbu froze. "Yaen?" "Avuku poi naanam kuduthaanga. Ava dhan seyal sari illa. Aana avuku naanam valikkudhu, adha thangala." Anbu looked down. "Amma, adhu periya vethanai dhan." Amma nodded. "Naanam irundha, uyira vida kooda kodukka thonnum. Adhu dhan naanam."
tamil_dialogues: Anbu: 'Amma, naanam irundha uyira vida kooda mudiyuma?'
Amma: 'Ama. Athan dhan naanam periyadhu. Aana atha sariyana vazhi la dhan vena.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the waterfall's edge, water misting around his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naanam periyadhu. Sari illadha naanam uyira kuda edukkum. Aana athai oru aayudhama matha koodadhu."
thirukkural: நாணால் உயிரைத் துறப்பர் உயிர்ப்பொருட்டால் நாண்துறவார் நாணாள் பவர்.
english_translation: For shame their life the shame-sensed give Loss of shame they won't outlive
moral: Those with true shame would give up life rather than live shamelessly.

title: The Red Face Uncle
hook: Kavin's uncle cuts the queue at the bus stop — and when called out, he laughs instead of feeling shame.
story: Long queue. Uncle walked to the front. "Naan first." People frowned. Kavin tugged at him. "Uncle, queue la nikka vendum." Uncle laughed loudly. "Ithu dhan time saving." Kavin's face burned. "Appa, avaruku naanam illa!" Appa quietly said. "Paaru — aram naanam aagudhu avanuku. Enna naanam varala. Athu dhan periya vethanai."
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Uncle, queue la nillunga!'
Uncle: 'Adhukku naanam aaguma? Haha!'
Appa: 'Aram avanuku naanam aagudhu. Athu dhan periya visesham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the front of the bus queue, looking back at Uncle.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thappu pannittu naanam varadha — adhu dhan periya vethanai. Aram dhan avanuku naanam aagudhu."
thirukkural: பிறர்நாணத் தக்கது தான்நாணா னாயின் அறம்நாணத் தக்கது உடைத்து.
english_translation: Virtue is much ashamed of him Who shameless does what others shame
moral: When a person feels no shame for shameful acts, virtue itself blushes for them.

title: The Family Stain
hook: Swetha's cousin lies to get a job — he gets it, but the family name is destroyed.
story: Cousin Raghu lied in his interview. "Degree mudichuten." He got the job. The family celebrated. But the truth came out. Raghu was fired. Worse, the whole street talked. "Adhu Raghu veetla" became a joke. Swetha saw Paati crying. "Paati, avaru dhan thappu." Paati wiped her eyes. "Oru paer irundha adhu kula. Adha kaapadradhu dhan naanam."
tamil_dialogues: Swetha: 'Paati, avaru dhan thappu sonnaaru. Enna kuda alugureenga?'
Paati: 'Kulam dhan pochu da. Naanam illadhavan kula kettu poidum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the street corner, the family's name board fallen in the mud.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Poi sonna oruthanuku naanam illa. Aana avan kula dhan kettu poidum. Naanam irundha dhan kulam kaapadum."
thirukkural: குலஞ்சுடும் கொள்கை பிழைப்பின் நலஞ்சுடும் நாணின்மை நின்றக் கடை.
english_translation: Lapse in manners injures the race Want of shame harms every good grace
moral: A lapse in conduct burns the family name; shamelessness destroys all goodness.

title: String Puppet Boy
theme: Modesty / Without Shame, Just a Doll
hook: Aravind does whatever his friends say — no shame, no brakes — the counselor shows him he's a puppet.
story: "Aravind, atha pannu" — he did it. "Inga kai podu" — he did it. He had no filter. No shame. The counselor pulled a string puppet from her shelf. "Nee dhan da." Aravind stared. "Naan puppet illa." "Ama irukka. Unakku naanam illana, nee yar sonna kettadha pannuva. Athu dhan puppet." Aravind started crying. First time he felt shame. First time he felt real.
tamil_dialogues: Counselor: 'Unakku naanam illana, nee puppet dhan.'
Aravind: 'En puppet illa!'
Counselor: 'Apdi na, unakku naanam varanum. Adhu dhan unmai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar holds the puppet, its strings dangling, looking at Aravind.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naanam illadhavan oru bommai dhan. Yar sonnadhum adha pannuvan. Naanam irundha dhan avan suthanthiram."
thirukkural: நாண்அகத் தில்லார் இயக்கம் மரப்பாவை நாணால் உயிர்மருட்டி அற்று.
english_translation: Movements of the shameless in heart Are string-led puppet show in fact
moral: A person without shame is just a wooden puppet — others pull the strings.

title: The Vow at Dawn
hook: Eswar's father loses his fishing boat — Eswar declares he will rebuild the family name.
story: The cyclone took the boat. Appa sat empty. "Ella pochu da." Eswar stood. "Appa, naan kudumbatha katti edukkaren." Appa laughed bitterly. "Unakku enna therium?" But Eswar worked — carried fish for others, saved every rupee. Six months later, he bought a net. Then a small boat. The village watched. Appa wept. "Nee dhan da... nee dhan namma kudumbatha katti edukka porenu sonna."
tamil_dialogues: Appa: 'Ella pochu da. Ippo enna pannradhu?'
Eswar: 'Appa, namma kudumbatha naan katti edukkaren. Paathukonga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the shore, one hand on the new boat's bow.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudumbatha katti edukkaren nu solradhu periya visesham. Adhu dhan unmai. Solli seyradhu dhan perumai."
thirukkural: கருமம் செயஒருவன் கைதூவேன் என்னும் பெருமையின் பீடுடையது இல்.
english_translation: No greatness is grander like Saying "I shall work without slack"
moral: There is no greater pride than saying "I will rebuild my family and I will not rest."

title: The Tea Shop Boy
hook: Ganesh runs his mother's tea shop after school and learns that effort plus brain raises a family.
story: Amma was sick. The tea shop would close. "Naan pathukaren Amma." Ganesh woke at 5am. Boiled milk. Made tea. But sales were low. Then he observed — tourists wanted filter coffee, not tea. He learned. Changed the menu. Sales doubled. Amma recovered, saw the accounts, cried. "Unakku vayasu 13 dhan, aana un ariivu 30."
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Enna da sale illa?'
Ganesh: 'Amma, ivanga filter coffee dhan venum. Naan karpom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the tea shop counter, a steel tumbler of coffee in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Velai mattum pothadhu. Ariivum vendum. Rendum irundha dhan kudumbam uyarum. Nee rendum pannra."
thirukkural: ஆள்வினையும் ஆன்ற அறிவும் எனஇரண்டின் நீள்வினையால் நீளும் குடி.
english_translation: These two exalt a noble home Ardent effort and ripe wisdom
moral: Hard work and wisdom together are the two pillars that lift a family high.

title: The Broken Coil
theme: Household Management / God Helps the Determined
hook: Bhuvana's mother loses her weaving job — Bhuvana resolves to restart the family loom alone.
story: The power loom broke. Amma lost work. "Saapda vechiko nu sandai." Bhuvana stood. "Amma, naan dhan kai thooka maatten. Naan katti edukkaren." She cleaned the old handloom. Learned from the neighbor. First day, the thread broke 20 times. Day 10, she wove her first full saree. The buyer paid double. Amma fell at her feet. "Deivam dhan unna anupa irukku."
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Vela illa da. Namma saapda vechikala.'
Bhuvana: 'Amma, namma kai thooka koodadhu. Naan paathukaren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the handloom, the golden thread glowing as Bhuvana weaves.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naan kudumbatha katti edukkaren nu solra oruthanuku, deivam dhan munnala pova. Adhu dhan dhairiyam."
thirukkural: குடிசெய்வல் என்னும் ஒருவற்குத் தெய்வம் மடிதற்றுத் தான்முந் துறும்.
english_translation: When one resolves to raise his race Loin girt up God leads his ways
moral: When you say "I will raise my family," God himself girds his loins and walks before you.

title: No Plan Needed
hook: Dinesh's father keeps planning but never starts — Dinesh just begins and succeeds.
story: "Plan pannanum da. Plan." Appa planned for months. "Business pannalam, aana..." Dinesh got tired of waiting. He took the old sewing machine, stood on the street, and offered to stitch school bags. No plan. Just action. The first week he earned 500. Appa stared. "Nee plan illame panna?!" Dinesh smiled. "Plan pannitu irundha, ella time-um pona. Start pannidha dhan!"
tamil_dialogues: Appa: 'Plan pannanum da. Ready aaganum.'
Dinesh: 'Appa, panna dhan ready aagalam. Varala?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the sewing machine, a half-stitched bag in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Plan pannitu irundha dhan azhagu. Aana start pannidha dhan periya visesham. Vela pannidha dhan ellam nadakkum."
thirukkural: சூழாமல் தானே முடிவெய்தும் தம்குடியைத் தாழாது உஞற்று பவர்க்கு.
english_translation: Who raise their races with ceaseless pain No need for plan; their ends will gain
moral: Those who work tirelessly for their family don't even need to plan — success comes on its own.

title: The Stainless Son
hook: Rajiv's father asks him to lie about a defective batch — Rajiv refuses and earns everyone's respect.
story: The export order had defects. "Rajiv, kovai nu sollidu. Yarukum theriyadhu." Rajiv looked at his father. "Appa, poi solla mudiyadhu." His father's face twisted. "Unakku kudumbam venama?" Rajiv walked to the buyer. "Sir, quality issue. Naan solradhu sariya vendamnu ungaluku velai poga vendam." The buyer was shocked. He respected it. The order was extended — because of honesty.
tamil_dialogues: Appa: 'Poi sollu. Kudumbam dhan periyadhu.'
Rajiv: 'Appa, poi sonna kudumbam thaan periya, aana atha nalla peru illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between father and son, a bolt of cloth in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudumbatha kaapadradhu dhan aram. Aana poi sonna kudumbam pudichi pogadhu. Nerma dhan nilaikum."
thirukkural: குற்றம் இலனாய்க் குடிசெய்து வாழ்வானைச் சுற்றமாச் சுற்றும் உலகு.
english_translation: Who keeps his house without a blame People around, his kinship claim
moral: The world embraces as family the one who builds his household without fault.

title: The Builder's Son
hook: Karuppu's father abandoned them — Karuppu builds the family from nothing at age 14.
story: Appa ran away. Amma cried. Karuppu's school friends played. He worked. "Nee enna pannra?" Thatha asked. "Kudumbatha katti edukkaren thaa." He packed fireworks. He delivered on bicycle. He fed his sisters. By 16, he rebuilt the house. Thatha hugged him. "Un appa vittutu poitaan, aana nee inga irukka. Nee dhan appa."
tamil_dialogues: Thatha: 'Un appa vittutu poitaan. Unakku bayam illa?'
Karuppu: 'Bayam irukku. Aana thappa dhan. Naan dhan appa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside Karuppu's bicycle, a box of fireworks tied to the back.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee sonnadhu dhan — than piradha kula vazhika katti edukradhu dhan unmaiyaana aanmai."
thirukkural: நல்லாண்மை என்பது ஒருவற்குத் தான்பிறந்த இல்லாண்மை ஆக்கிக் கொளல்.
english_translation: Who raise their race which gave them birth Are deemed as men of manly worth
moral: True manhood is not about strength — it's about lifting the family that raised you.

title: Roof Over Head
hook: Kumaresan's mother falls ill — he becomes the sole earner for 6 people at age 14.
story: Amma collapsed. Hospital. 50,000 needed. Kumaresan's hands shook. He was 14. But he sold his bicycle. Worked at the tea stall 4am to 10pm. Feet bled. One night he cried alone. "Amma, enal mudiyadha." But he didn't stop. He paid the bill. Amma came home. She saw his cracked feet. "En raja." Kumaresan smiled. "Kudumbam dhan periyadhu Amma."
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Nee sariya saapdrala da. Un kaal ellam jaarama irukku.'
Kumaresan: 'Parava illa amma. Kudumbathukku enna venumo adhu dhan mukyam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the hospital bench, Kumaresan asleep on his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudumbathin bojjam eduthu mathavangaluku oru gathi pannravan dhan veeran. Avar porumai pola porumai illa."
thirukkural: அமரகத்து வன்கண்ணர் போலத் தமரகத்தும் ஆற்றுவார் மேற்றே பொறை.
english_translation: Like dauntless heroes in battle field The home-burden rests on the bold
moral: The burden of a family rests on those who are as brave at home as soldiers in war.

title: Too Proud to Work
hook: Mani's elder brother is too "proud" to work — and the family falls apart while he watches.
story: Big brother had a degree. "Naan periya aalu. Small jobs enakku venam." He sat at home. Amma worked. Mani studied. The family sank into debt. "Anna, konjam velai kooda pannala?" "Naan un maadhiri illa da." The electricity was cut. The house went dark. Anna sat in the dark, still proud. Mani left the room. Pride without work destroyed them.
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Anna, oru small job pannu. Kudumbam dhan mukyam?'
Anna: 'Naan un maadhiri illa. En perumai dhan mukyam.'
Mani: 'Perumai irundhu velai illana, athu perumai illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the dark house, a single unlit lamp beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manam irundha dhan nalladhu. Aana adhu over a irundha, kudumbam kettu poidum. Velai pannradhu dhan perumai."
thirukkural: குடிசெய்வார்க் கில்லை பருவம் மடிசெய்து மானங் கருதக் கெடும்.
english_translation: No season have they who raise their race Sloth and pride will honour efface
moral: Those who build their family don't wait for the "right time" — sloth and false pride destroy honor.

title: The Human Shield
theme: Household Management / Shield of the Family
hook: Poongodi's father dies — she takes every blow meant for her family, like a human wall.
story: Appa died. The loan sharks came. "Veedu venum." Poongodi stood at the door. "Mudiyadhu." They pushed her. She fell. Got up. Fell again. She didn't move. Amma pulled her. "Vidu da." "Mudiyadhu amma." She worked in the salt pans. Skin burned. Hands cracked. She shielded her sisters from hunger, from shame, from the world. The loan was paid. She was 13.
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Poongodi nee vali pattuta. Vidura solra.'
Poongodi: 'Amma, naan iruka varaikkum yarum unga kuda varadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the salt pan edge, Poongodi's shadow long and tall.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kudumbathukku verum bojjam mattum illa. Adhai kaapadradhum dhan. Un udal kooda adhukku oru kaappu dhan."
thirukkural: இடும்பைக்கே கொள்கலம் கொல்லோ குடும்பத்தைக் குற்ற மறைப்பான் உடம்பு.
english_translation: Is not his frame a vase for woes Who from mishaps shields his house?
moral: Is the body of the one who shields his family from harm not a vessel for all sufferings?

title: The Cracking Pillar
theme: Household Management / The Pillar of the House
hook: Murugan's father is paralyzed — he becomes the only pillar of a collapsing house.
story: Appa's accident. Bedridden. Amma couldn't work. The house tilted. Murugan stopped school. "Naa dhan Amma." He loaded trucks. He delivered eggs. Age 12. One night, Appa saw Murugan's bleeding hands. "En thambi... un kaigal." Murugan hid them. "Parava illa Appa. Naan iruka varaikkum intha veedu vizhadhu." Appa turned his face and wept — his son had become the pillar.
tamil_dialogues: Appa: 'Murugan, un kai... ellam ratham!'
Murugan: 'Parava illa Appa. Naan irukken. Veedu kaapadren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside Murugan, hands on the boy's shoulders, steadying him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru aal illadha kudumbam vizhundudum. Aana oru nalla paiyan irundha, adhu dhan thoon. Murugan dhan andha thoon."
thirukkural: இடுக்கண்கால் கொன்றிட வீழும் அடுத்தூன்றும் நல்லாள் இலாத குடி.
english_translation: A house will fall by a mishap With no good man to prop it up
moral: A family without someone to lean on will fall at the first blow — be that pillar.

title: உழவு தான் முதல்
hook: Mani asks why his Thatha still ploughs with a cow when everyone uses tractors.
story: Mani watched Thatha guide the wooden plough behind the white bull. "Thatha, tractor la vela mudiyadha?" Thatha wiped his brow. "Tractor dhan vegama irukum. Aana maadu potta, mannu uyirodha irukkum." That afternoon, Mani tried the plough. His hands blistered. Thatha laughed gently. "Pathiya da — elaam udambu dhan. Aana un vaaiyila saapdu varadhu indha mannula dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Thatha, tractor vachu panni dhan patha, enna intha maadu?'
Thatha: 'Maadu dhan da namma veetu ponnu. Athu illama, mannu poradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands knee-deep in the flooded field, a handful of seed rice dripping from his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee sonna maadhiri, ulagam er thoda maari irukku. Aana, un thaanthan panna vela dhan ulagatha nadathudhu. Verum kai vela dhan thala."
thirukkural: சுழன்றும்ஏர்ப் பின்னது உலகம் அதனால் உழந்தும் உழவே தலை.
english_translation: Farming though hard is foremost trade Men ply at will but ploughmen lead.
moral: The world turns around the plough — all else follows the farmer.

title: எல்லாரையும் சுமக்கிறவன்
hook: Durai sees the paddy truck driver earn less than a shopkeeper and questions fairness.
story: Durai's uncle drove a lorry load of paddy to the mill. At the tea stall, the shopkeeper mocked him. "Unakku enna, veedu vecha, pona — ippadithan pona." Durai's uncle smiled silently. Durai got angry. "Appa, avan enna ninaikiraan?" Appa held his shoulder. "Avane theriyama avan veetla soru vaikkardhu avan maadhiri ulavaaligalala dhan, da."
tamil_dialogues: Durai: 'Appa, avan enna dhan sonnaalum, avan soru vaikkardhu ulavalaigalaala dhan!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — ulagathukku aaniye ulavalan dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a sack of paddy at the mill, grains spilling from his fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulaval thozhil dhan ulagathai nadathudhu. Avanai namba ullavangalaala dhan namma vaazhala. Avan dhan muttum paarvanai."
thirukkural: உழுவார் உலகத்தார்க்கு ஆணிஅஃ தாற்றாது எழுவாரை எல்லாம் பொறுத்து.
english_translation: Tillers are linch-pin of mankind Bearing the rest who cannot tend.
moral: Farmers are the nail that holds the world together — they carry everyone.

title: வயித்துப்பாட்டுக்காரன்
theme: Farming / Those Who Plough and Eat
hook: Venu's classmate says his father is "just a farmer" — Venu's Paati sets him straight.
story: At school, Karthi bragged his father was a "manager." Venu said his father ploughs fields. The class laughed. Venu came home crying. Paati was grinding masala. "Paati, avanga ellam nammala vida periyavangala?" Paati stopped the grindstone. "Kanna, namma dhan soru vaikkala. Avan college poitu un appa vachi dhan saapdraan." Venu stood tall the next day.
tamil_dialogues: Venu: 'Paati, avanga appa manager dhan, namma appa ulavalan.'
Paati: 'Manager sonnavanukku soru vaikkardhu namma ulavalan dhan, da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the grindstone, helping Paati pour the masala in.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Uzhavu panni saapdradhavan dhan unmaiyaana vaazhkai. Mathavanga ellam avan kai kekka dhan saapdraanga. Adhu dhan perumai."
thirukkural: உழுதுண்டு வாழ்வாரே வாழ்வார்மற் றெல்லாம் தொழுதுண்டு பின்செல் பவர்.
english_translation: They live who live to plough and eat The rest behind them bow and eat.
moral: Those who plough and eat truly live — all others live off their labour.

title: நிழல் தரும் உழவன்
hook: Kani is amazed that the man selling greens under a hot sun feeds the entire city.
story: At the market, Kani saw a farmer with a small mountain of greens. "Appa, ivar evlo periya aalu?" Appa said, "Ivar dhan mayoray vida periyavar." Kani didn't understand. Appa explained: "Mayorukku kudai irukku. Aana indha ulavalanukku kudai illa. Aana avan vilaiyadradhu dhan mayrorukku kudai vaangi kuduthadhu." Kani bought the farmer a buttermilk with her own money.
tamil_dialogues: Kani: 'Appa, ivarukku enna periya kudai illa?'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — avan thol dhan nammellam kudai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the farmer's vegetable pile, fanning him with a palm leaf.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulavan kai illana, ulagathula kudai illa, soru illa. Avan dhan ella nizhalum kodukkara. Adhu dhan avana perumai."
thirukkural: பலகுடை நீழலும் தங்குடைக்கீழ்க் காண்பர் அலகுடை நீழ லவர்.
english_translation: Who have the shade of cornful crest Under their umbra umbrellas rest.
moral: The farmer who feeds the world rests under no umbrella — all umbrellas rest under him.

title: வாங்காத கையும் கொடுக்கும் கையும்
hook: Selvi sees her father give a sack of rice to a beggar even though they are not rich.
story: A beggar came to the field. Selvi's Appa filled a sack with fresh paddy. The beggar wept, touched his feet. "Ayya, unakku irundhu vangadheenga, kodukkeenga?" Appa laughed. "Naan soru vaikkardhu dhan eduthukardhu. Indha mannu enakku koduthadhu romba. Konjam kodutha enna?" Selvi hugged him.
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'Appa, nammalayum nalla illa, aana kodukkeenga?'
Appa: 'Mannu dhan perisu ma. Athu unakku koduthadhai konjam vittu kodutha enna?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the field, the beggar's empty hands now full of grain.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulavan kaikku adakka dhairiyam irundha, avan kodukka bayapada maatan. Mannu avanuku koduthadhai avan mathavargaluku kodupaan. Athu dhan avan perumai."
thirukkural: இரவார் இரப்பார்க்கொன்று ஈவர் கரவாது கைசெய்தூண் மாலை யவர்.
english_translation: Who till and eat, beg not; nought hide But give to those who are in need.
moral: Farmers neither beg nor hoard — they give freely from what their hands have grown.

title: உழவன் இல்லா உலகம்
hook: Arun wonders what would happen if all farmers stopped working for one day.
story: Arun asked, "Thatha, ella ulavalaigalum oru naala vela pannama irundha enna aagum?" Thatha thought. "Kaal la kaapi illa, saapda soru illa, kade la pal illa, pettiyula soru illa." Arun laughed but Thatha was serious. "Paathiya da, namma manasula vaicha ellam avanga Kai mela dhan. Intha oorai yaarum illa nu ninaichu paar."
tamil_dialogues: Arun: 'Thatha, oru naal soru illa na enna?'
Thatha: 'Adu dhan — ulavalan velaiyai niruthina, ulagam ninnudu podhum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in an empty market, all stalls barren, holding a single grain of rice.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulavan kai mattina, ulagam soru illa. Sadhu- santhanam ellam soru thedum. Athu dhan ulavalan perumai — avan dhan ella thunai."
thirukkural: உழவினார் கைம்மடங்கின் இல்லை விழைவதூஉம் விட்டேம்என் பார்க்கும் நிலை.
english_translation: Should ploughmen sit folding their hands Desire-free monks too suffer wants.
moral: If farmers stop, even renunciates will go hungry — the world depends on them.

title: பொடி மண்ணும் பொன் விளைச்சலும்
hook: Murugan watches his father break clods by hand and learns why fine soil matters.
story: Appa was breaking every mud clod into fine dust. Murugan was bored. "Appa, ithu enna velai? Muttai adikkara maadhiri irukku!" Appa smiled. "Mannu pottathu dhan da. Nalla poochi illa, nalla mannu venum." He let Murugan try. By evening, Murugan's arms ached. But the field looked like powder. Appa said, "Indha poluthu mannula dhan nalla vida varum."
tamil_dialogues: Murugan: 'Appa, mannu pottam pottam kuda nalla irundha enna?'
Appa: 'Mannu kai pottam dhan nalla virumbum da. Uyiruku veesam venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels in the powdered soil, letting it run through his fingers like water.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kai pottam pottadha mannu, adhu maadhiri — namma manasa thudikradhu dhan arivu. Athu pola, mannu poluthula dhan vida valarum."
thirukkural: தொடிப்புழுதி கஃசா உணக்கின் பிடித்தெருவும் வேண்டாது சாலப் படும்.
english_translation: Moulds dried to quarter-dust ensure Rich crops without handful manure.
moral: Fine-tilled soil yields rich crops — the work is in the preparation, not the fertiliser.

title: எரு போட்டது போதுமா?
hook: Gowri thinks dumping more fertiliser will make plants grow faster — Paati corrects her.
story: Gowri's tomato plant was small. She grabbed a fistful of fertiliser. "Paati, ithu potta vedi vedi nu valarum!" Paati stopped her. "Adhu dhan da, mayir kedaikum, aana kai illa. Munnadi mannu nalla parichu, aprom thaazh natti pathukka vendum. Eriva vida kaappu dhan mukkiyam." Gowri learned: more is not better.
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Paati, evlo eri podraano, avlo valarum!'
Paati: 'Illada kanna. Kai nalla paathu dhan valarum — eri mathrama ille.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the tomato plant, gently tying it to a bamboo stick.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Eri vida kaappu dhan mukkiyam. Adhu pola, namma manasai kaapadradhu dhan, adha poluthu valardhadhu vida mukkiyam."
thirukkural: ஏரினும் நன்றால் எருவிடுதல் கட்டபின் நீரினும் நன்றதன் காப்பு.
english_translation: Better manure than plough; then weed; Than irrigating, better guard.
moral: Manuring is better than ploughing, but guarding the crop is better than all.

title: வயல் சண்டை
hook: Kannan's uncle leaves the field untended and the crop "sulks" like a wife.
story: Uncle was lazy. He'd go to the field, sit under the tree, come back. "Vela dhan pannuren." One week he didn't go at all. The sugarcane looked droopy, yellow. Mami dragged him. "Paaru, un vayal pathi — enna mathiri irukku?" Uncle was ashamed. "Sari, sari, inniki poi paathukaren." Kannan giggled. "Mami, vayalum ungala mathiri dhan sanda podudhu!"
tamil_dialogues: Kannan: 'Mami, vayalukum manasukku sanda podudha?'
Mami: 'Ama da. Vayal, pondaatti — rendukkum kasavaali venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the yellowing field, touching a drooping cane stalk.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kai mannu mela anbu illadha, mannu vayalukum sanda podum. Adhu dhan — kai mannu mela aasai irundha dhan, vayal palikkum."
thirukkural: செல்லான் கிழவன் இருப்பின் நிலம்புலந்து இல்லாளின் ஊடி விடும்.
english_translation: If landsmen sit sans moving about The field like wife will sulk and pout.
moral: If the farmer neglects his field, it will sulk like a wife — diligence is love.

title: பூமி சிரிக்கும்
hook: Ramesh complains they are poor — Thatha says the earth itself laughs at lazy people.
story: Ramesh sat on the beach, throwing stones. "Thatha, namma edhukku evlo kastam? Vela pannalum poraadhu." Thatha pointed at the barren field next door. "Adha paaru. Athu namma vayal. Atha vittutu namma kastam nu solrom. Mannu sirikudhu da nammala." Ramesh stood up, took the spade. "Sari Thatha, inniki vayal velai pannalam."
tamil_dialogues: Ramesh: 'Thatha, namma evlo vela pannalum poraadhu?'
Thatha: 'Mannu sirikudhu da. Vayala vittutu kastam nu solluravangala paathu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the beach, pointing at the uncultivated field, laughing gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kai vela pannadha, vayal mannu sirikkum. Mannuku bayam kedaiyaadhu, aana vela pannadha manushana paathu adhu sirikkum."
thirukkural: இலமென்று அசைஇ இருப்பாரைக் காணின் நிலமென்னும் நல்லாள் நகும்.
english_translation: Fair good earth will laugh to see Idlers pleading poverty.
moral: Mother Earth laughs at those who sit idle and complain of poverty.

title: வறுமை விஷம்
hook: Kavya asks why her friend Meena can't come to school anymore.
story: Meena's bench was empty for a week. Kavya went to her house. Meena was rolling agarbatti. "School varadha?" Meena didn't look up. "Veedla saapda vechidha? Naan vela pannanum Ma." Kavya ran home. "Amma, Meenakku school pakathula coffee kadai la vela kuduka mudiyadha?" Amma hugged her tight. "Paapoma, Kavya."
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Amma, Meena ku saapda dhan illa. School enna varum?'
Amma: 'Nalla kelvi da. Intha vazhkai dhan kashtam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Meena, helping her roll agarbatti with patient hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kavya, vayum vandhu udalukku vali kudukum. Indha vali vida veru oru vali illa. Athu dhan — vayum manushanai noki varum pothu."
thirukkural: இன்மையின் இன்னாதது யாதெனின் இன்மையின் இன்மையே இன்னா தது.
english_translation: What gives more pain than scarcity? No pain pinches like poverty.
moral: No pain is as sharp as the pain of not having enough.

title: வறுமைன்னு ஒரு பாவி
hook: Mano's father works double shifts yet can't buy him new shoes — Mano blames fate.
story: Mano's chappal broke at school. He walked home barefoot in the heat. "Appa, enakku puthu chappal vangi kudukala? Naan school la nalla dhan pannaren." Appa was silent. Appa had sold his own lunch to pay the rent. Mano saw his father's cracked heels. "Vidum Appa, vendaam." Mano cried that night — not for shoes, but for his father.
tamil_dialogues: Mano: 'Appa, matha pasanga kaila nalla chappal, enakku illa.'
Appa: 'Podhum da. Vera vazhi illa.'
Mano: 'Adhu dhan kastam Appa. Unakku vera vazhi illadhadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the floor beside Mano, holding his broken chappal.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vayum oru periya pAvi. Adhu oru varam vantha, indha ulagamum, maru ulagamum onnum pakaadhu. Aana adha mathikalam — nambikkai vidaadha."
thirukkural: இன்மை எனவொரு பாவி மறுமையும் இம்மையும் இன்றி வரும்.
english_translation: The sinner Want is enemy dire Of joys of earth and heaven there.
moral: Poverty is a great sinner — it steals joy from both this world and the next.

title: நிறம் மாறும் மனிதன்
hook: Priya's educated but poor uncle says something mean to a shopkeeper — she is ashamed.
story: Uncle was a graduate, couldn't find work. At the ration shop, the clerk said, "Nalla sapla irukka?" Uncle snapped. "Unakku enna? Kai la kodutha vaangu, illa poi!" Priya was shocked. Uncle had never shouted before. At home, he wept. "Priya, unakku theriyuma — panam illadha manushan paithiyam aaidaraan." Priya hugged him.
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Uncle, neenga apdi solla koodadhu.'
Uncle: 'Solladha, aana varum da — vayum varudhu, appo vaai vellum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the ration shop, his hand on Uncle's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Panam illadha pothu, nalla vamsathula pirandhavan kuda mosama solliduvaan. Athu dhan vayum — athu manasai maathirum. Aana adha purinjikka vendum."
thirukkural: தொல்வரவும் தோலும் கெடுக்கும் தொகையாக நல்குரவு என்னும் நசை.
english_translation: The craving itch of poverty Kills graceful words and ancestry.
moral: Poverty destroys even good breeding — want makes the tongue turn wicked.

title: பணம் இல்லாத பணக்காரன்
hook: Sekar's mother borrows rice from a neighbour — the neighbour's words cut deep.
story: Amma knocked on Akka's door. "Akka, rendu nazhi soru irundha..." Akka gave the rice, but with a look. "Nalla aalu dhan — inga kadan vaangitu, aprom approm thiruppi." Amma's face burned. She took the rice silently. At home, she cooked without speaking. Sekar tasted the food. "Amma, innoru naal kadan vaanga maata, sariya?" Amma smiled through tears.
tamil_dialogues: Sekar: 'Amma, avan dhan kudutha, apdi sollalama?'
Amma: 'Namma kai illadha pothu, sila vaarthai kekanum da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the kitchen, watching Amma stir the rice pot.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vayum ullavanukku sila vaarthaigal kuda vali kudukum. Nalla veetla pirandhavan kuda thaalnu varum. Athu dhan kastam."
thirukkural: இற்பிறந்தார் கண்ணேயும் இன்மை இளிவந்த சொற்பிறக்கும் சோர்வு தரும்.
english_translation: Want makes even good familymen Utter words that are low and mean.
moral: Poverty humiliates even the well-born — need forces bitter words.

title: துன்பம் தாண்டவம்
hook: Latha's father loses his fishing net — and suddenly everything goes wrong.
story: Appa's net tore on a rock. The fish catch was zero. That week, rent due, school fees due, Amma's medicine ran out. Latha watched Appa sit on the shore, head in hands. "Thatha, Appa enna?" Thatha said, "Oru vilai vittadha mathiri da. Rendavadhu vilai vida, andha valaiku puttadhu." Latha took her piggy bank, emptied 50 rupees into Thatha's hand.
tamil_dialogues: Latha: 'Thatha, enna Appa pakkam oru mosam?'
Thatha: 'Oru ilandha soru, oru kai illadha mathiri da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the shore, the torn net in his hands, looking at the sea.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru kastam vantha, adhu pilla vida 1000 kastam kondu varum. Adhu dhan vayum — athu oru kodi kastatha kondu varum."
thirukkural: நல்குரவு என்னும் இடும்பையுள் பல்குரைத் துன்பங்கள் சென்று படும்.
english_translation: The pest of wanton poverty Brings a train of misery.
moral: Poverty is not one sorrow — it brings a thousand sorrows with it.

title: வாய் திறந்தும் பேசாதது
hook: Divya wins the essay competition but the prize goes to the rich girl — just because.
story: Divya's essay on "My Mother" made the teacher cry. First prize was ₹500. But the winner was Rithika — the sponsor's daughter. Divya's essay was "noted." She came home quiet. "Amma, en essay dhan nalla irundhadhu." Amma held her. "Theriyum da, enakku theriyum." Divya tore the certificate. "Vaai irundha dhan sari illa Amma — kaiku panam irukka vendum pola."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Amma, enakku dhan prize kodutha irundha.'
Amma: 'Theriyum da. Aana namma kaiku panam iruntha dhan solla mudiyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the empty prize table, Divya's certificate in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vayum irundha, nalla sollu kuda yaar kaathula vizhadhu. Atha dhan Divya innaiki paatha. Aana adhu nijam — ithu dhan ulagam."
thirukkural: நற்பொருள் நன்குணர்ந்து சொல்லினும் நல்கூர்ந்தார் சொற்பொருள் சோர்வு படும்.
english_translation: The poor men's words are thrown away Though from heart good things they say.
moral: Even wise words from the poor fall on deaf ears — poverty silences truth.

title: அம்மாவும் அன்னியமும்
hook: Balu's own grandmother treats them like strangers because they are poor.
story: Balu's Amma took him to Paati's house for Pongal. Paati served everyone, but gave Balu the smallest piece. "Paati, enakku perusa?" Paati didn't answer. Later, Balu heard Paati whisper to Chithi, "Avanga kaila onnum illa. Kodutha kudutha dhan." Balu looked at Amma. Amma's eyes were dry, but her hand trembled. Balu held Amma's hand tight.
tamil_dialogues: Balu: 'Amma, Paati nammala patha mathiri illa.'
Amma: 'Vayum dhan da, Paatiya maariduchu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the Pongal feast, his plate empty, looking at Paati.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vayum irundha, periya paati kuda nammala aniyama paapaa. Adhu dhan — adhu thirumbi paakka vendiyadhu."
thirukkural: அறஞ்சாரா நல்குரவு ஈன்றதா யானும் பிறன்போல நோக்கப் படும்.
english_translation: Even the mother looks as stranger The poor devoid of character.
moral: Poverty makes even your own mother look at you like a stranger.

title: நேத்தும் கொன்னது, இன்னும் வரும்
hook: Raji's family escaped poverty once — but it returns like a recurring nightmare.
story: Last year, Appa got a bonus. They bought new clothes, a fan, full rice sack. Raji felt "normal." This year, factory closed. Same old struggle. Raji saw Appa counting coins for milk. "Appa, namma ippo mazhai kalam maadhiri?" Appa smiled bitterly. "Namma vayum vida da. Adhu mudinjadhunu ninaikalaam. Aana adhu thirumbi varum."
tamil_dialogues: Raji: 'Appa, namma ponnadhu thirumbi vandhuduchu.'
Appa: 'Vayum appadi da — oru naal poiduchu nu ninaipom, aana adhu thirumbi varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the empty factory, looking at the silent machines.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vayum endha naalum vittu vidaadhu. Oru naal ponaalum, adhu thirumbi varum. Adhu namma manasuku therinja dhan atha edhirkka mudiyum."
thirukkural: இன்றும் வருவது கொல்லோ நெருநலும் கொன்றது போலும் நிரப்பு.
english_translation: The killing Want of yesterday Will it pester me even to-day?
moral: The poverty that killed you yesterday may return again today — it never truly leaves.

title: நெருப்புக்குள் தூக்கம்
theme: Poverty / No Rest for the Poor
hook: Velu's mother can't sleep at night — not because of noise, but because of worry.
story: Amma lay awake every night. Velu pretended to sleep but watched her. She stared at the ceiling, counting. "Amma, thookam varadha?" Amma turned. "Illada, niraiya yosichu." Velu asked, "Enna yosikareenga?" She said, "Nalaiku saapdu, vara naalai school fees, veedu vaadagai." Velu closed his eyes tight so she wouldn't see him crying.
tamil_dialogues: Velu: 'Amma, enna yosikareenga raatri ellaam?'
Amma: 'Nalaiku enna pannadhu nu da. Athu dhan thookatha adikkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Amma's cot, the moonlight falling on her open eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neruppukulla thoonkalaam, aana vayum irundha oru nimisham kooda thoonka mudiyadhu. Vayum dhan periya neruppu."
thirukkural: நெருப்பினுள் துஞ்சலும் ஆகும் நிரப்பினுள் யாதொன்றும் கண்பாடு அரிது.
english_translation: One may sleep in the midst of fire In want a wink of sleep is rare.
moral: One can sleep in fire but not in poverty — want steals even sleep.

title: உப்பும் கஞ்சியும்
theme: Poverty / The Duty of the Poor
hook: Pappu's Thatha skips dinner so Pappu can eat — and calls it "duty."
story: Thatha drank only kanji water. "Thatha, soru saapdala?" Thatha smiled. "Enakku vayaru niraindhadhu da. Nee saapdu." But Pappu saw Thatha's stomach grumble. He pushed his plate. "Thatha, rendu perum pangu poduvom." Thatha laughed. "Nee valara vendum da. Naan irundha seri." Pappu later asked Amma. "Thatha enna virumbara?" Amma said, "Un sirippai patha dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Pappu: 'Thatha, neenga saapdala na naanum saapdamaatten.'
Thatha: 'Sari da. Nee valara vendum. Adhu dhan thala.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Thatha, sharing the single plate of rice between them.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vayum irundha, oruvanuku soru illa. Appo, avar soru vaikkama saavadha vida kuda koodum. Adhu dhan avanin periya kadan."
thirukkural: துப்புர வில்லார் துவரத் துறவாமை உப்பிற்கும் காடிக்கும் கூற்று.
english_translation: Renounce their lives the poor must Or salt and gruel go to waste.
moral: The poor must renounce — not from choice, but because there's barely enough salt and gruel.

title: கேட்டா கிடைக்கும்
hook: Kumar is too shy to ask his rich uncle for help — Amma teaches him there's no shame.
story: Kumar's Appa was sick. No money for medicine. Amma said, "Un periyappakitta po kattu." Kumar shook. "Amma, avar enna thittuvaara?" Amma said, "Adhu avar prachana. Un Appa ku marundhu venum. Keduka kettadhu dhan." Kumar went. Periyappa gave ₹500 without a word. Kumar ran back. "Amma, koduthuttaaru!" Amma smiled. "Paatha, kettadhu dhan kedaikum."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Amma, avar enakku kodukka maataaru pola.'
Amma: 'Kodukka maatavan kitta kettadhum sari — adhu avan thappu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at Periyappa's door, hand on Kumar's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kodukka theriyadhavan kitta kettadhu kuda thappu illa. Nee kettadhu dhan un kadamai. Adhu avan manasuku theriyum."
thirukkural: இரக்க இரத்தக்கார்க் காணின் கரப்பின் அவர்பழி தம்பழி அன்று.
english_translation: Demand from those who can supply Default is theirs when they deny.
moral: Ask those who can give — if they refuse, the shame is theirs, not yours.

title: இன்பமா இரந்தது
hook: Anitha is terrified to ask her Paati for money for school — but Paati gives gladly.
story: Anitha stood at Paati's door for ten minutes. "Kettu dhan aaganum." She knocked. Paati opened, smiling. "Enna ma?" Anitha stammered. "Paati, school la... oru... book." Paati pulled her close. "Sollu da, kelvi dhan. Ketkaama epdi kedaikum?" She gave ₹200. Anitha's heart lifted. "Paati, neenga kettadhu kuda sandhosham." Paati laughed. "Adhu dhan ma — kodukka therinjavanga kitta kettadhu inbam."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Paati, kettu dhan aaganuma?'
Paati: 'Kettadhu dhan kedaikum ma. Nee kedutha, adhu sandhosham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in Paati's doorway, the ₹200 note in his hand like a flower.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kodukka therinja nallavanga kitta kettadhu oru inbam dhan. Adhu kastam illa. Ketkardha vida, adha vida mudiyadhadhu dhan kastam."
thirukkural: இன்பம் ஒருவற்கு இரத்தல் இரந்தவை துன்பம் உறாஅ வரின்.
english_translation: Even demand becomes a joy When the things comes without annoy.
moral: Asking becomes a joy when the giver gives without making it painful.

title: கரக்காத நெஞ்சு
hook: Saravanan needs a notebook but the shopkeeper scoffs — his teacher steps in.
story: Saravanan's notebook was full. He asked the shop, "Saaar, oru pusthakam... aprom kuduthu...." The shopkeeper waved. "Kaasu irundha vaangu." The teacher, Miss Selvi, saw this. She bought the notebook herself. "Saravanan, kettadhu dhan nalla. Aana kodukka therinja nallavangalai kettu dhan kettu." She added, "Nee periya aalan aana aprom innoru payyanukku nee kodukka vendum."
tamil_dialogues: Saravanan: 'Miss, avan enakku kodukka maatennaaru.'
Miss: 'Avan thappu. Nee kettadhu sari. Nalla aalu kitta kettu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the shop counter, the new notebook in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karakkadha manasu irukka aalu kitta kettadhu oru aayudham. Adhu avan perumai. Nee adha kettu dhan therinjikka."
thirukkural: கரப்பிலா நெஞ்சின் கடனறிவார் முன்நின்று இரப்புமோ ரேஎர் உடைத்து.
english_translation: Request has charm form open hearts Who know the duty on their part.
moral: Asking from those with open hearts who know their duty has a beauty of its own.

title: கொடுப்பது போல இரப்பதும்
hook: Meena asks the rich man for money for temple festival — he gives, and she feels like a donor.
story: Meena was collecting for temple fund. She knocked on the rich man's gate. "Periya samy, kovil kumbabishekam..." He gave ₹1000. Meena wrote his name in the list. She felt... proud? "Appa, naan kaekara aalu, aana enakku sandhosham." Appa laughed. "Adhu dhan da — namma kettu kodukka vaikalaam. Athu kodutha maadhiri dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Appa, naan kettadhum, avan koduthadhum rendum ore maadhiri irundhadhu!'
Appa: 'Ama da. Avan kodutha maadhiri, nee kettu kudutha maadhiri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the temple donation box, holding the list of names.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee kettu kodutha maadhiri aaiduchu. Avan koduthaan, nee kettaai — rendum ore madhiri. Athu dhan ulaga nyayam."
thirukkural: இரத்தலும் ஈதலே போலும் கரத்தல் கனவிலும் தேற்றாதார் மாட்டு.
english_translation: Like giving even asking seems From those who hide not even in dreams.
moral: Asking from those who hide nothing in their hearts is like giving itself.

title: கண் முன்னால் நிற்பவர்கள்
hook: Mala asks why beggars exist at all — and Amma gives an unexpected answer.
story: At the bus stand, an old woman begged. Mala asked, "Amma, ivanga enna ippadi?" Amma said, "Kai illadhadhu dhan." Mala whispered, "Aana avanga thappu illa." Amma nodded. "Adhu dhan da — ulagathula karakkadha nallavanga irukka dhan, kaekara aalungalum irukkaanga. Avanga illana, namma kodukka theriyadhu." Mala put her ₹5 into the old woman's hand.
tamil_dialogues: Mala: 'Amma, kaekara aalu illana nalla irukum?'
Amma: 'Illada. Appo namma kodukka theriyadhu. Avanga dhan nammala nalla aalaga mathuraanga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the bus stand bench, the old woman leaning on his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulagathula karakkadha aalu irukka dhan, kaekara aalu irukkaanga. Avanga rendum ore naadagathula ore madhiri."
thirukkural: கரப்பிலார் வையகத்து உண்மையால் கண்ணின்று இரப்பவர் மேற்கொள் வது.
english_translation: The needy demand for help because The world has men who don't refuse.
moral: The reason beggars exist is that the world has people who don't hide their hearts.

title: வறுமை மாயும்
hook: A stranger gives Raja's family a bag of rice just when they'd given up hope.
story: Amma was crying. No rice for dinner. Appa sat silent. Then a knock came. A man in white shirt stood with a 20kg bag. "Idhu ungalukku." Appa stammered, "Yaaru neenga?" The man smiled. "Unga mathiri oruthan enakku munnadi saayam panninaan. Naan thiruppi koduthaen." The man left. Raja asked, "Appa, avar yaaru?" Appa said, "Nalla manushan. Ava kaal mela dhan namma innaiki saapdrom."
tamil_dialogues: Raja: 'Appa, avar nammala theriyuma?'
Appa: 'Theriyadhu. Aana avan koduthaan — adhu dhan porum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the rice bag, his hand resting on the grain.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ulagathula nallavanga irukka dhan vayum vidiyum. Avanga kodukka munnadi varaanga. Athu dhan nalladhu."
thirukkural: கரப்பிடும்பை யில்லாரைக் காணின் நிரப்பிடும்பை எல்லாம் ஒருங்கு கெடும்.
english_translation: The pain of poverty shall die Before the free who don't deny.
moral: When you meet those who never hide or hoard, even poverty's pain disappears.

title: இகழாமல் கொடுத்தது
theme: Begging / The Joy of Humble Giving
hook: Devi asks Mami for old clothes — Mami gives her new ones without a single taunt.
story: Devi's dress was torn. She went to Mami's house. "Mami, enakku oru pavadai... onnu irundha." Devi's voice shook, remembering how others mocked. But Mami smiled. "Kanna, naan enna chinna pasanga kitta kaekka maata? Nee nalla irukkanum." Mami brought a brand-new pavadai still in plastic. "Ithu un periya akka vaangi vachadhu. Aana unakku dhan seri." Devi's eyes filled.
tamil_dialogues: Devi: 'Mami, kaila illa na...'
Mami: 'Sollu da, kaekka bayapadadha. Naan irukken.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in Mami's doorway, the new pavadai folded over his arm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Igalnthu edhukkama, sandaikudama kodukradhu dhan kodukradhu. Adhu dhan kudutha inbam. Devi kitta adhu irukku."
thirukkural: இகழ்ந்தெள்ளாது ஈவாரைக் காணின் மகிழ்ந்துள்ளம் உள்ளுள் உவப்பது உடைத்து.
english_translation: When givers without scorn impart A thrill of delight fills the heart.
moral: When the giver gives without belittling — that gift fills the heart with pure joy.

title: மரப்பாவை உலகம்
hook: Pandi wonders why the world needs beggars — Thatha gives a koan-like answer.
story: Pandi watched a beggar on the street. "Thatha, avanga enna pannitu irukkaanga? Vela illaya?" Thatha sat down. "Adhu dhan da — kodukka therinjavanga irukka dhan kaekara aalungalum irukkaanga. Adhu dhan ulagam. Kodukka therinjavanga mattum irundha, ulagam marapavai aagidum." Pandi giggled. "Appo, namma kaekara aalungala purampokku nu solla koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Pandi: 'Thatha, kaekara aalungal illa na enna aagum?'
Thatha: 'Appo kodukkara aalungaluku yar munnadi perumai? Athu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between a beggar and a giver, one hand on each.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaekara aalu illa na, kodukara aalu illa. Rendukkum oru bandham. Athu dhan ulaga aattam. Nee adha purinjikka vendum."
thirukkural: இரப்பாரை இல்லாயின் ஈர்ங்கண்மா ஞாலம் மரப்பாவை சென்றுவந் தற்று.
english_translation: This grand cool world shall move to and fro Sans Askers like a puppet show.
moral: Without those who ask, the world would be a puppet show — asking and giving keep it real.

title: கொடுப்பவனுக்கு ஒரு தோற்றம்
theme: Begging / The Giver's Fame Depends on the Seeker
hook: Lakshman sees a rich man feed the crowd and asks who is greater — giver or asker.
story: The rich man distributed free meals. Everyone praised him. "Evlo periya manushan!" Lakshman asked, "Appa, avanukku inga perumai. Aana kaekara aalungal illa na, avan perumai yaar munnadi?" Appa laughed loud. "Sonna da! Avan perumai kaekara aalungal munnadi dhan. Adhu dhan avan perumai." Lakshman nodded. "Appo kaekara aalungalum mukkiyam dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Lakshman: 'Appa, avan perumai kaekara aalungal irukka dhan illa?'
Appa: 'Sonna da. Avan perumai avanga munnadi dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the free meal line, a steel plate in each hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kodukravan perumai, kedukravan irukka dhan. Athu dhan. Avan kodukradhuku karanam, kedukravan irukradhu dhan."
thirukkural: ஈவார்கண் என்னுண்டாம் தோற்றம் இரந்துகோள் மேவார் இலாஅக் கடை.
english_translation: Where stands the glory of givers Without obligation seekers?
moral: What glory would givers have if there were no one to ask and receive?

title: கோபமும் வறுமையும்
hook: Bhuvana's relative comes home furious after being refused help — and blames the whole world.
story: Periyappa had asked a rich man for a loan. The man refused. Periyappa came home, kicked the door, yelled at everyone. "Avana kaekara aalu! Yenna ninaichitu?" Bhuvana watched silently. Later she whispered. "Periyamma, Periyappa kovam dhan. Aana adhu sari illa. Ketkardhula kovam vandha, adhu ketkardhu dhan mosam." Periyamma was shocked. "Nee sonna maadhiri dhan, ma. Avan kovam dhan avanai mosam aakudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Bhuvana: 'Periyamma, Periyappa kovam dhan avanai vida perisu.'
Periyamma: 'Adhu dhan ma. Kanama kodutha, avan kovapada maatan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the well, drawing water silently, unaffected by the shouting.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ketkardhula kovam iruntha, adhu dhan periya thappu. Kekkardhu nijam — aana vayum irundha, manasai katupaduthanum. Kovapadavadhu dhan periya thappu."
thirukkural: இரப்பான் வெகுளாமை வேண்டும் நிரப்பிடும்பை தானேயும் சாலும் கரி.
english_translation: The needy should not scowl at "No" His need another's need must show.
moral: The one who asks must not get angry at refusal — your own need is evidence enough.

title: Kodai Poda Maaten
theme: Dread of Begging / Dignity of Not Asking
hook: Muthu's friend offers him a free ticket to the aquarium — but Muthu says no because he doesn't want to "ask" for anything.
story: Muthu's friend Karthi got two extra tickets to the aquarium. "Vaa Muthu, free dhan!" Muthu shook his head. "Venda." Karthi was confused. "En da, free dhan?" Appa overheard. "Muthu, enna achu?" Muthu whispered, "Appa, avan kudutha dhan... naan kaeta mathiri aagum." Appa smiled. "Nee sonnadhu sari dhan, da. Yaar kitta kai neetama koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Muthu: 'Appa, avan kodutha dhan, aana naan kaeta mathiri aagum.'
Appa: 'Unakku theriyama oru periya sangam irukku, da. Kai neetadhadhu periya kodai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands near the aquarium entrance, watching children wave at fish through the glass.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karavatha kuduthaalum, kaekkadhadhu dhan periyadhu. Athu dhan unmaiyana maanam. Nee adha pannitta."
thirukkural: கரவாது உவந்தீயும் கண்ணன்னார் கண்ணும் இரவாமை கோடி உறும்.
english_translation: Not to beg is billions worth E'en from eye-like friends who give with mirth
moral: Even from generous friends, not asking is worth more than receiving.

title: Kai Neeti Vazhala
theme: Dread of Begging / Life Over Begging
hook: Meena sees a beggar at the bus stand and says "at least he's alive" — Thatha corrects her hard.
story: At Mattuthavani, an old man begged with a bowl. Meena said, "Thatha, avarukku kaikedaikum. Avan uyiroda irukkar." Thatha's face turned serious. "Meena, irandhum uyirvazhadhu dhan thappu. Atha vida sethuduradhu nalla irukku. Namma kai neeti koodadha maadhiri irukka pannanum." Meena never forgot those words.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Thatha, avar kai neetri dhan uyir vazharaar.'
Thatha: 'Adhu uyir ille da, adhu sAvadhu. Kai neetadhadhu dhan uyir.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stand, hands folded behind his back, refusing every coin offered.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Irrandhu uyir vazhadhu vida sethu poidalam. Athu dhan periya vedam. Kai neeti uyir adhu dhan kedumai."
thirukkural: இரந்தும் உயிர்வாழ்தல் வேண்டின் பரந்து கெடுக உலகியற்றி யான்.
english_translation: Let World-Maker loiter and rot If "beg and live" be human fate
moral: If begging is the only way to live, it's better not to live at all.

title: Vaanga Kettudalam
theme: Dread of Begging / Hardship of Begging
hook: Raja's family has no food for two days — Amma suggests going to the temple free kitchen, and Raja sees her shame.
