story: Two days with only water. Raja's stomach ached. Amma said softly, "Raja, kovil la free sapadu..." Her voice cracked. She couldn't finish. Raja hugged her. "Amma, naan poren. Yarum therithu kala." But at the temple, when the server poured sambar, Raja couldn't lift his eyes from the floor. The shame was worse than the hunger.
tamil_dialogues: Raja: 'Amma, naan sapadradhuku dhan pora. Aana manasu kettudhu.'
Amma: 'Naan dhan kettavan. Unakku vayasula indha kashtam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the temple free kitchen, his own bowl empty, eyes closed in meditation.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ennai parthu sapdradhuku vida, innoru murai irandhu sapdradhu kasta dhan. Adhu dhan — irandhu pOradhuku oru manasukettu irukkum."
thirukkural: இன்மை இடும்பை இரந்துதீர் வாமென்னும் வன்மையின் வன்பாட்ட தில்.
english_translation: Nothing is hard like hard saying "We end poverty by begging"
moral: There is no hardship harder than deciding to beg away your poverty.

title: Kai Neetatha Perumai
theme: Dread of Begging / Greatness Without Begging
hook: Kani's Paati refuses free food from neighbors even when they're starving — Kani learns why.
story: The rains destroyed the vegetable cart. Three days with just kanji. Neighbor came with rice. "Vangikko, paati." Paati smiled. "Venda ma. Namakku irukku." After she left, Kani cried. "Paati, ena vituta?" Paati wiped her tears. "Vangitta na, nalla irukka mudiyum. Aana adhu pOna avalukku namma mElE perumai irukkaadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Kani: 'Paati, vangikka vendiyadhu dhan irukku!'
Paati: 'Theriyum da. Aana kai neetadhadhu dhan nammoda perumai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar walks down the rainy street, an umbrella in hand, passing the neighbor's house with a gentle nod.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Idam illadha nerathilum kai neetadhadhu, athu dhan sAlbu. Un paati oru periyaval. Nee adha purinjuko."
thirukkural: இடமெல்லாம் கொள்ளாத் தகைத்தே இடமில்லாக் காலும் இரவொல்லாச் சால்பு.
english_translation: All space is small before the great Who beg not e'en in want acute
moral: The greatness that refuses to beg even in dire poverty is larger than the world.

title: Kaivala Kanji
theme: Dread of Begging / Sweetness of Earned Food
hook: Kumar's father brings home a small wage after a day's work — but the simple meal tastes like a feast.
story: Appa came home late, hands black with grease from the mill. He placed 80 rupees on the table. "Indha kaiku kanji vaangu." Kumar bought dal and rice. Amma made a simple kanji. Kumar ate it like it was biryani. "Appa, indha kanji dhan periya sapadu." Appa's eyes welled. "Nee sonna maadhiri dhan, da. Namma sambathicha dhan adhu ruchi."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Appa, indha kanji hotel sapadradha vida ruchi-a irukku!'
Appa: 'Sambathicha dhan ruchi varum. Kai neetina kedaiyaadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the floor of their small kitchen, a clay bowl of kanji in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thaazh thandha sapadu dhan iniyadhu. Yaar kitta irandhu vangina sapadradu vida, sambathicha kanji dhan periya virundhu."
thirukkural: தெண்ணீர் அடுபுற்கை ஆயினும் தாள்தந்தது உண்ணலின் ஊங்கினிய தில்.
english_translation: Though gruel thin, nothing is sweet Like the food earned by labour's sweat
moral: Even plain gruel earned by your own labor is sweeter than any feast received.

title: Pasu Pasi
theme: Dread of Begging / Shame of Begging
hook: Gowri's cow is dying of thirst — Amma must beg for water from the landlord, and her voice shakes.
story: The cow hadn't drunk in two days. Summer. All wells dry. Landlord's borewell had water. Amma stood at his gate for 15 minutes. "Tha... thanni... pasuvuku." Her voice was a whisper. Gowri felt the shame burn in her chest. Landlord laughed but let them take water. Gowri held Amma's hand. "Amma, innoru murai naan varen. Neenga kai neetathinga."
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Amma, pasuvuku dhan illa? Namma venaama?'
Amma: 'Pasuvuku kaeta na koodum. Aana namma kaeta... adhu kastam da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the cow as it drinks, his hand on its neck.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pasuvuku thanni kaetalum, kaekkum naa dhan ilivu. Enna kaetu, yaar kitta kaetradhu — rendum ore dhan ilivu."
thirukkural: ஆவிற்கு நீரென்று இரப்பினும் நாவிற்கு இரவின் இளிவந்த தில்.
english_translation: It may be water for the cow Begging tongue is mean anyhow
moral: Even begging for a thirsty cow, the tongue that begs knows no lower shame.

title: Karapara Kaekadha
theme: Dread of Begging / Not Begging From Misers
hook: Sekar sees a rich miser refuse a poor boy water — and decides he will never ask anyone for anything again.
story: At Erode market, a small boy asked the rich provisions shop owner, "Sir, thanni kudunga." The man waved him away like a fly. "Poi! Poi!" Sekar's blood boiled. He took the boy to the public tap himself. "Nee yaar kitta kaetaadha." Suruli laughed. "Avan dhan eppO karaparaan." Sekar said, "Appo avanu kitta kaeka koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Sekar: 'Avan dhan karaparaan! Avan kitta kaeka koodadhu.'
Suruli: 'Enna pannalam?'
Sekar: 'Avan kitta oru vaati kaekka koodadhu. Athu dhan avanuku sabai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the public tap, letting water run over his fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karappar kitta kaetradhu vida, kaekkadhadhu dhan nalladhu. Adhu dhan avana thazhaikka vaikkum. Nee seidhadhu sari dhan."
thirukkural: இரப்பன் இரப்பாரை எல்லாம் இரப்பின் கரப்பார் இரவன்மின் என்று.
english_translation: If beg they must I beg beggers Not to beg from shrinking misers
moral: If you must beg, never beg from those who hoard — let them rot in their own greed.

title: Odainda Thoni
theme: Dread of Begging / The Shipwreck of Begging
hook: Mani watches a fisherman beg for a boat repair loan and get rejected — the ship of begging always sinks.
story: Velu the fisherman's boat had a hole. He went to five moneylenders. All said no. "Nee thirumbi vaanga mudiyadhu." Velu sat on the shore, head in hands. Mani's Chithappa whispered, "Parada, irandhadhu thoni maadhiri dhan. Adhu eppO mulaigum. Yarum nambi kadala vidamaata." Mani understood: begging is a leaky boat.
tamil_dialogues: Mani: 'Chithappa, avanuku yarum saayam kudutha nalla irukum.'
Chithappa: 'Saayam kaeta dhan adhu mulaikum, da. IngE andha dharama kedaiyaadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the shore, looking at the broken boat as waves slap its hull.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Irradhu thoni maadhiri — eppavum mulaikkum, eppavum kadala vidaadhu. Karappar kalla mithikka dhan mudiyaadhu."
thirukkural: இரவென்னும் ஏமாப்பில் தோணி கரவென்னும் பார்தாக்கப் பக்கு விடும்.
english_translation: The hapless bark of beggary splits On the rock of refusing hits
moral: The fragile ship of begging will always shatter on the rock of refusal.

title: Kai Urugudhu
hook: Divya sees her aunt turn away a beggar — and her aunt's heart breaks more than the beggar's.
story: Periyamma was in a hurry. A beggar touched her elbow. She pulled away. "Illada, illa!" The beggar walked off. Periyamma froze. Her eyes filled. "Divya, avan kai paatha? Valai. Enna pannitten?" She ran after the man, gave him 50 rupees. "Vangiko." Divya held Periyamma's hand. "Periyamma, un manasu dhan urugudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Periyamma, karapara adichutinga!'
Periyamma: 'Illada... manasu urugudhu. Poi koduthuten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the bus stop bench, watching the exchange with compassionate eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaeka varravanai paathadhum manasu uruganum. Karapara vida, adhu dhan periya kedumai. Un Periyamma sonnadhu sari dhan."
thirukkural: இரவுள்ள உள்ளம் உருகும் கரவுள்ள உள்ளதூஉம் இன்றிக் கெடும்.
english_translation: The heart at thought of beggars melts; It dies at repulsing insults
moral: The heart melts at the sight of a beggar; refusing them kills what little humanity remains.

title: Illa Solradhu
theme: Dread of Begging / The Weight of Refusal
hook: Anitha's father says "no" to a beggar woman with a baby — and the word haunts him all night.
story: At Trichy bus stand, a woman with a month-old baby begged. "Kodunga saami, pasikudhu." Appa said, "Illa." Turned away. All night he tossed. "Anitha, andha kuzhandhai pasikudhu nu sonnanga... naan illa nu sonnen." Next morning, Appa searched the bus stand until he found them. He bought three packets of idli, milk for the baby. "Naan innoruthar kitta illa solradhu illa."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Appa, avan kitta illa sonna, unakku enna?'
Appa: 'Avaloda uyir dhan pOchu ma. Naan adha konnen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the woman and baby at the bus stand, feeding the baby milk from a small cup.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Illa solradhu oru uyir konradhu mathiri. Karappavanuku enga Odi olikka mudiyum? Nee seidhadhu dhan sari."
thirukkural: கரப்பவர்க்கு யாங்கொளிக்கும் கொல்லோ இரப்பவர் சொல்லாடப் போஒம் உயிர்.
english_translation: The word "No" kills the begger's life Where can the niggard's life be safe?
moral: The word "no" takes the beggar's life — where can the miser hide from that guilt?

title: Manushan Mathiri
hook: Bala sees a well-dressed man kick a stray dog — and asks if that man is really human.
story: The man wore a crisp white shirt, gold chain. He was walking to the temple. A stray pup slept on the path. The man kicked it aside. "Poi da naai!" The pup yelped. Bala froze. "Amma, avar manushan dhan, aana?" Amma held Bala close. "Veliya manushan maadhiri irukaan, da. Aana ullae kadu." Bala looked at the pup. "Amma, nalla manushana irukanum nu irukku."
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Amma, avar manushan dhan, aana naaiya adichaaru.'
Amma: 'Manushan maadhiri irukaan, aana ullae naai vida mosam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple step, the pup in his lap, licking his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manushan maadhiri irundha pothum, seyal dhan sonnadhu. Nee paatha aalu, manushan dhan, aana avan kaiyila manushan illa."
thirukkural: மக்களே போல்வர் கயவர் அவரன்ன ஒப்பாரி யாங்கண்ட தில்.
english_translation: The mean seem men only in form We have never seen such a sham
moral: The mean-spirited look human only on the outside — no real likeness exists.

title: Kavalai Illadhavan
theme: Meanness / The Richness of the Base
hook: Pavi envies the rich boy who never studies and has everything — until she sees his emptiness.
story: Priya sat in the car, new dress, new phone. "Pavi, unakku car illa." Pavi felt small. She asked Appa, "Appa, Priya-va vida namma thappu dhan?" Appa smiled. "Priya-ku dhan ellam irukku. Aana avaluku kavalai illa." Pavi didn't understand. Next week, Priya's father was caught in a scam. The car was gone. Priya cried in school. Pavi hugged her. "Naam irukkom da."
tamil_dialogues: Pavi: 'Appa, Priya-ku ellam irukku. Namakku illa.'
Appa: 'Avanuku kavalai illa, da. Adhu dhan periya kedumai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, watching Priya's car drive away.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kavalai illadhadhu dhan kedumai. Nalla manushanuku eppavum kavalai irukkum. Adhu dhan avanai nalla manushan aakudhu."
thirukkural: நன்றறி வாரிற் கயவர் திருவுடையர் நெஞ்சத்து அவலம் இலர்.
english_translation: The base seem richer than the good For no care enters their heart or head
moral: The mean appear richer because their hearts feel no care or concern for anything.

title: Manasu Padi
hook: Rajesh's classmate does whatever he wants and says "I'm like a god" — Teacher gives a brutal lesson.
story: Ramesh never did homework. Never listened. "Naan enna ninaikkiraen adhu dhan pannuven. Dhevar mathiri." Teacher heard him. She smiled. "Sari da, nalla dhevar dhan. Aana dhevargalum nalla dhan seivaanga. Nee mathavangala thondratta?" Ramesh went silent. Rajesh understood: doing whatever you want doesn't make you a god — it makes you selfish.
tamil_dialogues: Ramesh: 'Naan dhevar mathiri. Naan ninaikira maadhiri dhan pannuven.'
Teacher: 'Dhevar ellarukkum nalladhu dhan seivaar. Nee enna seiyara?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the blackboard, chalk in hand, a gentle smile on his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Dhevarum nalladhu dhan seiyaanga. Nee enna ninaikira adhu dhan pannradhu, athu dhevar illa — athu kEvalam."
thirukkural: தேவர் அனையர் கயவர் அவருந்தாம் மேவன செய்தொழுக லான்.
english_translation: The base are like gods; for they too As prompted by their desire do
moral: The mean do as they please, like gods — but gods create good, the mean create chaos.

title: MelinjupOvan
hook: Karthi watches a bully beat up a smaller boy and then sees an even bigger bully come and beat him.
story: Ravi was the street bully. He pushed a small boy into the drain. "Nadunga da!" Then came Maaran, older, stronger. Maaran grabbed Ravi by the collar. "Nee dhan da inga nadunga vendiya aalu." He slapped Ravi twice. Ravi cried. Karthi's Periyappa said, "Pathiya? Keela aalu appO mEl aalu. Ithu dhan kEvalam."
tamil_dialogues: Karthi: 'Periyappa, Ravi dhan kEvalam. Aana Maaran?'
Periyappa: 'Adhu dhan — kEvalamuku melE kEvalam irukkum. Ithu mudivu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the two bullies, not interfering, simply observing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Keela aalu mele aala paathu aadraan. Adhu dhan kEvalam. Nee andha maadhiri irukka koodadhu. Ithu solli varudhu."
thirukkural: அகப்பட்டி ஆவாரைக் காணின் அவரின் மிகப்பட்டுச் செம்மாக்கும் கீழ்.
english_translation: When the base meets a rake so vile Him he will exceed, exult and smile
moral: When a mean person finds someone worse, he becomes even meaner to feel superior.

title: Bayam Dhan Asaaram
hook: Chitra's cousin bullies her at home but acts sweet when adults are around — she sees the fear behind it.
story: Cousin Rani pinched Chitra when no one watched. "Pathuko da." But when Athai came, Rani smiled. "Chitra, unakku enna venum?" Chitra was confused. "Rani, unakku enna da?" Rani whispered, "Summa iru. Amma kitta sonna adi dhan." Chitra told Athai. Athai said, "Adhu dhan da — bayam. Avanu yarum munnadi unmaiya irukka thairiyam illa."
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'Athai, Rani pincharanga, aana unga munnadi nalla mathiri!'
Athai: 'Athu dhan bayam, da. Bayapadradhu dhan avanoda aasaaram.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the dining table, watching Rani's face change when adults enter.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kezhaluku bayam dhan aasaaram. Adhu dhan avanai nadathudhu. Nee bayapadadha. Nee unmaiya iru."
thirukkural: அச்சமே கீழ்களது ஆசாரம் எச்சம் அவாவுண்டேல் உண்டாம் சிறிது.
english_translation: Fear forms the conduct of the low Craving avails a bit below
moral: Fear is the only code the mean follow; if they had desire, they'd have a shred of goodness.

title: Appa Virumbal
hook: Vinoth's best friend tells everyone his secret — and his father compares him to a beaten drum.
story: Vinoth told Siva his biggest secret — he was scared of the dark. Siva told the whole class. They laughed. "Vinothukku karuppa bayam!" Vinoth was furious. He told Appa. Appa was cutting a coconut. "Adha paaru, da. Andha parai adicha dhan sound varum. Siva-um adhu maadhiri dhan. Un secret solli kudutha, adha avan veliya sola vendiyadhu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Vinoth: 'Appa, avan en secret ellarukkum sonnan!'
Appa: 'Parai adicha dhan sound varum. Siva-um andha maadhiri dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the coconut grove, holding a dried drum under his arm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ketta kElvi kekkum podhu, adha udana veliya sola vendiyadhu dhan kezhaluku velai. Siva andha maadhiri. Nee avan kitta onnum solla koodadhu."
thirukkural: அறைபறை அன்னர் கயவர்தாம் கேட்ட மறைபிறர்க்கு உய்த்துரைக்க லான்.
english_translation: The base are like the beaten drum Since other's secrets they proclaim
moral: The mean are like a drum — whatever is heard, they beat it out for all to hear.

title: Kai Virikkadha
hook: Selvi watches the richest woman in the market refuse to give a single rupee to a blind man — but her mother gives all she has.
story: The blind singer sang beautifully. Rich woman Pazhani stood nearby, heavy gold in her ears. He held out his hand. She pulled her saree pallu away. "Enna da, thoduradhu?" Amma had only 20 rupees — meant for fish. She gave it all. "Eduthuko." Selvi whispered, "Amma, unakku enna dhan?" Amma said, "Avanka kai virikkal. Namakku kai irukku. Athu dhan periyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'Amma, avanga kai virikkala, aana neenga kuduthinga.'
Amma: 'Kai virikkadhadhu dhan kedumai, da. Atha vida periya thappu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the blind singer, a coin already in the man's bowl.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Irada kai virikkaadhadhu dhan kezhaluku adaiyalam. Nee kudutha, athu dhan nija dharam."
thirukkural: ஈர்ங்கை விதிரார் கயவர் கொடிறுடைக்கும் கூன்கையர் அல்லா தவர்க்கு.
english_translation: The base their damp hand will not shake But for fists clenched their jaws to break
moral: The mean won't even open their damp hand to give — only clench their fists to break jaws.

title: Karumbu Pole
theme: Meanness / Good by Words, Base by Force
hook: Kannan's Thatha shows him how some people are like sugarcane — you have to crush them to get anything good.
story: At the factory, the supervisor spoke sweetly. "Nalla velai pannu, da." But if workers slowed, he screamed, threatened. Kannan's Thatha worked there. "Thatha, avar nallavadhu maadhiri dhan pesuraar." Thatha bit into a sugarcane piece. "Paaru, da. Karumbu vaai kuchi, nallavadhu dhan. Aana adha kolla vendiyadhu irukku. Andha supervisor um adhu maadhiri dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Kannan: 'Thatha, karumbu kashtam dhan kaekudhu!'
Thatha: 'Nalla aaluku solli dhan puriyum. Kezhaluku adichu dhan puriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the factory gate, workers walking past him one by one.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla aalai solli dhan aakalam. Kezhalai adichi dhan aakala vendiyadhu. Karumbu maadhiri dhan. Nee adha purinjuko."
thirukkural: சொல்லப் பயன்படுவர் சான்றோர் கரும்புபோல் கொல்லப் பயன்படும் கீழ்.
english_translation: The good by soft words profits yield The cane-like base when crushed and killed
moral: The good yield to gentle words like ripe fruit; the base only yield to crushing force.

title: Udupu Saapadu
hook: Lakshmi's classmate criticizes everyone's clothes and lunch — until Lakshmi turns the mirror on her.
story: Meera was the fashion police. "Un dress color suit aagala." "Un sappadu waste." Everyone feared her comments. One day Lakshmi said, "Meera, nee ellar udupu sappatu pathi sola, aana un dress ellam enna?" Meera went red. The class laughed. Teacher said, "Pirar kuraiya paathu sollradhula dhan kezhaluku santhosham. Nee adha maatiko."
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Avala dress? Kuppai dhan!'
Lakshmi: 'Nee ellar kuraiya patha dhan paakura. Un kuraiyum appadi dhan irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the back of the classroom, watching the children with a quiet smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirar udupu saapadu pathi kura sollradhu dhan kezhaluku velai. Avanuku avanoda kura theriyadhu. Adhu dhan avan periya kurai."
thirukkural: உடுப்பதூஉம் உண்பதூஉம் காணின் பிறர்மேல் வடுக்காண வற்றாகும் கீழ்.
english_translation: Faults in others the mean will guess On seeing how they eat and dress
moral: The mean will find faults in others just by how they eat and dress — blind to their own.

title: Vilpanai
hook: Kumar sees a neighbor switch political parties overnight for money and calls him a "sell-out."
story: Neighbor came home with a new banner. Yesterday he was in Party A. Today Party B. "Enna, party maathiteenga?" The man laughed. "Kudikka thanni illa. Yaar kudutha avar kitta dhan." Appa closed the door. "Kumar, avan oru aalu. Yaar kitta kaiku kedaikudho avanu kitta nikkuraan. Athu dhan kezhaluku seyal."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Appa, avar romba kashtama irundha dhan maathuraar?'
Appa: 'Illada. Avanuku siddhatham illa. Yaar kudutha avaru dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the road junction between two party banners, his face expressionless.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kezhaluku siddhatham illa. Avaru vandha, avaru kitta nikkuraan. Athu dhan kezhaluku adaiyalam. Nee andha maadhiri irukka koodadhu."
thirukkural: எற்றிற் குரியர் கயவரொன்று உற்றக்கால் விற்றற்கு உரியர் விரைந்து.
english_translation: The base hasten to sell themselves From doom to flit and nothing else
moral: The mean will sell themselves in a moment to escape trouble — they have no loyalty.

title: Kannula Minnal
theme: Beauty Maddens the Mind / The First Glance
hook: Mano sees a girl at the temple and suddenly can't remember why he came there.
story: Mano was holding the temple pass. Then he saw her — a girl in a blue pavadai, jasmine in her hair. She smiled at the goddess. Mano forgot the pass. Forgot the queue. Forgot everything. Appa nudged him. "Mano, pass?" Mano blinked. "Ap... appa, naan." Appa smiled. "Pathiya, oru paarvai dhan. Manasu enna seidhadhu?"
tamil_dialogues: Appa: 'Enna da, temple la ninnu enna paathutu?'
Mano: 'Appa... ava. Onnum solla mudiyala.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the inner sanctum, a single jasmine flower in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru paarvai dhan — manasai kadakki kuduthudum. Adhu dhan alagu. Nee paatha alagu, adhu oru kAla alagu. Aana manasu madakka theriyum?"
thirukkural: அணங்குகொல் ஆய்மயில் கொல்லோ கனங்குழை மாதர்கொல் மாலும் என் நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: Is it an angel? A fair peacock Or jewelled belle? To my mind a shock!
moral: A single glance at beauty can stun the mind like a divine enchantment.

title: Suttu Paarvai
theme: Beauty Maddens the Mind / The Returning Glance
hook: Anbu accidentally locks eyes with a girl — and feels his heart explode like a thousand drums.
story: Anbu was running to class. She was coming out. Their eyes met. One second. Two seconds. Anbu felt his ears burn. His heart pounded like dhol. He stumbled. Books flew. She laughed. Kavi picked up the books. "Enna da, oombi poita?" Anbu couldn't speak. That night, he stared at the ceiling. "Ava paarvai... enna thagadu."
tamil_dialogues: Kavi: 'Enna da, ava paatha dhan oombi poita?'
Anbu: 'Theriyala da... ava kannula enna irukku!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans against the school corridor wall, a book in hand, observing quietly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ava paarvai unnai kaayudhu. Adhu dhan alagu. Kannuku kann edhira paarthadhu oru padai. Nee adha purinjiko."
thirukkural: நோக்கினாள் நோக்கெதிர் நோக்குதல் தாக்கணங்கு தானைக்கொண் டன்ன துடைத்து.
english_translation: The counter glances of this belle Are armied dart of the Love-Angel
moral: When eyes meet in a returning glance, it strikes like an army of divine arrows.

title: Kootruvan Pole
theme: Beauty Maddens the Mind / Beauty as Death
hook: Vicky says a pretty girl looks like an angel — his brother says she looks like death.
story: A girl walked past on the beach road. Fair skin, long hair, red kurta. "Anna, ava dheivam maadhiri irukka!" Anna laughed. "Illada, ava kootruvan." Vicky was shocked. "Enna?" Anna said, "Alagu dhan, aana adha patha udane manasu thirumbudhu. Bayangara kaadhal aagudhu. Adhu dhan — alavu theriyama irundha, adhu saavadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Vicky: 'Anna, ava dheivam. Kannula oru minnal!'
Anna: 'Dheivam illa da. Kootruvan. Avan paatha dhan uyir pOkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar walks along the beach, waves lapping at his feet, unbothered by the passing girl.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Alagu kootruvan maadhiri dhan. Parvalai — aana alavu theriyama irundha, saavadhu. Un anna sonnadhu sari dhan."
thirukkural: பண்டறியேன் கூற்றென் பதனை இனியறிந்தேன் பெண்டகையால் பேரமர்க் கட்டு.
english_translation: Not known before -I spy Demise In woman's guise with battling eyes
moral: Beauty can be like death itself — captivating but dangerous when not understood.

title: Uyir Kudikkum Kannu
theme: Beauty Maddens the Mind / Eyes That Drink Life
hook: Senthil sees a girl whose eyes are so deep he feels like his breath is being pulled out.
story: Senthil was buying vegetables with Chithi. He saw her. She was selling flowers. Her eyes were dark, deep — like well water. Senthil stopped breathing for a second. He dropped the bag. Chithi turned. "Enna da?" Senthil pointed. Chithi laughed. "Ava kannu paatha dhan un uyir pOkudhu? Ithu dhan alagu, da. Kavalai illa."
tamil_dialogues: Senthil: 'Chithi, ava kannula... enna irukku?'
Chithi: 'Adhu dhan alagu, da. Athu uyira kudi kum. Nee nalla irukiya?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the flower stall, picking up a single rose, examining its petals.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pethai kannu dhan pEdai. Adhu paathavara uyir kudikkum. Aana adhu silarukku dhan theriyum. Nee adha pathu."
thirukkural: கண்டார் உயிருண்ணும் தோற்றத்தால் பெண்டகைப் பேதைக்கு அமர்த்தன கண்.
english_translation: This artless dame has darting eyes That drink the life of men who gaze
moral: Her innocent eyes drink the life of everyone who looks into them.

title: Moonu Oru Kooda
theme: Beauty Maddens the Mind / Death, Eye, Doe
hook: Priya writes a poem comparing her friend's eyes to death, a deer, and a dagger — wins the school prize.
story: Priya's friend Madhu had strange eyes — soft, deep, piercing. Priya wrote a poem: "Kootruvano? Kannano? Pinaiyo?" Teacher was stunned. "Priya, evlo aazhama yosichiya?" Madhu blushed. "En kannu pathi dhan?" Priya said, "Un kannu paadhadhu — moonum ore aagidhu. Bayam, alagu, mayakkam."
tamil_dialogues: Madhu: 'Priya, en kannu pathi dhan kavithai?'
Priya: 'Un kannu paatha — kootruvan, maan, kooda. Moonum ore idam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the school library, reading Priya's poem with a quiet nod.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ava kannula moonu irukku — saavadhu, parval, mayakkam. Adhu dhan alagu. Nee adha kavithaiya sonna, athu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: கூற்றமோ கண்ணோ பிணையோ மடவரல் நோக்கமிம் மூன்றும் உடைத்து.
english_translation: Is it death, eye or doe? All three In winsome woman's look I see
moral: In a woman's glance you find death, the eye of a deer, and the arrow of love — all three.

title: Puruvam Kodukka
theme: Beauty Maddens the Mind / The Veil of Brows
hook: Ravi's classmate always looks down — he thinks she's shy, but learns it's her power.
story: Divya never looked up. Always down. "Ravi, ava kannu paatha?" He tried to catch her eye. She turned. Ravi thought she was scared. One day he asked, "Divya, un kannu enna maadhiri?" She finally looked up. Ravi's knees felt weak. "Ithu dhan da." Divya smiled. "Naan kannu kaatadhadhu dhan unakku nalladhu."
tamil_dialogues: Ravi: 'Divya, nee enna kannu kaatadha?'
Divya: 'Kannu kaatina approm enna aagum nu theriyuma? Adhu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the playground swing, watching Divya push a younger child gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ava puruvam kodukka, unakku oRu mayakkam varadhu. Adhu dhan ava kaapadradhu. Nee adha purinjuko."
thirukkural: கொடும்புருவம் கோடா மறைப்பின் நடுங்கஞர் செய்யல மன்இவள் கண்.
english_translation: If cruel brows unbent, would screen Her eyes won't cause me trembling pain
moral: When her arched brows veil her eyes, they spare you the trembling pain of her gaze.

title: Thoosi Thirai
theme: Beauty Maddens the Mind / The Blouse and the Eye-Cover
hook: Arul asks why married women cover their chest — Amma's answer is a Thirukkural lesson.
story: Arul saw a woman in a bright blouse passing the temple. "Amma, ava enna maadhiri irukka?" Amma smiled. "Adha thoosi nu solraam, da." Arul was confused. "Thoosi? Enna thoosi?" Amma said, "Kadai kazhudha kannu mathiri — athukku oru kambili irukkum. Adhu maadhiri dhan. Atha patha udane manasu kadakidha koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Arul: 'Amma, thoosi nu enna?'
Amma: 'Kazhudha kannu mathiri da — adha mOla kadakka vida koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands outside the Andal temple, a silk veshti glowing in the sunlight.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kadai kazhudha kannu mathiri dhan — un manasu kadakka vida koodadhu. Alagu irukku, aana kaakka vendiyadhu."
thirukkural: கடாஅக் களிற்றின்மேற் கட்படாம் மாதர் படாஅ முலைமேல் துகில்.
english_translation: Vest on the buxom breast of her Looks like rutting tusker's eye-cover
moral: The blouse on a woman's breast serves like the eye-cover on a rutting elephant — a necessary guard.

title: Nurai Sutti
theme: Beauty Maddens the Mind / The Shattered Fortress
hook: Balu, the toughest fighter in school, sees a girl and his bravery shatters like glass.
story: Balu never feared anyone. He fought seniors, stood up to teachers. "Naan yaar munnadiyum bayapadamaatten." Then he saw her at the circle. Long hair, red bindi. Balu froze. His mouth went dry. Anna laughed. "Enna da, un bayam enga?" Balu couldn't answer. Anna said, "Alagu patha dhan bayam varum. Adhu dhan alagu."
tamil_dialogues: Anna: 'Nee avan munnadiyum bayapada maatte nu sonna! Enna achu?'
Balu: 'Ada, ava... avan munnadi dhan bayam varudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the circle, traffic flowing around him like a river around a stone.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neeye sonna maadhiri — ava paarvai un veeratha thakuthu. Alavu theriyama irundha, alagu dhan nammoda vellum."
thirukkural: ஒண்ணுதற் கோஒ உடைந்ததே ஞாட்பினுள் நண்ணாரும் உட்குமென் பீடு.
english_translation: Ah these fair brows shatter my might Feared by foemen yet to meet
moral: Her fair brow shatters a courage that even enemies feared — such is beauty's power.

title: Maan Kannu
theme: Beauty Maddens the Mind / The Jewel of Modesty
hook: Mala asks Paati why the most beautiful girl in the village wears no jewelry — Paati says her modesty is her jewel.
story: At the plantation, Mala watched Valli — the village beauty. Valli wore no earrings, no chain. Just a simple pottu. "Paati, ava enna naga illama irukka?" Paati smiled. "Ava kannu dhan naga. Ava naanam dhan naga. Adha vida periya aabaranam illa." Mala didn't understand. Then Valli looked at her and smiled. Mala felt warm inside. "Ippo puriyudhu, Paati."
tamil_dialogues: Mala: 'Paati, ava naga illama irukka, aana romba azhaga irukka!'
Paati: 'Ava kannu, ava naanam — adhu dhan ava naga. Adha vida periyadhu illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands among the banana trees, a single golden banana flower in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ava kannu — maan kannu maadhiri. Ava naanam dhan ava aabaranam. Adha vida azhagana naga illa."
thirukkural: பிணையேர் மடநோக்கும் நாணும் உடையாட்கு அணியெவனோ ஏதில தந்து.
english_translation: Which jewel can add to her beauty With fawn-like looks and modesty?
moral: For one with fawn-like eyes and natural modesty, what jewel can add to such beauty?

title: Kalli Vali
theme: Beauty Maddens the Mind / Intoxication Without Drink
hook: Sekar feels dizzy after seeing a girl at the bus stand — Appa says it's worse than alcohol.
story: Sekar was waiting for the bus. Then he saw her. Green saree, wind blowing her hair. His head spun. He sat down. Held his chest. Appa noticed. "Enna da, bus illa?" Sekar pointed. Appa laughed. "Adha patha dhan unakku mayakkam? Adhu dhan — kaal dhan kudikka vendam. Ithu kaadhalaale dhan." Sekar was confused. "Appa, ithu kuda kudikka maadhiri dhan irukku."
tamil_dialogues: Sekar: 'Appa, enakku enna aagudhu? Thalai suthudhu.'
Appa: 'Ava paathu dhan. Unakku kal kudikala, aana kalla vida adhigam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a bench at the bus stand, hands folded, eyes half-closed in contemplation.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kal kudichavaru dhan mayangiduvanga. Aana kaadhal — adhu kalla vida periya mayakkam. Nee adha paathu mayangura."
thirukkural: உண்டார்கண் அல்லது அடுநறாக் காமம்போல் கண்டார் மகிழ்செய்தல் இன்று.
english_translation: To the drunk alone is wine delight Nothing delights like love at sight
moral: Only the drunkard is intoxicated by wine — but love at first sight intoxicates everyone it touches.

title: Two Looks
theme: Eyes Can Both Wound and Heal
hook: Anjana's Appa gives her a stern look that crushes her — then a soft look that fixes everything.
story: Anjana failed her maths quarterly. Appa's face turned stone. One look — sharp, cold — cut through her chest like glass. She ran to her room, sobbing. An hour later, Appa knocked. He sat beside her, said nothing. His eyes were different now — soft, wet at the corners. He just looked at her gently. Anjana cried again, but lighter this time. Appa's first look hurt. His second look healed.
tamil_dialogues: Anjana: 'Appa, anda paarvai enna kuththi moodidichu.'
Appa: 'Ama ma, sonna dhan theriyum — oru paarvai kolai pannum, innoru paarvai uyir kodukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple step between them, one eye bright, one eye soft like moonlight.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Appa sonna maadhiri — oru paarvai vali kodukkum, innoru paarvai adha sari pannum. Kannukku rendu pakkam irukku."
thirukkural: இருநோக்கு இவளுண்கண் உள்ளது ஒருநோக்கு நோய்நோக்கொன் றந்நோய் மருந்து.
english_translation: Her painted eyes, two glances dart One hurts; the other heals my heart.
moral: A single look can wound — another can heal. Choose your eyes wisely.

title: The Secret Smile
theme: A Glance Can Say More Than Words
hook: Mithran sees Nandini glance at him during the school play rehearsal — and suddenly forgets his lines.
story: Mithran had rehearsed for weeks. But on stage, when his eyes met Nandini's in the audience, she gave him one tiny side-smile — quick, like lightning. His mind went blank. He stood frozen. The teacher whispered the lines. Later, Nandini said, "Nee enna paathu line marandhutiya?" Mithran grinned. "Un oru paarvai pothum da."
tamil_dialogues: Nandini: 'Ithu dhan da — unakku edhuvum venam, oru paarvai pothum.'
Mithran: 'Un paarvai dhan enna stage la niruthi pottudichu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the back row of the audience, eyes twinkling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Adhu dhan — oru suttu paarvai. Aana adhu romba velakkam. Kannale yosichu pesradhu dhan uyaram."
thirukkural: கண்களவு கொள்ளும் சிறுநோக்கம் காமத்தில் செம்பாகம் அன்று பெரிது.
english_translation: Her furtive lightning glance is more Than enjoyment of sexual lore.
moral: A stolen glance can speak louder than a thousand words.

title: The Watered Seed
hook: Maya glances at her Appa after winning the first prize — then lowers her head, shy.
story: Maya won the district painting competition. Everyone clapped. Appa stood in the back, beaming. Maya looked straight at him — then ducked her head, blushing into her collar. Appa came over, knelt, lifted her chin. "Enna ma, enna munnadi thala kuniya?" Maya whispered, "Appa, unga munnadi enna thala nimirndhu nikkardhukku naan innum valaradhu." Appa laughed softly.
tamil_dialogues: Maya: 'Appa, unga munnadi thala kuniya koodadhu nu sonnaanga illa?'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan da — thala kuniradhu dhan anbu ku thanni.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the paddy field, gently pressing a sapling into the mud.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thala kuniradhu thevai da. Athu dhan anbu ku thanni. Mayavoda thala kuninjathula dhan anbu irukku."
thirukkural: நோக்கினாள் நோக்கி இறைஞ்சினாள் அஃதவள் யாப்பினுள் அட்டிய நீர்.
english_translation: She looked; looking bowed her head And love-plant was with water fed.
moral: Shyness is not weakness — it's the water that makes affection grow.

title: The Peekaboo Game
theme: When I Look, She Looks Away — The Dance of Love
hook: Gowri plays peekaboo with Thatha — whenever she looks, he looks away, and vice versa.
story: In the big temple courtyard, Gowri and Thatha played their secret game. Gowri would stare at Thatha. Thatha would stare at the ground. The moment Gowri looked away, Thatha'd watch her with a huge grin. She'd spin around. He'd look down fast. They laughed. "Thatha, nee enna look panna maatra?" Thatha winked. "Ithu dhan vilayattu, ma."
tamil_dialogues: Gowri: 'Thatha, nee edhuku thala kuniTHa?'
Thatha: 'Nee paarutha maadhiri naan paatha, vilayattu mudinjirum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans against a temple pillar, playing the same game with a little sparrow.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kann vilayattu dhan periya vilayattu. Oruvar paarka, innoruvar thala kuniradhu — athu dhan anbin aatam."
thirukkural: யான்நோக்கும் காலை நிலன்நோக்கும் நோக்காக்கால் தான்நோக்கி மெல்ல நகும்.
english_translation: I look; she droops to earth awhile I turn; she looks with gentle smile.
moral: Sometimes love hides in the spaces between glances.

title: The Sideways Winner
theme: Not Looking Directly — The Power of Sideways Glances
hook: Vetri wins the 100m race by NOT looking at the finish line — just a sideways glance at his rival.
story: Vetri's rival, Kumar, was faster. Everyone knew it. At the starting block, Chellappa Sir whispered, "Kumar kitta direct-a paathu odha da. Pakkama paaru." Vetri ran. He never looked forward. He just stole sideways glances at Kumar's shadow. Half a meter ahead, Vetri leaned. Won by a hair. Kumar gaped. "Nee enna pakkama paathu odhuna?" Vetri grinned. "Sir dhan sonnaaru."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Nee enna pakkama paarutha? Nera paathirukalaam!'
Vetri: 'Nera paathunda theriyirum. Atha vida ithu nalla irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the finish line, holding a yellow ribbon gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nera paarthadhu vida oor kaal paarthadhu dhan periya jeyippu. Adhu dhan unakku vellum thanthathu."
thirukkural: குறிக்கொண்டு நோக்காமை அல்லால் ஒருகண் சிறக்கணித்தாள் போல நகும்.
english_translation: No direct gaze; a side-long glance She darts at me and smiles askance.
moral: Sometimes not looking directly is the cleverest way to win.

title: The Grumpy Uncle
theme: Harsh Words Can Hide Caring Hearts
hook: Santhosh's Mami screams at him every day — then he discovers she paid his school fees secretly.
story: Mami was always yelling. "Padi da! School ku late aaiduva! Nikkadha!" Santhosh hated it. One day he found a receipt in her room — his school fees, paid for the whole year. Mami had sold her gold chain. He went to her. "Mami, nee yelluna... aana." Mami cut him off. "Poi padi da. Naan sonnadhu sari dhan." But her eyes said everything.
tamil_dialogues: Santhosh: 'Mami, nee epdiyum yelluva, aana nee dhan nalla?'
Mami: 'Ada, yelluvadhu dhan anbu da. Adhu therila unakku?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the terrace edge, watering a wilting plant.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sonna maadhiri, kovama irukkuravangaluku kuda manasu nalladhu dhan irukkum. Adha therinjudha mudiyum, konja naal la."
thirukkural: உறாஅ தவர்போல் சொலினும் செறாஅர்சொல் ஒல்லை உணரப் படும்.
english_translation: Their words at first seem an offence But quick we feel them friendly ones.
moral: Harsh words often hide a heart that cares deeply.

title: The Pretend Fight
hook: Poorni's Anna snatches her remote and yells — but his eyes are laughing.
story: Anna grabbed the TV remote, shouted, "Nee full-a cartoon paathutu!" Poorni yelled back. They fought. But mid-fight, Poorni caught his eyes — he was trying not to smile. The corner of his mouth twitched. "Anna, un munchi solludhu!" Anna burst out laughing. "Unakku onnum puriyadhu!" He handed the remote back. It was all a game.
tamil_dialogues: Poorni: 'Anna, nee yelluva, aana un kannu sirikku!'
Anna: 'Sss, evanum therinjudha koodadhu. Idhu namma secret.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands behind the TV, watching them with a suppressed grin.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anger irukka maadhiri irundha kuda, manasu nalladhu dhan irundhadhu. Adhu dhan anbu — kovathula cover pannitum."
thirukkural: செறாஅச் சிறுசொல்லும் செற்றார்போல் நோக்கும் உறாஅர்போன்று உற்றார் குறிப்பு.
english_translation: Harsh little words; offended looks, Are feigned consenting love-lorn tricks.
moral: Pretend anger is sometimes just love wearing a mask.

title: The Slim Smile
theme: A Tiny Smile is the Biggest Compliment
hook: Kani tells a joke — Sandhya barely smiles, but that tiny smile makes Kani's day.
story: Kani cracked her best joke. "Sandhya, enna chocolate dhan chocolate-a irukku? Dark chocolate!" Silence. Then Sandhya's lips curved — just a millimeter. That was it. But Kani felt like she'd won an Oscar. "Nee chirichuta!" Sandhya looked away. "Naan chirikkala." Kani knew she did. That tiny, hidden smile was more valuable than any loud laugh.
tamil_dialogues: Sandhya: 'Unakku enna sirippu? Sari pannu.'
Kani: 'Nee konja chirichale pothum. Athu dhan periya sirippu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the corridor window, smiling the same tiny smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Azhagu dhan. Avan sirichadhula dhan azhagu. Aana avanu adha maraikkuran. Adhu dhan avan azhagu."
thirukkural: அசையியற்கு உண்டாண்டோர் ஏஎர்யான் நோக்கப் பசையினள் பைய நகும்.
english_translation: What a grace the slim maid has! As I look she slightly smiles.
moral: The smallest smile from a quiet person is the loudest compliment.

title: Stranger Eyes at Home
theme: When Family Pretend to Be Strangers — for Fun
hook: Ravi's Chinna Thatha pretends not to know him in the market — and they play strangers.
story: At the Kanchipuram silk market, Chinna Thatha walked past Ravi without a glance. "Sir, idhu silk mathiri jora irukku!" Ravi played along. "Yes sir, romba quality!" They bargained seriously. Then Chinna Thatha leaned in. "Ivan avan dhan, aana theriya maatran." He pointed at Ravi. "Ivan en peran." The vendor cracked up. Ravi couldn't stop laughing.
tamil_dialogues: Chinna Thatha: 'Sir, ivana paathukingala? Namma oor kaaran mathiri irukku.'
Ravi: 'Adhan sir, naan dhan. Aana theriyama irukatum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the next stall, pretending to inspect silk but eavesdropping with a smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Therinjavara therIlama irundha maadhiri vilayadradhu — athu dhan anbukku oru vilayattu. Adhula periya azhagu irukku."
thirukkural: ஏதிலார் போலப் பொதுநோக்கு நோக்குதல் காதலார் கண்ணே உள.
english_translation: Between lovers we do discern A stranger's look of unconcern.
moral: Even between close ones, playing strangers can be a game of love.

title: Silent Agreement
theme: When Eyes Agree, Words Are Useless
hook: Divya and Amma have an argument — then one shared glance resolves everything without a word.
story: Divya wanted to go to the school trip. Amma said no. They argued, voices rising. Then both fell silent. Divya looked at Amma. Amma looked at Divya. Something passed between their eyes — a quiet understanding. Amma nodded slightly. Divya nodded back. No words needed. Divya packed her bag. Amma slipped extra money into it. They never spoke about it.
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Amma, un kannu sonna pothum. Vaai veNam.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da — rendu kannum onna paathuduthu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the kitchen window, a hand over his mouth, eyes wide and warm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kann rendu sernja pothum, vaai onnum sonnadhu pOla. Kannodu kann — adhu dhan nijamaana pesu."
thirukkural: கண்ணொடு கண்இணை நோக்கொக்கின் வாய்ச்சொற்கள் என்ன பயனும் இல.
english_translation: The words of mouth are of no use When eye to eye agrees the gaze.
moral: When two pairs of eyes truly meet, not a single word is needed.

title: Five Senses in One Smile
theme: A Person Can Hold the World's All Joys
hook: Meena's Appa blindfolds her on a forest walk — and she experiences all five senses through him.
story: Appa tied a cloth over Meena's eyes. "Nambikkai da — en kai vittitu." He guided her through the pine forest. She heard birds (he named each call), smelled wet earth (he described the stream), touched moss (he found the softest rock), tasted wild berries (he picked them). At the end, he lifted the cloth. Before her: a valley of mist and green. "Appa, un kai la dhan ivai ellam irundhadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Appa, naan kanda paathadhu, keta kELvi, adhellam un kai la irundhadhu.'
Appa: 'Amma dhan — naan irundha dhan unakku ivai ellam kedaiyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a pine stump, a blindfold in his hand, smiling at the valley.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Avan irundha dhan ivai ellam — paarvai, saththam, sUvai, vasanam, uruvam. Avana vida vEr vEndam."
thirukkural: கண்டுகேட்டு உண்டுயிர்த்து உற்றறியும் ஐம்புலனும் ஒண்தொடி கண்ணே உள.
english_translation: In this bangled beauty dwell The joys of sight sound touch taste smell.
moral: The one you love carries all the world's joys within them.

title: The Cure Is Herself
theme: The Best Medicine is the Person Who Cares
hook: Arun falls sick before exams — Akka doesn't give him medicine, she gives him herself.
story: Arun had fever. Exam the next day. He was panicking, sweating, crying. Akka came in with a bowl of soup. "Saapdu." He pushed it away. Akka sat on his bed, took his hand. "Naan irukken da. Nee padicha maathiri dhan irukku. Padichadhulama illa." She stayed the whole night, waking up to check his temperature. Morning, the fever broke. Arun passed the exam. Akka's presence was the real medicine.
tamil_dialogues: Arun: 'Akka, naan pass aagamaaten nu bayama irukku.'
Akka: 'Naan irundha dhan pOthum. Naan dhan unakku marundhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the foot of the bed, a bowl of soup untouched beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Marundhu ellam vEru. Aana oru uyir innoru uyirukku marundhaaga irukku. Akka dhan unakku marundhu."
thirukkural: பிணிக்கு மருந்து பிறமன் அணியிழை தன்நோய்க்குத் தானே மருந்து.
english_translation: The cure for ailment is somewhere For fair maid's ill she is the cure.
moral: Sometimes the best medicine is not a pill — it's a person who stays.

title: Heaven in a Hug
theme: A Loved One's Embrace is Greater Than Any Paradise
hook: Chitra asks Paati if heaven is real — Paati says she already knows heaven.
story: Chitra couldn't sleep. "Paati, swargam epdi irukkum?" Paati pulled her close under the stars. Chitra's head rested on Paati's soft arm. The night breeze smelled of jasmine. Somewhere a temple bell rang. "Paati?" No answer. Paati was already asleep, snoring lightly. Chitra snuggled deeper. This — Paati's arms, the jasmine air, the stars — this must be heaven. She slept.
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'Paati, swargam inga irukka?'
Paati: 'Un thalai en kada la irukku, athu dhan swargam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar lies on the courtyard cot, a single star reflected in his eye.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Paati sonna maadhiri — anbaa oruvarin kadai la thungradhu, athu dhan swargam. Athai vida periya swargam vErenna irukku?"
thirukkural: தாம்வீழ்வார் மென்றோள் துயிலின் இனிதுகொல் தாமரைக் கண்ணான் உலகு.
english_translation: Is lotus-eyed lord's heaven so sweet As sleep in lover's arms so soft?
moral: A loved one's arms are a heaven more real than any paradise.

title: The Strange Fire
theme: Love Burns When Apart, Cools When Near
hook: Bala visits Amma's office — she scolds him over the phone but hugs him tight in person.
story: Bala's Amma worked late. He called her ten times. On the phone, she snapped. "Enna da, edhuku repeat-a call pannura?" Bala felt burned. He went to her office. Amma saw him at the reception. Her face changed. She ran, scooped him up, held him close. "Appo phone la yelluna — aana ippo?" Bala whispered. Amma laughed. "Phone la bayam da. Ippo un kitta irundha adhu dhan kulir."
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Amma, phone la nee neruppu maadhiri irundha. Ippo thanni maadhiri.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da — doorama irundha erikkum, kitta irundha kulirum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the office entrance, a phone in one hand and a glass of cool water in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Doorama irundha erikkum, kitta irundha kulirum. Adhu dhan anbu. Anbu oru azhagiya neruppu."
thirukkural: நீங்கின் தெறூஉம் குறுகுங்கால் தண்ணென்னும் தீயாண்டுப் பெற்றாள் இவள்?
english_translation: Away it burns and cools anear Wherefrom did she get this fire?
moral: Love burns when distant and cools when close — it's a strange and beautiful fire.

title: Whatever I Need
theme: A Parent's Arms Give Whatever You're Missing
hook: Nithya misses her Amma — Appa wraps his arm around her and she feels everything she needed.
story: Nithya's Amma was in another city for work. At the dark bus stand, she felt cold, lonely, scared. Appa sat beside her. He didn't say a word. Just put his arm around her shoulder. Nithya leaned into him. The cold went away. The loneliness shrank. The fear disappeared. One arm — and it was a blanket, a shield, a home. She fell asleep against Appa's chest.
tamil_dialogues: Nithya: 'Appa, un kai vachi kollava?'
Appa: 'En kai unakku dhan. Unaku enna venum, adha kudukum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the next bench, his own arm around a stray puppy.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Appavoda kai — athu oru kai illa. Athu oru veedu, oru pothi, oru swargam. Avan sonna maadhiri, unaku enna venum adhu dhan."
thirukkural: வேட்ட பொழுதின் அவையவை போலுமே தோட்டார் கதுப்பினாள் தோள்.
english_translation: The arms of my flower-tressed maid Whatever I wish that that accord.
moral: A loved one's arms become whatever you need — home, warmth, shelter.

title: Nectar Arms
theme: A Hug That Brings Life Back
hook: Karthik comes home exhausted after a cricket loss — Thatha's hug makes him feel alive again.
story: Karthik dropped the catch. His team lost the finals. He walked home dead inside — legs heavy, chest hollow. Thatha sat on the porch. He opened his arms. Karthik fell into them. Thatha squeezed tight — not a word. Karthik felt something bloom in his chest. Like water on a dry plant. Like air in a flat balloon. "Thatha, un kai la dhan." Thatha hummed. "Adhu dhan da."
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Thatha, un kai la dhan manasu thirumbudhu.'
Thatha: 'Ama da. Kaiku oru sakthi irukku. Adha kai dhan marundhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the porch roof, stretching his own arms wide to the sunset.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru aLavu aRum — aana adhu uyir thirumbudhu. Thatha kai maadhiri. Anbaa oru kai — athu amirtham."
thirukkural: உறுதோறு உயிர்தளிர்ப்பத் தீண்டலால் பேதைக்கு அமிழ்தின் இயன்றன தோள்.
english_translation: My simple maid has nectar arms Each embrace brings life-thrilling charms.
moral: A hug from someone who loves you is better than any tonic.

title: Shared Rice, Shared Heart
theme: Being Together Makes Even Simple Food Special
hook: Thulasi and Appa share one plate of rice because that's all they have — and it tastes like a feast.
story: Only one measure of rice left. No curry. Appa cooked it, put it on one banana leaf. "Vaa, rendu perum saapdalam." They sat, cross-legged, sharing the same leaf. Appa rolled a ball, fed Thulasi. She rolled one, fed him. Plain rice — but it tasted of laughter, of Appa's fingers, of the clinking of two hands on one leaf. Thulasi never forgot that meal.
tamil_dialogues: Thulasi: 'Appa, ithu oru leaf, irandu peruku pothuma?'
Appa: 'Kai sernja pothum, irandu perukku ore leaf pothum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside them on the floor, his own empty leaf before him, watching with wet eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un Appa sonna maadhiri — ore ilai la irandu perum saapdradhu dhan suvai. Adhu dhan anbu. Vithiyasa veedu ille."
thirukkural: தம்மில் இருந்து தமதுபாத்து உண்டற்றால் அம்மா அரிவை முயக்கு.
english_translation: Ah the embrace of this fair dame Is like sharing one's food at home.
moral: Sharing what little you have with someone you love — that is the richest meal.

title: No Air Between Us
theme: The Tightest Hug Needs No Space
hook: Janani's Amma returns after a month away — and they hug so tight no air can pass between them.
story: Amma worked in Tirupur, lived in a hostel. Janani stayed with Paati. One month. When Amma got off the bus, Janani ran. They collided. Amma knelt, wrapped her arms around Janani. Janani squeezed back — so tight Amma gasped. "Ma, konjam vechu." Janani shook her head. "Illa Amma, vechu koodadhu. Ulla kaatu poga koodadhu." Amma laughed and cried at once. They stayed like that — no space, no air, just them.
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Amma, rendu perukum idaiyila oru kaatu kooda varakoodadhu.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da — namma rendum onna irundha dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stop, his dhoti blowing in the wind, watching them with closed eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Rendu uyir onna sernja pothum, avangalukku idaiyila eduvum vara koodadhu. Athu dhan nijamaana anbu."
thirukkural: வீழும் இருவர்க்கு இனிதே வளியிடை போழப் படாஅ முயக்கு.
english_translation: Joy is the fast embrace that doth Not admit e'en air between both.
moral: When two hearts meet, no space — not even air — should come between them.

title: The Fight, The Apology, The Hug
theme: Sulking, Understanding, Embracing — The Three Steps of Love
hook: Kumar and Selvi fight over a cricket bat — then makeup with the best hug of the year.
story: Kumar took Selvi's bat without asking. Selvi screamed. "Naan un kitta pesa maatten!" She sulked behind the neem tree. Kumar sulked on the swing. The whole lunch break passed. Then Selvi came, stood before him. "Nee enna thappa pannuna?" Kumar gave her the bat. "Sorry da." Selvi nodded. And then — they hugged. The whole class cheered. Teacher clapped from the window.
tamil_dialogues: Selvi: 'Kovam dhan da. Aana rendu perum sernjithaan aaganum.'
Kumar: 'Kovam, puriyardhu, sernjadhu — ithu dhan da friendship.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the neem branch above, a bat balanced on his knee.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Udal, unardhal, pUnerdhal — ithu dhan mU varaigal. Anbukku indha mU varai dhan aLavu. Adhu mudinja pOthu anbu valarum."
thirukkural: ஊடல் உணர்தல் புணர்தல் இவைகாமம் கூடியார் பெற்ற பயன்.
english_translation: Sulking, feeling and clasping fast These three are sweets of lover's tryst.
moral: Love's rhythm is simple — fight, understand, embrace. Then repeat.

title: The Deeper You Go
theme: The More You Know Someone, the More You Discover
hook: Kavya thinks she knows everything about Paati — then Paati shows her a passport from her youth.
story: Kavya bragged to friends. "En Paati enaku edhum secret illa!" That evening, Paati pulled out a dusty trunk. Inside: a passport from 1975. Paati had been a nurse in Singapore. Photos of her in a white uniform, standing beside a ship. Kavya's jaw dropped. "Paati, nee Singapore la?" Paati grinned. "Nee nenaikardhu vida naan romba deepam." Kavya hugged her. The more she knew Paati, the more she realized she knew nothing.
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Paati, un vida naan onnum therinjudha maatten pola irukku.'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan da — therinja therinja, theriyama irukardhu dhan jaasthi.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the trunk, holding the old passport open, his thumb on the black-and-white photo.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Therinja therinja, adhukulE innum theriyadhadhu irukkum. Anbu adhaaga dhan valarum — theriyama irukradhula irundhu."
thirukkural: அறிதோறு அறியாமை கண்டற்றால் காமம் செறிதோறும் சேயிழை மாட்டு.
english_translation: As knowledge reveals past ignorance So is the belle as love gets close.
moral: The closer you get to someone, the more you discover how much more there is to know.

title: Softer Than a Flower
theme: Some Love is Gentler Than the Softest Thing
hook: Pavi touches the softest flower in Ooty but says Appa's palm is softer.
story: At the Botanical Gardens, Pavi touched a lamb's ear flower — the softest leaf in the world. "Appa, ithu epdi irukku? Pattu maadhiri!" Appa smiled. Pavi grabbed his hand, pressed his rough palm to her cheek. "Appa, un kai dhan soft." Appa laughed. "En kai soft-a? Idhu velai pannina kai da." Pavi shook her head. "Velai pannina kai dhan soft. Ennoda flower vida soft."
tamil_dialogues: Pavi: 'Appa, un kai ella flower kum vida mella irukku.'
Appa: 'Velai pannina kai da, mella irukka?'
Pavi: 'Ama. En flower vida mella irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside a bed of lamb's ear flowers, his own hand extended, palm up.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anbaa oru kai — athu velai pannina kai, aana mella irukku. Ennadha? Adhula anbu irukku. Anbu dhan mella seyyum."
thirukkural: நன்னீரை வாழி அனிச்சமே நின்னினும் மென்னீரள் யாம்வீழ் பவள்.
english_translation: Soft blessed anicha flower, hail On whom I dote is softer still.
moral: A working hand that holds you gently is softer than any flower in the world.

title: Don't Compare
hook: Yazhini tries to compare the lake's beauty to her Chithappa's drawings — and fails.
story: Chithappa was an artist. He drew the Kodaikanal lake every morning. Yazhini looked from his sketch to the real lake. "Chithappa, un drawing lake vida azhaga irukku." Chithappa stopped. "Podhum. Atha sonna pothum." Yazhini didn't understand. "Un drawing ku, lake ku enna compare?" Chithappa smiled. "Compare pannadha, Yazhini. Rendume different. Rendume beautiful."
tamil_dialogues: Yazhini: 'Chithappa, un drawing lake vida azhaga irukka?'
Chithappa: 'Compare pannadha ma. Rendume different. Adhu dhan azhagu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Chithappa, a blank canvas in his lap, staring at the lake.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Compare pannadhadhu dhan azhagu. Irandu porul — rendume azhaga irukku. Athula ethuvum kuraivu illa."
thirukkural: மலர்காணின் மையாத்தி நெஞ்சே இவள்கண் பலர்காணும் பூவொக்கும் என்று.
english_translation: You can't liken flowers by many eyed, To her bright eyes, O mind dismayed.
moral: Some things are beautiful in their own way — and need no comparison.

title: The Smile Like Pearl
theme: A Smile is More Than Teeth — It's Light
hook: Meera's Athai never smiles — until one day Meera sees her smile and realizes it's like a hidden pearl.
story: Athai was always serious. "Enna Athai sirikave maata?" Meera asked Amma. "Sirichi paathu yera varusham aachu." Then one Sunday, Meera and Athai made kozhakattai together. Meera messed up — put too much coconut. They both bit into it at once. The coconut spilled everywhere. Athai burst out laughing. Her teeth — white like pearls. Meera stared. "Athai, un sirippu!" Athai covered her mouth. "Enna da?" Meera hugged her.
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Athai, un sirippu muthu maadhiri irukku. Enna maraikra?'
Athai: 'Ada, sirikka marandhudichu. Ippo dhan nyabagam varudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the kitchen, a half-made kozhakattai in his hand, smiling wide.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Athai sirikkardhu — muthu maadhiri. Ennadha ava sirikkardhe illa. Appo dhan adhu visesham. Adhu dhan azhagu."
thirukkural: முறிமேனி முத்தம் முறுவல் வெறிநாற்றம் வேலுண்கண் வேய்த்தோ ளவட்கு.
english_translation: The bamboo-shouldered has pearl-like smiles Fragrant breath and lance-like eyes.
moral: A rare smile is like a hidden pearl — more precious for being hidden.

title: The Lily That Bowed
hook: Eshwar boasts about his drawing skills — then sees a lily in the pond that humbles him.
story: Eshwar loved showing off. "Appa, enna drawing pathiya? Super illa?" At the Teppakulam tank, lilies bloomed. One white lily bent low to the water, touching its own reflection. "Appa, adhu enna kuniTHu irukku?" Appa said, "Adhu thannai thane paathu — ennala un azhaguku eRai illa nu nenaichu kuniTHadhu." Eshwar looked at his own drawing, then at the lily. He folded his sketch. Some things don't need showing off.
tamil_dialogues: Eshwar: 'Appa, adhu edhuku kuniTHadhu?'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan azhagu, da — azhaga irukardhuku kuniya theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels at the tank's edge, his reflection touching the lily's reflection.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Azhaga irukardhuku kuniya theriyum. Eshwar — adhu dhan unmai. Kuniradhu azhaguku oru aayudham."
thirukkural: காணின் குவளை கவிழ்ந்து நிலன்நோக்கும் மாணிழை கண்ணொவ்வேம் என்று.
english_translation: Lily droops down to ground and says I can't equal the jewelled-one's eyes.
moral: True beauty doesn't boast — it humbly bows before greater beauty.

title: The Broken Flower
theme: Even Broken Things Can Be Beautiful
hook: Pooja finds a crushed flower on the street and asks Paati why it's still beautiful.
story: A flower seller's basket tipped. A yellow kanakambaram fell, got stepped on, crushed. Pooja picked it up. "Paati, ithu murinjirukku. Eriyudhum?" Paati took it, tucked it into her own grey hair. "Paaru." The yellow against silver — it glowed. "Odainjadhu dhan special, da. Adhu konjam jOli pannidichu. Aana adhukula dhan azhagu." Pooja stared. The broken flower in Paati's hair was the prettiest thing she'd seen.
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Paati, adhu murinjirukku. Enna azhagu?'
Paati: 'Odainjadhu dhan da azhaga irukkum. Adhu dhan unmai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the flower stall, tucking a single crushed petal into his own white hair.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Odainjadhula dhan azhagu irukku. Ennadha adhu jOli pannidichu. Adhu dhan adhukku oru visesham."
thirukkural: அனிச்சப்பூக் கால்களையாள் பெய்தாள் நுகப்பிற்கு நல்ல படாஅ பறை.
english_translation: Anicha flower with stem she wears To her breaking waist sad-drum-blares!
moral: Even broken things carry a beauty that whole things cannot match.

title: Confused Stars
theme: Some Beauty Confuses Even the Stars
hook: Indhu stands at the beach at sunset — a star shines beside the moon, and Appa says even the star is confused.
story: At Kanyakumari, the sun sank into the sea. The sky turned orange-purple. A single star appeared beside the crescent moon. "Appa, adhu nakshatram? Illa moon?" Appa smiled. "Indhu, athu nakshatram. Aana adhu moon nu nenaichu bayapadudhu." Indhu giggled. "Star ku bayama?" Appa pointed at her face in the golden light. "Paaru, un mUcha star kuda confuse aagidum."
tamil_dialogues: Indhu: 'Appa, star ku epdi confuse aagum?'
Appa: 'Un azhagu paathu dhan, da. Star kuda therinjudha mudiyala.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the water's edge, one hand pointing at the confused star.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Indhu azhagu paatha, star kuda confuse aagidum. Moon um therinjudha mudiyadhu. Adhu dhan ayya azhagu."
thirukkural: மதியும் மடந்தை முகனும் அறியா பதியின் கலங்கிய மீன்.
english_translation: Stars are confused to know which is The moon and which is woman's face.
moral: Some faces shine so bright they confuse even the stars.

title: The Spotless Moon
theme: Flaws Are What Make You Perfect
hook: Latha is sad about her pimples — Amma points at the moon's spots and calls her crazy.
story: Latha stared at her face in the mirror. Pimples. "Amma, enna mUcha romba kettadhu." Amma dragged her outside. "Paaru, moon paaru." The full moon glowed over Arunachala hill. "Amma, athukulla poi irukku." Amma laughed. "Moon ku kooda poi irukku. Adhanala adhu azhaga illaya? Neeyum apdi dhan." Latha looked at the moon's spots, then her own. She smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Latha: 'Amma, en mUcha poi? Moon poi? Moon poi nalladhu. Enadhu kettadhu.'
Amma: 'Rendum ore mathiri da. Poi dhan — aana rendume azhaga dhan irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the Arunachala slope, staring at the moon's craters with wonder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Moon ku poi irukku. Aana adhu azhaga irukku. Unakkum poi irukku. Aana neeyum azhaga dhan irukka. Adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: அறுவாய் நிறைந்த அவிர்மதிக்குப் போல மறுவுண்டோ மாதர் முகத்து.
english_translation: Are there spots on the lady's face Just as in moon that changes phase?
moral: The moon has spots too — and it's still the most beautiful thing in the night sky.

title: Shine Like Her
hook: Varsha wishes she shined like the lighthouse — Thatha says someone already does.
story: The lighthouse beam cut through the dark. Varsha sighed. "Thatha, naan adhu maadhiri oLi vida mudiyuma?" Thatha switched on his phone torch. "Paaru. Chinna oLi. Aana kaRuthula adhu dhan theriyum. Un sirippum apdi dhan." Varsha didn't get it. That night, her cousin cried after a bad dream. Varsha held her hand. "Naan irukken, da." The cousin stopped crying. Thatha saw it. "Pathiya? Lighthouse vida periya oLi."
tamil_dialogues: Varsha: 'Thatha, naan periya oLi vida mudiyuma?'
Thatha: 'Nee sonna maadhiri — adhu dhan periya oLi. Athu dhan periya light.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the top of the lighthouse, the beam sweeping over his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Varsha sonna maadhiri — avanuku theriyala. Aana avan oLi vida mudiyum. Anbu dhan periya oLi."
thirukkural: மாதர் முகம்போல் ஒளிவிட வல்லையேல் காதலை வாழி மதி.
english_translation: Like my lady's face if you shine All my love to you; hail O moon!
moral: The greatest light is not in the sky — it's in the kindness you show others.

title: Don't Shine Too Bright
theme: Some Beauty Is Meant for Just One Person
hook: Abi sings a song at the beach loudly — Amma tells her some songs are for one person only.
story: Abi sang at the top of her voice. "Amma, naan nalla pAdren illa?" Amma covered her ears playfully. "Santhosham da. Aana, ellar munnadiyum pada koodadhu." Abi was hurt. Amma sat her down. "Paaru — oru muthu. Athu kadal la dhan azhaga irukku. Eduthu potta? Vithiyasam." Abi nodded slowly. She sang softly, for just Amma. Amma's eyes filled.
tamil_dialogues: Abi: 'Amma, naan ellarkum pada koodadha?'
Amma: 'Oru paattu oru aatukku dhan. Adhu visesham. Appo dhan adhu sundaram.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the sand, a conch to his ear, listening to the sea's private song.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru vishayam oru aatukku dhan. Athu marainjadhu dhan azhagu. Adhu dhan Abi ku Amma sonna lesson."
thirukkural: மலரன்ன கண்ணாள் முகமொத்தி யாயின் பலர்காணத் தோன்றல் மதி.
english_translation: Like the face of my flower-eyed one If you look, then shine alone O moon!
moral: Some beauty is private — meant for only one pair of eyes to see.

title: The Nettle Feet
theme: Even the Softest Things Can Hurt If Put in the Wrong Place
hook: Sruthi picks a fluffy nettle leaf and it stings her — Paati explains even softness can hurt.
story: Sruthi saw a fuzzy green plant. "Paati, ithu soft-a irukku!" She grabbed it. Screamed. Thorns in her palm. Paati rushed, applied aloe. "Sutha irukka? Paaru." Sruthi cried. "Soft-a irundhadhu, aana!" Paati held her hand. "Ama da. Ellam soft-a irundhadhu nalladhu illa. Apdi dhan — oru nalla vishayam kooda, thappana idathula potta, valikkum." Sruthi understood: softness has its place.
tamil_dialogues: Sruthi: 'Paati, adhu soft-a irundhadhu! Enna kuththudhu?'
Paati: 'Soft-a irundhadhu nalladhu illa da. Adhukku oru idam venum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar kneels in the herb garden, a nettle leaf balanced on his fingertip.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nalla vishayam kooda, thappana idathula pottha valikkum. Adhu dhan Paati sonna pOla. Anbukku kooda oru idam venum."
thirukkural: அனிச்சமும் அன்னத்தின் தூவியும் மாதர் அடிக்கு நெருஞ்சிப் பழம்.
english_translation: The soft flower and the swan's down are Like nettles to the feet of the fair.
moral: Even the softest thing can hurt if placed where it doesn't belong.

title: கையில காசு
hook: Selvam's mother can't afford his school notebook and he sees the family name mean nothing.
story: Selvam needed a 10-rupee notebook. Amma searched every drawer. "Illeda kutty, innikki kuduthura mudiyaadhu ma." Selvam slammed the door. "Namma periya veetula pirachadhuna, enna use?" Amma didn't scold. She just sat on the floor, folding old newspaper to cover his old book. Selvam saw her hands shake. "Amma... naan thappa soliten."
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Amma, namma periya veetla pirachadhukku enna use college la. Ellam poi!'
Amma: 'Illada kutty, namma periya veetla pirachom dhan. Aana kaasa illadhadhuna, adhukku approm namma perumai kedaiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a newspaper bundle at the market vegetable stall, folding an old notebook cover.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Selvama, un periya veetla pirachadhum, un amma sonna maadhiri, nalla kudumbam dhan. Aana nalla kedaiyadha pothu, adhu kuda unna viduradhu. Kaasu mattum dhan unmai illa — manasu nalladhu irundha adhu dhan periya perumai."
thirukkural: தொல்வரவும் தோலும் கெடுக்கும் தொகையாக நல்குரவு என்னும் நசை.
english_translation: The craving itch of poverty Kills graceful words and ancestry
moral: Poverty doesn't just empty your pocket — it strips away your family's name and your own dignity.

title: வார்த்தை தாழ்வு
hook: Geetha's father, a proud man, snaps at her for asking fees money — and later cries.
story: Geetha's school asked for quarterly fees. Appa had lost his daily wage work. "Appa, school la fees katradhukku sollittanga." Appa threw the ladle. "Ennadhu school? Saapda illadhadhuna enna pannuva?" Geetha ran to her room. Later, Appa came with watery eyes and kept 20 rupees near her plate. "Eduthuko da. Naan apdi solla koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Appa: 'Fees-a? Unga school ku mattum dhan kuduthutu irukkena? Enna kozhi mathiri?'
Geetha: 'Appa, naan venumna solladha?'
Appa: 'Illada kutty, nee venum nu sonnadhu dhan. Appa dhan mooda maaritten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands near the school gate, watching Appa count coins with trembling fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Geetha, un appa mooda maari sonnalum, adhu avan manasu illadhadhuna illa. Kaasu illadhadhuna dhan manushan mooda maaruvan. Aana avan manasula un mEla anbu dhan irukku. Un appa sari dhan."
thirukkural: இற்பிறந்தார் கண்ணேயும் இன்மை இளிவந்த சொற்பிறக்கும் சோர்வு தரும்.
english_translation: Want makes even good familymen Utter words that are low and mean
moral: Even the best of families feel the sting of poverty — it makes good people say bad things.

title: பசியும் பயமும்
hook: Kumar's family arrives at the station with just one bag — and one problem after another unfolds.
story: Periyamma brought Kumar and his sister to Madurai after the village flood. At the station, the bag tore. Clothes scattered. Kumar's sister lost her slipper. Then Kumar saw a man steal their rice packet. "Periyamma! Avan eduthutu poittan!" Periyamma just sat down, too tired to cry. A tea stall uncle saw everything and gave them two vadas. Kumar broke the vada into three pieces.
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Periyamma, namma soru porellam avan eduthutu poittan!'
Periyamma: 'Vidu da... innum vali varum. Ithu dhan nalikuravan vali.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the tea stall, pouring two extra cups of tea for Kumar and his sister.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kumar, nalikuravan oru vali illa — adhu oru koottam valiyai kootitu varum. Aana appo dhan unmaiyaana m驾nidhan yaarunu theriyum. The stall uncle maadhiri."
thirukkural: நல்குரவு என்னும் இடும்பையுள் பல்குரைத் துன்பங்கள் சென்று படும்.
english_translation: The pest of wanton poverty Brings a train of misery
moral: Poverty is not one sorrow — it drags a whole caravan of troubles behind it.

title: யாரும் கேக்கல
theme: Poverty / Poor Words Carry No Weight
hook: Rani knows the answer in class but the teacher ignores her because her uniform is torn.
story: The teacher asked, "Who built the Big Temple?" Rani knew. She raised her hand. But teacher picked Priya — the rich girl with new ribbons. Priya said "Raja Raja Chozhan." Teacher smiled. Rani whispered the same answer under her breath. Thatha waited outside. "Enna da, answer theriyadha?" Rani shook her head. "Therinjuma, yarum kettukka maataanga Thatha."
tamil_dialogues: Rani: 'Thatha, naan answer therinjuttu dhan kai kaattinen! Aana ava kai mattum dhan pakkaanga.'
Thatha: 'Theriyum da. Nalla kai kaatina dhan kai kaatradhu pola. Ungal kai kaatina, paadhi peru kooda paaka maataanga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar weaves a silk saree on a handloom, threads of gold and white crossing slowly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Rani, unakku theriyum, un answer sari dhan. Aana inga yarum kettukka maataanga. Nalla kai dhan theriyum. Aana adha sonnadhum theriyadhu pola — adhu dhan nalikuravan vali."
thirukkural: நற்பொருள் நன்குணர்ந்து சொல்லினும் நல்கூர்ந்தார் சொற்பொருள் சோர்வு படும்.
english_translation: The poor men's words are thrown away Though from heart good things they say
moral: Even truth spoken by the poor falls on deaf ears in a world that values wealth over wisdom.

title: மாறிய பார்வை
hook: Karuna's mother treats her differently after Appa loses his job — like she's a stranger in her own home.
story: Karuna's Appa lost his mill job. Amma started selling Karuna's old bangles. One night, Karuna asked for extra rice. Amma looked at her stone-faced. "Adhu dhan irukku. Vera enna venum?" Karuna felt a knife in her chest. "Amma, neenga enakku mathiri pesreenga?" Amma froze. She held Karuna's face. "Illada... nalikuravan dhan mathiri maatran. Naan illa."
tamil_dialogues: Karuna: 'Amma, ungaluku naan mathiri aagitten?'
Amma: 'Illada kutty! Amma dhan. Aana nalikuravan dhan Ammayum maaramaatran.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the kitchen floor beside the empty rice pot, his hand on Amma's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karuna, un amma unna mathiramaatan. Aana nalikuravan dhan amma kuda mathiri maatran. Adhu dhan periya bhayam — nalikuravan oru aayudham maadhiri, atha kondu yaarai vennaalum kaayama pannidum."
thirukkural: அறஞ்சாரா நல்குரவு ஈன்றதா யானும் பிறன்போல நோக்கப் படும்.
english_translation: Even the mother looks as stranger The poor devoid of character
moral: Poverty is crueler than a stranger — it can even change the way your own mother looks at you.

title: நேத்தும் இன்னிக்கும்
hook: Mahesh asks his father if poverty will ever go away — and Appa doesn't have an answer.
story: Every morning, same question. "Appa, inniku enna saapdu?" Every evening, same answer. "Edhavadhu vangi pota vendiya dhan." One night Mahesh couldn't sleep. "Appa, nethu kuda inga dhan irundhom. Inniku kuda inga dhan. Nalaiku enna?" Appa pulled him close. "Theriyadhu da. Aana namma irukkom. Adhu dhan periyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Mahesh: 'Appa, nethu pona maadhiri innikkum. Nalaikkum adhe maadhiri dhana?'
Appa: 'Theriyadhu da. Aana nalaiku vera maadhiri irukka koodadhum illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the street corner, lighting a single diya on the broken pavement.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mahesh, nalikuravan ku nethu illa, inniku illa, nalaiku illa nu bayam. Aana adhu dhan adhan vali — adhu niruthaama varum. Aana nalla naalum varum. Appa solla maata, aana avanukku theriyum."
thirukkural: இன்றும் வருவது கொல்லோ நெருநலும் கொன்றது போலும் நிரப்பு.
english_translation: The killing Want of yesterday Will it pester me even to-day?
moral: Poverty's cruelest weapon is the terrifying thought that tomorrow will be exactly like today.

title: தூக்கம் மறந்த
hook: Chitra's family of five shares one room — and sleep is a luxury nobody can afford.
story: Five people in one room. Amma snored. Appa tossed. The baby cried. Chitra's Akka — 16 years old — stayed awake, staring at the fan. "Akka, thookam varadha?" Akka didn't answer. Amma said earlier, "Rent kattu vida kaasu illa." Chitra held Akka's hand in the dark. "Sari Akka, naanum thoongamaataen." Akka finally closed her eyes.
tamil_dialogues: Chitra: 'Akka, unakku thoongama varadha?'
Akka: 'Illada. Kai la kaasu illadha nenachadhum, manasu ellam kaliya irukku. Thoongradhu epdi?'
Chitra: 'Apdi naa... naan un kooda irukken Akka.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the window sill of the small room, the cool night breeze touching everyone's face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Chitra, nalikuravan ku thoongama varadhu. Neruppukulla pOi thoongalam — aana kaasu illadha nenachu thoonga mudiyadhu. Aana nee un akka kai pudichadhu dhan oru thookam. Adhu dhan unmaiyaana urangu."
thirukkural: நெருப்பினுள் துஞ்சலும் ஆகும் நிரப்பினுள் யாதொன்றும் கண்பாடு அரிது.
english_translation: One may sleep in the midst of fire In want a wink of sleep is rare
moral: A person can sleep surrounded by fire, but poverty steals even the smallest wink of rest.

title: கடைசி மூச்சு
hook: Paandi's Paati says she'd rather die than beg anymore — and Paandi realizes the depth of her pain.
story: Paati was 74, alone. Every day she sat at the temple with a tin can. "Ammava, kai kudunga." One evening she returned empty. She sat on the cot. "Paandi, enakku innum jeevicha dhan enna? Uppum kadiyum illadha vaazhkai." Paandi hugged her. "Paati, apdi solladha." Paati wiped her eyes. "Saavadhu dhan nalla irukku da. Unakku vendiyadhu dhan innum irukken."
tamil_dialogues: Paandi: 'Paati, neenga jeevikanum Paati. Enakku vendiyadhu!'
Paati: 'Uppu mattum dhan saapdu, kaasu illadha, bayam bayama. Saavadhu dhan enakku nalla irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Paati, holding her wrinkled hand, his eyes closed in silence.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Paandi, un Paati sollradhu thappu illa. Oru level ku approm nalikuravan manushanukku saavadhu dhan nalladhu maadhiri theriyum. Aana adhu dhan periya bayam — vaazhkai mela aasai illadha pona, adhu life kooda illa."
thirukkural: துப்புர வில்லார் துவரத் துறவாமை உப்பிற்கும் காடிக்கும் கூற்று.
english_translation: Renounce their lives the poor must Or salt and gruel go to waste
moral: When poverty strips away all purpose, even life itself feels like a burden too heavy to carry.

title: கேக்க தெரியுமா?
theme: Begging / Ask Those Who Can Give
hook: Venba needs bus fare to reach school — and she must learn who to ask and who to avoid.
story: Venba missed the school bus. She needed 15 rupees for the town bus. A man with a briefcase stood nearby. She almost asked him. Then Chithappa appeared. "Chithappa! Enakku bus fare venum." Chithappa gave her 20. "Vaa, drop panniduven." The briefcase man was counting coins and got into a rickshaw without looking at her. Chithappa said, "Edhachu onnu theriyum da — yaar kitta kaekkanumnu."
tamil_dialogues: Venba: 'Chithappa, andha aalu kitta kaekka ponaen. Avar enna pakkala.'
Chithappa: 'Sari da panna. Andha maadhiri aalungalukku kudutha pOlum adhu avanukku dhan. Nee yaar kitta kaekkanumnu therinjuttanu irukkanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leans against the bus stand pillar, holding a ticket in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Venba, nee sonnadhu sari dhan. Yaar kitta irandhadhunu therinjudhadhu dhan mukkiyam. Karappadha manasu ullavan kitta dhan irandha nalla irukkum. Adhu dhan arivu."
thirukkural: இரக்க இரத்தக்கார்க் காணின் கரப்பின் அவர்பழி தம்பழி அன்று.
english_translation: Demand from those who can supply Default is theirs when they deny
moral: Beg from those who can give — if they refuse, the shame is theirs, not yours.

title: மனசு விட்டு
hook: Bala is embarrassed to take free food at the temple — until the server's smile changes everything.
story: Bala's Amma had no food at home. She sent him to the temple annadanam. Bala stood in line, head down. When his turn came, he couldn't look up. The server — a fat man with a gold chain — said, "Enna da, thottil kulla irundha maadhiri? Muttai eduthuko da!" He pushed a big banana leaf. Bala ate. The man refilled his rice twice. "Saapdu da, unakku dhan!" Bala smiled for the first time that week.
tamil_dialogues: Bala: 'Periyavar, enakku naanam aagudhu.'
Server: 'Naana maana edhukku da. Vaai vitta saapdu! Ithu unakku dhan. Naan manasu vittu dhan podren.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits next to the server, ladling rice onto Bala's leaf with a steady hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bala, unakku naanam aagura maadhiri, naanam aaganum. Aana andha periyavar sonna maadhiri — manasu vittu kudutha, adhu dhan vaanga santhosham. Irandhadhu kedachu, adhu dhan inbam."
thirukkural: இன்பம் ஒருவற்கு இரத்தல் இரந்தவை துன்பம் உறாஅ வரின்.
english_translation: Even demand becomes a joy When the things comes without annoy
moral: When what you ask for comes without shame or sting, even begging can feel like a gift.

title: கரப்பில்லா மனசு
theme: Begging / Open Hearts Make Asking Beautiful
hook: Little Esther needs to borrow salt from a neighbor — and learns that some hearts are open like the sea.
story: Amma was making fish curry. No salt. "Esther, akka pakkathu aunty kitta kai kudumma nu kaeku." Esther was scared. That aunty always frowned. She went slowly. Aunty saw her. "Enna da?" Esther whispered. Aunty laughed loud. "Uppu dhana? Edu da! Edhavadhu venum, kaeku." She gave a full bowl. Esther ran back. "Appa! Aunty sonna — kaeku nu! Santhosha dhan kuduthaanga!"
tamil_dialogues: Esther: 'Aunty, uppu... oru chinna...'
Aunty: 'Uppu dhana? Adhu dhan eduthutu po. Immathiri saamangalukku naanam dhan venam da. Ketta dhan kedaikum!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the shore, a bowl of salt in his lap, laughing with the waves.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Esther, karappadha manasu ullavar kitta kaekradhu dhan nalladhu. Avanga manasu dhaan uppu maadhiri — jeevan kodukkum. Appo dhan kaekradhum nalla irukku."
