thirukkural: கரப்பிலா நெஞ்சின் கடனறிவார் முன்நின்று இரப்புமோ ரேஎர் உடைத்து.
english_translation: Request has charm form open hearts Who know the duty on their part
moral: Asking from an open, generous heart has a beauty all its own — both the giver and receiver feel it.

title: மறைக்காத கை
hook: Siva watches his aunt ask for help from a stranger — and the stranger thanks her for asking.
story: Siva's Athai needed money for medicine. A stranger sat on the hospital bench. Athai approached. "Saar, enakku oru uDavi..." The man looked up. "Enna venum?" Athai told him. He gave 500 rupees and said, "Nee kaekradhuna naan dhan santhosham. Kaekama irundha, enakku theriyaadhu." Athai cried. Siva was confused. "Athai, avar dhan kaasu kuduthaar. Aana santhosham aaruku?"
tamil_dialogues: Siva: 'Athai, neenga kaettenga. Avar dhan kaasu kuduthaar. Aana avar dhan santhosham?'
Athai: 'Ama da. Karappadha manushanukku kaedradhu avanga santhosham. Avanga kaekama irundha, avanga kai kooda edukka mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the hospital bench, the 500-rupee note folded like a flower in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Siva, un Athai sonna maadhiri dhan — karappadha manushan kitta kaekradhu, avanukku kooda santhosham. Renduperukum dhan periya dhanam."
thirukkural: இரத்தலும் ஈதலே போலும் கரத்தல் கனவிலும் தேற்றாதார் மாட்டு.
english_translation: Like giving even asking seems From those who hide not even in dreams
moral: When a heart is truly open, asking and giving become the same sacred act.

title: கெடுதல் இல்லா மனிதர்
hook: Mala's family has no food for three days — until a stranger appears at their door.
story: Three days. Only kanji water. Mala's stomach hurt. Paati sat at the well, not drawing water. "Paati, enna pannradhu?" Just then, a vegetable cart stopped. The cart woman — face like leather, hands cracked — unloaded a bag of brinjal, some drumstick. "Eduthuko. Naan innaiku vira panna mudiyadhu, idhu mathi." Paati folded her hands. Mala asked, "Paati, avangaluku enna dhan vendum?"
tamil_dialogues: Mala: 'Paati, avanga nammala paathu vangi kuduthuthu. Enna dhan?'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan da — inga ippadi manushan irukka dhan, naama irandhu vaazha mudiyudhu. Avanga maadhiri manushan irukka dhan ella kaekradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the cart, a brinjal in one hand, a drumstick in the other, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mala, un Paati sonna maadhiri — karappadha manushan illadha pona, yaarum yaraiyum kaekka mudiyadhu. Adhu dhan ulagathula periya viswasam."
thirukkural: கரப்பிலார் வையகத்து உண்மையால் கண்ணின்று இரப்பவர் மேற்கொள் வது.
english_translation: The needy demand for help because The world has men who don't refuse
moral: Beggars can ask only because there are still people in this world whose hands are open.

title: பகிர்ந்த மனசு
hook: Jegan's vegetable cart fails and he can't feed his sister — until a competitor shares his own stock.
story: Jegan sold vegetables near the lake. A lorry accident spoiled his entire stock. Morning, nothing to sell. His sister hadn't eaten. The next cart owner — Muthu, his rival — walked over. "Enna da, cart la omum illa?" Jegan nodded. Muthu dumped half his tomatoes, half his beans onto Jegan's cart. "Vidra da. Naan un kitta kaasu vaangitu irundhen. Innaiku nee vira. Nalaiku naan."
tamil_dialogues: Jegan: 'Muthu, unakku dhan vendum. Nee enakku enna kuduka?'
Muthu: 'Saami dhan kuduthaan da. Naan kudutha maadhiri feel pannadha. Karappu illadha manushan iruntha, nalikuravanum odidum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the two carts, tomatoes and beans piled high on both.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Jegan, un competitor dhan unakku pottu kuduthaan. Karappu illadha manushan iruntha dhan nalikuravan odidum. Athu dhan parigaram."
thirukkural: கரப்பிடும்பை யில்லாரைக் காணின் நிரப்பிடும்பை எல்லாம் ஒருங்கு கெடும்.
english_translation: The pain of poverty shall die Before the free who don't deny
moral: In the presence of a truly generous heart, the ache of poverty simply vanishes.

title: நகைக்காத கை
hook: Pavi is scared of the annadanam because last time someone mocked her — but a new server changes everything.
story: Last month at annadanam, the server had sneered. "Innaiku veena dhan vandruka? Nethu vandha maadhiri illa." Pavi had run away crying. This time, a young woman served. She saw Pavi's face. "Enna da, bayama irukka? Vaa da, unakku dhan idhu." She filled her leaf. "Nee innum velaya dhan. Naan kuduthu dhan aaganum." Pavi ate until her stomach was round.
tamil_dialogues: Pavi: 'Akka, andha aalu ennai sirichaanga.'
Young Woman: 'Avalukku theriyadhu da. Unakku vendiyadhu dhan inga kodukradhu. Nee nenchula valikama saapdu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands behind the young woman, pouring a thin stream of sambar onto Pavi's leaf.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pavi, sirichu kodukradhu kudutha maadhiri aagadhu. Aana manasu vittu, santhosha kudutha — adhu dhan periya dhanam. Andha akka maadhiri."
thirukkural: இகழ்ந்தெள்ளாது ஈவாரைக் காணின் மகிழ்ந்துள்ளம் உள்ளுள் உவப்பது உடைத்து.
english_translation: When givers without scorn impart A thrill of delight fills the heart
moral: When someone gives without a hint of mockery, joy floods the heart like cool water on a parched day.

title: தேவைப்படும் மனிதர்
hook: Santhosh asks why beggars exist — Thatha's answer turns the world upside down.
story: Pilgrims walked the street. Beggars lined both sides. "Thatha, ivanga enna irukkaanga? Velaiku poga mudiyadha?" Thatha didn't answer immediately. He bought a packet of biscuits, gave one to a blind old man, one to Santhosh. "Namma kudutha dhan avanga irukkaanga. Avanga illadha pona, namma yaarukku kodukka mudiyum? Avanga dhan nammala nalla manushan aakuraanga."
tamil_dialogues: Santhosh: 'Thatha, ivanga illadha pona enna?'
Thatha: 'Avanga illadha pona, ungala maadhiri nalla manushanukku aasai irukkaadhu. Avanga irukka dhan naama kodukka therinjukkurathu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits cross-legged among the beggars, accepting a biscuit and passing it to the next person.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Santhosh, un Thatha sollradhu sari dhan. Kaekravan illadha pona, kodukravanum illa. Ulagam ore puppet show aagidum. Irandhu vaanga koodadhu nu nenaikuradhu thappu."
thirukkural: இரப்பாரை இல்லாயின் ஈர்ங்கண்மா ஞாலம் மரப்பாவை சென்றுவந் தற்று.
english_translation: This grand cool world shall move to and fro Sans Askers like a puppet show
moral: Without those who ask, this world would be nothing but a puppet show — giving gives life meaning.

title: யாருக்காக?
hook: Revathi's mother weaves silk for the rich and wonders — if nobody needed, would giving exist?
story: Amma wove the finest silk. Rich women bought her sarees. Sometimes they gave extra money. "Amma, avanga enakku kasu kuduthaanga." One day Revathi asked, "Amma, namma irandha dhan avanga kodukkaanga? Avanga kodukka vendiyadhu?" Amma stopped the loom. "Un sonna maadhiri dhan. Namma illadha pona, avanga kodukradhukku artham illa. Naama dhan avanga nalla manushan mathiri feel panna vaikrom."
tamil_dialogues: Revathi: 'Amma, avanga dhan kodukraanga. Naama dhan kaekrom. Appo naama dhan periyavanga?'
Amma: 'Illada. Naama irandha dhan avanga kodukraanga. Avanga kodukradhukku naama dhan karanam. Rendume ore.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the loom, pulling the shuttle, silk threads glowing between his fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Revathi, un Amma sonna maadhiri — kaekravan illadha pona kodukravan perumai irukkaadhu. Irandhadhum koduthadhum ore naadagam. Rendume mukkiyam."
thirukkural: ஈவார்கண் என்னுண்டாம் தோற்றம் இரந்துகோள் மேவார் இலாஅக் கடை.
english_translation: Where stands the glory of givers Without obligation seekers?
moral: The giver's glory exists only because there are those who ask — both are essential to the dance.

title: கோபம் வேணாம்
hook: Pandi gets angry when a shopkeeper refuses him free water — Chithi teaches him why anger helps nothing.
story: After the cyclone, the camp ran out of water. Pandi ran to a shop. "Saar, thanni kudunga." The shopkeeper waved him off. "Illada. Kaasu iruntha kuduppen." Pandi kicked the ground. "Chithi! Avan kodukka maatan. Naan avan kitta poi..." Chithi held his arm. "Vidu da. Avan kodukka maatran. Adhukku kovapadradhuna enna use?" She walked him to the camp tap. A trickle came. "Pathiya? Adhu dhan — kaekradhu sari, aana kovama irukka koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Pandi: 'Chithi, avan enakku thanni kuda kodukka maatan! Naan avan kitta...'
Chithi: 'Vidu da. Avan kodukka maatan. Nee kovama irundha, unakku dhan vali. Ketta pOthu, avan kodutha nalla illadhadhuna adhu avan paavam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the broken camp tap, coaxing a thin stream of water into a child's cup.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pandi, nee kaetten. Avan kodukka maatan. Aana athukku nee kovapadradhu dhan sari illa. Un nalikuravan unna kovapada vaikum. Ne adha vittutu pO."
thirukkural: இரப்பான் வெகுளாமை வேண்டும் நிரப்பிடும்பை தானேயும் சாலும் கரி.
english_translation: The needy should not scowl at "No" His need another's need must show
moral: A beggar must not get angry at refusal — his own poverty is proof enough of life's hardships.

title: வேணாம் சார்
hook: A rich man offers Karthik free clothes — but he refuses and earns something greater.
story: Karthik's Appa worked in a garment factory. A businessman saw Karthik's torn shirt. "Vaa da, unakku nalla shirt vangi kudhukren." Karthik thought about it. New shirt. Free. Then he said, "Vendam saar. Naa Appa kitta sonna, avan vangi kuduppaan." The man laughed. "Un Appa kaasu illadha aalu." Karthik's face burned. "Avan illadhavan dhan. Aana avan kaekka vaikka maatan." The man stopped laughing. He later gave Karthik's Appa extra overtime work.
tamil_dialogues: Businessman: 'Vaa da, venum na nalla shirt pottukko.'
Karthik: 'Vendam saar. Naa kaekka maaten. Naa nadandhu vaanga therinju dhan aaganum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the factory gate, handing Karthik a bundle of neatly folded clothes — earned.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Karthik, nee sonna maadhiri — nalla manushan kitta kooda irandha vida, nadandhu vaangradhu dhan periyadhu. Kodiyiruppadhu dhan periya selvu."
thirukkural: கரவாது உவந்தீயும் கண்ணன்னார் கண்ணும் இரவாமை கோடி உறும்.
english_translation: Not to beg is billions worth E'en from eye-like friends who give with mirth
moral: Not begging — even from the most loving giver — is worth more than billions.

title: சொந்த கால்ல
theme: Dignity of Not Begging / Standing Tall
hook: Ayyanar's mother loses her job and considers begging — Ayyanar refuses to let her.
story: Amma worked at the cracker unit. It shut down. She sat at home three days. "Ayyanar, paati veetuku poi, kaasu kettu vangren." Ayyanar grabbed her hand. "Amma, apdi pannadha! Naa velai pannitu dhan aaganum." He went to a tea stall. "Uncle, unga kitta velai venum." The uncle gave him cleaning work. Evening, he brought 50 rupees. "Amma, idhu dhan kuduthathu." Amma cried and held the note like gold.
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Ayyanar, naan paati veetuku poi kaettena?'
Ayyanar: 'Amma! Adhu dhan thappu. Naa velai pannitu dhan unga kuduthu vaikkum. Irukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the tea stall, wiping a glass with a cloth, watching Ayyanar work.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ayyanar, un Amma kaekka ponaa, nee thaduthadhu dhan sari. Velai panni, sambathichu, adha vaangradhu dhan perumai. Ulagathula vera vali illa."
thirukkural: இரந்தும் உயிர்வாழ்தல் வேண்டின் பரந்து கெடுக உலகியற்றி யான்.
english_translation: Let World-Maker loiter and rot If "beg and live" be human fate
moral: If the only way to live is by begging, then better to curse the world and work with your own hands.

title: எளுது தாழ்வு
theme: Dignity of Not Begging / Hard Truth
hook: Mari overhears someone say "just beg" — and learns why that's the hardest road of all.
story: Mari helped Uncle push the vegetable cart. A man passing by said, "Enna da kashtam? Edhavadhu kaettu vanga dhan." Uncle's face turned dark. He didn't say anything. Mari asked, "Uncle, avar sonna maadhiri kaekradhu nalla dhan illa?" Uncle stopped the cart. "Mari, kaekradhu dhan ulagathula periya kashtam. Adha vida kashtam vera edhavathu irukka? 'Saapda illa, kaekka pOren' nu sollradhu dhan romba kashtam."
tamil_dialogues: Mari: 'Uncle, kaekradhu nalla dhan pola irukku?'
Uncle: 'Illada Mari. Kaekradhu vida kashtam vera illa. Adha vida sulabam vera illa. Enna pannanum? Adhu dhan periya kadinam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar pushes the vegetable cart alongside Uncle, his shoulders against the weight.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mari, un Uncle sonna maadhiri — kaekradhu dhan periya kashtam. 'Kaekka pOren' nu sollradhu dhan ulagathula periya aayudham. Atha vida adikradhu vera illa."
thirukkural: இன்மை இடும்பை இரந்துதீர் வாமென்னும் வன்மையின் வன்பாட்ட தில்.
english_translation: Nothing is hard like hard saying "We end poverty by begging"
moral: The hardest cruelty in the world is the easy advice, "Just go beg."

title: கேக்க மாட்டேன்
theme: Dignity of Not Begging / Strength in Want
hook: Sengeni watches her Periyappa go hungry for three days rather than ask his wealthy brother for help.
story: Periyappa owned nothing but a small coconut grove. Drought hit. His rich brother had a borewell. "Periyappa, chithappa kitta thanni kaetu vaanga." Periyappa shook his head. "Mudiyadhu." Three days, no proper meal. Sengeni brought him a coconut. He drank the water. "Periyappa, enna chithappa kitta kaekka maatreenga?" He looked at the dry earth. "Sengeni, kaekradhu dhan paavam. Naan irandha paavam. Aana kaekka maaten."
tamil_dialogues: Sengeni: 'Periyappa, pOi kaettu vangalam. Chithappa nalla dhan irukkaar.'
Periyappa: 'Avadhu nalla irukalaam. Aana naan kaekka maaten. Paasi irukalaam, aana kaekka koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a coconut tree, a fallen coconut split open beside him, water glistening.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sengeni, un Periyappa kaekka maatan. Avan kashtapadraan, aana avan periyavan. Ulagam mothama irundhaalum avan kaekka maatan. Adhu dhan unmaiyaana perumai."
thirukkural: இடமெல்லாம் கொள்ளாத் தகைத்தே இடமில்லாக் காலும் இரவொல்லாச் சால்பு.
english_translation: All space is small before the great Who beg not e'en in want acute
moral: True greatness fills the universe — it never bends to beg, even in the worst poverty.

title: சொந்த உழைப்பு
theme: Dignity of Not Begging / Sweeter Than Gruel
hook: Velu is embarrassed that his family eats only kanji — until he learns it's the sweetest food on earth.
story: Velu's school friends ate biryani, fried chicken. Velu's lunch had only kanji — thin rice gruel. He hid behind a tree to eat. Appa saw him. "Enna da, marathukku pinnadi?" Velu cried. "Appa, kanji dhan. Yarum kaekka maatan." Appa sat beside him. "Velu, indha kanji dhan unakku innikku kedachadhu. Naan veyilla ninnu ulavinaen. Nee adha saapdra. Adhu dhan ulagathula periya ruchi."
tamil_dialogues: Velu: 'Appa, matta pasanga biryani saapdraanga. Namma kanji dhan.'
Appa: 'Avanga biryani avan appa kaasu koduthu vangi kuduthaadhu. Namma kanji naa veyila ninnu virai kudhuthu vaanginaadhu. Edhula periya ruchi? Nee sollu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the paddy field, a bowl of kanji in his hands, steam rising like a blessing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Velu, un Appa sonna maadhiri — namma velai panni vaangina soru dhan ulagathula periya ruchi. Adhu dhan amudham. Adha vida periya inbam illa."
thirukkural: தெண்ணீர் அடுபுற்கை ஆயினும் தாள்தந்தது உண்ணலின் ஊங்கினிய தில்.
english_translation: Though gruel thin, nothing is sweet Like the food earned by labour's sweat
moral: Even a bowl of thin gruel tastes sweeter than a feast when it's earned by your own sweat.

title: நாக்கின் தாழ்வு
theme: Dignity of Not Begging / Tongue Shamed
hook: Gomathi sees their neighbor ask for water for his cow — and the humiliation burns into her memory.
story: At the cattle market, neighbor Karuppan's cow was thirsty. He went to the rich man's tap. "Saar, pasukku oru lotta thanni." The rich man sneered. "Pasuvukka? Unakku dhan venum." Karuppan's face turned red. He walked away without water. Gomathi's Thatha whispered, "Paathiya? Avan kaekka ponaan. Naa pathaalum, avan nala idhu dhan olivil. Aana Gomathi, nee kaekka koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Gomathi: 'Thatha, avar pasukku dhan kaettenga. Athu thappu illa?'
Thatha: 'Thappu illa. Aana kaekradhu dhan naakku ilivu. Ethukku sonnaalum, kaekradhu dhan ilivu. Adhu dhan unmai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar leads the thirsty cow to the river, letting it drink freely without asking anyone.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Gomathi, un Thatha sonna maadhiri — ethukku sonnaalum kaekradhu dhan naakku ilivu. Pasuvukka aanaalum kooda. Adhu dhan periya thappu."
thirukkural: ஆவிற்கு நீரென்று இரப்பினும் நாவிற்கு இரவின் இளிவந்த தில்.
english_translation: It may be water for the cow Begging tongue is mean anyhow
moral: Even if it's water for a thirsty cow, the tongue that begs is still humiliated.

title: யாரிடம் போறது?
theme: Dignity of Not Begging / Choose Wisely
hook: Anjali must collect money for her sick brother — and Amma teaches her whom to approach.
story: Kutti was sick. Medicine cost 200 rupees. Amma had 50. "Anjali, sandhai la poi, nalla manushan kitta kaetu." Anjali stood at the market entrance. A fat man with a gold chain walked by. She almost spoke. Then she saw Muthulakshmi Akka — the flower seller who always shared her lunch. "Akka, en kutti... valikkudhu." Muthulakshmi didn't ask. She opened her pouch and gave 150. Anjali cried. "Amma sonna, sari aal kitta dhan kaekkanumnu."
tamil_dialogues: Anjali: 'Akka, ungalukku dhan theriyum nalla manushannu.'
Muthulakshmi: 'Adhu dhan da. Karappadhavan kitta kaekradhu vida, nalla manushan kitta dhan kaekkanum. Un amma solradhu sari dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the flower stall, weaving a garland, his eyes meeting Anjali's with warmth.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Anjali, un Amma sonna maadhiri — karappadhavan kitta kaekka koodadhu. Avan koduthaalum, adhu avanuku perumai illa, unakku ilivu. Nalla manushan kitta dhan kaekkanum."
thirukkural: இரப்பன் இரப்பாரை எல்லாம் இரப்பின் கரப்பார் இரவன்மின் என்று.
english_translation: If beg they must I beg beggers Not to beg from shrinking misers
moral: If you must beg, never beg from a miser — their giving only deepens your shame.

title: மூழ்கும் படகு
theme: Dignity of Not Begging / Shipwreck of Asking
hook: Selvaraj's father's boat is damaged — he asks a rich boat owner for help and gets destroyed.
story: Appa's catamaran broke. Three days of no fishing. Selvaraj said, "Appa, Ramanujam kitta kaetu vaanga. Avanukku moonu boats irukku." Appa hesitated. Then he went. Ramanujam laughed. "Enna da, unakku boat venuma? Nee enna thandhadhu?" He gave an old, cracked boat. Appa took it. It leaked. It sank mid-sea. Appa barely swam back. He sat on the shore, wet, broken. "Selvaraj, inime yaar kittaum kaekka maaten. Idhu dhan kai kudukradhu."
tamil_dialogues: Selvaraj: 'Appa, avar dhan boat kuduthaar. Adhu avan paavam.'
Appa: 'Illada. Boat kuduthaan, aana adhu saavadhu maadhiri irundhadhu. Karappadhavan kuduthaalum, adhu paavam dhan. Adhu dhan kaekradhal varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands knee-deep in the sea, pulling the broken boat ashore with steady hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Selvaraj, un Appa sonna maadhiri — karappadhavan kitta kaekradhu, avan kuduthaalum adhu sabam dhan. Adhu dhan kaekradhal kedaikkum. Appa dhan sari."
thirukkural: இரவென்னும் ஏமாப்பில் தோணி கரவென்னும் பார்தாக்கப் பக்கு விடும்.
english_translation: The hapless bark of beggary splits On the rock of refusing hits
moral: The fragile boat of begging shatters on the rock of refusal — better never to board it at all.

title: உருகும் மனசு
theme: Dignity of Not Begging / The Pain of Refusing
hook: Jayashree sees a beggar at the orphanage gate — and learns why her warden says no with tears.
story: A thin woman came to the orphanage gate. "Akka, kutti kulla irukku, saapda omum illa." Warden Akka went inside, came with rice. But the woman asked for money. Warden Akka closed her eyes. "Illada. Enaku kudutha dhan aaganum. Aana enakku idhu dhan irukku." She gave rice but not money. The woman left. Jayashree was angry. "Akka! Avanga kutti kulla irukkaanga! Neenga enna kodukka maatinga?" Warden Akka showed her the empty orphanage kitchen.
tamil_dialogues: Jayashree: 'Akka! Avanga kaettenga. Neenga kodukka maatinga!'
Warden Akka: 'Jayashree, namma kuda ore padangu dhan. Naan kodutha dhan aaganum. Aana andha amma ku irandhadhu dhan kashtam. Yenakku adha vida kodukka mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the orphanage gate, a bag of rice on his shoulder, tears in his eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Jayashree, un Warden Akka sonna maadhiri — kaekravan manasu urugudhu. Aana kodukka mudiyadha pothu, adhu dhan periya vedhanai. Renduperukum dhan vali."
thirukkural: இரவுள்ள உள்ளம் உருகும் கரவுள்ள உள்ளதூஉம் இன்றிக் கெடும்.
english_translation: The heart at thought of beggars melts; It dies at repulsing insults
moral: The heart melts at the sight of a beggar — but saying no kills something inside the one who refuses too.

title: வேணாம்னு சொல்லாத
theme: Dignity of Not Begging / The Word "No" Kills
hook: Priyan watches a rich tourist say "no" to a hungry boy — and that "no" changes Priyan forever.
story: On Marina Beach, a small boy — maybe 6 — approached a tourist. "Saar, enakku oru buns." The tourist didn't even look. "Illada pO." The boy stood there, frozen. His eyes went empty. He walked to the sea wall and sat. Priyan watched. He bought two buns, gave one to the boy. The boy didn't say thanks. He just ate. Priyan's Anna said, "Andha 'illada pO' nu sollradhu — adhu dhan avanai konjudhu. Paaru, avan kannula oru oli illa."
tamil_dialogues: Priyan: 'Anna, avan 'illada pO' nu sonnadhum, avan kaLichi poittan.'
Anna: 'Ama da. 'Illada pO' nu oru varthai. Adhu dhan avan uyira edukkum. Atha vida periya aayudham illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the sea wall beside the hungry boy, sharing the bun, looking out at the waves.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Priyan, un Anna sonna maadhiri — 'illada pO' nu oru varthai. Adhu dhan avan uyira edukkum. Karappadhavan 'illada' solli avan uyira edukraan. Adhu dhan periya paavam."
thirukkural: கரப்பவர்க்கு யாங்கொளிக்கும் கொல்லோ இரப்பவர் சொல்லாடப் போஒம் உயிர்.
english_translation: The word "No" kills the begger's life Where can the niggard's life be safe?
moral: When a miser says "no," it kills the beggar's spirit — where can such cruelty hide from justice?

title: மனுஷன் போல
hook: Rohit's classmate looks and talks like a normal boy — but his actions show a different truth.
story: Suresh sat next to Rohit. Same uniform, same age. But Suresh would push little kids, steal chalk, blame others. One day, a first-grader fell. Rohit helped him up. Suresh laughed. "Unakku enna da, periya amma?" Rohit looked at his teacher. Teacher said, "Rohit, Suresh manushan maadhiri dhan irukkaan. Aana avan manasula manushan illa. Athu dhan periya bayam."
tamil_dialogues: Rohit: 'Miss, Suresh namma maadhiri dhan irukkaan. Aana enna avan ipdi?'
Teacher: 'Adhu dhan da — avan manushan maadhiri dhan irukkaan. Aana ullae ellam kuppai. Manushan pola irundhu manushanaga illadhadhu dhan periya thappu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the playground swing, watching Suresh with sad eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Rohit, un teacher sonna maadhiri — manushan maadhiri irundhum manushanaga illadhadhu dhan periya kozhappam. Avan kadai, kai, kaal — ellam irukku. Aana manasu maathiri dhan irukkaadhu."
thirukkural: மக்களே போல்வர் கயவர் அவரன்ன ஒப்பாரி யாங்கண்ட தில்.
english_translation: The mean seem men only in form We have never seen such a sham
moral: Base people look human on the outside — but inside, there's nothing human at all.

title: கெட்டவனுக்கு கவல இல்ல
hook: Meenakshi sees a dishonest shopkeeper prosper — and asks why goodness doesn't pay.
story: Bazaar shop. The owner mixed old rice with new, charged double. Meenakshi's Appa bought from him — found worms inside. "Appa, avaru dhan mosadram pannraar. Aana avanuku nalla dhan pogudhu. Namma ebbadiyo irukkom." Appa didn't answer. Evening, they passed the shop. The owner was counting money, laughing. Appa said, "Meenakshi, avan sirikka dhan pannuvaan. Aana avan manasu la omum illa. Unakku theriyadha, aana avanuku oru nalla thoongam kooda kedaikaadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Meenakshi: 'Appa, avaru mosadram pannitu irukkaar. Aana avanuku nalla dhan irukku!'
Appa: 'Meenakshi, avan siripu paaru. Manasukulla ellam avulichi irukku. Avan seyal dhan avanuku sirikkum. Aana adhu oru nalla siripu illa — adhu dhan avanuku kedachadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits behind the shopkeeper's counter, silently weighing old rice against new, shaking his head.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Meenakshi, un Appa sonna maadhiri — kettavanukku nalla irukka mudiyum, aana adhu sari illa. Avan manasula aavali dhan — adhu dhan avanuku kedachadhu. Aana avanuku unmaiyaana sirippu kedaiyaadhu."
thirukkural: நன்றறி வாரிற் கயவர் திருவுடையர் நெஞ்சத்து அவலம் இலர்.
english_translation: The base seem richer than the good For no care enters their heart or head
moral: The wicked may grow rich — but their wealth comes with an empty heart that feels no shame.

title: Appa's Long Ride
theme: Agony of Parting / Separation from Family
hook: Divya's Appa works in Bangalore and only comes home once a month — she counts the days.
story: Divya marked 30 boxes on the calendar. "Amma, innum 20 days dhan?" Every night she kept Appa's photo under her pillow. When Appa finally came, she ran so fast she fell. Appa scooped her up. "Unaku miss pannittu irundhen da kanna." Divya held tight. "Naan miss dhan pannala, naan full-a miss pannitten!"
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Amma, Appa varadhu innum nerama? Enakku romba miss pannudhu.'
Amma: 'Adhaan da, avarukum apdi dhan. Velai dhan.'
Appa: 'Vandhuten da. Innum oru naalum late-a varamaaten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the bus stand bench, an old calendar in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirivendradhu oru kashtam dhan da. Aana atha sahadhikradhu dhan unmaiyaana kaadhal. Nee appa va miss pannradhu, avarukum unna miss pannradhu — adhu dhan kaadhal."
thirukkural: செல்லாமை உண்டேல் எனக்குரை மற்றுநின் வல்வரவு வாழ்வார்க் குரை.
english_translation: Tell me if you but do not leave, Your quick return to those who live
moral: Parting is painful, but love grows stronger through distance.

title: Thatha's Hug
theme: Agony of Parting / Fear of Losing Love
hook: Karthik's Thatha goes to the hospital and Karthik is terrified he won't come back.
story: Thatha was always home. One morning, he clutched his chest and Amma rushed him to the hospital. Karthik sat on the farm steps all day. "Adhu dhan en life-a." The cows mooed. The well pump squeaked. Everything felt empty. At 8pm, Thatha walked in, tired but smiling. Karthik didn't let go all night. "Paathukkaama irundha... enna pannuvene?"
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Thatha, neenga illama naan saapda maaten.'
Thatha: 'Naan irukken da. Enga poven?'
Karthik: 'Neenga illadha veedu, adhu veedu illa Thatha.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the farm well, watching the sunset.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirivendradhu bayam dhan da. Aana nee pathukitta maadhiri, kaadhal iruntha, pirivum sahadhika mudiyum. Bayatha vida kaadhal dhan periyadhu."
thirukkural: இன்கண் உடைத்தவர் பார்வல் பிரிவஞ்சும் புன்கண் உடைத்தால் புணர்வு.
english_translation: His sight itself was pleasing, near Embrace pains now by partings fear
moral: When love is deep, even the thought of parting brings fear.

title: The Transfer Letter
theme: Agony of Parting / Trust in Separation
hook: Swetha's best friend moves to Delhi — she wonders if friendship can survive distance.
story: Priya's father got transferred. "Delhi ku porein di." Swetha cried all night. "Amma, aprom ava enna marandhudama?" Amma sat beside her. "Nee avanoda nenachuko. Adhu dhan friendship. Dooram irundha odidhum nu bayam, aana nambikkai iruntha nadakkum." Swetha and Priya video-called every Sunday for 3 years.
tamil_dialogues: Swetha: 'Amma, ava dooram poitta. Ennakitta pesa maata pola.'
Amma: 'Nambikkai vacha dhan da. Pirivu irundha porumai venum.'
Priya (call): 'Dei, miss pannitu irukken di! Mudiya unna.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by a phone booth, receiver in hand, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirivendradhu oru theerpu dhan. Appadi irundha, nambikkai venum. Un nambikkai dhan atha niruthum."
thirukkural: அரிதரோ தேற்றம் அறிவுடையார் கண்ணும் பிரிவோ ரிடத்துண்மை யான்.
english_translation: On whom shall I lay my trust hence While parting lurks in knowing ones?
moral: Even wise hearts struggle with trust when separation looms.

title: The Promise Rock
hook: Murali's uncle promised to take him fishing but never showed up — Murali learns about real promises.
story: Uncle said, "Saturday, naangal meen pidikka ponom." Murali woke at 5am, packed lunch, waited at the gate. 10am. 12pm. Uncle never came. Murali's heart cracked. "Appa, avan sonna dhan! Yaarum nambikkai kuduthu apdi pannradhu?" Appa took him to the rock fort. "Nee sari dhan da. Promise kudutha katturadhu dhan nalla manushan."
tamil_dialogues: Murali: 'Appa, avan varala. Enakku romba feel achu.'
Appa: 'Unaku feel aagradhu sari da. Promise pannitu varaadhadhu thappu.'
Murali: 'Naan matum evanum kitta promise pannitu vara maaten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the rock fort, watching clouds drift.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Solli kuduthu adha kaapadradhu dhan unmai. Vaa maadhiri sonnu poidradhu — athula veruppu dhan. Nee solli katturadhu dhan periyavana aakum."
thirukkural: அளித்தஞ்சல் என்றவர் நீப்பின் தெளித்தசொல் தேறியார்க்கு உண்டோ தவறு.
english_translation: He parts whose love told me -fear not Is my trust in him at default?
moral: A promise broken is trust betrayed — never promise what you can't keep.

title: Don't Go Akka
hook: Pooja's elder sister leaves for college in Chennai — Pooja hides her chappal so she can't go.
story: Siddhu Akka got into college. Pooja was happy but also not. The night before, Pooja hid Akka's slippers. "Akka, unna vida maaten." Amma found them. "Pooja, akka padichu dhan nalla aaganum." At the station, Pooja hugged so tight. "Akka, every day call pannanum." Akka cried too. "Call pannuven da. Vera level-a miss pannuven."
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Akka, nee pona enakku yaaroda vilayaaduven?'
Akka: 'Naan varuven da. Velli than varuven.'
Pooja: 'Dei, nalla padichu va. Aana rendu naalukku oru vaati va.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the station platform, holding a single ticket.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirivendradhu nijam dhan. Aana adhu endradhu illa. Un akka pona, aana adhu kaadhala vida poradhu illa. Manasu iruntha, ellam seri."
thirukkural: ஓம்பின் அமைந்தார் பிரிவோம்பல் மற்றவர் நீங்கின் அரிதால் புணர்வு.
english_translation: Stop his parting -my life to save Meeting is rare if he would leave
moral: When you truly love someone, letting them go is the hardest thing.

title: The Hard-Hearted Friend
theme: Agony of Parting / When Someone Leaves Without Care
hook: Vinoth's best friend moves away without even saying goodbye — and Vinoth feels discarded.
story: Ramesh was Vinoth's benchmate for 4 years. One Monday, Ramesh's seat was empty. "Transfer aachi poittaru." No goodbye. No call. Vinoth felt like a used notebook thrown away. "Evanuku enna? Naan avanuku onnum illa pole." He tore Ramesh's photo from the class pic. That night, Amma said, "Adhukku neeyum periya manasu kattuda. Adhu dhan nee vellum."
tamil_dialogues: Vinoth: 'Amma, avan enakku oru varthai kooda sonna maatan.'
Amma: 'Adhukku avanuku nindradhu, unakku illa. Nee nalla iru.'
Vinoth: 'Naan avan indha maadhiri irukanum nu ninachen. Aana avan illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits alone on the playground swing, swaying gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru mathiri manushan kitta nambikkai vacha, adhu kashtam. Aana, avan apdi irundha, adhu avanuku periya nindradhu. Nee mathiradhu dhan periyadhu."
thirukkural: பிரிவுரைக்கும் வன்கண்ணர் ஆயின் அரிதவர் நல்குவர் என்னும் நசை.
english_translation: His hardness says, "I leave you now" Is there hope of his renewed love?
moral: Some people leave without a word — don't let their hardness make you hard too.

title: The Talking Bangles
theme: Agony of Parting / Objects That Remind
hook: Anitha's Paati gives her bangles that "talk" — they remind her of Paati when she's away.
story: Paati lived in Karur, Anitha in Erode. Every vacation ended with a bus ride home. "Paati, unna vida pidikkala." Paati slipped her own bangles onto Anitha's wrists. "Idhu dhan naan. Nee odachukitu, enna nenaichuko." On the bus, Anitha heard the bangles clink. 'Paati dhan.' She smiled through tears. The bangles talked when she missed her.
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Paati, unga veetu manam romba nalla irukku — naan poga maaten.'
Paati: 'Paaru, intha valaiyal — idhu dhan naan. Nee pottuko.'
Anitha: 'Paati, idhu kathukudhu. Nee dhan pesura.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the bus stop, bangles shimmering on his wrist.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirivendradhu oru kashtam. Aana, paati kudutha valaiyal mathiri, oru ninaipu iruntha, athu dhan aarudhal. Adhu dhan kaadhal."
thirukkural: துறைவன் துறந்தமை தூற்றாகொல் முன்கை இறைஇறவா நின்ற வளை.
english_translation: Will not my gliding bangles' cry The parting of my lord betray?
moral: Love leaves behind reminders — and those reminders keep love alive.

title: The New City Blues
theme: Agony of Parting / Being Alone in a Strange Place
hook: Sanjay moves to Chennai and hates it — no friends, new language, empty streets.
story: From Madurai to Chennai. No more Muthu, no more street cricket. "Amma, idhu veedu illa. Namma ooruku povoam." Amma was silent. School was worse. Everyone spoke different Tamil. Lunch alone. One day, a boy named Vishwa shared his vada. "Neenga Madurai dhana? En periya appa Madurai." Sanjay smiled for the first time in 2 months.
tamil_dialogues: Sanjay: 'Amma, namma ooruku povoam. Ivanga pesradhe vida maadhiri irukku.'
Amma: 'Poru da. Puthu idam — putha manushan — porumai venum.'
Vishwa: 'Neenga Madurai dhana? Nalla irukka? En periyappa Madurai dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the apartment compound wall, watching kids play cricket.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Puthu idathula kashtam dhan. Aana porumai iruntha, putha nanbar kedaipanga. Ooradhu kastam, aana odidhadhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: இன்னாது இனன்இல்ஊர் வாழ்தல் அதனினும் இன்னாது இனியார்ப் பிரிவு.
english_translation: Bitter is life in friendless place; Worse is parting love's embrace!
moral: A new place feels empty — but one friend can make it home.

title: The Love Fever
theme: Agony of Parting / Love's Pain Burns
hook: Janani's grandmother falls sick and Janani feels a burning pain in her own chest — is it love or sickness?
story: Paati was in hospital. Janani sat in the temple courtyard with Amma. "Amma, en nenju eriyudhu." Amma felt her forehead. "Fever illa da." Janani cried. "Paati ku enna aachunu naan ninaikumbodhu, en nenju eriyudhu." Amma hugged her. "Athu dhan kaadhal da. Un paati mela irukka kaadhal. Pirivendru adhu eriyudhu." Janani understood: love burns like fire.
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Amma, en nenju dha eriyudhu. Paati ku enna aachunu.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan kaadhal da. Kaadhal eriyum neruppu.'
Janani: 'Aama, paati illadha veedu enakku aagadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the temple shadow, light and dark on his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaadhal eriyum. Aana adhu nalla eriyum. Pirivula eriyudhu — adhu dhan kaadhaloda veli. Nee erindhadhu dhan nijamaana kaadhal."
thirukkural: தொடிற்சுடின் அல்லது காமநோய் போல விடிற்சுடல் ஆற்றுமோ தீ.
english_translation: Can fire that burns by touch burn like Parting of the hearts love-sick?
moral: Love's pain burns hotter than any fire — because it comes from the heart.

title: The Strong Ones
hook: Rajesh's elder brother joins the Navy — his mother doesn't eat for 3 days, but Rajesh learns to be strong.
story: Brother Karthik joined the Indian Navy. 2 years at sea. Amma didn't touch food for 3 days. Rajesh brought her coffee. "Amma, saapdu. Akka vanthu apdi paatha unakku kashtam dhan." Appa watched Rajesh. "Nee dhan ippo veetla periya aalu da." Rajesh's shoulders felt heavy but he stood straight. He learned: someone has to be strong when others break.
tamil_dialogues: Rajesh: 'Amma, akka nalla irukkaanga. Adhan therinja pothum.'
Amma: 'Unakku puriyala da. Oru thalai rendu uyira petha.'
Appa: 'Nee dhan ippo naalu perula oruthan da. Valarndhutu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the harbour, watching a ship disappear on the horizon.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirivula kashtam varum. Aana atha thangurathu dhan periyadhu. Nee un amma ku pathukka maadhiri, dhegiyam endradhu — athu dhan kaadhal."
thirukkural: அரிதாற்றி அல்லல்நோய் நீக்கிப் பிரிவாற்றிப் பின்இருந்து வாழ்வார் பலர்.
english_translation: Many survive pangs of parting Not I this sore so distressing
moral: Many survive the pain of parting — and those who do become stronger.

title: Waiting for Sundaram
theme: Yearning for Reunion / The Growing Pain
hook: Meena's pet dog Sundaram runs away — she searches everywhere, her longing grows every day.
story: Sundaram didn't come home that night. Meena searched every street. "Thatha, avan enna aachu?" Three days. Nothing. Meena's heart felt like a balloon filling with water. Heavy. "Thatha, en manasula ennamo romba kashtama irukku." On day 5, a bark at the gate. Sundaram limped home, dirty, tired. Meena cried. "Dei, enna vittutu pogaadha da!"
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Thatha, avan enga poi iruppan?'
Thatha: 'Adhu dhan kashtam — theriyama irukradhu.'
Meena: 'Sundaram vanthudan! Dei, naan unna miss pannittu irundhen da!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the river, a dog paddle-swimming toward him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaadhal iruntha, edhuru paarppu valikkum. Aana, adhu dhan kaadhaloda veli. Nee edhuru paathu, avan vantha — adhu dhan santhosham."
thirukkural: மறைப்பேன்மன் யானிஃதோ நோயை இறைப்பவர்க்கு ஊற்றுநீர் போல மிகும்.
english_translation: It swells out like baled out spring How to bear this pain so writhing?
moral: Longing grows the more you try to hide it — like a spring that can't be stopped.

title: The Secret Tear
theme: Yearning for Reunion / Shame in Longing
hook: Kavya's father works in Singapore — she pretends she doesn't miss him but breaks down in class.
story: "Enakku onnum aagala. Appa velai ku poittaru. Naan nalla dhan irukken." Kavya told everyone she was fine. But in the middle of math class, the teacher said "Singapore" in a geography lesson. Kavya's eyes flooded. She ran to the washroom. The counsellor found her. "Unakku feel aagradhu sari dhan. Marachikradhu dhan periya problem." Kavya finally cried in someone's arms.
tamil_dialogues: Teacher: 'Kavya, enna achu? Nee aduguthula irundhe alugura?'
Kavya: 'Onnum illa miss. Koncham kashtama irukku.'
Counsellor: 'Adha solla aaganum. Marachikradhu dhan periyavana aakadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands outside the classroom window, a hand on the glass.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kavalaiya marachikka ninaikkradhu dhan periya thappu. Adha solli kudutha dhan manasu ilagu. Nee sonnadhu dhan sari."
thirukkural: கரத்தலும் ஆற்றேன்இந் நோயைநோய் செய்தார்க்கு உரைத்தலும் நாணுத் தரும்.
english_translation: I can't conceal this nor complain For shame to him who caused this pain
moral: Hiding your pain only makes it grow — it's okay to admit you miss someone.

title: The Shame-Love Balance
theme: Yearning for Reunion / Pride vs Love
hook: Dhanya's brother moved to the US — she wants to cry but feels ashamed because "big girls don't cry."
story: Anand anna left for the US. "Nee nalla iru, Dhanya." Dhanya smiled. "Sure anna." But inside, she was screaming. At school, Preethi saw her staring at nothing. "Dhanya, unakku enna?" Dhanya shook her head. "Onnum illa." Preethi sat beside her. "Nee anna ku miss pannitu irukka. Adhula enna thapu?" Dhanya broke. "Enakku aluda bayama irukku. Periya ponnu mathiri irukka vendum." Preethi hugged her. "Nee periya ponnu dhan. Periya ponnu aluvaanga."
tamil_dialogues: Dhanya: 'Na dhan periya ponnu. Naan ala koodadhu.'
Preethi: 'Yaaru sonnanga? Periya ponnu aludha apdi dhan periya ponnu.'
Dhanya: 'Anna ku miss pannitu naan oru kaadalaiyum kala maaten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the school roof, a kite string in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naan periyavan nu ninaichu, un feel panna koodadhu. Kaadhal iruntha, nana dhan varum. Aana adhu thappu illa. Nee aludhadhu dhan nijam."
thirukkural: காமமும் நாணும் உயிர்காவாத் தூங்கும்என் நோனா உடம்பின் அகத்து.
english_translation: In life -poles of this wearied frame Are poised the weights of lust and shame
moral: Love and shame pull against each other — but true love isn't ashamed to feel.

title: The Sea of Missing
theme: Yearning for Reunion / Drowning in Longing
hook: Selvam's fisherman Appa is lost at sea for 3 days — he feels like he's drowning on land.
story: Appa went fishing. A storm hit. 3 days. No news. Selvam sat on the beach every morning. "Amma, appa varuvaara?" Amma had no answer. The sea looked endless. "Ithu dhan en manasum. Alaippu, alaippu." On the 4th morning, a boat appeared on the horizon. Appa's boat. Selvam ran into the water. "Appa!" Appa was weak but alive. "Nee dhan ennai kaapadhichu da."
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Amma, appa varuvaara? Innum varala?'
Amma: 'Theriyala da. Namma kadala kaetaaga.'
Appa: 'Nee dhan en manasu la irundhe, da. Athu dhan ennai kaapadhichu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands knee-deep in the sea, looking at the horizon.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kadalu dhan kaadhal — romba aazham, romba perisu. Athula vizhundha, nee moonji vita. Aana, nambikkai iruntha, karai sergalam."
thirukkural: காமக் கடல்மன்னும் உண்டே அதுநீந்தும் ஏமப் புணைமன்னும் இல்.
english_translation: My lust is a sea; I do not see A raft to go across safely
moral: Longing is a sea without a raft — only hope keeps you afloat.

title: The Wound That Smiles
theme: Yearning for Reunion / When Friends Bring Pain
hook: Gowtham's close friend makes fun of him in front of others — the wound from a friend hurts most.
story: Gowtham and Sathish were best friends. But Sathish laughed at Gowtham's old chappal in front of everyone. "Dei, ithu enna? Un paati chappal-a?" Everyone roared. Gowtham's face burned. He went home. "Appa, Sathish enna rooba pannitan." Appa said, "Nanba dhan kuthina, adhu dhan valikkum. Sathrunaal kuthina, adhu namma theriyum. Aana nanba?" Gowtham understood: the closest ones can wound deepest.
tamil_dialogues: Gowtham: 'Appa, avane dhan en kitta irundhu kuthinaan.'
Appa: 'Nanba kuthina, adhu athigama valikkum da. Adhu dhan kastam.'
Gowtham: 'Naan avan kitta pesavenam. Ithu dhan friends-a irundha.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the Bhavani river, touching his own chest.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sathru kuthina, adhu theriyum. Aana, nanba kuthina, adhu ullae poidum. Adhu dhan kaadhal — edhirpaarthu vali."
thirukkural: துப்பின் எவனாவர் மன்கொல் துயர்வரவு நட்பினுள் ஆற்று பவர்.
english_translation: What wilt they prove when they are foes Who in friendship bring me woes!
moral: A wound from a friend cuts deeper than any enemy's blow.

title: Ice Cream and Tears
theme: Yearning for Reunion / Pleasure vs Pain
hook: Abi's mother is in the hospital — he eats her favorite ice cream but feels empty.
story: Amma was in the hospital. Athai bought Abi his favorite — black current ice cream. "Saapdu da." Abi ate one spoon. Tasteless. "Athai, intha ice cream amma ku miss pannudhu." He pushed it away. "Amma varadhu varaikum enakku ennum inikkadhu." Athai's eyes welled. "Un amma ketta, athu dhan solvaanga — un kaadhal dhan periya ice cream nu."
tamil_dialogues: Abi: 'Athai, ice cream nalla irukku, aana enakku inikkala.'
Athai: 'Amma illadha podhu, ice cream kuda inikkadhu da.'
Abi: 'Amma varum podhu, naan adha kooda saapduvem.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the terrace, holding a melting ice cream cone.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Inbam irundha, athu perisu. Aana pirivula, athu dhan athigama valikkum. Kaadhal iruntha, inbam vida dhan perisudhu."
thirukkural: இன்பம் கடல்மற்றுக் காமம் அஃதடுங்கால் துன்பம் அதனிற் பெரிது.
english_translation: The pleasure in love is oceanful But its pangs are more painful
moral: When love is mixed with longing, even sweet things taste bitter.

title: Midnight Swimmer
theme: Yearning for Reunion / Alone in the Dark
hook: Revathi can't sleep at night after her mother goes to work on the night shift — the darkness feels endless.
story: Amma worked nights at the fish packing unit. Revathi lay awake. The fan hummed. The clock ticked. "Amma varadhu innum 4 manidhi." She counted sheep, counted waves, counted breaths. Nothing. She went to Paati's room. "Paati, thookam varala. Amma nenacha dhan irukku." Paati patted the bed. "Vaa. Naan oru kathai solluren." Revathi fell asleep in 5 minutes.
tamil_dialogues: Revathi: 'Paati, enakku thookam varadhu. Amma ku miss pannudhu.'
Paati: 'Iru da. Nee yen thooka varanu un amma ku theriyum.'
Revathi: 'Naan epdi thoonguven? Amma veetla illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the beach in moonlight, counting waves.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Iravu nerathula, kaadhal athigama varum. Nee thoongamal irukradhu athu dhan kaadhal. Aana nee thoonganum — adhaan amma solvaanga."
thirukkural: காமக் கடும்புனல் நீந்திக் கரைகாணேன் யாமத்தும் யானே உளேன்.
english_translation: Wild waves of love I swim shoreless Pining alone in midnight hush
moral: The night amplifies every feeling of longing — but love also brings comfort.

title: The Kind Night
theme: Yearning for Reunion / Night's Cruelty and Mercy
hook: Deepak's elder sister got married and left — the night feels like an enemy that took her away.
story: The house felt empty without Akka. Dinner was quiet. TV was quiet. Night was the worst. Deepak sat on the veranda. The moon was full. "Enga akka ippo indha moon dhan paathuta?" Thatha came. "Thoonga vara maata?" Deepak shook his head. Thatha sat beside him. "Paaru, moon dhan irukku. Avanum idha dhan paathuta. Renduperum ore moon dhan." Deepak felt less alone.
tamil_dialogues: Deepak: 'Thatha, iravu romba sathamana maadhiri irukku.'
Thatha: 'Adhu dhan da. Aana moon dhan nammoda.'
Deepak: 'Akka kuda intha moon dhan paathuta nu nenaikala?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the moon, one hand raised toward the sky.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Iravu dhan kaadhaloda sernthirukkum. Aana, moon mathiri, oru connesion iruntha, athu dhan aarudhal. Neeye thedikitta."
thirukkural: மன்னுயிர் எல்லாம் துயிற்றி அளித்திரா என்னல்லது இல்லை துணை.
english_translation: Night's mercy lulls all souls to sleep Keeping but me for companionship
moral: The night that silences the world can feel lonely — but look up, and you're never truly alone.

title: Waiting for the Sun
hook: Esther's father is a train driver — when he's on night duty, the hours crawl like years.
story: Appa drove the night express to Kanyakumari. Esther watched the clock. 9pm. 10pm. 11pm. "Amma, oru manidhi dhan achu? Adhu 4 manidhi maadhiri irukku!" Amma smiled. "Kaadhal iruntha neram dhan kammi." Esther sat at the window. Every train whistle — "Appa dhana?" Midnight. 1am. Finally, 4am. Appa's footsteps. Esther ran. "Appa, rendu manidhi rendu varushama irundhadhu!"
tamil_dialogues: Esther: 'Amma, neram dhan nikkradhu pole irukku.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan kaadhal da. Kettaal neram valikkum.'
Appa: 'Vandhuten da. Nee thoongaama irundha?'
Esther: 'Unna patha dhan thookam varum!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the railway crossing, lantern in hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaadhal iruntha, neram dhan kettadhu. Aana, adhaan kaadhaloda mayam. Nee kaathirundhadhu dhan kaadhal."
thirukkural: கொடியார் கொடுமையின் தாம்கொடிய இந்நாள் நெடிய கழியும் இரா.
english_translation: Crueller than that cruel he Are midnight hours gliding slowly
moral: When you're waiting for someone you love, every minute feels like a year.

title: The Flood Gates
theme: Yearning for Reunion / Tears That Won't Stop
hook: Latha's mother dies — she can't stop crying and thinks something is wrong with her.
story: After Amma passed, Latha cried every day. "Chithappa, en kannula thanni nikkadhu. Naan enna pannuven?" Chithappa took her to the Kaveri. "Paaru, intha kaveri kuda azhudhutu dhan irukku. Aana adhu nikkadhu. Nee maadhiri dhan — uyir iruntha thanni varum." Latha touched the water. "Amma kuda kaveri maadhiri dhana?" Chithappa nodded. "Adhu dhan — amma dhan un kaveri."
tamil_dialogues: Latha: 'Chithappa, en kannula enna achu? Nikkadhu.'
Chithappa: 'Adhu dhan kaadhal da. Un amma mela kaadhal.'
Latha: 'Ammava nenacha dhan kannula thanni varudhu. Adha nirutha mudiyala.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the riverbank, feet in the flowing water.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannula thanni varadhu dhan unmai. Adha nirutha ninaikkadha. Adhu dhan kaadhaloda veli. Nee azha, aana azha — adhu dhan sari."
thirukkural: உள்ளம்போன்று உள்வழிச் செல்கிற்பின் வெள்ளநீர் நீந்தல மன்னோஎன் கண்.
english_translation: Like heart, if my sight reaches him It won't in floods of tears swim!
moral: Tears are not weakness — they are the river of love finding its way out.

title: The Eyes That Betray
theme: Eyes Consumed by Grief / Tears Tell the Truth
hook: Malarvizhi's eyes water every time she remembers her late grandmother — she thinks her eyes are broken.
story: Paati died 6 months ago. But Malarvizhi's eyes still watered randomly. In class, during lunch, in the middle of a song. "Miss, en kannula enna problem? Naan ala ninaikkala — aana thanni varudhu." The teacher sat beside her. "Un kannu dhan unmai solludhu. Un manasukku Paati venum nu solludhu. Adhu thappu illa." Malarvizhi understood: her eyes weren't broken — they were honest.
tamil_dialogues: Malarvizhi: 'Miss, en kannula thanni thaane varudhu. Naan kala maaten.'
Teacher: 'Adhu un kannu solludhu — un Paati unaku venum nu.'
Malarvizhi: 'Ama, paati illadha veetu la en kannu dhan aludhutu irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the teacher, a handkerchief in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu dhan unmai solradhu. Vaai poi sollalam, aana kannu poi solladhu. Un kannu aludhadhu dhan kaadhal."
thirukkural: கண்தாம் கலுழ்வ தெவன்கொலோ தண்டாநோய் தாம்காட்ட யாம்கண் டது.
english_translation: The eye pointed him to me; why then They weep with malady and pine?
moral: The eyes that first showed love now weep — they cannot lie about what the heart feels.

title: The Careless Gaze
theme: Eyes Consumed by Grief / Regret of Looking
hook: Harini keeps looking at her late father's photo and cries — she regrets ever looking.
story: Appa's photo sat on the shelf. Every day, Harini looked at it. And every day, she cried. "Naan paathaa dhan aluven. Paakka maaten nu irundhen. Aana mudiyala." Athai held her. "Kannu dhan un appa vida mudiyaadhadhu. Adhu paakka dhan pannum. Athu dhan kaadhal." Harini kept the photo. She cried. But she also smiled. "Appa, unna paakradhu dhan enakku pidicha vishayam."
tamil_dialogues: Harini: 'Athai, appa photo paatha dhan aluven. Paakka maaten.'
Athai: 'Kannu dhan unmai sonnadhu. Atha nirutha mudiyaadhu.'
Harini: 'Ippa naan appa photo paathu aluven, aana sandhosha dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the palace garden, looking at his own reflection in a pond.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu dhan kaadhala kattum. Appadiye, adhu dhan pirivula kashtama irukkum. Nee paathadhu dhan sari, azhudhadhu dhan sari."
thirukkural: தெரிந்துணரா நோக்கிய உண்கண் பரிந்துணராப் பைதல் உழப்பது எவன்?
english_translation: Why should these dyed eyes grieve now sans Regrets for their thoughtless glance?
moral: The eyes that once looked with joy now look with grief — both are forms of love.

title: The Funny Tragedy
theme: Eyes Consumed by Grief / Irony of Tears
hook: Kani's aunt gave her a beautiful dress on her birthday — Kani laughed and cried at the same time.
story: Athai bought Kani a yellow dress. "Unakku pidikum nu vanginen." Kani looked at it. Yellow. Amma's favorite color. She smiled. Then her eyes filled. Then she laughed through tears. "Appa, enakku sandhosha illa? Aana aludhutu irukken." Appa smiled. "Athu dhan kaadhal da. Nee sandhosham iruntha kooda, amma ninaivu varum. Adhu dhan manasu." Kani wore the dress. Yellow. Like Amma's smile.
tamil_dialogues: Kani: 'Appa, dress nalla dhan irukku, aana en kannula thanni!'
Appa: 'Un sandhosam un amma kooda kalandhu irukku. Athu dhan.'
Kani: 'Enakku sandhosama alutha maadhiri irukku. Funny dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the sweet stall, a yellow flower in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sandhosam iruntha kooda, pirivula kannu aludhum. Adhu dhan kaadhal — ore nerathula rendum varum."
thirukkural: கதுமெனத் தாநோக்கித் தாமே கலுழும் இதுநகத் தக்க துடைத்து.
english_translation: Eyes darted eager glance that day It's funny that they weep today
moral: The same eyes that laugh can cry — love is joy and grief intertwined.

title: The Dry Well
theme: Eyes Consumed by Grief / Tears Running Dry
hook: Usha's brother died in an accident — she cried until no more tears came, and that scared her more.
story: When anna died, Usha cried for 3 days straight. On day 4, she sat on the bed. Dry-eyed. "Chithi, en kannula thanni varala. Naan anna va ninaikala nu arthama?" Chithi sat beside her. "Illada. Un kannu dhan aasi pochu. Adhu kuda kaadhal dhan." Usha touched her dry eyes. "Anna, unaku naan ipdi dhan irukken. Aana unna miss pannitu dhan." Months later, one tear came. She smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Usha: 'Chithi, kannula thanni ninnu pochu. Enakku bayama irukku.'
Chithi: 'Adhu dhan kashtam da. Aana adhu artham kaadhala illa nu illa.'
Usha: 'Anna, kannula thanni illa. Aana manasula nee dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside a dry village well, a single drop of water on the rim.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannula thanni nikkalaam. Aana adhu kaadhal illa nu artham illa. Manasula irukka kaadhal dhan periyadhu."
thirukkural: பெயலாற்றா நீருலந்த உண்கண் உயலாற்றா உய்வில்நோய் என்கண் நிறுத்து.
english_translation: These eyes left me to endless grief Crying adry without relief
moral: Even when tears run dry, love remains — grief has no expiration date.

title: The Sleepless Eyes
theme: Eyes Consumed by Grief / Love's Insomnia
hook: Bhavani can't close her eyes at night — every time she does, she sees her mother's face.
story: After Amma died, sleep became a stranger. "Thatha, kannu moodina, amma munnadi varanga." Thatha was worried. "Ponnu, nee thoonganum." Bhavani tried. Closed her eyes. There was Amma, smiling. "Pathiya Thatha! Amma!" Thatha understood. "Un kannu dhan un amma va miss pannudhu. Athu dhan thoongamal irukku." Bhavani learned to sleep with the photo beside her.
tamil_dialogues: Bhavani: 'Thatha, kannu moodina, amma varanga. Appo thoongamaten.'
Thatha: 'Adhu dhan kaadhal da. Kannu dhan avangala kattudhu.'
Bhavani: 'Naan thoongala, aana amma va miss pannitu dhan iruppen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the village house steps, eyes wide open, looking at the stars.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaadhal iruntha, kannu thoongadhu. Adhu dhan kaadhaloda veli. Nee thoongala, aana un kannula irukka amma dhan unmai."
thirukkural: படலாற்றா பைதல் உழக்கும் கடலாற்றாக் காமநோய் செய்தஎன் கண்.
english_translation: My eyes causing lust more than sea Suffer that torture sleeplessly
moral: Love keeps the eyes open when the world sleeps — remembering is more important than rest.

title: The Eye That Cried for Me
theme: Eyes Consumed by Grief / Eyes as Victims
hook: Muthulakshmi's eyes have been red and swollen for a week — the nurse says her eyes are crying for her.
story: Muthu's pet cat Mili ran away. She cried for days. Her eyes were red, swollen, burning. "Miss, en kannu valikkudhu. Enna pannuven?" The nurse checked. "Un kannula problem illa. Adhu dhan un kosama aludhutu irukku. Adhu dhan vali." Muthu touched her eyelids gently. "Sorry, kannu. Unaku kooda vali dhan." She gave her eyes a cold compress. "Nee epdi irukka? Nalla irukkiya?"
tamil_dialogues: Muthulakshmi: 'Miss, kannu vali, aana naan alala.'
Nurse: 'Adhu dhan — un kannu aludhutu irukku. Unakaga.'
Muthulakshmi: 'Kannu, sorry da. Unakum kooda vali dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the clinic, a cold cloth over his own eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu dhan kaadhala sahum. Athu dhan valiyum eduthukum. Nee kannu kitta sorry sonna, adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: ஓஒ இனிதே எமக்கிந்நோய் செய்தகண் தாஅம் இதற்பட் டது.
english_translation: Lo! eyes that wrought this love-sickness Are victims of the same themselves
moral: The eyes that bring the pain of love also suffer it — be kind to your own eyes.

title: One Last Look
theme: Eyes Consumed by Grief / Looking with Longing
hook: Ezhil's father left for Dubai 2 years ago — she keeps looking at the sea, hoping to see him return.
story: Every evening, Ezhil sat at the Sunset Point. Looking at the sea. "En Appa inga dhan poitaru. Kadal la." Viji sat beside her. "Ippo varuvaara?" Ezhil shook her head. "Theriyala. Aana naan paathutu dhan iruppen." Her eyes never left the horizon. "En kannu dhan avan kitta connect aakum." Viji didn't understand. But she sat with her. Sometimes, love is just sitting beside someone who's looking.
tamil_dialogues: Viji: 'Nee enna paathutu irukka? Appa varuvaara?'
Ezhil: 'Theriyala. Aana en kannu dhan avan kitta serthu vakkum.'
Ezhil: 'Kadal dhan unaku amma, enakku appa. Rendukkum oru seri.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the Sunset Point, facing the sea, unblinking.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu dhan edhirpaarkkum. Aana adhu dhan kaadhal — edhirpaarkkadhu dhan kaadhal. Nee paathutu irukka, adhu dhan porumai."
thirukkural: உழந்துழந் துள்நீர் அறுக விழைந்திழைந்து வேண்டி அவர்கண்ட கண்.
english_translation: Let tears dry up pining pining In eyes that eyed him longing longing
moral: Eyes that keep looking even when there's nothing to see — that is the truest love.

title: The Smile That Lied
theme: Eyes Consumed by Grief / Deception in Love
hook: Sumathi's friend says "I'll always be your friend" but leaves without a trace — and her eyes knew all along.
story: Jamuna was Sumathi's shadow. "Namma rendum forever dhan." But then Jamuna got a new group. New friends. Sumathi was forgotten. "Un kannu dhan sonnadhu, avan poi solluva nu. Naan keta maaten." Sumathi's eyes had known first. They grew heavy. "En kannuku dhan theriyum. Aana naan solla maaten." She learned: eyes can spot lies that the heart refuses to see.
tamil_dialogues: Sumathi: 'En kannuku dhan theriyum — avan poi sonna dhan.'
Jamuna: 'Naan forever dhan sollala?'
Sumathi: 'Un kannu dhan sonnadhu. Naan paatha dhanda.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the textile market, holding a mirror.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vaai sollum, aana kannu solladhu. Nee sonna maadhiri, kannu dhan truth sollum. Adhu dhan visudha katchi."
thirukkural: பேணாது பெட்டார் உளர்மன்னோ மற்றவர்க் காணாது அமைவில கண்.
english_translation: Ther's he whose lips loved, not his heart Yet my eyes pine seeing him not
moral: The eyes know what the heart refuses to accept — trust what you see, not what you hear.

title: The Come-and-Go Sleep
theme: Eyes Consumed by Grief / Restless Vigil
hook: Kathiravan's Appa is a truck driver — when he's away, sleep plays hide and seek.
story: Appa drove trucks across Tamil Nadu. "Nalla iru da." He'd leave. Kathiravan couldn't sleep. If he slept, Appa might come and he'd miss him. If he didn't sleep, morning felt years away. "Amma, Appa varum podhu naan thoongitu irundha?" Amma said, "Appa vandha, nee thoongina kooda, avan unna paathutu dhan pova." Kathiravan finally slept. And when Appa came, he watched his son sleep, smiling.
tamil_dialogues: Kathiravan: 'Amma, naan thoongina, appa vandhu poidhuvaara?'
Amma: 'Un appa vandha, nee thoongina kooda paathutu dhan pova.'
Appa: 'Thoonguravan dhan lucky da. Naan paathutu irukken.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a parked truck, watching a sleeping child through the window.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaadhal iruntha, thoongal varadhu — appa varuvaara, maatara nu bayam. Aana, kaadhal iruntha, vandhu paarppanga. Nee thoongu."
thirukkural: வாராக்கால் துஞ்சா வரின்துஞ்சா ஆயிடை ஆரஞர் உற்றன கண்.
english_translation: He comes; no sleep; he goes; no sleep This is the fate of eyes that weep
moral: Love steals sleep — whether the loved one is here or away, the eyes stay watchful.

title: The Drumming Eyes
theme: Eyes Consumed by Grief / Public Grief
hook: Vanitha can't hide her sadness — her red eyes tell the whole school her father lost his job.
story: Appa lost his job. Vanitha cried all night. At school, her eyes were red. "Dei, Vanitha kannu sivi-aa irukku." "Avanga appa velaiya poittaru." Everyone knew. Vanitha wanted to hide. "En kannu dhan en katha sollidudhu. Enakku bayama irukku." Her class teacher said, "Un kannu dhan unnoda vali. Atha kaatradhula enna thappu? Avanga therinjutu poga." Vanitha stopped hiding. "Yaaruku venum na theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Classmate: 'Vanitha, enna achu? Kannu sivi-aa irukku.'
Vanitha: 'Onnum illa. Sari illa.'
Teacher: 'Kannu dhan un feeling sollum. Atha marachikka vendam.'
Vanitha: 'En kannu dhan en kosam pesudhu. Avanga ketuta poga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the school gate, his own eyes red, unashamed.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu dhan kaadhala maraikka mudiyaadhu. Adhu dhan unmai. Nee marachikka ninaicha, athu dhan thappu. Un kannu sollum unmai."
thirukkural: மறைபெறல் ஊரார்க்கு அரிதன்றால் எம்போல் அறைபறை கண்ணார் அகத்து.
english_translation: Like drum beats eyes declare my heart; From people who could hide his secret?
moral: Your eyes beat like drums — they announce your heart's secrets to the world, and that's okay.

title: Paati's Grey Hair
theme: Pallor of Love / Separation Changes Us
hook: Nila notices Paati's hair turned white in just one month since Thatha went to Chennai.
story: Thatha went to Chennai for work. Every day Paati sat at the window, staring at the gate. Nila combed Paati's hair one evening and gasped. "Paati, un mudi full grey aaiduchu!" Paati smiled weakly. "Intha grey dhan un thatha love, ma." Nila hugged her tight, understanding that love leaves its mark on the body.
tamil_dialogues: Nila: 'Paati, un mudi grey aaiduchu! Thatha pona aprm dhan grey aachu.'
Paati: 'Ama ma. Kaadhal grey aakidum. Athu dhan adhan aatharam.'
Nila: 'Paati... unakku thatha miss aavadha?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the window where Paati sits, a single grey hair in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Paati grey aavadhu dhan kaadhalin aatharam. Pirindha pothu udalum manasum maari poidum. Adhu dhan unmai kaadhal."
thirukkural: நயந்தவர்க்கு நல்காமை நேர்ந்தேன் பசந்தவென் பண்பியார்க்கு உரைக்கோ பிற.
english_translation: My lover's parting, I allowed Whom to complain my hue pallid?
moral: Love's separation leaves its mark on the body — and that's its truest proof.

title: The Chalk That Turned Yellow
theme: Pallor of Love / Blame and Change
hook: Meena's hands turn yellow from turmeric — and she blames her sister for leaving for college.
story: Akka got admission in Coimbatore. One week after she left, Meena's palms turned pale yellow. Amma took her to the doctor. "Pasalai dhan saapdra, athaala dhan?" But Meena knew. "Akka, unnaala dhan en kai yellow achu. Nee pona aprm dhan!" Akka laughed, but her eyes welled up.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Akka, nee pona aprm dhan en kai yellow aachu! Unnaala dhan!'
Akka: 'Eda, adhu manjal dhan da!'
Meena: 'Illada, adhu kaadhal dhan!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stop where Akka left, holding a yellow chalk piece.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirindhavar sonnaal dhan — athu kaadhal, manjal illa. Namma udale namma manasoda pesum. Adhu dhan pachchai."
thirukkural: அவர்தந்தார் என்னும் தகையால் இவர்தந்தென் மேனிமேல் ஊரும் பசப்பு.
english_translation: Claiming it is begot through him Pallor creeps and rides over my frame.
moral: The body shows what the heart hides — separation writes itself on the skin.

title: The Doll Without Colour
theme: Pallor of Love / Beauty Fades in Absence
hook: Anitha's favourite doll slowly loses its colour after she forgets to play with it — just like her face since Appa left.
story: Appa went to Singapore for work. Anitha stopped playing. Her favourite doll — a Thanjavur bommai — sat untouched. One day she picked it up. The paint had faded. "Amma, bommai colour poiduchu!" Amma looked at Anitha's pale face. "Un mugam bommai maadhiri dhan aaiduchu, ma." Anitha cried. "Appa vara varaikkum kaathu iruppen."
tamil_dialogues: Anitha: 'Amma, en bommai colour poiduchu! Yaaru poiduchu?'
Amma: 'Neeye dhan ma. Appa pona aprm, neeyum colour aaiduva.'
Anitha: 'Appa vandha pramodhum colour varuma?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the faded doll, repainting its cheek with a tiny brush.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirindhavarukkaaga udalum manasum vaadi poidum. Aana avanga vandha pramodhum — ellam sari aagum. Kaadhal dhan marunthu."
thirukkural: சாயலும் நாணும் அவர்கொண்டார் கைம்மாறா நோயும் பசலையும் தந்து.
english_translation: He seized my beauty and modesty Leaving pangs and Pallor to me.
moral: When love goes away, it takes colour and laughter with it.

title: The Photo That Talks
theme: Pallor of Love / Speaking of the Departed
hook: Kavya's Appa left for the Gulf country — she talks to his photo every night and her face grows pale.
story: Appa's photo hung on the wall. Every night Kavya said, "Appa, naan nalla irukken. Neenga epdi irukkeenga?" Amma noticed her cheeks sinking. "Kavya, unakku colour illa da." Kavya smiled. "Appa kitta colour irukku. En kitta illa." Amma hugged her. "Appa varaikkum, nee dhan avaru colour."
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Appa, neenga pona aprm dhan en colour pochu.'
Amma: 'Kavya, nee photo kitta dhan pesura?'
Kavya: 'Ama. Avanukku dhan en colour kudukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands behind Kavya, one hand on the photo frame, one on her head.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirindhava kitta pesradhu dhan — athu kaadhal. Udal colour pochu naalum, manasu colour aagadhu. Adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: உள்ளுவன் மன்யான் உரைப்பது அவர்திறமால் கள்ளம் பிறவோ பசப்பு.
english_translation: He is my thought, his praise my theme Yet this pallor steals over my frame.
moral: When your only company is memory, the body fades but love never does.

title: Where Colour Went
theme: Pallor of Love / Exchange of State
hook: Sruthi's Amma is in Chennai hospital — Sruthi's face grows pale as she waits for news.
story: Amma was in Chennai for treatment. Sruthi sat by the window every day. The Courtallam falls roared in the distance. "Appa, Amma colour ellam edukka poita? Enna vittutu?" Appa held her. "Illada, avanga un colour edukka illa. Avanga un colour edukka poitanga." Sruthi's face was white as paper. The falls kept falling.
tamil_dialogues: Sruthi: 'Appa, Amma pona idathula en colour irukka?'
Appa: 'Illada kutty. Un colour dhan avanga kitta irukku.'
Sruthi: 'Appo avanga colour enga? Enakku kedaikuma?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands under the Courtallam falls, water streaming over his closed eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaadhal endradhu — rendu uyir ore aagradhu. Oruva colour pochu na, adhu mathavan kitta irukku. Iruvarum ore colour dhan."
thirukkural: உவக்காண்எம் காதலர் செல்வார் இவக்காண்என் மேனி பசப்பூர் வது.
english_translation: My lover departed me there And pallor usurped my body here.
moral: Love shares one colour between two — when one leaves, the other fades.

title: The Light That Waits
theme: Pallor of Love / Pallor Waits for Love's Return
hook: Kumar's Chithappa shows him how darkness waits for a lamp to go out — just like pallor waits for love to leave.
story: Every evening, darkness filled Kumar's room the moment the lamp went out. "Chithappa, iruttu epdi dhan varudhu?" Chithappa smiled. "Adhu wait pannittu irukku. Lamp off aana dhan adhu varum." Kumar thought of Periyappa who went abroad. "Appo en colour, Periyappa pona aprm dhan vanthuchu?" Chithappa nodded. "Avan varum varaikkum, iruttu irukkum."
tamil_dialogues: Kumar: 'Chithappa, iruttu lamplight enna wait pannudhu?'
Chithappa: 'Ama da. Adhu dhan pachchai — kaadhal pona aprm dhan adhu varum.'
Kumar: 'Appo Periyappa varum varaikkum iruppa?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the dark room, holding no lamp, yet his face glows softly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Iruttu light-a wait pannudhu, aana adhu varadhu. Pachchaiyum adhe maadhiri — kaadhal thirumba varum varaikkum, pachchai irundhudum."
thirukkural: விளக்கற்றம் பார்க்கும் இருளேபோல் கொண்கன் முயக்கற்றம் பார்க்கும் பசப்பு.
english_translation: Just as darkness waits for light-off Pallor looks for lover's arms-off.
moral: Pallor waits for love to weaken, just as darkness waits for light to die.

title: The Hug That Left
hook: Pooja hugs Amma at the railway station — the moment she lets go, she feels the pallor creep in.
story: Amma was going to her native for a month. At the station, Pooja hugged her tight. "Amma, poitu vaa." When she let go, something changed. Her arms felt cold. Her face felt heavy. By evening, Pooja looked at the mirror. Her skin was pale. "Paati, naan enga Amma kitta colour edukka poiten." Paati wiped her eyes.
tamil_dialogues: Pooja: 'Amma, en colour enga pochu?'
Amma: 'En kitta irukku ma. Nee kudutha dhan.'
Pooja: 'Appo naan enna pannuven?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the railway platform, a train disappearing in the distance.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru athisayam — kaadhal pirindha udane, udal colour marudhu. Athu dhan kaadhalin veli. Adhu romba vegama nadakkum."
thirukkural: புல்லிக் கிடந்தேன் புடைபெயர்ந்தேன் அவ்வளவில் அள்ளிக்கொள் வற்றே பசப்பு.
english_translation: From his embrace I turned a nonce This pallor swallowed me at once.
moral: The moment love's embrace ends, pallor rushes in to fill the space.

title: The Blame Game
hook: Divya's classmates tease her for looking pale — nobody blames her mother who left.
story: "Divya, un mugam yellow! Enna achu?" "Saapdade illaya?" "Dosa saapdu da!" Divya's classmates laughed. But nobody asked why. Amma had gone to her sister's house after a big fight with Appa. One month. No calls. Divya's face showed what her mouth couldn't say. Teacher called her aside. "Un amma pona aprm dhan, illa?" Divya broke down.
tamil_dialogues: Classmate: 'Divya, un mugam yellow aaiduchu! Saapdade illaya?'
Divya: 'Saapdraen dhan.'
Teacher: 'Avanga amma pona aprm dhan. Nee sollaama irundha pramodhum avanga dhan theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the teacher's desk, a yellow chalk in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un mugam colour aavadhu dhan — un amma pona dhan kaaranam. Aana yaarum adha solla maata. Avanga pona varutham dhan undu seyyudhu."
thirukkural: பசந்தாள் இவள்என்பது அல்லால் இவளைத் துறந்தார் அவர்என்பார் இல்.
english_translation: On my pallor they cast a slur But none says "lo he parted her".
moral: Everyone sees the pallor, but only love knows the reason.

title: If It Makes You Well
hook: Vanitha's Appa is sick in hospital — she tells God, "Take my colour, give him health."
story: Appa lay in the hospital bed, pale as the white sheets. Vanitha sat beside him. "Appa, un colour poiduchu." Appa smiled weakly. "Kavalai padaadha." That night, Vanitha prayed. "Kadavule, en colour ellam edukkitu appa colour thirupi kudu." Next morning, Vanitha's face was yellow. But Appa sat up for the first time. Amma cried.
tamil_dialogues: Vanitha: 'Appa, unakku colour illa. Naan en colour kudutha?'
Appa: 'Enna da solra? Adhu dhan unmai kaadhal.'
Vanitha: 'En colour ponadhu paravala. Appa nalla irundha pothum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between the hospital beds, transferring colour between two hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Adhu dhan dhanam — oruvar colour pOi innoruvarukku uyir varudhu. Athu dhan kaadhalin perumai."
thirukkural: பசக்கமன் பட்டாங்கென் மேனி நயப்பித்தார் நன்னிலையர் ஆவர் எனின்.
english_translation: Let all my body become pale If he who took my leave fares well.
moral: True love would rather fade itself than see the beloved suffer.

title: Don't Call Me Pallid
theme: Pallor of Love / Protecting the Lover's Name
hook: Bhavani's relatives gossip about her pale face — she refuses to let them blame Appa.
story: Bhavani's Appa was a fisherman lost at sea. Her face had turned grey with grief. Relatives whispered. "Paaru, avanuku colour illa." "Avanuku enna achu?" Bhavani heard them. She stood up. "En colour ponadhu dhan paravala. Aana en appa peru solla koodadhu." The relatives fell silent. Mala hugged her. "Un appa pera kaapathura da."
tamil_dialogues: Relative: 'Bhavani mugam yellow aa irukku. Appa pona varutham dhan pola.'
Bhavani: 'En mugam enna irundha dhan. Appa peru matum solladhadha.'
Mala: 'Nee sari dhan da. Appa pera kaapathura.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the harbour, looking at the empty sea, his face serene.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bhavani sonna maadhiri — udal colour vida, avan peru perisudhu. Kaadhal adhu dhan. Avan peru thappu sollaama irukradhu dhan."
thirukkural: பசப்பெனப் பேர்பெறுதல் நன்றே நயப்பித்தார் நல்காமை தூற்றார் எனின்.
english_translation: Let people call me all pallid But my lover let them not deride.
moral: Let them call me pale — but never let them speak ill of the one I love.

title: The Stoneless Fruit
hook: Subha sees a fruit with no seed and asks Thatha why some love has no return.
story: At the Palani market, Subha saw a bundle of seedless grapes. "Thatha, ithukku kozhi illa!" Thatha smiled. "Adhu dhan kama medhu. Aana, rendu perum ore mathiri ninaichadha vida inbodha dhan." Subha thought. "Appo, amma appa rendu perum ore mathiri nenaikravanga — adhu dhan periya kozhi illadha pazham?" Thatha nodded.
tamil_dialogues: Subha: 'Thatha, kozhi illadha pazham dhan nalla irukku.'
Thatha: 'Ama. Adhu dhan kaadhal — rendu perum ore mathiri nenaikradhu.'
Subha: 'Appo ore oruthar mathiri nenaikradhu... adhu kozhi illadha pazham illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple steps, a bunch of seedless grapes in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Rendu perum ore aasai. Adhu dhan nijamaana kaadhal. Oru oruthar dhan aasaipadradhu, adhu kova kozhi maadhiri — kathikkum."
thirukkural: தாம்வீழ்வார் தம்வீழப் பெற்றவர் பெற்றாரே காமத்துக் காழில் கனி.
english_translation: Stoneless fruit of love they have Who are beloved by those they love.
moral: Mutual love is the sweetest fruit — without a seed of doubt.

title: The Rain That Loved
hook: Raji watches rain pour on the tea plants and realizes love must flow both ways.
story: The tea garden was dry for months. Then the rain came. The leaves turned green overnight. "Amma, mazhai varadhu — therillai!" Amma said, "Adhu dhan — kaadhala kaekradhu mazhaila oru pakkam dhan peiyadhu. Iru pakkamum peiyanum." Raji's eyes widened. "Appo — namma veetla Appa dhan mazhai, Amma dhan mazhai?" Amma laughed.
tamil_dialogues: Raji: 'Amma, mazhai ore pakkam peiyala, rendu pakkamum peiyudhu.'
Amma: 'Ama. Kaadhalum adhe maadhiri — rendu perum mazhai maadhiri kudutha dhan valarum.'
Raji: 'Appo naan... Amma kitta mazhai maadhiri irukkena?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the tea garden, rain falling on his open palms.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mazhai ore pakkam peiyadhu. Kaadhalum adhe maadhiri — kudukradhu, vaangradhu rendum irukka vendum."
thirukkural: வாழ்வார்க்கு வானம் பயந்தற்றால் வீழ்வார்க்கு வீழ்வார் அளிக்கும் அளி.
english_translation: The lover-and-beloved's self-givings Are like rains to living beings.
moral: Love must flow both ways — like rain that gives and earth that receives.

title: The Girl Who Walked Tall
theme: Solitary Anguish / Pride of Being Loved
hook: Deepa's classmate is always happy and confident — Deepa learns it's because her parents love her fiercely.
story: Pooja never complained. She smiled even on exam days. Deepa asked, "Pooja, unakku enna vishesham?" Pooja laughed. "Enakku Amma Appa dhan matter. Avanga enna miss pannara mathiri feel panna koodadhu." Deepa realized — she had everything, but at home, nobody hugged her. That night Deepa hugged Amma. "Enakku oru vishesham venum."
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Pooja, unakku enna vishesham? Nee always happy!'
Pooja: 'Enakku Amma Appa dhan ellam. Adhu dhan en perumai.'
Deepa: 'Naanum Amma kitta kaekratha? Ennai kuda love pannuva?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the school verandah, watching children run.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaarukku kaadhal kidachudho, avanga dhan vaazhga. Adhu dhan perumai. Kaadhal irukradhu dhan periya perk."
thirukkural: வீழுநர் வீழப் படுவார்க்கு அமையுமே வாழுநம் என்னும் செருக்கு.
english_translation: The pride of living is for those Whose love is returned by love so close.
moral: Being truly loved is the greatest pride a person can have.

title: The Lonely Kite
hook: Selvam flies a kite alone — it soars but no one on the ground looks up at it.
story: Selvam loved flying kites. But the other kids didn't want to play with him. "Oru kutti singal dhan, avanuku enna theriyum?" They said. Selvam's kite soared highest, but nobody looked. He came home silent. "Amma, yaarum enna paarvaradhu illa." Amma wiped his face. "Naan irukken da. Naan dhan un kuda." Selvam hugged her. "Neenga dhan pothum, Amma."
tamil_dialogues: Selvam: 'Amma, naan kite udhattinen. Aana yaarum enna paarvaradhu illa.'
Amma: 'Naan pathuten da. Un kite sky la romba azhaga irundhadhu.'
Selvam: 'Appo Amma dhan enakku kaadhal.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the ground, looking up at the highest kite, smiling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru pakkam dhan kaadhal iruntha — athu kova pazham maadhiri. Kadikkum. Aana, oruthar dhan namma kuda iruntha pothum."
thirukkural: வீழப் படுவார் கெழீஇயிலர் தாம்வீழ்வார் வீழப் படாஅர் எனின்.
english_translation: Whose love is void of love in turn Are luckless with all esteems they earn.
moral: Unreturned love is like a kite that nobody watches — still flying, but lonely.

title: What Will You Give Me?
hook: Esther keeps making gifts for her best friend who never gives anything back — Paati teaches her real love.
story: Esther made a shell necklace, a drawing, a flower crown. Each one she gave to her best friend Deepa. Deepa took them without a word. Never gave anything back. "Paati, avan enakku onnum kudukala." Paati held her. "Kaadhal, kudukradhukkaaga kudukradhu. Vaangradhukkaaga kudukradhu illa." Esther looked at the sea. "Appo — dhan avarukku kaadhal kudukaama irukken?" Paati smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Esther: 'Paati, naan avanuku romba kuduthen. Avar enakku oru push um kudukala.'
Paati: 'Kaadhal adhu dhan, ma. Kudukradhuku dhan perusa — vaangradhuku illa.'
Esther: 'Appo... enakku kuda kudukala, aana love pannuva dhan?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the beach sand, making a small shell necklace.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaarukku kaadhal kudutha, avar kaekradhu dhan — avanga kaekkala naalum. Kudukradhu dhan kaadhal. Vaangradhuku kaaga illa."
thirukkural: நாம்காதல் கொண்டார் நமக்கெவன் செய்பவோ தாம்காதல் கொள்ளாக் கடை.
english_translation: What can our lover do us now If he does not requite our love?
moral: Love given without return is still love — but it aches like the sea.

title: The Balance Beam
hook: Janani walks on a balance beam and realizes love is like carrying a load on one shoulder only.
story: PT teacher made the class walk the balance beam. Janani carried her water bottle on one side. She wobbled. Fell. "Miss, ennaala balance panna mudiyala!" Teacher smiled. "Rendu pakkamum sama irukkanum. Ore pakkam dhan weight iruntha — balance break aagum." Janani thought of her friendship with Keerthi. She always gave, Keerthi always took. That day, she stopped.
tamil_dialogues: Janani: 'Miss, balance panna kashtama irukku!'
Teacher: 'Iru pakkam sama weight irukka vendum. Illana — vizhundhuduve.'
Janani: 'Adhu dhan... nalla friendship la iruvarum sama kuduka vendum.'
