thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar walks the balance beam effortlessly, arms wide.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Balance beam maadhiri dhan kaadhalum — ore oru pakkam weight iruntha, adhu vilundhudum. Iru pakkamum sama irukka vendum."
thirukkural: ஒருதலையான் இன்னாது காமம்காப் போல இருதலை யானும் இனிது.
english_translation: One sided pains; love in both souls Poises well like shoulder poles.
moral: Love balanced on both sides is sweet — one-sided love is heavy and painful.

title: The Arrow That Only Hits Me
hook: Arul's Thatha shows him a bow and arrow — Cupid only aims at one side, and it hurts.
story: Thatha made Arul a small bow. "Adhu dhan villu. Ithu dhan ambu." Arul shot an arrow. It hit the target. "Thatha, ambu average dhan!" Thatha smiled. "Kaman villum adhe maadhiri dhan. Aana avan oru pakkam dhan ambu vitudraan." Arul thought of his Appa — always working, never playing. "Appo — en Appa enakkaaga dhan, avanuku enna kaadhala?'"
tamil_dialogues: Arul: 'Thatha, kaman enna ore pakkam dhan ambu vitu?'
Thatha: 'Ama — adhu dhan kaadhalin kastam. Ore pakkam dhan thallum.'
Arul: 'Appa enna miss pannra — aana naan avanuku kaadhal kuduthutu irukken.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar holds the bow, not aiming it at anyone, just holding it peacefully.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaman ore pakkam dhan adikkum. Athu dhan vali. Aana, namma kaadhal avanga kitta irukka vendumnu ninaikradhu dhan periya vali."
thirukkural: பருவரலும் பைதலும் காணான்கொல் காமன் ஒருவர்கண் நின்றொழுகு வான்.
english_translation: This cupid aims at me alone; Knows he not my pallor and pain?
moral: One-sided love feels like Cupid only ever shoots at you.

title: The Girl Made of Stone
hook: Muthu never cries, never complains — until teacher learns her parents are always abroad.
story: Muthu was the strongest girl in class. Never cried. Never asked for help. Teacher called her. "Muthu, un Amma Appa enga?" "Singapore, ma'am. Dubai." "Varuvanga?" "Theriyadhu." Teacher saw her steel exterior crack for a second. "Muthu, nee ala koodadha. Enakku sollalam." Muthu's eyes filled. "Yaarum en kitta nalla solla maata. Naanum adhukkaaga kaththukiten."
tamil_dialogues: Teacher: 'Muthu, un Amma Appa varum pothu — unakku santhosham illaya?'
Muthu: 'Varum pothu... aana adhu korachu dhan. Naan oruthan dhan.'
Teacher: 'Nee dhan powerful. Aana vali irukradhu dhan manushan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside Muthu's desk, a stone in one hand, a flower in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaarum nalla sollama iruntha, manasu kallai maadhiri aagidum. Aana adhu dhan vennaiyum irukka mudiyadhu."
thirukkural: வீழ்வாரின் இன்சொல் பெறாஅது உலகத்து வாழ்வாரின் வன்கணார் இல்.
english_translation: None is so firm as she who loves Without kind words from whom she dotes.
moral: The hardest hearts are forged by love that never received kind words back.

title: The Sweetest Voice
hook: Thenmozhi's Appa is far away, but even his voice on the phone is enough to make her day.
story: Appa worked in Bangalore. He called once a week. Thenmozhi waited by the phone all Saturday. When it rang — just one ring — and she grabbed it. "Appa!" His voice, just "Hello kutty," made her heart full. Akka watched. "Appa voice kaelka aprm dhan un mugam colour varudhu." Thenmozhi smiled. "Adhu dhan pothum Akka. Avan voice mathum pothum."
tamil_dialogues: Thenmozhi: 'Appa, neenga enakku phone pannala! Naan wait pannittu irundhen!'
Appa: 'Sorry kutty. Office la busy.'
Thenmozhi: 'Paravala Appa. Unga voice kaelkradhu dhan pothum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the landline phone, one hand on the receiver.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirindha pothum — avan sonna ore vaarthai, athu dhan madhuri. Athu dhan kaadhal. Ore vaarthai pothum vaazhka."
thirukkural: நசைஇயார் நல்கார் எனினும் அவர்மாட்டு இசையும் இனிய செவிக்கு.
english_translation: The lover accords not my desires And yet his words sweeten my ears.
moral: Even a single kind word from the one you love is sweeter than anything.

title: Don't Tell the Sea
theme: Solitary Anguish / Pouring Heart to the Wrong Ears
hook: Kani tries to tell her cousin about missing Appa — cousin doesn't care — so she tells the sea instead.
story: "Uncle, en Appa missing pannraen. Avan en kuda vilayaada maata. Enna pannuven?" Cousin was on his phone. "Hm. Ama." Not listening. Kani walked to Pamban Bridge. The sea roared below. She shouted into it. "APPA! NEE ENNA MISS PANNUVA? NAAN UNNA MISS PANNREN!" The waves answered. A fisherman nodded. "Adhu dhan, kadal kaelkum. Kadal kuda kaelkkuma, manushan kaelkamaat."
tamil_dialogues: Kani: 'Uncle, naan unga kitta pesren! Nee kaelka maata!'
Fisherman: 'Kadal kitta sollu ma. Adhu kaelkum. Manushan kaelka mattan.'
Kani: 'Kadal... en Appa kitta solluvena?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on Pamban Bridge, the sea wind blowing his white hair.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaarum kaelkadhadha, kadal kitta sollu. Kadaluku kastam theriyum. Adhu dhan kaadhalin nijam."
thirukkural: உறாஅர்க்கு உறுநோய் உரைப்பாய் கடலைச் செறாஅய் வாழிய நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: You tell your grief to listless he Bless my heart! rather fill up sea!
moral: Pouring your heart to those who don't care is like trying to fill the ocean.

title: Sweeter Than Jaggery
theme: Sad Memories / Love Sweeter Than Wine
hook: Manju tastes fresh panai vellam and says it's sweet — but not as sweet as Appa's laugh.
story: Paati bought fresh palm jaggery at the market. Manju bit a piece. "Paati, romba sweet!" Paati smiled. "Vellam vida sweeta enna irukku?" Manju thought. "Appa siripu! Avan siripu mathiri inbodha enakku onnum kedaiyaadhu." Paati hugged her. "Appa Singapore la irukka, aana avar siripu un kitta irukku, ma."
tamil_dialogues: Manju: 'Paati, panai vellam super sweet!'
Paati: 'Athu vida sweeta enna irukku?'
Manju: 'Appa siripu! Vellam vida 100 maadu sweetu!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the jaggery stall, holding a piece up to the sun.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vellam vida kaadhal dhan inbodhu. Appa siripu mathiri — atha ninaichadhe pothum. Athu dhan kaadhalin medhu."
thirukkural: உள்ளினும் தீராப் பெருமகிழ் செய்தலால் கள்ளினும் காமம் இனிது.
english_translation: Love is sweeter than wine; for vast Is its delight at very thought.
moral: Love is sweeter than anything — just the memory of it brings joy.

title: Every Thought Is Sweet
hook: Velu remembers the time Appa scolded him — and realizes even that memory is sweet now.
story: Appa was strict. Once he scolded Velu for breaking a coconut sapling. "Enna da panna? Maram valara vendum!" Velu cried that day. Now Appa was in the Gulf. Velu sat under the same sapling — now a tall tree — and smiled. "Amma, Appa enna kaththina dhan, athu kuda sweeta irukku." Amma wiped her eyes. "Adhu dhan kaadhal, da."
tamil_dialogues: Velu: 'Amma, Appa enna kaththina dhan — athu kuda nenaikradhu sweeta irukku.'
Amma: 'Kaadhal adhu dhan. Kadhichalum, athu kuda inbodhu.'
Velu: 'Appa intha marathai paathunda? Valandhu irukku.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the coconut tree, a fallen coconut at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Appa kadhichadhu kuda — athu kaadhal dhan. Pirindha pothu, adhu kuda ninaikradhu inbodhu dhan."
thirukkural: எனைத்தொன்று இனிதேகாண் காமம்தாம் வீழ்வார் நினைப்ப வருவதொன்று ஏல்.
english_translation: Pains are off at the lover's thought In all aspects this love is sweet.
moral: Love turns even painful memories sweet when the one you love is far away.

title: The Sneeze That Stopped
theme: Sad Memories / Thought at a Distance
hook: Rohini sneezes and Amma says "Yaro ninaikra" — Rohini wishes it's Appa thinking of her.
story: Rohini sneezed suddenly. "Yaro ninaikra, ma!" Amma laughed. Rohini looked up. "Appa? Appa ninaikra?" Amma paused. "Ama, ma. Appa dhan." Rohini smiled. But then she wondered — Appa might not even be thinking of her. "Amma, Appa ninaichaa dhan sneeze varuma? Illana?" Amma kissed her forehead. "Nee ninaikradhu dhan perisu. Appa ninaiklanaalum, nee ninaikra."
tamil_dialogues: Rohini: 'Amma, Appa enna ninaikra? Sneezu vanthuchu!'
Amma: 'Ama ma, Appa dhan.'
Rohini: 'Avar ninaiklana...? Sneezu varaadha?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the tea plantation, a single tea leaf in his hand, sneezing gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Sneezu vantha, Appa ninaikka poraar — athu dhan nambikkai. Aana, Appa ninaikkala naalum, nee dhan ninaikra. Adhu dhan kaadhal."
thirukkural: நினைப்பவர் போன்று நினையார்கொல் தும்மல் சினைப்பது போன்று கெடும்.
english_translation: To sneeze I tried hence but could not Me he tried to think but did not.
moral: We hope our loved ones think of us — and every little sign feels like their love.

title: The Mirror In My Heart
theme: Sad Memories / He Lives In Me
hook: Sandhya's Chithi asks if Appa ever thinks of her — Sandhya says yes, because he never left her heart.
story: "Sandhya, un Appa Bangalore la irukka. Avan unna ninaikka poraan?" Chithi asked. Sandhya didn't hesitate. "Ninaippar." "Epdi theriyum?" "En nenchula avan irukka. Adhu dhan pothum." Chithi was stunned. Sandhya continued, "Naan avana ninaikkradhu dhan — avan enna nenaiklanaalum, avan en nenchula irukka." Chithi hugged her. "Unakku dhan theriyum, da."
tamil_dialogues: Chithi: 'Appa unna ninaikra poraan nu epdi theriyum?'
Sandhya: 'Avana en nenchula irukken. Advu dhan pothum. Avan en nenchula iruka varaikkum — avan en kuda irukka.'
Chithi: 'Unakku 10 vayasu dhan, aana teriyama pesura.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the beach, one hand on his chest, eyes closed.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Avan nenchula irukkana, irukkaatanu theriyadhu. Aana nee ninaikkradhu dhan — athu dhan avan un nenchula irukka."
thirukkural: யாமும் உளேங்கொல் அவர்நெஞ்சத்து எந்நெஞ்சத்து ஓஒ உளரே அவர்.
english_translation: Have I a place within his heart? Ah from mine he will never depart.
moral: I may not know if I'm in your heart — but I know you'll never leave mine.

title: The Open Door
hook: Murali's Appa always enters his dreams, but Murali can never enter Appa's — and it hurts.
story: Every night Murali dreamed of Appa. They played cricket, ate mangoes, laughed. When he woke up — empty. "Appa, nee en kaNava varuva. Aana naan un kaNava varala." Amma heard him. "Kaadhal adhu da. Ore oruthar dhan thiruppi thiruppi varuvanga." Murali looked at the apple trees. "Appa en kaNava vara vendum. Illana — naan avara vittutu varala."
tamil_dialogues: Murali: 'Amma, Appa en kanavula varuvar. Aana avar kanavula naan varala.'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan kaadhal — nee dhan avara ninaikra.'
Murali: 'Naan avan kanavula varanum. En kanavula mathum pothum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between two apple trees, a half-eaten apple in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru nenchil dhan nirantharam irukradhu. Appa un kanavula vara, nee avan kanavula varala — adhu dhan kaadhalin kastam."
thirukkural: தம்நெஞ்சத்து எம்மைக் கடிகொண்டார் நாணார்கொல் எம்நெஞ்சத்து ஓவா வரல்.
english_translation: Shame! My heart often he enters Banning me entry into his.
moral: Love means one heart always has an open door — even when the other doesn't.

title: The Day We Met
theme: Sad Memories / Living in the Memory
hook: Lavanya tells Paati she doesn't know who she is without Appa's visits — she only lives for those days.
story: Appa came home once in six months. The rest of the time, Lavanya just waited. "Paati, Appa varadha naalukkaaga dhan naan irukken. Vera enna?" Paati held her. "Nee unakkaaga dhan irukka, ma." Lavanya shook her head. "Paati, Appa kuda irundha 5 naal dhan naan nijama irukken. Bakki naal — wait pannittu irukken." Paati cried silently.
tamil_dialogues: Lavanya: 'Paati, Appa pona aprm naan irukadha maadhiri — athu dhan nijam.'
Paati: 'Illada, nee irukka. Appa varadhadhuku kaathu irukka.'
Lavanya: 'Adhu dhan — kaathu irukradhu dhan en life aachu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the harbour, looking at an empty ship dock.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Appa varum naal varakkum kaathu irukradhu dhan — athu dhan vaazhkkai. Aana, namma oru naal dhan vaazhnthom nu ninaikka koodadhu."
thirukkural: மற்றியான் என்னுளேன் மன்னோ அவரொடியான் உற்றநாள் உள்ள உளேன்.
english_translation: Beyond the thought of life with him What else of life can I presume?
moral: Without the one you love, life feels like waiting — not living.

title: The Memory That Burns
hook: Karuna tries to forget Appa's last angry words — but remembering burns like fire.
story: Appa's last words before leaving were angry. "Nee pannadha da idhu!" Karuna tried to forget. But every time he remembered, his chest burned. "Thatha, maraka mudiyadhu. Enna pannuven?" Thatha lit a lamp. "Neeye dhan lamp. Adha otta — kaedpadhu dhan. Illana, kaedpadhu dhan." Karuna didn't understand. "Aana marakuven? Appa sonnadha?" "Marakka vendam. Nee adha eduthuko da."
tamil_dialogues: Karuna: 'Thatha, Appa sonna kovam — atha maraka mudiyala. Enna pannuven?'
Thatha: 'Marakka vendam. Atha eduthuko. Athu dhan kaadhal.'
Karuna: 'Kaedpa kaedpa... adhu kEDumma?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the lamp, its flame reflected in his eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ninaivugal sutradhu dhan — adhu kaadhal. Maraka mudiyadha ninaivu, athu dhan nijam. Aana, adhu kedpadhu namma kaiyila dhan."
thirukkural: மறப்பின் எவனாவன் மற்கொல் மறப்பறியேன் உள்ளினும் உள்ளம் சுடும்.
english_translation: What will happen if I forget When his memory burns my heart?
moral: Some memories burn — but forgetting the one you love would burn worse.

title: The King of Memory
theme: Sad Memories / Boundless Freedom of Love
hook: Indhu tells Appa on the phone that she thinks of him all day — Appa never gets angry.
story: "Appa, naan unna 1000 times ninaichuten!" Appa laughed on the phone. "Sari ma, 1001." Indhu kept counting. "Appa, unaku kovama illa? Romba ninaiku?" Appa said, "Nee ninaikradhu dhan enakku periya gift, ma. Atha viral la ennikadha." Indhu smiled. "Appo appa — evlo naalum ninaikalaam?" "Nee ninaikra varaikkum, naan irukken."
tamil_dialogues: Indhu: 'Appa, naan unna 1000 times ninaichuten! Kovama?'
Appa: 'Kaadhalukku kovam varadhu kutty. Nee ninaikra varaikkum naan irukken.'
Indhu: 'Appo evlo naalum ninaikalaam? Endless ah?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the lake, counting ripples on the water with his finger.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaadhal — avan evlo ninaichalum avanuku kovam varadhu. Athu dhan adhan perumai. Nee ninaikradhu avanukku periya kaanugai."
thirukkural: எனைத்து நினைப்பினும் காயார் அனைத்தன்றோ காதலர் செய்யும் சிறப்பு.
english_translation: I bring him to ceaseless memory He chides not; and thus honours me.
moral: True love never tires of being remembered — it's the greatest honour.

title: The Dying Light
theme: Sad Memories / Love Clings to Life
hook: Ezhil falls sick with fever — the doctor says it's nothing, but Ezhil knows it's because Appa didn't call.
story: Ezhil lay in bed, burning with fever. "Amma, Appa phone pannala." Amma gave medicine. "Adhukku dhan fever." Ezhil shook her head. "Illama. Appa varadha varutham." Amma called Appa. The phone rang. Appa answered. "Hello kutty!" Ezhil's fever didn't break — but she smiled. "Amma, Appa voice kaelka aprm... enakku konjam nalla irukku." Amma cried.
tamil_dialogues: Ezhil: 'Amma, Appa phone pannala. Athaala dhan enakku fever.'
Amma: 'Appa voice kaelkradhuku dhan fever?"
Ezhil: 'Ama. Avan voice kaelka aprm... enakku bayam illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by Ezhil's bedside, one hand on her forehead, the other on the phone.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Appa voice kaelkradhu dhan — athu dhan marunthu. Pirindha pothu, uyir kooda melindhu pogum. Kaadhal adhu dhan."
thirukkural: விளியுமென் இன்னுயிர் வேறல்லம் என்பார் அளியின்மை ஆற்ற நினைந்து.
english_translation: Dear life ebbs away by thought Of him who said we are one heart.
moral: Love makes life cling by a thread — the thought of the beloved is both poison and cure.

title: Moon, Wait For Me
hook: Thenpaavi watches the moon rise and begs it not to set — because Appa might see it and think of her.
story: Thenpaavi sat by Kodaikanal lake. The moon rose, huge and golden. "Amma, moon enakku Appa kitta message eduthutumaa?" Amma laughed. "Epdi?" "Moon ellarum patha maadhiri — Appa pathena moon a pathu, enna nenaikra?" Thenpaavi begged the moon. "Moon, set aavadha! Konjam iru. Appa varum varaikkum iru." The moon stayed. Or so she believed.
tamil_dialogues: Thenpaavi: 'Moon, set aagadha! Appa paathu enna ninaikra varaikkum iru!'
Amma: 'Moon dhan irukkum, ma. Adhukku amma soldradhu kaelkadhu.'
Thenpaavi: 'Appa kitta sollu moon. Naan miss pannrennu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the lake bank, the full moon reflected in the water before him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Moon dhan ennavu sollum. Appa kuda moon pathu, unna ninaippara. Moon dhan thoodhu, ma."
thirukkural: விடாஅது சென்றாரைக் கண்ணினால் காணப் படாஅதி வாழி மதி.
english_translation: Hail moon! Set not so that I find Him who left me but not my mind.
moral: The moon connects all hearts — look at it and know your loved one sees it too.

title: கனவில் வந்த அப்பா
hook: Kavin dreams of Appa who's working in Chennai — the dream feels more real than waking.
story: Appa went to Chennai for work three months ago. Every night Kavin kept the photo near his pillow. One night he dreamed Appa walked through the temple gate carrying mangoes. "Kavin ma, sapta vaa!" He woke up sweating. "Amma, naan kandaen — Appa vandhaar!" Amma smiled through tears. "Kavin, kanavu dhan unmaiyaga irukkum — aana un anbu nijam."
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Amma, naan kandaen! Appa mango kitta vandhaaru!'
Amma: 'Kanavu kandhaalum athu un anbin sathiyam da kutty.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple entrance, a mango leaf garland draped over his shoulder, smiling gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kanavil varavum anbin thoothu. Nee kanda kanavu unmaiyaga illa irundhalum, un anbu nijam — athu dhan mukkiyam."
thirukkural: காதலர் தூதொடு வந்த கனவினுக்கு யாதுசெய் வேன்கொல் விருந்து.
english_translation: How shall I feast this dream-vision That brings the beloved's love-mission?
moral: Dreams carry the message of love — even if they are not real, the love behind them is.

title: தூங்கச் சொல்லும் கண்கள்
hook: Meena hasn't spoken to her best friend Divya in a week after a fight — she begs her eyes to close so she can see Divya in dreams.
story: Meena and Divya fought over a pencil. Divya stopped talking. Meena couldn't eat, couldn't study. That night she whispered to her eyes: "Kanngala, ungalukku thaan theriyum Divya enga irukkaanga — thungungaa, naan kandaen Divya kitta solunga!" She fell asleep fast. She dreamed Divya smiled and gave her the pencil back. Next morning, Meena ran to school. Divya was at the gate. "Meena, naanum kandhen — nee varuva nu!"
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Kanngalaa, neengalukku thaan theriyum — thungunga, Divya kitta sollunga!'
Divya: 'Naanum kandhen nee varuva nu! Vaa povoam!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a neem tree near the school gate, a pencil balanced on his open palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un kannu un kanavuku thoothu poardu. Athu un anbin sathiyam — theriyum Divya unna ninaikkiraanga nu."
thirukkural: கயலுண்கண் யானிரப்பத் துஞ்சிற் கலந்தார்க்கு உயலுண்மை சாற்றுவேன் மன்.
english_translation: I beg these fish-like dark eyes sleep To tell my lover how life I keep.
moral: When you miss someone dearly, your heart sends messages even through your dreams.

title: நனவில் இல்லார் கனவில் வர
theme: Of Dreams / Meeting Absent Beloved in Dreams
hook: Arjun's grandfather passed away — he only meets Thatha now in dreams.
story: Thatha used to take Arjun to the tank every morning. After Thatha died, mornings felt empty. One night Arjun dreamed Thatha sat on the steps, feeding fish. "Thatha, neenga inga!" Thatha laughed. "Naan eppavum inga dhan, da — un kanavula!" Arjun woke up smiling for the first time in weeks. He told Amma. She held him close. "Kanavil kandhaalum — athu un thaaithin anbu."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Thatha, neenga inga! Naan ungalai MISS pannen!'
Amma: 'Kanavila irundhalum anbu nijam da kutty.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Thatha on the tank steps, both watching the fish swim.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nanavil kaanaathavarai kanavil kaanalaam. Athu kanavalla — un anbin valimai."
thirukkural: நனவினால் நல்கா தவரைக் கனவினால் காண்டலின் உண்டென் உயிர்.
english_translation: In wakeful hours who sees me not I meet in dreams and linger yet.
moral: Love does not end with separation — dreams keep our loved ones close to our hearts.

title: கனவில் கிடைத்த காய்கறி
hook: Priya is in boarding school — she dreams Amma cooks her favorite keerai sambar.
story: Priya missed Amma's cooking. The hostel food was bland. She cried at night. That night she dreamed Amma served hot keerai sambar with crispy vadam. "Amma, intha vasanai!" She woke up with wet cheeks but a full heart. She wrote a letter: "Amma, kanavula un sambar saaptene — romba nalla irundhu!" Amma replied: "Innu oru maasam than, da. Naa unakku nereya keerai saapda venum nu aasai pannuren."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Amma, kanavula un sambar kandhen! Romba nalla irundhu!'
Amma: 'Innu oru maasam da kutty. Appo vandhu nereya saapdu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by a cooking fire, a ladle in his hand, steam rising gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kanavu unmaiyaga irukkaadhu — aana un anbu nijam. Athu dhan uyirukku thaunbu."
thirukkural: கனவினான் உண்டாகும் காமம் நனவினான் நல்காரை நாடித் தரற்கு.
english_translation: In dreams I enjoy his love-bliss Who in wakeful hours I miss.
moral: Even in dreams, the love we feel fills our hearts when waking life cannot.

title: கனவும் நனவும்
hook: Varun argues with Akka that dreams are better than reality — until reality catches up.
story: Varun loved his dream world. In dreams he scored goals, won races, met cricketers. Akka teased: "Nee kanavula dhan hero!" One day he won a real race at school. He came home beaming. "Akka, inniku naan NANJI kandaen!" Akka laughed. "Ippo theriyudha — kanavum nalla, aana nanavum nalla!" Varun agreed. "Rendume nalla than — aana reality-la jeya feel vera level!"
tamil_dialogues: Varun: 'Kanavula ethume kadaikkaadhu — aana adhula happy ah irukkum!'
Akka: 'Ippo nanavum nalla nu theriyudha? Rendum nalla than!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar holds a medal in one hand and a dream-catcher in the other, balancing them.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kanavum intha — nanavum intha. Rendum anbin sirappu. Aana nanavil nadakkathai kanavil kaanbu athu dhan visesham."
thirukkural: நனவினால் கண்டதூஉம் ஆங்கே கனவுந்தான் கண்ட பொழுதே இனிது.
english_translation: Dream-sight of him delights at once Awake — What of seeing him — hence.
moral: Both dreams and reality have their sweetness — cherish both.

title: நனவு இல்லா உலகம்
theme: Of Dreams / If No Waking, Dreams Keep Lover Close
hook: Deepa wishes she could live in her dreams where Appa is always home.
story: Appa works on ships — he comes home only twice a year. Deepa drew pictures of him every day. "Appa, neenga varum podhellam nalla irukkum — aana poirum podhu evlo kashtama irukkum!" Appa asked: "Kanavula naan iruppen la?" Deepa nodded. "Athuvum oru ulagam dhan — nanavum kanavum rendume nalla. Aana kanavula neenga irukkeengala?"
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Appa, kanavula neenga eppavum irukeengala?'
Appa: 'Iruppen da. Anbin thoothu kanavu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the Rockfort summit, looking out at the distant sea.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kanavu illai naalum anbu irukkum. Athu kanavukkulla irukkaadhu — un manasukkulla irukkum."
thirukkural: நனவென ஒன்றில்லை ஆயின் கனவினால் காதலர் நீங்கலர் மன்.
english_translation: If wakeful hours come to nought My lover in dreams would never depart.
moral: Even when separated, love finds its home in our dreams.

title: கனவில் வேதனை
theme: Of Dreams / Rejected Awake, Tormented in Dreams
hook: Kavi's cousin ignores him at family gatherings but appears in his dreams teasing him.
story: Kavi's cousin Suri always ignored him at functions. "Avan enna kaara minukkar nu theriyadhu!" Kavi was upset. That night he dreamed Suri played with him, laughing. He woke up confused. Periyamma said: "Kanavula avan un friend — aana nanavula avan thappu panraan. Nee maaf pannu, adhu dhan valimai."
tamil_dialogues: Kavi: 'Periyamma, avan enna maadhiri kanavula varaan — aana nanavula solraan!'
Periyamma: 'Kanavu un anbu — nanavula avan unmaiyai kaattum. Nee maaf pannu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple steps, a garland of jasmine in his lap.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nanavil kaanaathavarai kanavil kaanalaam. Aana nanavil avan enna panraan un — athu dhan unmaiyai kaattum."
thirukkural: நனவினால் நல்காக் கொடியார் கனவனால் என்எம்மைப் பீழிப் பது.
english_translation: Awake he throws my overtures Adream, ah cruel! he tortures!
moral: Dreams may show us kind versions of people — but reality teaches us truth.

title: தோள்மேல் தூங்கினேன்
theme: Of Dreams / Asleep on Shoulders, Awake in Heart
hook: Meera falls asleep on Amma's shoulder during a bus ride — when she wakes, Amma is gone but the warmth remains.
story: They traveled to Thiruvalluvar temple by bus. Meera slept on Amma's shoulder the whole way. When she woke up, Amma was praying at the shrine. Meera felt the shoulder was gone but the warmth stayed. She held Amma's hand tight. "Amma, neenga irukkum podhu romba nalla irukkum — aana poirum podhu manasukulla irukeengala?"
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Amma, neenga irukkum podhu shoulder la irukeengala?'
Amma: 'Eppavum iruppen da. Thungumbodhu irukken, azhumbumbodhu manasula irukken.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Amma in the temple, a child sleeping peacefully against his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thungumbodhu thoonmai irukkum — azhumbumbodhu manasukkull irukkum. Athu dhan kadhalin sirappu."
thirukkural: துஞ்சுங்கால் தோள்மேலர் ஆகி விழிக்குங்கால் நெஞ்சத்தர் ஆவர் விரைந்து.
english_translation: Asleep he embraces me fast; Awake he enters quick my heart.
moral: When someone truly loves us, they are with us in body and in spirit — always.

title: கனவில் காணாத ஏக்கம்
theme: Of Dreams / Missing Even in Dreams
hook: Arjun's best friend moved to Bangalore — he can't even dream of him.
story: Arjun and Rajan were inseparable. Rajan's family moved to Bangalore. Arjun tried dreaming of him but couldn't. "Akka, naan kooda kanavula kankaataan!" Akka said: "Kanavu varum podhu varum — forced ah varadhu. Aana nee avanai ninaikkaradhu — athu kooda oru thooram kanavu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Akka, naan Rajan ah kanavula kankaataan! Enna pannanum?'
Akka: 'Forced ah kanavu varadhu. Aana ninaippu — athu kooda kanavu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the river bank, a paper boat floating past his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kanavu varum podhu varum — athai nee kattuppadi seya mudiyaadhu. Aana ninaippu thanimaiyai nokaadhu."
thirukkural: நனவினால் நல்காை நோவர் கனவினால் காதலர்க் காணா தவர்.
english_translation: In dreams who don't discern lovers Rue their missing in wakeful hours.
moral: Even without dreams, love and memory keep our friends close to our hearts.

title: ஊர்க்காரர் சொன்ன சொல்
theme: Of Dreams / Town Says He Left, Dreams Can't Find Him
hook: Kavin's friend moved away — the neighborhood kids say he's gone forever but Kavin dreams of finding him.
story: Senthil moved to Mumbai. The kids in the street said: "Avan ponaan, varmaataan!" Kavin felt angry. "Illai! Naan kandhen avanai kanavula!" That night he dreamed Senthil stood at the bus stop, waving. He woke up crying. Paati held him. "Kanavula kandhalum un anbu irukku — athu poagadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Paati, avan ponaan nu yaarum sollanum! Naan kanavula kandhen!'
Paati: 'Kanavu un anbu sathiyam da. Athu poagadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the bus stop, a travel bag at his feet, watching the bus depart.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oor karaar solla koodadhu — un kanavu un anbu. Athu sathiyam."
thirukkural: நனவினால் நம்நீத்தார் என்பர் கனவினால் காணார்கொல் இவ்வூ ரவர்.
english_translation: The townsmen say he left me thus In dreams failing to see him close.
moral: What others say does not change the truth of our love and our dreams.

title: மாலை வந்தது
theme: Grief at Eventide / Evening as a Killing Dart
hook: Meena waits for Amma to return from work — every evening feels like a painful knife.
story: Amma worked at the hospital. She came home late every day. Meena sat at the window watching the sky turn orange. "Amma varum podhu maadhiri — aana maadhukku maalai varum podhu bayama irukkum!" Amma came home at 9. She hugged Meena. "Maadhuku bayama irukkaadhu — naan eppavum varuven."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Amma, maalai varum podhu bayama irukkum — neenga varum podhu dhan comfort.'
Amma: 'Naan eppavum varuven da. Maadhuku bayama irukkaadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple gate, holding a lamp against the darkening sky.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Maalai oru velai — athai bayam maadhaadhu. Un amma varuvaargal — athai nambu."
thirukkural: மாலையோ அல்லை மணந்தார் உயிருண்ணும் வேலைநீ வாழி பொழுது.
english_translation: Bless you! you are not eventide But killing dart to wedded bride!
moral: Evening may seem scary, but the wait for a loved one ends with their arrival.

title: மாலையின் துணை
theme: Grief at Eventide / Sad Evening Has Cruel Companion
hook: Varun fears the evening because it always brings loneliness as its companion.
story: Varun's Appa was in the army. Every evening when the sun set, Varun felt alone. Periyappa noticed. "Maadhuku bayama irukka? Aana bayam unnai thunichu thaan vaazhkkai — athai overcome pannu!" That evening Varun sat by the Teppakulam and watched the sunset without fear. "Periyappa, maalai bayama irukkala — aana naan irukken."
tamil_dialogues: Varun: 'Periyappa, maalai varum podhu bayama irukkum — bayam dhan thunichidum.'
Periyappa: 'Bayam unnai thuniakkaadhu — nee bayathai jeyikkaraan nu ninachchu paaru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the edge of the Teppakulam, the reflection of the sunset on his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Maalai bayathoda varum — aana bayathai thaniaga ninakkaadhu. Un valimaiyai ninachu paaru."
thirukkural: புன்கண்ணை வாழி மருள்மாலை எம்கேள்போல் வன்கண்ண தோநின் துணை.
english_translation: Hail sad eventide dim and grim Has your mate like mine, cruel whim!
moral: Fear comes as evening's companion — but courage can send it away.

title: பனிக்கால மாலை
theme: Grief at Eventide / Wet Evening Trembling with Pain
hook: Lakshmi watches the evening mist cover the garden — it trembles like her own heart when Thatha is away.
story: Thatha went to the hospital for a checkup. Lakshmi stood at the garden gate. The evening mist crept over the flowers like a ghost. "Thatha, intha maalai romba bayama irukku — athu enna maadhiri thalai aadudhu!" Thatha returned that night. He held her hand. "Maalai bayama irukkum — aana naan varuven, nu ninachu paaru."
tamil_dialogues: Lakshmi: 'Thatha, intha maalai enna maadhiri thalai aadudhu! Adhu bayama irukku!'
Thatha: 'Bayam varum — aana naan varuven. Ninachchu paaru da kutty.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the misty garden, a shawl wrapped around him, breathing into the cold air.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Maalai bayam varum — athu bayam dhan. Aana bayathai nee thaniaga ninakkaama, valimaiyai ninachu paar."
thirukkural: பனிஅரும்பிப் பைதல்கொள் மாலை துனிஅரும்பித் துன்பம் வளர வரும்.
english_translation: Wet eve came pale and trembling then Now it makes bold with growing pain.
moral: The cold evening may bring fear, but remembering our loved ones brings warmth.

title: கொலைக்களத்து மாலை
theme: Grief at Eventide / Evening Comes Like a Slayer
hook: Arjun watches the evening shadows creep through the old fort — they look like soldiers approaching.
story: Arjun and Amma visited the Vellore Fort. As evening fell, long shadows crept across the courtyard. "Amma, intha shadows enna maadhiri soldier maadhiri varudhu!" Amma held his hand. "Adhu shadow dhan — bayama irukkaadhu. Naan irukken." They walked out of the fort together. The shadows couldn't touch them.
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Amma, shadows soldier maadhiri varudhu! Adhu bayama irukku!'
Amma: 'Adhu shadow dhan da — naan irukken, bayama irukkaadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the fort entrance, his shadow stretching long across the courtyard.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bayam enbadhu oru naadagam — athai nee thaniaga face pannaadha. Un amma irukkiraargal — adhu dhan valimai."
thirukkural: காதலர் இல்வழி மாலை கொலைக்களத்து ஏதிலர் போல வரும்.
english_translation: Lover away, comes eventide Like slayer to field of homicide.
moral: When someone we love is near, even the scariest shadows cannot harm us.

title: காலையும் மாலையும்
theme: Grief at Eventide / What Good to Morning, What Evil to Evening
hook: Deepa wonders why mornings feel happy and evenings feel sad — Appa explains.
story: Deepa noticed mornings were always good — school, friends, fun. Evenings were always bad — homework, tiredness, missing Appa. "Appa, enna maalai maathiri mathavathai? Kalai nalla, maalai theeya?" Appa smiled. "Maalai theeyilla da — nee kalaiyil irukkum anandhai maadhuku maalaiyil miss pannura. Adhu love dhan, theeyilla."
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Appa, kalai nalla irukkum — maalai enna theeyaa irukkum?'
Appa: 'Theeyilla da. Neenga kalaiyil irukkum happy ah miss pannura — athu love dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between a rising sun and a setting moon, his arms open wide.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kalai nalla irukkum — maalai theeyalla. Neenga kalaiyil irukkum anandhai maalaiyil ninaikkiraargal — athu kadhal."
thirukkural: காலைக்குச் செய்தநன்று என்கொல் எவன்கொல்யான் மாலைக்குச் செய்த பகை?
english_translation: What good have I done to morning And what evil to this evening?
moral: The sadness of evening is not its fault — it is the echo of our morning happiness.

title: மாலை நோய் தெரியாது
theme: Grief at Eventide / Never Knew Evening Grief When Lord Was Near
hook: Meera never knew evening sadness until her best friend moved to another school.
story: Meera and Thamarai walked home together every evening. Then Thamarai's family moved. The first evening alone felt strange. "Paati, naan innum Thamarai kooda maalai la pogala — adhu enna feel nu theriyadhu!" Paati smiled. "Unakku theriyadhu — yenna nee avanoda irundha nee maalai bayathai kandaadha. Ippo theriyudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Paati, maalai enna feel nu theriyadhu — naan Thamarai kooda irundhen!'
Paati: 'Avanoda irundha nee bayathai kandaadha — Ippo theriyudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the palace steps, a friend's shadow cast beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thunimai theriyum podhu — neenga nanbirar kooda irundha podhu bayathai kandaadhu nu theriyum. Athu dhan kadhal."
thirukkural: மாலைநோய் செய்தல் மணந்தார் அகலாத காலை அறிந்த திலேன்.
english_translation: Evening pangs I have not known When my lord never left me alone.
moral: We only know the value of someone's presence when they are gone.

title: காலை அரும்பும் மாலை மலரும்
theme: Grief at Eventide / Disease Blooms at Dawn, Peaks at Dusk
hook: Kavin feels fine in the morning but gets anxious every evening — Amma teaches him why.
story: Kavin noticed something strange — every morning he was fine, but by evening his stomach hurt. "Amma, enna kalai la nalla irukkum, maalai la maathiri irukkum?" Amma checked with the doctor. "Athu stress da — nee maalai la ninaikkira bayam adhukku kaaranam." Kavin learned to take deep breaths at sunset. The evening pain faded.
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Amma, kalai la nalla irukkum — maalai la enna bayama irukkum?'
Amma: 'Adhu stress da. Maalai la deep breath edukku — bayam pogum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple garden, a flower bud opening at dawn and wilting at dusk.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kalaiyil arumbum, maalaiyil magizhum — athu poovin kavidhai. Un bayamum adhu maadhiri — athai nee thariaga ninaikkaadha."
thirukkural: காலை அரும்பிப் பகலெல்லாம் போதாகி மாலை மலரும்இந் நோய்.
english_translation: Budding at dawn burgeoning all day This disease blooms in evening gay.
moral: Evening sadness is just morning joy waiting to bloom again tomorrow.

title: குழல் வாசிக்கும் ஆயன்
theme: Grief at Eventide / Shepherd's Flute Announces Evening
hook: Every evening a shepherd plays his flute near the river — the sound makes Priya miss her Appa.
story: Every evening at sunset, a shepherd boy played his flute near the river. Priya would sit at the window and listen. The music reminded her of Appa's old violin. "Thaai, intha kuzhal enna maadhiri Appa oda violin maadhiri irukku — adhu bayama irukkum!" Thaai held her close. "Kuzhal bayathoda varum — aana athu anbin sathiyam."
tamil_dialogues: Priya: 'Thaai, intha kuzhal Appa oda violin maadhiri irukku — adhu bayama irukkum!'
Thaai: 'Kuzhal anbin sathiyam da — bayathoda varum, aana athu love dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a banyan tree, a flute resting on his knees, eyes closed.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kuzhal bayathodu varum — aana athu bayam alla. Athu un anbin sathiyam, un amma anbin sathiyam."
thirukkural: அழல்போலும் மாலைக்குத் தூதாகி ஆயன் குழல்போலும் கொல்லும் படை.
english_translation: A deadly arm, this shepherd's flute Hails flaming eve and slays my heart.
moral: The evening's music may sting our hearts, but it carries messages of love.

title: மாலை மயக்கம்
theme: Grief at Eventide / Deluding Evening Prolongs Pain
hook: Varun feels dizzy every evening — he thinks the evening is bewitching him.
story: Varun noticed the evening light made everything look strange. Trees looked like giants, shadows like monsters. "Appa, intha maalai enna maadhiri MAYAKKAM maadhiri irukkum!" Appa laughed. "Adhu light game da — maalai la sun low-a varum, adhanaala shadows neenga think panna padhathai kaattum. Adhu mayakam illa, light magic."
tamil_dialogues: Varun: 'Appa, maalai la everything bayama irukkum — enna mayakkam maadhiri!'
Appa: 'Adhu light magic da — sun low-a varum, shadows bayama kaattum. Adhu mayakam illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the hilltop, the evening light casting a golden glow around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Maalai mayakkam — athu un bayathin vilai. Un bayathai nee namachchu paar, adhu mayakam pogum."
thirukkural: பதிமருண்டு பைதல் உழக்கும் மதிமருண்டு மாலை படர்தரும் போழ்து.
english_translation: Deluding eve if it prolongs The whole town will suffer love-pangs.
moral: The evening's bewitching light is just our own fears reflected back at us.

title: பொருள் தேடும் மாலை
theme: Grief at Eventide / Thinking of Wealth-Seeker, Life Outlasts Twilight
hook: Meena's Appa goes to work early and returns late — she wonders if the evening will ever end.
story: Appa left before sunrise and hadn't returned by sunset. Meena sat at the door. "Amma, Appa enna pannuraargal? Maalai aagudhu — avanga varala!" Amma said: "Appa unakku food vaanguradhu kaga panraargal. Maalai aagum podhu avanga varuvaargal." At 8 PM, Appa arrived with a packet of sweets. "Kutty, naan unakku laddu vandhuten!"
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Amma, Appa varala — maalai aagudhu! Avanga enna pannuraargal?'
Amma: 'Appa unakku sweets vaanguradhu kaga panraargal. Avanga varuvaargal.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the market gate, a bag of sweets in his hand, smiling at the children.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un Appa unakku vaangi panraargal — athu kadhal. Maalai varum, aana Appa varuvaargal."
thirukkural: பொருள்மாலை யாளரை உள்ளி மருள்மாலை மாயும்என் மாயா உயிர்.
english_translation: Thinking of him whose quest is wealth My life outlives the twilight stealth.
moral: When we know our loved ones are working for us, even the longest evening passes.

title: கண்ணில் மலர் நாணம்
theme: Wasting Beauty / Eyes Blush Thinking of Beloved
hook: Kavin draws a picture of his best friend and his cheeks go red — Akka teases him about blushing.
story: Kavin drew a picture of his friend Karthik. His cheeks went pink. Akka saw. "Kavin, un kanna enna red-a irukku?" Kavin hid the paper. "Illai! Naan drawing panren!" Akka laughed. "Un kanna solradhu — nee avanai romba ninaikkira nu. Adhu love between friends — adhu nalla love."
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Akka, naan drawing panren — en kanna enna red-a irukkum?'
Akka: 'Un kannu solradhu — nee avanai romba ninaikkira nu. Adhu nalla love da.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside a temple sculpture, his eyes lowered with a gentle smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannil nanaamal — un anbu kanavaathu. Adhu unmaiyana anbu — athu pugazh."
thirukkural: சிறுமை நமக்கொழியச் சேட்சென்றார் உள்ளி நறுமலர் நாணின கண்.
english_translation: To lift from want he left me afar His thought makes my eyes blush the flower.
moral: True affection shows in our eyes — even when we try to hide it.

title: கண்ணீர் சொல்லும் உண்மை
theme: Wasting Beauty / Pale Tearful Eyes Betray Absence
hook: Meera's teacher notices she looks tired — the truth is she misses her Appa who's traveling.
story: Teacher asked: "Meena, nee enna ivlo tired-a irukka?" Meena's eyes filled up. "Teacher, naan Appa ah MISS pannuren — avanga kaiyalil work panraargal!" Paati was called. She looked at Meena's eyes — red, swollen. "Kannu solradhu da — nee Appa ah bayama ninaikkira nu. Adhu kadhalin valimai."
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Teacher, naan Appa ah MISS pannuren — avanga kaiyalil work panraargal!'
Paati: 'Kannu solradhu da — nee Appa ah bayama ninaikkira nu. Adhu kadhalin valimai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside the canal, watching the water flow steadily.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu kannai kaatradhu — un anbu kannu kaatradhu. Adhu valimai, adhu kadhal."
thirukkural: நயந்தவர் நல்காமை சொல்லுவ போலும் பசந்து பனிவாரும் கண்.
english_translation: My pale tearful eyes betray The hardness of my husband, away.
moral: Our eyes reveal what our lips try to hide — love is always visible.

title: தோள் சுருங்கியது
theme: Wasting Beauty / Shrunk Arms Proclaim Absence
hook: Arjun notices his old cricket bat feels heavier — his arms have grown thin from not playing since Appa left.
story: Arjun tried lifting his cricket bat — it felt heavy. "Amma, en kaai enna maadhiri intha bat weight ah irukkum?" Amma checked. "Un kaai瘦瘦-a irukku da — nee innum cricket vilayadala adhanaala." Arjun realized he stopped playing since Appa went to Mumbai. "Appa irundha naan vilayaden — aana avanga illama bayama irukkum."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Amma, en kaai enna maadhiri瘦瘦-a irukku — bat weight ah irukkum!'
Amma: 'Neenga cricket vilayadala adhanaala da — Appa vandhu vilayaattunga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar holds a cricket bat, his own arms thin but steady.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un kaai瘦瘦-a irukkaradhu — adhu un anbin valimai. Neenga Appa ah ninaikkiraargal — adhu love."
thirukkural: தணந்தமை சால அறிவிப்ப போலும் மணந்தநாள் வீங்கிய தோள்.
english_translation: These arms that swelled on nuptial day Now shrunk proclaim "He is away."
moral: The body shows what the heart feels — separation leaves its mark.

title: விழுந்த வளையல்
theme: Wasting Beauty / Bracelets Slip Off Wasted Arms
hook: Deepa's old bangles slip off her wrist — Akka says it's because she's been missing Amma.
story: Deepa tried wearing Amma's old bangles. They slid right off. "Akka, intha bangles enna maadhiri loose-a irukkum!" Akka looked. "Deepa, nee innum nalla saapdala — Amma ah MISS pannu — adhanaala un kaai瘦瘦-a irukku." Deepa's eyes filled. She put the bangles in a box. "Naan Amma vandhu varuven — appo naan bangles poduven."
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Akka, intha bangles loose-a irukkum! Enna aachunu theriyadhu!'
Akka: 'Neenga Amma ah MISS pannu — adhanaala kaai瘦瘦-a irukku. Avanga vandhu saapduvu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by a jewellery box, a bangle in his hand, the morning light catching its gold.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Vazhayaal thool aagum — aana anbu aagadhu. Un anbu valimai, athu poagadhu."
thirukkural: பணைநீங்கிப் பைந்தொடி சோரும் துணைநீங்கித் தொல்கவின் வாடிய தோள்.
english_translation: Bracelets slip off the arms that have Lost old beauty for He took leave.
moral: When someone we love is away, even our ornaments cannot stay — love gives beauty its weight.

title: கொடிய சொல்
theme: Wasting Beauty / Bereft of Beauty, Arms Tell Cruelty
hook: Varun's friend calls him thin — the truth is Varun stopped eating since his best friend moved away.
story: Suresh laughed: "Varun, nee enna skinny-a irukka?" Varun felt hurt. Periyamma heard. "Suresh, Varun avanoda best friend ah miss panraan — adhanaala avan nalla saapdala. Adhu cruel ah irukkum." Suresh felt sorry. "Varun, sorry da — naan un feeling ah purinjukkala."
tamil_dialogues: Suresh: 'Varun, nee enna skinny-a irukka? Enna aachu?'
Periyamma: 'Avan avanoda best friend ah miss panraan — adhanaala saapdala. Adhu kashtam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between two boys, one thin and one muscular, both looking at each other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un aatkal瘦瘦-a irukkaradhu — adhu un bayathin vilai. Aana avan maaf pannan — adhu valimai."
thirukkural: கொடியார் கொடுமை உரைக்கும் தொடியொடு தொல்கவின் வாடிய தோள்.
english_translation: Bereft of bracelets and old beauty Arms tell the cruel's cruelty.
moral: The signs of missing someone are not weakness — they are love's testimony.

title: வளையல் கழன்றது
theme: Wasting Beauty / Arms Thin, Armlets Loose — Pain of Being Called Cruel
hook: Meena's armlet falls off during class — she feels embarrassed and the real reason comes out.
story: During science class, Meena's armlet dropped. Everyone looked. Divya whispered: "Meena, nee enna ivlo瘦瘦-a irukka?" Meena cried. After class she said: "Naan Amma ah MISS pannuren — avanga Chennai la irukkanga. Adhanaala saapdala." Divya hugged her. "Sorry Meena — naan therinju solala."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Meena, nee enna ivlo瘦瘦-a irukka? Armlet vizhundhuchu!'
Meena: 'Naan Amma ah MISS pannuren — adhanaala saapdala. Sorry.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in a classroom, an armlet balanced on a desk.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un aatkal瘦瘦-a irukkaradhu — adhu un anbin vilai. Adhai yaarum kuttavaadhadhu — adhu kadhal."
thirukkural: தொடியொடு தோள்நெகிழ நோவல் அவரைக் கொடியர் எனக்கூறல் நொந்து.
english_translation: Arms thin, armlets loose make you call My sire cruel; that pains my soul.
moral: The pain of missing someone should not be mocked — it is a sign of deep love.

title: நெஞ்சே சொல்லு
theme: Wasting Beauty / Go Tell the Cruel One About My Arms
hook: Kavin wants to tell his traveling Appa how much he's changed — he sends a photo through Paati.
story: Kavin stood before the mirror. He looked different — thinner, paler. "Paati, naan Appa kitta solanum — enna maadhiri aayitten nu!" Paati helped him take a photo. "Intha photo la un kannu solradhu — Appa ku adhu theriyum." Kavin wrote on the back: "Appa, neenga varum podhu naan marubadiyum nalla aayitten — aana neenga illama kashtam."
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Paati, naan Appa kitta solanum — enna maadhiri aayitten nu!'
Paati: 'Intha photo la un kannu solradhu — Appa ku theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar holds a photograph, his thumb gently touching the edge of the frame.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un kannu solradhu — un anbu solradhu. Appa ku theriyum. Avanga varuvaargal."
thirukkural: பாடு பெறுதியோ நெஞ்சே கொடியார்க்கென் வாடுதோட் பூசல் உரைத்து.
english_translation: Go and tell the cruel, O mind Bruit over my arms and glory find.
moral: Sometimes we need to let our loved ones know how we feel — even through a photo.

title: பேதை நுதல் பசந்தது
theme: Wasting Beauty / Fair Forehead Paled as Arms Were Released
hook: Lakshmi's friend notices her forehead has lost its glow — she realizes she's been crying too much.
story: Lakshmi's friend Divya said: "Lakshmi, un frontal enna pale-a irukku? Enna aachu?" Lakshmi touched her forehead. She had been crying every night because her Paati was in the hospital. "Naan Paati ku kashtama irukku nu bayama irukkum — adhanaala en forehead glow pochu!" Amma applied sandanam. "Cry panna koodum — aana over ah cry pannaadha."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Lakshmi, un frontal enna pale-a irukku?'
Lakshmi: 'Naan Paati ku kashtama irukku nu bayama irukkum — adhanaala glow pochu!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar gently places a hand of sandal paste on a stone, its surface cooling.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bayathin vilai — un mukam adhai kaattum. Aana bayam pogum — Paati ku nalla aagum."
thirukkural: முயங்கிய கைகளை ஊக்கப் பசந்தது பைந்தொடிப் பேதை நுதல்.
english_translation: The front of this fair one paled As my clasping arms loosed their hold.
moral: When someone we love is far or unwell, our body shows our worry — but it will pass.

title: குளிர்காற்று பிரித்தது
theme: Wasting Beauty / Cool Breeze Between Embrace, Eyes Paled
hook: Meera and Thaai sit together on a boat — a cold breeze separates them and Meera's eyes fill with tears.
story: On the boat, Meera sat close to Thaai. A cold breeze blew between them. "Thaai, intha kaatthu enna maadhiri enna ungalukkukkitt irundhu piritthudhu!" Thaai laughed. "Kaatthu pirikkaadhu — neenga close ah ukkandhaa pugundhudhu. Aana kaatthu varum podhu neenga enna maadhiri thin-a irukkiraano adhu kaattum." Meera held Thaai's hand tight.
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Thaai, intha kaatthu enna maadhiri enna ungalukkukkitt irundhu piritthudhu!'
Thaai: 'Kaatthu pirikkaadhu — neenga close ah ukkandhaa pugundhudhu. Neenga thin-a irukkiraano adhu kaattum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the edge of the boat, a gentle breeze ruffling his hair.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaatthu un bayathin vilai — aana neenga Thaai kitta irukkiraargal. Adhu dhan valimai."
thirukkural: முயக்கிடைத் தண்வளி போழப் பசப்புற்ற பேதை பெருமழைக் கண்.
english_translation: Cool breeze crept between our embrace Her large rain-cloud-eyes paled at once.
moral: The cold between us is just the breeze — love stays warm in our hearts.

title: நெற்றியின் பசப்பு
theme: Wasting Beauty / Pale Eyes, Pained Forehead
hook: Arjun's friend notices his forehead has lost its shine — Arjun realizes he's been worrying too much about Appa.
story: Arjun's teacher said: "Arjun, un mukam enna tired-a irukku? Enna aachu?" Arjun thought. Appa was away for three months. Every night Arjun stayed up worrying. "Teacher, naan Appa ah worry pannuren — adhanaala en mukam glow pochu." Appa called that night. "Kutty, naan varuven — nee nalla irukku nu sollu." Arjun smiled. The glow returned.
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Teacher, naan Appa ah worry pannuren — adhanaala en mukam glow pochu!'
Appa: 'Kutty, naan varuven — nee nalla irukku nu sollu. Enna worry pannadha.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside the botanical garden's oldest tree, its leaves shedding and regrowing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un mukam un bayathai kaattum — aana bayam pogum. Appa varuvaargal — un mukam marubadiyum magizhum."
thirukkural: கண்ணின் பசப்போ பருவரல் எய்தின்றே ஒண்ணுதல் செய்தது கண்டு.
english_translation: Pale eyes pained seeing the pallor Of the bright forehead of this fair.
moral: When we worry about someone we love, our face shows it — but their return brings back our glow.

title: நெஞ்சோடு பேச்சு
hook: Tharun sits on the beach talking to himself after his best friend moves to another city.
story: Tharun walked to the shore alone. The waves crashed like they always did, but everything felt different. His best friend Karthik had moved to Hyderabad. "Nenjama, nee enna panra?" Tharun whispered to himself. A fisherman overheard and laughed. "Kutty, nee un nenjoda pesura maadhiri irukku!" Tharun smiled. "Adhu dhan, uncle. Nan nenjoda pesikuren — adhu dhan enaku theerpu."
tamil_dialogues: Tharun: 'Nenjama, nee enna panra? Karthik illama nee valikira maadhiri irukku!'
Fisherman: 'Kutty, nee un nenjoda pesura maadhiri irukku!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the sand, drawing a heart shape in the wet shore.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee un nenjoda pesradhu — adhu dhan unmai. Un nenjuku unakaaga enna cheyya mudiyum nu ninaikira maadhiri, adhu dhan arivu."
thirukkural: நினைத்தொன்று சொல்லாயோ நெஞ்சே எனைத்தொன்றும் எவ்வநோய் தீர்க்கும் மருந்து.
english_translation: Think of, O heart, some remedy To cure this chronic malady
moral: Talking to your own heart is the first step to finding answers within.

title: பேதைமை நெஞ்சு
hook: Divya cries because her classmate ignores her — Akka teaches her about pining in vain.
story: Divya sat in the temple garden, tears rolling. Her classmate Anjali had stopped talking to her. Akka found her there. "Divya, enna achu?" Divya sobbed. "Anjali enna paakala akka!" Akka sat beside her. "Ava unakaaga feel panradha illa-nu nee thaan therinjukkala. Appo nee enna valikira? Adhu dhan begai." Divya wiped her eyes. "Nijama dhan akka."
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Akka, ava enna mukkiyamave illa-nu solli irukkanga!'
Akka: 'Ava unakaaga valikala-nu nee thaan therinjukkala. Appo nee enna valikira?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a peepal tree in the garden, a gentle breeze around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakkaaga valikathavar unakkaaga valikama irundha, nee enna valikira? Adhu dhan begai. Nijamaga therinjukkol."
thirukkural: காதல் அவரிலர் ஆகநீ நோவது பேதைமை வாழியென் நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: Bless O mind! you pine in vain For me he has no love serene
moral: Pining for someone who doesn't care about you is foolishness — recognize and move on.

title: மனசு ஏன் வாடுது?
hook: Rohan asks Paati why his heart hurts even though the one who hurt him doesn't care.
story: Rohan sat on the temple steps, holding his knee where he'd fallen during cricket. "Paati, naa valikiren, aana avan valikira maadhiri therila!" Paati nodded. "Nee valikiradhu un manasu. Avan valikiradha-nu nee enna therinjukka pora? Adhu dhan un periya kashtam." Rohan thought. "Appo naa epdi irukka venam Paati?" Paati smiled. "Un manasu un manasu — adha neeye kaapathu."
tamil_dialogues: Rohan: 'Paati, naa valikiren, aana avan valikira maadhiri therila!'
Paati: 'Nee valikiradhu un manasu. Avan valikiradha-nu nee enna therinjukka pora?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple steps, one hand on Rohan's shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un manasu unakaaga valikudhu. Aana adhai seidhavarukku athu theriyadhu. Appo nee dhan adhai kaapathu vendiyavan."
thirukkural: இருந்துள்ளி என்பரிதல் நெஞ்சே பரிந்துள்ளல் பைதல்நோய் செய்தார்கண் இல்.
english_translation: O mind, why pine and sit moody? Who made you so pale lacks pity
moral: The one who hurts you doesn't feel your pain — so why destroy yourself for them?

title: கண்கள் பசிக்குது
hook: Meena's father works in Gulf — she tells her eyes to go find him.
story: Every night Meena stared at the sky. "Amma, appa enga irukkar?" Amma said, "Gulf la kutty." Meena closed her eyes. "Kanuga, neenga pOi appava paaru-nu solla venam!" Amma laughed. "Kanuga pOumaa?" Meena said, "Kanuga pOadhaa-na, nenju pOum. En appava paakka venam!" Amma hugged her tight.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Amma, kanuga pOi appava paaru-nu solla venam!'
Amma: 'Kanuga pOumaa kutty?'
Meena: 'Kanuga pOadhaa-na, nenju pOum! En appava paakka venam!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the top of Rockfort, holding a lantern against the night sky.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un kanukku appava paakka aasai irukku. Adhu dhan un nijamaana anbu. Aasai irundha, ellaarum kooduvaanga."
thirukkural: கண்ணும் கொளச்சேறி நெஞ்சே இவையென்னைத் தின்னும் அவர்க்காணல் உற்று.
english_translation: Take these eyes and meet him, O heart Or their hunger will eat me out
moral: When you miss someone deeply, even your eyes ache with longing.

title: விடமாட்டேன்
theme: Soliloquy / Not Letting Go of the One Who Spurns
hook: Vicky's friend betrayed him — but he refuses to let go of the friendship.
story: Vicky walked the Yercaud hill road, kicking stones. His friend Kathir had spread a lie about him. Amma followed. "Vicky, avan unakku nalla panla-nu nee therinjukittaiye?" Vicky nodded. "Aana naan vida maaten Amma. En kooda vilayadina kaalam marakka mudiyadhu." Amma touched his head. "Un perasai dhan da nalla irukku. Adhai vidathey."
tamil_dialogues: Vicky: 'Amma, ava pannatha naa marakka maaten!'
Amma: 'Nee therinjukittaiye. Appo enna valikira?'
Vicky: 'Vida maaten Amma. En kooda vilayadina kaalam marakka mudiyadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the hill road, a gentle mist swirling around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Unakku theriyudhu — aana nee vida maattai. Adhu dhan un perasai. Perasai irundha, ellaarum thirumbi varuvaanga."
thirukkural: செற்றார் எனக்கை விடல்உண்டோ நெஞ்சேயாம் உற்றால் உறா அதவர்.
english_translation: He spurns our love and yet, O mind, Can we desert him as unkind?
moral: Even when someone rejects you, your heart can still choose to hold on with love.

title: பொய்க்காய்வு
hook: Kiran pretends to be angry at his sister — but runs to hug her the moment he sees her.
story: Kiran and Akka fought over the TV remote. "Naan un kooda pesa maaten!" Kiran slammed his room door. One hour passed. He peeked out. Akka was crying in the kitchen. Kiran's heart sank. He ran and hugged her. "Akka, sorry! Naan poi sonnen!" Akka laughed through tears. "Nee enna da — enna maadhiri dhan irukka?"
tamil_dialogues: Kiran: 'Akka, sorry! Naan poi sonnen!'
Akka: 'Nee enna da — enna maadhiri dhan irukka?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the river, watching the water flow peacefully.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kovama irukka poren-nu poyi solradhu, aana kanlae theriyudhu adhu poi-nu. Manasu adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: கலந்துணர்த்தும் காதலர்க் கண்டாற் புலந்துணராய் பொய்க்காய்வு காய்திஎன் நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: Wrath is false, O heart, face-to face Sans huff, you rush to his sweet embrace
moral: True love makes your anger melt the moment you see the person you love.

title: விடு விடு
hook: Sathya is torn between love and his family's honor — Thatha helps him choose.
story: Sathya liked a girl in school. But his family said, "Ippo padikka venam." He was stuck. "Thatha, enaku love-um venum, aana naanum nanna irukkanum!" Thatha smiled. "Kaatku love-a vidu, illa naanum-nu nenaikku. Rendum irukka mudiyadhu. Enna dhan mukkiyam-nu ninaikira?" Sathya paused. "Nan nanna irukkanum." Thatha nodded. "Appo adha choose pannu."
tamil_dialogues: Sathya: 'Thatha, enaku love-um venum, aana naanum nanna irukkanum!'
Thatha: 'Rendum irukka mudiyadhu. Enna dhan mukkiyam-nu ninaikira?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands under a temple arch, holding a small oil lamp.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaamam-um naanam-um rendum vitradhu mudiyadhu. Adhula enna mukkiyam-nu therinjukol — adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: காமம் விடுஒன்றோ நாண்விடு நன்னெஞ்சே யானோ பொறேன்இவ் விரண்டு.
english_translation: Off with love O mind, or shame I cannot endure both of them
moral: When torn between desire and duty, choose wisely — both cannot always be held.

title: பின்செல்லும் நெஞ்சம்
theme: Soliloquy / Following the One Who Left
hook: Ananya keeps following her cousin who moved away — until Amma explains it's her heart that follows.
story: Every weekend Ananya asked, "Amma,avinudiya address enna?" Amma would say, "Chennai, kutty." Ananya drew maps in her notebook. Amma watched. "Nee ava vaasathukku pOma?" Ananya said, "Illai Amma. Aana en nenju pOudhu." Amma held her hand. "Adhu dhan vaa — un nenjuye ava kitta pOudhu. Adhai vida unakku vera enna thevai?"
tamil_dialogues: Ananya: 'Amma, ava address enna? Naan pOkanum!'
Amma: 'Nee ava vaasathukku pOma? Illai — un nenju pOudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on Race Course Road, pointing at a bird flying across the sky.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nee pogalaam — aana un nenjuye pOudhu. Adhu dhan anbu. Anbu irundha, poi enna?"
thirukkural: பரிந்தவர் நல்காரென்று ஏங்கிப் பிரிந்தவர் பின்செல்வாய் பேதைஎன் நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: Without pity he would depart! You sigh and seek his favour, poor heart!
moral: Your heart follows the one you love, even when your feet cannot.

title: யாருக்காக?
theme: Soliloquy / Whom Do You Think Of?
hook: Ravi can't concentrate on studies — Paati asks him who he's really thinking about.
story: Ravi stared at his math book but saw only his friend's face. Paati noticed. "Ravi, nee padikira maadhiri illaiye — nee yaarai nenakira?" Ravi blushed. "Paati!" Paati laughed. "Adhu dhan un manasu. Yaarai nenakira adha neeye aala mudiyadhu. Adhai vida padippu mukkiyam." Ravi agreed. "Sari Paati, naa try pannuren."
tamil_dialogues: Ravi: 'Paati, naa padikkuren, aana en manasu vera engaey pOudhu!'
Paati: 'Nee yaarai nenakira? Adhai vida padippu mukkiyam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the rock, a book open on his lap.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un nenju yaarai nenakudho, adhu unakaaga dhan. Aana adhai vida mukkiyamana vishayam irukku — adhu un arivu."
thirukkural: உள்ளத்தார் காத லவரால் உள்ளிநீ யாருழைச் சேறியென் நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: The lover lives in Self you know; Whom you think, mind to whom you go?
moral: Your mind follows whom you love — but you must guide it towards what truly matters.

title: இழக்க மாட்டேன்
theme: Soliloquy / Not Losing More to the One Who Left
hook: Kavin's senior ignores him after years of friendship — Appa teaches him not to lose more.
story: Kavin's senior Arjun stopped talking after getting into a new college group. Kavin kept trying. Appa saw. "Kavin, avan nee illaama irukka mudinja, nee avan illaama irukka mudiyadhaa?" Kavin thought. "Appa, aana naan avana panikka maaten!" Appa said, "Panikka venamilla. Aana un kalvi, un neruppu — adhai vidaama iru. Adhu dhan un selvam."
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Appa, avan enna vituttaaru. Naan enna pannanum?'
Appa: 'Avan nee illaama irukka mudinja, nee avan illaama irukka mudiyadhaa?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the library entrance, holding an ancient palm-leaf manuscript.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Avan nee illaama irukka mudinjaa, nee avan illaama irukka mudiyadhaa. Un selvam un kalvi — adhai vidaama iru."
thirukkural: துன்னாத் துறந்தாரை நெஞ்சத்து உடையேமா இன்னும் இழத்தும் கவின்.
english_translation: Without a thought he deserted us To think of him will make us worse
moral: Don't lose your self-worth chasing someone who walked away without a thought.

title: நாணத்தின் கதவு
theme: Reserve Overcome / Passion Breaks the Door of Reserve
hook: Thulasi who never talks to boys accidentally blushes when one compliments her kolam.
story: Thulasi drew a big kolam at the temple entrance. A boy from school walked by. "Wow, Thulasi! Romba azhaga irukku!" She froze. Her face turned red. She ran inside. Akka laughed. "Enna achu? Oru paiyan sonnadhu!" Thulasi whispered, "Enakku ipdi dhan aagum akka!" Akka said, "Adhu dhan naanum solren — un nanaamaiyaaga irukkudhu, aana adhu vidum."
tamil_dialogues: Thulasi: 'Akka, naan apdi dhan aagum!'
Akka: 'Un nanaamaiyaaga irukkudhu, aana adhu vidum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the kolam, admiring the pattern with a gentle smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaamam vandha udane, nanaamai oLLikum. Adhu dhan unmai — nanaamai enbathu oru kathavu, adhai kaamam thirakkum."
thirukkural: காமக் கணிச்சி உடைக்கும் நிறையென்னும் நாணுத்தாழ் வீழ்த்த கதவு.
english_translation: Passion's axe shall break the door Of reserve bolted with my honour
moral: Even the strongest reserve can be overcome by genuine affection.

title: நள்ளிரவு ஆட்டம்
theme: Reserve Overcome / Lust Rules the Midnight Mind
hook: Meera can't sleep at night — her mind keeps thinking about her crush and she asks Amma why.
story: Meera tossed in bed. "Amma, en manasu night-la araama irukku!" Amma sat up. "Enna nenakira?" Meera whispered, "Adhu dhaan problem — en manasu araama irukkudhu." Amma held her hand. "Adhu dhan kaamam. Adhu raatiri varum, pagal-la pogum. Adhai bayappaadadha — adhu oru nilai. Adhai thandi poradhu dhan vellum."
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Amma, en manasu night-la araama irukkudhu!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan kaamam. Adhu raatiri varum, pagal-la pogum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple floor, the moonlight streaming through the pillars.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaamam enbathu oru nilai — adhu raatiri varum, pagal-la pogum. Adhai bayappaadadha, adhai thandi poradhu dhan vellum."
thirukkural: காமம் எனவொன்றோ கண்ணின்றென் நெஞ்சத்தை யாமத்தும் ஆளும் தொழில்.
english_translation: The thing called lust is a heartless power It sways my mind at midnight hour
moral: Desire is powerful but temporary — recognizing it is the first step to mastering it.

title: தும்மல் மாதிரி
theme: Reserve Overcome / Hidden Feelings Sneak Out
hook: Kiran blushes every time his crush's name is mentioned — like a sneeze he can't control.
story: Teacher said, "Next project — Kiran and Thamizh." Kiran's face turned red. He tried not to smile. Paati saw him at home. "Enna da, sirichu sirichu irukka?" Kiran said, "Paati, Thamizh-nu sollikkaanae sirikkanum!" Paati laughed. "Adhu dhan kaamam — thummaala varumaa-nu pOdhu, adhu udané vandhurum. Adhai thookki viduvadhilla."
tamil_dialogues: Kiran: 'Paati, Thamizh-nu sollikkaanae sirikkanum!'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan kaamam — thummaala varumaa-nu pOdhu, adhu udané vandhurum!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside a pillar, a knowing smile on his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaamam-a maraikka mudiyadhu — adhu thummaala varumaa-nu pOdhu, adhu udané vandhurum. Adhai accept pannu."
thirukkural: மறைப்பேன்மன் காமத்தை யானோ குறிப்பின்றித் தும்மல்போல் தோன்றி விடும்.
english_translation: How to hide this lust which shows Itself while I sneeze unawares!
moral: Feelings you try to hide will always find a way to show — just like a sneeze.

title: மறையிறந்த ரகசியம்
hook: Deepa writes secretly about her crush — Amma finds the diary and teaches her about hidden things coming to light.
story: Deepa hid her diary under her mattress. Amma found it while cleaning. She read one page. "Deepa!" Deepa ran. "Amma, athu en diary!" Amma didn't scold. "Un manasu unmaiyuma? Adhai mariyaadavaaga vaai, adhu dhan mukkiyam. Aana adhai maraikka mudiyadhu — un seyal dhan adhai solli vidum." Deepa nodded, embarrassed.
tamil_dialogues: Deepa: 'Amma, athu en diary!'
Amma: 'Un manasu unmaiyuma? Adhai mariyaadavaaga vaai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a park bench, a butterfly resting on his shoulder.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un nenjula irukkudho, adhu maraika mudiyadhu. Un seyal dhan adhai veli paadum. Mariyaadavaaga iru."
thirukkural: நிறையுடையேன் என்பேன்மன் யானோஎன் காமம் மறையிறந்து மன்று படும்.
english_translation: I was proud of my sex-reserve Lo lust betrays what I preserve
moral: What you hide inside will eventually show through your actions.

title: காம நோய்
theme: Reserve Overcome / Love-Sickness Knows No Dignity
hook: Vignesh sees a dignified teacher acting strangely after falling in love — and asks Appa why.
story: At the temple, Vignesh saw his teacher — usually calm and proud — pacing, looking at his phone. "Appa, sir enna panra?" Appa whispered, "Avanukku oru pullaiyai pidichirukku. Kaamam vandha, pudhumaiyaga aayiduvaanga." Vignesh was shocked. "Sirukkumaa Appa?" Appa smiled. "Sir-nu aalum illa. Kaamam varadhukku munnaadi, ellaarum samam."
tamil_dialogues: Vignesh: 'Appa, sir enna panra? Sirukkumaa?'
Appa: 'Sir-nu aalum illa. Kaamam varadhukku munnaadi, ellaarum samam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the pond's edge, the golden temple reflecting behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaamam vandha, periya aalum chinna aalum. Pudhumaiyaaga aayiduvaanga. Adhu dhan kaamam — adhai yaarum thappi mudiyadhu."
thirukkural: செற்றார்பின் செல்லாப் பெருந்தகைமை காமநோய் உற்றார் அறிவதொன்று அன்று.
english_translation: Dignity seeks not a deserter But Love-sick is its innovator
moral: Love makes even the most dignified people act differently — it's a universal human experience.

title: துயரத்தின் கேள்வி
theme: Reserve Overcome / Grief of the Forsaken
hook: Swathi cries because her friend left without reason — Akka helps her understand grief.
story: Swathi sat on the verandah, crying. Her friend Divya had gone abroad. "Akka, ava enakku enna pannanga-nu theriyama poitaanga!" Akka sat beside her. "Adhu dhan vaa — un thunaiyai vida poguthu, aana nee adhai bayappaada koodadhu. Un thunai unakku irukku — neeye un thunai." Swathi wiped tears. "Sari akka, naa try pannuren."
tamil_dialogues: Swathi: 'Akka, ava enakku enna pannanga-nu theriyama poitaanga!'
Akka: 'Un thunai unakku irukku — neeye un thunai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by a lamp post, the evening light soft around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un thunai pogudhu — aana nee adhai bayappaada koodadhu. Un thunai neeye, neeye un thunai. Adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: செற்றவர் பின்சேறல் வேண்டி அளித்தரோ எற்றென்னை உற்ற துயர்.
english_translation: O Grief, my deserter you seek Of your caprice what shall I speak!
moral: When someone leaves, the grief is yours to carry — but so is the strength to heal.

title: நாணம் மறந்த நேரம்
theme: Reserve Overcome / Shame Forgotten in Love
hook: Nithya who never talks loudly starts singing when her friend arrives — Amma notices shame disappears.
story: Nithya was the quietest girl in class. When her best friend Thamizh arrived from Chennai, she jumped and shouted, "Thamizh!" Amma watched, smiling. "Nee ipdi urakkama karaaga pesra?" Nithya blushed. "Amma, Thamizh vandha udane, enaku ethuvume illa aayiduchu!" Amma said, "Adhu dhan kaamam — naanamai enbadhu adhu vitradhu. Adhu dhan unmaiyaana anbu."
tamil_dialogues: Nithya: 'Amma, Thamizh vandha udane, enaku ethuvume illa aayiduchu!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan kaamam — naanamai enbadhu adhu vitradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the tank, water lilies blooming around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kaamam irundha, naanamai poyirum. Adhu dhan unmai — anbu irundha, ellaarum viduvaanga."
thirukkural: நாணென ஒன்றோ அறியலம் காமத்தால் பேணியார் பெட்ப செயின்.
english_translation: When lover's love does what it desires We forget all shame unawares
moral: True love makes you forget your shyness and express yourself freely.

title: பன்மாய கள்வன்
theme: Reserve Overcome / The Cheater Breaks Reserve
hook: Harini's friend pretends to be shy but is actually a smooth talker — Akka opens her eyes.
story: A boy in Harini's class acted shy. "Avan romba maanama irukkaan akka!" But Akka saw him charming another girl at the market. "Harini, avan maanam illa — avan oru kallan! Maadhiri maadhiri nadikkiraan." Harini was shocked. "Akka, avan enakku sonnadhu poiyo?" Akka nodded. "Adhu dhan — poi pesuravanai therinjukol. Adhu dhan un veli."
tamil_dialogues: Harini: 'Akka, avan romba maanama irukkaan!'
Akka: 'Avan maanam illa — avan oru kallan! Maadhiri maadhiri nadikkiraan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the market, watching the crowd with calm eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Poi pesuravan maadhiri maadhiri nadikkiraan. Adhai therinjukol — adhu dhan un veli. Poi solravarkku idam kodukkaadha."
thirukkural: பன்மாயக் கள்வன் பணிமொழி அன்றோநம் பெண்மை உடைக்கும் படை.
english_translation: The cheater of many wily arts His tempting words break through women's hearts
moral: Beware of those who use sweet words and multiple faces to break your reserve.

title: கலத்தல்
hook: Arjun storms off angry at Amma — but comes running back when he sees her crying.
story: Arjun shouted, "Naan pogaren!" and ran from the house. He sat under a tree. After ten minutes, he peeked home. Amma was at the door, crying. Arjun's heart broke. He ran and hugged her. "Amma, sorry! Naan poi sonnen!" Amma held him tight. "Un manasu enakku theriyum da — nee enna panra, adhu unma."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Amma, sorry! Naan poi sonnen!'
Amma: 'Un manasu enakku theriyum da — nee enna panra, adhu unmai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the temple entrance, a garland of jasmine in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kovama pOi, aana kanla therinjaa udané thirumbi varuvaai. Adhu dhan unmai — manasu dhan solli tharum."
thirukkural: புலப்பல் எனச்சென்றேன் புல்லினேன் நெஞ்சம் கலத்தல் உறுவது கண்டு.
english_translation: In huff I went and felt at ease Heat to heart in sweet embrace
moral: Even when anger takes you away, love pulls you back into a warm embrace.

title: நெருப்பில் கரையும்
theme: Reserve Overcome / Faking Dislike When You Love
hook: Tharani pretends she doesn't like a boy — Paati says it's like putting ghee in fire and pretending it won't melt.
story: Tharani told Paati, "Naan avanukku onnum feel pannala!" Paati smiled. "Adhu poi da. Neeye un manasula avan irukkan. Adhai vidu — adhu oru nilai. Neruppula naini potta, adhu uruki uruki pogum. Adhe maadhiri, anbu irundha, adhu bayangarama urukkum." Tharani blushed. "Paati, neenga evlo unmai solreenga!"
tamil_dialogues: Tharani: 'Paati, naan avanukku onnum feel pannala!'
Paati: 'Adhu poi da. Neeye un manasula avan irukkan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits inside the Big Temple, the massive lingam behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ninaiyiradhai maadhiri nadakkadhu. Anbu irundha, adhu unmaiyaaga urukkum. Adhai vidu — adhu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: நிணந்தீயில் இட்டன்ன நெஞ்சினார்க்கு உண்டோ புணர்ந்தூடி நிற்பேம் எனல்.
english_translation: To feign dislike is it not rare For mates who melt like fat in fire?
moral: You cannot pretend to dislike someone when your heart truly loves them.

title: நாள் எண்ணும் விரல்
theme: Longing for Reunion / Counting Days Since Departure
hook: Vennila's father went to work in another state — she counts days on her fingers by the lake.
story: Every evening Vennila sat by the lake. She counted on her fingers: "Oru naal, rendu naal, moonu naal..." Amma joined her. "Enna panra?" Vennila whispered, "Appa poitaal edho naal enna eduthu varaan-nu solli irundhaaru. Naan naal enna ennikiren." Amma held her hand. "Un viral thadiyum en kanum — rendum appava thudikkaradhu."
tamil_dialogues: Vennila: 'Amma, appa epdi varuvaaru? Naan naal enna ennikiren!'
Amma: 'Un viral thadiyum en kanum — rendum appava thudikkaradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the lake, the misty mountains behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un viral thadi naal enna ennikudhu — adhu dhan un aasai. Aasai irundha, appa thirumbi varuvaar."
thirukkural: வாளற்றுப் புற்கென்ற கண்ணும் அவர்சென்ற நாளொற்றித் தேய்ந்த விரல்.
english_translation: My eyes are dim lustre-bereft Worn fingers count days since he left
moral: The pain of separation is measured in days counted by lonely fingers.

title: அழகு மங்குது
theme: Longing for Reunion / Beauty Pales Without the Beloved
hook: Thamizh stops dressing up because her friend left — Akka teaches her beauty needs an audience.
story: Thamizh stopped wearing bangles. Her hair was messy. Akka noticed. "Thamizh, nee epdi irukka?" Thamizh said, "Akka, aval illaama enakku azhagu enna?" Akka smiled. "Azhagu unakku dhan irukku. Aval thirumbi vandhaa — nee azhaga irukkanum illa? Adhu dhan mukkiyam." Thamizh slowly put on her bangles.
tamil_dialogues: Thamizh: 'Akka, aval illaama enakku azhagu enna?'
Akka: 'Azhagu unakku dhan irukku. Aval thirumbi vandhaa — nee azhaga irukkanum illa?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the temple courtyard, a jasmine garland in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Azhagu unakku dhan irukku — aana adhu arasiyalaga irukkakoodadhu. Aval varumbodhu nee azhaga irukkanum."
thirukkural: இலங்கிழாய் இன்று மறப்பின்என் தோள்மேல் கலங்கழியும் காரிகை நீத்து.
english_translation: Beauty pales and my bracelets slide; Why not forget him now, bright maid?
moral: Don't let your beauty fade with grief — prepare for the joyful reunion.

title: துணை போல சென்றார்
theme: Longing for Reunion / Will as Guide, Living to See Again
hook: Surya's Appa left for a job abroad with a promise — Surya lives each day waiting.
story: On the Pamban Bridge, Appa waved goodbye. "Surya, naan varuven. Nee nalla iru." One year passed. Surya studied hard. "Appa, naan naalaikku varuven-nu sonnaaru. Naan wait pannuren." Amma said, "Avan unakku vandhu varuvaar. Nee avanukkaaga nalla iru." Surya nodded. "Amma, naan avan varaiyil nanna iruppen."
tamil_dialogues: Surya: 'Appa, neenga varuva-la? Naan wait pannuren!'
Amma: 'Avan unakku vandhu varuvaar. Nee avanukkaaga nalla iru.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the bridge, the sea stretching infinitely behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un nenju avan kooda pogudhu — aana nee inga irukka vendiyavan. Avan varum — nee nanna iru."
thirukkural: உரன்நசைஇ உள்ளம் துணையாகச் சென்றார் வரல்நசைஇ இன்னும் உளேன்.
english_translation: Will as guide he went to win Yet I live to see him again
moral: When someone dear leaves, living with hope is itself an act of courage.

title: இதயம் துள்ளுது
theme: Longing for Reunion / Heart Leaps at Return
hook: Meena's cousin is coming home after two years — her heart jumps every time she hears a vehicle.
story: "Paati, avanga varuvaangala?" Meena asked every hour. Paati laughed. "Ippo dhan solli irukkaanga — 2 manikku varuvaanga. Un nenju romba uchchiya irukku da!" Meena smiled. "Paati, en nenju — ippo dhan urakkama irukku!" The car honked outside. Meena ran. Paati shook her head. "Ippo therinjuchu — un nenju epdi irukkum."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Paati, avanga varuvaangala?'
Paati: 'Un nenju romba uchchiya irukku da!'
Meena: 'Paati, en nenju ippo dhan urakkama irukku!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the courtyard, a warm smile on his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un nenju thunbaaga irukkum — aana varuvadhukku munaadi, adhu romba uchchiya aagum. Adhu dhan anbu."
thirukkural: கூடிய காமம் பிரிந்தார் வரவுள்ளிக் கோடுகொ டேறுமென் நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: My heart in rapture heaves to see His return with love to embrace me
moral: The heart leaps with joy when the one you love is about to return.

title: கண்ணாரக் காண்பேன்
theme: Longing for Reunion / Just Let Me Gaze
hook: Vignesh prays every day to see his father — Amma says just seeing him will heal everything.
story: Vignesh prayed at the hilltop temple. "Amma, en appa mattum paarthen, podhum!" Amma smiled. "Adhu dhan un aasai. Appa varumbodhu, un kanlae paaru — adhu podhum. Un melaga enna thevai?" Vignesh waited. The day Appa came, Vignesh stared and stared. Amma laughed. "Ippo podhumaa?" Vignesh said, "Illai Amma!"
tamil_dialogues: Vignesh: 'Amma, en appa mattum paarthen, podhum!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan un aasai. Appa varumbodhu, un kanlae paaru — adhu podhum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the temple summit, the sky vast above him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Un kan avanai kaanum podhu, un melaga enna thevai? Adhu dhan anbu — oru kaatchi podhum."
thirukkural: காண்கமன் கொண்கனைக் கண்ணாரக் கண்டபின் நீங்கும்என் மென்தோள் பசப்பு.
english_translation: Let me but gaze and gaze my spouse sallow on my soft shoulders fades
moral: One loving gaze from the one you cherish can heal all pain.

title: ஒரு நாள் போதும்
theme: Longing for Reunion / One Day of Return Ends All Pain
hook: Kavitha's brother is studying far away — Thatha says one day with him will erase all pain.
story: Kavitha counted the days. "Thatha, avan 3 months-ku varuvaan!" Thatha smiled. "Adhu podhum da. Oru naal avan kooda irundha, un ella kashtamum poyirum." Kavitha doubted. "Oru naal-la?" Thatha nodded. "Ama — anbu oru naal-la ella kashtamum maraikum." When her brother came, Kavitha held his hand and cried. "Thatha sari dhan!"
tamil_dialogues: Kavitha: 'Thatha, avan 3 months-ku varuvaan!'
Thatha: 'Adhu podhum da. Oru naal avan kooda irundha, un ella kashtamum poyirum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the Cauvery, the setting sun painting the water gold.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru naal anbu kooda irundha, ella kashtamum maraikum. Adhu dhan anbin valimai."
thirukkural: வருகமன் கொண்கன் ஒருநாள் பருகுவன் பைதல்நோய் எல்லாம் கெட.
english_translation: Let my spouse return just a day Joy-drink shall drive my pain away
moral: One day with someone you love can heal months of loneliness.

title: திரும்பி வந்தா
theme: Longing for Reunion / What to Do When They Return
hook: Bharathi can't decide if she should scold or hug her brother when he returns from college.
story: "Paati, avan vandha enna panren?" Bharathi asked. "Kovava iruppen, illa sirippen-nu therila!" Paati laughed. "Rendum pannu da — kovuva, siru, katti pidichikko. Adhu dhan anbu."
