Bharathi waited. When Akka came, she first scolded, "Nee enna maadhiri irukka!" Then hugged her tight. Paati nodded.
tamil_dialogues: Bharathi: 'Paati, avan vandha enna panren? Kovava iruppen, illa sirippen?'
Paati: 'Rendum pannu da — kovuva, siru, katti pidichikko. Adhu dhan anbu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the Rockfort, a gentle smile playing on his lips.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kovuvaam, siruvaam — rendum pannalam. Adhu dhan unmaiyaana anbu. Oru mattum irukka koodadhu."
thirukkural: புலப்பேன்கொல் புல்லுவேன் கொல்லோ கலப்பேன்கொல் கண்அன்ன கேளிர் விரன்.
english_translation: If my eye-like lord returneth Shall I sulk or clasp or do both?
moral: When someone you love returns, it's okay to feel both angry and joyful — that's real love.

title: வெற்றி விருந்து
theme: Longing for Reunion / Victory and Feast Together
hook: Arjun wins a state-level quiz and wants to celebrate only when his father returns.
story: Arjun's team won the state quiz. "Amma, naan appa varumpodhu dhan kongu pannuven!" Amma smiled. "Adhu dhan da — victory-oda appa irukkumbodhu dhan full-a irukkum." Arjun practiced every day. When Appa came, they celebrated together. Appa said, "Un victory dhan en victory." Amma wiped a tear.
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Amma, naan appa varumpodhu dhan kongu pannuven!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da — victory-oda appa irukkumbodhu dhan full-a irukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple entrance, a garland in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Victory kidaichaa, kooda irukka vendiyavaroda irukka vendiyadhu. Adhu dhan perumai — periya anbu."
thirukkural: வினைகலந்து வென்றீக வேந்தன் மனைகலந்து மாலை அயர்கம் விருந்து.
english_translation: May the king fight and win and give And with my wife I will feast this eve!
moral: Victories are sweeter when shared with the ones you love.

title: ஏழு நாள் போல
theme: Longing for Reunion / One Day Seems Like Seven
hook: Tharun waits for his birthday when Appa promised to come — each day feels like a week.
story: "Amma, innu edho naal irukku!" Tharun said every morning. Amma smiled. "Un appa 7 naal-ku varuvaaru. Aana nee 7 naal-a irukka mudiyadhaa-nu paakuren!" Tharun waited. Each day felt like seven days. When Appa finally came, Tharun jumped and shouted. "Amma, ippo nalla irukku!" Amma laughed. "7 naal ozhungaa pOchu!"
tamil_dialogues: Tharun: 'Amma, innu edho naal irukku!'
Amma: 'Un appa 7 naal-ku varuvaaru. Aana nee 7 naal-a irukka mudiyadhaa-nu paakuren!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands on the hill, the morning mist around him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru naal ezhu naal-a theriyum — adhu dhan aasai. Aasai irundha, ella naalum nee ezhu naal-a nenaippai."
thirukkural: ஒருநாள் எழுநாள்போல் செல்லும்சேண் சென்றார் வருநாள்வைத்து ஏங்கு பவர்க்கு.
english_translation: One day seems as seven to those Who yearn return of distant spouse
moral: When you long for someone, time stretches — but love makes the wait worth it.

title: உடைந்த இதயம்
theme: Longing for Reunion / What Use is Meeting When the Heart is Broken
hook: Swathi's grandmother tells her that meeting someone won't fix a broken heart — only healing will.
story: Swathi cried every night. Her best friend had left without saying goodbye. "Paati, ava thirumbi vandhaa naanum avanum serndhu aadi paapom!" Paati shook her head. "Kutty, ava thirumbi vandhaalum, un manasu udainjaa irukkum — adhu un maadhiri irukkum. Adhai mudhal seeri vaai, adhu dhan mukkiyam." Swathi nodded slowly. "Sari Paati, naa try pannuren."
tamil_dialogues: Swathi: 'Paati, ava thirumbi vandhaa naanum avanum serndhu aadi paapom!'
Paati: 'Kutty, ava thirumbi vandhaalum, un manasu udainjaa irukkum — adhai mudhal seeri vaai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the temple courtyard, a single lamp burning beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manasu udainjaa irundha, avan vandhaalum enna? Manasai mudhal seeri vaai — adhu dhan unmai. Adhai vida mukkiyamana veli illa."
thirukkural: பெறின்என்னாம் பெற்றக்கால் என்னாம் உறினென்னாம் உள்ளம் உடைந்துக்கக் கால்.
english_translation: When her heart is broken, what is The good of meeting and love-embrace?
moral: Before meeting the one you love, first heal your own broken heart.

title: Kannin Unmai
theme: Reading the Signs / Hidden Feelings in Eyes
hook: Meera notices Akka's friend Varun always looks away when Akka passes by — but his eyes follow her even after she's gone.
story: Meera waited for the 27 bus with Akka. Varun stood nearby, pretending to read. Every time Akka moved, his eyes flicked up — just for a second. "Akka, Varun onnu paakuraan!" Akka blushed. "Illa da, poi paaru." But Meera saw it — his eyes stayed on Akka even after she walked ahead. Amma always said, "Kannukku poi solla mudiyadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Akka, Varun onnu paakuraan — aana solra illa!'
Akka: 'Adhu poi da. Avanuku enna irukku paakradhuku.'
Meera: 'Akka, kannu poi sollaadhu — nee poyappuramum paarudhu!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a bus stop bench, a child on each side, watching the road calmly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannellam unmaidaan solum. Orutharukku vera oruthar mele irukkira manasu, kannu maraichinaalum theriyum. Kannaal solra unmaiyai kekka koodadhu."
thirukkural: கரப்பினுங் கையிகந் தொல்லாநின் உண்கண் உரைக்கல் உறுவதொன் றுண்டு.
english_translation: You hide; but your painted eyes Restraint off, report your surmise.
moral: Eyes never lie — they speak what the heart hides.

title: Kaadhalin Azhagu
theme: Reading the Signs / Hidden Beauty Revealed
hook: Kavi's friend doesn't look like the "beautiful" girls in magazines — but Paati says real beauty is something else entirely.
story: Paati took Kavi to the temple. A girl helped an old man carry his basket. Her face lit up when she smiled at him. Kavi stared. Paati nudged him. "Paaru da — aval azhaga irukkaama irukkalam, aana avanukku panniya kai, anbu irukku. Athu dhan azhagu." Kavi thought about it all evening.
tamil_dialogues: Kavi: 'Paati, magazine la irukkura pullaingala vida azhaga irukku.'
Paati: 'Nalla irukkira kai, nalla irukkira manasu — athu dhan unmai azhagu.'
Kavi: 'Adhu enna Paati, magazine la kaanaadhadhu?'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan — kannalae kaanum, aana kitta vandhaal dhan theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple entrance, a small flower garland in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Azhagai aaraadhavan, athai kai vida maattaan. Azhagu mannukkullae irukkum, aana manasu therinjaal dhan theriyum."
thirukkural: கண்ணிறைந்த காரிகைக் காம்பேர்தோட் பேதைக்குப் பெண்நிறைந்த நீர்மை பெரிது.
english_translation: With seemly grace and stem-like arms The simple she has ample charms.
moral: True beauty lies in character and kindness, not just appearance.

title: Maniyin Ulagam
theme: Reading the Signs / Inner Brilliance Shines Through
hook: Aadhira sees a plain glass bangle among gold ones and asks why Amma picks it — Amma shows her how it glows in sunlight.
story: The shop was full of gold. Amma picked a glass bangle with a thin gold thread inside. "Amma, idhu enna special?" Amma held it up to the light. The thread inside caught the sun and blazed like fire. "Paaru — bahullya gold irukku, aana intha chinna thread dhan ellathayum mael adikkudhu." Aadhira wore it proudly. Everyone noticed the glow.
tamil_dialogues: Aadhira: 'Amma, indha bangle la enna irukku? Kanchipuram la intha alavukku?'
Amma: 'Ullae irukkura chinna thread dhan adha azhaga aakkudhu.'
Aadhira: 'Adhu enna, mani kulla irundha maadhiri?'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan — veliya thread aaga irukkum, aana ulle theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar holds the glass bangle up to the sunlight, the thread inside blazing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru mani azhaga irukkum — aana ullae irukkura thari dhan athai mael adikkudhu. Athu pola, oru aalukkum ullae irukkura guNam dhan athai azhaga aakkudhu."
thirukkural: மணியில் திகழ்தரு நூல்போல் மடந்தை அணியில் திகழ்வதொன்று உண்டு.
english_translation: Something shines through her jewelled charm Like thread shining through wreathed gem.
moral: Inner qualities shine brighter than outward decorations.

title: Poovin Scent
theme: Reading the Signs / Secrets in a Half-Smile
hook: Vignesh notices Deepa smiles differently when her favorite teacher comes — a half-smile that hides something sweet.
story: Deepa never fully smiled at anyone. But when Miss Kamala entered, a small corner of her mouth lifted. "Vignesh, ava enna maadhiri sirikkudhu?" His friend laughed. "Theriyadha? Ava Miss ku pudikkum — aana solra illa." Vignesh watched. The next day he saw the same half-smile when Miss Kamala praised Deepa's drawing. That was it — the secret inside the bud.
tamil_dialogues: Vignesh: 'Daa, ava enna half-la sirikkudhu?'
Friend: 'Theriyadha da? Athu poovula irukkura scent maadhiri — mela theriyadhu.'
Vignesh: 'Adhu enna, poova pattaamana?'
Friend: 'Adhu dhan — manasu poovula irukkum, aana veliya konjamae dhan theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under a mango tree in the school garden, a half-open flower in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruthar mele irukkira anbu, siripplae theriyum. Aana athai muzhukkaaga solradhu sulabam illa — adhu poovula irukkura scent maadhiri."
thirukkural: முகைமொக்குள் உள்ளது நாற்றம்போல் பேதை நகைமொக்குள் உள்ளதொன் றுண்டு.
english_translation: Like scent in bud secrets conceal In the bosom of her half smile.
moral: The sweetest feelings often hide behind the subtlest smiles.

title: Tholilirundha Marundhu
theme: Reading the Signs / Hidden Cure for Pain
hook: Arjun's friend Arvind has been silent for days — Chithi says his hidden worry has a cure, but you have to find it.
story: Arvind stopped eating. Stopped playing. Everyone worried. Arjun tried everything — cricket, bikes, jokes. Nothing. Then Chithi whispered, "Arjun, avanukku Amma vandhaa nu bayama irukku — athan avan oadudhu." Arjun told Arvind, "Da, nee bayapadadha — naa un Amma kitta pesuven." Arvind's eyes lit up. That night he ate two plates of rice.
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Arvind, enna da? Neenga enna thindra illa?'
Arvind: 'Enakku bayama irukku...'
Arjun: 'Adhai dhan Chithi sonna — tholil irukkura marundhu!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the hill steps, a small clay medicine pot in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruthanukku bayam enna nu therinjaa, athukkana marundhum kedaikum. Avan manasai arignar — adhu dhan valiya marundhu."
thirukkural: செறிதொடி செய்திறந்த கள்ளம் உறுதுயர் தீர்க்கும் மருந்தொன்று உடைத்து.
english_translation: The close-bangled belle's hidden thought Has a cure for my troubled heart.
moral: Understanding someone's hidden pain is the first step to healing it.

title: Migavum Nalla Per
theme: Reading the Signs / Over-Affection Feels Hollow
hook: Swathi's mother's boss praises everyone too much — but Swathi feels his hug is cold despite his warm words.
story: At the office party, the boss hugged everyone. "Swathi ma, nee romba azhaga irukka!" He squeezed tight. But his eyes were on his phone. "Amma, avan romba nallaa irukkar — aana avan manasu irukkudha?" Amma smiled sadly. "Illa da — solradhellam nalladhu dhan, aana manasu illa. Adhu dhan poi." Swathi hugged her Amma tight — that one felt real.
tamil_dialogues: Swathi: 'Amma, avan enna maadhiri hug pannraar?'
Amma: 'Azhaga irukkum — aana manasu illa.'
Swathi: 'Adhu enna Amati, solradhu nalladhu, aana manasu irukkala?'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan problem — migavum nallaa irukkira perukku innum sirai irukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the party entrance, watching the boss hug a child without looking at them.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Orutharukku migavum nalla per irundhaalum, avanukku manasu illai naa athu periya ilavu. Nalla seyal, nalla manasu — rendum irukka vendum."
thirukkural: பெரிதாற்றிப் பெட்பக் கலத்தல் அரிதாற்றி அன்பின்மை சூழ்வ துடைத்து.
english_translation: His over-kind close embrace sooths; But makes me feel, loveless, he parts.
moral: Excessive praise without genuine love is hollow and fleeting.

title: Valai Kaal
theme: Reading the Signs / Bracelets That Know First
hook: Kavya's grandmother's old bangles rattle before it rains — and Kavya wonders if they know something the sky doesn't.
story: Kavya noticed Thatha's bangles always rattled before rain. "Thatha, ungakku muzhukka theriyum!" Thatha smiled. "Indha valai kaal ku athuku aaga theriyum — mazhai varum pothu, avanga odambukku uravu varum." That evening, the bangles rattled again. Within minutes, the sky opened. Kavya hugged Thatha. "Ungal valai kaal kunju!"
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Thatha, mazhai varum pothu neenga bayapaduveengala?'
Thatha: 'Bayam enna, indha valai kaal ku athuku aaga theriyum — adhai vida naan bayapadaama irukkaen.'
Kavya: 'Adhu enna valai kaal, avangalukku manasu irukkuma?'
Thatha: 'Indha udambukku uravu varum pothu — valai kaal ku aaga theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the temple tank, a single bangle in his hand, watching the clouds gather.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru valai kaalukku muzhukka theriyum — aana avan manasu avanukkaga bayapadum. Nee avan maadhiri irukkanum — avan manasu un manasukkaga bayapada vendum."
thirukkural: தண்ணந் துறைவன் தணந்தமை நம்மினும் முன்னம் உணர்ந்த வளை.
english_translation: Quick, my bracelets read before The mind of my lord of cool shore.
moral: Love knows before the mind does — it senses what's coming.

title: Ezham Naal
theme: Reading the Signs / The Seventh Day of Separation
hook: Meena's cousin hasn't heard from her boyfriend in seven days — and her face has turned pale with worry.
story: Meena's cousin Priya stopped eating properly. Day 7, her face was pale. "Priya akka, nee enna maadhiri irukka?" Priya sighed. "En kadhali pogaal — ezham naal aachu." Meena brought her to the beach. The waves rolled in. "Akka, kadalukkum kadhali irukku — avungalukkum neenga irukkeenga." Priya smiled a little. The color came back slowly.
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Priya akka, nee mattum thindra illa — ezham naal aachu!'
Priya: 'Avan pogaal — en manasu veezhudhu.'
Meena: 'Akka, kadalu avanaalae irukku — neenga avanaalae irukkeenga. Aaga vandhuvaar.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the shoreline, the seventh wave crashing at his feet.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pirivu varum pothu, manasu maarum. Aana avan aaga vandhaal — athu dhan uzhaippu. Ezham naal, ezham naal — athu dhan kaathiruppadhu."
thirukkural: நெருநற்றுச் சென்றார்எம் காதலர் யாமும் எழுநாளேம் மேனி பசந்து.
english_translation: My lover parted but yesterday; With sallowness it is seventh day.
moral: Separation drains the color from life, but reunion restores it.

title: Aval Kaal
theme: Reading the Signs / The Woman's Secret Steps
hook: Kiran watches a girl look at her bangles, then her arms, then her feet — before walking toward someone she loves.
story: Near the tank, a girl stood alone. She looked at her bangles, touched her arms, then stared at her feet. Paati nudged Kiran. "Paaru da — avanukkaga nadakkira pullai, avanukkaga dhan ellathayum seira." The girl walked toward a boy waiting under a tree. Kiran understood — she was preparing herself, step by step.
tamil_dialogues: Kiran: 'Paati, ava enna da pannudhu?'
Paati: 'Avanukkaga nadakkira pullai — avanukkaga dhan ellathayum paarkkira.'
Kiran: 'Adhu enna, avanukkaga dhan?'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan — oru pulli orutharukkaga ellathayum seira.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the tank steps, watching the girl walk slowly.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oru pulli orutharukkaga ellathayum seira — valai kaal paarkkira, kaai paarkkira, kaal paarkkira. Athu dhan azhagu."
thirukkural: தொடிநோக்கி மென்தோளும் நோக்கி அடிநோக்கி அஃதாண் டவள்செய் தது.
english_translation: She views her armlets, her tender arms And then her feet; these are her norms.
moral: Love makes one careful and thoughtful in every small detail.

title: Kannil sollum Kadhal
theme: Reading the Signs / Eyes Speak the Language of Love
hook: Arjun sees a girl silently ask her lover to come using only her eyes — and realizes love doesn't always need words.
story: Arjun walked along Rock Beach with Amma. A girl sat alone, looking at the waves. A boy approached. She didn't move. Didn't speak. But her eyes — they softened, widened, pleaded. The boy sat beside her. "Amma, avanga onnum solala, aana kannu dhan sonnathu!" Amma laughed softly. "Adhu dhan pulli — pulli dhan solradhu. Kaanalae puriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Amma, avanga enna sonnanga?'
Amma: 'Onnum solala — kannu dhan sonnathu.'
Arjun: 'Kannu dhan? Adhu enna, kaanalae theriyum?'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan pulli — pulli enna nu kannu dhan sollum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the edge of Rock Beach, a couple sitting peacefully behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pulli enna nu kannu dhan sollum. Kaanalae puriyum. Athai yaarum poi solla maattaanga — aana kaanalae unmai."
thirukkural: பெண்ணினால் பெண்மை உடைத்தென்ப கண்ணினால் காமநோய் சொல்லி இரவு.
english_translation: To express love-pangs by eyes and pray Is womanhood's womanly way.
moral: The eyes are the truest messengers of the heart.

title: Kallum Kadhalum
theme: Desire for Reunion / Love Is Not Wine
hook: Thatha says love is like wine — Kavya disagrees until she sees the difference for herself.
story: Thatha drank a small cup of arrack. "Kavya, kadhalum kallum ore maadhiri." Kavya frowned. "Illa Thatha — kallu manasai maraikkum, aana kadhal manasai thiruppidum." Thatha paused. "Sari da — nee correct." That evening, Kavya's mother called — her father was coming home after three months. Kavya's heart leaped. "Paaru Thatha — kadhalukku kallu venama?"
tamil_dialogues: Thatha: 'Kavya, kadhalum kallum ore maadhiri — rendum manasai maarappidum.'
Kavya: 'Illa Thatha — kallu kudichaa manasu maarum, aana kadhal kaanalae manasu thiruppidum.'
Thatha: 'Unakku periya aarivu! Athu sari.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits by the river, a small cup in his hand, but his eyes are on the horizon.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kallu manasai maraikkum. Aana kadhal manasai thiruppidum — avan kaana maatrathukkaga. Adhu dhan velai."
thirukkural: உள்ளக் களித்தலும் காண மகிழ்தலும் கள்ளுக்கில் காமத்திற் குண்டு.
english_translation: Rapture at thought and joy when seen Belong to love and not to wine.
moral: Wine dulls the mind; love makes it bloom with joy.

title: Panaiyum Thinaiyum
theme: Desire for Reunion / Don't Sulk When Love Grows
hook: Rohan sulks because Amma is busy — Amma compares his sulking to a tiny millet plant next to a tall palm tree.
story: Rohan wanted Amma to play. She was cooking. He sulked. "Amma, neenga enakku illa!" Amma wiped her hands. "Rohan, un aasai panai mara maadhiri valandhudhu — aana nee thinai maadhiri kochchapadra." Rohan didn't understand. "Adhu enna Amma?" Amma pointed to the farm. "Panai mara valarnthaalum, nee kochchapadradhu thinai maadhiri chinnadhu. Aaga vandhu vilayadalaam."
tamil_dialogues: Rohan: 'Amma, nee enakku illa! Nee vera yaarukkaaga pannura?'
Amma: 'Panai mara valarnthaalum, un aasai chinna thinai maadhiri irukkum. Kochchapadadha.'
Rohan: 'Adhu enna Amma, panaiyum thinaiyum?'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan — aasai panai maadhiri valarum, kochchu thinai maadhiri chinnadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between a tall palm tree and a tiny millet plant, his hands raised gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Aasai panai mara maadhiri valarnthaalum, kochchu thinai maadhiri irukka koodadhu. Adhai vida — avanukkaga vandhu, avanoda vilayadradhu dhan."
thirukkural: தினைத்துணையும் ஊடாமை வேண்டும் பனைத்துணையும் காமம் நிறைய வரின்.
english_translation: When passion grows palmyra-tall Sulking is wrong though millet-small.
moral: Don't let small jealousies grow when love is becoming big.

title: Kaadhalin Kaatchi
theme: Desire for Reunion / Eyes That Won't Look Away
hook: Meera's cousin refuses to look at the boy who hurt her — but her eyes keep searching for him anyway.
story: Akka said, "Naan avana kaanamaatten!" But Meera saw — her eyes kept darting to the road. "Akka, nee solradhu poi da! Kaanamatten nu solra, aana kannu avanai paakkudhu." Akka blushed angrily. "Illa! Naan kaanala!" Meera smiled. "Kannu poi sollaadhu Akka — adhu avanukkaga dhan nadakkudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Akka: 'Naan avana kaanamaatten!'
Meera: 'Akka, kannu avana paakkudhu — nee solradhu poi da!'
Akka: 'Illada! Kaanala!'
Meera: 'Kannu poi sollaadhu — adhu avanukkaga dhan nadakkudhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the top of the Rockfort steps, watching a girl look away while her eyes search.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu poi solla maattaadhu. Orutharukkaga manasu irundhaalum, kannu avanai thedi paarkkum. Adhu dhan kadhal."
thirukkural: பேணாது பெட்பவே செய்யினும் கொண்கனைக் காணா தமையல கண்.
english_translation: Though slighting me he acts his will My restless eyes would see him still.
moral: Even when hurt, the heart still searches for the one it loves.

title: Oodhalum Koodalum
theme: Desire for Reunion / Sulking and Longing to Reunite
hook: Arjun's friend is fighting with his girlfriend — Arjun watches her walk away, then immediately turn back.
story: Arjun and Appa walked on the beach. A girl stomped away from a boy. "Avanukku naan onnum illa!" But before she reached the gate, she turned. The boy was still standing there. She ran back. "Ippo vaa! Naan oodhal pannitten — aana vandhu vaa!" The boy smiled. Appa laughed. "Arjun, athu dhan pulli — oodhal pannum, aana koodalukkaga thirumbum."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Appa, ava poyirukku!'
Appa: 'Illa da — paar paar, thirumbudhu!'
Arjun: 'Adhu enna Appa, oodhalum koodalum?'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — kochchu pannum, aana adukkullae koodalum irukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the turning point on the beach path, one foot forward, one foot back.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oodhal pannumbodhu manasu poi sollum — aana kaadhal adhai vida valimai irukkum. Adha vida valimai — koodalukkaga thirumburadhu."
thirukkural: ஊடற்கண் சென்றேன்மன் தோழி அதுமறந்து கூடற்கண் சென்றது என் னெஞ்சு.
english_translation: Huff I would, maid, but I forget; And leap to embrace him direct.
moral: The heart's desire to be together always wins over the mind's desire to stay apart.

title: Kollai Kaanadha Kannu
theme: Desire for Reunion / Eyes That Don't See Flaws
hook: Kavi notices Paati never sees the flaws in Appa's old shirt — and asks why.
story: Appa's shirt had a big tear. Paati stitched it and wore it. "Paati, athu Appa shirt dhan — athuku enna special?" Paati smiled. "Naan paakradhu dhan illa — athu avan shirt nu dhan theriyum." Kavi stared. "Adhu enna Paati, kollai illama kaanradha?" Paati laughed. "Adhu dhan — kannu kaanum, aana kollai illama."
tamil_dialogues: Kavi: 'Paati, athu Appa shirt — kollai irukku!'
Paati: 'Naan athai kaanala — athu avan shirt nu dhan theriyum.'
Kavi: 'Adhu enna Paati, kollai kaanadha kannu?'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan — kannu nalladhu mattum dhan kaanum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the silk shop, a torn but neatly stitched shirt in his hands.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kannu nalladhu mattum dhan kaanum. Athu dhan kadhal — avan kollai enna enna nu paakkaadhu, avan azhagai mattum dhan kaanum."
thirukkural: எழுதுங்கால் கோல்காணாக் கண்ணேபோல் கொண்கன் பழிகாணேன் கண்ட இடத்து.
english_translation: When close I see not lord's blemish Like eyes that see not painter's brush.
moral: Love's eyes see only the beauty in the beloved, never the flaws.

title: Kaanum Podhu Illai
theme: Desire for Reunion / Together — No Faults; Apart — Only Faults
hook: Meena's parents argue when apart but are perfect together at the waterfall — Meena notices the difference.
story: At home, Appa and Amma argued. "Nee dhan thappu!" "Illai, nee!" But at Courtallam, holding hands in the waterfall, they were silent, smiling. "Amma, neenga rendu perum kootiya irukkumbodhu enna maadhiri irukkeenga?" Amma laughed. "Avan kitta irukkumbodhu, avan kollai enna nu kaanadhu. Pogaal pozhudhu dhan ellam theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Amma, neenga veetla enna fight pannringa?'
Amma: 'Avan kitta irukkumbodhu, avan kollai enna nu kaanadhu.'
Meena: 'Adhu enna Amma, kaanum podhu illai?'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan — kaanum pothu kollai illai, pogaal pozhudhu dhan kollai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the shallow waterfall, water flowing past his ankles.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruthar kitta irukkumbodhu, avan kollai enna nu kaanadhu. Pogaal pozhudhu dhan kollai theriyum. Adhu dhan kadhal."
thirukkural: காணுங்கால் காணேன் தவறாய காணாக்கால் காணேன் தவறல் லவை.
english_translation: When he's with me I see not fault And nought but fault when he is not.
moral: Presence brings understanding; absence brings misunderstanding.

title: Oodhalin Vilayaadu
theme: Desire for Reunion / Feigning a Sulk Like Jumping in a Stream
hook: Vicky's friend jumps into a dangerous current to show off — Bharath says love is like that, you know the danger but still dive in.
story: Bharath jumped into the fast stream near the dam. "Vicky, paaru — naan kallu aaga odiruven!" He surfaced, laughing. Vicky's heart raced. "Da, nee bayamaata? Kallu aaga poiruvan!" Bharath grinned. "Adhu dhan — oodhal pannum podhukkum, bayam irukkum. Aana athaiyum thandi, koodalukkaga odiruvan."
tamil_dialogues: Vicky: 'Da, nee bayamaata? Kallu aaga poiruvan!'
Bharath: 'Bayamaatten — aana athu enna, koodalukkaga bayapada koodadha?'
Vicky: 'Adhu enna, oodhalin vilayaadu?'
Bharath: 'Adhu dhan — bayam irukkum, aana koodalukkaga odiruvom.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the stream's edge, watching the boy surface safely.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Bayam irukkum — aana koodalukkaga bayam edukka koodadhu. Oodhal pannumbodhu koodalukkaga thirumburadhu dhan."
thirukkural: உய்த்தல் அறிந்து புனல்பாய் பவரேபோல் பொய்த்தல் அறிந்தென் புலந்து.
english_translation: To leap in stream which carries off When lord is close to feign a huff.
moral: Sometimes you know the risk but still dive in — for love.

title: Kallin Suvai
theme: Desire for Reunion / Love's Intoxicating Embrace
hook: Arjun's Paati compares love's pull to the taste of toddy — sweet, then bitter, then impossible to resist.
story: Under the palace garden tree, Paati told a story. "Arjun, oru kalathula pulliinga kallu saappiduvaanga — aana kadhal kudichaa, athu vera maadhiri irukkum." Arjun laughed. "Paati, neengalum kadhal pannirundheengala?" Paati smiled. "Pannala — aana oru naal, avan kitta irundha, naan kooda aaga poitten. Adhu dhan kadhal — kallin suvai."
tamil_dialogues: Paati: 'Arjun, kadhal kallu kudichaa maadhiri irukkum.'
Arjun: 'Paati, neengalum kadhal pannirundheengala?'
Paati: 'Pannala — aana oru naal, avan kitta irundha, naan kooda aaga poitten.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the garden tree, a small cup of toddy beside him, smiling gently.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kadhal kallu kudichaa maadhiri irukkum — adhu maraikka mudiyadhu. Oru naal, avan kitta irundhaalum, athai vida valimai irukkum."
thirukkural: இளித்தக்க இன்னா செயினும் களித்தார்க்குக் கள்ளற்றே கள்வநின் மார்பு.
english_translation: Like wine to addicts that does disgrace Your breast, O thief, is for my embrace!
moral: Love is intoxicating — once you taste it, nothing else compares.

title: Malarai vida Mel
theme: Desire for Reunion / Love Is Softer Than a Flower
hook: Aadhira picks a rose and crushes it — Amma says love is even more delicate than that.
story: At the rose garden, Aadhira picked a rose. The petals fell. "Amma, intha malar enna maadhiri irukku?" Amma held another. "Paaru — ithu malarukkum mel azhaga irukku. Aana malarai pinchinaal, athu kuzhivayidum." Aadhira thought. "Adhu enna Amma, kadhalaa?" Amma smiled. "Adhu dhan — kadhal malarai vida mel azhaga irukkum, aana pinchinaal, kuzhivayidum."
tamil_dialogues: Aadhira: 'Amma, indha malar enna maadhiri irukku?'
Amma: 'Ithu malarukkum mel azhaga irukkum — aana pinchinaal, kuzhivayidum.'
Aadhira: 'Adhu enna Amati, kadhalaa?'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan — kadhal malarai vida mel azhaga irukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the rose garden, a single petal balanced on his open palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kadhal malarai vida mel azhaga irukkum — aana adhai handle panna therinjaal dhan azhaga irukkum. Adhai pinchinaal, adhu kuzhivayidum."
thirukkural: மலரினும் மெல்லிது காமம் சிலர்அதன் செவ்வி தலைப்படு வார்.
english_translation: Flower-soft is love; a few alone Know its delicacy so fine.
moral: Love is the most delicate thing in the world — handle with care.

title: Poozhiyin Vilayattu
theme: Desire for Reunion / She Pretended to Resist — Then Rushed
hook: Akka says she won't go to the concert — then runs ahead of everyone when the music starts.
story: "Naan vara maatten!" Akka said about the concert. She sulked all morning. But the music started. A single flute note floated. Akka's feet moved. She ran. "Akka, nee onnum illai nu solliya? Enna da nee?" Akka laughed. "Naa oodhal pannitten — aana kootalaam nu irukku!"
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Akka, nee onnum illai nu solliya!'
Akka: 'Naa oodhal pannitten — aana kootalaam nu irukku!'
Meera: 'Adhu enna Akka, poozhiyin vilayattu?'
Akka: 'Adhu dhan — oodhal pannum, aana adhu vilayattu dhan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the corner where the girl turned, a flute in his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oodhal pannum — aana adhu vilayattu dhan. Kootalaam nu irukkumbodhu, oodhal ellam maaridum."
thirukkural: கண்ணின் துனித்தே கலங்கினாள் புல்லுதல் என்னினும் தான்விதுப் புற்று.
english_translation: She feigned dislike awhile but flew Faster for embrace than I do.
moral: Pretended resistance often hides the deepest desire to be together.

title: En Nenju
theme: Complaint to One's Own Heart / Why Don't I Control Myself?
hook: Arjun sees a boy following a girl who clearly doesn't like him — and asks Appa why people lose control.
story: At Marina Beach, a boy kept following a girl. She ignored him. He kept coming. Appa shook his head. "Avanukku theriyum aval pidikkala — aana avan nenju avanukku onnum illa." Arjun laughed. "Adhu enna Appa, avan nenju avanukku sollaadha?" Appa said, "Adhu dhan — oruthanukku avanudaya nenju adhimaiyaa irukkum, oruthanukku adhimai illa."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Appa, avan avalukku pidikkala nu theriyudhu — aana avan pinjar!'
Appa: 'Avan nenju avanukku onnum illa da — adhu dhan problem.'
Arjun: 'Adhu enna Appa, nenju adhimaiyaa irukkum?'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — oruthanukku nenju adhimai, orutharukku adhimai illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar walks along Marina Beach, the sea breeze in his hair, watching the boy from afar.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruthanukku avan nenju adhimaiyaa irukkum — athu nee aaraaya padadhu. Nee avanukkaga pona, unnudaya nenju unukku onnum illa."
thirukkural: அவர்நெஞ்சு அவர்க்காதல் கண்டும் எவன்நெஞ்சே நீஎமக்கு ஆகா தது.
english_translation: You see, his heart is his alone; Why not my heart be all my own?
moral: Why does our own heart not obey us when we need it most?

title: Poi Nenju
theme: Complaint to One's Own Heart / You Clasp Him Despite His Coldness
hook: Akka keeps going back to someone who ignores her — Meera asks why her heart doesn't listen.
story: Akka walked to the market every evening, hoping to see Varun. He never looked. "Akka, avan nee paakaraan nu theriyudha? Illa! Nee enna pannura? Adimaiya pogo!" Akka's eyes filled. "Theriyum da — aana nenju maatradhu." Meera held her hand. "Akka, nee unnudaya nenjukku solra — aana athu kekka maattaadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Akka, avan nee paakaraan nu theriyudha? Illa! Enna pannura?'
Akka: 'Theriyum da — aana nenju maatradhu.'
Meera: 'Adhu enna Akka, nenju poi claspadha?'
Akka: 'Adhu dhan — nenju poi solradhu, aana manasu adhai vida valimai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a temple step, a girl's silhouette reflected in a shop window beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nenju poi solradhu — avan nee illa nu solrum. Aana manasu adhai vida valimai irukkum — athai vida valimai dhan adhimai."
thirukkural: உறாஅ தவர்க்கண்ட கண்ணும் அவரைச் செறாஅரெனச் சேறியென் நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: O heart, you see how he slights me Yet you clasp him as if friendly.
moral: The heart clings even when the mind knows it's futile.

title: Nenju Podradhu
theme: Complaint to One's Own Heart / You Follow Him at Will
hook: Kavya's friend follows the boy who rejected her — Kavya asks Paati why some hearts just won't learn.
story: Divya walked past the boy's house every day. He never came out. "Paati, ava enna da pannudhu? Avan pidikkala nu sonnaatum, aval pogo!" Paati smiled sadly. "Nenjukku theriyum — aana athu onnum illa nu solradhu. Adhu dhan adhimai." Kavya thought. "Paati, naan adhu panna maatten!" Paati nodded. "Adhu dhan unmai."
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Paati, ava enna da pannudhu? Avan pidikkala nu sonnaatum!'
Paati: 'Nenjukku theriyum — aana athu poi sollum.'
Kavya: 'Adhu enna Paati, nenju adhimaiyaa irukkum?'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan — nenju onnum illa nu solrum, aana pinjarum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the river ghat, watching the water flow in one direction.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Nenjukku onnum illa nu sollum — aana athu poi. Adhai vida valimai — unnudaya nenju unnukkaga irukkum."
thirukkural: கெட்டார்க்கு நட்டார்இல் என்பதோ நெஞ்சேநீ பெட்டாங்கு அவர்பின் செலல்.
english_translation: You follow him at will Is it "The fallen have no friends" my heart?
moral: Don't let your heart follow someone who has already let you fall.

title: Oodhalum Adimaiyum
theme: Complaint to One's Own Heart / You Won't Sulk First Then Submit
hook: Arjun's friend says he'll never talk to the boy who ignored him — but by evening, he's begging for forgiveness.
story: Appa and Arjun walked the temple corridor. A boy told his friend, "Naan poi solli, then aaga poitten!" But two hours later, he was back, begging. Appa laughed. "Paaru da — oodhal panna mudiyadhu, aana adhimai irukkum." Arjun asked, "Adhu enna Appa?" Appa said, "Nee oodhal pannu — aana pinnaadi, adhimai varum."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Appa, avan onnum solli aaga poitten nu sonna — ippo pogo poraan!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — oodhal panna mudiyadhu, aana adhimai varum.'
Arjun: 'Adhu enna Appa, oodhalum adhimaiyum?'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — oodhal pannum, aana pinnaadi adhimai varum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the end of the temple corridor, watching the boy return.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oodhal pannu — aana pinnaadi adhimai varum. Adhai vida valimai — oodhal pannumbodhukkum adhimai irukkum."
thirukkural: இனிஅன்ன நின்னொடு சூழ்வார்யார் நெஞ்சே துனிசெய்து துவ்வாய்காண் மற்று.
english_translation: You won't sulk first and then submit Who will then consult you, my heart?
moral: Pride creates the sulking, but love demands submission.

title: Padaivum Bayamum
theme: Complaint to One's Own Heart / Fear of Gaining and Losing
hook: Meera's friend got a puppy — then spent every day afraid it would run away. Meera asks Amma why love makes us scared.
story: Amma brought home a puppy. Meera named it Chinna. Every day, she feared Chinna would run. "Amma, Chinna poirumaa?" Amma smiled. "Unakku Chinna kedaiyavum bayama irukku, pogaavum bayama irukku — adhu dhan kadhal." Meera held Chinna tight. "Adhu enna Amma, padaivum bayamum?" Amma said, "Adhu dhan — kadhalukku kedaiyaavum bayam, pogaavum bayam."
tamil_dialogues: Meena: 'Amma, Chinna poirumaa? Enakku bayama irukku!'
Amma: 'Unakku Chinna kedaiyavum bayama irukku, pogaavum bayama irukku — adhu dhan kadhal.'
Meera: 'Adhu enna Amma, padaivum bayamum?'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan — kadhalukku kedaiyaavum bayam, pogaavum bayam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits with the puppy on his lap, both watching the sunset.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Kadhalukku kedaiyaavum bayam, pogaavum bayam. Adhu dhan — adhai vida valimai irukkum, aana adhai vida valimai bayam irukkum."
thirukkural: பெறாஅமை அஞ்சும் பெறின்பிரிவு அஞ்சும் அறாஅ இடும்பைத்தென் நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: Frets to gain and fears loss in gain O my heart suffers ceaseless pain.
moral: Love brings both the fear of not having and the fear of losing.

title: Orendru Irukkumbodhu
theme: Complaint to One's Own Heart / Alone, the Mind Eats You
hook: Arjun sits alone at the meditation center and feels his mind "eating" him — Appa says it's normal.
story: At Matrimandir, everyone meditated. Arjun sat alone. His mind kept replaying the fight with Varun. "Appa, en manasu enna pannudhu? Adhu enna maadhiri aattudhu!" Appa smiled. "Nee orendru irukkumbodhu, manasu unna saapudhu — athu dhan kadhal." Arjun stared. "Adhu enna Appa, manasu saapudhaa?" Appa said, "Adhu dhan — orendru irukkumbodhu, manasu unna pizhaikkum."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Appa, en manasu enna pannudhu? Adhu enna maadhiri aattudhu!'
Appa: 'Nee orendru irukkumbodhu, manasu unna saapudhu — athu dhan kadhal.'
Arjun: 'Adhu enna Appa, manasu saapudhaa?'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — orendru irukkumbodhu, manasu unna pizhaikkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits alone at the Matrimandir, the golden structure glowing behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Orendru irukkumbodhu, manasu unnai saapudhu — athu kadhal dhan. Adhai vida valimai — unnudaya manasu unnai aattum."
thirukkural: தனியே இருந்து நினைத்தக்கால் என்னைத் தினிய இருந்ததென் நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: My itching mind eats me anon As I muse on him all alone.
moral: When alone, the mind turns love into a sweet torment.

title: Naanum Mariyen
theme: Complaint to One's Own Heart / I Forget Shame But Not His Thought
hook: Akka forgets all her shyness when Varun is nearby — Meera teases her about it.
story: Akka never talked to boys. Never looked. Never smiled. Then Varun arrived. Akka walked up to him. "Hi!" Meera's jaw dropped. "Akka! Neengale! Naanum mariyen nu sonneenga!" Akka blushed. "Adhu enna — naan avan kitta ponadhum, naanum mariyen nu sonnen. Aana avan kitta poondadhu — adhu dhan."
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Akka! Neengale! Naanum mariyen nu sonneenga!'
Akka: 'Adhu enna — naan avan kitta ponadhum, naanum mariyen nu sonnen.'
Meera: 'Adhu enna Akka, naanum mariyen?'
Akka: 'Adhu dhan — naanum mariyen nu solren, aana avan kitta poga maatten!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a hill town wall, watching the girl walk boldly toward the boy.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Naanum mariyen nu solrum — aana avan kitta pogaadhu. Adhai vida valimai — mariyen nu solradhu, aana manasu adhai vida valimai irukkum."
thirukkural: நாணும் மறந்தேன் அவர்மறக் கல்லாஎன் மாணா மடநெஞ்சிற் பட்டு.
english_translation: I forget shame but not his thought In mean foolish mind I'm caught.
moral: Love can make you forget your shyness, but you never forget the beloved.

title: Ellinaalume Ullam
theme: Complaint to One's Own Heart / Heart Ignores Blame, Hails Glory
hook: Vicky's friend got a bad name in school but keeps praising the boy who gave him that name — Vicky asks Paati why.
story: Kiran got a nickname — "Donkey Kiran." Everyone laughed. But he still followed the boy who gave it. "Paati, avan enna maadhiri solraan — aana Kiran adhai vida valimai solraan!" Paati smiled. "Adhu dhan kadhal — orutharukku ellam solralaam, aana avan azhagai mattum solrum." Vicky thought. "Adhu enna Paati, ellinaalume ullam?"
tamil_dialogues: Vicky: 'Paati, avan enna maadhiri solraan — aana Kiran adhai vida valimai solraan!'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan kadhal — orutharukku ellam solralaam, aana avan azhagai mattum solrum.'
Vicky: 'Adhu enna Paati, ellinaalume ullam?'
Paati: 'Adhu dhan — orutharai azhaga nu solradhu, adhai vida valimai irukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the temple entrance, a garland of flowers around his neck, watching the boy praise his friend.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Orutharai azhaga nu solradhu — adhai vida valimai irukkum. Adhai vida valimai — orutharai pizhaikkiraalum, avan azhagai mattum solrum."
thirukkural: எள்ளின் இளிவாம்என்று எண்ணி அவர்திறம் உள்ளும் உயிர்க்காதல் நெஞ்சு.
english_translation: My heart living in love of him Hails his glory ignoring blame.
moral: A loving heart ignores blame and celebrates the beloved's goodness.

title: Thunai Yaar?
theme: Complaint to One's Own Heart / Who Is Your Support in Grief?
hook: Meera cries because no one understands her problem — Amma asks a simple question that changes everything.
story: Meera cried. "Yaarum enakku thunai illa!" Amma sat beside her. "Meera, un nenjukkaga thunai yaar?" Meera stared. "Adhu enna Amma?" Amma said, "Nee avanukkaga thunai yaara irundhaa — athu dhan kadhal. Aana unnudaya nenju unakku thunai illa naa — adhu dhan kashtam."
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Amma, yaarum enakku thunai illa!'
Amma: 'Meera, un nenjukkaga thunai yaar?'
Meera: 'Adhu enna Amma?'
Amma: 'Nee avanukkaga thunai yaara irundhaa — athu dhan kadhal. Aana unnudaya nenju unakku thunai illa naa — adhu dhan kashtam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the temple entrance, a woman crying beside him, his hand raised in comfort.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Oruthanukku thunai yaar? Avanudaya nenju avanukku thunai illa naa — adhu dhan kashtam. Nee avanukkaga thunai yaara irundhaa — athu dhan kadhal."
thirukkural: துன்பத்திற்கு யாரே துணையாவார் தாமுடைய நெஞ்சந் துணையல் வழி.
english_translation: Who support a man in grief If lover's heart denies relief?
moral: The greatest grief is when your own heart won't support you.

title: Thamara Nenju
theme: Complaint to One's Own Heart / When Your Own Heart Is a Stranger
hook: Arjun realizes he doesn't know his own heart — Appa says that's the biggest journey of all.
story: Arjun sat on the steps. "Appa, en nenju enakke onnum illa. Naan avanukkaga pona — athu dhan. Aana naan enakke theriyaadha aalaa irukken." Appa sat beside him. "Adhu dhan kadhal — unnudaya nenju unnukke thamara aalaa irukkum. Adhai purinjukollradhu dhan periya yatrai."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Appa, en nenju enakke theriyaadha aalaa irukku!'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan kadhal — unnudaya nenju unnukke thamara aalaa irukkum.'
Arjun: 'Adhu enna Appa, thamara nenju?'
Appa: 'Adhu dhan — nee thamara aalaa irukkumbodhu, unnudaya nenju unnukke theriyaadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the cathedral steps, a candle in his hand, the flame steady in the evening breeze.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thamara nenju — unnudaya nenju unnukke thamara aalaa irukkum. Adhai purinjukollradhu dhan periya yatrai. Aana adhai purinjukondaa — athu dhan unmai."
thirukkural: தஞ்சம் தமரல்லர் ஏதிலார் தாமுடைய நெஞ்சம் தமரல் வழி.
english_translation: Why wonder if strangers disown When one's own heart is not his own?
moral: The deepest stranger is the one within — learn to know your own heart.

title: சிறிது பார்க்கலாம்
theme: Feigned Anger / Pouting to Test Love
hook: Aadhini watches a couple on the beach — the girl is ignoring the boy, and the boy is pacing.
story: On the beach, a girl sat with her back to a boy. The boy paced, sat, paced again. Aadhini laughed. "Thatha, ava enna pannudhu?" Thatha smiled. "Pout pannudhu da. Avanukku theriya varum — ava enna feel panradhunu." Ten minutes later, the boy brought ice cream. The girl turned, smiling through pretend anger. Thatha winked. "Paar, avanukku ippo therinjudhu."
tamil_dialogues: Aadhini: 'Thatha, ava kovama irukko?'
Thatha: 'Illada, ava ava feel aagradha kaatradhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a beach rock, waves lapping at his feet, watching the couple with a knowing smile.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout pannradhu — adhu love-oda sign dhan. Avan therinjukkavaaga pannradhu. Adhu dhan unmaiyana love."
thirukkural: புல்லா திராஅப் புலத்தை அவர்உறும் அல்லல்நோய் காண்கம் சிறிது.
english_translation: Feign sulk; embrace him not so that We can see his distress a bit
moral: Sometimes pretending to be upset helps us see how much the other person truly cares.

title: உப்பு அளவு
theme: Feigned Anger / The Right Amount of Pouting
hook: Amma's friend argues with her husband over a small thing — Amma explains the balance.
story: At a tea shop, auntie fought with uncle over buying the wrong saris color. "Neenga enna solringalo, naan pannalaam!" Vignesh whispered, "Amma, ivanga perusa fight panringala?" Amma laughed. "Adhu pout da, Vignesh. Konjam pannaa love-ah irukkum. Adhigam pannaa kovam aayidum." At home, Amma pouted when Appa forgot milk. He laughed and went to buy it. Vignesh understood — balance is everything.
tamil_dialogues: Vignesh: 'Amma, athi varukkumo?'
Amma: 'Aama, adhigam panna love aayidum. Konjam dhan sweet.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands beside a salt merchant's stall, holding a pinch of salt between his fingers.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Uppu maadhiri dhan pout. Konjam panna taste-ah irukkum. Adhigam panna uppu too much aayidum. Adhu dhan balance."
thirukkural: உப்பமைந் தற்றால் புலவி அதுசிறிது மிக்கற்றால் நீள விடல்.
english_translation: Sulking is the salt of love; but Too much of it spoils the taste
moral: A little pouting keeps love alive, but too much ruins it.

title: அழுதாள் பாரு
theme: Feigned Anger / Pouting Hurts the Other
hook: Meera's Akka is upset because her boyfriend didn't call — Meera sees the pain.
story: Akka sat on the Rockfort steps, phone in hand, eyes red. "Akka, enna achu?" Akka sniffled. "Avan call pannala." Meera saw the hurt. "Akka, neenga avanukku pout pannengalo? Avanukku valikkudhu pola." Akka stared. Meera was right. She had been pouting for three days. Now both were hurting.
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Akka, neenga avana vittu pout pannadhala, avanukku valikkudhu pola.'
Akka: 'Enakku dhan avan vittu valikkudhu...'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits beside Akka, a hand gently on her shoulder, the Rockfort shadow long behind them.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout pannravanukku valikkum nu ninaikkira per avanukkum valikkum. Rendu perukkum valikum — athu dhan pout-oda consequence."
thirukkural: அலந்தாரை அல்லல்நோய் செய்தற்றால் தம்மைப் புலந்தாரைப் புல்லா விடல்.
english_translation: To leave the sulker unembraced Is to grieve the one sorely grieved
moral: Pouting not only hurts you but also the person who loves you.

title: வாடிய பூ
theme: Feigned Anger / Ignoring Pouting Wastes Love
hook: Karthik's parents haven't spoken properly for two days — the house feels cold.
story: For two days, Amma didn't talk to Appa. She pouted, turned away, made food silently. Appa pretended not to notice. The house was like a freezer. Karthik whispered to Appa, "Appa, Amma pout panradhu ungaluku theriyadha?" Appa sighed. "Theriyum da. Aana naan response pannaala." That night, Appa brought jasmine flowers. Amma's eyes softened. Karthik learned — ignoring a pout is like cutting a dying plant.
tamil_dialogues: Karthik: 'Appa, Amma kova irukkanga, neenga onnum panringale!'
Appa: 'Panren da, avasaram illa nu nenaichen.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the temple garden, holding a wilting jasmine vine, its roots exposed.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout pannravanai ignore pannradhu, vadhaya pudhu poovai vettura maadhiri. Adhu kooda kaapadhalaana, love maaridum."
thirukkural: ஊடி யவரை உணராமை வாடிய வள்ளி முதலரிந் தற்று.
english_translation: To comfort not lady in pout Is to cut the fading plant at root
moral: Ignoring someone who is pouting is like pulling a plant by its roots — love withers.

title: பூ கண்ணார்
theme: Feigned Anger / Pouting Looks Beautiful in Love
hook: Diya sees Paati pout at Thatha and asks why she looks so pretty doing it.
story: In the temple garden, Paati arranged flowers. Thatha forgot to bring water. Paati pouted — eyebrows raised, lips pushed out. Diya giggled. "Paati, neenga pout pannumbodhu romba azhaga irukkeenga!" Paati blushed. "Adhu love dhan, kanna. Pout pannumbodhu kooda azhaga iruppom — nallavara irundha." Thatha brought water, smiling. Diya watched them and clapped.
tamil_dialogues: Diya: 'Paati, neenga pout pannumbodhu azhaga irukkeenga!'
Paati: 'Nallavara irundha, pout kooda azhaga irukkum da kanna.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits among the temple flowers, holding a single lotus, watching the old couple with gentle eyes.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout-oda azhagu — adhu love-la dhan theriyum. Nallavara irundha, pout kooda azhaga irukkum. Adhu dhan love-oda beauty."
thirukkural: நலத்தகை நல்லவர்க்கு ஏஎர் புலத்தகை பூஅன்ன கண்ணார் அகத்து.
english_translation: Pouting of flower-eyed has To pure good mates a lovely grace
moral: When love is true, even pouting looks beautiful.

title: கனியும் கருக்காயும்
theme: Feigned Anger / Love Without Pouting is Incomplete
hook: Arjun sees Paati and Thatha argue over a mango tree — then they laugh together.
story: Paati wanted mangoes pickled. Thatha wanted them ripe. "Neenga romba aasai!" "Nee dhan romba!" Arjun thought they were fighting. But five minutes later, Thatha gave Paati the ripest mango. Paati gave Thatha pickle. They both smiled. Arjun understood — the fight was the fun part. Without it, love is like a fruit that's neither ripe nor raw.
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Paati, neenga Thatha-oda fight pannringala?'
Paati: 'Adhu fight illada, adhu love. Adhu illama love dry aayidum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits under the mango tree, a ripe mango in one hand, a pickle jar in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout illaama love — adhu kanri pazhama irukkum. Pout irundha dhan love-ukku taste varum. Rendum venaam."
thirukkural: துனியும் புலவியும் இல்லாயின் காமம் கனியும் கருக்காயும் அற்று.
english_translation: Love devoid of frowns and pets Misses its ripe and unripe fruits
moral: Love without a little playful anger is like fruit that never ripens.

title: எப்போது சரியாகும்?
hook: Nithya's Akka is waiting for her boyfriend to text back after a pout — and she's anxious.
story: Akka checked her phone every two minutes. "Avan message pannuvaanoo? Panna maataano?" Nithya watched. "Akka, neenga avanukku pout pannengale — avan varuvaano?" Akka sighed. "Theriyadhu da. Adhu dhan pout-oda bayam — oru doubt varum, avan varuvaano, maataano." An hour later, a text. Akka's face lit up. Nithya exhaled.
tamil_dialogues: Nithya: 'Akka, avan message pannuvaanoo?'
Akka: 'Theriyadhu da. Adhu dhan pout-oda bayam.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a mall bench, a phone-shaped stone in his hand, looking at it with calm patience.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout pannumbodhu oru doubt varum — avan varuvaano illayaano. Adhu dhan pout-oda taste. Aana neram aana varuvaan."
thirukkural: ஊடலின் உண்டாங்கோர் துன்பம் புணர்வது நீடுவ தன்று கொல் என்று.
english_translation: "Will union take place soon or late?" In lover's pout this leaves a doubt
moral: Pouting brings uncertainty — but the reunion is always sweeter.

title: யார் இல்லை?
theme: Feigned Anger / Pouting is Pointless Without the Beloved
hook: Kiran sees a girl pouting alone on the riverbank — the person she's angry at isn't even there.
story: By the Vaigai, a girl sat alone, arms crossed, pouting at the water. Kiran asked Amma, "Amma, ava yaarukkaga pout panradhu?" Amma looked. "Avan illada — avan engaye irukaan nu theriyadhu. Adhu dhan pout-oda kastam." The girl's friend came. "Ava avanukku pout panradhu, aana avan innu city la irukaan." Kiran understood — pouting needs the beloved to make sense.
tamil_dialogues: Kiran: 'Amma, ava yaarukkaga kovama irukkanga?'
Amma: 'Avan illada. Adhu dhan pout-oda kashtam — yaarukkagano, avar illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands by the river, the girl's empty shadow beside him on the water.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout pannradhu — avan illaama pannradhu adhu kashtam. Yaarukkagano, avar irukka vendum. Illaana pout-ukku artham illa."
thirukkural: நோதல் எவன்மற்று நொந்தாரென்று அஃதறியும் காதலர் இல்லா வழி.
english_translation: What's the good of grieving lament When concious lover is not present?
moral: Pouting without the beloved nearby is pain without purpose.

title: நீரும் நிழலும்
theme: Feigned Anger / Pouting in Love is Natural as Water in Shade
hook: Thatha compares pouting to water seeking shade — it's the most natural thing in love.
story: By the tank, Shreya watched ducks follow each other. One duck turned away. The other followed. Thatha smiled. "Paaru, andha oru duck pout panradhu — adha vida naturally love pannradhu maadhiri." Shreya laughed. "Thatha, duck-ukkum pout irukku?" Thatha said, "Pout irukku, love irukku — adhu nature. Nee grow aanaalum theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Shreya: 'Thatha, duck-ukkum pout irukka?'
Thatha: 'Irukkum da. Adhu love-oda nature. Nee grow aanaalum theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the tank's edge, a duck resting beside him, the water calm and reflective.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Neer nizhal kitta irukkum — adhu nature. Adhe maadhiri, pout love-la irukkum — adhu kooda natural dhan. Adhai vida azhagu illa."
thirukkural: நீரும் நிழலது இனிதே புலவியும் வீழுநர் கண்ணே இனிது.
english_translation: Water delights in a shady grove And sulking in souls of psychic love
moral: Just as water naturally flows to shade, pouting naturally exists in love.

title: என் நெஞ்சம்
theme: Feigned Anger / The Heart Wants What It Wants
hook: Rohit's friend is pouting at his girlfriend but secretly wants to reconcile — Amma explains the heart.
story: Rohit's friend Ravi said, "Naan avanoda maatenn! Naan avana vida maatten!" But that night, Ravi called ten times. Amma overheard. "Amma, Ravi enna panradhu? Pout panradhu, aana avana vida maataano?" Amma smiled. "Adhu dhan manasu da. Velila pout, ulla love. Rendum ore neram nadakkum."
tamil_dialogues: Rohit: 'Amma, Ravi pout panradhu, aana avana kaanum nu nenaikiraan!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan manasu da. Ulla love, veliya pout.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple pond, his reflection split between surface and depth, one hand reaching down.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Manasu oru poal illa — veliya pout, ulla love. Rendum ore neram nadakkum. Adhu dhan manasoda beauty."
thirukkural: ஊடல் உணங்க விடுவாரோடு என்நெஞ்சம் கூடுவேம் என்பது அவா.
english_translation: My heart athirst would still unite With her who me in sulking left!
moral: The heart may pout, but it always longs for reunion.

title: கண்ணின் பொது
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Jealousy in Love
hook: Kavya watches her sister's boyfriend talk to another girl — and sees the jealousy.
story: In Pondy Bazaar, Akka's boyfriend waved at a girl from school. Akka's face changed. She turned away. "Akka, enna achu?" Akka muttered, "Avan vera aval kitta pesaaraan!" Kavya saw the jealousy. It wasn't anger — it was fear of losing love. "Akka, neenga avanukku pout pannuringale?" Akka nodded, eyes wet.
tamil_dialogues: Kavya: 'Akka, neenga avanoda pout pannringala?'
Akka: 'Pannren da, aana avan therinjukkaan illa.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the busy bazaar, surrounded by shoppers, yet his eyes fixed on the couple apart.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout-oda oru kaaranam — jealousy. Avan vera oruthiya pesaaradhunu therinjaa, manasu valikkum. Adhu dhan pout-oda reason."
thirukkural: பெண்ணியலார் எல்லாரும் கண்ணின் பொதுஉண்பர் நண்ணேன் பரத்தநின் மார்பு.
english_translation: I shrink to clasp you bosom lewd To the gaze of all ladies exposed
moral: Jealousy is the hidden root of many lovers' quarrels.

title: தும்மினாள்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / The Sneezing Trick
hook: Thatha sneezes and Paati refuses to say "live long" because they're in a pout — Aryan laughs.
story: Thatha sneezed loudly. Everyone waited for Paati to say "Irupiyaazhga!" But Paati stayed silent, reading her magazine. Aryan whispered, "Thatha, Paati sonnala!" Thatha grinned. "Avan pout-la irukkanga — adhu dhan fun." He sneezed again. Paati's lips twitched. Finally she muttered, "Irupiyaazhga..." and smiled. Aryan clapped. "Paati, neenga thooki vida maatteenga!"
tamil_dialogues: Aryan: 'Thatha, Paati live long sollala!'
Thatha: 'Pout-la irukkanga da. Adhu dhan love-oda fun.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the temple steps, pretending to sneeze, one eye on Paati.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout-la irukkumbodhu kooda love varum. Thumannalum 'live long' sollaama irukkalaam — aana love irukkum."
thirukkural: ஊடி இருந்தேமாத் தும்மினார் யாம்தம்மை நீடுவாழ் கென்பாக் கறிந்து.
english_translation: He sneezed while we went on sulking Expecting me to say "live long"
moral: Even in a pout, love finds ways to express itself.

title: கோட்டுப்பூ
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Flowers and Jealousy
hook: Amma wears flowers and Appa asks "for whom?" — Meena learns about playful jealousy.
story: Amma wore jasmine in her hair. Appa raised an eyebrow. "Indha poo yaarukkaga?" Amma laughed. "Ungalukkaga dhan!" Appa smiled but still looked suspicious. Meena giggled. "Amma, Appa kitta pout pannringala?" Amma whispered, "Adhu love da, kanna. Appa-ku enakku aaru varsham aachu, aana innum avanuku doubt varum — adhu dhan love."
tamil_dialogues: Appa: 'Indha poo yaarukkaga?'
Amma: 'Ungalukkaga dhan! Aana neenga doubt pannringa — adhu dhan love.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the garden, a jasmine garland in his hands, one flower falling loose.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Poo poottikittu 'yaarukkaga' nu kelvi — adhu dhan love. Aaru varsham aachu aana innum doubt — adhu dhan unmaiyaana love."
thirukkural: கோட்டுப்பூச் சூடினும் காயும் ஒருத்தியைக் காட்டிய சூடினீர் என்று.
english_translation: "For which lady?" she widely cries While I adorn myself with flowers
moral: Even after years together, a little jealousy keeps love fresh.

title: யாரினும் யாரினும்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / "More Than All" — Never Enough
hook: Siddharth overhears his sister's boyfriend say "I love you more than anyone" — and the fight begins.
story: On the beach, Divya's boyfriend said, "Naan ungarukkaga ellaarai vida kadaisilum valakkuren!" Divya pouted. "Yaarai vida? Yaarai vida?" He stammered. She smiled and walked away. Siddharth laughed. Divya turned. "Neenga enna sirikkeenga?" Siddharth said, "Pout pannradhu love dhan. Aana yaarai vida nu kelvi enna aagum?"
tamil_dialogues: Divya: 'Yaarai vida? Yaarai vida nu sollunga!'
Boyfriend: 'Ungalai vida yaarum illa!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on a stone by the shore temple, the sea breeze ruffling his hair, a smile playing.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Yaarai vida nu kelvi pout varum — adhu love-oda natural. Aana adhukku uru laabam illa. Adhu dhan pout-oda beauty."
thirukkural: யாரினும் காதலம் என்றேனா ஊடினாள் யாரினும் யாரினும் என்று.
english_translation: "I love you more than all" I said "Than whom, than whom?" she sulked and chid
moral: In love, "more than all" is never enough — the beloved always wants more.

title: கண்நிறை நீர்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Words That Move to Tears
hook: Amma tells Appa "we'll never part" — and both cry at the temple.
story: In the temple, Amma held Appa's hand. "Indha janmathula naama piriyama iruppom." Appa's eyes filled. Amma's too. Ananya watched. "Amma, neenga enga edhukku azhuringa?" Amma wiped her eyes. "Santhoshama da, kanna. Avanoda irukka mudiyum — adhu periya vaazhkai." Ananya didn't understand then. But she remembered.
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Indha janmathula naama piriyama iruppom.'
Appa: 'Avanoda irukka mudiyum — adhu periya vaazhkai.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands inside the temple, his own eyes glistening, the Meenakshi idol glowing behind him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Piriyama iruppom nu sollumbodhu kanla neer varum — adhu love-oda deep-ness. Adhu dhan love-oda peak."
thirukkural: இம்மைப் பிறப்பில் பிரியலம் என்றேனாக் கண்நிறை நீர்கொண் டனள்.
english_translation: "In this life we won't part" I told Her eyes at once with tears were filled
moral: The deepest love makes us weep with joy at the thought of never parting.

title: நினைத்தேன்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / "I Thought of You" — Trust and Doubt
hook: Varun tells Amma he thought of her all day — she pouts saying he forgets her often.
story: Varun said, "Amma, naan ungalai patti ellaa neramum yosichen!" Amma smiled but pouted. "Ella neramum? Innalu unga friend kitta phone-la pesittu irundheenga!" Varun laughed. "Amma, naan ungalai ninaichen — aana neenga pout panreenga!" Amma said, "Pout dhan da — unakku theriyum naan feel panren nu."
tamil_dialogues: Varun: 'Amma, naan ungalai yosichen!'
Amma: 'Yosichenaa? Phone-la pesittu irundheengale!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits on the terrace, stars above, a single thought-bubble floating from his hand.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Ninaichen nu solli pout pannradhu — adhu trust-oda sign. Unakku theriyum naan feel panren nu ninaikkiraan nu avanukku theriyum."
thirukkural: உள்ளினேன் என்றேன்மற் றென்மறந்தீர் என்றென்னைப் புல்லாள் புலத்தக் கனள்.
english_translation: I said I thought of you She left Her embrace crying "Oft you forget"
moral: Even reassurance can trigger a pout — because love wants constant proof.

title: தும்மினேன்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / The Blessing Trap
hook: Hari sneezes and Paati says "live long" — then she pouts because she thinks he was thinking of someone else.
story: Hari sneezed. Paati smiled. "Irupiyaazhga!" Then she paused. "Yaarai ninaichu thumannineenga?" Hari laughed. "Paati, enakku cold dhan!" Paati pouted. "Cold-nu solli thappikittenga!" Hari hugged her. "Paati, naan ungalai ninaichu thumannen!" Paati's eyes softened. "Adhu podhum."
tamil_dialogues: Paati: 'Yaarai ninaichu thumannineenga?'
Hari: 'Paati, enakku cold dhan!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the temple entrance, a handkerchief in his hand, waiting to sneeze.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thumannal 'live long' solradhu, adhu love-oda small joy. Aana pout pannumbodhu kelvi varum — yaarai ninaichu thumannina?"
thirukkural: வழுத்தினாள் தும்மினேன் ஆக அழித்தழுதாள் யாருள்ளித் தும்மினீர் என்று.
english_translation: I sneezed; she blessed; then changed and wept "You sneezed now at which lady's thought?"
moral: In love, even a sneeze becomes a reason to question and pout.

title: எம்மை மறைத்திரோ
theme: Pleasures of Variance / The Hidden Thought
hook: Nisha sees Akka cry because her boyfriend sneezed without saying "who" — it's about trust.
story: At the dam, Akka's boyfriend sneezed. He didn't look at her. He just wiped his nose. Akka's eyes filled. "Avan yaarai ninaichu thumanninaaro nu sollala!" Nisha was confused. "Akka, adhu thanni dhan?" Akka shook her head. "Adhu nambikkai da. Avan enna ninaikiraan nu solla vendiyadhu." That night, he called and explained. Akka pouted but smiled.
tamil_dialogues: Akka: 'Avan enna ninaikiraan nu sollala!'
Nisha: 'Akka, adhu thanni dhan!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the dam's edge, water rushing below, a single thought hidden behind his calm face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout pannumbodhu — manasula enna ninaikkiraan nu theriya vendum. Adhu trust. Illaana pout kashtama irukkum."
thirukkural: தும்முச் செறுப்ப அழுதாள் நுமர்உள்ளல் எம்மை மறைத்திரோ என்று.
english_translation: I repressed sneeze; she wept crying "Your thoughts from me you are hiding"
moral: Pouting is often about hidden fears and the need for transparency.

title: பிறர்க்கும் நீர்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Coaxing Triggers More Pouting
hook: Aditya tries to calm Amma's pout — but every attempt makes her pout more.
story: Amma pouted because Appa forgot her birthday. Aditya tried. "Amma, Appa marandhutaanga!" Amma pouted more. He brought coffee. She turned away. He told a joke. She smirked, then pouted again. Aditya gave up. "Amma, neenga pout pannumbodhu naan enna pannalum kuraikkaadha!" Amma laughed. "Adhu dhan da — pout pannra podhu coax panna koodadhu."
tamil_dialogues: Aditya: 'Amma, enna pannalum neenga pout aagaleenga!'
Amma: 'Adhu dhan da — pout pannra podhu coax panna koodadhu.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the garden, holding a watering can that pours on a plant that grows more with each drop.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Coax pannumbodhu pout adhigam aagum — adhu love-oda game. Adhu dhan pout-oda speciality."
thirukkural: தன்னை உணர்த்தினும் காயும் பிறர்க்கும்நீர் இந்நீரர் ஆகுதிர் என்று.
english_translation: I try to coax her and she remarks "Your coaxing others thus this marks"
moral: The more you try to calm a pout, the more it grows — that's the game of love.

title: யாருள்ளி நோக்கினீர்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / The Accusing Gaze
hook: Rohan stares at a girl's painting in the temple — Amma pouts and asks "who were you looking at?"
story: In the temple museum, Rohan admired a painting of a girl. Amma saw. "Yaar adhu? Neenga enna paathuteenga?" Rohan laughed. "Amma, adhu painting dhan!" Amma pouted. "Painting-naalum, neenga ava kitta nalla paarvinga!" Rohan understood — Amma wanted to be the only one he looked at.
tamil_dialogues: Amma: 'Adhu yaar? Neenga ava kitta nalla paarvinga!'
Rohan: 'Amma, adhu painting dhan!'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the museum, pointing at a painting of a woman, his expression unreadable.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout pannumbodhu — yaar kitta paarkiraan nu kelvi varum. Adhu love-oda jealousy. Painting naalum kooda kelvi varum."
thirukkural: நினைத்திருந்து நோக்கினும் காயும் அனைத்துநீர் யாருள்ளி நோக்கினீர் என்று.
english_translation: I think and gaze at her; she chides: "On whom your thought just now abides?"
moral: In love, even looking at a painting can trigger jealousy and pouting.

title: தவறிலர்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Pouting Even When Innocent
hook: Thatha didn't do anything wrong but Paati still pouts — Meenakshi asks why.
story: Thatha brought Paati's favorite laddu from the temple. Paati still pouted. "Paati, Thatha laddu vandhurukku!" Paati looked away. Meenakshi whispered to Thatha, "Paati enna panringa? Unmaiye theriyama pout panringa!" Thatha laughed. "Adhu dhan love, kanna. Thappu pannalum pout, sari pannalum pout. Rendum ore love."
tamil_dialogues: Meenakshi: 'Paati, Thatha enna thappu pannanga?'
Paati: 'Thappu pannala. Aana pout panradhuku thappu venaama?'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the temple corridor, a laddu in his palm, offering it to no one.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Thavariilarkku kooda pout varum — adhu love-oda speciality. Thappu illaamaalum pout pannalaam. Adhu dhan unmaiyaana love."
thirukkural: இல்லை தவறவர்க்கு ஆயினும் ஊடுதல் வல்லது அவர்அள஧க்கு மாறு.
english_translation: He is flawless; but I do pout So that his loving ways show out
moral: In true love, pouting happens even without reason — it's the language of the heart.

title: சிறுதுனி நல்லளி
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Small Pouts Lead to Big Love
hook: Amma says every small pout adds beauty to love — like flowers after rain.
story: On the boat, Amma and Appa teased each other. Appa forgot the sunscreen. Amma pouted. Five minutes later, Appa shared his hat. Amma smiled. "Paaru, Sneha — chinna chinna pout pannumbodhu love adhigam aagum." Sneha thought. "Amma, pout illaama love irukkaadha?" Amma laughed. "Adhu kai kaachi love — adhu dry aayidum."
tamil_dialogues: Sneha: 'Amma, pout illaama love irukkaadha?'
Amma: 'Irukum, aana adhu kai kaachi love. Pout irundha dhan fresh-a irukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits in the boat, a single flower blooming from his fingertips, floating on the lake.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Chinna chinna pout — adhu love-ukku poo maadhiri. Muzhukka poo peyrum. Adhu dhan pout-oda valimai."
thirukkural: ஊடலின் தோன்றும் சிறுதுனி நல்லளி வாடினும் பாடு பெறும்.
english_translation: Fading first, love blooms and outlives The petty pricks that pouting gives
moral: Small pouts are like seeds — they bloom into deeper love over time.

title: புத்தேள்நாடு
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Heaven in Pouting
hook: Amma says the heaven of pouting is when two hearts join like water and earth.
story: At the beach, waves touched the sand and retreated. "Amma, kaadal enna?" Amma pointed at the waves. "Paaru — neerum mannum serum podhu, athu dhan heaven. Adhe maadhiri, rendu per pout pannikittu serum podhu — athu dhan puthu heaven." Kabir watched the waves. "Appo pout-oda love dhan periya heaven?" Amma smiled. "Nee purinjukitte."
tamil_dialogues: Kabir: 'Amma, kaadal enna?'
Amma: 'Neerum mannum serum podhu — athu dhan heaven. Pout pannittu serum podhu kooda.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits where the sea meets the sand, water lapping at his toes, merging with the earth beneath.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout pannittu serumbodhu — oru puthu ulagam varum. Neer mann serum podhu enna, adhe love-la kooda irukkum."
thirukkural: புலத்தலின் புத்தேள்நாடு உண்டோ நிலத்தொடு நீரியைந் தன்னார் அகத்து.
english_translation: Is there a heaven like sulk beneath Of hearts that join like water and earth?
moral: The reunion after pouting is heaven itself — like water merging with earth.

title: உடைக்கும் படை
theme: Pleasures of Variance / The Weapon of Pouting
hook: Amma says pouting is like a weapon that can break even the strongest heart.
story: Aravind watched a sculpture being carved. "Amma, kal enna panradhu?" Amma said, "Kal-ukku vali illa. Aana kaathalukku vali irukku. Pout pannumbodhu, oru weapon maadhiri adhu manasai udaikkum." Aravind laughed. "Amma, neenga Appa kitta pout pannumbodhu, Appa enna panraaru?" Amma smiled. "Udane varuvaaru. Adhu dhan weapon-oda power."
tamil_dialogues: Aravind: 'Amma, pout enna panradhu?'
Amma: 'Manasai udaikkum oru aayudham. Aana adhu love-oda aayudham.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands between two Rathas, a stone chisel in one hand, a delicate carving in the other.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout — adhu aayudham maadhiri irukkum. Manasai udaikkum. Aana adhu love-oda aayudham, adhu destroy pannadhu, connect pannum."
thirukkural: புல்லி விடாஅப் புலவியுள் தோன்றுமென் உள்ளம் உடைக்கும் படை.
english_translation: In long pout after embrace sweet A weapon is up to break my heart
moral: Pouting is a gentle weapon — it breaks through walls and reaches the heart.

title: மென்றோள் அகறலின்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Soft Arms, Sharp Pouts
hook: Meera watches Paati's soft hands wave goodbye but sharp words in pout — both are love.
story: Paati waved at Thatha at the temple gate. "Poi va, poi va!" Her hands were soft. But when Thatha forgot to bring her medicine, "Neenga enna manushan?! Enna marandhutaenga?!" Meera laughed. "Paati, neenga romba soft-a wave panninga, aana kitta vandha kovama solreenga!" Paati smiled. "Adhu dhan love, kanna. Soft-um, sharp-um rendume love."
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Paati, neenga enna romba kovama solringa?'
Paati: 'Adhu love dhan, kanna. Kitta irukkaravara kitta dhan solvaanga.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple gate, Paati's wave still in the air, Thatha's retreating figure in the distance.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Mennaga irundhaalum, pout sharp-a irukkalaam. Aana rendume love. Adhu dhan love-oda beauty."
thirukkural: தவறிலர் ஆயினும் தாம்வீழ்வார் மென்றோள் அகறலின் ஆங்கொன் றுடைத்து.
english_translation: Though free form faults, one feels the charms Of feigned release from lover's arms
moral: Even the softest arms can push away in pout — both hardness and softness are love.

title: ஊடல் இனிது
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Pout Sweeter Than Food
hook: Amma says pouting in love is sweeter than eating — Kavin doesn't believe until he sees Appa.
story: In the palace kitchen, Amma cooked. Appa tasted the sambar. "Uppu kammi!" Amma pouted. Appa laughed. "Uppu kammi-nu solradha vida, nee pout panradhu innum sweet." Kavin was eating. "Amma, saapdadhuku vida pout sweet-aa?" Amma looked at Appa. "Paaru — saapdrom, aana pout dhan innum irukkum. Adhu dhan love-oda taste."
tamil_dialogues: Kavin: 'Amma, saapdadhuku vida pout sweet-aa?'
Amma: 'Sapdadhuku vida pout innum sweet. Adhu dhan love-oda taste.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the kitchen table, a plate of food untouched, savoring the sweetness of the air.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Saapdadhuku vida pout sweet — adhu love-oda taste. Uppu irukkum, innum pout irukkum, rendume innum irukkum."
thirukkural: உணலினும் உண்டது அறல்இனிது காமம் புணர்தலின் ஊடல் இனிது.
english_translation: Sweeter than meal is digestion And sulk in love than union
moral: The sweetness of pouting surpasses even the sweetness of food — it's love's special flavor.

title: கூடலிற் காணப்படும்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / The Winner is Found in Reunion
hook: Diya's friend says whoever gives in during a pout wins — the proof is in the reunion.
story: Akka and her boyfriend hadn't spoken for two days. Then he came to the temple. He brought her favorite jasmine. "Neenga thooki vida maatteengala?" He smiled. "Naan thookiten." Akka smiled too. Diya whispered, "Akka, avar thooki vituttaaru, avan dhan jeichaaaru?" Akka laughed. "Adhu dhan — pout-la yaaru jeichadhu nu koodal-la dhan theriyum."
tamil_dialogues: Diya: 'Akka, avan dhan jeichaaaru?'
Akka: 'Aama, pout-la yaaru jeichadhu nu koodal-la dhan theriyum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar sits at the temple entrance, a jasmine garland in his hands, a smile on his face.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout-la yaaru jeichadhu nu koodal-la dhan theriyum. Koodumbodhu — yaarum jeichathu, rendume jeichathu."
thirukkural: ஊடலில் தோற்றவர் வென்றார் அதுமன்னும் கூடலிற் காணப் படும்.
english_translation: The yielder wins in lover's pout Reunited joy brings it out
moral: The one who gives in during pouting truly wins — the proof is in the joy of reunion.

title: உப்பு தோன்றிய கூடல்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Salt of Pout in Reunion
hook: Vishal tastes tears of joy during reunion — like salt that appears after pouting.
story: Vishal's parents argued over a phone bill. Two days of pouting. Then Appa came home early. "Ippo enna?" Amma said. Appa sat close. "Naan thappu pannen." Amma's eyes filled. Vishal saw the tears. "Amma, neenga azhuringalaa? Santhoshama?" Amma wiped her eyes. "Aama da — pout mudinjathuku aprom varra neer, athu uppu maadhiri irukkum. Aana athu santhoshathu uppu."
tamil_dialogues: Vishal: 'Amma, neenga azhuringalaa?'
Amma: 'Santhoshama da. Pout mudinjathuku aprom varra neer, athu uppu maadhiri irukkum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands in the home, a single tear drop crystallizing into salt in his palm.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout mudinjathuku aprom varra neer — adhu uppu maadhiri irukkum. Aana athu santhoshathu uppu. Adhu dhan love-oda taste."
thirukkural: ஊடிப் பெறுகுவம் கொல்லோ நுதல்வெயர்ப்பக் கூடலில் தோன்றிய உப்பு.
english_translation: Shall not our pouting again give The dew-browed joy of joint love?
moral: The tears of reunion taste like salt — the beautiful residue of love's pout.

title: ஊடுக மன்னோ
theme: Pleasures of Variance / Sulking On, Night Prolongs
hook: Amma says "let her pout, let the night last" — Arjun learns that prolonging pout prolongs love's sweetness.
story: At the sunrise point, a couple sat apart. The girl pouted at the sea. The boy sat beside her, quiet. Arjun asked, "Amma, avanga enna pannringa?" Amma whispered, "Pout pannringa. Avan 'night neenga pogattum' nu nenaikiraan." Arjun watched the sunrise. "Appo pout pogama irundha, night neenga pogattum nu sollalaam?" Amma smiled. "Adhu dhan love — night neenga pogattum, pout neenga pogattum."
tamil_dialogues: Arjun: 'Amma, pout pogama irundha, night neenga pogattum nu sollalaam?'
Amma: 'Aama da. Adhu dhan love — night-aiyum pout-aiyum neenga pogattum nu ninaikiraan.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the sunrise point, the night sky behind him, the first light touching his face, a girl's silhouette pouting beside him.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout pogama irukkattum, iravu neenga pogattum — adhu love-oda deep desire. Pout irukkumbodhu dhan love-oda night azhaga irukkum."
thirukkural: ஊடுக மன்னோ ஒளியிழை யாமிரப்ப நீடுக மன்னோ இரா.
english_translation: Sulking on O belle of shining jewels! Prolong O night! our delight swells!
moral: When love is deep, even pout and night are welcomed — they extend the sweetness of togetherness.

title: கூடி முயங்கப் பெறின்
theme: Pleasures of Variance / The Final Joy of Reunion
hook: The ultimate joy — pout and reunion together make love complete.
story: In the temple, Amma and Appa held hands. "Amma, neenga Appa kitta pout pannringala?" Amma laughed. "Pannren da. Appa thooki vaaruvaaru. Aprom — adhu dhan full joy." Meera thought. "Appo pout-ukku aprom dhan love-oda peak?" Amma looked at Appa. "Aama da — pout pannittu, koodi, rendume santhoshama irukkumbodhu dhan love complete aagum. Adhu dhan last joy."
tamil_dialogues: Meera: 'Amma, pout-ukku aprom dhan love-oda peak?'
Amma: 'Aama da — pout pannittu koodumbodhu dhan love complete aagum.'
thiruvalluvar_appears: Valluvar stands at the temple sanctum, hands folded, the divine light between him and the deity, all love complete.
thiruvalluvar_explanation: "Pout pannittu, koodi, rendume santhoshama irukkumbodhu dhan love complete aagum. Adhu dhan kaadal-oda final destination."
thirukkural: ஊடுதல் காமத்திற்கு இன்பம் அதற்கின்பம் கூடி முயங்கப் பெறின்.
english_translation: Bouderie is lovers' delight Its delight grows when they unite
moral: The final joy of love is the reunion after pout — when both hearts become one again.
